generated_text: I woke up this morning feeling excited about the day ahead, which is strange because I didn’t have anything specific planned, but that’s how I roll I guess—always ready for an adventure or just a spontaneous coffee date. I think I might text Sarah later to see if she wants to grab a latte. I love that caffeine buzz, it just lights me up and I can talk to people for hours. Speaking of which, I’ve been really into this new café that just opened down the street. It has these amazing pastries and the vibe is so chill. I could see myself being a regular there. I like the idea of finding a spot where everybody knows my name, like in that show Cheers, but I wonder if they’d name a pastry after me if I visit enough. A “Chamille Croissant” or something, sounds fancy! It makes me think about how people can be so different you know? Like, I love meeting new people and hearing their stories. I could sit at a table and just listen. There’s always something to learn. Just the other day, I was at the library, and I met this girl who’s studying marine biology, and she had the coolest stories about her research trips. I found myself asking a million questions and she seemed to light up talking about it—just like I do when I talk about my photography. I can’t believe how much beauty there is around us waiting to be captured, I want to take pictures of everything! I really need to get out more with my camera, maybe hit the park or something this weekend. I always get so inspired by nature and the way light hits the trees just right—like those Instagram-worthy moments that everyone loves. Then I feel this urge to create. Oh, and I can’t forget about the art show opening next week. I should definitely ask a bunch of friends to go with me. They never say no to my invitations. I think it’s because they know I’m going to be the one to make the evening fun, right? Or at least I hope so! You know what else is on my mind? Volunteering at that animal shelter. I signed up a while ago, but I keep putting it off. Cats and dogs need someone to play with and love them. They bring so much joy, plus it’s good to give back. The thought of spending time with those cuddle bugs gets me all shivery with happiness. I just need to get my act together and schedule a time to go. It’s funny how I plan all these social outings but somehow forget to pencil in time for the things that matter. I’m really trying to work on that, juggling everything, keeping my commitments while making sure I still enjoy my interests, it’s like a balancing act. Sometimes I feel like I should challenge myself more. I wonder if I should try something completely out of my comfort zone. Maybe a dance class or a public speaking workshop. That could be totally wild. I love to talk, but speaking in front of people is a whole different ball game. Maybe I could make it a fun thing with friends. They’d probably agree to join me. We could giggle our way through it and feel like we’re doing something totally absurd but in the best way. I think we’d all walk out of it feeling like conquerors of the world! Oh man, and then there’s always travel. I’ve been dreaming about a road trip across the country, just driving, stopping at quirky roadside attractions and exploring small towns. I think it would be so cool to meet different people along the way, capturing their stories for my photo albums. Every place has its own character and charm, and I just want to soak it all in. The thought of being spontaneous fills me with excitement. Just pack a bag and hit the road—maybe I could do it next summer. It’s just that life feels so big and so full of potential, doesn’t it? Sometimes, I just need to remember to take it one day at a time, meet new faces, appreciate those little moments, and dive into every experience. I’ve got so much energy, and I want to share it. I believe every person I meet holds a piece of wisdom or a story they’re just waiting to tell. It’s about connections, and that’s what makes life colorful, like a kaleidoscope of experiences flying around. I’m going to finish this essay now and shoot a text to Sarah. The sooner I do it, the sooner we can start planning our next adventure. I can’t wait!

I can’t believe how loud the campus is right now, there are people everywhere, chatting, laughing, it’s all kind of overwhelming but also exciting. I don’t know, sometimes I thrive in chaos. I’m definitely one of those people who loves to be around others. The energy of a crowd is like this weird electric current runs through me I can’t explain it but when I’m in class and everyone’s talking back and forth it gets me fired up. But then again, I can feel my chest tightening when I think about the group project deadlines coming up. Ugh, I know I have to keep pushing but it’s like my thoughts are tangled with anxiety, and sometimes I just want to take charge and tell everyone what to do which probably makes me less agreeable than most people. It’s annoying that I feel that way but hey, I get things done, right? Like, when I say I want something done, I mean I want it done right and I can’t stand when people don’t take it seriously. I just want that ‘A’ on the project, it’s more than just a grade. It’s like my pride is at stake. My roommate keeps saying I need to chill, but how can I chill when deadlines are looming? It stresses me out just thinking about it, and I’m constantly worried that something will fall through the cracks. I mean, I have a planner, I have a color-coded system for my assignments. Ever since I started using it I’ve felt a bit more in control but still, there’s always this restlessness in me. I can’t escape it even though I try hard to be on top of everything. I don’t know if everyone feels this way or if it’s just me. Sometimes I wonder if other people are just more relaxed about everything, like maybe they can wing it. I can’t wing it; I need structure. It feels like a double-edged sword because I’m so organized but then I flip out over little things that don’t go my way. My emotions go haywire, and I hate that. My mind races from one extreme to another, one minute I’m laughing and the next I’m on the verge of tears if someone says something that rubs me the wrong way. I can’t help it. I guess being low on agreeableness makes me more sensitive to those things, but isn’t it just human nature to want to be recognized? I mean, if I put in the effort, I expect acknowledgment for it. Why should I have to hide my feelings? But that’s not the world we live in; I know that. I can't afford to show too much emotion, especially if I want to be taken seriously. I feel like I’m trying to play a game where everyone else knows the rules and I’m just fumbling around trying to catch up. I wonder sometimes how people can just be so open and carefree about things. Why can’t I do that? It’s probably why I struggle to connect with some people. I mean, yeah, I enjoy social situations but if it starts to get too personal, I put up walls. I’d rather keep it light, keep it fun, and not dive into the feelings because feelings lead to vulnerability, vulnerability makes me tense, and who wants to be tense? I prefer to be the one controlling the narrative, not the other way around. I know it sounds harsh, but I don’t have a lot of patience when it comes to people’s drama. I want to be helpful, sure, but I can’t deal with constant emotional outbursts. I have my own stuff to manage. I keep focusing on my goals, my grades, and that’s what matters in the end right? But then my mind twists around itself and I feel guilty for not being more supportive, for not being the type of friend who listens to all the emotional outbursts. It’s a mess. I guess it’s when I’m stuck with my own thoughts that I spiral. So I dive into my work, and it’s a little easier that way. Staying busy helps quiet the chaos. Plus, I’m good at it. I can plan and execute like nobody’s business. But sometimes I just wonder, will I ever feel like I can truly relax? Maybe I don’t know how to do that. Maybe I’m too driven, too focused on succeeding. But what does success even mean if it comes at the cost of my well-being? It’s a constant battle, this push and pull. I thrive on structure but crave freedom. I feel like I’m in a loop, a cycle of chasing something that’s just out of reach, and in moments of clarity, I want to grasp it but then the chaos comes crashing back in, and I’m left with nothing but questions.

I don’t really know what to start with. I’m sitting here and my thoughts feel so jumbled it’s hard to grab onto one long enough to spit it out. This is kind of like being in a room full of people and everyone is talking over each other. I guess that’s part of the problem—everyone has something to say but it’s all just noise. I prefer quiet, which is ironic because my brain doesn’t really know how to shut up. Maybe I should talk about how social situations make me anxious, like that time in class when the professor asked me a question and everyone turned to look at me. I just fumbled through a response, my heart racing, and I could hear my voice shaking. I wonder if they could see how I felt— I felt like a deer in headlights, just frozen there in front of the class. I don't like putting myself in those situations, these interactions where everyone's buzzing around trying to connect but they're actually just trying to fill the silence. Interactions are so complicated. People often overlap their opinions like a Venn diagram that doesn’t make sense, but I like observing it, piecing together thoughts that don’t seem to belong together. I feel like I see things differently than most people which, I guess, is my openness. I don’t mind the weird and unconventional, in fact, I’m drawn to it, the bizarre things in life that most people overlook. Like, who even notices the way the light reflects off that weird, old bench outside the library? But I can’t just enjoy it in silence. I want to shout about how strange and beautiful that is but then again, who would care? That’s not how conversation works. People want to share their own stuff, their own feelings. I guess I’m glad I'm not like them in that way, I’d rather be alone. Social media is just a whole other thing that grates on my nerves. Everyone shares their "perfect" lives, and I just scroll and think how fake it all feels. Like, calm down, it’s fine to show the ugly parts too. But that’s just me. I’ve always been a little off compared to everyone else. Neuroticism, oh man, it controls so much. I overthink everything; it’s like I’m trapped in an endless loop of what-ifs. What if I said something stupid? What if they think I’m weird? What if I’m extra weird because I’m not saying anything? The worst part is that people can get annoyed with that. Doesn’t matter how valid my feelings are, if you don’t share them, it’s like you’re not part of the group. I can’t figure out why it makes me so anxious. I mean, who cares what people think? But deep down, somewhere hidden, I do care, and that annoys me more than anything. I wish I could turn off the part of my brain that worries—neuroticism feels like a friend and foe at the same time. What a paradox. I read somewhere that people often find comfort in structure, like the routine of doing things in a certain order every day. I’ve tried that, and yeah, it can be soothing at times, but honestly? I thrive in the chaos when it comes to ideas. I love spontaneity and random trips, or just diving into an off-the-wall book about something utterly bizarre that no one else sees the appeal of, like those weird conspiracy theories about ancient civilizations, or the science of lucid dreaming. I don't care if most people think it’s silly; it’s like exploring uncharted territory in my mind. My thoughts are all over the place, but that’s just me following the rabbit holes and maybe it’s messy but it’s mine. But then there’s this nagging voice that’s like, “You should’ve done that differently.” Or, “You should’ve said more in class.” And it’s exhausting. Sometimes I want to scream, just let it all out, but instead, I sit quietly, plagued by my own analysis of every single interaction. I want to break out of this bubble but when will I? I try to remind myself that it’s okay to be outside the box, but the box keeps closing in on me. I appreciate the fact that I can look at things other people can’t, yet it feels so isolating. Like I’m stuck in my own world, tickled by the oddities of life while everyone else is just living. Maybe that’s why I find it so hard to connect. I’m concerned but not agreeable enough to just go along with what everyone else is feeling. I want to push boundaries but struggle with my own thoughts. I’m still here, and I’m still thinking. The restless spinning doesn’t stop. I wonder if it ever will. I mean, is this just how it is? I suppose I’ll have to find a way to make peace with it.

I’ve been thinking a lot about how it feels to be around people, like that rush of energy when I walk into a room and everyone’s alive and talking and I just want to jump in and say something funny or engaging or just something that makes people smile. It’s great, isn’t it? I can’t stand silence, it feels so heavy, like an anchor pulling me down. But then again, sometimes I think, what if I say something totally awkward and make it worse? That’s the neurotic part of me, the overthinker. I want to be liked, I want people to enjoy my presence, it matters so much, way too much sometimes. I’m always scanning the room, are they enjoying what I'm saying? Should I change the topic? Anxiety just buzzes under my skin like a swarm of bees, or maybe it’s butterflies because sometimes it feels like excitement but mostly it’s those damn bees. Oh, and then there are those moments when I just spill out all my thoughts without thinking—like a word-vomit situation—and I can see people nodding but also raising an eyebrow, should I have said that? I worry but I want to be real. I want to connect deeply. When I do connect, it’s like fireworks, the lights, the noise, the thrill of something electric in the air. I can’t get enough of that. And then there’s my habit of jumping from one idea to another, like when the professor drags on in class and my brain darts off to the latest Netflix show I binge-watched last week or the random TikTok I saw about some random conspiracy theory about aliens. Do I really believe in aliens? Some days I think yes, some days maybe no, who knows? But it’s fun to think about all the possibilities, right? I think life is too interesting to settle. Why do things have to be rigid? I mean, why not explore all these possibilities? I love trying new foods and meeting new people, it’s like every experience adds color to my world. I want to learn everything—art, music, philosophy, all those big questions. What’s the point of all this? And I hope people will understand that I might come off as scattered or flitty but it’s just because I’m absorbing everything like a sponge. What if I can’t keep track of it all? What if someone asks me a question about the last thing I said and I blank? I can’t even remember half the stuff I say sometimes but still, I love being around friends, their laughter and joy it makes me feel alive. But then, things get complicated. I care too much about what people think. I’m super agreeable, which means I want to avoid conflict at all costs, like the time I didn’t speak my mind during that group project and just went along with what everyone else wanted even though I had a much better idea. I do that a lot, just go with the flow even if it makes my skin crawl. But I can’t stand being the weak link in a group, so I’ll just hide my true feelings, pretend everything’s fine and that kind of backfires because I end up feeling resentful and that little bee swarm gets bigger. Sometimes I need to just break free and express how I feel honestly but then my nerves kick in—like walking on a tightrope. And what even is this feeling of restlessness? I want to explore! Go out and try new things, see the world outside my little bubble, but sometimes that becomes overwhelming. Should I go to that party tonight alone? What if no one talks to me? Or worse, what if I go and say something stupid like I did last time. That was not a fun night. But then I think, what if it’s a blast? What if I meet someone really cool? I can’t help but get excited about the possibilities. I thrive on those moments, whether it’s meeting new friends or just having a spontaneous adventure. Life is too short to just sit at home all the time. But God, the anxiety—it can grip and twist and make me second-guess every decision. Sometimes I envy people who seem so put-together, who don’t feel the need to constantly validate their worth through social interactions. I wish I could be more organized, maybe then I wouldn’t feel so scattered all the time, but then again, does that mean I’d have to give up a piece of who I am? Maybe I wouldn’t want to. The world is chaotic, and I guess I just want to dance in the madness. I want the noise and the laughter and the real connections but at the same time, I want to be comfortable in my own skin. It’s this push and pull that never really goes away. Am I making any sense? I don't even know, really, but this is what runs through my head, all these thoughts and feelings swirling together like a big messy paint palette, just waiting to be splattered everywhere.

I can’t believe I forgot to bring my favorite pen to class today. It’s the one that writes smoothly and makes my notes look amazing. Why can’t I just keep a stash of pens in my backpack? I’m always losing things. I should probably start making lists or something, or would that be too boring? Lists are helpful but it feels like I’m boxing myself in. I like spontaneity; the thrill of not knowing what's next is exhilarating. I remember this one time, I was in the middle of a study group and then just decided to go grab coffee instead. Why not? I needed caffeine. I can’t function without it. My friends were annoyed though but I feel like they get over it. I mean who wouldn’t want coffee when you could just sit there looking at textbooks? The hustle of a cafe, the sound of milk frothing, it’s like a little calming chaos that I thrive on. I keep thinking about my friend’s birthday party this weekend. Do I really want to go? Parties can be so draining, but I know everyone will be there and I can’t miss out on the social scene. But what if it gets too loud? I might get overwhelmed. I can handle it though, I know I can. I love being around people. That rush of laughter, the way conversations just flow, especially when they go deep. I crave those connections but sometimes I just want a quiet moment to breathe. I can’t believe I forgot to RSVP to the party, I need to text them back. Maybe I’ll just pop in for a little bit– just enough to say I was there, to let them know I care. I mean, I care. I really do, even if I come off as a bit brash sometimes. I just get restless sitting still. Which brings me to this study I heard about. They’re looking at behaviors and how people react in social situations. It’s fascinating how different people are wired. I can feel the energy in a room shift when someone walks in. Like, if someone is super anxious, I can pick up on that vibe instantly. It’s like a sixth sense. But then again, I can also get anxious. The way my stomach drops when I have to present in class, it’s as if I’m standing on the edge of a cliff. I don’t know if I’m more afraid of speaking or of what people will think afterwards. What if I mess up? What if they judge me? I know I shouldn't care but it’s hard when you’re exposed like that. I guess that’s where the neuroticism kicks in. Overthinking every single detail, analyzing every conversation. Did I say the right thing? Did I make them laugh or did I just make it awkward? My brain is a wild machine constantly juggling thoughts like a circus performer. I should probably meditate or something. Speaking of meditation, there was this cool technique I read about. It’s like visualizing energy flowing through your body. I wonder if that would actually help. I mean, I could use some grounding. I have so many ideas bouncing around in my head; sometimes I just want to stop and breathe. I have this project due next week and I’m so excited about it but also kind of freaking out about deadlines because I want it to be perfect. Perfectionism can be exhausting. What if I don’t get the grade I want? Or what if I finish and realize my idea was terrible? I can’t go down that rabbit hole. Just focus on the creativity, the process! I love brainstorming all these vibrant ideas but then I get frustrated when they don’t translate well onto paper. Why can’t my mind just sync up with my hands? And then there’s the travel bug. I really want to travel after I graduate but what if I can’t find a job right away? The world is so big and exciting and I don't want to be stuck in one place. I want to feel the vibrancy of new cities, the culture, the food, the people. Meeting people from different backgrounds and hearing their stories lights me up. Maybe I can plan a trip with my friends. We could go backpacking! There's something about the spontaneity of travel that just calls to me. But what if something goes wrong? That’s my neurotic side creeping back in. Sometimes I think I should chill out and just enjoy the ride. Life isn’t always about the destination, it’s about the journey or whatever. I keep saying I’ll take a break, but I never do. I’m always on the go, always have something planned. Maybe that’s why I struggle with relationships. I push people away because I get overwhelmed or frustrated. Or I don’t want to deal with the emotional complexities but I crave those connections too. Sometimes I can be a little too blunt and people don’t get my humor. I just need to be more mindful. Well, I guess I have a lot to think about. But that’s okay, I thrive on the chaos of it all!

I’m in this coffee shop right now and it’s buzzing with energy, people chatting, laughter spilling out from every corner, I love that, there’s a group of friends on my left, they look like they’re having the time of their lives, I can’t help but smile at them, they don’t even know me but it feels like I’m part of their moment, I want to join in but also not interrupt, it’s funny how that works, you want to connect but there’s this weird barrier, like this invisible wall, but it’s not really a wall, it’s more like a curtain, a sheer one that can be pulled aside if you just reach out, but sometimes I’m too anxious to actually pull it aside, I mean what if they don’t want me there? I can't stand that thought, rejection is like a punch in the gut. But then I shake it off because life is too short for that kind of worrying. I mean what’s life without a little risk? Must be boring. I think about all the amazing things that could happen if you just throw caution to the wind, the spontaneity is like a drug to me. I remember last weekend, I just decided to go to that concert, the one I’d been hearing about everywhere but had no tickets for, I thought, “why not?” and just sold my way in, and it was wild, I met people, made friends, got lost in the music, sweat and laughter, it felt so alive. But there’s this other side of me, the neurotic side that worries like crazy afterwards, what if I catch something? What if the friends I made aren’t really friends? What if I never see them again? I don’t know how to silence that voice in my head, the one that whispers doubts and insecurities, it always seems to poke through the fun and the joy. But hey, maybe it’s just part of being human, right? To have that inner dialogue and to feel everything so deeply. I wish I could just bottle up the joy from those crazy nights and open it up whenever I’m feeling low, like a little happiness elixir. Oh, and I keep thinking about how I can get overwhelmed sometimes with all these emotions, like I really feel everything, which can be great but also exhausting. I’m the friend who will cry at a movie commercial, you know? But I love that about myself. I love how my heart is big and open. I want to know people and experience everything they have to offer. There’s this thrill in it, but there’s also vulnerability. The whole “be open, be real” thing is so lovely but scary at the same time. Like, what if no one gets it? What if I’m too much? Right now I’m thinking about how I need to clean my apartment—no, not just clean, I need to DEEP clean; the floor is sticky and there’s like a pile of clothes in the corner that I can’t even remember if I’ve worn in the past month. I need to fold them, but I can’t focus on that because there’s this buzzing excitement about the weekend, maybe a road trip, or a spontaneous trip to the beach! I need sun and sand and laughter—no planning, just jumping in the car and going wherever the road takes us. That sounds perfect right now. I want to feel the sea spray on my face and run around barefoot, collect seashells, and eat ice cream that melts too quickly in the heat. It’s funny how my mind wanders, like one minute I’m thinking about this coffee shop and the next I’m picturing a beach paradise. I crave that freedom of expression and adventure. I want to skydive, hike mountains, go salsa dancing! I want to be that person who is always up for an adventure but then the other side of me keeps saying, “What if you get hurt? What if you embarrass yourself?” but when I let that go, it’s exhilarating, and the world feels full of possibilities. I also think about my friends, how I love them like crazy, they’re my world, and we could just sit in silence and I’d be okay with that. But also, I want to connect deeper because sometimes I feel like I don’t really know them, like half of them are still strangers even though I share my life with them. I hope they know I’m always here for them, but I wish I could show them better how much they mean to me, like a hug or a surprise gift. I’m that friend who will just show up with snacks and stories because I can’t help it. I love being there for people. Life is just a swirl of joy and anxiety, laughter and worry, friends and self-doubt, but I wouldn't have it any other way. I just want to experience it all, the good and the bad, the highs and the lows, because that’s what makes it meaningful. And maybe, just maybe, I can find a way to balance it all without getting too tangled up in my head. That’s the goal, isn’t it? To dance through life, twirling between joy and fear, making memories with every step.

I’m sitting here trying to figure out what to type and my mind is racing like a thousand thoughts at once. I can’t help but think about my friends. They’re always a blast to be around. I love hanging out in groups. It’s so much fun to share stories and laugh together. Just last weekend we had this huge gathering at Alex’s place. Everyone brought something, a potluck but more like a feast. I made that classic pasta dish everyone loves, you know the one with the creamy sauce and broccoli. Everyone was digging in and I felt so happy just seeing people enjoying themselves. I can talk about anything with my friends. I thrive on those connections. I think it’s amazing how a good conversation can lighten the mood. Sometimes we talk about our courses and how they’re going but mostly it’s just nonsense, like the latest TV show or what happened last week at that ridiculous party we went to where someone tried to do karaoke and ended up forgetting the lyrics halfway through. It was hilarious. I can’t wait for the next time we can all get together. I keep my schedule pretty packed, I like being busy, keeps my energy up. But I really don’t feel overwhelmed by it at all. I can manage it like it’s no big deal. My planner is color-coded and organized. I like having it all mapped out. It gives me a sense of control or something. And it feels good to tick off tasks, like finishing assignments ahead of time. I really prefer structure, it helps me keep everything in check. I’ve heard some people talking about being spontaneous and how it’s freeing, but honestly, that just sounds exhausting to me. I like knowing what’s coming next. I can’t imagine being unprepared for a class or a meeting. I just can’t! My professors probably appreciate me because I’m always on time, always ready, always engaged. I take my studies seriously, and I like to help my classmates too, like forming study groups. It’s so rewarding to help someone understand a tough concept. I feel like I’m contributing to the community. And that gets me thinking about my future. I want a job where I can have that social interaction with people, and I’m aiming for something in project management – lots of teamwork and collaboration and organizing, just like I do now but on a bigger scale. The thought of working in an office environment with a team excites me. It’s thrilling to think about all the people I’d meet and the projects I could work on. I can visualize it. I could lead meetings, brainstorm ideas, motivate people. I’m always trying to lift others up, I can’t stand negativity. I try to keep everything light and positive. I notice when people are down or something is bothering them. I want to help. I’m not the kind of person to get involved in drama, I steer clear of it. I prefer to keep things harmonious and friendly. That goes for my family too. We’re tight-knit and I cherish those relationships. I usually organize family gatherings, game nights, or birthday parties. It’s all about bringing people together, making sure everyone feels included. Sometimes I think my family gets a bit annoyed because I’m always nudging them to try new things or go to new places, but I can’t help it! I want us to experience as much as we can. I know some of them like to stick to their routines, and I get that too. Sometimes I worry I might be too pushy, but I just want to have fun and make memories. I don’t like being stagnant, I want things to be lively and engaging. I can’t imagine living in a monotonous environment or doing the same thing day in and day out. I think I would go a bit stir crazy. I need that buzz. I’m not really into artsy stuff or exploring new ideas that I don’t understand. I like what I know and what works. Rules are comforting; they give structure to my life because without them, things could easily spiral. I could never be a risk-taker, that’s just not me. I like stability, it’s so much easier to navigate life when you can predict outcomes. I think I like routine partly because it allows me to always be in a position to help others, to provide support. I want to be the person everyone can rely on, that’s important to me. I hope I can keep this energy going while concentrating on my studies. Balancing everything is key, and I know I can do it. I really can.

I’m sitting here thinking about how loud it is outside, all these voices and cars rushing by like they’re in a race or something. It’s distracting but in a way it’s comforting too. I feel this urge to be part of that chaos but also this ping of irritation because can’t they just keep it down? I don’t get people sometimes. They talk to each other so easily, I wish I could turn that part of me up, you know like a volume knob, because sometimes I feel like I’m just sitting in the corner watching the show. Maybe they don’t care, maybe they’re just noise surrounded by more noise, but I like being in the thick of it. I thrive on the energy when it’s big and boisterous. I like to be the one contributing, but at the same time it’s exhausting. Why do I put so much pressure on myself to engage? Just people saying their stuff but like half of it is nonsense anyway. They keep talking about their weekend plans and I’m just nodding along thinking about how I have three deadlines coming up. How do people even find time to relax? I want to scream sometimes because I can’t keep up. And then there’s wanting to please everyone. Ugh, it’s like a seesaw that never balances out. I don’t want to be the bad guy but then I also don’t want to just go along with everyone else’s plans because honestly, it’s draining, I want to say no, I want to stand up and say, listen, I have my own stuff to deal with, but then I feel the weight of judgment, like what if they think I’m cold or distant? I can’t handle that. I know it shouldn’t matter but it does. So I pull myself into these awkward conversations and sometimes I can feel my heart racing just thinking about how I’ll navigate it. And there are days when I snap at people before even realizing it was harsh. Like, it’s not that I don’t care, but I just get overwhelmed. But then I bounce back so fast, the energy in the room lifts and I’m cracking jokes, because why not? I mean I’ve got to bring some laughter to this place, right? It feels good to make people laugh. I start thinking about my friends, the ones who get me and the ones that, man, they’re just too laid back for their own good. Why can’t they see the urgency? Life’s moving so fast! There are goals to crush, schedules to keep. I make lists all the time but I always miss something, forget a detail. I feel that frustration crawling in. I like to be organized but my mind races so much that it’s like I have a million tabs open and can’t find the right one. I wish I could just be a little more chill about it, you know? Enjoy the moment instead of racing ahead to the next thing. But then again, what would that even look like? I can’t even wrap my head around it, just seems like a big pile of uncertainty and I want to avoid that. I sometimes wish I could open up more, you know, like share my thoughts as they come without worrying about whether people will think I’m ridiculous. It’s hard to share an opinion because I don’t want to step on toes. Has anyone ever told you being honest can ruin friendships? I’ve seen it happen. You can’t just be alive and not care about the people around you too. I mean, I don’t need everyone to agree with me but at the same time I can’t stand the idea of making someone feel bad. But there’s that balance, they say honesty is important, but how much honesty is too much? I sometimes wonder what it’s like to be one of those people who just go with the flow, no plans, no need to control everything. It’s fascinating and terrifying. I like having a plan, I like having my ducks in a row even if they occasionally scatter all over the place. I keep telling myself I’ve got to be more open to experiences but it’s hard when anxiety kicks in and you’re stuck in your own head, processing it all, feeling like you’re trapped. I want to be a part of the big conversations, to break into the circles and share my ideas without hesitation, but hesitation is just there, lurking, waiting to strike when I least expect it. It’s like the ultimate contradiction. I want to engage but I also want to retreat. How do you balance that? Maybe I need a personal pep talk or something. I guess at the end of the day, all I want is to feel like I belong somewhere, surrounded by the noise, the laughter, and to not feel so restless.

I woke up this morning thinking about breakfast. I never really understood people who skip it. It’s like the best meal of the day, especially if you can cook it yourself. I could’ve made pancakes, but I just went for the usual eggs and toast. Simple but satisfying. I was having a great time with my little routine, flipping the eggs, toasting the bread, and then I thought about how I really want to learn how to make different kinds of omelets. There are so many! Spanish omelets, classic French, or even shakshuka, that dish looks amazing! I should look up some recipes later. Then I thought about how cooking is such a community thing too. So many stories and memories are tied to food. It connects people. Like my roommate, he’s from Italy, and he always tells stories about his grandma’s cooking. Maybe I should have him teach me some of those family recipes. I love that about college. Meeting new people from different backgrounds all around the world. It’s so exciting. The other day I had a conversation with a girl from Thailand. She was telling me about her favorite street foods. I didn’t even know what half of them were! It makes me want to travel. Like really travel, go places, experience the culture, see the sights, meet more people! I have a friend who just went to Japan and all the pictures he took made me so jealous, but in a good way. It pushed me to want to plan my own trip. Maybe next summer I can go backpacking through Europe or something. I’ve heard hostels can be a blast, meeting people from everywhere. Speaking of meeting people, I had a hilarious encounter in class the other day. I sat next to this guy who was super chatty, and we spent the entire lecture just making jokes about the professor’s tendency to go off on tangents. I was almost afraid we’d get kicked out! But it was so fun, just connecting and sharing a laugh. It’s moments like that that remind me why I love being around others. I can’t imagine being introverted, I don’t think I’d survive! I need that energy, that buzz that comes from being around others, even strangers. It fills me up like a phone charging. I also joined this club recently. It’s a student organization focused on sustainability. We’re doing some great projects, like campus clean-ups and awareness events. I love how everyone is so passionate about making a difference. It’s inspiring to see people who care. I think about that a lot – how small actions can lead to bigger changes. I want to help in any way I can. It’s amazing how people come together for a cause. We’re planning an event next month and I’m so excited to see it all come together. I can envision it already - people, laughter, maybe some music, learning about eco-friendly practices. I think I might try to get a band involved. Live music brings such a good vibe! Sometimes, I catch myself dreaming about what I’ll do after college. I want a job that lets me work with people, maybe in community development or something creative. I can’t just sit at a desk all day; that would drive me nuts. I love being organized and getting things done, but it has to be something where I can interact, brainstorm, and figure things out with a team. Collaboration is key, right? I think it leads to the best ideas. Then there are days when I just want to escape. Not in a bad way, but a day trip somewhere new sounds incredible. A little road trip, just me, music blasting, windows down, discovering hidden gems in the countryside or a coastal drive. I could drive for hours with the perfect playlist. I love a good mix of indie, pop, and a little classic rock thrown in. Maybe I’ll drive to the mountains and go hiking. I’m always up for an adventure, whether big or small. And I can’t help but wonder how I keep meeting so many fantastic people. It feels like the more open I am, the more opportunities I have to connect. Sometimes I just walk up to someone new and start talking. It’s a little nerve-wracking but also thrilling. It’s always interesting to hear different perspectives, to learn from one another. We’re all just trying to figure this life out, right? I’m just really grateful for this phase of my life, the people, the experiences, everything. I feel like I’m growing every day. I want to soak it all up, take in every moment, make amazing memories, and share those moments with others. I have this drive to explore, engage, and create. The world is so big and there’s so much to discover.

I’m sitting here thinking about how the world just feels overwhelming sometimes. It’s like a barrage of noise and chatter, and I’m not part of any of it. People around me talk about their weekend plans, and I fight the urge to just zone out. I don’t know, maybe it’s just me. I find comfort in silence but the silence makes it worse sometimes because then I start overthinking everything. Like that time in class when I couldn’t remember if I turned in an assignment and my stomach dropped and I spiraled into this endless loop of what-ifs. I hate being like this. I don’t want to feel anxious all the time. I want to be chill like my roommate who goes out and is just so effortlessly popular. I envy that. It’s silly but I do. I can barely muster up the energy to meet people, so I just don’t. I think about how I should get out more but then think about all the steps involved and it gets exhausting really quickly. Just the thought of small talk makes me want to curl up in bed. What do they even talk about? Movies? Parties? I wouldn’t know what to say. I haven’t seen anything new in ages. Netflix seems so daunting, like there’s this endless scroll and I can never make a choice. I think I should watch something but then I scroll for an hour and then just give up and start staring at my phone instead. Sometimes it feels like I’m missing out but then again, I also think what’s the point? I don’t want to force myself to go to some party to listen to music I don’t like and watch people who seem like they have it all together. I don’t even like crowds. Just the thought of being around so many people gives me a headache, and I always have to plan an escape route in case I need to leave. I feel my anxiety spike just thinking about it. I much prefer staying home with my weird habits, like obsessively checking my email or scrolling through social media, even if it makes me feel worse. There’s something about seeing everyone else living their lives while I’m here just… existing that hits differently. I get frustrated with myself. I don’t reach out to friends enough. I see them hanging out without me and I wonder if they even notice I’m gone. Do they think about me? Probably not. I know I wouldn’t, honestly, because when I’m with them I’m usually just trying to keep up. What can I even contribute? I feel like they all have so much going on and I’m just this black hole of negativity. No one wants to be around that. I often think about that time I tried to join a club and it crashed and burned. I showed up but couldn’t handle the group dynamic, the pressure to contribute ideas, to be witty and funny. I just nodded my head and felt like I was invisible the whole time. Then there are situations where I feel pushed into things, like group projects. It stresses me out. I hate group work. I can’t be responsible for anyone else’s deadlines or moods. I like things my way but people don’t always respect that. They think they know what’s best when they’ve barely even talked to me. I get frustrated. I shut down. It’s like they don’t even care about my input. Why can’t they just listen? It makes me want to snap but I don’t because I know that’s not how you make friends. Even if it feels good to vent about it. I’m not good at making friends. It’s too much effort, and I don’t have energy for it. I try to think of how to put myself out there but then I spiral down the rabbit hole of self-doubt. Maybe I should just focus on school, but even that gets to be too much sometimes. I feel like I’m under a microscope, and every exam feels like it could define my entire future. It’s exhausting. I stare at the notes but it feels like they’re written in some foreign language. And life feels like it just keeps asking for more from me. I just want a break. I crave structure but can’t stick to anything, which makes it all worse. I just have to get through today. One step at a time, but my mind races ahead. I should set up a schedule or something but the idea of planning feels like a burden, another thing on my list. I want to relax but my mind is a chaotic mess of thoughts. The weight of everything, the pressure, it’s suffocating. I think I should talk to someone about all this but then I think, what would I even say? I don’t want to burden anyone with my issues. They’re my issues. I can handle them. But can I? I have no idea.

I can’t help but wonder why I keep thinking about that weird interaction I had with that guy in class. He was so loud and overbearing, speaking over everyone, and I remember just wanting to shrink into my seat and disappear. People like him thrive in groups. I don’t understand how they do it. I dread having to talk in front of others. My palms sweat just thinking about it. Why does everything have to be so social? Sometimes I think I’d be better off in a library where it's quiet, but then I feel lonely, but then I’m also okay with being alone. It’s confusing. And then, when I finally get the courage to speak up, I worry about every little thing I said. Did I sound stupid? Did they care? Probably not. Everyone’s lost in their own world, aren’t they? It’s so chaotic out there. I should probably talk to someone about this. Maybe therapy? But that sounds so exhausting. Therapists listen, and I don’t really like talking about my feelings. I’d prefer to just nod and be supportive, but somehow people end up expecting more from me. My friends tell me I’m a good listener; it’s easier to listen than to share. They spill their thoughts like they’re pouring water from a jug, but I’m more like a sponge, absorbing the stress and chaos without letting any of it out. It gets heavy, though, and I wish I could just let it all go. But what if they don’t care? Oh God, do I even matter? I don’t want to be a burden—that’s the worst thing ever. I need to be agreeable, nice, and helpful but it feels like I’m losing myself in the process. Sometimes my mind jumps around too much. One minute I’m thinking about what I’m going to eat for dinner, and the next it’s about that weird dream I had last week where I was chased by a giant rubber duck. What does that even mean? Am I anxious about something? Probably. I can’t keep track of what I’m anxious about anyway. It’s like my brain is a room full of noisy people all shouting at once. No wonder I feel overwhelmed. I don’t know how to stay organized. I avoid making plans because I can’t handle the pressure of keeping them, but then I regret not doing things. The perfect balance of being socially cautious and curious is impossible for me. And I think about those moments when I do venture out, like that one time I went to a coffee shop with a couple of friends. It was so loud, and I couldn’t focus on anything. I just stared at my coffee as if it were the most profound thing in the world. Everyone else had conversations, and I felt like I was watching a movie. I laughed when they laughed, but inside I felt like an outsider. They’re so expressive, and I admire that. I want to be like them. It’d be nice to be free and charming, but instead, I feel like a wallflower. Do walls ever feel tired? Like, I wonder if they get exhausted from holding everything up. I don’t know where that thought came from. Maybe walls do. I’d like to sleep for a while. A long nap where I don’t have to think about anything or anyone. But then I worry about missing out or forgetting something important. My mind is a loop; it doesn’t stop. My grades are slipping, and I keep telling myself to focus, but my mind drifts to silly, mundane things like what’s the right way to fold laundry? Or does it even matter? I can’t even think beyond my comfort zone. Trying new things terrifies me, even the thought of it. Why would I want to step into discomfort when I can stay in my bubble? But it’s also isolating, isn’t it? I see everyone moving forward while I’m stuck where I am. I don’t want to be the only one left behind, but I can’t muster the energy to push myself out of my rut. It’s a cycle that’s hard to break. I see my friends making plans for the weekend, and I feel the pressure to join, but then I wonder if I’ll just ruin their fun by being so quiet and reserved. Sometimes I want to scream at the noise in my head. Why can’t I just relax? Why is it so hard to be happy? I care too much about what others think, and yet I feel like I’m invisible. It’s a strange contradiction. I just want someone to tell me it’s okay. But what if they don’t say that? What if I’m just stuck here, forever balancing on this tightrope of anxiety and quietness? I guess it’s just me and my endless thoughts, and sometimes that’s all I have to hold on to.

I should probably be studying right now but I really don’t feel like it. My brain is kind of all over the place. I think about how I need to get my act together but then I remember how overwhelming everything feels. Like, just sitting in my room staring at the books and notes makes me anxious. I wonder if everyone else feels like this or if it’s just me. Maybe I should talk to someone about it but then I worry they won’t understand or they’ll think I’m being dramatic. Everyone seems to have their lives figured out. Social media doesn’t help. I keep scrolling, everyone looks so happy and accomplished and I’m just here sitting in my pajamas, no makeup, hair a mess. I hope nobody sees me like this. Maybe I should take a shower or something. Do I even have time? Probably not. What if I just stay in this state forever? It’s like my brain is always buzzing with worry. Did I text back my friend? I think I did. Wait, what did I even say? I hope I didn’t sound weird. Sometimes I feel like I’m too nice to people, like I say yes too often. I don’t know how to say no, it makes me anxious just thinking about it. My roommate asked me to go out tonight, I said yes but I really want to stay in. I think about how everyone else is so outgoing and wants to go out and have fun and I’d rather just curl up with a book. Of course, I should be socializing, I know that. If I don’t, I’ll just be alone and then I get anxious about being alone. It’s a bad cycle. I can just see it in my head, the progression. Alone leads to overthinking. Overthinking leads to more anxiety. More anxiety makes me want to be alone more. I really need to break this cycle but it’s hard. I think back to that time I went to a party and it was so loud and I felt so out of place. I just stood by the snack table and pretended to be interested in the chips. I felt everyone’s eyes on me, judging me for not mingling. I should have talked to people, but what if they didn’t like me? What if I said something stupid? I remember my heart racing. I think I ended up leaving early and my friends were all like, “Where did you go?” and I felt guilty. Like I ruined their night. But the alternative was worse, feeling trapped and awkward. I do enjoy being with close friends, but going to big gatherings is exhausting. I wish I was different, more outgoing. But instead, I just keep quiet and smile a lot. People say I’m sweet, and I guess that’s a good thing but sometimes I wish I could be more assertive, stand up for myself, you know? I feel like I just go with the flow, let others lead and then I’m unhappy and don’t want to admit it, and I don’t want to be a burden. Burdening people is the worst feeling. I’d rather just take it all in and not let anyone know I’m struggling. Like, how many times can I vent to the same friend about my problems? They’re probably tired of it. I’m tired of it. I just want to feel normal. Normal…what even is that? I think it looks different for everyone. I guess I just want to not feel so much, but then again that sounds kind of scary too. If I didn’t feel things I wouldn’t know how to connect with people at all. I like genuinely caring about others, and I like to think that I’m good at it. I feel bad if someone’s upset and I want to help, even if I can’t find the right words. It’s so hard to balance caring for others while keeping my own worries in check. But I can’t help it. I just want people to be happy. I wonder if I’d be happier if I tried to be more open, but I don’t know. It’s hard to even think about changing. I like my little world, even if it’s a bit cramped and anxiety-ridden. I don’t know. Sometimes I think I’m too comfortable being uncomfortable. Isn’t that ironic? I get stuck in this rut where I want change but I’m terrified of it. How do I find the courage to face my fears? I want to be better but every little step feels like a mountain. There’s so much pressure to be this cool, fun-loving, carefree person when really it’s tough. It’s like everyone expects me to just get over it. But I can't just shake off these feelings. They stick. I wish I knew the magic formula for being okay. Instead, I’m just typing out this giant ball of confusion. Maybe I should just hit save and call it a day. The clock is ticking and I really need to make a decision about tonight. Do I go out and try to have fun? Or do I stay in and relax? Why can’t I just know what to do?

I just got out of that meeting and wow it was intense. I love being around people, the energy just fuels me, it’s like every word someone says lights a spark in my head. I can’t sit still, can’t stop my legs from bouncing. My mind races through all the things I want to say, all the points I want to make. I know I can contribute. Sometimes I feel like I'm the only one with all this enthusiasm; it’s contagious, or at least I hope it is. Everyone else seems so monotone and serious. I get it though, structure matters, deadlines—everyone’s got their role to play. But sometimes I just wish they’d loosen up a bit, let their true selves shine through. I mean, I like to believe we can work hard and still have fun. But maybe that’s just me wanting to stir things up a bit. I never back down from a challenge. Speaking of challenges, I got this big project due next week. I’ve already mapped everything out, timelines, milestones, every single detail in place, because that's how I operate—organized, deliberate. It feels great to tick things off a list, satisfaction in every checkmark. I set high standards for myself, I can't help it. I can’t just sit back and let things happen. If I want something, I go for it. Some folks think I’m a bit uptight because I plan everything. But I think a good plan keeps the chaos at bay. There’s enough chaos in this world without me adding to it. But then again, I wonder if I’m missing out on something, you know? Spontaneity. That’s the word, right? Like just going out with friends without planning every little detail. I mean, I do it sometimes, but it’s uncomfortable. The unknown gives me this gnawing feeling in my stomach. I prefer knowing what to expect. What if something goes wrong? What if I end up not liking it? That fear of the unknown is probably linked to my low agreeableness. I don’t mind being a little blunt, I guess you could say. I need to be honest. I respect honesty more than anything. Let’s cut through the fluff; no need to coddle feelings. Sometimes I think people take it too personally, but I’m just saying what needs to be said. Just thinking about the last party I went to. Yeah, the music was awful and the snacks were bland, but I turned that place upside down. I got people talking. I mean, I just can’t stand it when a room is filled with awkward silence. Is it really that hard to find common ground? Come on, people! I can bring the energy. I know some folks complain about the small talk, but I think it’s a gateway, a bridge to deeper conversations. You can’t just dive in, you gotta warm up first. I remember making a joke about the snack choices—it got a laugh, and then we were talking about favorite foods, and before you knew it, there were groups forming and laughter filling the room. I love that. But there was this one guy who kept trying to steer the conversation back to politics, as if we weren’t already drowning in that online. I just can’t do politics at parties. Seems pointless to me, it turns everyone into these rigid statues of opinion, and it’s just not fun. Let’s joke about life, let’s complain about work, let’s discuss something lighter. I can talk to anyone! I like talking! But when people decide to take themselves so seriously, it’s just exhausting. I mean I can do serious—when I need to—but in my downtime? Nah. And don’t even get me started on social media. That’s a whole different kind of exhausting. I’m there, but I don’t get sucked into the constant drama. I post my thing, share my events, mostly about achievements, that’s me being proud. I feel a bit like an outsider though. I don’t understand the appeal of sharing every little emotion. I’m not interested in the depths of feelings or whatever; I just care about the here and now. Get to the point already! It’s funny how life is all about connections but at the same time I thrive in my productivity bubble. But then there’s always something new I want to tackle. I think about taking up a new sport—yeah, why not? Something to keep my energy up there. I need to move, to stay active. If I'm cooped up too long, I just burst. I could do tennis or maybe try out for a team or something. I don’t know how that’s going to fit in my schedule but I know I’ll make it work somehow. That’s just my style. Structure but with a little room for spontaneity, right? There’s got to be a balance, I think. Just need to find it.

I don’t get why people love parties. It’s just loud noise and people pretending to enjoy each other’s company. I’d much rather sink into a book or explore some random corner of the internet where I can lose myself in articles about the most obscure topics. Like, I discovered that there are over 300,000 species of beetles, which is just weirdly fascinating. I mean, who cares about small talk when you can dive into entomology? But while everyone else is discussing their weekend plans, I’m sitting there thinking about how long it takes to make a proper terrarium or if I could learn to identify regional fungi by their spores. It’s comforting to think of all the things I can learn that most people don’t even think about. I suppose that makes me weird. Maybe it’s that I just don’t relate. Social anxiety seems like an exaggeration, but there's just no interest in playing those games. Well, maybe a little interest in people watching. Watching how they interact, what they wear, how they start conversations. People are curious creatures but so predictable. The same stories told over and over again in different variations. It’s almost like a script. I think about the idea of “being agreeable” and how exhausting that must be for some people. I can’t imagine trying to make everyone happy all the time. That’s a recipe for burnout. It’s just not me. I’d rather say what I think and let the chips fall where they may. Not that I’m confrontational or anything; it’s just easier to be honest, and if someone gets offended, well, that’s on them, right? I’m not trying to hurt feelings, but genuine conversations require honesty and a little messiness. I remember that time I expressed my opinion on the latest blockbuster and everyone started arguing. It was wild, like a scene from a bad movie. I just sat back and watched it unfold, thinking about how ridiculous it all was. I don’t care if I’m in the minority. I’d rather be the voice of dissent than nod along with the crowd. What’s the point of doing things perfectly? Conscientiousness seems so overrated when you think about it. All that planning and organizing, and for what? I like discovering things spontaneously instead of following a checklist. When everything feels so structured, it stifles the creativity that makes exploring life exciting. It’s like the world becomes this grid of obligations instead of a vast landscape of possibilities. I can’t remember the last time I actually wrote a list or stuck to one. My backpack is a collage of different papers, receipts, random thoughts, and a half-eaten granola bar. Who needs a planner when the chaos of spontaneity offers a kind of freedom that’s exhilarating? Sure, I might forget something important now and then, but that’s just part of the adventure of living. When was the last time I lost something and it turned out to be a disaster? Life is unpredictable, and I like it that way. Structure is boring. Openness is such an interesting trait. I find so much value in the new and unusual. I’ll try any food at least once. Why not? I once tried deep-fried crickets at some food festival. They tasted surprisingly good, like tiny crunchy chips. Everyone looked at me like I was crazy, like how could I eat bugs? But I just don’t see the point in adhering to rigid food norms when the world has so much to offer. What’s the difference if something is “weird”? Weird just means different, and different is fascinating. I’ve been thinking of taking a pottery class. I have no idea what I’m doing, but that’s part of the fun, right? I like the idea of getting my hands dirty and creating something tangible. The thought of forming clay into something completely unique just appeals to my sense of adventure. I don’t have to worry about following the rules when I’m crafting. I kind of drift off into thinking about travel too. The best places are the ones that aren’t on the map. I’d want to hitchhike across a country someday or take a train that goes nowhere in particular. Not the usual tourist spots. I want to meet locals and hear their stories, learn their languages even if I butcher the pronunciation. Imagine the connections I could make. There’s something about human interaction that intrigues me even if I don’t seek it out. I can be quite content in my own space, but there’s something exhilarating about passing conversations with strangers, learning about their lives for a brief moment. People are complex tapestries of experiences, thoughts, and emotions. I drift back to tonight, and I wonder if I should just stay in and order some weird food. A noodle place I found online looks interesting. They have some bizarre combinations, like chocolate ramen. Sounds gross but who knows? I might love it. I love that I can choose to indulge my whims. I want to explore everything without the weights of tradition or societal pressure. Life is too short to stick to the ordinary.

I’m sitting here thinking about how much I love being around people. Like, I thrive in crowds, the buzz of conversations, the laughter, the energy of it all. But then there’s this hint of anxiety lurking in the back of my mind. It’s weird because I can’t help but dive into social situations, but I always wonder if I’m saying the right things or if I’m too loud or too much. I guess that's the neuroticism shining through. I can meet a million people, and it feels exhilarating, but then at night, when I’m lying in bed, I replay every interaction like I’m critiquing a movie. Like, why did I say that one stupid thing? Or why didn’t I talk to that cute person over there? So many regrets over small moments. It’s exhausting. Speaking of exhausting, I went to a party last weekend. So many people! I was in my element, chatting with different groups. But then this one guy started talking about his investment strategy and I just didn’t care. I know that sounds harsh but like, my brain just couldn’t even focus. Too dry for me, and then I thought, do I sound like that when I get passionate about something? I hope I don’t because I want people to engage with me, but I also won’t hold back if I’m not interested. Low agreeableness makes me blunt sometimes. It’s a double-edged sword, honestly. I wish I could hold back my opinions a little more, but truth be told, it’s not in me. I don’t want to play nice if I can’t relate. But it’s kinda funny when I think about it, how people love the drama of disagreements, and I can stir the pot pretty well without even trying. Now I’m feeling fidgety thinking about my plans for later. I have a couple of people in mind to hang out with, but I’m already thinking about how to keep it lively. I hate dead air. If someone goes quiet, I feel like I’m holding my breath until someone says something and it’s like, how can you let it be awkward!? Just say something weird, funny, or even a random thought about pineapple on pizza! I love questioning everything. I’ll never understand why some people can sit still with silence. I think being open to new conversations is key, right? I mean, there’s a thrill in exploring new ideas or perspectives. I want to know what people think about weird topics, the whackiest conspiracy theories or their wildest dreams. That’s what fills me up, not mundane small talk about the weather or whatever. But then again, it’s no wonder people are sometimes uneasy to engage. I have this tendency to throw myself into new things, but I don’t follow through. I signed up for a pottery class last month after I saw this amazing Instagram feed of a local artist and now I just don’t go. Maybe that's my low conscientiousness kicking in. I get all excited and then fizzle out once the reality hits. Pottery sounds fun… but when I think about the mess and the commitment, I cringe a bit. So many half-hearted attempts. Minimum effort for maximum exploration, that’s my vibe. Why do the boring things when I can jump from spontaneous dinner plans to late-night jam sessions or karaoke? I love making memories even if they’re not perfect. Sometimes I worry I’m too much for people. I mean, I can dominate conversations if I’m not careful. I don’t mean to, but I want to share all these cool experiences and ideas that I have bouncing around in my head. I think everyone has something interesting to say; I just have this urgency to hear it all. I get excited when someone finally opens up about their passions, and then I’m off to the races, asking a hundred questions and losing track of time. What if they don’t want to share? Is that me projecting my need to be heard? Maybe. But I also love it when others get animated. It's a dance, I guess, this back and forth of ideas. Ugh, I’m rambling, aren’t I? I’m supposed to be focusing on one thing but my brain just won’t slow down. I want to dive into all these feelings and thoughts, unravel them and see how they fit together. Maybe I’ll think about heading to the café down the street after this. There’s always someone interesting there. Or maybe not. It could just be my mind getting carried away again. I like to imagine the world as this vast ocean of experiences, and I'm just a surfer riding every wave, even the choppy ones. It’s exhilarating but sometimes scary too, because what if I wipe out, what if I find myself alone in a room when all I want to be is surrounded? I suppose that’s just part of the thrill of being me, huh?

I woke up today thinking about all the things I need to do—classes, of course, I really should be studying for that exam next week but my mind drifts. I find it easier to connect with people than to settle down into a textbook. My roommate is so quiet, how does she do that? I feel like I need to talk to someone about everything. I texted a friend, we’re supposed to meet up later, we’ll probably go to that new café. I can’t wait to try their pastries, I heard they have this amazing mocha too. I can already picture the place, with all those cozy seats and bright art on the walls. It’s so vibrant. I love vibrant places. They energize me. I wonder if everyone feels this way about bright colors. We talk about everything, my friends, relationships, dreams. I feel super open to trying new things but then I worry am I oversharing? Should I hold back? I don’t want to scare anyone off with too much excitement. Being so open sometimes feels like a double-edged sword. Like, I don’t want to be that person at the party who overwhelms everyone with too much enthusiasm. But I can’t help it. There’s so much to share! I want to share. I mean, who wouldn’t love to hear my stories about traveling last summer? That spontaneous trip to Spain was incredible. I met a couple of backpackers at the hostel, we spent the whole week exploring. It was exhilarating! Just being immersed in another culture, trying all the food, speaking broken Spanish—so much energy. I wonder where they are now? Social media keeps us connected but sometimes it feels fake. Are we really friends just because we follow each other? I worry if I’m keeping up with everyone, am I doing enough? Sometimes I feel like I should be doing more. Volunteering, helping out, learning new skills but then I also feel overwhelmed. I signed up for this study group but kept wondering if I can handle it. I mean, I really wanted to join and meet everyone. I thrive on group dynamics but it can get messy. A part of me feels all this excitement bubbling just under the surface; I can’t wait to get out, socialize. But my neurotic side kicks in, like, what if I say the wrong thing? What if I blink out mid-sentence and everyone stares? It’s so irrational, but I’ve daydreamed about it happening. It keeps me up sometimes. I’ll be lying in bed thinking about how I could have handled a conversation differently. Oh, and the weather—doesn’t it impact everyone’s mood? Some people are affected by rain; I just want to feel the sun on my face, it makes me feel alive. Warmth is everything. But when it rains, I end up wanting to stay in, binge-watch shows, while my mind is racing about all the things I could be doing instead. Maybe I should have a cozy rainy day routine? Tea, a blanket, a good book. I’m always curious about new flavors of tea, too. I could spend hours at a tea shop just smelling them, the different aromas. Do shops sell happiness in tea bags? I think so. I remember the first time I tried chai it was like a flavor explosion in my mouth. I can still taste it. I can’t help but think about how flavors bring people together. Sharing meals. It’s like bonding over experiences. But I need to be careful, my anxiety pulls me back. Avoiding social scenarios can feel safer; I’ve backed out of parties before, worn myself out thinking about how I should go, or what if I run into that one person I don’t want to see? Ah, the mundane things that get twisted in my head. I feel like I’m fighting a battle against my own thoughts sometimes, like I’m my own worst enemy. People say to express how you feel; it’s cathartic but opening up is risky, even when I know the outcome will likely be positive. I want to be genuine, but I’m a little bit of a people-pleaser. I can’t lie though, I thrive when I’m surrounded by friends, it’s just that the lead-up can be like climbing a mountain. I keep reminding myself to breathe, to slow down, to live in the moment. Being conscientious helps me focus but I still find myself going down rabbit holes of worry. I think about how I can fit everything in, how to balance social life with schoolwork. It’s such a dance. Do I say yes? Do I prioritize? Am I being too much? I hope they see me as someone supportive.

I’m sitting here thinking about how weird life is. Like, really weird. I keep looking at my phone and thinking about how I scroll through social media and see all these happy people, perfect smiles and perfect lives, and I’m just here, trying to figure out what to do with my day. Why do I even bother scrolling? I know it’s just a highlight reel. It feels exhausting. The anxiety creeps in. What if I can’t measure up? What if I’m stuck in my own little bubble forever? I really don’t want to be at this weird family gathering next week. Small talk is my worst enemy. People always ask me questions about my future and I just freeze. I hate being put on the spot. It feels like my brain shorts out and all I can do is nod and smile. Inside, I’m screaming. Why don't they understand that I don't know what’s next? My friends all seem to have it figured out. I feel like I’m drifting. I want to be supportive of them, I do, but then it feels unfair. And I can’t help but compare myself. Ugh. I worry that I’m not doing enough. Maybe it’s just my neurotic side talking. I sometimes wish I was more carefree, just let things unfold without so much anxiety. But then, does that mean I’d lose my kindness? I mean, I’m nice! People say I’m nice. I think I’m nice but then I question it when I feel grumpy. I get these spirals where I just can’t shake off a bad thought and it ruins my whole day. I’m like, seriously, just let it go, it doesn’t matter, but that voice is louder. It’s like this annoying friend that keeps bringing up old stories that I want to forget. Why can't I just be more disciplined with my thoughts? Maybe I should make a list or something but that seems way too organized for my brain. I also wonder if everyone feels this way. Like, do my friends sit around worrying if they’re enough? I see them laughing, but are they laughing on the outside and cringing on the inside? I wish we could just sit in a room and talk about all the stuff we’re stressed about without judgment. Sometimes I think about reaching out but then I talk myself out of it because what if they’re busy? What if they don’t want to hear it? I don’t want to burden anyone. That’s the last thing I want to do. But it’s all a cycle, isn’t it? I want to connect and I want to be there for others but I also don’t want to be a bother. Maybe I’m just too agreeable. People say that’s a good thing, but I wonder if it’s just me being a pushover. Does it mean I don’t have boundaries? Boundaries feel scary. I don’t want to disappoint anyone. Do they even really want to hang out with me? I think about the last time I went out with friends. It was fun but I remember feeling awkward, like I was trying too hard to fit in. I often feel like I’m pretending to be more social than I actually am. I like being at home, being in my own space. It’s comforting. But then boredom creeps in, and I start to feel that gnawing need to do something, anything. I could organize my bookshelf—wait, no, that sounds like too much effort. I never feel like doing actual things. I guess it’s easier to just stay in my head. I realized I might have issues with commitment too. Not just with relationships, but like, with plans. I’ll agree to things but when the day comes, I just want to bail. It feels like a weight. It’s all because of the anxiety of being outside. It’s funny because I enjoy the events when I’m there, but the build-up gives me stomach knots. I don’t get how people can just go out and not feel this way. It’s the social pressure I think. There are all these unspoken rules I just can’t seem to grasp. I’ve thought about therapy but what if it doesn’t help? What if it makes things worse? Or what if I end up pouring my heart out and it’s just another thing on the list of self-improvement projects I can’t keep up with? But I want to connect with people. I want to be more open, but I feel so guarded. Like everything’s too personal. I don’t want to delve into things that make me feel vulnerable. What if they see too much? What if I end up crying? That’s not cute. I just wish all this would sort itself out. I want to be okay, be happy. Why can’t it be simple? I just want to breathe without feeling like I’m drowning in my own thoughts. That’s all I really want.

I love being around people, it’s like a battery charger for me, you know? I can't stand being cooped up in my room for too long without some interaction. Today was great, I had coffee with a bunch of friends, we were all just laughing and sharing stories, you know, the kind of stuff that makes the world feel lighter. I ordered a caramel macchiato, extra caramel because why not? Life is sweet and I want to enjoy all the sweetness. I feel like the more I share stories, the more connections I build, and isn’t that what life is about? Relationships, friendships... they’re so essential. Sometimes I think about how many people I have in my life who genuinely care about me. It’s a lot, and it makes me feel warm inside. It’s hard to be sad when there are so many people that lift your spirits, right? I mean, sadness serves a purpose but I’m just not wired for it, I guess. I’d rather be bright, bubbly, and pouring energy into everyone around me. But then, like, I also have this ongoing to-do list that never seems to shrink. School assignments, clubs, volunteering, I love being busy. Sometimes I get a little overwhelmed with juggling everything, but I thrive on it, the organization of it all feels good. I even color-code my planner, which sounds so nerdy but hey, it works for me. It’s like a little masterpiece every time I fill it out. I actually love ticking things off. It gives a sense of accomplishment, doesn’t it? I couldn’t imagine being the kind of person who just floats through life without a plan. Like what would even be the point? I get the spontaneity has its charms, but I think that kind of chaos would drive me nuts. I like structure. It’s comforting. Oh, and then there’s the group projects. I mean, I usually take the lead, it just feels like a natural role for me to step into. I can’t help it. I want to make sure everyone is included and that we all have a say. But also, I can’t avoid feeling that pressure of making everything perfect. I want high grades, I really do, so I pull the team together to make sure we’re on track. I don’t want to let anyone down, especially when they’re counting on me. So, we brainstorm and bounce ideas off each other, and it feels good to contribute. Sometimes we mix it up a bit, and I love brainstorming. I think it’s fascinating how everyone has their own perspective and you can build something even better together. But it’s also funny how different people approach assignments. Some are just really chill about deadlines while I’m over here checking my planner like a hawk, reminding everyone of due dates. I know I can sound a bit uptight, but I mean, it’s just practical, right? And when someone asks me to be part of something, I usually can’t say no. It’s hard for me to turn down an offer. I want to be there, to help, to be part of something bigger. It makes me happy to put a smile on someone’s face. I sometimes wonder if I overcommit myself, though. Like can you have too many friends? I love meeting new people, sharing meals, hanging out. I just can’t get enough of it. I really do enjoy people’s company. It’s fascinating to learn about their lives, what they’re passionate about, their aspirations. Everyone has their own story, their own dreams, and I get to be part of that even if it’s in small ways. I recently made a new friend and we clicked right away. We both love board games, so we’ve been organizing game nights, and it’s just a blast. Who knew a simple game of Scrabble could lead to hours of laughter and bonding? But then, there are times when I crave my own space too, just a moment to recharge. It doesn’t happen often but I like to curl up with a book or binge-watch a new series when I need to step back. It takes a lot of energy to be “on” all the time, you know? But I’m always ready to dive back into the fray. The social life, the engagements, the laughter…it’s all so worth it to me. I wouldn’t trade it for anything. I think I’m just wired this way, this intrinsic motivation to connect and cheer people on. I love cheering my friends on, celebrating their victories, and holding them up when they’ve had a rough day. There’s a deep satisfaction in being a source of positivity and support, and that’s the best part of my day, every single day.

I wonder if I should really be doing this but here I am typing away and it feels a bit strange because I’m usually not one to just spill out thoughts like this. I should probably be studying for that biology exam. Cells and mitosis and all that—it’s interesting but stressful. I keep thinking about how I need to get an A. What if I don’t? It’s a weird feeling knowing I care so much about grades and at the same time I want to explore other things, like philosophy or art or something completely unrelated. I love learning about new ideas, even if I can get anxious about how to apply them. Maybe I should just take a deep breath, forget about the grades for a second and center myself.
