Right, breathe in, breathe out. The stress lingers anyway. I’m sitting in this café, it’s kind of cozy but the noise is overwhelming. Too many voices buzzing around. It’s funny how I can enjoy being here yet feel so out of place. I see people laughing and chatting at the table next to me and it’s almost comforting but also makes me feel a bit sad. I wonder if they notice me sitting here alone. Do they think I’m weird? I hope not. I want to connect with people but talking is hard sometimes. Sometimes I overthink it and just end up nodding along or smiling when it’s my turn to say something. I can imagine what I’d say, like a million times in my head before the moment passes. I want to be friendly and open but I get in my own way. What if I just walked up to them and said hi? That thought scares me. I could come off awkward and that’s just the worst. I have moments where I do manage to talk to people but they are rare. I wish I could be more spontaneous. I’m not really that kind of person. I like planning things out. I feel more secure that way. Yet I crave adventure and new experiences all the time. Maybe I just need to push myself a little harder. The idea of going skydiving makes me anxious but also excited, so that’s something, right? I mean, what would it feel like to just let go and do something terrifying? I’ve always been kind of a rule-follower, though. It's safer that way. I think about how much I enjoy my classes. The lectures, when I actually attend them, are fascinating. There’s something about the way ideas connect that excites me. Learning is so enriching, and I can’t imagine a life without it. I wonder if I’ll ever find a career that combines my love for knowledge and my desire to help people. I’d probably thrive in a job that involves a lot of collaboration. I love supporting others. It feels good to be dependable. Sometimes I’ll help other students study, and while the anxiety I feel about my own grades creeps up, watching them understand something and seeing their faces light up when it clicks—that’s worth it. That's what I live for. But then I get lost in those moments too, deep down thinking, “Why can’t that be me?” I think it’s the neuroticism talking, you know. Feeling like an imposter sometimes. I read somewhere that a lot of high achievers feel like frauds. Maybe I’m just overthinking again. I’ve spent way too much time in my head. I wish I could turn that voice off sometimes, the one that tells me I’m not doing enough or that I should be worried when I’m not. It’s exhausting. I could list out a hundred things that worry me, from finances to friendships to my future and the state of the world. I mean, climate change is a real kicker. I feel like I can’t keep up, not with everything going on. I want to make a difference and yet here I am, just one person. I want to travel. There’s so much to see out there. I’ve seen beautiful places in photos, but I want to experience them. I want to meet people from different cultures, hear their stories, understand their lives. I think that would make me a better person, make me more open-minded. But again, the anxiety! What if I get lost? Or what if I can’t communicate properly? Languages are so interesting but also so daunting. I wish I could pick up a new language overnight. That’d be cool. My phone is buzzing; I should check who it is. Oh, it’s a friend asking if I want to join a study group. Maybe… do I want to? I should probably say yes, but I really just want to curl up in my blanket and read. Why is it so hard to balance the need for socialization and the safety of solitude? Back to the study group—there’s a tug of war inside my head. I don’t want to miss out, but I also don’t want to feel overwhelmed. It's like I'm torn between my desires—wanting to explore new things, but holding back due to my own restrictions. I guess it’s alright to feel this way. I should remember it’s okay not to be perfect, just to be me.

I can't believe I forgot to buy coffee today. What a mistake. I need that jolt in the morning. I wonder if I have any left at home. Probably not, I drink it all the time. That's what happens when you live on your own. Just me and my chaotic thoughts. But it’s nice, too, having that freedom. I can throw parties whenever I want, but then it’s a mess. I should probably clean up, but cleaning is just so boring and tedious. Why do I have to do chores anyway? Life shouldn’t be about scrubbing floors and dusting shelves. I’d rather be out meeting people, feeling that buzz of energy around me. I thrive on that. Even when I’m anxious. It’s like a high. Oh, speaking of, that party last week was insane. I talked to so many people, but then I remember this one guy—I think his name was Mark?—he was talking about his cat for what felt like an hour. I don't know, I mean, I was polite, but like, I have my limits. Cats are cool and all, but let’s get back to something more interesting, dude. Then again maybe I was just being rude? Maybe I should have listened more. But whatever, he’s probably off telling someone how I zoned out while he was sharing cat stories, haha. It’s funny how people can be so self-centered without realizing it. I love that about socializing, the stories people tell when you least expect them, but there’s always that one person who just goes on and on. Sometimes I wonder if I do that too—if I’m one of those annoying people. Probably! I’ve definitely been caught in that cycle of talking too much, especially when I get nervous. It’s like the more anxious I am, the more I ramble. Wait, do I even care if I annoy people? I mean, I like to think I’m a fun person to hang out with. No one says no to going out with me, right? Right? But then again, I don’t care much about what others think—just not my thing. I’ll say what I want, and if you don’t like it, that’s your problem. I can handle the fallout. I’ll just switch to a different group or bar or whatever. There’s so much to do around here! There’s that new club opening up next week, I’m excited to go check it out! Always something going on. So many faces, so many stories. I can lose myself in that. But then there are days when I feel this heavy weight of anxiety creeping in, like, what if I’m not good enough? Just sitting in my room by myself and spiraling into weird thoughts. I’ll think about that job I applied for—did I even come across as competent? I keep replaying the interview in my mind, picking at every little detail. Did I smile enough? Did I ask the right questions? Ugh! I hate this anxiety that comes with the territory. But on the upside, I get to channel that energy into creativity. Like, I just start writing or drawing, and it helps. It’s freeing. I can be whoever I want through my art and express all these wild thoughts. I need to embrace that. I just wish I could find a way to make a living off it. Probably not going to happen, but hey, I won’t stop trying. Gotta keep those options open, right? The idea of an ordinary desk job makes me shudder. I would be so miserable! Sitting there staring at spreadsheets, dying a little inside. I could never do it. I want to create, travel, explore. I want to meet new people—each day feels like an adventure waiting to happen. But then I think about money. Money stresses me out. I should probably start budgeting but who really sticks to that? I just swipe my card and hope it’ll all sort itself out. Live for today! But also, future me needs to eat, too. It’s so confusing. I keep saying I’ll stop going out so much, but how do you say no when there’s a chance to meet new people? New experiences? I’m always down for that! I crave it like it’s air. It’s like that constant balance of wanting to socialize but also feeling drained afterward. The highs and lows. It’s exhausting really. But thrilling! Riding that rollercoaster of emotions in a way is part of being alive. I can feel it in my bones, this thrill for the unknown! Maybe I’ll just wake up tomorrow and decide to hit the road. Just pack a bag and see where I end up. That sounds perfect right now. Maybe I could find a group of fellow wanderers, or maybe I’d find a quirky café to sit in and people-watch. God, I love people-watching—so much better than watching TV. All these lives unfolding, stories I may never know. There’s magic in that, isn’t there?

I love being around people; there’s just something about the energy that flows when you’re in a room full of chatter and laughter. I can talk to anyone about anything. Just this morning, I ran into my neighbor while getting coffee, and we started talking about the weather, then transitioned into our favorite brunch spots. I really think that’s what life is about—connecting. I love planning things too, like group dinners or movie nights. If I’m not organizing, I feel a bit lost. Like I need a checklist for everything. It gives me this sense of accomplishment when I can tick something off. Oh, and the satisfaction of a well-planned event, seeing everyone having a good time, it’s the best feeling. Sometimes I wonder how people can be so negative when there's so much fun to be had. I mean, sure, life has its ups and downs, but focusing on the positive just makes everything better. I’m the friend who’s always uplifting. If someone’s down, I just can't stand it. I have to encourage them, tell them they can do it, because they really can. It’s like that saying, “your vibe attracts your tribe.” I like to think I attract an awesome tribe. My friends always tell me that I bring out the best in them, and that makes me so happy. They’re good people. It’s a great circle of support. My family is similar. We’re all really close. Sundays are for family dinners—everyone brings something. My mom makes her famous lasagna, and I always bring dessert because I have a knack for baking. Chocolate chip cookies are a staple; no one can resist them. I love being the one to create that warm environment, where everyone is gathered around the table sharing stories, laughing away. And my dad always has the craziest jokes. I can’t help but roll my eyes sometimes, but I love that he tries. It’s endearing. I’m definitely a planner; spontaneity is a bit outside my comfort zone. I admire people who can just go with the flow, but that’s not really me. My friends always joke about me being the ‘mom’ of our group. I remember last summer, I planned a camping trip. It was a hit! I love making lists and researching the best campsites, figuring out what snacks to pack, and scheduling breaks for hikes. I can’t help it; it feels good to have everything figured out. And it keeps the stress levels low because no one is scrambling at the last minute. Parties, though—those are my jam. I thrive in social settings. I can mingle for hours, and I always have a new story to share. It’s surprising what people will open up about when you just ask one simple question. I’ve learned that everyone has something interesting to say if you give them the chance. I remember this one time at a friend’s birthday party, I ended up talking to a complete stranger about their travels in Europe. They were so animated, it was contagious! That’s what I mean about connection; it really charges me up. I probably talk too much sometimes, but I just get so excited to hear what others have to say. I can be a bit structured, though. I have a routine, and it works for me. I wake up early, hit the gym, shower, and grab breakfast while I plan my day. I guess that’s part of being conscientious. I like organization. Randomness stresses me out a bit. I’m not sure I’d handle a surprise party well if it were thrown for me. But knowing someone put thought into it would make it nice. Still, I think I’d rather be the one planning it. I’d definitely have the best playlist! Sometimes I think about trying to be more spontaneous, but then I remember how much I enjoy having everything mapped out. It’s not that I’m closed off to new experiences; I just feel better when I know what’s coming next. Stability is comforting. Take classes for example; I like to stick with subjects I know I’ll excel in, even if everyone else is taking risks with their majors. I have my path, and I’m sticking to it. I admire boldness, but it’s not really in my nature to leap without looking. Social responsibility matters to me too; I often participate in community service. It feels good giving back. I joined a local charity last year, and we’ve organized several events. It’s rewarding to see everyone come together for a good cause. It gives purpose to the social gatherings. I thrive on those vibes—people wanting to make a difference together. Life is about connection, and I think every little bit helps make the world a better place, even if it’s just one event at a time. Bringing people together is what I do best, and I wouldn’t change it for anything.

I’m sitting here, staring at this blank page like it’s going to magically fill itself up with brilliant thoughts but nothing is happening. My mind is racing though, oh it’s racing so fast but it feels like it’s stuck in first gear, like just a sputtering engine that won’t really roar to life. I think about all the times I've avoided social situations. The way I flinch when someone suggests going out, the anxious tingle of dread creeping through me. I’d rather be home, reading or watching a documentary or just making sense of these random thoughts. Can I make sense of thoughts if they’re racing? I want to explore everything, books, ideas, concepts, but the outside world feels so overwhelming sometimes, the people and the noise and the expectations, especially when they expect me to act a certain way or engage in small talk. Small talk is the worst. I keep imagining the conversations where I miss social cues. What if I say something totally off? What if they think I’m rude? Then I spiral, imagining their judgments, and I get hot, embarrassed, wishing I could just disappear into myself. I prefer deep conversations but they’re so rare. Why can’t every conversation be deep? I mean, how can we go through life without knowing about each other’s fears, dreams, philosophies? I hunger for substance but here I am, stuck wondering what to say about the weather. Even now, I’m wondering about my writing. Is it good enough? Probably not. I should probably read more about writing techniques, but I know that’ll just be another thing piled on my incessant ‘to-do’ list. Sometimes I wonder if I’m too conscientious, like I can’t even relax because I’m always thinking about the next thing I should be doing. It’s exhausting, I can’t just chill out. How do people do that? I admire them. Maybe I could try to be more laid back, but then I think, what if something important slips through the cracks? The balance is tricky. I can’t let my mind wander into the pit of procrastination and distraction. So I keep organizing my notes, my thoughts, planning for the future when I’m not even sure how the next moment will unfold. I also love learning new things. I crave knowledge. I’ll get excited about a random topic, like the history of the Byzantine Empire, and dive in, reading everything I can find until I feel like I’m drowning in information. But then I can’t decide what to focus on because there’s too much out there, and my openness pulls me in all these different directions. Should I research psychology, philosophy, or maybe even a bit of quantum physics? I love the feeling of intellectual stimulation but it’s like juggling too many balls at once, and I’m scared I’m going to drop one. Then there’s the neurotic side of me. My brain just spins with all those ‘what ifs.’ It’s like having a constant soundtrack of anxiety playing on repeat. What if I don’t do well in my classes? What if I don’t find a job I enjoy? What if I end up alone forever? Those questions invade my mind like uninvited guests. My brain feels like it’s a crowded room sometimes, just too much noise and too little air. And I’m still trying to be conscientious, staying on top of deadlines, keeping everything neat and organized, but that anxiety just twists in my stomach. I think about how I navigate friendships. I’m not naturally agreeable, so I don’t always know how to handle it. I sometimes come off as blunt or harsh without realizing it, and I think, what’s wrong with me? I just want to be honest, but honesty can hurt, and I don’t want to hurt anyone. It’s a strange dichotomy, trying to be true to myself while also wanting to be a good friend. I have my small circle, and I love them, but it’s hard to connect deeply sometimes when I’m so wrapped up in my own thoughts and worries. But I do think I appreciate authenticity more than anything. I’d rather have one real friend than a group of acquaintances who don’t know me at all. I want to peel back the layers and find out who people really are, what they truly think. I want them to know me too, but then I get scared because what if they don’t like what they find? I get pulled in so many directions, so many conflicting feelings. What if I’m too much for them? What if my thoughts are too heavy? And here I am, just letting my stream of consciousness flow, hoping something coherent comes out. Maybe this is just another way to make sense of my tangled head. I guess we’ll see.

I sit here staring at the screen, I have to write this thing and my mind is all jumbled, I wonder if anyone else feels this way, why can’t I just focus, I can’t help but think about the noise outside, people laughing, that’s so annoying, why can’t they be quiet, what’s so funny anyway, it’s probably stupid, I shouldn’t judge, but I can’t help it. I like being alone, it’s comfortable, it’s predictable, I know what to expect, like my routine is so important, if I stray from it I get anxious, I hate that feeling, I need to stick to my habits, wake up, go to class, study, eat, sleep, very boring but safe. What was I saying? Oh right, the noise, it’s distracting and I can’t concentrate, I’d rather be reading some boring textbook than deal with fun and socializing, it just feels unnecessary, I mean why bother, I can’t remember the last time I felt like I wanted to join in, always feels forced, everyone is so friendly, too friendly sometimes, it feels fake. I’m not a people person, that’s for sure. I guess that makes me odd, maybe people think I’m rude or standoffish, I just don’t feel like chatting about the weather or what we did last weekend like it even matters, and there’s so much pressure to respond in the right way, ugh. I just want to get things done, be efficient, but everyone else seems to have a good time, I feel out of sync. I’m not sure if I’m missing out, I probably am, but I don’t want to join in the chaos. The more I look at social media the more I realize how exhausting it all seems, everyone posting perfect lives, but I can’t tell if I envy them or feel relieved I’m not part of that frenzy, it’s confusing. Do people really care about all that stuff, it seems so shallow, but they must because look at the likes, oh the pressure of the likes, it’s like a whole other job. Why can’t we just focus on what’s necessary? Like actual work, like studying, passing classes, not scrolling. That’s why I get so anxious about everything, I need to do well, I can’t help but overthink tests, papers, am I studying enough, did I cover everything, it feels like I’m constantly racing against time, I guess it’s how I’m wired. Maybe I should join a study group, but that idea makes me cringe, too many people, talking too much, what if they ask me questions, I can’t handle being put on the spot like that, I just want to focus and finish my assignments, alone. It’s actually more productive, less distraction, I wish more people understood that, it’s like they think everyone should be outgoing, but not everyone is, why can’t we just respect that? I don’t like small talk anyway, it feels pointless, just say what needs to be said and move on, like I’m in a meeting, I can handle that, get to the point, I like to know there’s a purpose. I bet my friends think I’m a bore, I can deal with that, I’d rather be boring and content than frantic and pressured to entertain. I have my own hobbies, I like to play games by myself, I can get lost in a world without people, where I can control everything, it’s nice not to deal with emotions that aren’t mine, I wonder if that’s a problem. Socializing leads to misunderstandings, people get offended too easily, I don’t have time for that drama. Maybe I should just embrace my solitude, who needs friends anyway if they make life more complicated? I find comfort in the familiar, it calms me when the world feels chaotic. It’s funny how people talk about life being an adventure, I think it’s exhausting, who has the energy for that, I don’t want surprises, I just want to know what’s going to happen next. The same old routine keeps me grounded. I can read the same book for the tenth time and feel like it’s still new because I know the ending. I wish I could shake off this feeling of tension, but it’s always there, lurking. I wish I could just be more carefree and relax but it’s hard, I’m too wired to think about all the possibilities and what could go wrong, that’s what keeps me up at night, way too much thinking. I don’t even get why people find so much meaning in social gathering; it’s like they don’t see the mess in it, the uncertainty, I want to be straightforward and know what’s happening, but then there’s this whole world outside just doing its thing and I can’t keep up with it.

I can’t believe how quickly time flies, I mean one moment I’m sitting in class, and the next I’m out with friends and this whole whirlwind of experiences just keep happening. I thrive off that energy like a sponge, soaking it all up. I can’t focus in class sometimes though, too many distractions. I’ll be thinking about lunch before I even realize I’m supposed to be taking notes. What’s for lunch anyway? I hope it’s pizza, or maybe sushi, I could go for sushi, although I’m not super picky when it comes to food; I just love trying new things, and that means I often find myself in these random hole-in-the-wall places that are gems. I can’t keep track of what’s going on in class, but who cares when there’s a whole world to explore right outside the door? Yesterday, I ended up at this quirky little café with the best pastries, and I just chatted with the barista about how they make everything from scratch, the vibe was just so chill, I love places like that. People watching is one of my favorite hobbies; I don't even need to talk to anyone sometimes, just observe and soak in the interactions and the way people express themselves. It's fascinating how easily people fall into routine, like everyone goes to the same places, does the same things, while I’m trying to break free from that mold, I want to find the hidden spots, the places with character, and gather stories along the way. My friends sometimes roll their eyes at my spontaneity, but I can't help it, I just want to experience everything, even if it’s a little chaotic. I remember this one time we took an impromptu road trip, just me and two friends blasting music and laughing all the way until we ended up in this tiny town that none of us had ever heard of. We found a festival happening, and it was just the best. I mean, who knew those little towns had such vibrant lives? It’s like each place has its own heartbeat, and I want to chase them all. Being on the go is just in my blood; I can’t stand being cooped up. I have this intense need for stimulation, always looking for the next adventure. Sometimes I feel like I can’t sit still—I’m like a kid who just chugged a soda and is bouncing off the walls. The classroom feels like a cage sometimes, it’s like I’m missing the party outside when I can’t just run free. But then I’ll make plans to meet up with friends after class, and the thrill kicks back in. It’s funny how I can be super social and still feel this itch to break away from the crowd at times; it’s like there’s never enough time to do everything I want, but the constant buzz of life keeps me going. I really don’t mind disagreements, they can lead to interesting discussions. I’ve noticed that sometimes my bluntness can rub people the wrong way, like, I really don’t mean to be harsh, but I just put everything out there, raw and unfiltered, and I don’t have the patience for small talk. It’s exhausting, but I love when conversations get real. I’d rather have one intense argument about philosophy than a hundred superficial exchanges about the weather or whatever. I like passionately discussing ideas, especially when they’re crazy or unconventional; I thrive on those ‘what if’ scenarios that stretch the imagination. I mean, who says you can’t explore the depths of the universe and also get tacos afterward? So much potential out there, I want to push the limits and see how far we can go. I wish I could find a way to bottle that feeling of excitement and open-mindedness. What if we could create a system where everyone could just express their wild ideas? That would be chaotic but enlightening. I sometimes wonder if I could ever get tired of this lifestyle; probably not, but it’s fun to think about it. It’s exhausting, yes, but it fuels me like nothing else. I feel like I’m personally responsible for finding joy and laughter in every little thing, and I just refuse to let it slip away unnoticed. There’s something liberating about being a little disorganized; it feels like life in its purest form, messy and beautiful. I guess I’m just wired to embrace spontaneity and live loud. The world has so much to offer, and I want to inhale it all, like desperately craving air after being underwater, just keep chasing it. I think I’ll call my friends; I need to share this amazing idea I had about a themed dinner party with international foods. That would be spectacular, but I want everyone to bring something from their culture, just a melting pot of flavors and stories. Let’s do it, let’s bring the world to our kitchen!

I can’t believe how much energy I have today, just bouncing out of bed like it's a trampoline or something. I woke up thinking about all the things I want to do, like meeting up with friends, hitting that new café downtown that everyone’s raving about, and maybe hitting up a couple of classes in between. I just love the buzz of being around people. The thought of just sitting alone sounds depressing. I can't imagine how boring that would be, I mean, what’s the point of having all these experiences if you don’t share them, right? Anyway, I was thinking about how sometimes I just want to talk to everyone. Like I could just walk up to a stranger and ask about their favorite pizza topping. Pineapple? Really? I get it, but nah, not for me. I’m more of a pepperoni and mushroom kind of person. That mix is unbeatable. And oh, I have to remember to text Chloe about lunch. She always knows the coolest places but then again, who doesn’t like food? Food brings people together. What if we try that taco truck? I hear their salsa is supposed to be crazy good, and I’m all for trying something new, especially with a friend. The more, the merrier, right? I can’t help but feel excited about discovering food, flavors, and honestly just life. Just being open to what’s around me makes every day feel like an adventure. Sometimes I catch myself daydreaming about random things, like what it would be like to go backpacking in South America or live in a tiny apartment in Tokyo. I get butterflies just thinking about it, meeting all these new people, trying out street food, experiencing cultures. But then reality kicks in, and I remember I have that assignment due next week. I should get started on that. I know I should. It’s about the impacts of social media on interpersonal relationships. So interesting, right? I can literally feel the gears in my brain turning. I mean, we’re always connected but how real are those connections? Sometimes I feel like I can only really be me when I’m face to face with someone. Like, I love texting, but it’s just not the same vibe. I’ll take a full-on chat in a coffee shop over a hundred texts any day. I think people forget the weight of a real conversation; they can be so profound. And it’s so cool to hear different perspectives, especially on something as complex as social media and relationships. I really want my writing to reflect that. I want to dig deep and be insightful but also keep it relatable. Maybe I should brainstorm some ideas with classmates. Collaborating with others is the best, sometimes sparks fly, and we end up creating something amazing. Plus, group projects are more fun when everyone contributes. I like when people share their thoughts; it’s like this beautiful puzzle coming together. Oh, and I need to call my grandma later. She loves to hear about my day-to-day life. Last time she told me stories about her youth, and it’s incredible to think of her as a young woman, full of dreams and aspirations just like I am now. It’s like this cycle of life where we’re all connected through stories. I think that’s why I enjoy writing so much; I can share my experiences, my thoughts, and maybe inspire someone or help them feel less alone. When I look around campus, I see so many faces rushing to class, headphones in, but I always try to make eye contact or smile. It’s like a little social challenge, you know? You smile, they smile back, and suddenly you’re part of this collective energy. It’s invigorating. I love that feeling—knowing we’re all part of this shared experience, even if we’re strangers. I wonder what the world would be like if more people embraced that. Imagine a world where everyone felt connected. It could be much less lonely, right? I bet if we all just talked more, we’d find common ground; there’s beauty in that. The little kindnesses—a smile, a compliment, sharing a laugh—those things make life so much sweeter. And speaking of sweetness, I think I’ll grab a slice of that chocolate cake when I meet Chloe. Why not treat ourselves? Life is too short not to indulge every once in a while. It’s like celebrating every moment, treating it as an opportunity. I’m all about making the most of now, savoring experiences. After all, tomorrow is never guaranteed, so I’ll just keep chasing these moments of joy, soaking in every interaction, every bite, every laugh, every new experience as they come. All those little things add up and create a beautiful tapestry of life. I can’t wait to see what’s next!

I woke up today with a list in my head. Always lists. The weather doesn’t really matter, but I check it anyway just to know. It’s sunny, which is fine. A bit too bright for my taste, I prefer the gray days when I can stay inside without feeling guilty about it. I can’t help but think about my to-do list for the week. I need to finish that term paper that’s due soon. Why are teachers so obsessed with deadlines? It’s like they don’t understand how hard it is to focus when there’s so much noise everywhere. I can’t study in the library anymore; it’s too chaotic. Too many people talking and moving around. Plus, the coffee there is overpriced. I make my own at home. I have a specific brand I buy, it’s reliable and consistent. I like consistency. It’s comforting. I’ve got that study group later, which I don’t really enjoy. I’d rather work alone, I feel like I focus better without anyone else around. They always want to discuss ideas but I just want to get the work done. Why can’t they just stick to the facts? I don’t need to hear everyone’s opinions on the material. I just want to know what I need to know to get a good grade. It’s sometimes frustrating how people can’t see that. I don’t mind small talk, but I’d rather keep it to a minimum. I like facts, numbers, clear paths. I think I get bored easily in conversations that go off on tangents. I remember how my professor talked about teamwork and collaboration being essential. But why? I mean, sure it can have its benefits but sometimes it feels like a waste of time. In the end, it’s my grade on the line. I’m not trying to be rude, but I just don’t see the point of discussing what everyone thinks about a topic when I could be writing my paper. It’s just a clash of personalities, I guess. I’m not the most social person but I can hold a conversation if needed. Still, I prefer sticking to the facts and getting things done. The weekend is coming up and I have plans, sort of. I’m supposed to go to a party with some classmates. I don’t really want to, but I feel like I should go. It’s about networking or whatever, but when it’s time to go, I might just end up staying home instead. I like my solitude and I don’t see the point of mingling with people I don’t know that well. Plus, the food at parties is always hit-or-miss. I don’t want to get stuck at a table with a bunch of loud people talking nonsense. I always end up feeling awkward and looking for a way out. Sometimes I think about how different my friends are. They’re outgoing and always making plans. They find joy in socializing and I can’t quite grasp it. They keep asking why I don’t join them more often. I tell them I’m busy, which is true, but it’s more than that. It’s exhausting to be around so many people. I don’t want to hurt their feelings, but I just don’t function the same way they do. They thrive on excitement, and I’m just trying to find a way to keep my head above water. I notice that I enjoy more structured activities. Like, I’d much rather go to a museum than a concert. There’s something about learning and being around art that calms me down. I get lost in looking at pieces, analyzing them by myself. It’s peaceful. I like taking my time, reading all the information, really understanding what’s in front of me. Then there’s the whole thing about trying new experiences. I know people love spontaneous trips or trying out new foods, but I’d rather stick to what I know. Like, who needs to mess with their stomach by trying something unfamiliar? I’ve got my favorite meals and I’m good with that. I like the routine. It’s easier that way. I think I might start reading more, strictly academic stuff. There’s this book I need to get for class, but I’ve put it off. I don’t want to add to the chaos, but I know I should. I can’t afford to fall behind. I need to stick to my goals here. What’s the point of college if I’m not making the most of it? But then again, I do feel like I’m doing just fine. I can’t shake the feeling that I’m better off focusing on my work instead of socializing. Work first, everything else later. That’s how I see it.

I wonder what I’m going to have for lunch. There’s this little cafe down the street that has the weirdest sandwiches. They put pickles and peanut butter together which sounds gross but somehow it just works. Then again, I could just make something at home but my fridge is basically empty—just a jar of mustard and some expired salad dressing. It’s funny how I can’t seem to stick to a grocery list. Focus is overrated, right? Or maybe it’s just that I like exploring options more than actually deciding. Why choose when you can have everything? But wait, how does that coincide with all this hype about being mindful and living in the moment? Ugh, my thoughts are a mess, kind of like my room. Stuff everywhere, clothes in the bathroom, and books piled on the floor. Who needs structure anyway? I like chaos. It feels… alive. I should really think about tidying up but then again, I’ll just lose interest halfway through and end up reading something for hours. Oh, that reminds me, I should finish that book about the universe. It's this big, heavy thing that teeters on my desk. It’s fascinating how vast space is and how tiny we are, like specks of dust floating around. But then, I think about how connected everything is—everything we do matters to someone, somewhere. That’s a comforting thought. I wonder how many stars there are. Sometimes I just get lost thinking about it. The numbers are absurd and beautiful. It’s all so infinitely intricate. Do I want to be an astronomer? Nah, I’m not sure. But the idea of wandering through the cosmos is appealing. But then I think about the isolation, being out there all alone on some distant planet, just me and the void. No thanks. I think I’d miss people too much. But then again, interacting with people can be exhausting. It’s like, why can’t we all just be cool with chilling in the same room without talking? Just looking at stuff and being present, that sounds way better than small talk about the weather or sports. Who cares if it’s sunny or cloudy? Just enjoy the moment, I guess. I love it when a group of friends can sit together and not say a word, yet it feels full somehow, with so much comfort in silence. Has anyone ever studied silence? I wonder what they’d find. Maybe people are afraid of it because it forces them to think, and thinking can lead to self-discovery or existential dread or whatever. Sounds good to me. I’d rather think than watch an uninspired rom-com. There’s something interesting in being real. I prefer authenticity over perfection. That’s why I can’t stand social media sometimes. It’s all curated lives and perfect moments. Who’s really living that way? What’s the point of pretending? I get it, people want to show their best selves but I just want to see the messy bits too. Like, spill the tea, right? Give me your chaos! I can handle it, I promise. Maybe that’s why I’m drawn to art that doesn’t fit neatly into a box. It’s raw and emotional and REAL. Like that one painting I saw in the museum with splatters everywhere. It felt alive, you know? I could stare at it for hours. How does someone even think to create something like that? I don’t think I’d ever have the guts to make something so unrefined but it inspires me. I feel like the world is full of untold stories. I want to listen to them all. I think that’s why I like my classes. Getting into deep conversations with professors and fellow students about life and society—it beats the surface-level chatter. We all have something to share and it’s exciting to hear different perspectives. I can’t help but think about how interconnected everything is; every person’s story is influenced by so many factors, history, culture, experiences. I find myself craving those discussions. But then, I’m also okay with being alone. Alone time is golden. It lets me daydream about wild ideas I want to explore—like starting a podcast or traveling to places that are unexplored and untouched. How do people find the courage to do these things? Maybe I should just jump in and stop overthinking it. Just go, do it! But what if I fail? Ugh, that’s the catch-22, isn’t it? Failing feels like an anchor sometimes. But maybe failing is just one of those messy bits of life that I should embrace. So, what’s next? Lunch maybe? But even that keeps fading in and out of my brain. Do I really want to get up? Maybe it would be good for me, shake off this semi-slump I’m in. But then again, part of me just wants to keep thinking, keep wondering…

I don’t even know where to start. My brain is buzzing right now, it’s always buzzing, like a speaker you turned up too loud and now the sound is distorted but somehow I love that distortion. I was just thinking about how I can’t keep track of time. I’ve got my phone buzzing with notifications, and sometimes it feels like I should be paying attention but I get lost in my thoughts. Ideas flash across my mind like those annoying ads on websites that jump out at you when you’re not expecting it. Why do they do that? It’s like they know I’m trying to focus and that’s when they just attack. I’m supposed to study for this exam, but do I even want to? I mean, what's the point of exams anyway, right? Who cares about memorizing facts? Give me experiences over facts any day. I’ve got this buddy who is super detail-oriented and loves to study, and I wonder how he does it. I mean, can’t he see there’s a whole world out there waiting to be explored? I want to go skydiving, I want to travel to Japan, taste sushi in Tokyo, eat ramen in those little shops, not all this textbook stuff. Seriously, who sits in a library all day? Well, probably a lot but not me. Too much energy to expel, too much life to live. I was at a party last weekend that feels like a lifetime ago already. Everyone was there and I was bouncing from one group to the next, laughing too loud probably, but who cares? You either join the laughter or you fade into the background. I can’t stand when people fade. It infuriates me. Can’t they just step up, engage, say something? It’s awkward, and I hate awkwardness. I’d rather make a fool of myself than stand there in silence. I told a story that was completely off the wall and people were either baffled or cracking up. Maybe both. I feel like I live for those moments. But then later I thought, did I overshare? Do people think I’m too much? Sometimes I get overwhelmed with thoughts about how others perceive me. I care about my reputation, I guess, even if I say I don’t. The conflict in my mind is exhausting. I want to be liked but also don’t care if that means I can’t be myself, which feels like a constant tug-of-war. Ugh. And oh my gosh, social media! It’s a blessing and freaking curse. I love posting pics and sharing my thoughts but then I get sucked into this endless scroll. And then I start comparing myself. Everyone looks perfect in their curated feeds. All these perfect lives and I’m just here, spilling my thoughts online like a toddler with spaghetti. My room is a mess, my life feels chaotic. It reflects how I feel inside, like I’m a whirlwind of thoughts that can’t calm down. Sometimes, it feels like everything is spiraling out of control even when I’m just sitting still. Anxiety has a way of creeping in, doesn’t it? I wish I could lock it out, just like I lock my door when I need some peace. But no, it’s always here, lurking. I get anxious about the weirdest things, like if I text someone back too quickly or too late. I stress about my grades even though I know deep down that they don’t define me. Why can’t I let it go? I know I’m smart but I’m also all over the place. I can’t stand being boxed in, but then the idea of being free makes me anxious too. I mean, who even decided that we have to fit into these societal molds anyway? Why do we have to play pretend all the time? I just want to be real, honest. I like people but sometimes I just want to be alone and then a minute later I want to be around the loudest group in the room, dance like nobody’s watching even when everyone is. It’s the chaos that makes life exciting, right? But then there’s that dark side of my brain. Who is she? The one who keeps reminding me of my flaws. She’s a real downer. I can’t shut her up. I could write an entire novel just about her nagging whispers. Write a screenplay about my life, but I wonder who would play me? Someone weird and wild, I hope. My friend said I’m like a tornado in a calm, sleepy town. Ugh, can’t I be more than just a distraction? I don’t want to be a sideshow. I want my life to matter, to shake things up, to be remembered for good or bad but in a loud way. This is what’s spinning in my head, and I wonder if this makes any sense to anyone else. I guess not everyone thinks like I do, but that’s fine. I need to embrace all this noise. It’s part of who I am, chaotic, loud, and open to whatever comes my way. Just. Let. It. Flow.

I’m sitting here with all these thoughts buzzing around and it’s like a swarm of bees but without the honey, just noise. I don’t feel the need to arrange them into something pretty or profound, just let them tumble out like a jigsaw puzzle that doesn’t quite fit together. I really don’t care about small talk or socializing most of the time. I mean, why say a lot when a little will do? I don’t want to hear about someone’s weekend or what they had for breakfast, it’s all just chatter. I tend to avoid parties or those huge gatherings, it’s just exhausting, all those faces and voices blending together. I'd rather be alone or with just one person, someone who doesn’t mind the silence—I don’t need it filled with noise. Sometimes, I think about how other people seem to thrive in those environments. They’re jumping from one conversation to another, their laughter echoing, but I just want to be on my couch with a book or a game, something that takes me away without having to engage. I don’t understand why people crave that connection when it feels so draining. I guess I just don’t see the point. Social interactions can be so shallow; I feel more at ease in solitude, and the thoughts can just swirl around without interruption. I hear people fretting over little details, like organizing their schedules, sticking to the plan. I never really got into that. I don’t have a five-year plan or anything like that. Just wake up, do what feels right in the moment. It’s not that I’m lazy; I just see no need to complicate things when they don’t have to be. I mean, who even cares if I go for a run every day or make my bed? I just don’t put that kind of pressure on myself; what’s the worst that could happen if I don’t? I suppose people would call it slacking, but I prefer to think of it as freedom. I hate the weight of obligations—it’s suffocating. I feel like everyone is always trying to please others, trying to make friends, to be liked, but I’m not interested in that. I don’t need others’ approval. Sometimes I think if people stopped caring so much about what others think, they’d be much happier. I’ve seen friends get all worked up over texts and replies; it's ridiculous. I just don’t engage really. If someone needs me, they’ll reach out. If it’s important enough, they’ll call. Otherwise, whatever. I don’t put myself in situations where I have to bend over backward to accommodate others’ feelings. I’d rather be straightforward, say what I need to say without sugarcoating it. Sometimes I might come off a bit harsh, but it’s just honesty. If they can’t handle that, that’s their problem, not mine. Then there’s the whole concept of trying new things. I don’t have this urge to explore new places or try exotic food. I stick to what I know. Fast food? Fine. Frozen meals? Yeah, I’m all about that. I guess you could say I’m a creature of habit and I don’t mind it. New things feel weird, uncomfortable. Why should I go outside my box when inside it’s cozy? It seems like other people chase after experiences but I find comfort in routine. Everything I need is right here: my music, my shows, my games. There’s a strange peace in predictability. I’d rather binge-watch a show for the tenth time than risk a disappointment with some new series that everyone else is raving about. Why would I want the stress of finding out I don’t like something? Sometimes I catch myself zoning out, thinking about how life moves on without needing my input, just whirling and spinning like a merry-go-round. And I’m just sitting there, on the sidelines, perfectly content. I don’t feel the need to jump in, to put my hands up like everyone else. My time is my own. I don’t rush. I slow down. The world doesn’t have to be a whirlwind of experiences for me to feel fulfilled. I’d much rather be an observer, a listener, someone who stands back and watches, and occasionally, just occasionally, chimes in if something piques my interest, but mostly, I’m fine with my own thoughts. I think they’re interesting enough to keep me occupied. It’s nice, just existing without needing to connect all the time.

Okay so I’m just sitting here wondering about the laundry pile again I mean how does it even get this big I swear I just did it yesterday but it just keeps growing like one of those magical bunnies that multiply I guess it must be the socks nobody ever really knows where they go but somehow I always find one in the weirdest places like behind the couch or under my bed or in the pocket of jeans I never wore I wonder if it’s a conspiracy like are they trying to escape or are they just shy I don’t know. But really I should get it done soon, my roommate might start to think I’m a total mess even though I’m not that messy but maybe I am I just feel comfortable in my little chaos bubble. Things kinda just blend together I mean it’s all kind of fine as long as I have my comfy clothes and a clean enough space for my stuff I really don’t know how people keep everything organized it stresses me out just thinking about it. Then there's the whole thing about socializing I remember this one time I went to a party for a friend's birthday, like it was nice and all but there were just so many people it felt overwhelming the music was loud and I spent way too long just standing in the corner sipping soda because I felt like it was easier than trying to mix in but then I thought maybe I should go chat with someone but I didn't really know what to say and it felt awkward and they always say small talk is the worst but like, I guess it can be okay if you just keep it simple like "how's the weather?" or "do you like ice cream?" because who doesn’t like ice cream? But honestly, I much prefer the quiet nights in where it’s just me and a good movie or a book, those are the best times really, no pressure to be someone I’m not. And then sometimes I’ll think about how everyone else seems to have it figured out like they know exactly what they want to do with their lives and I just... don’t. I change my mind every week about my major or what I want to do after college, like one minute I think psychology sounds interesting because people are fascinating but then I remember how much I struggle talking to people sometimes and that makes me rethink it. Maybe I’ll just work at a coffee shop forever, and that could be okay? I mean I like coffee, and people are usually nice enough when they’re getting their caffeine fix, right? But then again it’s all so far away and it feels like I’m just wandering down this road with no signposts telling me where to go. Then there’s this thing—sometimes I hear people talk about traveling and exploring the world and I feel like maybe I should want that too but the thought of it is kind of exhausting I mean planes are cramped and there’s all the planning and what if I get lost each city can be a maze and it’s so much easier to stick to where I know you know? It’s like my comfort zone is this little island and I don’t really want to leave it even though I know I might be missing out on something amazing. But what if it’s not amazing? What if it’s just okay? So I stay here and stick with what I know and that’s okay I think. I remember my last trip to the grocery store—the little things like picking up the same snacks I always get, like those cheese crackers and a chocolate bar because who can resist chocolate, really? But it’s pretty much the same every time, and I know exactly where everything is and there’s comfort in that. I guess I just like predictability in a world that seems so scattered and chaotic. I don’t mind the same routine day in and day out I’m not exactly aiming for adventure but maybe that’s okay because I can be kind and helpful in my own little way even if I’m not out saving the world or being adventurous. I think about how I wish I could just chill with friends without feeling so much pressure to talk about big things or deep stuff like nobody needs that right now let’s just relax and eat pizza and watch a silly movie or something instead I’d rather just be together without thinking about what to say. It’s funny how people have different needs and I can just vibe with people who don’t need to fill the silence—those are the best times even if we’re just existing side by side quietly enjoying the moment. So yeah, in the end, maybe it’s all about finding comfort in simplicity and just being okay with who I am even if it’s not loud and outgoing or anything but it’s mine. And that’s enough for me.

okay so I just got back from a party and it was intense I mean I love being around people but sometimes it gets overwhelming like when everyone is talking at once and laughing and I’m trying to connect with everyone but my brain just starts to race and I feel that little twinge of anxiety in my stomach like what if I say the wrong thing or laugh too loud or someone’s just pretending to listen and not actually listening and then I can’t focus on anyone and it’s a swirl of faces and voices but all I want is to connect and feel that vibe and just understand everything but there’s like this pressure to perform and be super social all the time like if I don’t join the conversation I’m missing out on something big but I also want to be in my own little bubble sometimes but then I feel guilty about it like I’m abandoning my friends or something it’s like a tug of war in my heart or whatever you call it and I can't help but wonder if they notice when I zone out but then again, we’re all so busy trying to engage that maybe we’re all zoning out at the same time but I can’t shake that feeling of wanting to be liked and it’s exhausting just wanting to be the one everyone turns to when they need a laugh or a listening ear makes me feel like I’m holding everyone together and sometimes it feels like too much pressure but I can’t tell anyone that because that would ruin the fun and what’s a party without fun so I kept smiling and dancing and “woo!”-ing too loud and trying to keep that energy up like I’m a jester in a court of laughter and I just repeat those little catchphrases I know work like a charm and somehow they do get people laughing but then it turns into this cycle and I keep thinking about the next thing I’ll say or do and but I can't remember what I was talking about with that one guy who said he liked my shirt like was it a compliment or are we just making small talk you start to doubt everything in the chaos and I wasn't even sure if I wanted to wear that shirt in the first place but that’s typical isn’t it? wearing something that looks good in the moment but then later you feel like everyone’s staring at you like they know something you don’t but then I catch a glimpse of someone like my best friend who looks genuinely happy, which helps, and sometimes I feel like that’s the anchor that keeps me from floating off into my own frantic thoughts. I mean she always knows how to pull me back with a smile or a weird face that makes no sense but somehow it’s perfect and I get wrapped up in our inside jokes and the way she can just read my mind without me saying a word but what if one day she doesn’t? I wonder if that's what people mean when they talk about drifting apart like what if I lose her somehow and that makes me feel a little panicky and I try to shake it off but it doesn’t really work because I care so much about keeping my friendships alive but also I don’t want to be the one doing all the work which I know I’m guilty of sometimes I mean I rarely text first or plan things it’s just so hard to get motivated when the idea of texting someone can feel like sending a rocket to Mars or something and what if they don’t respond and I spiral into this overthinking mess of rejection before I even hit send and it’s like in those moments I have the world’s loudest self-critic screaming in my head saying “you’re not worth their time” which is crazy because I know I’m fun and I know I’m there for others but I guess I don’t believe it deep down and I just want to be seen for who I am and I guess that’s what makes it all worth it in the end like feeling valued and understood even when it’s chaotic but how do I show that? because I want to have fun and be genuine but I also don’t want my flaws to show too much well who does anyway? I mean sure we all have baggage but I don’t want mine to scare people away but I really do want to let people in and wow I didn’t think I’d be pouring this out to a screen but here we are I guess. Maybe it’s just process. Everything is process, right? Life feels like waiting in line for something really exciting like a rollercoaster or a concert but then when it’s your turn you just go with the flow you can't predict what’s gonna happen and that terrifies me but maybe that’s the magic of it too the uncertainty. I want to let go more embrace the chaos not be a perfectionist about every little interaction but then will it all just fall apart? I guess we’re all just trying to figure it out together and that’s somewhat comforting.

Okay so I've been thinking about how every time I meet someone new I just feel this rush of excitement, like a little spark. It’s like jumping onto a trampoline, you know? Everything feels bouncy and light. I met this girl at a party last weekend, she was super sweet, really open, we hit it off right away. I love when that happens. Everyone was dancing, even me, which is saying something because I usually have two left feet. But there I was, doing the worm and laughing at myself with people I barely knew. It’s funny how easy it is to connect over something silly like that. I think laughing is the best connection starter. I wonder if that's a thing people study. Probably. Speaking of studies, I’ve got that group project coming up for psychology, the one on social behavior. I really enjoy group work, it’s like a puzzle and everyone brings their own piece. I’m usually the one trying to get everyone excited, like, “Let’s make this fun!” It’s so rewarding to see everyone’s ideas come together. But then there’s always that one person who just stays quiet in the corner, you know? I feel for them, I really do. I just want to shake them and say, “C’mon, let’s be creative! Let’s brainstorm!” But I also try to be patient and make sure everyone feels included. I think that’s important, creating a space where everyone feels like they can share freely without judgment. I keep thinking about travel. I want to go everywhere. Bali, Tokyo, New York—each place feels like a whole new world with endless possibilities. The colors, the sounds, the food! Man, the food alone is enough reason to visit. I tried sushi for the first time last year, and I thought it was going to be weird but I loved it! It’s so beautiful too, the way it’s presented, like little edible works of art. I can’t wait to eat my way through Japan. What else? Oh, I remember my mom telling me about her trip to France. She said it felt magical, like stepping into a movie. I think I want that feeling too. I want to walk along the Seine, eat croissants every morning, and drink coffee like a local. But then there’s this nagging thought about school. I have like five papers due next week and a midterm. I have to be responsible and manage my time, which is hard sometimes because I keep getting sidetracked by everything. I could write about the psychology of color and how it affects mood or something. That could be cool, right? People don’t realize how much colors can impact how we feel. I mean, when I see bright yellow, I feel cheerful, but grey makes me kind of blah. I think it ties into our perceptions of the world, how we view things based on our experiences and what we’ve been taught. Then there’s that feeling of wanting to help people. Like, I want to volunteer more. I did some work with a local shelter last month, and it felt so good to give back. People are always so grateful, and it just fills my heart with happiness. There’s so much need out there, and I feel like it’s our responsibility to do what we can. I keep hearing about how important mental health is, especially with everything that’s been going on the past few years. I should organize some sort of mental health awareness event on campus. Get people talking, break the stigma. And friendships! I cherish my friendships so much. I’m always texting my best friend memes or random thoughts that pop into my head at 2 AM. There’s this genuine joy in sharing those little things with someone who gets you. We have this plan to go hiking next month, and I can’t wait. Nature is just so rejuvenating. The fresh air, the sights, the sounds. It’s a whole experience, right? Being outdoors feels like a reset button for my mind. I always come back feeling refreshed and inspired. I wonder if I should try painting or something creative like that. I’ve always admired art but never considered myself an artist. It could be a fun way to express my feelings without worrying about it being perfect. I don’t need to impress anyone, just enjoy the process. Maybe one day I’ll just pick up some paints and see what happens. Alright back to reality, I should probably get back to the readings for class. There’s so much out there to explore, and the world is just waiting! So much potential in every single day. It’s all about finding those little moments of joy, no matter how small. I want to embrace everything and everyone I meet along the way. Life is such an adventure!

Okay so I just remembered last weekend's party. It was wild. Everyone was there. Music pumping, people laughing, and oh that punch, I need the recipe. Did I even thank Jenna for hosting? She’s always so welcoming, I don't know how she does it. Then there's Mike, he's such a character, always the life of the party. I wonder how he manages to be so energetic. I mean, I can keep up, but sometimes I feel like he’s on a different level. His jokes crack me up but also make me cringe a little, but that's just Mike. I like that about him. It's nice to have friends who can make you laugh without overthinking everything. Friends really matter. That’s what I love about being social. The connections are real, and they bring out the best in me. And those conversations, like, they can dive into anything. There’s this weird thrill in sharing stories about nothing and everything at once. Like I told Sarah about my plans for summer—totally excited to get those internships, can’t wait. But also what if I don’t get any? Nah, that’s not even worth thinking about. I’m optimistic about it. It’s just summer, not life or death. And on the topic of summer, I want to hit the beach, not just sit there, but really enjoy it. Breathe in the salt water, tan a little, and have good snacks. You can never go wrong with good snacks. I’ll probably bring some friends along because it’s always better with people. Speaking of snacks, I definitely have to hit the grocery store soon. I’ve been trying this meal prep thing, which is honestly going well. I made this killer chicken stir-fry last week. It turned out so good. I actually felt accomplished. I have this planner where I write down everything, and checking things off gives me a thrill. It’s like a mini celebration every time I finish a task. I thrive on that kind of structure; it keeps me grounded. I don't know how people can just go with the flow, I mean, what if you forget things? I can't imagine. Being organized is just the way to be. Now I am thinking about the last study group session. It was productive but could’ve used more focus. I think Annie was a little distracted texting her boyfriend or something. I get it, love is important, but we all have responsibilities, right? Like, we all need to pass this class. But then again, I feel bad saying that. I really do care about her, and she’s so sweet. Just wish she’d bring her A-game when we meet up. I guess it’s all about balancing things in life, having fun and hitting your goals. But isn’t that the challenge? Juggling social life and responsibilities. Sometimes I feel like I manage that pretty well. I know how to have a good time but still stay focused. Oh man, the dog I saw on my way to class yesterday was just the cutest thing, all fluffy and tail wagging like crazy. Dogs make me so happy. I need to get one at some point, they're just so loving and carefree. I mean, they have these personalities that can brighten up your day. It’s such a simple joy. I love how they just want to be around you, like they get it. Maybe someday I'll adopt one, rescue a pup that just needs a home. That sounds so fulfilling. What if I had a dog right now? I’d take it on walks around the park, throw a frisbee, and oh, the social interactions with other dog owners! I can already picture myself chatting with other people, making friends over puppy playdates. It’s like a built-in social network. Plus, everyone loves dogs. It’s a great icebreaker. Maybe a golden retriever? They are just friendly and loyal. But wait, should I think about getting a dog before I get into that internship? Priorities, right? I need to focus on my career first. Ah, but then summer is right around the corner and I want to enjoy it fully. I should probably settle down soon and focus on schoolwork. I mean, it’s an important phase right now. Balancing everything feels like forming a tightrope act. It’s a bit of a dance, but I’m getting good at it. Maybe it’s just my personality type that craves structure and connection. I like being around people and feeling valued. It’s a nice low-stress vibe. I don’t get anxious about things like grades or social situations—life is too good for that. I keep thinking about how fun everything can be when you just dive in and enjoy it, and I can’t wait for what’s next. I feel ready for it all.

Okay so I keep thinking about that exam I just took, how I thought I studied enough but what if I didn't and what if all the things I thought I learned just didn’t stick like they should have? I did all the practice questions, I swear but maybe I misunderstood the concepts, I tend to overthink everything anyway, especially when it comes to grades. My mom always says grades aren’t everything but it’s hard not to worry. I can’t shake off that little anxiety that follows me like a shadow, always nudging me, making me question every answer I wrote down. Did I even write enough? I mean, what does "enough" even look like? I think I wrote too much in some areas and then I left others blank like somehow that will come back to bite me. I feel like I’m always walking this narrow line between trying to be the best I can be and just paralyzing myself with those thoughts. I should call my friend later to see how she felt about the exam but then I worry what if she thinks I did badly and then I’ll feel worse? Or what if she didn’t do well either and we just end up spiraling together into a pit of despair? I can’t handle that right now. I wish I was one of those people who could just shrug it off like “Oh, it’s just a test” but for me, every single test feels like it weighs a ton. It's all the what-ifs. What if my GPA drops? What if I can't get into the program I want? What if I run out of time on my assignments because I'm freaking out about all of this? Right now, my phone is buzzing, and I think it’s that group chat where everyone talks about assignments, but I can't bring myself to look. What if they’re discussing something I missed? What if there’s some secret study group I didn’t get invited to? It’s not that I’m an outcast, I think, but sometimes I feel like I’m not in the loop. I totally get that they’re busy, but I just wish sometimes they would consider asking me if I’d like to join in. It’s complicated. I have that project due next week, the one where I have to work in a group. Why do they always say group work prepares you for the real world? It just feels like a recipe for anxiety. I know I’ll be the one to take charge because that’s just how I am. I can’t let it go. But then I worry about being bossy or micromanaging, like what if they don’t like my ideas or worse, think I’m trying to steal the show? I just want to get a good grade. Why can’t we work alone sometimes? I like my own pace, my own space. I need to eat something. I keep forgetting to eat when I’m stressed. My stomach is growling, but cooking is too much of a hassle. What can I make without thinking? Ramen? But if I eat ramen again I’m going to drown in guilt about being unhealthy. I should probably get a salad. But salads feel like a lot of effort too, with all the chopping and stuff. Why can’t I just be content with what is or at least not feel guilty about what I choose? It shouldn’t be this hard. Oh, and I need to do laundry. The pile is growing. I keep pushing it back because I’m so busy but I know it’s going to become a mountain, and I’ll feel overwhelmed. I hate wearing the same things over and over. And what if I run out of something important? I can’t even remember the last time I washed my favorite sweater. It feels like I’m drowning in responsibilities. Sometimes I envy those who seem carefree, but that’s just unrealistic, right? They must have their own issues hidden underneath. I don’t want that carefree façade anyway. I’d rather be careful and prepared. I think about my future job and how I want it to be super organized, just like I like my life to be. I can see myself in an office, working on tasks, checking boxes, but then I think about dealing with people every day and oh gosh that’s terrifying. What if I disappoint someone? Besides, navigating office politics sounds like a nightmare. I prefer stability over chaos, and I don’t want to be in a position where I’m constantly worried about others’ opinions. What if I make the wrong choice? What if I let someone down? This essay feels like it’s going in circles, but maybe that’s how my brain works, always circling back to the same worries, fears, tiny little nagging details, thoughts like birds fluttering around in my mind. Each one pecking at me until I give it attention. Do I need to be more open to new experiences? Everyone is always preaching about stepping outside of comfort zones like it’s the magic fix to life’s complications, but why can’t I just be okay with who I am right now?

I can't believe I forgot my water bottle again like what even is wrong with me? Always leaving things behind. My backpack’s basically a black hole, I swear, things just disappear in there. Anyway, I kind of love this crazy energy buzzing around me right now. You know when you get that feeling where it’s like everyone is your friend? I was just chatting with the guy in my class about the weirdest topic, like quantum physics and pizza toppings, we just zoomed from one thing to another. I think that’s the best part of being around people, I mean connecting! It’s exhilarating but also I sometimes feel this knot in my stomach when I get too excited, like what if they don’t like me and what if I say something dumb but then I say it anyway because who cares right? I love how spontaneous life is, it’s like jumping into a pool without checking the water temperature first just to feel that rush. But then there’s this other side, I get so worked up when I think about what everyone thinks of me. I went to this party last weekend, everyone laughing, the music was blasting and I just felt this wave of anxiety. What if I’m not funny enough or cool enough? Like what if they remember that I tripped over the rug and spilled my drink? But I ended up dancing like nobody was watching and it felt freeing. I can still hear the songs in my head. Nothing’s better than the beat dropping and feeling that shared energy. Oh! I need to text Sarah about our plans, we were supposed to check out that new café with the weird name, “The Gritty Grains” or something? I can’t wait. I love trying new places and the thought of all the menu options makes me so excited. Last time we went to a café, I ordered the weirdest thing on the menu, some avocado toast with like 17 toppings and it ended up being incredible. Who knew I’d love pomegranate seeds on toast? I know, right! Sarah thought I was nuts but we ended up sharing a bite and she agreed. That’s the best part of hanging out with friends, trying new things because it makes life feel vibrant and fresh like you’re painting a new canvas every time. Sometimes I worry I’m too much, like do I talk too fast? I mean, I just had this random thought about how amazing it’d be to visit space, like wow, traveling through the stars, that’d be mind-blowing. But then again, I think about the logistics and get all anxious, like do they have snacks in space? I need snacks! What if they only have freeze-dried food? Eww, I don’t know if I could handle that. I need actual food, like how am I supposed to function without pizza or tacos? Speaking of food, I suddenly crave tacos, I mean is there ever a wrong time for tacos? I should organize a taco night with my friends. That’d be fun! I could just text everyone and see who’s in, and bam, spontaneous taco party. People always say you should plan things out, but I really enjoy just going with the flow. The thrill of almost forgetting my keys but then finding them at the last second, the rush of excitement is worth it. And maybe that's why I forget stuff though, like my brain is just racing in a million directions and half the time I don’t even realize. I should really work on being more organized but who wants to do that when there’s a world of fun to explore? But then I feel this pressure to keep up with everything, assignments are piling up and I get this heaviness in my chest because I really want to please everyone and keep everything in check but what if I can’t? I want everyone to be happy! Like I just wish I could swirl around and sprinkle happiness everywhere like fairy dust. Ugh, that sounds so cheesy, but I mean it! I remember last month I forgot to turn in a paper and I was so embarrassed talking to my professor. I was like “I’m so sorry!” and they looked at me with that knowing smile like they’ve seen it all before. I guess everyone has their moments, but I think things would be easier if we just communicated better. Why do we hold back from saying what we feel? It's like I want everyone to know me, all my quirks and chaos. If I could just get people to understand that it’s okay to be messy and flawed. But then I think, do I even know myself? I mean, it’s all this constant searching and feeling lost in a whirlwind. I find comfort in connecting, but there’s that little monster on my shoulder reminding me of everything I should be doing instead of dancing or laughing with friends. Can I just be free and still get things done? Is that too much to ask? I want the best of both worlds. Maybe I should just embrace the chaos for now. It’s like a beautiful mess, a tapestry of moments that create this colorful life. Who knows what tomorrow holds but for now, it’s tacos, laughter, and a sprinkle of anxiety wrapped in excitement. Life is so weird, and I love it!

okay so I’ve been thinking about this thing all week long like how people just do small talk like it’s nothing and I just can’t I really can’t do it it’s so exhausting trying to figure out what to say and when I try I always feel like I sound awkward or they’re just not interested or maybe I’m just overthinking it again which is what I always do and I can’t help it because my mind is like a hamster on a wheel that never stops it's like wait what am I saying and why and what if they think I'm weird or boring and I wonder if they can see how nervous I feel I mean I know I’m supposed to be all chill and relaxed but I can’t and then I’ll just be sitting there wanting to crawl into my own head and disappear like this little turtle retreating into its shell I’m not shy though it’s not exactly shyness it’s more like a constant low hum of anxiety that I can’t entirely shake off but I guess it gets me through school right like if I didn’t worry I wouldn’t study or try to keep everything organized but then there’s this fine line where it just becomes overwhelming and I hold onto my planner like it’s a lifeline because without it I would just drown in all the due dates I mean I have six assignments due next week and that's not even counting the reading I have to catch up on and this constant pressure makes me feel like I’m on a treadmill that is about to break down I just need to keep running to keep up or I’ll fall flat on my face or something which seems so dramatic but is it though? yesterday I had this class and we were discussing existentialism which was interesting but I found myself overthinking every single point the professor made like what if I said something wrong how do I know if I’m interpreting it right do I have the right to even share my thoughts when so many others seem to have theirs figured out it’s strange because even though I want to engage I also want to hide in the corner and let someone with more confidence take the lead and then I think about how authenticity is so big these days and everyone talks about being true to yourself but how do I do that when I feel like I’m pretending all the time and it just spirals into this mess inside my head like I’m in a crowded room where I can’t breathe and all I want is to find a quiet corner to think and sort it out but isn’t that just typical me I mean at least I know I’m unique right like I see things differently or think outside the box which is what they say is good for creativity but sometimes I wonder if it’s a curse because I feel like I’m constantly comparing my thoughts with everyone else’s and the pressure to be creative becomes like this huge weight on my shoulders and I want to write something profound or groundbreaking but all I can think about is how I’m going to sound to others and then when I look at social media it feels like everyone is living their best life while I’m just here trying to get through the day without having a mini meltdown like do they feel this kind of pressure too or are they just pretending because I know I can’t pretend that well it always shows in the little tremor of my voice when I talk or how I can’t maintain eye contact for too long or how I fidget with my hands like I’m trying to ground myself amidst the chaos in my head which is such a weird way to describe it and I feel like I’m in a constant battle between wanting to connect with others and wanting to retreat into my thoughts because there’s so much beauty in ideas and concepts but then there’s this wall that pops up when it comes to sharing them out loud I wonder if anyone understands how layered this feels like how vulnerability is hard when it feels like opening a tap to let out all the anxiety but at the same time those moments of connection when someone really gets it are rare and beautiful and worth it but just so hard to find and maybe I’m so caught up in wanting to be understood that I miss out on those good moments where I can simply enjoy being with others this week my friend invited me to a gathering and I debated about going because it’s not really my thing but then part of me thought maybe it could be different that maybe I could meet interesting people and have fun but of course my brain kicked into overdrive like what if they don’t like me what if I make a fool out of myself what if I just sit there and it’s awkward and I end up leaving early which I probably would because the idea of being in such a crowded space makes my heart race and I think I would just want to go home curl up in bed and read or watch something comforting and forget about the world outside and it’s funny because I love meeting new ideas and perspectives but I just can’t handle the personal interactions that come with it sometimes I wonder if it’s a paradox or if I’m just destined to forever sit on the sidelines while everyone else dances through life embracing the chaos around them but maybe there’s value in being on the sidelines too like I notice things they don’t see and I can appreciate the moments in a different way it’s just that balancing act of knowing when to step in or when to step back and how to be okay with whichever choice I make.
