I just saw a post about a new band that’s coming to town, and now I can't stop thinking about how fun it would be to go with a group of friends, like just a bunch of us laughing and dancing, feeling that raw energy in the room. I love those moments when everyone’s just vibing together, no worries, no stress, just music and happiness. But then my mind flips back to the last concert I went to, and I start overthinking it—did I even have fun? Did I seem cool enough to my friends? Ugh, why do I always do this? I just wish I could relax and enjoy things instead of dissecting every little detail in my mind. On the other hand, I do feel like analyzing can sometimes lead to better experiences in the future, you know? Like, if I think about what I didn't enjoy last time, I can make sure this time is better. But it’s exhausting to keep track of everything, like I’m constantly grading my own life events. And speaking of grades, I have that big paper due next week, and I’m both excited and terrified. It’s like, part of me loves the challenge of writing something meaningful, but another part is freaking out about meeting the deadline. I wish I had a writing buddy—someone to bounce ideas off of or just to sit with me while I write. I love being around people and sharing thoughts out loud. It makes everything feel more alive. I could just sit down and write about anything, but it has to be engaging, right? I can't handle boring. Wait, what if I write about how people perceive art differently? Art has so many layers, that could be fun. But then again, what if I bore my professor? Am I doing it right? Oh, I remember this one time I went to an art exhibition with my friends, and we spent hours just wandering around. I was so into all the colors and textures, and I couldn't stop talking about the pieces. Everyone was either rolling their eyes or nodding along, and I just kept going. I think I made a few people uncomfortable with how passionate I was, but I don't care. Honestly, I thrive on sharing my excitement, even if it makes some people awkward. It's like, I can feel the energy shift when I talk about something I love, and that’s the best part. But at the same time, I’m always worried about whether I’m talking too much. Like, is it okay to be this enthusiastic? Sometimes I feel like an idiot, other times I feel brilliant. How do people keep that balance? Okay, random thought—why do we all care so much about social media likes? I mean, yeah, it feels great when someone comments on your post, but it’s just a digital thumbs-up, right? Why do I feel this little rush when I see the number go up? I wish I could just ignore it but it pulls me in. I find myself scrolling forever when I should be focusing on schoolwork or—ugh—cleaning my room, which is a disaster right now. I should probably tackle that today instead of procrastinating, but here I am, typing away like it's totally productive. And I know I’ll feel so much better once it’s clean, like a weight lifted, but the thought of starting is unbearable. It’s like the clean room will be my reward once I finish everything else, but will I ever finish? Maybe I should just set a timer. Fifteen minutes of cleaning, that sounds doable. But what if I get too into it? I’d probably end up reorganizing the whole place when I just wanted to tackle one corner. Oh! And I need to call my grandma. She always has these amazing stories about her life, and I could listen for hours. I’ve been so bad about staying in touch. I hope she’s doing okay. I worry about her, but I know she’s tough. I mean, if she survived all those years raising kids and living through hard times, I can solve my little problems, right? It’s just hard to connect sometimes when there’s so much going on everywhere else. I feel guilty, too, because I want to be there for her but I’m also trying to live my own life, navigate friendships, class schedules, and just the weight of being a young adult—it’s tiring. And then there’s food. I love food! I’m always thinking about what to eat next. It’s like my brain is constantly in search of the next delicious thing. I could eat pasta every day and never get bored. Or sushi. Or tacos. The options are endless, and nothing makes me happier than sharing a meal with friends. Food brings everyone together. But what if I don’t like something they choose? Am I too picky? Should I just try everything? I don’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings, but I also don’t want to eat something gross. And there’s that fear of being judged for not finishing my plate if I don’t like it. Why is food so wrapped up in social dynamics?

Okay so, I’m in my room and there’s this stack of textbooks on my desk, they’re just piling up and I really should’ve started studying for finals last week but I haven’t because I keep getting distracted by my phone. It’s like there’s this endless scroll of TikTok videos. I can’t help it. My friends are texting me about last night’s party too, the one that I almost didn’t go to but ended up having a blast at. Everyone was there and it felt so good to be in the mix, laughing, dancing, just being around people who really get it, you know? But I get all these anxious thoughts in my head afterwards like did I say something weird or did I laugh too loudly? I can’t shake them off, I just keep replaying the whole night in my mind. I like being social but the anxiety that follows is exhausting. What if they think I’m annoying? I hope they don’t. Anyway, I’m trying to stick to my study schedule. It’s all about routines for me, or at least that’s what they say, right? I write down each hour of my day in my planner. It’s like I’ve gotta keep everything in check. If I don’t have a plan I feel like my brain will just explode. Plus, it helps me feel accomplished. I cross off tasks and it’s like this little burst of joy every time. But it can also be overwhelming trying to get everything done. I hate that feeling when you’re cramming for exams and all of a sudden it hits you that you forgot to study a whole chapter. Stress levels start rising, heart starts pounding, and then I’m just like, okay breathe. Deep breaths. It’s just school, right? But for me, school is everything. I have to keep my grades up. My parents are really supportive, but I know they have high expectations and I want to make them proud. So I guess that’s why I stress so much. If I don’t do well, I feel like I’m letting everyone down. But it’s not just my parents – it’s my friends too. I feel this pressure to be the best version of myself all the time, like they expect me to be the fun one, the life of the party, and sometimes I can’t keep up. I worry about letting them down if I don’t show up in a good mood. When I’m feeling low, I’d just rather be alone and recharge but then I think about how they’ll notice and maybe worry about me. I don’t want that. And then there’s those people in my classes who seem so carefree, they do whatever they want and just chill. I can’t relate. I have to plan everything. I remember this one time in a group project, I was the one making all the schedules and checking in on everyone, I mean someone has to keep things organized, right? But some of the others were like, “Oh, chill out, we’ll get it done.” It drives me nuts how they could just relax about it, like don’t they care? Are they going to pull their weight? I end up feeling resentful even though I know it’s not their fault. It’s just that my brain can’t handle that kind of uncertainty. Oh, and social media? I can’t even with all the comparisons. People posting their perfect lives while I’m sitting here worrying if I cleaned my apartment well enough before they come over. I have this ongoing battle with wanting to be more open to new experiences but the anxiety just holds me back. I see friends trying new restaurants or going to spontaneous trips and I’m like, cool, I’ll just be here with my meal prep and my three Instagram accounts. They always seem so effortlessly fun, so carefree. Sometimes I wish I could be like them, just dive into things without overthinking and worrying about every little detail. But then again, I really can’t imagine life without the routine, the organization. It’s comforting. I keep a checklist for everything. Even my workout schedule is mapped out. I have to know when I’ll go to the gym or I just won’t go at all. I think I’m realizing that maybe it’s okay to be this way. Not everyone needs to fit into this mold of being adventurous or spontaneous. I think I’ve got my own strengths, like being dependable and responsible. My friends always say they can count on me, which is nice, I like that. I mean, who doesn’t want to be the reliable one? Maybe I just need to find that balance, the middle ground where I can enjoy the social aspects but also hold on to my need for structure. It’s a work in progress, but hey, everyone’s figuring it out, right? Time to tackle that study schedule.

Okay so I just saw this flyer about a concert happening next weekend and it made me think of all the concerts I’ve been to and how electric the atmosphere is everyone just vibing together and singing like we’re best friends. Music is such a connector right? It brings all these different people together and suddenly you’re all one big happy family for a few hours. I remember that time I went to that outdoor festival last summer. The sun was about to set, and the sky was this perfect mix of orange and purple. I was with my friends, and we were dancing like nobody was watching, and it felt so freeing. I mean, just being surrounded by that energy, everyone laughing, cheering, it’s like why can’t every day feel like that? I don't even care if it was a little messy, people spilling drinks and tripping over each other, it just added to the fun. Anyway, I can’t wait to go to this concert. I wonder who will be there. I always get excited thinking about the potential to meet new people. I wonder if I’ll make some friends in line or even in the crowd, just chatting about music or finding common ground with some random stranger. I love that! It's so easy for me to strike up a conversation. Did I mention I once talked to a total stranger during a break at a seminar for like 45 minutes? By the end of it, we were planning to meet up for coffee. I really hope that friendship blossoms. It’s funny how people connect over the smallest things, like a shared interest in a band or that one song that gets stuck in your head. The park is another place I love. Just the thought of it makes me smile. You can see families picnicking, kids flying kites, dogs running around, and it feels alive. I could spend hours there just soaking in the atmosphere. I remember one time I just plopped down on the grass and started chatting with the person next to me. We ended up talking about everything from travel plans to favorite snacks. Do you know how satisfying it is to share your love for nachos with someone? It’s like instant friendship. I’ve met some of the coolest people in parks. I sometimes think maybe I should go there more often, but then again, I get distracted thinking about all the other places I could go. Oh, speaking of which, I still need to check out that new café on Main Street. I heard they have the best pastries. My roommate is always suggesting we try something new, and I’m all about that. I mean, what’s life without a little adventure? So we decided to sign up for a pottery class even though neither of us knows anything about pottery. It’ll be hilarious, and even if our pots end up looking like blobs, at least we’ll have the experience to laugh about. I can’t wait to see what we create. I just love the idea of trying things that push me out of my comfort zone. Like that time I signed up for a dance class on a whim. I stumbled through the steps, but I had the best time. We should all be a bit more spontaneous! I’ve always believed in being open to new experiences. Sometimes people worry too much about what might go wrong, but life is too short for that. I mean, sure, I might forget to study for an exam here and there, but that’s not the end of the world, right? I guess my family always taught me to take things easy. I love that about them. We’re all pretty laid back, and it’s nice to have that kind of environment. I can just be myself without feeling judged. That’s why I don’t get stressed out by mistakes. I mean, everyone makes them. We’re all human. I think embracing that is really important. I feel like it helps me connect with others too. Nobody likes a perfectionist anyway. Who even has time for that? But okay, getting sidetracked. I could go on about how I prefer spontaneity over structure for ages. I think there’s something about being open to the universe like standing on a cliff and just feeling like you could fly if you wanted to. Although I’m not one for extreme sports or anything, just the idea of it is thrilling. I’d rather be exploring a new city or venturing out to a cool art gallery. Art is such an incredible outlet – like, it shows feelings words can’t capture. Sometimes I just wander around galleries, talking to strangers about what the pieces make us feel. I guess I just love people. I love being around them, hearing their stories, sharing laughs. There’s always something new to discover about someone. I thrive on those interactions, those moments of connection, and it fills my heart. Each one adds to my collection of experiences, like little tokens of joy. And it’s funny, the more I collect, the more I realize how rich life is when you embrace it all.

Okay so I just remembered that time at the party last week when I started talking to that guy about philosophy and it turned into a debate about free will and determinism but like in a fun way. I think that’s what I love—getting into deep chats with people but also having a good laugh. There's this energy in these discussions, and I thrive on it. It was such a random party too, just a few friends decided to throw something together last minute, and I didn’t even plan to go, but I'm so glad I did. You never know where you’ll find interesting people, right? I’m always up for meeting someone new. It's wild how a single conversation can spark so much thought. I can’t stand small talk. I’d rather dive right in. Speaking of diving in, I really want to travel more. I need to plan a trip. My friends keep talking about going to Europe next summer. Seriously, the thought of wandering around Paris, or hanging out in Berlin, just makes my skin tingle. The energy there is so different. Everyone’s living their lives and there are so many stories to discover. I want to soak that up. Just being in a café where Picasso once sat or walking down streets where history was made—that’s where I feel alive. I like the idea of going to places that challenge my perception, too. Trying street food from a market in Bangkok or visiting a gallery in Amsterdam. The thought alone gives me butterflies. I want to experience cultures, not just see them in a textbook or a video. Oh, but that reminds me of that one time I tried cooking Thai food at home after watching too many YouTube videos. Total disaster. The kitchen was a mess, I think I used too much lemongrass or something. It was hilarious. I’m usually pretty competent in the kitchen, but that night? No idea what happened. But it was fun! Just experimenting and throwing things together. I think culinary adventures are like mini-trips without ever leaving home. My roommate thought I was going to burn the place down, and he kept joking about calling the fire department. It’s those moments that make living with him entertaining. We just balance each other. He’s so laid back, and I’m always buzzing with ideas. Sometimes I wish he’d join me on my escapades, but he’s more of a ‘watch Netflix and chill’ type. That’s cool, too! I’m also starting to think a lot about my career. I want to do something that allows me to express myself and innovate, maybe graphic design or marketing, something with a splash of creativity. I can't see myself stuck in an office all day, every day. I need variability. I guess that’s the openness in me pushing forward. I want to be part of projects that excite me. I want to wake up every day feeling like what I’m doing matters. That’s why I’ve been applying for internships that align with those values. I can’t imagine being in a job that doesn’t inspire me—I’d be bored out of my mind! Connecting with people in these industries is so important, too. I’m the person who sends LinkedIn requests to speakers at events just to chat, and honestly, I think that’s how you build a network. You never know who might become a mentor or offer opportunities down the line. I really believe in the power of relationships. It feels more genuine than just cold applications. Networking can be so daunting for some, but I actually enjoy meeting people. There’s something about sharing experiences that just fuels me. I’ve got this overflowing energy to engage with others. Can we talk about music? I love finding new artists. Spotify’s Discover Weekly is a game-changer. I stumbled upon this indie band last week, and now I can’t stop listening to them. Their sound is so unique and raw, it just draws me in. I find new genres that push the boundaries of what I thought I liked or could connect with. It’s like a never-ending exploration. When I hear a new song that resonates, I feel like that artist is speaking straight to my soul. That’s how I know I need to keep exploring. Music shapes so much of my life; it’s a soundtrack for every moment, from studying to just chilling with friends. And what about those spontaneous road trips? Driving with friends blasting music, stopping at weird roadside attractions, grabbing snacks. There’s a freedom in hitting the road without a strict itinerary, just figuring it out as you go along. Sometimes we make the best memories when we’re not trying too hard to plan. Just hanging out, being silly, and laughing way too much to care about where we end up. I think that’s what life should be—a series of adventures sprinkled with laughter and creativity. That’s how I want to live my life. Always exploring, always learning, and always open to the next big thing.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how everyone else seems to know how to act and say the right things and here I am second guessing every little interaction I have. It’s like I’m constantly flitting between this urge to be part of a conversation and this overwhelming desire to just disappear into the background. Like why can’t I just be loud and fun and not care? It’s not that I want to be alone but being around people feels like an endurance exercise sometimes. It’s exhausting to think about saying something clever or witty. What if I sound stupid? I’m not the best at small talk and I feel like everyone expects it. I drift through classrooms and hallways, my mind a swirl of ideas and what-ifs while I watch others easily engage with one another. And then there’s that constant inner dialogue that just never shuts up. Like, am I really good enough? Am I studying enough? I can’t tell if I’m doing the right things with my life. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop of self-doubt and overthinking. When I do manage to step out and talk to someone, I can feel that knot in my stomach tighten because, what if they don’t like me? I mean, I’m not the kind of person who just fits right in. Sometimes I wonder if that makes me unlikable and then I just spiral down that rabbit hole of inadequacy. It’s hard being in a world that seems to favor the outgoing and confident. And I think about my friends, or maybe I should say acquaintances, because the truth is, they are not really friends at all. There’s this distance that I can’t seem to cross, and I feel like I’m always teetering on the edge of connection but never really stepping over it. With them, I just get a bit irritated by the trivial things they say, like when they’re so preoccupied with the latest gossip or dating drama. It’s like, can we not talk about something meaningful? I want to dive deep, explore ideas, thoughts, possibilities. But they seem to want to skim the surface, and to be honest, it just makes the whole interaction feel shallow and pointless. And man, when it comes to planning or organizing things, I’m just not that kind of person. I mean, sure I can have a lot of ideas but getting them into action is a whole different game. My room is a mess and assignments are on the edge of not being done or even started sometimes, and it’s a chaotic blend of creativity and procrastination. I look at my friends who seem to have everything organized, their lives laid out like a neatly folded paper, and I just feel this tightness in my chest. It’s hard to ask for help, and somehow I feel that asking is a sign of failure. It’s annoying to see them handle their stress and get things done while I’m here struggling to put one foot in front of the other. And yet, I can’t help but enjoy the world around me. I read a lot, and the experience of diving into different ideas and philosophies is exhilarating. I feel more connected to the thoughts expressed on a page than in a conversation sometimes. It’s weird how I can be so open to ideas yet so closed off to people. Like, I crave exploration but only when it’s within the confines of my mind. I wonder if this is normal. Is it strange that I feel more at peace dissecting a book rather than human interactions? Would anyone even understand that? And then there are times when I’m caught off guard by a fleeting moment that I find beautiful and I’m hit with this overwhelming sense of appreciation for it. Like a flower growing through a crack in the sidewalk or the way the sun sets behind the buildings casting a warm glow that almost makes me forget all the chaos in my head for a second. Those moments make me feel alive but also isolated because I have no one to share them with. The thought of sharing it seems pointless because would anyone really get it, you know? I feel pressure to express these things verbally but I’m so much better at writing them down. I need the words to flow out without the filter of judgment. Navigating this whole existence is a daily challenge, and I often feel like I'm in a bubble. Totally open to the world’s offerings but too neurotic to engage with them fully. It’s like I’m living two lives—a creative one that loves to explore and one that’s constantly bracing itself for social confrontation that could lead to rejection. I think of those little quirks that make me who I am, and I wonder if they are strengths or if they are just making life heavier. But I guess being aware of it all is a start. Maybe one day I’ll find the balance between being open and brave enough to reach out.

I keep thinking about how people see me, like do they really even see me or just some version of me they’ve created in their heads based on whatever they think they know. I mean, I’m not a social butterfly. I wouldn’t even be a social caterpillar. I have this weird mix of feeling alone but also kind of content in it, like I can wander through my thoughts and reality without the noise of a million opinions pushing against me. Anyway, I was walking through the campus yesterday and noticed a bunch of students outside, laughing, and I felt that twitch in my stomach like I should be out there too joining in, but also, ugh, no. Just no. Ripe chaos, I don’t need that. But I do wonder about how they’re all so at ease, like they were born with this invisible manual that tells them how to connect with each other, while I’m just here fumbling through this social labyrinth with no exit in sight. I always end up saying the wrong thing or being too abrupt. Conversations tend to be awkward. I think about how every time I try to make small talk, I overthink it down to the last detail. What if they think I’m weird? Well, they probably do, but the irony is that I like weird. Weird is interesting. I would much rather be sitting in a corner reading one of my oddball fantasy novels than trying to fit into whatever mold society has for chatting about the weather. The weather is so boring though, speaking of. I read somewhere that people are scared of silence, and I’m like, why? Silence is a gift. It’s like a blanket, comfortable and warm. I’d take silence over forced conversation any day. And then I think, okay, maybe I’m going too deep into this silence thing, maybe it’s just a defense mechanism. Who cares? Honestly, I think that’s part of why I score all the low points on those personality tests. Like, I just want to explore ideas and question everything, but it always circles back to my anxieties. Like, what am I doing with my life? I’m in college, sure, but does anyone really have it figured out? I mean it’s dizzying how much information is out there. I’m open to everything – ideas, cultures, other people’s problems—but I can’t help but feel this overwhelming pressure to have an opinion, to sound smart, to contribute meaningfully. It’s exhausting. I want to question why we’ve built this life where everyone has to appear busy and fulfilled. Is it a performance? It feels like we’re all actors in some bizarre, nonsensical play where the script doesn’t even exist, yet we’re all expected to improvise flawlessly. Sometimes I think about my friends, and then I spiral into neurotic thoughts about whether they’re really my friends or just tolerating my quirks. They say they like me but do they really? Do I even like myself? I mean, I love my passions and interests, the weird stuff that piques my curiosity—museums, obscure films, odd historical facts—but when it comes to my day-to-day interactions, I’m just kind of a mess. It’s like there’s a chasm between my inner world and the outer world, and I can never quite bridge it. And then there’s this paradox of being open to experiences but also terrified of them. I mean, I want to try new things, but the prospect of potential failures looms large. I can imagine the embarrassment if I mess up in front of people, like suddenly I’m the center of attention, and all eyes are on me, and my brain just shuts down. It’s funny, in a way, how I crave adventure but also dread it. Sometimes, I curse myself for not doing enough with my life. I should be hiking mountains or traveling abroad or at least finding a side gig, but instead, I’m here pondering the meaning of existence in the quiet of my room. Also, what’s with all the emphasis on productivity anyway? We’re all running this endless race to become more accomplished and fulfilled, yet we seem to forget to enjoy the little everyday moments. Maybe I need to embrace my low conscientiousness more. I mean, who cares if my laundry is piling up or my dishes are in the sink? Does it really matter if I’m not the college success story? I think I’d rather be known for my thoughts. Those chaotic, random thoughts that make the world a little less ordinary. It’s such a rollercoaster, the thrill of letting my brain wander while also grappling with the insecurity of just not fitting the mold. Like, how do I balance wanting the deep conversations that go beyond the surface with the fear of losing myself in the noise? It’s exhausting to think about.

Why does it feel like I’m always doing the same thing? It’s like I’m stuck on a loop. I can’t even remember the last time I actually socialized beyond small talk. Just a quick nod to the classmate down the hall, a polite wave to the barista. Sometimes texting is easier. A text message has that nice buffer of time to think, to organize my thoughts without the pressure of face-to-face interaction. I don’t dislike people but the whole small talk thing? So draining. It's like a chore. Anyway, I’ve been thinking about starting a new project. Maybe a blog or something where I explore topics I find interesting. I’ve got this huge list of books I want to read, why not write about them? Film analysis could be cool too. There’s so much to dive into, so many different perspectives. I love dissecting those layers, finding out what makes a character tick or how a plot twist was foreshadowed. But planning can feel tedious sometimes—it’s tough to balance that with just jumping in. Earlier I found a really interesting article about the psychology of color in art. It’s always fascinating to me how colors can evoke emotions and thought patterns. Blue can be calming, yellow can feel cheerful or even overwhelming, depending on the context. I think about this with my own artwork. I’m not great, but I experiment with colors. There’s something about creating that feels freeing, like I’m giving myself permission to mess up and try again. I wonder if I’ll ever really perfect a style or if that’s just a myth everyone chases. Perfectionism can be suffocating. My journal is filled with half-finished sketches, half-baked ideas. Yet, each one holds some part of me. I could go back and finish them... but who am I doing that for, really? I want to travel. The world is so vast, so full of opportunities for discovery. Just imagining wandering through ancient ruins or exploring bustling markets makes my heart race. But then I remember the logistics—money, planning routes, dealing with people. It becomes a barrier that feels insurmountable sometimes. I can picture it all in my head—the rich history, the different cultures—but the thought of navigating it all? That’s where it gets complicated. And then there are the people. People everywhere. I’ve always been more comfortable taking it all in from the sidelines, observing rather than jumping into the fray. It’s a little lonely sometimes, but perhaps that’s just part of it. I love watching how people interact, how they communicate without words. The subtle body language, the unspoken tension. It’s fascinating. But real friendships? They take effort, and I wonder if my low agreeableness is a hindrance. Do I push people away without realizing it? I’m not rude or anything, I just don’t feel the same impulse to connect, to share every little thought. It feels like a dance I never learned the steps to. And then there’s my courses. I thrive in them. The workload is heavy but manageable. Assignments don’t faze me, I break them down into tiny tasks and tackle them with my trusty planner. The sense of organization, the satisfaction of crossing things off a list—it's oddly fulfilling. I wonder if that’s common, this intrinsic need to tick boxes and quantify progress. It’s almost like a game, a self-imposed challenge. I really should get a hobby outside of schoolwork though; sometimes I get too absorbed in it. It’s 3 PM already? Where did the time go? I was supposed to call Mom earlier. How do I even start that conversation? She always asks about my social life and what friends I’ve made. It’s awkward, telling her that my idea of a perfect Saturday is cozying up with a new book and some snacks. I know she means well, but it feels like I’m letting her down every time. Yet, isn’t it my life? Why should I care? But maybe I do care. Maybe I want to meet her expectations even when I don’t quite fit the mold. Speaking of expectations, I’ve been thinking about graduate school too. The idea of pushing myself, furthering my knowledge, it’s thrilling. But the thought of the applications, the potential rejections? That’s a different story. Is it too much to want to find something I truly love doing? Something that combines all my interests? It feels like a lofty goal, but in that uncertainty, there’s a spark. Perhaps the answer lies in staying curious, in letting my openness guide me through this maze of possibilities.

okay so I’ve been thinking about how I always notice the little things like a leaf falling from a tree or the way the light hits the pavement after it rains it's almost meditative to me I guess it’s why I like taking walks alone or maybe that’s just because I don't mind being alone like really alone it’s kind of peaceful to just observe the world without feeling the need to interact I often wonder if other people feel that way or if they’re just looking for the next social event or something I hear people say they get bored when they’re alone but I find it fascinating to think about all the things I could do like reading that book I’ve been meaning to get to or writing in my journal or sketching things like that butterfly I saw last summer its wings were so vibrant I almost wish I could capture that moment forever but then again memories are kind of like snapshots in your mind not perfect but still beautiful I think about how easy it is to overlook the beauty in simple moments just because everyone is rushing around it makes me smile when I see someone stop to appreciate something small like a sunset or a child laughing those moments feel genuine like they bring out the goodness in people every time I hear laughter it’s like a reminder that life can be lighthearted and fun but there’s a balance because sometimes I feel like maybe I care too much about others like I put everyone else's needs before my own which is a little exhausting but at the same time it's rewarding I think the world needs more kindness like genuine kindness and not just the act of being nice for the sake of it but real empathy it’s fascinating how quickly a simple smile or a small gesture can shift someone's day I remember this one time at the grocery store an older lady smiled at me when I held the door open for her and it made my day I wonder if she knew how much it meant to me or if she was just being polite everyone gets caught up in their own lives and forgets that small acts can create ripples it’s like we’re all connected in this giant web of experiences emotions and kindness if only everyone could realize that life is less about competition and more about lifting each other up but then again people have their reasons for being defensive or guarded I think it comes from fear maybe fear of rejection or fear of being vulnerable and I get that it’s hard to be open with others when you’re worried about getting hurt but in those moments when I allow myself to be vulnerable and share my thoughts I feel so free like I’m shedding a layer of skin that no longer serves a purpose how do you find that balance between being true to yourself and also being mindful of others' feelings it’s a delicate dance and I think being conscientious helps me navigate it but I still struggle sometimes with the pressure to meet others' expectations or be the perfect friend or student or whatever it is I want to be real and authentic yet I also want to be considerate I guess that’s just part of being human and maybe everyone feels it in their own way I’ve been thinking about how to cultivate deeper connections with people but also retain that sense of self I wonder if I put too much pressure on myself to maintain friendships and if it’s okay to take a step back every now and then because I need space to recharge I think that’s a real thing it’s like social batteries they need recharging and sometimes all I want is to curl up with a good book and a cup of tea and just lose myself in a different world but then the guilt creeps in like am I abandoning my friends or neglecting them and I don’t want to come off as standoffish or unapproachable sometimes I think about reaching out and organizing a meet up but then the thought of planning makes me anxious and I wonder if I’m overthinking everything it happens a lot I find that I can mentally spiral in situations that seem trivial but then there are moments of clarity when I can step back and assess what’s really important and I think it’s crucial to keep the lines of communication open with friends like being honest about needing that space but also showing them that I care I guess it’s all about balance and that’s what makes life intricate one moment I’m feeling inspired to create and connect and the next I’m just wanting solitude it’s all part of the ebb and flow of existence and I have to remind myself that it’s okay to feel both ways I hope that my friends can understand that about me because at the end of the day it’s all about understanding and compassion towards one another and I find comfort in knowing that even when I’m quiet or withdrawn I still value those relationships deeply even if it doesn’t always look like I’m actively participating sometimes just being present is enough and I wonder if that’s what makes life beautiful to begin with

Okay, so the clock says 10:10. I should probably be studying or something but I just don’t feel like it. I just can’t get into it today, too much noise outside, I’d rather scroll through my phone or play that game I’m hooked on, the one where you build cities and then have them fall apart because people are never happy. It’s funny how real life mirrors that. I mean, why do people have to be so needy and complicated? Can’t we just get things done and move on? I get it, emotions and all that, but sometimes it’s just exhausting. I can’t even deal with small talk; it’s the worst. Like when someone asks me, “How’s your day?” and I just wanna say, “It’s fine, leave me alone.” So much easier, right? Food. I should probably eat something, but what? I have those weird frozen meals in the fridge that I bought on sale. I mean, they look okay, but the texture is always off. Plus, do I really want to microwave something that looks like it was made ten years ago? Gross. Can’t believe I let myself run low on snacks again. I guess I just don’t plan for that kind of stuff. Why is meal prep such a huge deal for people? My friends are always talking about their meal prep Sundays, but I can’t be bothered with that. Just give me something I can eat without thinking too much about it. I really don’t get social media sometimes. I mean, people post their entire life stories, and it’s like, who cares? I scroll through, and it’s just people showing off their food, their vacations, their perfect smiles. They’re all just asking for validation. I don’t want to be part of that. Just feels like such a facade. Why do they need so many followers? What’s the point of that? I’d rather have three genuine conversations a week than deal with endless comments and likes. What’s next? The weather? It’s cloudy again, surprised it hasn’t started raining. Can’t complain though, I prefer this over a heatwave. I mean, I’d take chilly over sweltering any day. Why do people complain about the cold? Just wear layers; it’s not that hard. But then again, I guess I’m just not into the whole “fun in the sun” thing, anyway. Beach trips are overrated. Sand gets everywhere, and who actually enjoys sunburn? Not me, thanks. My roommate talks about how she wants to travel after graduation. I just don’t see the appeal. Like, do I really want to sit on a plane for hours just to see a landmark? What’s the big deal about traveling? I mean, I could just look it up online and it’s basically the same experience, right? People act like going to a different country is some kind of life-changing experience, but it sounds exhausting. The planning, the packing, the worrying about money. Nah. I’m fine with my little corner of the world. My phone buzzes. Probably a group chat about weekend plans, something I’m totally not interested in. I avoid the notifications. I can’t stand the logistics of organizing something; it’s always someone wanting one place, then someone else wants another place, and I’m just like how about we do nothing instead? That sounds nice. But people love their plans, their schedules. I like knowing what’s next but only in my own dull, organized way—not the chaotic, spontaneous types who want to go out and socialize. Sometimes I think about what would happen if I just didn’t show up to things. Would anyone even notice? Probably not. I mean, life goes on, right? People fill those gaps with other people, friends who are more engaging or whatever. My parents always say I should “join clubs” or “meet new people,” but I just don’t see the value in that. I keep it simple. There’s a comfort in being alone. So, what’s the rush to connect? Why should I have to? I look around the room, clothes piled up in the corner, an empty pizza box on the floor from two days ago. Didn’t feel like cleaning it right away. It’s just... there. I’ll get to it, maybe. Nothing feels pressing. I guess I should probably care more about the mess. But honestly, I find it kind of funny. Like, my life is messy and unstructured—not everything has to be clean and perfect. That’s just a lot of pressure. Am I allowed to be okay with that? I think about how I should probably get some sleep soon; I need to be awake for class tomorrow. But what if I don’t? What if I just pull an all-nighter and wing it? That would be fun, right? Probably not, better not.

The clock says 3:15 pm and I’m thinking about how I should probably be studying for that exam next week but honestly who cares when I could just go hang out with friends and do something fun instead. Like, I know I should be responsible but why when the sun is shining outside and everyone is going to the park? I could just text Mark and see if he wants to play frisbee. That sounds way better than reading that boring textbook about psychology or whatever. Ugh, textbooks are the worst, all those words and so many pages. My brain just shuts off halfway through, it's like finding the off switch. I was talking to Jessie last night about that concert coming up next month. I can’t wait! I don’t even care who’s playing. Just give me loud music and a crowd, that’s all I need. I might wear that shirt I got from the thrift store. It's bright and colorful, something that stands out. I think people notice me more when I wear cool stuff. It’s a confidence booster, for sure. Okay, so speaking of confidence, I was at the last party and everyone was just chilling in the living room, but I was like, let’s take this outside! So we ended up playing beer pong under the stars, and I totally crushed it. It felt amazing to win, even though I was pretty tipsy. Parties are the best, the energy just gets me going. People laughing, music blasting, everyone just letting loose. I can’t help but want to be in the middle of it all, talking to everyone, making jokes. Then there's that guy from my math class, he asked me for help with some problems the other day, and I said sure, but honestly, I didn’t really want to help him. But whatever, I guess I didn’t say no outright, just tried to steer the conversation away. I mean, math is just numbers, who actually wants to learn that? Not me. I’ve got better things to do. I don’t get how some people can do math homework and get all into it. Like, what’s the point? Just vibe with life instead. I’d rather be out playing basketball or doing anything that gets my adrenaline pumping. I should call my brother later too. He’s always got crazy stories about the wild stuff he does, and I love hearing about it. Last time he told me about this road trip with his friends and how they got lost in Vegas. I can’t even imagine being in a place like that with all those people. I feel like I’d be overwhelmed but he just loves it. He thrives in chaos. I get that energy from him. It’s like I feed off other people’s excitement. Like when I see someone smile or laugh, it makes me happy just being around them. Back to that math guy, he really annoyed me when he kept asking questions. I’m not a tutor, dude. Just figure it out! But maybe I should’ve been nicer. I mean, I wouldn’t want someone to be rude to me, right? But I also don’t want to waste my time with stuff like that. I guess I’m not the best at being sensitive. I like it when people are direct and say what they want. Oh, I need to remember to grab snacks later. Chips or something. I live for snacks. And I know pizza is just around the corner. I can order it and have it delivered right to my place, that’s the life! Eating pizza while watching some ridiculous movie or whatever. That sounds like a perfect evening. I mean, who actually needs plans? Sometimes it’s just nice to chill and do nothing. Ha, speaking of nothing, I really need to clean my room. It’s a total disaster. Clothes everywhere. What even happened? It’s like a tornado hit. I don’t even remember the last time I put stuff away properly. But whatever, it’s not like anyone is going to judge me. People see me as this fun, carefree person anyway. That’s the vibe I give off, I guess. I’d rather be the person who’s spontaneous than one who’s all uptight about cleaning. Okay, back to the park plan. Frisbee sounds about right. I bet the weather’s nice enough for that. Plus, it means getting out of my room, which is definitely a win. I hope everyone’s free. Mark always has something going on, and I know Jessie will want to come along too. It’s gonna be a good time. Like seriously, why waste time inside when there’s a whole world out here waiting for us? Let’s just live in the moment, right? That’s what it’s all about!

I keep thinking about how everyone else seems to be in their own world and I’m just kind of floating around, you know? Like, it’s not that I don’t want to be social or anything, it’s just that the thought of making small talk makes my stomach feel twisted. What’s the point of talking about the weather or that show everyone is binge-watching? I mean, I guess it’s nice to know everyone is on the same page but it feels so superficial and I feel like I can’t get through it without having a little anxiety bubble in my throat. And then I start worrying that if I don’t say something interesting, everyone will judge me or just lose interest. But if I say something wrong, then that’s embarrassing too. So I just end up nodding a lot, which is a great strategy because it keeps me out of trouble but then I end up feeling even more isolated, like I’m not really part of it. Last week, I was in this group study session and I could feel my heart racing every time someone asked me a question, like they were looking right at me waiting for me to come up with something brilliant when all I could think about was how I’d rather be anywhere else. Everyone else was talking over each other, sharing their opinions and ideas, and I just sat there trying to remember the last time I said something worth saying, but my brain just froze. I hate how my mind goes blank when I need it to work and I start thinking maybe I’m not cut out for this. Maybe I should just be alone all the time. I mean, I like being alone, but then I end up overwhelmed by my own thoughts, which is such a whirlwind, like an emotional rollercoaster I didn’t sign up for. It’s wild how much I worry about what people think of me. I mean, I want to be liked. I want to be the person everyone thinks is nice and approachable. But then I think, who’s going to actually be friends with me when I can’t even keep up with what’s happening in their lives? It’s exhausting. My phone has messages piling up, and I just can’t bring myself to respond. I want to but then I just get trapped in this cycle of overthinking—what should I say? Will it be awkward? So I just stare at my screen and let the anxiety build until I close the app again like I’m putting something dangerous away. I’ve noticed that I’m easily overwhelmed by even the simplest things. Like grocery shopping? Ugh, don’t even get me started. It’s not even that big of a deal, but I get so distracted by all the choices. Do I want the gluten-free brand or the organic one? Should I just stick with what I know? Well, I don’t really know what I like anyway. I get to the store and get all flustered and then I can’t even remember what I came there for, and I just hope no one is watching me as I traverse the aisles like a lost puppy. I once spent over an hour just wandering around trying to decide on a snack because I felt pressured to make the right choice. By the time I left, I didn’t even want the snack anymore. I keep thinking about how my friends invite me to things, and I always say yes because I don’t want to let them down, but as the date comes closer, I start to dread it. I can already feel the pressure of being in a room full of people and having to interact, feeling that knot in my stomach. I feel like a burden sometimes because I can be so anxious. I wish I could just break out of my shell, but then I worry if I do, will I still be the same person they like? Is it worth it to try? Today, I think I’ll just stay in my room and maybe binge-watch something boring. I can always say I’m doing something productive like studying or whatever. That feels safer. Sometimes I think about trying to change, like being more open to new experiences or whatever, but honestly, it’s just hard. What if I try and fail? Then I’ll just feel even worse. It’s easier to just be in my shell where I already know all the quirks and shadows and where I fit into everything. Why do I put so much pressure on myself? I think I want to be better. I really do. But every little step feels like climbing a mountain, and I’m at the bottom just staring up and feeling paralyzed. It’s frustrating, like I can see the summit, but the idea of getting there is too overwhelming. Is there a guidebook on how to be less anxious or more social? I don’t know. I just hope someday I can find a balance.

Okay so I just saw this ad for that new video game that’s coming out. It's like, every time I get excited about a game, I always think of how it feels to jump into that world and interact with people online and how cool it is to meet strangers. I mean, it’s weird in a way, like I can be super social, but I can also be a bit of a lone wolf sometimes. How does that work? Anyway, the last game I got really into was that RPG with the dragons, and I literally spent all weekend on it. I should probably get out more instead of sitting at home, but honestly, who has the time when there are quests to complete? Maybe I can convince some friends to join in next time. Multiplayer is the best. Speaking of friends, I was just at this party last week, and it was such a blast! Everyone was dancing, and I found myself in a ridiculously funny conversation about pineapple on pizza. Some people are so passionate about it! Like, why do they care so much? It's just food! I mean, I love it, and I don’t care who knows. But then again, the whole debate felt like a metaphor for life—everyone’s got their opinions, you know? And it’s all noise at the end of the day. And then there was this guy at the party, super chill but also intense, talking about his travels. He went to Thailand and Vietnam, and I was just sitting there like, “Whoa, can I borrow your passport?” I want to go everywhere. The world is so big, and there is so much to see. My bucket list is ridiculous. I want to sky dive in New Zealand, explore the markets in Marrakech, eat street food in Bangkok. Just thinking about it makes my heart race. I love the idea of being in a new place, trying new things, meeting new people. The other day I walked into this thrift store, and it's like a treasure hunt every time. I found this cool vintage jacket that looks like it came straight out of the 80s. I can’t help but think about the stories behind every piece of clothing, who wore it, what they did in it. It’s kinda haunting but fascinating. I love mixing styles, taking risks with fashion, just throwing things together that maybe don’t match but somehow do. Life is too short to wear boring clothes. I honestly don’t get how people stick to one aesthetic. And I wonder about people's routines. Like, do they wake up and follow the same boring schedule over and over? I mean, that sounds exhausting. I like spontaneity. I get an idea, and I just go for it. My friends call me unpredictable, but isn’t that just being alive? I can’t stand staying stagnant. Like, yesterday I just decided to hop on a bus and go to the beach. It was chilly, but the ocean is just… it calls to me. Those waves crashing, the salty air—it clears my mind. Back to food for a sec, because what’s life without food? I tried to cook this new recipe last week, and wow, the kitchen looked like a disaster zone. It was supposed to be this fancy pasta dish, but I ended up with something that looked like a science experiment gone wrong. I always think I can wing it in the kitchen, but let’s be real: I can barely boil water without burning it. Still, I like the thrill of trying, even if I end up with takeout more often than I’d like to admit. At least I’m supporting local businesses. And there’s music, oh man, I can’t get enough of it. I was jamming out to some indie band yesterday while cleaning my room, and I realized that my taste is all over the place. One minute I’m vibing to some chill acoustic stuff, and the next, I’m belting out pop bangers like nobody’s business. It’s like every song transports me, you know? I can totally lose track of time, and then suddenly I’m late for class or meeting a friend. But the rush of it is fantastic. I thrive on that energy, the moment when a song hits just right, and the world feels alive. So wait, where was I? Oh right, I need to figure out a new plan for the weekend. Maybe a road trip or a concert. I could drive up the coast with some friends, blast the music, and just go wherever the road takes us. Who needs plans? I want freedom! Life should be an adventure every day, and I need to dive into it headfirst.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how I really enjoy meeting people. Like, every time I go into a room or a gathering, my brain's just buzzing with excitement and I can't wait to get to know everyone. Yesterday at that event, I met this dude who was super into rock climbing and he had the best stories. I mean, who knew climbing could be so dramatic? One story about rescue missions and crazy climbs just made me think about how many different lives people lead, you never know what someone’s been through until you ask. I feel like asking people questions is my superpower. I can get anyone talking and it makes me happy seeing them light up about their passions. I wonder if there’s a way to make a career out of just being really curious about what makes people tick. Like a professional converser or something. And all this talking makes me think about how, even when I'm at a gathering, I can still have a sense of responsibility, like keeping the energy high and making sure everyone feels included. I can't stand it when someone’s sitting alone. It just feels wrong, you know? So I’m like, “Hey! Come join us!” It’s like my internal hostess kicks in and I want to create this perfect atmosphere where everyone’s having a blast. I've got that checklist in my head, making sure the food's stocked, the music is right, and everyone is mingling. I actually enjoy planning events, too, like I threw a party last month and it had the best vibe. Everyone was mingling, great snacks, and I even set up a little games corner. People were laughing and it just feeds into this wave of happiness. I think that’s what drives me to be conscientious – making sure things go smoothly and everyone feels good. Then there are days where I really think about how we’re all in this together. I mean, the world can feel so divided sometimes. People just want to be understood and I get that. It’s like I can sense the collective mood when I walk into a space. If someone seems off, I can't help but try to check in or lighten the mood. Maybe it's because I score low on neuroticism, so I can stay calm even when things get a little crazy. Like last week, a friend was having a rough day, and instead of letting it drag the whole vibe down, I threw in some spontaneous karaoke. We ended up belting out our favorite songs and I could see the stress melting away. It’s things like that that remind me how powerful positivity can be and how it often starts with one person deciding to change the energy. The other day, I was talking to a friend about how I really value structure and routine. I mean, I love it when things are organized. I can’t handle clutter or chaos for too long, it just makes my brain feel all jumbled. I stick to my planner like glue. If something pops up, I have to rearrange but I get a little stressed if it conflicts with existing plans. I guess that’s why I’m on top of deadlines and schoolwork, because being conscientious drives me to plan ahead. I wouldn’t want to leave things to the last minute and risk disappointing others, you know? I have a project coming up and I’m already brainstorming ideas and setting timelines. I just feel more comfortable when I know what’s next and when I can be there for others, whether it’s helping them with studies or planning a group outing. It’s funny how much I find myself gravitating towards familiar faces too. I don’t really seek out new adventures outside my comfort zone. A bit low on that openness, I guess. I prefer sticking to what I know and enjoy. Familiar coffee shops, favorite hangouts, and people. It’s kind of like a safety net. It makes me happy knowing I have solid, reliable friendships that I nurture. I don’t jump into new friendships that easily, I like to build a strong foundation first. And while some people crave change or novelty, I’m just over here promoting the “loyalty is key” agenda. But, you know, sometimes I wonder if it would be fun to try something completely out of character. Like, what if I went skydiving? I feel like that rush would be exhilarating but part of me is like, “Why would you do that?” But then again, I can’t deny it’d be a great story to share at the next gathering. Ah, the balance of comfort and adventure is tricky. Just seeing all the smiling faces around me and sharing these moments of joy feels so fulfilling.

my phone just buzzed again—another notification probably but I don’t want to check it. I should probably check it. I mean it could be important but what if it’s just a reminder for that group project I really don’t wanna do? why do I keep getting stuck in these group projects it’s like I can’t escape them. I hate the pressure, people relying on me, even though it’s always the same two people doing all the work and I’m just here like can I please just sit in the corner and hope someone forgets I’m in this? makes me anxious just thinking about it. sometimes I wish I could just be one of those people who doesn’t care, you know? like my roommate, she just laughs and shrugs it off when things go wrong. I’m always thinking what if, what if, what if. what if I failed the exam last week, what if I get a bad grade, what if I ruin everything for everyone? I guess it’s always easier to focus on the negative. sometimes I think I’m too nice. I mean, I say yes to things I shouldn’t just because I don’t want to upset anyone but then I end up feeling overwhelmed. it’s like I’m so agreeable that it turns into a problem. this morning I told myself I wouldn’t help Sarah with her paper. but then I saw her looking all stressed and I thought, okay, okay I’ll just read it and give feedback, but then you know how that goes, it turned into a two-hour session of her reading every line and me nodding my head like I’m offering something worthwhile. I really hope I don’t end up writing her conclusion for her. I’m too tired for that. it’s raining outside. I can hear the drops hitting the window. why am I always so tired? is it the weather? probably just the classic college student struggle of not sleeping enough while trying to balance everything. I need to work out because that always makes me feel better. but then I see the gym, the people there, and I just want to turn around and go home. how do people actually make friends there? I can’t even talk to someone at the vending machine without feeling my heart race. it’s a simple conversation, why can’t I just ask if they know how the machines work? what if they think I’m stupid for not knowing? okay so sometimes I think about what kind of job I want after all this. but then I get hit with the realization that I have no idea. something simple, something where I won’t have to deal with too many people. is that selfish? my parents always say I need to be more ambitious, aim higher but I can’t handle the idea of high-pressure roles and constant interactions and deadlines—ugh deadlines, they hang over my head like a dark cloud. what if I just ended up working at a library? that sounds nice. quiet. but then the thought of having to deal with patrons who ask questions makes my stomach churn. why is it so hard to just be an adult? I see everyone else moving on, socializing like it’s second nature. I can’t even fake it. I just want to blend into the background and observe everything but if I do that too much, I start feeling like I’m missing out, but at the same time, if I join it’s all too much. it’s like a trap. I remember going to a party last month. I told myself I would go for just an hour but once I got there, the noise was overwhelming. people everywhere, laughter, shouting, I felt like I couldn’t breathe. I ended up hiding in the bathroom just to catch my breath. that was embarrassing but at least I didn’t cry. okay, time for another deep breathe. it’s silly how often I have to tell myself to breathe. sometimes I think it would be easier to just stay home and watch Netflix forever. but I want to be better—better at being social, better at handling stress, better at just being in the moment instead of worrying about every little thing. I just keep getting anxious that I’ll say the wrong thing or someone will notice I’m nervous and then I spiral. I hate that I can’t just relax. I should probably text my friend but what do I even say? hey wanna watch a movie and not talk about our problems? or I could do the classic “how’s it going?” but even that feels like a trap. what if they’re having a great day and I’m just bringing my gloomy self into their world? I don’t want to be a burden. it’s hard to remember that everyone has their own stuff going on too. maybe I should just go for a walk instead. the rain looks nice from the window. that could be calming, but then I’d have to put on shoes and that’s already too much effort. I don’t know what I’m doing today.

I'm thinking about how the sun is out today and I really should be outside instead of stuck in here typing. It feels like such a waste of a day. My friend texted me this morning asking if I wanted to hang out later and I said yes without even checking my schedule. I mean, what’s the point of studying all day when you can just be out having fun right? I hope they want to go to that new ice cream place. The one with all the wild flavors like lavender and spicy chocolate. Super weird but also intriguing. Totally not my usual flavor choices but why not? I love when everyone is together, the noise, the laughter, and just the energy of being around people. It’s like an instant boost. I was at that game night last week and it was just chaos but the good kind—you know? People arguing over card games, someone getting overly competitive about Monopoly. It’s hilarious how into it everyone gets. I got way too carried away with my team, shouting and high-fiving. I lost track of time and didn’t even realize I had an assignment due the next day. Not that it matters, I usually wing it, and somehow it works out. I mean, I could probably be super organized if I wanted to but why bother when I can just enjoy life on the fly? Speaking of assignments, I should probably check my grades sometime soon but honestly, it’s a bit daunting. I know I’m not going to see anything too bad but I just have this thing about avoiding things that require focus. I’d rather plan a spontaneous trip to the beach with friends instead of worrying about research papers. The idea of sunbathing and playing beach volleyball sounds so much more appealing right now. Also, my roommate has this weird habit of hoarding snacks. Like, how do you even eat that much popcorn? I’m more of a chips and salsa kind of person, but she just piles up these bags. Whenever she’s not around, I tend to sneak in and grab a handful. I feel a bit guilty but then I remember she doesn’t seem to care much. It’s like we have this unspoken agreement: I’ll eat your snacks if you don’t ask too many questions. I really don’t know how some people are so serious about their diets and workout regimes. Who has the time? I think exercise is great and all but I’d rather have a burger with friends than hit the gym for hours alone. We’ve got to enjoy life, right? I can think of a hundred places to grab food but only a couple that have a decent gym. That’s the real question—where’s the nearest taco stand? Remember that one time we went to that taco truck right after finals? It was so liberating to just eat whatever without thinking about school. And I’m not going to lie, we probably ate way too many but who cares! We laughed so hard and made a complete mess everywhere. I wonder if that guy ever got his phone back from the fountain. Okay, I really need to get back on track. I should probably do some readings tonight or finish that one class I’ve been neglecting. It’s all about balance, right? Also, why does everyone keep saying you need to be ‘well-rounded’? I mean, I just want to be good at the things I enjoy. Why try to improve stuff you’re just not into? I’d rather be the best at throwing a party than sit in a lecture making notes that I’m definitely not going to use in the future. Time is precious! What’s it going to be like after graduation? That thought is creeping in. I’m not ready for that. I just want to keep the fun going. Maybe I’ll find a job that lets me socialize all day, like an event planner or something. Who knows, maybe I’ll start my own little business, make it all about community events. That sounds perfect. I can invite everyone I know and just create this amazing atmosphere. Wait, did I just think about actual responsibility? No way, let’s put that aside for now. I’m young; I need to make memories and experience life! Who cares about the future? Speaking of memories, I need to get some photos from last weekend. The pictures from the party we went to are the best; everyone looks so happy and carefree. I can always count on good friends to lift my mood. They’re all amazing, just how everyone meshes together. It’s goes to show how when you put a bunch of fun people in one place, magic happens. Alright, enough of this rambling. Time to plan that ice cream trip!

okay so I've been thinking about how crowded my room feels sometimes but I know it's just the piles of clothes and books and it always feels like such a mess but then I just close the door and pretend it's not there or maybe I don't pretend it's there it's just easier to ignore it than to deal with it but then I think I should deal with it and feel guilty like oh man I really should clean up but then cleaning sounds like so much effort and when I clean I just get distracted like I start cleaning and then find a random picture and get lost in memories and then I look at the clock and realize three hours have passed and I haven't done anything so what was the point of that? but the memories are nice and then I get anxious about time and I feel like everyone else is doing something worthwhile and I’m just here folding laundry or scrolling through my phone wasting time it's like I can feel that weight of expectation and I can't even live up to it but I try to be okay with it like oh it's fine it's just me but then I worry what people think and I can see their faces in my mind as I think about all the times I let them down or didn’t respond quickly enough to texts and I know that I should be more reliable because people depend on me in some way but it all feels too much so I avoid making plans too often because I'm scared of letting people down but then I feel lonely which is a whole other thing it's like a spiral that never ends and what do I even like to do for fun? I can't remember the last time I genuinely enjoyed something I think the last time I laughed was watching that dumb movie with the group but I didn’t even want to go but they all insisted and I felt bad saying no so I went and it was fine I guess but I didn’t participate in the jokes much because I was worried I wouldn’t be funny or they’d think I was awkward but then I just sat there and laughed at their jokes but also felt a little sad because I was on the outside kinda like looking in and it’s been like that for a while doesn't help that I see people post on social media having so much fun and I feel like I'm not part of it because I don’t know how to reach out sometimes it feels like all these walls and I feel so comfortable in my little bubble but I know I shouldn't be in it so much but leaving it is hard so then I think about what I could say if I do reach out and it feels heavy like weight lifting and I just don't know if it’s worth the hassle sometimes I just want someone to text me first but then I worry about being annoying if I do it too much but what if they don’t want to talk to me but I crave that connection too but it’s just so confusing and exhausting sometimes and then I feel like crying but I don’t want to because it’s embarrassing and then my mind just spirals more and I think about everything like what happened last week I forgot to reply to that one message and now it feels too late but I should have replied sooner but if I reach out now they might think I’m weird for taking so long or am I overthinking this? It’s so hard to keep track of it all I feel like my brain is a jumbled mess of self-doubt and worry and then I remember all the things I have to do like that paper and the group project that’s looming and I can’t concentrate on anything because I’m stuck in this spiral of thoughts that don’t even make sense half the time and then I think about just dropping out but then I think about what my parents would say but then I also feel like they don’t get it and I can’t really explain how I'm feeling without sounding dramatic but it all just feels heavy and I want to escape but I don’t know where to escape to and just thinking about having to get up to do things makes me feel tired like all I want to do is curl up in a ball and sleep forever but then I feel guilty for wanting to sleep too much because there are people out there who are doing important things like saving lives or changing the world and I can’t even send a text or finish a simple task but I'm not even sure what changing the world looks like and what would that even mean for me? I could volunteer but then that feels stressful because what if I'm not good at it and let them down and I can't deal with those thoughts so I push them away but they come back and it’s like I'm on a merry-go-round that I can’t get off and my heart feels tight and I just want to breathe but then I think about how I should be better like everyone else but I’m just stuck feeling like this and it’s hard not to think about how the future looks bleak sometimes and I don’t want to think about it and just want to stay in this moment but I know I can’t and that’s what stresses me out so yeah I guess I’m just here in this whirlwind of thoughts.

okay so I’m just sitting here and I can’t stop thinking about how the world feels so chaotic like everywhere I look there’s something new to worry about the news is always blaring something alarming and I think about all the people who are just living their lives like they don’t see it or maybe they do see it but they just don’t care is that a way to live though I mean how can you not care when everything seems so heavy like the weight of it all just piles on and on and I wonder if I’m the only one who feels it I don’t like big crowds or small talk it all feels pointless sometimes and honestly I’d much rather be lost in my thoughts wandering through a gallery of ideas rather than interacting with people who seem to float by on the surface it’s exhausting when I try to connect but the connection is always like a thin thread that might break any second I remember this one time at a party where I just stood in the corner and watched everyone dance and laugh and make small talk and I felt like an outsider like a ghost I wanted to join in but I also felt this suffocating pressure to be someone I’m not or to fit a mold that I didn’t ask for and then I think about social media and how it’s all just a curated performance everyone’s showing their highlights like that’s real life but it isn’t right it feels like everyone else is having fun in a way that I can’t quite get into I wonder if they’re as anxious as I am do they ever feel like they don’t belong like there’s a wall between us and I can’t break through it I hate how I worry about what others think of me I guess that’s just the neurotic side creeping in but still it’s hard when you overthink every little thing like sending a text and then staring at the screen waiting for a reply hoping it won’t be awkward but what if it is and I just don’t know how to handle it so I avoid situations that might make me feel worse I think about my friends and how some of them seem so carefree and I wonder if they realize how fast time is passing like do they ever stop to think about how fragile everything is it’s like this ticking clock in my head reminding me that life is short but when you’re not happy it feels even shorter everything is just fleeting and I get tired of pretending I’m okay but I don’t want to burden anyone with my feelings either so I sit in silence my own thoughts swirling around in a tornado of anxiety and hope like wanting to embrace the world but being afraid it’ll just reject me or laugh in my face I want to explore but I’m paralyzed by the fear of failure or judgment and then I think about art like the way it pulls me in like a warm hug I love how creative people can express the chaos I feel inside like when I hear music that resonates with me it feels cathartic the way a painter splashes colors onto canvas I want to do that but I get scared that it won’t be good enough or that no one will understand what I’m trying to say but I know I have things to say like there’s this entire universe in my head waiting to burst forth and yet it gets stuck sometimes like ideas trapped behind a glass wall that I can see through but can’t reach out and touch I’ll read something that speaks to my soul and I want to share that energy with others but then I see their eyes glaze over and it’s this reminder that maybe I see the world differently maybe I’m too intense or too deep for the average conversation but that’s okay isn’t it it’s okay to be a little messy a little off the beaten path at least I still feel things so deeply it’s like life is a canvas and I’m just a splash of paint that doesn’t quite fit anywhere but I’m here and I exist in this chaotic blend and I want to make something beautiful out of it all or at least something raw that doesn’t shy away from the dark parts I think about how I wish I could travel like exploring new places but even that holds daunting fears what if I get lost or what if I can’t communicate with others and I get trapped in my head instead of enjoying the experience and I see pictures of places I long to go but the thought of actually doing it gives me this pit in my stomach but see here’s where that openness comes in to play because I still want to see those things feel those things even though I might not be comfortable a part of me wants to dive headfirst into the unknown to challenge the anxieties even if it terrifies me because I believe there’s beauty in the struggle and I know that the world is vast and filled with colors I just wish I could reach out and touch it without the weight of everything pulling me under so for now I’ll keep dreaming and thinking and maybe one day I’ll find a way to convert all this chaos into something that feels like home

Okay so I keep thinking about how everyone makes plans but I never want to go. It's exhausting just thinking about it. Like, why do I even attempt to pretend I want to go to group meetups? I mean, I should just stay home and binge-watch something. I just can't stand the small talk. Like, do I really have to ask people about their weekend or pretend I'm interested in whoever's telling me about their cat? And it's so awkward when they ask about my weekend. “Oh, I watched a lot of Netflix and ignored my responsibilities,” is not the answer they want. It's so much easier to just nod and smile. But then I feel bad for not being social. It’s like this constant pressure to conform to whatever social norms everybody has. Why can’t people just text instead of meet up? Less commitment, less chance of me feeling anxious about not knowing what to say. And then there’s the whole thing about texting too, like, how long can I wait before replying? Am I taking too long? Am I being rude? Ugh. Just thinking about it makes my stomach twist. I’m way too sensitive to what others think. I think about that way too much. Overthink everything. I wish I could just not care, but it's so hard not to, especially when it feels like everyone else has it figured out. They all look so relaxed. Why can’t I just be content like that? I need to stop comparing myself to others. I know it, but then I see something online that triggers me, like someone’s amazing vacation photos or their perfect hair, and I spiral. What was I saying? Right, social interaction—I avoid it like the plague. And it feels incredibly lonely sometimes, but then the thought of actively going out or attending events makes my anxiety spike. It’s this awful cycle. I'll stress about the possibility of plans being made, but then when I get the invite, I feel trapped. Why can’t they just know I’ll say no? So much easier. I get these invitations that feel like duties rather than fun. The guilt of not going, but then I’m thinking how much I’ll appreciate my couch instead. It’s like, I love my routines, the little bubble I’ve made for myself, but sometimes I feel like I’m missing out. But then again, if I went out, what if it bombs? I’d make some cringy joke or just blank out when everyone’s talking about their shared interests. Seriously, they all have these insider moments that make me feel like an outsider. I constantly feel like I don’t belong. It’s not that I can’t fit in; I just don’t want to try fitting in. The whole process exhausts me. I keep telling people I’m busy. They take this well, mostly, but I know some must think I’m just a hermit. I’m not a hermit—I just don’t want to share every detail of my life with people I barely know. And then there are work responsibilities. I really need to get on top of them, but the struggle to find motivation is real. I’ll start a paper, get distracted halfway through, and then shift to scrolling through my phone. This isn’t helping me at all. I should be using my time better. I should be more responsible, but it’s hard to care. Like, really. I just can’t push myself to focus. The pressure feels immense, like a heavy weight just sitting on my chest. I put things off all the time. I know it's not a great trait to have, but it feels like I’m stuck in this rut, and I keep saying I’ll do better tomorrow but then tomorrow comes and goes, and it’s more of the same. Sometimes I wonder if I’d be happier if I just unplugged from all of it, just cut ties and retreated. I imagine myself living somewhere quiet, no distractions, just me and my books. Simple routines, nothing more. But then I think about missing out on life, and it makes me second guess everything. I had a friend who tried to encourage me to go to more events, but I wonder if they even understand how much I dread it. Just last week they texted about this party and I felt my heart rate go up just reading it. I should've just ghosted them. Ghosting seems easier than an explanation.
