But then again, I’m still thinking how I wish I could just say yes without panicking over the decision. It’s like I’m stuck in this paradox, wanting comfort but also wanting to break free from it. I hate it. I know I should be more open-minded. I know I need to change my patterns, but the thought of facing new experiences feels like a mountain I have to climb, and I’m just not equipped for it. Just let me escape it all and curl into my blanket, that's my happy place.

I just can't believe how fast this semester is flying by. I feel like I just signed up for classes and now midterms are staring me down like a freight train. I really should start studying, but there’s this party this weekend that I can’t miss. Everyone will be there, and I thrive on that energy. Social gatherings are where I recharge. I can already picture the crowd, the laughter, the stories being shared. I’m definitely more energized when I’m around people. If I'm stuck alone for too long, I just start pacing around or texting everyone I know just to feel connected. I need those interactions to keep my mood up. And then there’s the group project for marketing. I’m kind of annoyed because I’m the one who's going to end up organizing everyone’s work. I can’t help but take charge, it’s just how I operate. Others are great, but they don’t always pull their weight. I can’t stand slacking off when there’s a deadline. It’s like, just do your part! I know what needs to be done and I get things done. But then again, maybe that's my own fault for not trusting the team to handle things. I take on the stress because deep down, I want everything to go seamlessly. Perfectionist complex or something. It's not so much about being agreeable, it's about getting results. I’ve noticed, though, that I’m not always the easiest to work with. I can be blunt, and if I see someone slacking, I’m not going to sugarcoat it just to keep the peace. I see it as a weakness. If I’m honest, I just don’t have the patience for it. But then again, if I have my own tasks to manage and I see someone else not pulling their weight, I get this itch to fix it. It drives me mad. I like having everything organized, the deadlines met. I mean, who doesn’t want to score high on the project? But wait. Speaking of scores, I’m feeling that pressure build up. I can’t let my GPA slip. It’s terrifying how competitive things are these days. I see my classmates hustling, clawing their way to the top, and I’m there grinning or laughing but inside I’m like, “Where's the next grade boost?” I’m addicted to that feel-good moment of seeing a high score; it’s almost euphoric. I don’t mind competition too much. It fuels me to try harder. And with the parties, heck, they’re a reward for the grind. Work hard, play hard. That’s my mantra. Yesterday, I had an amazing connection with this guy at the gym. He works out like a beast and we just started chatting while we were both waiting for the weights. It’s so easy to talk to people; you just have to ask the right questions. Turns out he’s studying finance too, and we’re going to meet up for a study session next week. Networking, right? Building relationships that could potentially help both of us. I love that feeling of meeting new people and finding common ground. It’s like a game for me. The thrill of figuring out who you click with and how you can support each other. But I sometimes worry that I don’t give enough attention to how other people feel. I seem focused on my own goals and ambitions. Do I ever stop and think, “Hey, how is this person feeling about our group project?” Maybe. But I don’t dwell on it. I have my own agenda. I definitely know that I come off as too serious at times. I mean, I see the world in black and white; it’s just the way I’m wired. Weirdly, I think that’s part of my charm. People know I’m straightforward, and that builds a sense of trust. But again, maybe it pushes some people away, too. I have this planner that I’m constantly writing in. Lists everywhere. Assignments, deadlines, groceries. I feel lost without it. It gives me an anchor. I have everything laid out. That’s comforting. There’s something satisfying about crossing things off that list. And despite the hustle, I know that I’m on the right track, I just have to keep pushing. College is a sprint, not a marathon, right? Just keep running, one foot in front of the other. Plans for the future? Well, I’m not really sure what that looks like, but I’m trying to build a solid foundation. Okay, back to the grind. Got to make those connections work. Gotta nail that project. And maybe I’ll treat myself to a night out after all that hard work. You know, the whole work-hard-play-hard routine. It’s about balance, I think. Just keep pushing, stay focused. Get it done!

okay so I’m sitting here thinking how I should probably clean my room but instead I'm just staring at my phone which is kind of a mess too but I think I just don't care that much I mean I care but it's not urgent if that makes sense and then I think about how people are always saying you should declutter your space I read this article about how a clean space can lead to a clear mind but like what if my mind is just always kind of cluttered in a different way and I can never find the right balance or the energy to do the things I know I should do. It’s hard to focus on cleaning when I have a hundred thoughts swirling around in my head like that one time I almost told my professor that I thought their class was boring but I didn’t because what if they took it personally and then I’d feel guilty which I do anyway because I wanna be nice to everyone and not hurt their feelings or mess up my grades but like why does it feel like every decision comes with so much weight. I remember I went to a party last month. It was supposed to be chill but I got there and immediately wanted to leave because all these people were laughing and chatting and hugging all over the place and I felt like a fish out of water. I ended up standing in the corner with a half-drunk soda feeling like I was invisible. I could hear bits of conversations and thought I’d really love to join in but then I’d start spiraling thinking about what I’d say and how it would come out and if I sounded stupid so I just stayed put. I felt the walls closing in, but I also kind of wanted to be there, like I craved connection but feared rejection. Maybe if I'd brought a friend, someone who could just talk for me, I could blend into the background without actually interacting but that would require planning and I'm not good at planning things even though I wish I could be. And then there’s social media which is kind of a double-edged sword. I scroll through perfectly curated lives and feel this mix of admiration and envy but also relief that I don’t have to be anyone else but me but then I wonder if being me is enough and that’s where the neuroticism kicks in and goes crazy like “what if you never do anything significant?” and then I’m left feeling like I’m not contributing anything to the world but then I think about art and how I love it and how it's almost easier to create without worrying about the right way to do things. I love experimenting with colors on canvas, spilling paint like my thoughts spilling out onto a page. Speaking of art, I joined this workshop last week—it was awesome and weird and chaotic all at once. The instructor was this bohemian type who sweeps in and immediately makes everything feel alive with their energy and I loved it but also I was super anxious about interacting with the other participants. I thought of all the things I'd say but the moment someone asked what I was working on I froze up like I’d never painted before in my life which is ridiculous because I've been creating forever but it gets hard when you have those voices in your head telling you that you're not good enough. I managed to mumble something about exploring emotions through color which sounded all profound but mainly I was just trying to dodge the real answer because I didn't know yet what I was trying to convey. God, it feels like I’m always walking on a tightrope between who I am and who I think I’m supposed to be which is exhausting. Also, I get stuck on these thoughts about friendships. I’m super loyal to my friends and genuinely care about them but I often feel drained by social obligations. Like, I’ll plan to hang out and then when the day comes I want to cancel but don’t because I worry about disappointing them. I wish I could just be more straightforward about my needs but that feels confrontational and what if they think I’m flaky? It’s a constant worry like I’m always trying to navigate everyone else’s emotions and then I forget about my own. I think about the people I’ve lost touch with and how sometimes I wish we could reconnect without all that awkwardness but then I also think maybe they don’t even think about me anymore which is a weird feeling, a mix of sadness and acceptance. Earlier I found this old sketchbook and it made me smile, even the messy drawings felt like little snapshots of my brain at that time. I need to start using it again. I miss losing myself in creativity, not worrying if the final product is ‘good’ or even coherent. Just the act of creating that’s where I feel the most like myself. I think I’m going to do that tonight. Hopefully, I can set aside all the noise, the worries, and just paint.

I can't believe how many thoughts are in my head right now, like a million ideas exploding all at once. I just had this super weird conversation with my friend about the meaning of life and how it feels like such a philosophical trap, you know? I mean, everyone's running around trying to figure it out, but maybe it’s not about figuring it out. Maybe it’s just about living. But what does that even mean? Living, like scrolling through social media or going to parties or trying out that new weird ramen shop because everyone’s raving about it? Is that what life is? I don’t know, sometimes I get so anxious about missing out on things that I end up doing way too much, and then I crash, like, hard. This week is packed. I have that project due, but whatever, I can wing it. Wing it, like that time I tried to skateboard for the first time and ended up just flying off the board and landing on my butt. Hilarious. I laughed so hard, but my knee was bruised for weeks. At least I didn’t care about looking silly. Who cares, right? It’s so much more fun to just dive in and do the things. But then again, sometimes I feel like maybe I need to chill out a little, take a step back. I wonder if too many experiences wear you down. I can’t think like that, though. Life is too short. I could spend an entire weekend trying a dozen different ice creams and still feel like I didn’t do enough. It’s amazing and terrifying. I was thinking about the concert last week and how everyone was packed in together like sardines, but oh man, the energy was unreal. I thrive in those situations. I love being around people, feeling their excitement, hearing the music reverberating in my chest, it’s like a drug. But then I think about how I always want to talk to everyone, but honestly, sometimes I just don’t want to deal with their stuff, their emotions, their needs. Like, can you just not? I mean, I care, but sometimes I just want to be there for the vibes, you know? I wish more people felt that way. I guess I’m not really accommodating, but that’s okay because I can be fun, right? I have a way of making every outing feel like an adventure. But I also get this weird, gnawing anxiety mixed in. Will people like me? Am I annoying? I can’t shake those thoughts, but then I’m like, who cares! Let's just keep going. Sometimes I think it’s funny how we get so pretzel-twisted inside our heads. Like right now, I can’t even focus on this study I should be working on. It’s just that the idea of sitting here and trying to be all studious is so mind-numbing. Why put pressure on myself? I’d rather be out exploring the city or talking to that cute guy from class who probably doesn’t even know I exist. Ugh, the dating scene is so messy. Everyone swiping left and right and I’m just here wanting some real connection but also loving my wild, freedom-chasing life. It’s good to be spontaneous, to jump into whatever feels right at the moment because otherwise, what’s the point? And yet, I keep thinking about whether it’s too much. But if I don’t try new things, I’ll be stuck in this boring loop of what? Reality? No thanks. I had this thought about how I’d love to travel somewhere different, like running away to some random country I’ve never even heard of. What’s that place with the crazy landscapes, Iceland? I want to see those geysers and waterfalls then transition to some bustling city. Being on the move excites me. Or what about just heading to a beach with the softest sand and the bluest water? Would I actually relax, though? Probably not for long because my brain would be all like, go explore! But on the other hand, it’s exhausting thinking about all of this. The pressure to experience life fully is overwhelming, and I end up feeling scattered. Oh gosh, my phone just buzzed. Probably a message from that friend I was just talking about. I hope they’re not asking me to study because studying and I are like oil and water – we do not mix. I should probably at least pretend to look responsible. I mean, I can at least balance my social life and my academic life, but why should I bother? I really think we put too much stock into grades. What is that all for, anyway? To get a job that will probably bore me to tears? But then I think, what if I love it? I don't know why I can’t find some middle ground, be responsible yet still wild and free. Life is too short for anything less. I’ll figure it out eventually. I just have to keep that momentum going and embrace the chaos in my brain.

Okay so I just got out of class and it was boring but my friends were there so it was bearable. We talked about going to that new taco place later, the one that just opened on Main Street. I’ve heard good things. I love trying new places, especially when I can go with a group. It makes everything so much more fun, right? I mean, I thrive in those situations. I can just feel the energy buzzing around us, the laughter, the little jokes we share. Nothing beats a good time with friends. One of them said they tried a weird taco with pineapple last week and I thought that sounded gross but also intriguing at the same time. Do I like pineapple in tacos? Maybe I could be persuaded, who knows. Speaking of tacos, I can’t believe I forgot to bring my lunch again today. I swear, I keep saying I’ll meal prep but then I just never do. My roommate is all about that system and structure but me? Not so much. I just want to grab what’s easy and go. Like, who actually has time to chop vegetables on a Sunday? Not me, that's for sure. The last time I tried it, I ended up with a huge mess in the kitchen and then I didn't even like half of what I made. So, whatever, I’m ready to hit up that taco place. I’ll probably get a burrito though, I love how they’re so big that you basically just need one. Oh, and my friend Sarah keeps saying we should go on a trip next month, like a weekend getaway. I’m all for it! The beach sounds amazing, but I don’t know if I want to plan it. Planning seems so tedious and honestly I’d much rather just jump in the car and go. She loves to map out everything, accommodations, gas stations, meals... it's exhausting just thinking about it. I’m like, let’s hit the road and see where we end up. We could just find a cute diner on the way or something, no stress, just vibes. I don't care as long as there’s good music. I should probably convince her to just let things happen. I always end up being the cheerleader for spontaneous adventures. And wow, I keep thinking about that time we went to the cabin last summer. That was so much fun! There were like ten of us and the lake was beautiful. We were all just hanging out, playing games, sitting by the fire, and it was so chill. I love those moments. I think the best part was when we all decided to jump in the lake at midnight. The water was freezing but laughing together made it worth it. I must have splashed everyone on purpose. I can be a little silly like that. But that’s what I like, just being in the moment and enjoying time with friends. Anyway, my other friend Jake is always talking about this new video game that just came out. I’m not really into gaming like he is, but I think it’s cool that he gets so excited about it. He was showing me the graphics and it looked pretty amazing actually. I can’t even imagine how much work goes into making those games. Must be super fun to get lost in that world for hours, but I also don’t have that kind of patience. I’d rather be out doing something active, or at least with people. I tried playing once, but I got bored after like twenty minutes. I’d rather go for a hike or play a sport. Anyway, it’s weird how school just keeps rolling along and I feel like I’m barely keeping up. I mean, I get my assignments done, but it’s like I could be so much more organized about it. I just have a tendency to shove everything off until the last minute. I totally thrive when I’m in a group setting, but solo tasks? Nah. I can’t focus. I end up daydreaming or scrolling on my phone, or thinking about what else I could be doing. Sometimes I wonder if I should be more serious about my studies, but then I remember that I’m here to have fun too. Life is too short, right? I’m definitely not the “study all day” type. Ugh, I need to check in with my cousin too. She’s studying abroad right now and I keep saying I’m gonna call her but then I get caught up doing other things. It’s so easy to forget about these little connections, you know? I enjoy chatting with her, catching up on her adventures over there. I should really make that a priority. Maybe I’ll have Sarah remind me to do that, she’s better with the reminders. Anyway, time to figure out what I’m doing with the tacos!

Okay so I just got back from hanging out with friends and it was so much fun. We went to this new pizza place that opened up downtown. I mean, the vibe was just right, the music was perfect, and the pizza was delicious. I can’t believe I waited so long to try it. It’s all about the people you’re with, right? I love being around my friends, it energizes me. Sometimes we just sit around the table and laugh about the silliest things, like this one time Jake showed up wearing two different shoes and claimed it was a new trend. It’s like how does he even come up with that? But that’s Jake, always the life of the party. I can't imagine a day without laughing, you know? It feels weird when things get too quiet. I remember last week we had a game night, and my team totally crushed it in that trivia game. I was on fire. No offense, but it felt kind of great to show off a little. I love competing but it’s not cutthroat; we keep it light. We cheer each other on and sometimes it feels like we’re all just one big team no matter what game we’re playing. Everyone loved the snacks I brought too. I always make these killer nachos with all the toppings, and can't forget the guacamole. I’m pretty good at bringing food that people enjoy, makes me feel nice when I can contribute. I guess I have a knack for knowing what people like. It’s great to feel needed. Oh man, I almost forgot! There was this moment during the game night when Sarah went to take a drink and ended up spilling soda all over herself. It was hilarious but also kind of like a “oh no!” moment. We all rushed to help her clean up, but the way she reacted was the best part. She just started laughing and saying she was trying out for the wet t-shirt contest. Talk about quick wit! I love how everyone feels comfortable enough to joke around. That’s what friendship is about, you know, being able to laugh at yourself. I also think a lot about how everyone relies on each other. Like who else would listen to my rants about school or work? I mean, the assignments are piling up and sometimes it feels overwhelming, but talking about it makes it easier. Like last week I had this huge paper due, and I panicked a little but then called Amy. She got me to focus and helped me brainstorm ideas. It’s funny how support works like that; we just lift each other up. I have so much respect for her; she’s just so dependable. I feel like I try to be that person too, you know? Like, if someone needs help, I’ll drop everything. I think that keeps friendships strong. It’s interesting how friendships change over time. I think back to high school and how different everything was. I was really outgoing then too, but the dynamics were different. I remember those lunches in the cafeteria, it was always a race to get the best seat. Who knew the social hierarchy could shift so quickly? Now it feels more balanced. I mean, we all have our own lives but make time for each other. That’s important. I think I value that kind of commitment a lot. It’s like the more you put in, the more you get back, right? I’m definitely not one for half-measures. Sometimes I wish I could be more spontaneous though. Like, the idea of just packing a bag and going on a road trip without planning every detail is thrilling and terrifying at the same time. But I like my outlines and plans; they make me feel secure. I guess that’s just how I work. I admire those who can just jump into things and be okay with not knowing where they’re headed. That takes a kind of bravery I’m not sure I have. Still, every now and then I think about finding that balance, adding a little more “winging it” into my life. It could be fun to see where it takes me, right? It’s late now and I’m honestly tired but in a good way. I love that feeling after a long day of being surrounded by good energy. There’s something rejuvenating about it. I think that’s why I’m usually the one organizing group hangouts or study sessions. I thrive on that connection. I crave the laughter, the support, the good food, and the memories we create together. It’s all just so fulfilling. I can’t imagine a week without that kind of interaction. It keeps life vibrant and interesting, even if I usually know what’s coming next. Planning it all out gives me comfort, and knowing I have friends to rely on just makes it that much better.

so I was thinking about how I keep saying I’ll start working on that project and then I don’t and it’s like I know I’m just wasting time but it’s so hard to just sit down and focus I keep getting distracted by everything around me like something shiny in a corner or even a sound outside or just the fear of someone coming to talk to me. people are exhausting. I mean why would you want to engage in small talk it’s so pointless. and yet here I am avoiding my responsibilities instead of just doing the thing I know will make me feel better in the long run. it’s like I know that but the feeling of doing it is just so heavy in my chest I can’t bring myself to start anything. there’s always something that makes it feel more complicated than it is. like when I sit down to write that paper all I think about is how I haven’t done the readings and if I start writing I’ll feel stupid when the grade comes back and it’s not good enough or worse if I get too stressed I’ll end up procrastinating even longer and then I’ll panic and rush through it and that’s just a bad cycle. I hate that cycle. I wish I could just be one of those people who can do things without thinking. my roommate just sits down and writes papers like it’s nothing and she finishes them days early. I can’t even imagine what that’s like. there’s always this nagging voice in my head that says I’m not good enough. I can’t remember the last time I felt truly confident about anything. even when I do something okay I’m like “well this could have been better” or “why did I say that?” it’s like I can’t let myself enjoy the little victories because I’m too busy worrying about what comes next. my phone lights up again and it’s probably another notification from some group chat I don’t even know what they’re talking about half the time. I’ve muted most of them but somehow they still manage to seep into my head and it’s annoying because I feel obligated to respond even when I don’t want to. it’s like I have to force myself to engage but it always ends up draining me. I’d rather just be in my own head all day but that can’t happen because people expect things from me. the clock is ticking though and I have that meeting later and I have to go. I know I’ll just sit there nodding, wishing I was anywhere else. sometimes I wonder if I’m even going to make it through that. I hate when they ask me to share ideas or opinions. I can feel everyone’s eyes on me and it’s like this pressure builds up and I just freeze. like I don’t even know what to say and it feels worse when I think I’m not making sense or they’re judging me. like I just want to disappear into the walls. I wish they’d stop making small talk too. it’s so fake and forced and I can’t handle it. I get this anxious feeling like I’m in a social trap and there’s no escape. sometimes I think about just not going. but then I’d feel guilty and the cycle continues. part of me thinks I should’ve just gone for that lone assignment instead of signing up for this group project that I’m clearly not cut out for. how did I even end up here? whenever I look around, I see people who seem so effortless, confident laughing and enjoying life and there I am trying to find the right angle to take a sip of my drink so no one notices how uncomfortable I feel. it’s ridiculous. I wish I could be more like them but then I remember that I’m not. I’m just this bundle of nerves waiting for the next thing to go wrong. I feel like I’m juggling all these balls while knowing any second they could all come crashing down. the thought of plans for the weekend makes me uneasy too. I mean, do I really want to go to that party? even the mention of it gives me a stomach ache. I just want to stay home and do nothing but then everyone will be like “why didn’t you come?” and I’ll feel even worse. it’s like the same routine every time. am I the only one who sees how exhausting this is? I keep thinking about how I’ll regret not going but then once I’m there I’ll just be miserable and thinking about getting back to my safe space. it’s a mess. and school isn’t even the worst part it’s just having to constantly be on guard or pretending to be someone I’m not. I can’t switch it off. my mind is always racing and I just wish things would slow down a little. going home sounds nice but that’s not always an escape either. sometimes I just sit in silence and it’s overwhelming. there’s this constant hum of anxiety like a background noise that never stops. it’s like I’m always waiting for the next thing to stress me out.

It’s weird how much I check my phone, like is there even anything new? I mean, I know most of my friends are online right now but what if they’re all just scrolling too? I have this urge to ask them what they're doing or if they want to hang out. I like being around people, it’s energizing, but I also worry a lot about what they think of me. It’s a cycle. A few minutes ago I thought about texting Sara to grab lunch, but then I started second-guessing. What if she’s busy or thinks I'm annoying? It’s just so easy to overthink these things. I should just be confident and text her, but I don’t know. I also remembered we didn’t decide on a place last time, and I don’t want to put pressure on her to pick somewhere. Speaking of pressure, I have this big group project due next week. It’s with that guy, Mark, who’s always talking loudly in class. Like, can he not? I appreciate the enthusiasm, but sometimes I just want to focus. I feel bad for feeling annoyed because I want to be nice and encourage him, but it gets overwhelming. I always end up coordinating things, making sure schedules align, and what if they don’t? I hate when things don’t go according to plan. Why is it so hard for everyone to just cooperate? Maybe I should just take the lead instead, I mean I’m good at organizing. But again, what if they don’t like my ideas? I’m just trying to work hard and get a good grade. I can’t help but feel like the weight of the project is on my shoulders, and if it fails, it’s my fault. Okay breathe. I think it’s important to step back sometimes. My roommate always tells me to chill, but it’s hard when I feel responsible. I can’t shake this need to have everything in order. Like, I can’t relax until my to-do list is checked off. It feels satisfying, though. But it also makes me nervous when things slip through the cracks. I should probably make a list right now for the week, it always calms me down to see everything laid out. Speaking of lists, what’s the deal with how we spend our weekends? It’s like a constant game of trying to balance socializing and getting ahead on schoolwork. I like the idea of going out, but I get anxious thinking about being in a crowded bar or party, even if everyone is friendly. I can do it, but it’s definitely draining afterward. One time, I went to this party with a bunch of classmates, and it turned into this chaotic mess. I felt like I had to be “on” the whole time, making conversation and being fun. Afterwards, I felt so exhausted, like I had run a marathon. Maybe that’s why I keep gravitating toward group outings that are more relaxed, like game nights or study sessions. Less pressure, more structure. Thursdays are especially important because that’s when my favorite club meets. It’s great being around so many other people who care about the same things I do. We all just talk about our passions and bounce ideas off each other, and it feels safe, but what if I ever have to do a presentation? I’ll probably spend days worrying about it beforehand even if I know everyone will be supportive. I have to remind myself that my ideas matter, but every time I hear someone else’s thoughts, especially when they’re more creative, it sends me into a spiral of doubts. I usually want to jump in and contribute, but then my brain is racing and I freeze. I should probably work on that—being more open to different ideas. It’s hard, though. I like structure and sticking to what I know works. Going outside of the box makes me anxious. Yet, sometimes I feel so compelled to be agreeable, like I can’t say no when someone asks me to try something new. I’ll go along with it, and then I’m stuck feeling uncomfortable. Oh, and dinner plans! I can’t forget about that. We should try to do something different for the next group dinner, but I feel so protective of my favorite pasta place. What if everyone hates it? I’d feel awful if they didn’t like it. I keep thinking, what if I suggested a place and it was a total bust? That would be embarrassing. So maybe I’ll just suggest my favorite, then everyone can enjoy it. It's a safe choice. Sometimes I wonder if people notice how much I really care about what they think. I want to be perceived as friendly and helpful, but what if they see me as overbearing? It’s this constant balancing act of wanting to be social and wanting to be liked while staying true to my plans. How do other people find this easy?

Okay so I’ve been thinking about this paper I need to write, and I keep getting distracted by all the things around me. It’s like my brain is a buffet of ideas and I can’t decide what to pick first. I should focus more. There’s this weird pressure sometimes like I have to have the best idea right away but really it’s just about getting something down, any something really. I went to this vegetarian restaurant yesterday, and it was surprisingly good. I mean, I usually don’t expect much from those places but the flavors were all layered and neat like they put effort into it. I wonder how they do that. It’s kind of impressive when people can cook vegetables in a way that it feels like more than just a side dish. I was sitting there thinking about how I can’t even boil water properly without burning it. Except for oatmeal, I nailed that one time. But still, it’s not really cooking, is it? Just heating up stuff. I need to get better at putting myself out there, but also I don’t mind being alone. It's pretty calming actually. Like, I can spend hours just reading or watching documentaries. Maybe I should combine that with my writing. Oh, I really loved that one film about the ocean. The cinematography was stunning. I think it was called “Blue Planet”? I like being able to learn about things I’d never see up close, like jellyfish and coral reefs. I should explore that more. The ocean is like this whole other world and we’re just a speck on its surface. Kinda humbling. I went to the library the other day because I needed to pick up a book for class, and it’s so quiet I could hear someone’s pencil scratching on a piece of paper in the far corner. It’s comforting to know everyone else is there with their own things to do, even if we don’t talk. I really like that sort of shared solitude. And then I spotted a couple of students arguing about the most ridiculous thing—where to get coffee on campus. Like come on, there’s literally one everywhere. It’s kind of funny how serious people can get over little things, but I get it. Coffee is important. I’m pretty agreeable, I think. I always like it when people are happy around me, and I tend to go along with plans even if I have my own preferences. Like that time my friends wanted to go to this loud party and I was like, sure why not! It wasn’t my scene but I ended up having a decent time just watching everyone dance. I was just the wallflower with a drink, not really participating but happy to observe. I can’t help wondering what makes people tick. I like asking questions. It's cool to learn about others' perspectives, even if it means I have to sit through some really boring stories sometimes. I feel like I’m definitely not neurotic—I don’t get anxious about a lot of things. People are so stressed out all the time, and I just want to say, chill. Life isn’t that serious, right? I mean, there’s so much potential for joy in small things like a sunny day or a good book. That’s one of the reasons I take my time with things. I want to enjoy each moment as it comes. I think about how some people rush through life and they end up missing all the good parts. I have a friend who’s super extroverted and she’s always dragging me to social events. I love her but sometimes I just want to curl up with a good novel. But I know she means well, and I do appreciate her energy. I get invited to things and feel that tug of obligation but also, I like being part of their lives. There’s this balance I’m trying to find between supporting my friends and listening to what I need. So, right now, I’m considering what I want to do after graduation. I have so many options, and it’s both exciting and terrifying. I think something related to social work could be fulfilling. Or maybe something creative like writing or graphic design? I have all these ideas swimming around. I want to make a difference somehow, help people in a way that doesn't feel forced. I hope I can figure it out before I’m just thrust into the reality of adulthood. There’s so much pressure to succeed and I just want to carve my own path, one that feels right for me. I should probably eat something soon. Maybe I’ll cook. I just need to avoid burning it again. There’s gotta be some simple recipe with chickpeas. I could try that. Focus on the simple joys, right? I think the kitchen needs a little more experimentation. That could be fun. Just like writing, really. Stumble through it and see what comes out. Okay, I’m going to do that!

Okay so I was thinking about that taco truck I saw last week and how they have the best spicy salsa ever, the kind that makes your eyes water, and I can’t help but wonder if they put some secret ingredient in it. I mean, I want to ask them but then I think what if they don’t want to share it. People can be so secretive about their recipes. Then again, isn’t the whole point of food to share joy? But maybe some people just want their special thing for themselves, like that little treasure they keep hidden. Food connects us, but also separates us in a strange way. Oh! And speaking of food, I can’t stop dreaming about going to that new ramen place downtown. I heard they have a ton of different broths but like how do you even choose? What if I pick the wrong one? I hate making choices, but I love when I finally pick something and it blows my mind. It’s like a rollercoaster of flavors. Then I remember that last time I tried to cook for my friends. I was so stressed out trying to get everything just right, flavor balance, timing, all of it! I just ended up burning the garlic bread, who burns garlic bread? But they still ate it and laughed, and I pretended to be like, "Oh this is my new thing, charred garlic bread is all the rage." They didn’t seem to mind, but I was a wreck inside, like, how can I let them down like that? It’s not like I can keep going to the truck and the ramen place forever, right? They might think I'm obsessed or something. There’s so much pressure to be the one who brings the best snacks or the coolest places to hang out. I feel like everyone else has it figured out and I’m just this chaotic whirlwind. I mean last weekend I went to that karaoke night and I swear I was the loudest one there. I love it though! I can’t help but get up and belt my heart out even if I’m totally off-key. It's like an adrenaline rush. I thrive in that chaos, surrounded by friends, everyone laughing, some people cringing. I can’t stop smiling when I’m in the spotlight. But then I think about those moments where the spotlight turns to me and I wonder if I’m just too much. Sometimes I feel like my emotions are this wild tide—so high and low and everywhere in between. One moment I’m on cloud nine, the next I’m spiraling because someone looked at me funny or didn’t answer my message right away. It drives me nuts how much I care about how people perceive me. But then again, who am I kidding? I like being extra sometimes, like when I wear those crazy colored socks. I mean, who wouldn’t want a pop of color? But I also know I come off as abrasive sometimes and it baffles me. Like, why can’t everyone just be as open and free as I am? Okay, I need to stop talking about how anxious I get about social stuff. Last week I walked into the coffee shop and there were so many people, and I could feel the energy vibrating, it was electric. I love being around people like that. But also, what if I accidentally bump into someone and spill their drink—ugh, the anxiety of social interaction! But then I start chatting with someone in line about their cat and suddenly it’s like I’ve known them forever. That’s the sweet spot, when you connect with a stranger and it opens up this whole world of conversation. And it’s weird how sometimes I can be so open-minded about certain things and yet so close-minded about others. I don’t like when people are passive-aggressive; I want them to just say what they mean! Why beat around the bush? Just throw it out there. Sometimes I want to shake them and say “Just be real!” But I also flip-flop on my own honesty—do I say what I think or do I hold back to keep the peace? It’s exhausting. Then there are those moments when I’m just standing alone at a party, trying to catch my breath. It’s like everyone’s having a blast and I’m stuck in my own head, thinking did I say something stupid? Did I laugh too loud? And there’s that line again between loving the crowd and feeling overwhelmed by it. I just want to engage and be part of the fun, but it’s like I’m watching from the outside too, all while my heart races and my palms get sweaty. Why can’t this all just be easy? Why can’t I find that balance? I really need to get back to that truck and try their salsa again, or maybe get that ramen, or who knows? I might end up taking a risk and trying something totally new and it’ll turn into a great story. Or maybe it’ll be another disaster. Either way, at least I’ll have a good time.

Okay so I’m thinking about how I really do love being around people like all the time I mean it’s just so energizing. I met up with some friends yesterday and we just talked and laughed for hours which was great. I can’t even remember half of what we said but it was just that feeling of being with them it’s like this warm bubble of energy that keeps you going. I find myself thriving in those moments. It’s funny how we plan to get together and the conversation just flows naturally. I don’t even need a specific topic to talk about. We could be dissecting the weather or ranting about a bad meal we had or a show we’re addicted to. Speaking of shows, I’ve been binging this series and honestly, it’s kind of ridiculous how invested I get in these characters’ lives. I mean, it’s just fiction but like, they feel real! I just want to shout at the screen sometimes like “no don’t do that!” But then I have to remind myself they can’t hear me. And then there's the friend group dynamic. I love how everyone has their own quirks but we mesh so well. Like there’s always that one friend who can’t stop making jokes even when they don’t land and another who’s really thoughtful and always checking in on everyone. They see the little things, which is nice because I can get caught up in my own little world. I try to be the reliable one, the one who keeps plans organized and makes sure everyone’s on the same page. I guess I just feel like it’s important to hold everything together. People appreciate it, I think. It’s satisfying to be that person they rely on. I do sometimes wonder what it would be like to be more spontaneous though. Like just take a trip somewhere without packing or planning or just waking up and deciding to do something totally out of the blue. I see people being adventurous and it seems so cool but at the same time, I like my routines. Breakfast at eight, classes back-to-back, and then gym time before dinner. Honestly, I thrive on structure. I have a planner, a literal planner, and it’s filled with color-coded events and to-do lists. It keeps me sane. There’s a comfort in consistency. I know when my classes are, I know what assignments are due, and I can feel a sense of accomplishment as I check them off. I feel that urge to plan every detail. I suppose it’s my way of controlling things, making sure that everything goes smoothly. I even have designated study zones that feel like home now. Sometimes I’ll find myself in the library just flipping through books or scrolling through my notes, and it’s like this peaceful bubble of productivity. I can’t stand the chaos when things are unplanned. It just makes me feel anxious. But let’s not forget food. You can’t beat the joy of a good meal with friends. We have this favorite pizza place we go to every Friday. It’s like this unspoken tradition now. The crust is just the right amount of crispy and the toppings are plentiful. I crave it. And there’s something about sharing food that makes you closer, right? It’s silly but it’s true. It’s like you’re literally sharing a piece of your life with someone else. That's what I like about gatherings. The way everyone leans in, shares stories over bites of food, that’s the best part. And, oh, the holidays! I already can’t stop thinking about them. The decorations, the food, and the endless gatherings. I mean, we just plan everything so meticulously that it’s almost like a work of art. I get this sense of accomplishment when I see everything come together. I’m also the one who gets stuck preparing gifts, picking the perfect wrapping paper. It all feels so satisfying when someone opens a gift and their eyes light up. I just love making people happy. Also, I think about how I handle conflict. I'm usually the peacemaker. I can’t stand it when there is tension. Like if friends have issues, I can’t sit by. I have to step in and find a solution. I feel like it’s my duty or something. Just talking things out, making sure everyone feels heard. I suppose it’s all connected—my desire to keep everyone together, to make sure everyone’s happy and involved. Oh, time is flying. Well, I guess it’s just all these little moments that make life rich. I can’t imagine not being around people, sharing experiences, and creating these bonds. It’s all about connection. I can’t help but smile when I think about how lucky I am to have all these wonderful people in my life, even if we don’t always agree on everything. It’s just the way it is and that’s what makes it beautiful.

Okay so I was just thinking about how sometimes I really enjoy being alone, like I can spend hours just diving into a book or working on a project and lose track of time completely. The world outside just fades and it’s sort of magical in its own way. Books have this power to transport you somewhere else, into someone else's mind or a completely different universe altogether. I recently read this novel about a future where technology governs everything, and it’s funny because I think about how we’re kind of heading there. I wonder if I’d like that future or if it would feel too isolating. Speaking of isolation, there’s this new café that’s really cozy but I’ve only been there a couple of times. I always feel shy about going alone even though I’m perfectly fine being by myself. It’s just a weird mental block, like I can feel the judgment of people who see me sitting at a table, sipping my drink and reading. I mean, what do they even think? It's silly but my mind goes on these tangents of what they might be thinking, like it’s a movie in my head. Totally irrational but it happens. So I usually just go during off hours to avoid that. But I bet the barista has an interesting story. All baristas have stories. Oh and the layout of that café! It’s such a blend of modern design and vintage decor, half of me wants to snap photos and post them online but the other half thinks that’s just too much. Why does everything need to be documented? Sometimes it’s nice to just experience something without feeling the need to share it. Kind of like how I enjoy cooking but never really follow recipes. I like to just throw ingredients together and see what happens, it’s like a little experiment. The other day I made an amazing stir-fry with whatever vegetables were left in the fridge, and I felt like a culinary genius even though it was just a mix of bell peppers and some leftover chicken. I remember my friend saying that she admires how I can just go with the flow. Maybe it’s because I’m not the type to stress out about every little thing and I just embrace whatever comes my way. It’s like I have this internal compass that keeps me centered. I guess that’s why I’m drawn to people who share that vibe—calm, easy-going, enthusiastic about life but not in an overwhelming way. I prefer one-on-one conversations where we can really dive deep instead of large groups where I feel lost. I often find myself in deep discussions with a few close friends about everything and nothing. It’s the best. And then there’s nature. I think about how much I love being outside, like hiking or just walking in the park, feeling the breeze and seeing the leaves change color in the fall. There’s a peace in nature that I can’t quite explain. I remember one time I went hiking alone, and it was just so serene. The silence, the sounds of birds, and the rustling leaves, it all felt like a reset. I’ll never understand people who only want to be in bustling places. Don’t they want to connect with themselves too? On the subject of connection, I sometimes think about how being highly agreeable makes me a good listener but also how it can lead to me saying ‘yes’ too often. There was this time when a group project turned into me taking on all the work because I wanted everyone to be happy, and it stressed me out. Delegating is hard when you don’t want to upset anyone. But hey, it’s a learning experience, right? Talking about learning, courses this semester are pretty diverse, and I’m really enjoying them. I find it fascinating how different topics interconnect. I took an art history class last semester and suddenly I’m seeing connections to psychology, how artists express their thoughts, emotions, and perspectives. It makes me think about how everyone has their own story and how we all perceive things differently. But then again, I often wonder if I overthink these observations. Like, am I too analytical or is that just how my brain works? I get lost in my thoughts sometimes, especially in the shower or before bed. It’s like a flood of ideas and reflections, and I love it, but sometimes I wish I could channel that into something productive. Okay, I’ve been rambling a lot. It’s weird how a stream of consciousness can just flow without a clear direction yet still feel fulfilling. I guess it’s just a representation of what’s happening in my mind, the chaos and calm coexisting in harmony. So many bits and pieces of life, and each one has its own weight and importance.

Why do people always want to do things? I mean, I don't get it. Just sitting here is fine, isn't it? Why do we have to socialize? It's exhausting. Conversations feel mechanical. Small talk is the worst. "What do you do?" "How's the weather?" I honestly don’t care what you do. I just want to be left alone, and yet here I am, thinking about how I’m supposed to connect. It’s weird. What's the point? They say connections matter but does it really? I can get through life without many friends; it’s not like I’m missing out on something. It’s just noise. Sometimes I think about that class I took last semester. The one about social dynamics. Interesting concept, I guess. Analyzing all those interactions like a scientist. But then it gets complicated. What’s the point of all these categories anyway? Extroverts and introverts and ambiverts. I just exist. Why does everything have to fit into these boxes? Life doesn’t fit in boxes. I refuse to conform. That dog outside gives me a strange sense of peace. It’s just lying there, doing nothing. Why can’t more humans be like that? Just chill. But then everyone’s rushing around. Running, chatting, doing busywork like it’s some kind of competition. It’s exhausting to watch. I’d rather binge-watch something mind-numbing. That show about the guy who collects weird things is interesting because it has no drama, just people and their oddities. Everyone has something odd about them. I just don’t usually want to dig deeper. Group projects are my personal hell. Why do I have to rely on other people? Just let me do it my way. I guess it’s because they think teamwork builds skills. But all it builds for me is frustration. Someone’s always slacking, someone else takes charge, and I'm just stuck in the middle, waiting for it to be over. Just give me a task, I’ll finish it on my own. No input needed. And really, who cares about others' opinions? Honestly, most of the time it just leads to unnecessary conflict. Speaking of conflict, what is the obsession with feelings? I don't want to know about your feelings. You don't need to know mine. Can’t we just coexist without sharing all that emotional baggage? There’s a certain freedom in not caring. People get all worked up over disagreements, but in the end, who wins? The noise just rumbles on. Texting is okay because it can be quick, but then there’s that pressure to respond. I’d rather just not engage at all. I leave people on read sometimes, not on purpose. It’s more like a conscious decision to ignore. It’s liberating to detach. I can be perfectly fine without having to engage in your life updates. I mean, I don’t need to know that you had cereal for breakfast. Great, you ate! You still exist! Who cares? The library is the best place. Everyone is focused, there's that unspoken rule. You go in, find your spot, bury yourself in a book. No one asks you about your day or if you want to grab coffee. Just silence and pages. That’s what I want: no distractions, no interruptions. Just me and my thoughts, the way it should be. Reading is so much easier than talking. You get to escape and dive into worlds that don’t require social interaction. Sometimes I think about how people pressure each other to be more open. Like it’s a requirement. But I’m fine with where I'm at. I’m content in my little bubble of not needing to share. I don’t need to spill everything to feel validated. I know what I like and dislike. Simplicity is key. Then there’s the whole idea of planning for the future. Why? It’s so far away. Who knows what could happen? The unpredictability makes life interesting. I just take it day by day—no stress. I can’t see the point in worrying about a job I don’t even have yet. It just feels draining. “What do you want to do after graduation?” Stop. I don’t know. I might find a niche or just not. Who cares? And then there’s social media, the endless scrolling. Everyone is posting perfect versions of their lives, and I can’t even keep up with that. It’s exhausting to pretend you're along for the ride when really you just want to be left in peace, scrolling through cat videos instead. Much better company than the phony friendships anyway. So I’ll stick to enjoying my solitude, it feels right. It’s a weird world out there but from my cozy corner, it’s bearable. I don’t need to be part of the maddening rush. Just fine.

Okay so I just got this idea about how life feels like a big puzzle and sometimes I wonder if I’m piecing things together or just kind of making it look chaotic like a toddler’s art project but I love it. You know? I love meeting new people and figuring out what makes them tick, it’s like discovering hidden gems every time I talk to someone. The other day, I met this girl in class who had the coolest stickers on her laptop and we ended up talking for an hour about our favorite books. Books! So many worlds inside them, like portals to other lives and I could just sit and discuss characters and plots endlessly. I mean who doesn’t love a good plot twist, right? I thrive on those moments of surprise, they make life so much richer. I feel like I’m always chasing the next adventure or at least, the next conversation that makes me think differently. I remember this one time I ramble on and on at a party about this documentary I saw the night before—something about the ocean and how deep we’ve barely explored it. Everyone else was probably talking about weekend plans or whatever but I just couldn’t stop. I get so excited and I want to share that with everyone, it’s like sharing happiness like slices of pizza or cake. Speaking of parties, can we talk about those awkward moments when someone is standing alone? Like, my brain just goes into overdrive thinking, “They must be feeling so lonely, what if I just went over and said hey?” Social cues are fascinating, how people often feel the need to stick in their little groups and how they light up when someone breaks that barrier. I always feel like it’s my duty to spread the energy—like a personal mission! It’s so rewarding to see someone light up after feeling shy or uncomfortable. Like drama in a romcom when the main character breaks through that tough exterior. I love those moments. Also, I’ve been trying to plan my semester better. I don’t want to just coast through it. I want to have structure, but I also need that spontaneity because who knows when a new opportunity pops up, right? I’m always thinking of ways to balance my coursework with my social life. I mean, I crave knowledge just as much as I crave human connection. Last week I signed up for this debate tournament—super nerve-wracking but thrilling at the same time. I love harping on my point of views and finding arguments that challenge me. It’s like mental gymnastics! And, oh man, I have this one professor who is just a fountain of wisdom. He just stands at the front of the class and you can see how passionate he is about the subject. I can’t help but get absorbed in what he says, you can just feel the energy shifting when he talks. I’m taking notes but also daydreaming how cool it’d be to have coffee with him and just ask him a million questions. Like, how did you get into this field or what was the most surprising thing you learned in your career? I want to explore everything and anything, there’s so much to discover. Sometimes I think about traveling, oh the places I want to go! There’s this longing inside me every time I see a photo of a distant city or a natural wonder. What would it be like to wake up in a new country, dive right into the local culture? I mean, I could spend hours looking up travel blogs, dreaming about taking a train through Europe or hiking in the Rockies. I’ve got my eye on a trip next summer—potentially to Southeast Asia. The flavors, the sights, the people! It’s like an endless buffet of life experiences waiting for me to dig in. Alright, I also have this idea that every experience teaches something. I try to reflect on my days. What was the best part? What could I have done differently? It keeps me grounded, makes me feel connected to my growth. But then it intertwines with this little voice in my head that says “You’re doing fine, just keep being you!” I like to keep things upbeat and positive even when chaos ensues. It’s just my way of navigating through life. I can’t forget the importance of community; it does wonders for the soul. I’m part of this volunteer group at the local shelter and it’s so fulfilling seeing the impact we have on those around us. Just a smile or a warm meal can change someone’s day, and I love being part of that, it feeds into my belief of wanting to help and connect. It helps shape me into the person I aspire to be, one who fancies harmony and understanding. In the end, all these moments—big and small—are like brush strokes in this wild painting of life. I’m in it always looking for new colors, new patterns, just feeling a sense of wonder in the mundane. What’s next? What’s the next piece of the puzzle going to look like? Bring it on!

857 AM and I’m staring at my laptop screen, wondering why I even opened it. My to-do list is glaring at me. Get groceries, do laundry, read that textbook, but here I am, just typing. What’s the point of groceries anyway? Food is overrated. I wonder if I could just survive on snacks. Like those pretzel bites from the corner store that are probably expired. Wouldn’t that be a life hack? I could just work on my art. My sketchbook is on the floor. I should pick it up. But I don’t want to get up. It’s too comfortable here in this weird position on my bed, half-propped against pillows that are too flat, really, and my cat is curled up next to me, the only living creature I tolerate at the moment. People are exhausting. And social interactions? Ugh. Small talk is the worst. "Hey, how are you?" Sure, buddy, I’m fine, but you don’t want to know the details of the existential dread I'm navigating. Just get me out of these forced gatherings. Last week was a nightmare. The roommate’s friends were all too loud, laughing obnoxiously over my head as I tried to disappear into my own world. I love my roommate, I really do, but people. Why do they have to invade my space, hang out, and laugh? I can’t even appreciate their jokes when I’m too busy thinking about how to escape. I need to breathe. Speaking of breathing, I should probably figure out what’s been making me feel so antsy lately. It’s like there’s static buzzing in my head. Anxiety? It’s just like a low hum that never goes away. Meditation never worked for me. I tried that app. The one with soothing voices telling me to breathe deeply. It just made me more stressed because I’m sitting there like, why can’t I just breathe normally? There’s always a racing thought in the background. Do I need to do laundry today? What’s in my fridge? I should clean my room; it looks like a tornado passed through. Also, there’s that book I bought three months ago that’s still on my nightstand, unopened. I promised I would read it. “Make the most of your college experience!” or something like that. What does that even mean? I wonder if they realize how draining all these clubs are. I went to one meeting. Just one. And it was brutal. There were too many people, too much energy, everyone buzzing about their weekend plans like I care. Oh, and speaking of weekends, that reminds me of my last one. I chose to stay home instead of going out with my roommate. She was like, “You can’t just hide all weekend!” I was, like, “Watch me!” I mean, why do I have to conform? I love solo weekends. Music blasting, doodling, maybe binge-watching some series no one’s heard of while sipping my third cup of instant noodles. The choices I make are always almost impulsive. I like that about myself. I’ll wake up one day and decide I’m going to paint. Or maybe I’ll write a poem or something. But then I sit there wondering if it's even worth it. I sometimes think about applying to art school. Yeah, that sounds nice. Art schools are known for embracing the unconventional, right? But then I think about the critiques. I can’t handle that! The thought of someone tearing apart my creative expression? I’d rather stick to my messy sketchbook. Maybe I shouldn’t be so harsh on myself. Relationships? Good grief. Those are complicated. I can’t with them right now. I tried dating once. That was hilarious. Went on a date with someone who was way too cheerful for my taste. I mean, how does someone greet me in public with that much enthusiasm? “Hey, I’m so excited to meet you!” Nah, just… no. I want to meet someone who’s equally as weird as I am, who knows how to appreciate silence, and who won’t insist they have to go out every Friday night because that’s what "normal" people do. What even is normal? Normal is overrated. I could write poems about how overrated it is, but not in a public space. I couldn’t even do that in front of my roommate. I’d feel too exposed. Now I’m thinking about how much I value my freedom, though I can’t quite grasp what that means anymore. I feel so restless all the time. I know I have this huge potential inside me, but it feels like it’s tangled in a web of self-doubt and weird expectations. Do I really care what anyone thinks? I shouldn’t. So many thoughts and they’re all fighting for attention. It's exhausting.

I just got back from a really great lunch with Sarah. We went to that new place downtown where everything is super colorful and vibrant, and the food matches. I ordered the kale salad, which is kind of cliché but it was really good. I had to take a picture for Instagram, you know how it is. Everyone’s doing these things. It’s like a requirement now. I wonder if there’s an unwritten rule about posting food pics that I missed somewhere? The waiter was so friendly, though, like he genuinely wanted to chat. Made the experience even better. I love when people are just nice, it makes everything so much brighter. Being friendly makes life easier, right? Why wouldn’t you want to talk to someone? I can’t believe how quick everything is moving this semester. I have classes every day except Friday, and honestly, I’m okay with that. It gives me something to do, keeps me busy. I told my roommate the other day that I thrive on structure. I don’t understand how people can just go with the flow. I mean, I get it sometimes, but too much spontaneity stresses me out. I like my planners and calendars. When I see my week laid out in front of me, I feel accomplished already, even before I do anything. Prioritizing is where it’s at. It helps me stay super focused. Oh, and we have a group project in Psych this week. I’m kind of excited about it because we’re supposed to collaborate on this presentation about social interactions. I mean, it’s right up my alley! I can’t wait to bring in some real-life examples. I’ve been thinking about how people interact on social media versus in person. It’s wild how different things can be. I noticed that when I post something funny, it can get likes right away, but in person, you have to wait for reactions. It’s kind of fascinating to dig into what makes people tick, how they respond, and what makes them laugh. I’m pretty good at getting along with others. I’m good at picking up on vibes; I just seem to know what people want or need in the moment. I really enjoy working in groups. The energy is just different. You can feed off each other’s excitement, and even if someone has a crazy idea, it can spark something awesome. I think I find myself naturally becoming the one who organizes everything. I don’t mind taking charge; I think it brings out the best in everyone. Plus, there’s something fulfilling about being a leader, even if it’s just for a school project. I want to help everyone feel included because when they do, everybody has a more fun time. I try hard to make sure everyone’s voice is heard. But honestly, I’m also just super passionate about it. I guess that’s part of me being conscientious. I like checking off boxes, making sure everything is running smoothly. Eddie in class is pretty quiet. He seems nice though. I’m tempted to pull him into the group just to see how he thinks. Sometimes, I feel like I’m too bubbly, though. Like, does it annoy people when I’m too friendly? I don’t want to come off as overbearing. But then again, I can’t help it; it’s just how I am. I bet some people appreciate it. I hope he does. I could invite him to lunch next time, see if he opens up more. It’s cool getting to know different kinds of people; everyone has something to teach you. On a different note, I finally decided to join the student government. I’ve been thinking about it for a while. I guess I’m just ready to make a difference on campus. I want to take part in planning events, and I love bringing people together. We have a big welcome back event in the spring, and I’m already brainstorming themes. It’s so exciting – I have a million ideas. I want to do something fresh but also not too out there. Gotta keep it relatable, right? And speaking of relatable, I just realized I haven’t picked out my outfit for the weekend. What to wear for the party on Saturday? I have that cute red dress that makes me feel good. Everyone says red is a bold choice. I’m all for it; sometimes you just need to be a little daring. Must find a pair of shoes that match, though. I can’t go too crazy; I like sticking to what feels comfortable. Okay, I should wrap this up. I have to get back to studying for my midterms. Just a few subjects to hit between now and then, but I’m feeling good about it. Setting a study schedule always helps me stay on track. I like to reward myself with little breaks after each section. It’s all about balance. Focus, work hard, play harder. That’s my motto.

I can’t believe I forgot to bring my backpack again. It’s like I’m absent-minded lately, just rushing around. I can’t help it though, there’s so much going on. I really like being around people, but sometimes it gets overwhelming, like in the dining hall when everyone’s talking. When I walk in and see friends, it’s like a rush of energy, but then I want to chat with everyone and I can never settle. The noise levels rise and it gets chaotic. I wish I could just sit down and focus, but the laughter and chatter pull me in like a magnet. It’s hard not to engage. The food smells good too, what did I even eat yesterday? Right, that weird mix of pasta and salad, can you mix those? Why do we eat so many carbs? I saw my roommate piling fries on her plate yesterday, like, she’s not even worried. I’m freaking out over calories and body image while she’s just living her life. Must be nice. What time is it? I should probably check my phone. I feel like I’m glued to social media. The notifications are constant, and I can’t help but jump on Instagram every few minutes. Even TikTok has its grip on me. Those videos are addictive. I can lose hours scrolling and laughing. But then I feel anxious about what I’m missing or if I’m not as funny as those creators. I want to post something clever but can never think of anything. I just want to be liked, you know? Is that so wrong? I don’t think so. But then I get nervous about how people perceive me. Are they judging me? I can’t tell if I’m being too much or not enough. It’s like I walk a tightrope between being the life of the party and making sure I don’t overstep. Now my thoughts are all jumbled. I love hanging out, but there are days when I feel so fragile. What is it about those days? I can’t seem to pin it down. I’ll be laughing one moment and then suddenly feel this weight in my chest. I hate when that happens, especially when I’m with friends. They’ll ask me what’s wrong, and I can’t even explain, because it feels so silly. I might just be tired or overwhelmed by the energy. Or maybe it’s the pressure of academics. Can’t stand the idea of getting a bad grade. I seem to care even when I don’t want to. It’s annoying. College is supposed to be fun, right? Yet here I am, worrying about every single assignment. I see those people who don’t even seem to care about grades. How do they do it? Then there’s the whole group project situation. I mean, talk about stress. I hate depending on others, but I also love collaborating. Ugh. It’s like those friends who don’t pull their weight. Why is it always me who ends up doing extra work? I want everyone to be happy and succeed, but sometimes I want to shake them and say, "Come on, let’s get this done!" Last time I partnered with Sarah, she kept texting me about hangouts instead of the project. I know she wants to have fun, but come on, we have deadlines! Speaking of fun, I should really try to plan a party or something. I thrive in those environments. Last month’s party was epic, so much dancing and laughing. I was so pumped up just being around everyone. I want to recreate that. But then again, the stress of organizing it all makes me hesitate. I just wish someone would take charge. I want to be in charge but can’t stay organized long enough to follow through. I respect the people who can plan and make things happen. Like my friend Jen, she’s so good at that. I should ask her to co-host. Maybe it’ll be easier together. But then what if I annoy her? I don’t want to ruin the friendship over logistics, but I can’t do it alone. And then there’s the whole dating scene. Oh my god. It’s a mess. I mean, I want to meet someone but I freeze up when I try to flirt. Do I even know how? It’s like every time I see a cute guy, I turn into a blushing mess. How do people do it? It’s nerve-wracking! I keep putting myself out there, swiping on dating apps, but the conversations always fizzle out. Is it because I overthink my messages? I feel like a high schooler again, all awkward and unsure. Why is it so complicated? I want connection, but it just seems like another source of anxiety. I should reach out more, but what if they ghost me? I hate rejection. I’ve been really trying to be more open, but I guess it’s a process, feels like I want to be more spontaneous but then bam, the anxiety hits. Ugh! Okay, what’s next? I’ve spent too long just typing. Should probably wrap this up soon, but there’s so much more to say about life. College life is this strange whirlwind of socializing and stress and I’m just trying to ride the wave without drowning.

It's weird how sometimes my thoughts just race and other times they barely crawl along like they’re stuck in quicksand. I can think of a million things to say but then again I don’t want to say anything at all. Everything feels pointless sometimes. Like, why bother? My room is a mess, half-empty cans of soda litter my desk like some sad monument to procrastination. I really should clean that but cleaning is just so boring and I always think of all the things I could be doing instead but never actually do them. It's a cycle. I start scrolling through my phone, looking at memes, and then I think about how I’m just wasting time instead of studying or getting my life together. Essays are due, exams are coming, and yet here I am, typing random thoughts instead of actually being productive. Speaking of productivity, I always wonder what everyone else is doing. Do they actually have their lives together? I mean, do you even have to put effort into that? I see people hanging out, laughing, and it just seems so effortless for them. I can’t imagine being that social, feeling the need to go out and mingle. But then again, being alone can feel heavy sometimes. Like, does anyone really care? It’s exhausting to think about. I start thinking about group projects and how I hate when people expect me to carry the weight. If they just did their part, maybe it wouldn’t feel like I’m doing everything alone. Or do they even notice? Probably not. I bet they just assume I’m okay with it. And then there’s the anxiety. It creeps in like a fog, just when I think I’m okay, it’s suddenly there gnawing at me. What if I fail? What if I never get a good job? What if I disappoint everyone? I can feel my heart race, and all I want is some sort of reassurance, but it’s always just out of reach. I see people posting about their accomplishments, internships, trips, and I just sit there scrolling thinking, “Nice, good for you,” but inside I’m screaming with jealousy and self-doubt. Why can’t I just be like them? I remember last week, I went to this party that I didn’t even want to go to, friends coaxed me into it. The moment I walked in, I felt my stomach drop. It was loud, too many people, and I just wanted to slip away. Socializing is like a performance I never signed up for. I stood in the corner nursing a drink, trying to look casual, but I could feel the judgment, even if it was all in my head. People laughed and danced while I was just trying to blend into the background, but it felt impossible. All I could think about was how my hair looked and if someone was looking at me too long. They probably were, thinking I was weird. So many thoughts racing, it’s suffocating. If only I could just be more chill about everything. I see people making friends at the blink of an eye, but I hold onto every slight I’ve received like it’s a trophy. Someone said something rude once, and it’s replayed in my head for weeks. I convince myself I’m so awful at conversations, I can’t find the right words, and then I just shut down. Why did they even invite me anyway? I’m just here to fill a quota, or maybe they feel sorry for me. Or maybe no one even thinks about it at all, it’s just me spiraling again. I’ll lay awake at night, my mind racing through everything I messed up that day or the week before. It’s annoying, the way my brain works. I’m not sure if it’s always like this or if it’s just because I overthink every little detail. But sometimes there’s a flicker of hope. I think about what it’d be like to break free from all of this, a quiet life away from the noise. A library, endless books, maybe a cat or something. But then the reality crashes in again. I can’t even take care of myself, how would I handle a pet? I’d probably just forget to feed it. It’s ridiculous to even think that I could manage anything. I think maybe I should just stick to video games or binge-watching shows. At least they don’t require anything from me but the ability to sit in silence and zone out. It’s so much easier to do that than face the world. But then I hate myself for not trying, for not pushing through the noise, for being comfortable in my cocoon. What am I trying to say? Nothing, really. Maybe just that life feels like a heavy weight sometimes, and every day is just a juggling act that I’m failing at spectacularly. So now I’ll go back to staring at my mess of a room and just wish things were different without actually doing anything about it.

That clock on the wall is ticking too loudly, like it's judging me for not being productive enough. I wonder if everyone feels like they’re in a race every minute of every day. Sometimes I sit in class and feel like the quietest person in the room even when I’m thinking of contributing. What if I just blur out my thoughts? I usually think it’s better to hang back, wait for the right moment, you know? I’ve always been a good listener, like, I can sit there for hours and just absorb everything without having to say much. It’s a comforting thing to just agree with people too, but sometimes I wonder if that’s too passive. But who needs conflict? No drama for me, maybe I should start a blog about peaceful living. Just a place to share ideas and thoughts without the noise of arguments and negativity. Oh, but then again, sharing thoughts in a blog is also sort of like shouting into a void because who’s really listening? There must be a million blogs out there, so would mine even matter? I like the idea of collaborating with others though. I mean what if we start a community project? Tree planting or a book swap or something like that. It would be so fulfilling to see people come together for a purpose. I could get a bunch of people together, design some flyers, and hit the local coffee shops. I love the smell of fresh coffee! Do people even read flyers anymore? All this technology is overwhelming. Everyone's staring at their screens all the time. I sometimes lose track of time scrolling through random things. It’s kind of fascinating, though, how people open up online. It's a different kind of vulnerability. I get that. The anonymity is liberating. I’m all for expressing ideas, maybe that's why I like art so much. I love creating, it sparks something inside me, even if it’s just doodling in the margins of my notebooks during boring lectures. I doodle flowers and stars and spirals. It’s the small colors that brighten my day, and they reflect my mood somehow. But then I think about how not everyone sees that. Some people just want to get straight to the point. I mean, I get it, we’re all busy. But stepping back from the craziness of schedules, deadlines, and all those expectations is key, right? I’d rather take a long walk in the park than rush through life. The trees and flowers, they don’t care about deadlines. They just exist and bloom. Sometimes I wish I could just be a bird flying through the trees, no responsibilities, just gliding through the air. There’s something so freeing about the thought. And then again with travel. I could wander around the world, meet new people, learn new languages. Language is so beautiful, each one has its own rhythm, its own dance. Sometimes I think about picking up a new language. Maybe Spanish or something completely different like Japanese. There’s so much to learn and experience. What a rich tapestry of stories and cultures! I want to explore them all. Yet here I am, sitting at my desk, staring at a computer screen. But do I really want to jump into that whirlwind of crowds? A concert maybe? They’re always so full of energy. I like the thought of it, but the actual crowd is a bit much. I’d probably hang back and enjoy the music, but I wouldn't want to be up front where it feels chaotic. I think I’d be the one just swaying a bit, feeling the rhythm on the edges rather than right in the middle of the action. Oh, I need to go back to that book I started. It was about different philosophies and how they influence the way we live. It’s still sitting on my shelf, half-read. I should really dedicate more time to reading. But sometimes it’s so hard to focus when my mind wanders off to so many places. I want to dive into new ideas, new concepts, but it takes commitment, doesn’t it?
