A commitment that feels overwhelming at times. These thoughts just swim around in my head. Are they ever going to lead to something? I hope so. And I hope whenever I do put something out into the world, it reflects the good I see in it. The kindness and simplicity. It can’t just be about me. I want to make a connection, help others. I want to be a force for good. That must mean something. I feel like I’m always looking for that spark, that moment when everything comes together. Maybe it’s a journey, and that’s okay. I’ll embrace the winding roads. It reminds me that life is about the little things too.

Okay so I just hit this weird groove where my mind is racing and I can’t stop thinking about how many people I’ve met this week. The party Friday was wild. I just love being around people – it’s like I draw energy from their vibes. Met this guy named Kyle. He thinks he’s the next big thing in music. Whatever, that’s fine if he thinks that. I like calling out people’s BS sometimes. It keeps conversations lively. People love talking about themselves, and I’m here to listen but also throw a few curveballs. Like, “Oh really? You think that’s a good song? No way!” It gets them fired up. I’m just not super into being nice all the time. I guess that’s not my style. So much time wasted on politeness when I could be getting to the good stuff—like their true opinions on pineapple on pizza. Speaking of food, I still can’t believe how many toppings people want on their pizza these days. What’s up with that? Less is more. Just give me cheese and pepperoni and let me enjoy the classics. Out of control is what it feels like, I swear. Oh, and don’t even get me started on the kale craze. Like, come on, if you’re in a pizza joint, you’re there to eat not to pretend you’re on some cleanse. Come to think of it, I don’t even care about cleanses. I just want to eat whatever I feel like when I’m hungry. Ugh, I need to plan my week better but planning feels like such a chore. I always end up just winging it. Like, I’m great at spontaneous plans. Hit me up and say, “Hey, want to grab lunch?” and I’m down. But seriously, if you asked me to pencil something in? It’s not happening. I’d rather be living in the moment, whatever that means. So what if I missed a few deadlines? Let’s not focus on that. My roommate, Sam, is like the opposite. She color codes her planner, has notes everywhere and honestly, I kind of admire that even though I would never do it. Sometimes I feel bad for her—like she’s always stressing about getting things done when I’m just living my best life. But I guess that’s just how she is, and how I am too. It’s not a bad thing, it’s just different. Can’t everyone just chill a little? I mean, I’m not trying to be rude; I just think people sometimes need to take a breather. And why do people expect me to be their cheerleader? I’m not responsible for your happiness. I’m happy, right? Like, I’m not here to validate your life choices. If you’re sad about a breakup, that’s tough, but let’s grab drinks and stop dwelling on it. I did that last week with my friend Alex, and it was fun! An hour later, we were laughing about how dumb her ex was and how she deserved better. That’s the vibe I’m into. Who has time for moping around when there’s a world to explore and things to experience? Oh, and I really want to go to that concert next month. A bunch of my friends are going, but I was thinking about just showing up to the venue whenever. Why wait for a group when I can just walk in and enjoy the music? So what if I go solo? I don’t mind getting lost in a crowd, feeling the bass thumping in my chest. There’s something magical about music that just connects us, right? That high-energy rush—it’s intoxicating. Remember when I hit that karaoke night with some random people? I ended up singing “Livin’ on a Prayer” and I swear, everyone was joining in. It was crazy fun. That’s the thing about being around people; it can spark these wild connections. You just start singing together, laughing, bonding over something silly. I think that’s the best part of life; all the random moments where you don’t even need to plan anything special. Just being there and experiencing it. Work? Who cares about that? I mean, I have a job, but I’m not married to it. People are always stressing about their 9 to 5s when they could just be living a little. I like hanging out, going places, living like there’s no tomorrow. I’d rather be at a bar with friends than stuck behind a desk staring at a screen. Life’s too short for that nonsense. Let’s go out, hit the town, make ridiculous memories. So okay, the study thing? I guess if they want to see how I handle all this social stuff, good luck to them. I’m just here for the laughs, the chaos, and every little moment that pops up. Who needs a plan when life is this vibrant? Let’s just embrace the crazy and enjoy the ride.

okay so I’ve been thinking about this assignment and how I really don’t like group projects they’re the worst. people never do their part and then I end up doing everything and it’s so frustrating I don’t get why it’s so hard for them to just follow a schedule or something. deadlines matter. it’s like everyone else thinks time is just this fluid thing and it bothers me like do they really not care or are they just so disorganized? I don’t get it. I have my planner, color-coded and everything, and if I stick to it, I can get so much done. but they just... don’t care. I’ve always been like this, I remember even in high school I’d keep track of my grades, would make lists for everything. I think it’s just logical to plan things out, but people are always like “oh just relax” and I really don’t know how to respond to that. like I am relaxed but I also have to get things done. what’s the deal with social events, too? I mean why do people love them? they’re exhausting, all the small talk and trying to connect with people I don’t even know. I’d rather be at home organizing my bookshelf or doing some random research online. the internet is fascinating, so many topics and I feel like I could deep dive into so many things. right now I’m actually trying to learn more about data organization and analysis. it feels productive. last week I spent hours looking up various databases and how to interpret data and it was great. no socializing required, just pure focus. I think that’s where I thrive. some people need nightlife or parties or whatever to feel fulfilled but not me. I get so tired just thinking about it plus people always ask questions that lead nowhere and it feels like a waste of time. I just want to have meaningful conversations, is that too much to ask? and then there’s always the weather. I mean I feel like I talk about it a lot because it’s such a neutral topic. today was cold and that makes me feel a bit more productive; I can wrap up in a blanket and work. the rain is kind of nice for studying too. but then there are those people who think rainy days are sad and it really doesn’t have to be like that. they get all mopey and I just don’t get it. I like how cozy it is, how everything smells fresh afterward. I’m just a bit of a homebody I guess. it’s not that I can’t function socially, I just prefer it the other way. so this study I’m in is about personality types and I’m just so aware of how low I score on extraversion and how low I am on agreeableness, but I think that’s a good thing for certain situations. I don’t need to please everyone or always fit in. I have my own opinions and I’m fine with standing by them even if people don’t like it. I know it can make me seem standoffish but honestly sometimes people just need to hear the truth even if it’s uncomfortable. I feel like most people would rather keep the peace than actually address issues, which is why group work is such a pain. I’m not going to sugarcoat things just to make someone feel good. then again, that conscientiousness is my strong suit. I get things done and I’m reliable. part of me wishes people appreciated that more, but I guess they’re more focused on their own drama or whatever. like I can’t even keep track of half the nonsense that goes on in my classmates’ lives, it’s too messy. sometimes I wish I could shut it all out. but being low on openness makes it hard to handle change. I like routine. I wake up at the same time every day, I eat similar things, and I study in specific blocks because it’s what works. what I don’t get is why everyone else has to disrupt that with their spontaneous plans. I’ve had friends who just drop everything for an impromptu trip or whatever, and I’m just like why? how? don’t they see that it’s all chaotic? I need structure. I respect people who are well-organized but they seem to be more of a rarity. sometimes I think it’s a lost art, like how did we get to the point where people don’t even plan their week ahead? it’s like they leave it all to chance. I can’t do that. I can’t even fathom doing that. there’s something to be said for stability and knowing what to expect. I guess I just keep circling back to how I can explain myself to others. not that I owe them an explanation but still I’m constantly assessing and coming to terms with who I am. why can’t they just see that it’s okay to be different?

Okay so I just remembered that time last week when we all went out for tacos and somehow spent three hours just laughing and talking about the most random stuff like who would win in a fight between a bear and a shark and then someone suggested a bear in the water which was hilarious and so random I mean a bear would never even think about swimming unless it was for food right? Anyway, I just love how easy it is to connect with my friends – like we just click immediately, you know? And I could talk for hours, it feels good to share ideas and laugh together. People sometimes say I’m too much, too loud, but I don’t care, I love being around people, the more the merrier, right? Then there's that one class I’m taking this semester, it's super boring and the professor drones on and on, I can't even remember what we talked about last week. I mean, who cares about the history of some economics theories? But I still go cause the people in that class are funny, we always end up making jokes in the back and the way they look at me when I make some dumb comment, it just makes my day. I would rather be with them than sitting at home alone. Speaking of home, my roommate is pretty chill, we vibe well together but she’s always like “can you keep it down?” when I’m laughing too loud on the phone. It’s not my fault I’m just having a good time, right? So I’m planning this party for next weekend, it’s gonna be awesome. I’ve never actually done this before but I just feel like putting it together will be a blast. I don’t even care if it’s messy or if people don’t show up, I just want to hang out, dance a little, you know, and eat snacks. Snacks are like, essential. I’m thinking chips and salsa, maybe some brownies too. Gotta have sweets! And I'll probably end up making way too much because I always do, but that's fine. I just get really excited thinking about it, like, who doesn’t love a good party? It’s a chance to let loose and just be yourself. I mean, people always come together when there’s food involved, right? I’ll probably forget most of the music because I’m so busy chatting, but I’ll throw together a playlist with the classics that everyone likes. Then there’s that guy from bio class, Jeff, he’s super funny and I think he might ask me to study with him. He was joking about getting pizza and I just laughed and said “why not?” but then I realized that I’m probably going to be the one talking more than studying, I can’t help it! If I could just focus on something for like five seconds straight. Ugh, but that’s fine, it’s better to have fun and connect than to be a perfectionist about grades, right? Who needs perfect grades when you have good vibes? I keep thinking about that one time we went hiking – oh wait, is it called hiking? I don’t even know, we just kind of walked up a hill and ended up at a really great view. I was so pumped because everyone was there, and we took a bunch of pictures. I love capturing moments like that, but at the same time, I just want to live in the moment instead of staring at the screen all the time. But every time my phone buzzes, I can’t resist checking it. Social media calls to me like a moth to the flame. I mean, who doesn’t want to know what everyone is doing? I love scrolling through stories and seeing what my friends are up to, and it makes me feel connected even when I’m not there. My friend just texted me about a karaoke night next week, and I’m like “yes please” because why wouldn’t I want to belt out some random pop songs and make a fool of myself? Sometimes I think it’s crazy how much energy I have, it’s like I can’t sit still. I just want to go and have fun, why waste time overthinking things? People are always telling me to chill out, but come on, life’s too short to be serious all the time. I sometimes wonder if I should be more serious about certain things, like job stuff or future plans, but whenever I start thinking about it too much, my brain just goes blank. I can’t come up with anything legit, I guess I’m just too busy living in the moment. Friends are my anchor, and as long as I have them around, everything seems less daunting. All I know is I want more nights filled with laughter and tacos, so here’s to that.

okay so I just remembered that time at that party where we all ended up playing that ridiculous game with the giant inflatable chicken like what even was that I don't think I’ve laughed that hard in a long time so absurd but everyone was into it and it’s like that’s what I love about parties the spontaneity of it all I’m not into the same conversations about the weather or school stuff like can we get deeper or just do something totally out there like jumping on a trampoline or karaoke on a whim because why not I love that feeling of just throwing caution to the wind and seeing where it goes my friend Katie just does that she walks into a room and suddenly it feels electric we’ll be talking to people and she’s like “hey let’s do a dance-off” and I’m like yeah let’s let’s go for it and suddenly it’s competitive but also silly and who cares if we look ridiculous because that’s the point right? Finding joy in the madness. I start thinking about how some people take life too seriously like they’re always glued to their phones or staring into space and I get why sometimes but come on there’s so much fun to be had, like the other day I was in class and the professor was droning on about something I can’t even remember what it was but it wasn’t interesting at all so I started doodling on my notebook and then this idea hit me what if I just threw a party for the people in that class just like a huge blowout where we could all just unwind and not think about assignments or grades or whatever but just have fun but how do you even plan that out I don’t even know but if someone else could take the wheel I'd be in I'd buy the snacks and bring the music like who doesn’t love music and dancing and suddenly you’re not just classmates anymore you’re friends or at least you are for that night and isn’t that what it’s about connecting with people then I get distracted thinking about that one classmate who always has their headphones in like do they ever listen to anything other than music do they even talk to people or do they not realize how awesome it is to just joke around with someone for five minutes before class starts it’s like you miss so much if you just stay in your bubble even if it’s a comfy bubble sometimes it’s worth it to pop it just for a second to see what’s going on outside like the other day I sat next to someone I’d never spoken to before and we ended up bonding over this shared love for that weird indie film that came out last year and it’s crazy how fast it happened we were laughing and sharing memes and suddenly it’s like I had a new friend and I don’t get how some people are okay with just having the same small group for years when there’s so many people out there but then maybe they do find some comfort in familiarity ugh and speaking of comfort sometimes I wish I could press pause on my life and just dive into a new city or country I daydream about traveling everywhere like one day I’ll just book a flight to Tokyo or Barcelona and I’ll just go and eat street food and get lost in the streets and meet locals who show you the secrets of their culture I love how every place has its own vibe like New Orleans is wild with the energy and colors and the music you can breathe it but then New York is like this rush of people and opportunity it makes my heart race just thinking about it I kind of live for those moments when you meet someone from a different world and they open your eyes to a new perspective but you have to be willing to just let go of your own expectations and embrace the chaos yeah the chaos is where the magic is even if it's a little terrifying sometimes heh I can’t help but be a little chaotic myself like last week I signed up for that improv class even though I’d never done anything like that before but why not right? I mean what’s the worst that could happen it’s just a bunch of people being ridiculous together and sometimes I just want to laugh and do something different and maybe I’ll totally bomb at it but even that could be funny so who cares but I can’t believe how many people avoid risks like they’re afraid of failing when in reality failing can be the best teaching moment the stories you can tell from the mess-ups that’s where the growth is but I like to think that I can handle whatever comes my way even if I get a little disorganized or forgetful because spontaneity is what makes life so vibrant and colorful I want a life that feels like a whirlwind of experiences that I can look back on and think wow I really lived it all not just sat back and watched life go by because there’s so much out there and I want to soak it all up every drop the laughter the highs the lows and everything in between

Okay, so I was just thinking about how I love being around people, like, seriously, there’s nothing better than a big group of friends just hanging out, joking around, I mean those moments when everyone is laughing and there's that shared energy in the air, like at a party last weekend getting everyone hyped up over some silly game, it felt so alive you know? I thrive on that energy, it’s like oxygen, I can't imagine being stuck in a room alone for too long, I'd go crazy or something. I was talking to my roommate about this, how different we are, he likes his alone time and that's cool, but I just want to be out there, meeting new people, telling stories, every time I meet someone new I feel a buzz, like learning their life quirks and what makes them tick. Then again, I do have this tendency to forget about things I’m supposed to be doing, like I’ll start cleaning and then get distracted by a YouTube video or someone texts me and suddenly I’m deep in a conversation about an event coming up, what’s the plan, who’s going, and I get so wrapped up in the fun that I’ll just completely lose track of time. Like, I was supposed to study for an exam last week, and instead I ended up organizing a movie night, I mean, everyone was saying yes and I couldn’t pass that up, who would want to miss out on a night with popcorn and laughter? But then I was scrambling to catch up last minute, and it kind of stressed me out but not in a bad way, more like an exciting challenge. And oh my god, the other day I saw this cute dog at the park, and I had to stop and pet it because how can anyone resist a dog, right? The owner was super chill too, and we started chatting, I like how easy it is to make connections with random people, like how a dog can open up a whole conversation, it’s amazing how small things can lead to fun interactions. I sometimes wonder if people realize how just saying hi to someone can change the course of their day, like, if you're in a hurry but take that second to acknowledge someone, you might brighten their mood or even make a friend. And then there are those moments that don’t go as planned. Like when someone doesn’t vibe with the rest of the group, it happens and I can’t help but try to include them, it’s like I want to make everyone feel welcome because I know how hard it can be to be the odd one out. But sometimes, those efforts backfire, and they’re moody or just don’t get the jokes, which is such a bummer, but I mean, I can’t change who they are, right? Just yesterday, we had a gathering and one friend was acting all grumpy about the music choice, I tried suggesting a couple of songs to lighten the mood, but they were just not having it, I guess everyone has their off days. Oh! Speaking of gatherings, I’m already thinking of the next party, maybe I can get a big blow-up movie screen in the backyard, I love the idea of outdoor movies, popcorn, fairy lights, bringing that chill vibe. I need to nail down a good theme too, because that always gets people excited, I could do something totally random like ‘80s night and just blast retro jams and dress up, I could see everyone loving that. Planning it is half the fun, I just have to get people to pitch in, I know I can convince them to help if I just keep pushing it, people love to join forces for a fun project, and I’m pretty good at rallying the troops, it’s like a game to see who can bring what. But then there’s the cleanup afterward, and oh wow, that’s the part where I usually just try to walk away and hope for the best, like, hey, I’m not the only one here, right? If everyone chips in, it can’t be that bad, plus, I think it’s worth it for the memories we create, some people take it way too seriously though, like the ones who clean up while the party's still going, totally kills the vibe. I like to ride that wave of fun till the end, who cares about a little mess, that’s life, isn't it? I know I’ll look back and remember the laughter over how messy it was later, that’s what it’s all about, good connections, new friends, making the most of every moment, even if it means neglecting that pile of laundry in the corner, it’ll be there later. I just need to keep pushing for those good times, and everything else can sort itself out.

Okay so I’ve been thinking about how social situations can be so draining yet so exhilarating at the same time like I can’t help but dive into a conversation with someone but then I get overwhelmed by their energy and it’s like a rollercoaster of emotions every time like I might be the life of the party but then I’m also that person who needs to recharge alone after it’s just so weird how that works sometimes I wonder if it’s a problem or just the way I’m wired maybe I thrive on chaos and excitement but my brain flips into overdrive and I can’t keep up with my own thoughts sometimes like I’m juggling too many balls in the air and one of them is bound to drop but I always manage to catch them all because I’m conscientious like that and I hate letting things fall through the cracks. I keep thinking of that time at a friend’s birthday party when I just talked to everyone like I was the host even though it wasn’t my party then I ended up in this deep conversation about philosophy and life choices with this guy I barely knew and by the time I looked around, no one else was around it was just us in this intense bubble of conversation while the rest of the party faded into the background I felt super connected but at the same time super anxious like did I dominate the conversation was I too much do people even like me or are they just being polite and smiling because they feel obligated. And I wonder if I even care deep down like I like being liked but I don’t think I need everyone’s approval but is that just me talking big because I’m projecting confidence that I don’t always feel I mean it’s exhausting keeping up a façade and maintaining social relationships all the time I can’t help but feel like I’m always on some kind of performance stage or something even when I’m not trying to be and I think about how social media amplifies that feeling like I’m comparing myself to other people’s highlight reels and that nagging feeling creeps in like am I doing enough am I engaging enough am I interesting enough or do I just come off as too harsh because I know I can be direct and blunt I don’t sugarcoat things and sometimes I think maybe I should tone it down but that just seems fake and I can’t deal with fake. I keep a planner. Everything is scheduled. I won’t let anything slip by because I need structure to counteract my chaotic thoughts it gives me the illusion of control and sometimes I wish I could just let go and be spontaneous but then I think about how my to-do list is never-ending and I feel guilty for not crossing everything off every day it’s like a constant pressure pushing me to succeed and I love the thrill of achieving my goals but it can be suffocating too what if I fail what if this next project doesn’t go as planned then I spiral into insecurity and doubt like how did I even get to this point when did my inner critic get so loud I wish I could silence it but it’s like having an annoying roommate who just never shuts up. Remember that time traveling to that concert? I couldn’t stop talking about it to everyone for weeks afterward I was so high on the experience and I brought everyone with me by sharing every detail but then I look back and think was I overbearing or did I just want everyone to feel how alive I felt in that moment the music was so intoxicating and I felt so free like I could conquer the world but then the next day the crash came and I was alone with my thoughts again feeling like I had to plan the next social outing to feel that rush again but no one can match that thrill and that’s when my anxiety kicked in because what if it’s just a cycle and I’m never satisfied do I seek validation in friendships or am I just really trying to find my place amid the noise. And yet I crave experiences I want to explore new places and meet new people but what if I can’t just be me without the mask of confidence covering my insecurities like I’m constantly riding this wave of openness where I want to dive right into everything yet I’m scared of the consequences scared of being misunderstood or judged maybe I should just embrace it all the chaos the uncertainty the emotional whirlwind because it’s all part of being human right? But why can’t I just chill and let it be what it is without analyzing every tiny detail and worrying about the outcome. Sigh. I need a break from all this like a retreat from my mind but it never shuts up. I think I’ll need to call someone later just to let the thoughts out maybe that’ll help or will it just lead to more spiraling—ugh who knows. Maybe I’ll just binge something on Netflix and zone out for a while I could use that as a distraction at least until the next big social situation rolls around.

So I was just thinking about that time I went to that party last weekend. It was wild, like a complete whirlwind of laughter and music and people everywhere—it’s like the universe just exploded with energy. I love that feeling, when the bass drops and everyone’s on the same wavelength, you know? It’s like I can feel the pulse of the room and it’s addictive. I can’t stand quiet, dull moments; I just want to dive into chaos and joy. That’s my jam. You know what else? I met this random guy named Jake, he just walked up and started talking about video games and how he’s streaming now. I didn’t even know who he was but he had this awesome energy, so I just started riffing off him. We ended up discussing everything from pixel art to philosophy — total different subjects but it flowed naturally. I mean, I should probably be more cautious about who I engage with, but it’s like, why waste time on negativity or judgement? It helps to be open, like really open. I feel like life is too short to be sitting around judging others. The other day, I was in a group chat with my friends and they’re all worried about their grades and getting internships, and I get that’s important but a part of me just wanted to shake them and say “What about living? What about experiences?" It frustrates me—but I guess they’re just different. I’m all about chasing thrills and stories, and they’re worried about the future. I mean, I get it. It can be overwhelming, but I think they miss the point. I want to travel, explore new places. Last summer I went to that random beach festival on the coast, and it was just the best. I met a bunch of travelers, and we spent the whole week just being free. No plans—just vibes. I’m regretting not taking that spontaneous trip to New Orleans this winter. My friend had mentioned it, but I was like “Nah, I should probably save money.” But thinking about it now, I could've gotten some crazy stories, some delicious food experiences, and met people with wild tales. Opportunities like that don’t come around every day. I don't know why I ever hesitate. I need to push myself more. And then there are these random thoughts about my classes—it’s so hard to concentrate sometimes. I keep doodling during lectures, especially the Philosophy 101 class, which is ironic because I love the subject. But I just want to jump in and debate everything. It’s like, why can’t we just discuss ideas instead of sitting in structured rows? Why not make it more interactive? I could absolutely do with fewer rules. If I ran that class, we would have debates, and everyone would just share random ideas without worrying about grades. Oh, and the other day I was in a thrift shop hunting for treasures, and I found this amazing vintage jacket. It was like screaming my name. It’s so eclectic with these weird patches—totally the kind of thing that makes a statement. I love wearing things that spark conversations. Like, one time I wore this shirt that had a weird graphic on it, and someone approached me just to ask about it, which started a whole discussion about art styles. It was awesome! Why can’t life be more like that? I’d wear something that makes people think. I can’t help but wonder what it would be like if everyone just threw their hair down, you know? Just let loose and stopped caring about being perfect or fitting in. Society is so caught up in boxes and rules, but I want to break all of them. I just want to feel that thrill of freedom that comes when you throw caution to the wind and just embrace the moment. That’s where the magic happens, right? I want to be that person who creates experiences that you can’t find in textbooks or neatly organized academic lectures. Who cares about being agreeable? Sometimes you just need to shake things up and challenge opinions. I don’t mind stirring the pot. Life’s too vibrant and chaotic to not be honest, and I’m all about being real. I’m not saying I need to be rude, but there’s something liberating about being frank and open. Like that time during a group project when everyone was tiptoeing around the real issues, and I just blurted out the truth. The looks on their faces were priceless! I could dive into new hobbies, I’ve been thinking of picking up photography. Just snapping moments that capture joy and spontaneity. My phone’s full of candid pictures of friends being silly—we need to remember those moments, those spur-of-the-moment adventures! I feel like life is a series of snapshots waiting to be taken, and I want to collect as many memories as possible. Okay, I should probably wrap it up, but these thoughts just keep bouncing around in my head. I can’t help but get excited about everything and anything.

That new coffee shop opened down the street and it’s such a great vibe. Super friendly people, I love how they greet everyone by name like we’re all family. I’ve been there three times already this week, and I swear I’ve already made friends with half the staff. I should probably ask them if they have a punch card or some rewards system. A dollar off after every ten lattes or something, right? Oh, I need to remember to tell Sarah about it. She loves trying new coffee spots, but this one has that cozy atmosphere that just feels right. And then there’s the guy who always sits in the corner with his laptop. He’s there every time I go and I’m kind of curious what he’s working on. I wanna ask him but my brain is like, is that too forward? What if he’s busy? But then again, I could just strike up a conversation and be like “Hey, what’s up?” I mean, what’s the worst that could happen? I feel like people love chatting in coffee shops. It’s more social than just passing someone on the street. Speaking of Sarah, we have plans this weekend. She wants to go to that outdoor festival. I love festivals; there’s always so much to do and see. So many booths with cool stuff, local vendors selling handmade crafts, food trucks everywhere, live music, it all gets me so excited. I’ve gotta remember to bring cash, though. Vendors like cash. Plus, it’s easier for donating to the little causes they always have going on. I like to donate a couple bucks when I can. It feels good, doesn’t it? Helping people out even in small ways. And then there’s the food. Oh my god, the food. I love trying new snacks, especially if they have something unique. There’s this booth that sells gourmet grilled cheese. I’m gonna hunt that down. I can already smell it. Just the thought of melted cheese and crispy bread—it’s mouthwatering. I hope I don’t get too full and miss out on dessert. I need to remember to pace myself, though. After the festival, we’re planning to go out for dinner. I think we’ll hit up that new Italian place she mentioned. Authentic pasta. I feel like I can already taste their marinara sauce. It better be good. I’m picky with my Italian food. It’s got to be just right or I’ll be disappointed. I hope they have a nice wine selection too! Sarah is great company, though, and it won’t matter too much how the food is because we always have fun together. I love how upbeat she is. I can’t wait to share all this with my friends. There’s something so satisfying about telling stories and hearing their reactions. Nothing beats a good laugh. It’s interesting how our group gets along so well. We kind of balance each other out. I’m the one who keeps things lively, and then there’s Jason, who’s more laid-back, keeps us grounded, and Mia is just full of amazing ideas that always get us pumped. I can’t help but feel good about our friendship. We’re all supportive, always hyping each other up. Oh, I should probably text Mia about the festival and see if she wants to come along too. The more the merrier, right? Could be such a fun group outing! I don’t want her to feel left out. She’s been talking a lot about wanting to get out more. I get it. Life can get so busy with classes and work. Sometimes you just need to recharge with friends and fun activities, especially when the weather is nice. I’ll bet it’s beautiful out there. Sometimes I think a lot about how important it is to have those connections. And not just fun hangouts but also being there for each other during tough times. Just the other day, I helped Jason through a rough patch. Just being a good listener. It felt rewarding to know that I was there when he needed me. I mean, I love being that supportive person! It’s part of who I am. I don’t even want it to be a big deal; it’s just natural. And who doesn’t love a good excuse to get together? Whether it’s a festival, a casual dinner, or just hanging out at someone’s place with games, it’s all good in my book. Speaking of which, we should do a game night soon too. I’m thinking board games, the usual suspects—Catan, maybe some Cards Against Humanity. Nothing beats the laughter that comes from those chaotic moments! I could go on and on about all this. Life feels so full, and I love that energy. I don’t mind being around people all the time. It’s where I shine! I like being there for others, making new connections. The world can feel overwhelming sometimes, but it’s all about those moments we share. I feel lucky to have a solid circle of friends around me.

Okay so I was thinking about how much I love stumbling across new ideas, like that time I read about this ancient civilization where they traded salt for gold and I just got lost in that rabbit hole for like a whole day, can’t believe how deep this history goes, and then I thought about how we take so many things for granted, like water or air, but people were literally trading it, there was a time when that was a thing, and now we just turn on the tap and don’t even think. Why do we take everything for granted? It’s strange. I wonder if that’s where consumerism comes from or if it’s just about wanting more than we need, can’t it just be about wanting to explore? I wish I could just hop on a plane and go to the places that light me up, but then again, sometimes the thought of interacting with all those people kind of tires me. So much energy goes into socializing and I guess it’s not that I don’t like it but I just don’t see the point sometimes? Like, small talk just feels so shallow, can’t we just dive into the good stuff? But then I feel bad, like I want to be nice and agreeable, I don’t want people to feel uncomfortable around me, but what if we could skip all that? Talking about the weather is just a waste of time when there’s a whole universe of topics out there. Speaking of which, there’s this documentary about the ocean I’ve been meaning to watch, it seems so fascinating, like how deep does it really go? When they say unexplored, what does that even mean? My mind keeps wandering to images of colorful fish and coral reefs, I want to see that but I also dread the idea of being on a crowded beach, ugh, just too many people. Sometimes I think I’d rather just sit in my room and read about it all, dive into a book instead of the sea. Books are safe, they don’t judge, they just take you to places you never knew existed. But then, there are these times when I’m just drawn to art, like going to a gallery and feeling those feelings wash over me, or the weird thrill that comes when I see something that doesn’t make sense but somehow resonates. The color! The shapes! It’s like opening a door to someone’s mind and stepping right in. But, on the other hand, being around too many people at once can be overwhelming and sometimes I just want to retreat to my thoughts, you know? I can get lost in my thoughts for hours, sometimes writing them down helps me process them, but sometimes I just let them wander. And then I think about how fleeting everything is. Tomorrow? It’s just a day, but it’s always got too much pressure and expectations, why can’t we just have a blank slate? Even in college, the pressure to perform and get everything right, it’s insane. I mean, grades are just numbers and everyone’s in this rat race while I’m just standing there like why is this a thing? Let’s talk about ideas or culture or philosophy, but nope, it’s always about exams and deadlines. I do wish I were more organized, though. Sometimes I can’t find my notes or I forget about assignments until it’s too late, and even though I’m agreeable, I can’t help but feel a little frustrated with myself. I want to get it together, but then again, structure feels so restrictive, right? I like the freedom of waking up and deciding what to explore today, even if that means indulging in music or a random YouTube video. Oh, the music! I’ll follow a genre or an artist down the rabbit hole, and the way sounds can evoke memories, like the first time I heard that particular song that took me back to summer nights with no responsibilities. I’d love to go listen to live music again but I guess that also feels like a social obligation rather than genuine interest. I want to have deep conversations with people who understand, not just talk about the latest trends or what’s cool. But what’s cool anyway? It changes every week, and I can’t keep up. I think about how every person I meet could have a different perspective, and that’s such a magical thing. I want to absorb that, to learn from it, but what if I'm just standing there listening and not really participating? Is that even okay? I wonder if that’s part of the reason I’m low on extraversion. Not that it’s bad; it’s just me. But even in my quiet moments, there’s this vast world of thought. Sometimes I think I could create something amazing, just with my imagination. Man, I get carried away.

Okay so I just got a text from my friend Sarah asking if I want to join her for that new Italian place downtown. I heard it’s amazing! I love trying new restaurants. Food is literally my favorite way to spend time with friends. I hope they have good pasta. I mean, who doesn’t love pasta? It must be around 5 PM but I’m not even looking at the clock. I should probably check my planner too because I always forget stuff. Planning is key. I can’t stand it when I forget to write down something important. Like, I need to pick up my laundry after class tomorrow. I wonder if I should ask Sarah if she wants to join me for that too? She’s always down for a quick errand after hanging out. I was cleaning my room earlier, and it’s such a weird mix of things. I have textbooks piled up, random shoes, and some of my workout gear just lying around. Why do I have so many sneakers? I guess I like to have options. Nothing wrong with being prepared! I’ve got those bright blue ones I never wear and then my black pair that’s falling apart. I should probably just toss the blue ones, but they’re so cute! I feel like I have a connection to them. Okay wait, I just remembered I need to text my group for the project. I hope everyone is as on top of this as I am. Group projects can be chaotic, but I like to keep things organized. I really want us to get a good grade. It was funny last week when we all met up to brainstorm. Jenna brought cupcakes, and I can’t resist sweets. I have a sweet tooth! I should probably cut back on that, but it’s just so delightful. Like, if someone offers me dessert, I’m definitely not turning it down. I wonder if I’ll ever grow out of that. Probably not. Maybe I should get into baking instead that would be fun. Bake-offs with friends would be a great way to bond! I can see it now, everyone in the kitchen laughing and helping each other. I don’t know how I’d deal with a serious competition, though. I hate pressure. Ugh, speaking of pressure, exams are coming up. I need to make sure I study in advance this time. I really want to avoid that last-minute cramming. It stresses me out too much. I found this great study method where you break your notes into smaller sections and review them over days rather than trying to cram it all in one night. It’s so much more effective. I wish everyone did it this way. I mean, not everyone can be as organized as me. That's fine! I really like helping my friends out when they’re stressed. Like, I’ll sit down and study with them, make sure they understand the important stuff. I can’t help it! I remember last semester when Mel was freaking out about a sociology paper. She wasn’t sure how to start, and I literally just sat next to her and went through ideas. I think she ended up getting an A! I felt so proud. It feels good to help people. It’s kind of my thing, you know? Nothing beats that feeling of seeing someone succeed. Oh, I still need to buy a birthday gift for my roommate. Her birthday's in a week! I already got her a cute card, but now I’m torn on the gift. Should I get her a nice piece of jewelry or a cozy sweater? She loves comfy clothes. I could go with the sweater and do something personal like a handwritten note. I’ll think about it more later. I like the idea of putting thought into gifts. It shows you care. I have a scanner app on my phone now, which makes it so easy to keep track of my notes. I can’t believe I didn’t use it sooner! I always end up with paper all over the place. This definitely gives me some peace of mind. Oh, and I’ve been looking into joining that campus volunteering club. I think it would be a great way to meet more people and be productive. Helping out in the community sounds perfect. I enjoy working with others, and it seems like something I’d be good at. Plus, I love the opportunity to connect with different people. Gosh, I need to stop thinking about all of this at once! Anyway, I’m excited about dinner later. I hope Sarah’s into sharing because I want to try a bit of everything. I mean, what’s the point of eating out if you can’t sample a few dishes, am I right? Sharing food is the best part of dining out. I guess that’s a little bit of my extroverted side coming out—always wanting to be surrounded by friends and enjoying every moment. I just love being social! Life is too short not to enjoy it with others.

It's Thursday evening and I’m sitting here with a bunch of thoughts swirling. I should be studying for my psychology exam. Or I could be scrolling through Twitter, but what’s the point? It’s always the same stuff, just recycled content that makes me anxious. My phone buzzes—it's a notification from a group chat. I dread those. What if I don’t have anything to say? What if they’re all making plans and I’m not invited? I get that sinking feeling, like I’m missing out, but I also just don’t want to go out. It’s exhausting. I think about the last time I was at a party. It was overwhelming. The noise, the lights, the crowd. The pressure to be “on.” I spent most of the time on the couch, scrolling my phone while pretending to enjoy the conversation. It was more comfortable that way. At least I could disengage when it got too much. I wonder if that makes me weird? I mean, what is it with everyone wanting to be around so many people all the time? But then I feel bad too because my friends really want me there. They genuinely miss me. I feel this tug between wanting to please them and not wanting to push myself too far into social situations that drain me. They’re happy and fun, and it makes me feel even more anxious about my own tendencies. I should probably go to therapy again. It’s been a while. I know I’m neurotic, and there are days when that voice in my head gets louder and louder, telling me I’m not enough. Not enough fun. Not enough engaging. Just not enough. But then I think about the things I am curious about, like that new exhibit at the museum or the book club that meets every week. I love learning, exploring ideas, getting lost in thoughts. Yet, the same curiosity that drives me also leads me down paths that spiral out of control, like the time I tried to understand existentialism and got stuck there for weeks, questioning everything. I have so many ideas, but I’m too scattered to follow through. One moment I’m fascinated by art, next I'm delving deep into philosophy and then suddenly I’m trying to draw but it’s a mess. Who knew doodling could become an anxiety trigger? I try to draw a flower and it turns into this weird blob and I think about how I’ve disappointed myself again. My mind races back to all those guidelines from art classes—don’t focus too much on perfection, but there I go, crumpling the paper. What if I could capture the chaos instead? I think about that a lot. Maybe I could create a project about feelings, raw and unfiltered. I could use color, movement, texture. But then my mind flips. What would my professor think? Would they see it as a real project or just another stream of consciousness? Is that all I am? A stream of thoughts that never lands anywhere? I want to create but there’s this internal critic that judges every little thing I try. Switching gears. I remember when I joined that hiking club. I felt so excited, but then dread hit me on the day of the first hike. What if I’m slow? What if I can’t keep up? Everyone’s climbing those mountains like they were born on those trails and I’m huffing and puffing. It was beautiful, though. Nature has a way of soothing those frazzled thoughts, like the time I found a quiet spot by the lake and just sat there for hours, watching the water ripple, allowing my mind to drift. That little moment of peace felt like a slice of heaven, but then the thoughts creep back in. What if I never find that again? I think of my friends again, the ones who reach out. They really do care. I need to remind myself of that. But why can’t I just be spontaneous? Why is that so hard for me? My mind jumps to all the times I hesitated to say yes. The fear always seems to win. Conversation is wild, you never know where it’ll go. It’s like walking a tightrope, balancing between what to say and what to hold back. I want to express my thoughts, but that anxiety grips me tight. I wonder if it’s because I care too much? Maybe it’s my agreeableness at play. I’d rather avoid conflict than push my opinion forward, and then it feels like I’m just going through the motions. But then I think, maybe I can use my openness to explore this! I can dive into the reasons behind my introversion, my struggles. Make it part of my story, not a hindrance. But the clock is ticking, there’s still that looming exam and I really need to focus. But I can’t. I’m too busy wrestling with these thoughts, emotions, connections. It’s a tangled web. There’s a comfort in knowing I’m not alone in feeling like this, right? So many people navigating their own thoughts, and somehow we’re all managing to hold it together. Or not.

Okay so I just dropped my phone in the water fountain and it still works somehow which is amazing because I thought for sure it was done for but I really love that phone because the camera is great and I need it for all the events coming up this week. Seriously, it's like every club is hosting something and I want to go to all of them because meeting new people is the best, right? I mean, what's the point of college if you don’t make connections? Like this one time in my sociology class I sat with this girl who was super into philosophy and we just kept talking about how the world is so interconnected and how every little choice can lead to something big. It’s kind of wild when you think about it, like every decision I’ve made has led me to this moment where I am hunched over my laptop typing a stream of thoughts. Speaking of connections, there's this new event for that environmental club I'm part of. They’re organizing a cleanup at the river this Saturday and I definitely want to go because I’m really passionate about sustainability and it’s a great chance to meet others who care about the same things. Plus, I’ve always thought community service should be fun too, like why not make it a picnic after? I could bring some homemade sandwiches and snacks and we could all chill by the water for a bit. Everyone loves food, right? I know I do especially if it’s good food. What's the most popular sandwich? I feel like it has to be turkey with a bit of mustard and lettuce but then again I could just make a bunch of different kinds because variety is the spice of life! Oh wait, I need to get back to studying for that exam next week and I have a plan for that too! I’m going to gather a group of friends and we’ll go over the material together because studying is always better in groups. They always say two heads are better than one but I think we need like at least four heads for all the complicated theories we have to memorize. I really hope everyone can make it and if they can't I’ll just talk to them one-on-one. I love explaining things; it feels rewarding when someone gets it after I break it down. I feel like people need more of that patience and understanding—I have this belief that everyone can learn if given the right tools and encouragement. If I could teach a class, I would definitely start with a class on communication skills. It’s just so vital in everything we do. People get so caught up in their own thoughts and fears and I just want to say, “Hey, let’s talk!” Like I want to help people express themselves better because I truly believe everyone has something valuable to share. I mean just the other day I was chatting with a classmate who was shy and didn’t think she had much to say. But once we started talking about our favorite shows, she just opened up! It was like a light switch turned on and I ended up finding out about her interest in animation and now we’re thinking of starting a little project together. This whole spontaneity idea is fantastic—like, you know, just going with the flow can lead to the best experiences. I admire people who are adventurous and take risks because that feels liberating, doesn't it? Exploring new things! That’s why I signed up for that dance class and even though I’m not the best dancer, it doesn’t even matter. I just love moving and having fun with others. There's something about being in a room filled with laughter and music that makes everything else fade away. It’s like the ultimate stress relief. By the way, I need to remember to pick up some coffee for that study group because caffeine definitely makes everything more lively. I’ve been gradually getting into that whole coffee-snob thing where I know the difference between good coffee and bad coffee. Why not invest in something nice for yourself? That’s a life lesson I think everyone should learn. Enjoy the little things, right? Like the taste of that perfect latte or the satisfaction of a well-organized planner. It’s almost time to head to class, actually. I should probably wrap this up. I wonder what we’ll talk about today. Maybe something on social behavior or ethics—both are super fascinating! I could talk about them for hours; it’s just another reason I love college so much. There’s always something to learn, always someone new to meet, and every conversation feels like a little adventure in itself. Life is like a buffet of experiences, and I'm definitely here for it!

Okay so I was just thinking about how much I love trying new food. There's this little Mexican place I found last week, and they had the most incredible tacos. I can't believe I never went there before. Sometimes I wonder if I spend too much time eating out, but it just feels so exciting to discover new flavors. What is it about food that makes everything so much better? I mean, sharing a meal with friends is such a bonding experience. I love how food can bring people together. Speaking of friends, I need to text Jenna. She was talking about that new escape room that just opened, and I love escape rooms. They're such a great way to bring out everyone’s creativity and teamwork skills, and I really enjoy how you can see everyone’s personalities come out when the pressure is on, like who takes charge and who just cracks jokes to lighten the mood. I thrive in those environments. All the energy buzzing around just makes me feel alive! I thrive on connections, all those little moments when you just click with someone. Ugh, I still need to finish that biology assignment due Friday. Procrastinating again. I just get so distracted by everything else going on. Like, I was thinking about getting back into photography. I used to love it, but I haven’t picked up my camera in ages. There’s something cool about capturing a moment. I love how art can communicate feelings words can’t express. Maybe I could take some photos for the school paper. They're always looking for fresh content, and I just think it would be fun to combine two things I love. Of course, I need to figure out a theme or something. Maybe I could focus on candid shots of campus life? There’s always so much happening. Oh, and I still need to return that library book! Why do I always wait until the last minute? It’s like I have a mental block when it comes to returning stuff. I don’t know if it’s the rushing or the fear of what happens if I lose it. But, honestly, I find it kind of thrilling to see how many books I can read before I finally have to return them. I love being surrounded by stories and ideas, and the library is like a treasure chest. Imagine having that much knowledge all at your fingertips. I was thinking about self-improvement the other day. It’s fascinating how many resources are out there to help people grow. I've been dabbling with journaling more these days – just writing down all my thoughts as they come. Sometimes it feels like a scramble in my head, but I know it helps sort things out. It's like an outlet, you know? And it keeps reminding me how much there is to explore within myself. I wonder if I could organize a campus event about mental health awareness. Having open conversations could really help break down all those stigmas. I feel like so many people are struggling, especially with the pressures of school. Maybe I could partner up with some clubs or get some guest speakers. I love being involved and supporting others. Plus, it aligns with my values. Isn’t it cool how being passionate about things can lead to so many opportunities? I once ended up volunteering at a local charity just because I wanted to help out after hearing about it from a friend. That turned into a whole summer project that brought in more volunteers and raised funds. I love how small actions can create ripples. It gives me hope, like if we all just try a bit harder, we can make a difference. What’s next on my bucket list? I want to travel more. I’ve been meaning to explore national parks. The outdoors always fills me with a sense of peace. I can visualize myself hiking through some breathtaking trails, taking in the scenery. Maybe I could bring a group of friends along; it’s always more fun when you can share experiences. I just get so excited about the idea of packing up, hitting the road, and experiencing new adventures. I hope this study I’m a part of gives me more insights into how I interact with others. I love meeting new people. Everyone has a unique story to tell, and I want to hear them all. Life is too short not to connect with as many souls as possible. There’s a certain beauty in diversity, you know? I feel like the more I learn, the better I can understand not just other people but myself too. Okay, I think I’ve rambled enough for now. But I just love pouring my thoughts out like this. It feels like a release. I can't wait to see how it all shapes out in the end. Wish me luck on those tacos!

say I should be better with my time but maybe I don’t need to be better maybe I just need to be me but what does that even mean I feel like everyone has their life together like they’ve got everything to connect and share experiences and the anxiety that comes with it the fear of rejection but when someone laughs at my joke or smiles at a story I told it’s like a shot of adrenaline and suddenly all the worries fade away and I just want to bottle that feeling up and keep it forever because it’s magic this weird beautiful dance between my extroverted side that loves being in the spotlight and the neurotic side that’s always worried about how I come across to others I wonder how they see me do they think I’m just this bubbly person or do they see that underneath it all I’m a bundle of nerves and insecurities that I’m constantly trying to shove down when I’m out with friends but I guess we all have something like that we all have our battles and I don’t know maybe that’s what makes us human just this chaotic intersection of emotions and experiences and whatever makes this life so strange and beautiful at the same time.

I can't believe I spent all day thinking about that one weird video I saw. The one with the guy trying to eat a whole watermelon in under a minute. Why watermelon? So random. What’s the obsession with challenge videos? I guess people need to find some form of entertainment, but I can't get over the sheer absurdity of it. I wonder if they ever think about their health afterward. Probably not. I don’t even know why I care. I should probably focus on my own life. But these thoughts swirl, don’t they? Like, I sit here with a million ideas, but then nothing really feels worth saying. It's like the more I try to settle on something concrete, the more my thoughts splatter everywhere. I can feel the anxiety creeping in, like a low hum in the back of my mind. Maybe I should doodle. I used to draw but it became too much pressure. Each line felt like it needed purpose, and I hate that. I just want to create without it becoming a thing. That’s why I like writing like this. Just spilling thoughts on the page. So what else? Oh, that latest Netflix doc about social media and mental health. It feels so real. I relate to it more than I’d like to admit. Scrolling through feeds and feeling that weird mix of connection and isolation. I should really delete some apps. It leads me down this rabbit hole of comparing myself to people who seem to have their lives figured out. But it’s all fake. Filters galore. That perfect photo just masks chaos. Everyone’s scrambling, feeling lost, yet we’re all pretending to be fine. It's exhausting. Why can’t we just be honest? I find honesty refreshing, even when it’s brutal. But when I try to express my true feelings, I often feel like people don’t get it. Or they don’t want to. So I just pull back, retreat, and maybe that makes me a bit prickly. What’s that weird sensation I get when I think about talking to people? It's a mix of dread and fascination. Like, I want to know them but worry they’ll see through me, expose all the messy bits. I avoid situations where I have to engage in small talk. I’d rather dive into the deep stuff right away. “So what’s your biggest fear?” Okay that’s excessive, but wouldn’t it be nice to just cut through the surface level junk? And then there’s school. Man, the stress is real. I’m not organized, never was, and I always seem to forget about deadlines until they’re basically knocking on my door demanding attention. It’s like my brain is a swirling vortex of ideas but the practical stuff just spills right over the edge. I keep telling myself I’ll get better. I’ll take notes, I’ll plan my days, but then I forget where I put my planner. I have this constant battle against chaos while loving the chaos at the same time. Life is messy and unpredictable, and I think that’s fascinating. Maybe I should take more risks, but I get paralyzed at the thought of failure. Sometimes I avoid it all just to avoid the hurt. So ironic. Thinking about all my creative pursuits, I wish the world felt more accepting of unconventionality. I want to wear my weirdness like a badge. I get ideas for art projects, music, writing, but the moment I think about sharing them, I turn into this anxious wreck, fearing judgment. It’s easier to keep things hidden like treasures you’re afraid to lose. But at the same time, sharing feels like a release. I’m torn. I want to be understood but can’t find the right words. I also think about nature a lot. There’s something about the trees, the way they sway in the wind, that calms my racing thoughts. I don’t get out much, and maybe that’s a problem, but there’s this part of me that genuinely loves the solitude. The quiet. Just me and my thoughts. I feel most free when I’m walking in a park or staring at the sky. Each moment feels expansive and full of possibility. It’s like I can breathe again. I should probably spend more time doing that instead of scrolling endlessly. But then again, the internet is full of ideas! I follow artists, writers, thinkers, and I love that exchange of inspiration. I often find myself wondering if I can ever reach that level of creativity, but then the little critic in my head says “No, you can’t.” I wrestle with this back-and-forth of self-doubt and creative excitement. It’s exhausting, but for some reason, I keep coming back to it. Maybe one day I’ll find a way to quiet that inner critic. Or at least learn to coexist with it.

disaster but at least my desk is clean which is the one thing I have control over but then again I think about how I spend too much time trying to perfect stupid things instead of just living or maybe I’m living in a very boring way but it feels safe and I don’t want to push the boundaries because what if I fail what if I mess it up what if it’s uncomfortable like I can’t stand discomfort it’s like my skin crawls at the thought and that’s why I don’t try harder to branch out and make new friends because it feels like such a big risk and I hate feeling like I’m taking risks that don’t add up to anything valuable and that’s why I stay in my little box because it’s familiar and I know the walls and they know me back but I wonder if I’m just missing out and what if there’s something amazing waiting outside those walls. I almost texted a friend today but then I thought about what I would say and how they probably don’t have time to hear about my existential dread over lunch or dinner like who wants to listen to that nobody wants to carry the emotional baggage of someone else when they’re just trying to vibe with life I mean I get it and I do want to be supportive but why does it always feel like I’m the one who needs the support and I just can’t find it sometimes I can feel so understood by a song or a book but people are different and they change and it’s terrifying to think about getting close to someone knowing they could just leave or drift away or get tired of my stuff and that’s why keeping to myself might be the easiest way to go.

Okay so I’m thinking about all the work I have to do. I have this paper due in three days and I’ve barely started. I can’t believe I always procrastinate like this. I hate it so much. Why do I do this to myself? I know I should’ve started it earlier but I just kept putting it off. I get so anxious when I think about it. What if I don’t do well? What if I fail? I would just die. No one would even care. They wouldn’t even notice. My grades are okay but they could be better if I just got my act together. I have so many assignments piling up and it’s so overwhelming. I should probably make a list. Lists help. I like lists. They make me feel organized even if I’m not. Speaking of organization, I really need to clean my room. It’s a mess right now. Clothes everywhere, dirty dishes because I keep telling myself I’ll do them later, and I just never do. It’s like I’m in this cycle of chaos. I can’t stand it. When it’s messy, I can’t focus on anything. It’s like the mess is trying to distract me from my thoughts, but the problem is the mess makes me more anxious. So frustrating. I hate small talk too. Why do people insist on asking about your weekend or what you think of the weather? I just want to scream. I’d much rather talk about the latest episode of that show I’m watching, which is kind of embarrassing because it’s reality TV and I’m not even sure why I’m watching it. It’s not intellectually stimulating at all. I should be watching documentaries or something smart, but there’s something oddly comforting about watching other people’s drama unfold. But then I feel guilty about it because I know I should be productive. I think I’d prefer to just be alone. Being with people is exhausting. I can’t follow conversations because I’m too busy worrying about how I’m perceived. Do they think I’m boring? What if I say something stupid? I get all caught up in my head and forget what I was going to say. Then I just nod or smile or pretend I’m listening when really I’m off in my own world. It’s easier to just avoid all that nonsense altogether. I don’t get why some people thrive in social settings. It’s like they’re happy to be there talking and laughing. I don’t get it. I could just be at home reading or working on school stuff. That seems so much more satisfying. There’s something nice about the quiet. The silence and the peace. I can think clearly in silence. And I feel accomplished when I can tick things off my list. But then there’s that nagging feeling. I think I’m missing out on something. But what? I never know. I see my peers hanging out, making connections, building relationships, and I’m just sitting here hoarding my energy. I can’t help but wonder if I should be more open to new experiences. But then I remember the last time I tried to be open—I went to that party, and it was a disaster. I just stood in the corner, feeling awkward, and then I left early. My heart racing the whole time. Social anxiety is real. And then there are the nights when I can’t sleep. My mind races with thoughts about everything. Did I say the wrong thing to that professor? Am I behind on my readings? What if I forget to study for that test next week? It’s like a merry-go-round of anxiety and the worst part is that no one really understands. Everyone else looks so composed, but I’m this bundle of nerves just trying to keep it together. I love routines though. They make my life feel secure. I wake up, review my to-do list, have my breakfast, and then tackle one thing at a time. I get so much satisfaction from checking things off. I wish I could bottle that feeling up and use it when I’m feeling overwhelmed. It’s like a small win every time I finish something. But the moment I get distracted or something derails my routine, it’s like I’m thrown into chaos again. It’s so easy for me to dwell on what’s wrong. I see the flaws in everything. My papers could always be better, my grades could be higher, I could exercise more often. I’m too hard on myself. I know that. But I can’t help but think that if I just push harder, I could achieve something great. But what is something great anyway? Part of me just wants to hide from the world. I’m just so tired of all this pressure and expectations. I want to be the person who has it all figured out but I’m not. Just a mess trying to pretend like I’m okay. And it’s exhausting. There’s always a clock ticking, deadlines looming, and I just want to hide away sometimes. But then I think about the future, and what does that even look like? It’s unclear. Scary. Uncertain. Well what if I just focus on today? But that in itself is overwhelming.

okay so I was thinking about how I just don’t really care about keeping things organized like I know I should but it’s too much effort and I don’t have the patience for it and it’s like the mess kind of becomes its own thing like a weird little ecosystem of papers and receipts and who even needs receipts anyway sometimes I just throw them in a box because it feels like a little time capsule of my life those receipts tell stories like that time I spent too much on takeout because I was too lazy to cook but you know what sometimes that’s just life and people make it seem like you have to have everything under control but I find beauty in this chaos so yeah the boxes and clutter have a sort of charm to them and it’s interesting how it reflects who I am or at least who I think I am though I wonder if that’s too deep for a Tuesday afternoon. I had this thought about how like every single person is a universe unto themselves like this whole expansive galaxy of experiences and ideas and dreams and passions and it’s wild to think about when you’re just sitting alone in a corner of a coffee shop eavesdropping on conversations that swirl around like clouds it makes me feel so connected to everyone like I could just jump in and be part of whatever lives they’re leading but I never do because what if they don’t want to include me like the introvert in me just kind of shrinks back and watches but I’m okay with that sometimes like I can be okay with just existing on the periphery that’s where I find the juiciest bits anyway the quiet moments the side glances the little flickers of emotion that people don’t even realize they’re giving off I could study that forever like someone should do a study on the nuances of human connection though I think most people just want surface-level chit chat I mean there’s so much boring stuff out there but at times you get those little nuggets of humanity that make you stop and think and want to pull someone aside and be like hey do you know you just said something that made my brain light up? but then I worry about how they might react and what if they think I’m weird or too intense or something like that. I once talked to this girl at a bus stop and we bonded over how hard it was to find good vegan food and that moment felt electric like it was a brief snapshot of collaboration in the universe and it was spontaneous and so simple and no one else around realized how special it was but I replay these small interactions in my head sometimes, they feel like little gems tucked away in my memory. makes me wonder why I even bother with social media anymore because it feels so flat and manufactured like everyone’s just showcasing the best parts of their life and it’s hard to find the authenticity I crave. I mean, I could post about my day but what would that even look like a stack of books I’m reading half of them I never finish but that’s okay I embrace that too it’s just the act of starting something new that excites me it’s like opening up these fresh portals to different ideas it’s all overwhelming and fascinating like I’m standing in a library and there are millions of stories just waiting for me to explore. and music too, wow, music pulls me in like a tidal wave sometimes like I just get lost in the beats and rhythms and lyrics that resonate with some part of my soul it’s like that one song that gets played at a pivotal moment and suddenly it will forever be connected to that feeling whether it’s heartache or joy or the thrill of new love and I want to capture that feeling in a jar and keep it with me always. I mean, who wouldn’t want to bottle up moments that make their heart race and their spirit lift? it’s the little things that stick with me I guess like the way sunlight filters through trees or the smell of wet pavement after rain and I get lost in those thoughts it’s like a spiral of wonder and I can never quite find my way out but I don’t mind being lost there it’s a warm feeling like sitting beside a fire with friends but not the big loud gathering just the intimate ones where you can really talk about things that matter and I don’t always know what matters but I think it’s okay to be on that journey of figuring it out even if it feels daunting and I feel this urge to reach out to people but also stay within my bubble and I like that bubble it’s comfortable but I don’t want to close off I want to explore and find connections and it feels like a constant tug-of-war inside my head. I wonder if everyone feels this way or if it’s just me being my usual strange self and can you really be strange if you’re just being you?

something but the thought of interviews makes me want to curl up into a ball under my comforter and just stay there forever I mean what do I even say when they ask me about my strengths and weaknesses like my weaknesses are basically my entire personality like is it bad if I just say I’m awkward and avoid confrontation because what’s the point in pretending otherwise people do that all the time like this facade of being collected and put together when inside it feels like a chaotic tornado that’s neverending which is funny because I love chaos in art and books and movies but in real life it but then I think if I let it slide all the time is that just me being walked all over or am I just a little too sensitive to these things oh and the dreams I’ve been having are so bizarre like the like how you should have a job and a plan and the right kind of connections and I don’t even know what connections mean like is there a secret handshake involved like it’s all so confusing I keep imagining myself in weird situations like what if I just walked up to a group of people and said “hi I love bees” would they welcome me with open arms or would they look at me like I’m a crazy person which I guess I am but in a good way like I want to experience different things and travel but then I also want to hide away in my room and scroll through social media where it feels safe but then you see people posting photos of their perfect lives and it’s hard not to compare myself to others and think about how I haven’t done nearly enough with my life yet the anxiety creeps in and it’s hard to into and feel so out of touch like why can’t I just be part of it, maybe if I could get into the groove of things I wouldn’t feel so disconnected, but then I spiral again into a pit of contemplation about whether I'm being true to myself or just trying to fit into someone else's idea of what life should look like and I wonder if I’d be happier living in a cabin in the woods surrounded by trees and books instead of worrying about climbing the corporate ladder or finding a “real” job but that sounds exhausting in a different way and then there’s the question of whether I even want to be around people at all or if I just need more one-on-one interactions because those are sometimes easier, like a conversation over coffee as opposed to larger gatherings where I feel like I’m held Two o'clock in the afternoon and my phone just buzzed with a meme from Jake, it’s a cat falling off a counter and I’m laughing out loud. He always knows what to send me to lighten the mood, I mean it’s wild how people communicate these days, like, remember when we used to just send text messages? Now it’s all about the memes and GIFs, such a rapid-fire exchange of humor. Sometimes I think about how the more we share, the more connected we feel, like I could be sitting anywhere but if I see something funny, it’s like we’re together, right? Friendship feels so interwoven with social media, I wonder if that’s a good thing or a bad thing. Okay so, last night’s party was off the charts. I think everyone had a great time; the music was bumping, and I met so many new people. I love how you can just walk up to someone and start chatting, like is that a skill or just something that comes naturally? I introduced myself to this girl, Lana, she has the coolest vintage clothes and a smile that lights up the whole room. We talked about our favorite bands, and she loves The 1975 too! There’s something about music that connects us, right? It’s like an instant bond, and then we spent the next hour dancing like no one was watching. I think we need more nights like that, just pure fun and no worries. It was one of those moments where you think, wow, life is good. I need to remember to call my mom later. She always has that way of calming me down after a long week. It’s amazing how just hearing her voice can make me feel grounded. She wanted to hear all about college life and my latest adventures. I should probably get her an update about my history project, which is coming along nicely. I really enjoy researching different periods. Right now I’m diving into the Renaissance, and wow, the art, the revolution in thought! I wish I could just travel back in time to see it all happening. It makes me think about how creativity evolves and how it’s so integral to who we are as people. Oh! I need to finish that book for my literature class. It’s about this guy who travels through time and I’m really intrigued by the possibilities of different timelines. Imagine if we could see alternate versions of ourselves, like what would my other selves be doing right now? Would they be more introverted? Less adventurous? It fascinates me how choices shape our paths and sometimes I wonder if I’ve made the right ones. But then again, that’s the fun of it, right? Just embracing every twist and turn, every new experience. Friend groups are so fluid in college, aren’t they? I feel like I’m constantly meeting new people but also maintaining these old friendships.
