Like Emma, we’ve been friends since freshman year, and even though our lives are taking us in different directions, we still check in regularly. It’s so important to have those connections, like anchors in the sea of change. I should probably set up a game night with her soon, we love that board game everyone’s obsessed with, the one with the trivia questions. I think I’d crush it if we teamed up. And then there’s another party this weekend. I’m kind of excited but also exhausted just thinking about it. But once I’m there, I know the energy will just sweep me up. I’ve got to find the perfect outfit though. Something that pops but feels cozy too. I always end up dressing for fun and comfort at the same time. The right outfit can really boost your confidence, like stepping out and feeling like a million bucks. Also, why does every coffee shop I go to have to have such a complicated menu? I just want a latte, not a physics problem. I mean, I like trying new things but sometimes I just want to know what I’m getting. Maybe I should just be brave and try something wild next time, like a matcha latte or whatever it is that everyone is raving about. I think food is another way we bond—like sharing a meal or getting brunch with friends; it’s such a social activity. Okay, I really need to get back to that history project. I’ve been bouncing around from thought to thought but I can’t lose sight of what’s important. Focus! Maybe I can reward myself with a fun outing after I get some work done. A little balance between productivity and play is key. Life is all about finding that rhythm, right?

Okay so, the other day I was walking through the park and there were these kids playing tag, and it seemed like so much fun, I mean like why do we stop playing as adults? Like, what happened to just running around and not caring? I just wanted to join in, get sweaty and laugh, but then I thought, would that be weird? Maybe I should just start a spontaneous game, but then you'd need a bunch of people and it’s like, do I have the energy for that? Maybe. Maybe not. Like, sometimes I just wanna be free, you know, no boundaries, just vibes. Speaking of vibes, I met this girl at a party last week, and she was just radiating positivity. Like, we instantly clicked. I told her I loved her outfit, and it wasn't like some cheesy line, I genuinely thought she looked great. We ended up talking for hours about everything and nothing, music, travel, dreams, like, how does that happen? It's so easy to lose track of time when you vibe with someone. It’s like the universe is telling you, "Yeah, you’re meant to meet this person." We should have exchanged numbers or something, but then again, do I even wanna be that committed? Sometimes I feel like just being in the moment is enough. Oh, and I’ve been thinking about traveling again. I love the feeling of just being in a new place where everything is unknown. The smells, the people, the food, oh my god, the food! I could literally eat my way through a city, just explore what makes it tick. Street vendors are the best, you're munching on something delicious while chatting with locals, it's so organic, spontaneous, and real. I remember this time I was in Mexico, and I had these tacos from a hole-in-the-wall—so good, I could taste the love. Food has this way of connecting people, doesn’t it? Like, why do we even need fancy restaurants? Just give me raw authenticity over polished perfection any day. Wait, but back to meeting people. I sometimes wonder if I should take that leap more often. I mean, I’ve got friends, and they’re great, but I just crave more experiences. Like, every time I meet someone interesting, I feel like my world just expands a little. Last week I ran into this old classmate at a bar. We hadn’t seen each other in like five years. We picked up right where we left off and discussed a million things in a matter of minutes. I could feel that connection and it’s just so easy to fall into deep conversations when you’re open to them. I love that about myself, how I can just engage with others without thinking too much about it. Sometimes, though, I wonder why I’m not as good at sticking to plans or being organized. I mean, I totally forget about chores until the mess becomes unbearable, or I leave assignments until the last minute. I guess I’m just too wrapped up in what’s happening now. It’s like planning for the future seems pointless when there's so much excitement in the present. But then I think, should I care? Does it matter if my room is a mess or if I barely scraped by on that paper? I don’t stress about stuff like that, and I’m okay with it. Like, it’s just a phase. But then again, what will my mom say? Ah, my mom, she always has some thoughtful advice. She’s the kind of person who believes in planning everything down to the minute. I guess I got my personality from her but then also not. I just thrive on spontaneity, I mean, I don’t even mind if we end up on some random road trip, no destination in mind. Just listening to music with friends, the windows down, laughing until we can't breathe. That’s the good stuff, right? Ooh, speaking of music, I’ve been obsessed with this band lately. They just dropped an album that feels like a journey. I listen to it on repeat and every note resonates with me. I feel like I could dance anywhere, in my living room, on the street, like a wild child. Then there are those moments when I just wanna cry with the music, you know? How does it touch my soul like that? Just the right song can hit you and suddenly you’re shaking your head, lost in a wave of emotion. I think life is just a series of perfectly imperfect moments that come together, like a constellation. Each person I meet, each experience I have, it all contributes to the tapestry of my life. Who needs a plan when you can just live? And I’m here for it, every second, every heartbeat, just taking it all in.

I shouldn’t have eaten that leftover pizza, it always makes me feel sluggish. Not that I had a ton of choices, the fridge is practically empty. A pack of mustard and that weird sauce from takeout last week, do I even want that? Maybe it’s too late to go to the store. I’ll just have to deal. I’m not really a fan of crowds anyway. Sometimes I think about how much I avoid social events. Not that I mind being around people, but big gatherings make me anxious. It’s not the people, it’s the noise. It’s like they all want to talk at once and I can’t follow anything. I’d rather sit back, listen in but not really engage. Just nodding along like I’m part of it without actually being in it. It’s fine, I guess. I’ve always been like this. My friends are okay with it—most of them. They know I’m not the life of the party. I wonder if they think I’m boring. Sometimes I worry about that, but then I remember that they still invite me. What was I saying? Right, the pizza. I always do this—get sidetracked by food. It’s comforting, though. Food is reliable, you can count on it. Well, I guess you can’t count on leftovers being good, but most of the time they are. I’m a creature of habit, so I always order the same thing. Pepperoni, cheese, classic. Nothing fancy, that’s me in a nutshell, I suppose. I wonder how people can go for sushi or something adventurous. That’s too much. I need comfort food. I’m not one for trying new things. Even when friends drag me to new places, I just go along to not disappoint anyone. I really do try to be agreeable. It’s just easier to say yes. My roommate is always talking about how I let people walk all over me. I don’t think so, but maybe I do. I guess that’s why I avoid conflict, but I really don’t mind. It’s just simpler to go with the flow. Why would I want to argue about which movie to watch or what restaurant to go to? Isn’t that what voting is for? It’s all a compromise anyway. I’d rather let someone else make the choice. Sometimes I’ll even enjoy it more than I thought I would. I’m good at going with the flow. It’s like riding a wave, as long as it’s not too rough, just a nice little surf. I can get stuck in my own head, though. When I think too much about what I could’ve said or how I could’ve contributed to the conversation, that’s when I hesitate more. I don’t mind being quiet, but every once in a while, I wish I could just jump in, you know? It’s like there’s a wall between what I’m thinking and what I’m saying. I’m not worried about rejection, I just don’t know how to bridge the gap, and it’s frustrating sometimes. They say that being low on neuroticism makes life easier. I don’t sweat the small stuff, but then I tend to just ignore big stuff too. A lot of my friends have these massive goals, this drive that I just don’t get. I mean, I want to be happy and not stressed, but I can’t imagine being super ambitious. What’s the point? It’s exhausting. I’m okay with the simple things, really just enjoying being where I am. Isn’t that what life is about? There’s this pressure to do more, be more, but I just don’t feel that pull. Wait, what time is it? I should probably check my phone. I wonder if anyone has messaged me. Probably not. I’m fine with being alone most of the time. Social media gives me a glimpse into people’s lives, but I don’t feel the need to be part of every little detail. I scroll through, like the cute puppy photos, the travel pics, and feel at peace with being home. Home is my space, my comfort zone, and I don’t need to go out if I don’t want to. But sometimes, I feel like I’m missing out on something. Like there’s a whole world out there, and I’m just sitting on the sidelines. But then I remind myself that it’s okay to be content. Some people thrive on that energy, and I just don’t. I like my quiet nights in. A book, a movie, a cozy blanket. No expectations, just being. I can’t imagine wanting anything more. I hope my friends don’t think badly of me for it. I miss them sometimes, but the thought of going out makes me tired. I don’t know, it’s just how I am. And maybe that’s enough.

The pen's cap is rolling off the desk. I should probably pick it up. Focus. Okay, focus. I’ve got this assignment to finish, but my mind just feels like it’s bouncing around like a pinball machine. I wonder why people think it’s weird to prefer being alone. It’s calming to just be in my own little world. What’s so wrong with not wanting to be at a party or a crowded place? I don’t get it. I mean, you can still have fun just by reading a good book or exploring something new. I like the idea of exploring. The world is vast with so many ideas and cultures. I can get lost in thought about faraway places. Imagine wandering through the streets of Tokyo or sitting in a small café in Paris, people-watching. Just the thought of it makes me feel alive. There’s so much noise everywhere but still a kind of quiet when you’re just taking in the sights. I should probably write that down. Tokyo, Paris, maybe even Cape Town someday. There’s so much to learn from other cultures, different ways of living, different foods. I love trying new things. Food is an avenue I explore more than I probably should. I should really make a list of all the different dishes I want to make from around the world. I think about learning to cook Ethiopian food. Injera looks interesting. Different, too. The texture is so unique. I’ve seen videos of people sharing it and eating with their hands, but could I do that? I might just enjoy it too much. I love the idea of community around food, but I don’t necessarily want to share that space with a crowd of strangers. I’m definitely more of a solo chef in my kitchen. Or maybe just a couple of friends. I have to admit I hate the pressure of being social. But that leads to another thought. What do people really think when they post on social media? I scroll through all those perfectly curated lives and wonder if it’s genuine or just a façade. Everyone seems so perfect, but I know that’s not true. Everyone has issues, and there’s something refreshing about realizing that no one really has it all figured out. I should remember to remind myself of that because it’s easy to get lost in the comparisons. I get obsessive about things like that, especially when I’m doing research for class. I can lose hours diving deep into topics I didn’t even know excited me. I just keep falling down the rabbit hole, from one academic paper to another, and the information gets jumbled up in my brain. But I love the thrill of discovery, seeking out unique perspectives. It’s exhilarating but also daunting. How do I condense everything I learn into a coherent paper? I remember this one assignment where I had to write about the socio-economic impacts of climate change on coastal cities. I became so engrossed. There’s so much data, so many studies looking at how different areas might adapt or fail to adapt. It’s mind-blowing. I could see the world changing, and the way policies could make or break communities. I could talk about that forever. I get carried away sometimes. Thinking of that makes me wonder how I fit into all of this. I mean, my drive for doing well academically is pretty high. I can’t let it slip. I feel like I need to keep pushing myself. I want to be the best at everything, or at least put in the best effort, which often means more hours spent at the library or researching than socializing. Does that make me a loner? Maybe. I just find so much satisfaction in checking things off the list, in shaping my future, in knowing I really dug into something. But then again, does it have to be that way? What if I tried to strike a balance? I don’t even know how to start that. If I want to experience life more, I should give myself a chance. But those awkward moments of trying to connect with people, they make me cringe. It’s easier to sit back and observe rather than deal with the unpredictability of interactions. I want to be more open, but it’s really like stepping into a whirlwind. Back to thinking about travel and new tastes. If I could, I would visit those vibrant street markets. I bet they’re full of colors and sounds, unfamiliar scents wafting through the air. I’d want to capture it all but somehow remain invisible while doing it. What do they say about disappearing into the background? It sounds so peaceful. Just floating through life, not needing to stick out but still tasting, experiencing, absorbing the beauty of everything around me. I can’t stop pondering the why behind every dish or every painting in a gallery. It’s what drives my curiosity. What makes people tick? I wish I could understand that more. Anyway, maybe I’ll go check on my plants after this. They’re thriving, which feels like a small victory. I can manage that. Nurturing a living thing feels like a good way to connect to the world without the small talk. Just me and my plants in our little cocoon. That’s good enough for today.

I’ve been thinking about that essay due next week and how I really should start it but then I think what’s the point? I know what I want to say, I just have to lay it out logically. It annoys me how other people just write whatever nonsense comes to their minds, and then somehow it all looks pretty and enchanting. I prefer things to be structured, I like bullets and clear points, like a checklist. I need to write this down because I won’t remember it later. What’s the topic again? Oh right, something about social media and its impact on communication. It feels like a waste of time to analyze something everyone already knows is bad for us. I mean, it’s kind of obvious why I don’t go on social media too much. It drains me, it just feels like a cacophony of voices shouting over each other—no substance, just noise. I don’t care for the cat videos or the political rants. Just give me facts. Mom keeps asking me why I don’t go out more, and it's like, I get it, but also I don’t want to socialize for the sake of it. I have my few close friends and we text, that’s enough. I can’t stand small talk, it just feels pointless, people talking in circles about the weather and weekend plans. I’d rather talk about something real, something with depth. But can you really do that in a crowded room? I don’t think so. Too many distractions, too many people trying to be funny or charming. I’m not into charm, I just want to get to the point. Sometimes I wonder if my preferences are weird or if everyone’s just pretending to want social interaction when they’d rather be at home reading or organizing their books. I mean organizing books is like a therapy session for me. I enjoy color coding them or arranging them by genre and author. Feels good. Clean and neat. It’s satisfying when it all comes together. I hear people say they love that feeling of a good night out, but I can’t relate. I’d much rather have a good night in with a book and some tea. Maybe a bit of classical music in the background, just enough to help me focus. But then there’s that looming pressure to be involved, especially on campus. Group projects make me edgy because I can’t control how others work. I have little patience for people who don’t take things seriously. It’s not like I’m a perfectionist, but if we’re in a group, I want to make sure it goes well. I’ll do my part and I expect others to do theirs. If they don’t, it drives me insane. I don’t want to carry the load for someone else who just thinks they can coast. It’s frustrating. I can deal with stress but only when I know it’s under my control. I keep telling myself to stop overthinking everything. But when I try, I get sucked back into the same patterns. The more I try to break free from analyzing every detail, the more I analyze. It’s like a treadmill I’m stuck on—no way to get off. Sometimes it’s easier to disengage completely. I remember this one time in class when the discussion turned into a debate and people just couldn’t let go of their opinions. I shut down. Just sat there, listening, not wanting to participate. Their passion was exhausting. Do they not see how futile it is? Everyone is entitled to their opinions but it all feels so futile when no one is actually listening. I just want to roll my eyes and be done with it. Maybe I should try to be more open-minded, but then again, why? I’m okay with my routine and my thoughts. I don’t feel the need to explore new ideas or perspectives that don’t bring me any value. I know I’m not going to be everyone’s cup of tea and that's fine. I’d rather be a strong cup of black coffee. Straightforward and no fluff. And then I think about decisions I have to make in the coming weeks. Like, what classes to take next semester? Do I want to stick to my major or take that random elective everyone keeps raving about? It’s hard to weigh options when I prefer not to stray too far from my path. I like my path, it makes sense, it feels safe. The unknown is where chaos lies, and who needs that? I keep reminding myself that I need to stay focused on what matters. I need to keep my grades up, finish this essay, and not get sidetracked by the chaos around me. That’s what’s really important, isn’t it? Just figure out what I need to do and do it—no nonsense, no distractions, and definitely no small talk. Just productivity.

Okay so I’m just sitting here thinking about how people always say to find your passion, like it's this magical thing hidden under a rock or something. I mean seriously what if my passion is binging Netflix and eating nachos? I guess that’s not very glamorous but it’s real. I just watched this show that was so intense, I had to pause it every ten minutes to process what was happening. And it made me think about how drama in my life is always turned up to eleven too, like does everyone around me feel this way or is it just me? I thrive on chaos, even if sometimes it drives me a little nuts. Maybe that’s the neuroticism talking but I feel alive when there’s stuff going on, like the louder the environment, the better. Speaking of environments, I went to this party last week and wow it was a total blast until someone spilled red wine on the carpet. I was laughing and chatting with people and then all of a sudden BAM, chaos mode activated. People were running around trying to clean it up while I was just standing there thinking this is ridiculous. Why do we care so much about the carpet? It’s just a thing. But then again, maybe I care too little about things but I guess that’s the low agreeableness in me - I just don’t feel like soothing the chaos like everyone else does. I mostly let it wash over me. The anxiety kicks in a bit though when I think about the consequences of my indifference, like are they gonna charge the host for the cleaning? But then I remember that I should probably just focus on enjoying the music and the dancing and the people. I swear, I love people but sometimes they drive me absolutely crazy, like a constant need for validation or approval, like do they think I’m judging them? I’m not. I’m just vibing. Speaking of vibes, I met this incredible person at the party who talked about wanting to travel to Mars. Yes, Mars! How cool is that? I mean, I’m all for adventure but space is a whole other level. I jump from one thought to another so fast sometimes I feel like I’m on a flipping rollercoaster. Can you imagine being the first person on Mars? I would totally sign up. There’s something exhilarating about that idea. But it also makes me feel a bit overwhelmed thinking about being that isolated, like what if I start having panic attacks up there? But then the thrill kicks in again. The openness inside of me wants to just explore, to dive into the unknown. But I’ve got this swirling mix of wanting to be right there in the moment with all its unpredictability and this underlying sense of “what am I doing with my life” type anxiety. I should probably be studying but here I am dreaming about space, parties, and nachos. I’m a bit of a mess, honestly. I mean I have this vision of myself as this super organized person, like a planner with color-coded sticky notes but instead, I’m here with crumpled papers and random thoughts flitting around like crazy. And it’s exhausting too, like even organizing my thoughts is a project that seems too big for my current state of mind. I’ll start thinking about what I really want to do with my life and then flip-flop to “what’s the next good taco truck?” like it’s a priority or something. Maybe it’s the low conscientiousness, I kinda just float from thought to thought, focusing on the immediate gratification things instead of any big picture stuff. I just want the here and now to feel good, right this second. Also, the other day I got into a heated argument over the best pizza toppings. I know that sounds trivial but I was adamant that pineapples belong on pizza and this friend of mine was acting like I just declared war or something. Like chill dude, it’s just toppings. I relish those debates even if my neurotic side kicks in afterward and I worry whether I pushed him too far. Do I even care? Maybe not. But then I think about what it means to care or not care, and how that affects my relationships. It’s so complicated, isn’t it? The social dynamics swirling around and figuring out where I fit in all of it like a puzzle that won’t come together. And then I think about how strange it is to balance these different sides of me. Like today I want to go out and meet a million people but tomorrow I might just want to curl up and binge-watch documentaries about the world's most dangerous snakes. What a combination. It’s like a rollercoaster that doesn’t ever stop, it’s always in motion. I’m just here letting it take me on wild turns, and who knows—maybe all these chaotic thoughts will lead me to something epic.

I can’t believe I forgot my pencil, why do I keep doing this, it’s like I lose things on purpose sometimes? Okay, maybe not on purpose but sometimes I swear I walk in circles just to forget everything. Speaking of circles, I feel like life is a series of circles, right? Like you meet people, have fun, and then you lose touch, and then you meet up again somehow. It's always this weird cycle of connection and disconnection, like that time I reconnected with Jessica from freshman year. She was so different, but also the same, you know? I mean, who can forget that wild night in the dorms? Everyone was just like shouting and laughing, and I could feel this energy buzzing. Or the time at that party at Kevin's place, it felt like we were all in our own music video or something, dancing like no one was watching, but yeah, everyone was totally watching, and I was so aware of it but didn’t care because everyone was just vibing together. Ugh, why do I get all jittery sometimes? It’s like a weird mix of excitement and anxiety. I wonder if it’s because I care too much about what others think about me. It’s so frustrating. I mean, I love meeting new people and going to parties, it’s exhilarating but then I can feel my stomach drop when I think about what they might say. I just want everyone to get along! Why can’t we all be best friends? I sometimes want to put everyone in a room and just hope they all mesh, like some kind of weird social science experiment, which I guess it sort of is? Oh, speaking of experiments, I don’t even know if I remember what the study I’m in is about. Is it about personality? Do I seem extraverted? I feel like I’m just overthinking everything again. But then again, isn’t it nice to connect with people? I loved that time I went to that art gallery with everyone, all those colors, and the discussions about what’s good and what’s not and how art speaks to the soul. What if someone just walked in and thought everything was terrible? I mean, we all have opinions, right? And I’m such a sucker for hearing what people think. I could listen to someone passionately talk about a topic for hours, and I’d just soak it all in, even if I never cared about it before. That openness to experience is probably why I find myself at random events, like underground poetry slams or improv nights, just hoping to soak in whatever crazy vibe is happening that evening. That’s probably why my plans are all over the place. Sometimes I wonder if I take on too much. Like, am I saying ‘yes’ to everything? Probably! But it’s all so exciting. I hate being bored. I can’t stand it. Like, I use my phone to scroll endlessly when I feel bored, but then I’m just more anxious because I’m comparing myself to everyone online. The anxiety just spirals, and I start thinking, “Am I doing enough?” and “Should I be more serious or focused?” But that thought makes me feel trapped. I can’t stand the idea of being boxed in with strict plans. I just want to be spontaneous, but then I feel bad when plans fall through. I think about how much I want to reach out to people and make sure everyone’s okay, but I’m also a mess myself. Oh, that reminds me of Greg from last week, remember the way he was so chill but then got this sudden wave of panic at the trivia night? He had the funniest answers, but when it came down to winning, he just got so nervous and I wanted to tell him to chill out. We were all there to have fun anyway. It’s just a game, right? Why stress about it? But why are there always some people taking it way too seriously? Do they forget that it’s just trivia? So many questions swirling, like how I can maintain my open-mindedness while also juggling everyone’s feelings. It can’t be that difficult, can it? I often feel like I’m juggling everything, and I don’t care about the mess as long as I’m having fun. But sometimes that leads to me dropping things. Like, I can easily forget my responsibilities, like that project due next week that I’ve barely started. I should really get on that, but there’s always something more exciting popping up, like Josephine inviting me for karaoke. You know I can’t say no to karaoke! I get that it’s important to find balance, but honestly, I thrive in the chaos, even if it makes my brain feel like it’s on fire. But whatever, it's all part of the ride, right? I just want to keep meeting new people, keep exploring new ideas, keep feeling all the feels without holding back. Life’s too short to be anything but alive and vibing.

So, I just got off the phone with Jess, and honestly, she always knows how to light up my mood, even if I’m feeling a little all over the place. It’s funny how we can just chat for hours about nothing, like the latest series on Netflix or her new plant obsession—even though I don’t get how people can take care of plants. Like, do they really need that much attention? I mean, I get it, they’re living things, but sometimes I feel like my phone gets more love than my own well-being. I can’t remember the last time I cooked anything other than ramen or microwaved something. I should probably eat healthier but who has time for that? Ugh, and don’t even get me started on the gym. I really should go more often, but it’s just so hard to be motivated. But I think the real issue is that I can’t ever seem to settle down or focus long enough to get anything done. I keep telling myself I’ll clean my room or organize my notes, but then I just end up scrolling through TikTok or Instagram for way too long. My friends always tease me about my messy space and how I can never find anything, and yeah, I know it’s a mess. I get anxious just thinking about it. I’m not disorganized on purpose! It’s just that I’m constantly distracted by everything around me. I hate that feeling of overwhelm when responsibilities pile up, but I’m also so good at making excuses and just avoiding it all. Why does everything feel so heavy? Speaking of heavy, I really should check in on Mike too. He sounded off last night when we were texting, and I don’t want to be that friend who doesn’t notice when someone’s struggling. I’m always the one trying to cheer everyone up, but I also feel like I take on everyone’s stresses. It’s exhausting sometimes, you know? Like it’s always me trying to help others, but I just need someone to pull me out of my own head, even if it’s just for a little while. But do I tell him that? Nah, I can’t be that vulnerable. That’s just not how I roll. I was talking to Jess about these weird dreams I keep having—these scenarios where I’m just racing against time, trying to get somewhere but I never make it. It’s like a constant loop. That must say something about my anxiety, right? I just wish I could turn it off sometimes. I’m always in my head, spinning in circles, which makes me wonder if maybe it’s a problem of not letting go. I’ve canceled plans so many times lately. I just want to be excited about things again. But every time I put something on my calendar, I end up worrying about the details of how it’s going to go instead of just enjoying it. I really love being around people, though, it’s just that the whole anxiety piece ruins it sometimes. It kind of snatches away that joy. I live for those moments when everyone’s laughing and letting loose. It’s like a balm for my soul, even if I’m an anxious wreck underneath it all. I’m so good at connecting with people, and it usually makes me feel alive, but the pressure to have fun is always there, looming over me like clouds before a storm. And then there’s the whole issue of social media. I can’t help but scroll through everyone’s perfect lives and think, ‘Why can’t I just be like that?’ But I know it’s all curated nonsense. I just hate feeling inadequate. I mean, I’m so agreeable that I want everyone to like me. I want to be the person people think of when they have good news or need a laugh. But balancing that need with how I feel is just… hard. I end up nodding along, agreeing with everyone’s plans, even when I’m secretly dreading them. It makes me think about how people project this confident exterior, but it’s all just a facade sometimes. Everyone struggles! We’re all just out here trying to do our best while also trying not to freak out. But I put on that brave face and march out into the world, even if I’m terrified of what comes next. It’s such a balancing act that I sometimes forget to prioritize me. It’s always easier to worry about friends and their needs, and then I’m left feeling depleted. I should really take more time to recharge, but that’s so hard to do when everyone around me is expecting me to be on all the time. I feel like I’m the life of the party but also stuck in chaos. I want to be free and open but the walls feel like they’re closing in. I really need to talk to someone about all this, but therapy is expensive and honestly, I’m terrible at finding solutions. I get all tangled in my thoughts and can’t figure out where to start. But anyway, I’m just rambling now. It’s a lot today.

Just saw a cute dog across the street, like a little fluff ball with a wagging tail, reminded me of my childhood dog who used to chase the mailman every day and bark like he owned the whole neighborhood. I wonder if those dogs ever get tired of barking? I mean, don’t they ever think, “maybe we should just chill out and enjoy the sunshine”? Speaking of which, the sun is totally shining today, and it makes me want to grab my sketchbook and head to the park. There’s something about drawing people, capturing moments, watching their expressions change—it’s like freezing time for just a bit. I love how everyone has their own story just waiting to be uncovered. What’s on the agenda today? Classes, definitely. I have that big psychology lecture at 10, which is always fascinating. I can’t get enough of how the brain works and motivations and behaviors and all those fun things. I love chatting with classmates after lectures, bouncing ideas around, hearing different perspectives. Keeps the energy up! I should probably organize a study group. I think a few of us could really benefit from that. Teamwork makes the dream work, right? What do they say, creativity loves collaboration? Thinking about this group project too—ugh, group projects can feel like herding cats sometimes, but I actually enjoy it. I like giving everyone a chance to shine, pulling out those hidden talents. There’s this one guy, Mark, who has amazing ideas but never really shares them unless prompted, so I’m always like “hey Mark, what do you think?” And then he lights up! It’s so satisfying to see someone’s face light up when you ask for their input, like you’re giving them this little boost of confidence. I wish everyone had that experience more often; it would make the world a friendlier place. Okay, but also, I’m thinking about what breakfast I should grab. I usually go for those smoothie bowls. So colorful with fruits and nuts on top! And it’s like a mini-art piece that you get to eat. I can’t wait to make it, I think I’ll add some chia seeds and maybe some coconut flakes—simple pleasures. Food is such a love language, right? Cooking with friends, trying out new recipes, laughing over the mess we make in the kitchen, that’s the best. I just realized I haven’t been to that new Thai restaurant that opened downtown. I heard it’s like a fusion place with crazy flavors. I want to go, but it’s about getting the crew together. I should send out a group text! Something like “who’s in for Thai food adventure?” That’ll get them going. I think everyone’s been feeling the mid-semester slump, and a fun dinner could lift spirits. It’s funny how food has this magical ability to bring people together; like, put a bowl of ramen in front of someone and suddenly everyone’s best friends. Now my mind is wandering a little to thinking about travel. I’ve been daydreaming about going to Japan. The cherry blossoms! The bustling streets of Tokyo! I’ve read so much about the culture and the history, it’s rich and layered. I want to soak it all in, the food, art, language, people. I feel like there’s always something new to discover. Maybe I’ll plan a trip for next summer. I should start a savings jar—oh, and I could get my friends on board too. Road trip, maybe? Just throwing ideas around. And what about the environment? I feel like I want to contribute more, do more for sustainability, there’s so much we could do as a community. Organizing a cleanup day at the beach or a tree-planting event! It’s so important to give back, to leave the world a little better than we found it. The more people join in, the more we can make a difference, and it feels good to be part of something bigger. Thinking about that, I should reach out and see if there are any campus clubs focused on sustainability. I bet they’d love new members. I’ve been putting it off, but I’m feeling motivated all of a sudden! Collaboration, community, creativity—it’s all just connected, you know? I can’t shake the thought of how different life is when you surround yourself with positivity. It’s like a chain reaction, everyone feeds off good vibes; it’s contagious. I believe in that! Okay, back to reality, I need to get ready for class soon. I just hope I remember to bring my laptop. That’s always the last-minute scramble. But, whatever happens, I know I’ll figure it out. It’s all about the adventure!

Okay so, I just thought about how weird it is that I can be really chatty and then suddenly the anxiety kicks in like a wave crashing over me, and I'm all over the place. Life feels like a rollercoaster, like one minute I'm laughing with friends about that time we got lost on the way to the concert and the next I’m spiraling into thoughts about whether they really enjoyed the night or if they were just being nice. Why do I even care? I mean, I know I care, but sometimes it feels like there's this little monster in my head just poking at all my insecurities, like “Oh, you thought they liked you? Think again!” Super fun, right? But then I think, wow, I really want to make everyone happy! So I pour all my energy into planning the next hangout. Did I text Julie about that new café? I really hope she’s free this weekend. It looks so cute on Instagram. I’m so excited to try it, and if I can get everyone to come, it’s going to be a blast. I love when everyone's laughing together. Ah, speaking of laughter, that reminds me of last week when we were playing that ridiculous board game that nobody knew the rules to. I ended up making a total fool of myself trying to explain the game, and of course, everyone just kept making fun of me for it, but it was hilarious. We were all crying with laughter. I just hope no one actually thinks I’m incapable or something. Like, what if they think I’m dumb because I couldn’t explain it? But then again, does it really matter? We had fun, right? This is where I get caught in those loops of overthinking. And it’s so exhausting. Do I need to meditate more? I read somewhere that mindfulness could help, but then there’s that anxiety about not being good at meditating. Ugh. Anyway, I need to finish that paper that’s due next week. It’s kind of stressing me out because I really want to do well. I mean, not just for the grade but to prove to myself that I can manage all this craziness. Balancing my social calendar with studies is like juggling fire while riding a unicycle—some days I feel like I’m gonna drop everything and catch fire! I can’t let that happen. I wonder how others manage it. Do they have secret tips or tricks? I should probably ask someone. Maybe even post about it on that group chat we're all in. Everyone’s always so supportive. And it’s nice to have that connection, knowing that we’re all in this together, somehow. It’s comforting. And then there’s the project coming up in sociology, which I’m super excited about! We’re going to look at community dynamics and cultural exchange. It aligns with my interests, which is great, but then there’s that nagging voice that wonders if I’m actually enough to pull it off. Why do I do this to myself? There’s a rush of excitement thinking about interviewing people and getting those captivating stories, though. I just hope I can handle the pressure of deadlines and still keep my social life intact. Maybe I could do both? I daydream about showcasing what I find in the project to my friends. The thought of them being impressed gives me this little buzz of motivation. I love sharing insights, especially when they’re surprising. Oooh, what’s going on with my family this weekend? I need to call mom and see if she’s feeling okay. Last time we spoke she mentioned feeling a little stressed with work, and I want to make sure she knows I’m here for her. I’ve always been the one who wants to fix things, and sometimes that can be a lot to handle, but it’s also really rewarding when she laughs, or when we reminisce about childhood memories. It’s those little moments that can turn a tough week around. But then it hits me again, that sense of worry. What if I’m not doing enough? Am I being the best friend, the best daughter? I don’t want to let anyone down. I crave that affirmation that I’m on the right track. I think if I can keep connecting with people and learning new things, that’ll help me feel more stable. Behind all that nervous energy, there’s this hope that I can keep growing, stretching myself. I saw a documentary about creative people last night. They all talked about how vulnerability is part of the process. It resonated with me because I feel like I’m often on the verge of bursting with ideas and projects but carry this heavy bag of anxiety at the same time. If I could unleash all that, I might feel a bit lighter. But then, what if they don’t like it? Gah, the cycle continues... Okay, I need to snap out of this spiral. Just breathe, right? Deep breaths...

I really should be working on my assignment but here I am, staring at my screen, my mind flitting from one thought to another like a butterfly that’s lost its way. I keep thinking I could have gone into a different major. Maybe psychology or biology? But I chose this path and I’m here, buried under a mountain of deadlines. My phone vibrates; it’s a group chat message. I really don’t want to respond. I never want to respond. I dread those group chats, they make me anxious. I’m not good at small talk, never have been. Just a rapid-fire exchange of pleasantries that lead nowhere. What do they want from me anyway? I’d rather be learning something new, diving deep into theories or concepts that excite me. The other day I found this fascinating article on quantum physics. Why does it have to be so complicated? It’s incredible how the universe works, yet it’s mind-boggling. I often wonder if I could understand it, if I just had more time, but then that thought spirals into the usual worries about grades and whether I’m good enough. I mean, do I even belong here? Sometimes, I question my choices. Was this the right major? I mean, I’m conscientious enough. I’ve got my priorities straight. I do the readings, I make notes, I color-code everything in my planner. It’s just the social aspect that haunts me. I hear the laughter of classmates outside. I don’t know how they do it, maintaining all those friendships and casually joking around. What are they even talking about? Probably something fun and light, like the latest Netflix show or a party coming up. Parties always make my stomach drop. I can't breathe in those crowded spaces. What if I trip over air and end up embarrassing myself in front of everyone? I remember the last event I attended. So many faces, so much noise. My heart was racing. It was like a fight or flight response but I was stuck there, unable to choose. I ended up leaving early, feeling like a ghost. But no one probably even noticed I was gone. That’s what I tell myself. It's better that way. Better to be invisible than to risk rejection or worse, pity. I’d rather spend my evenings reading alone with a cup of tea, which sounds so much nicer anyway. Still, that nagging thought creeps in, why don’t I reach out more? I want to be more open. I love learning about others, their lives, their stories, but the effort it takes to connect is exhausting. So many things to consider. What if I say something wrong? What if they don’t like my opinions? What if I overanalyze everything they say? I hate the thought of it. I’d much rather focus on my work, dive into a book, or experiment with new art projects. I bought those paints the other day but I’m scared to use them. What if I mess up? I want everything to be perfect, the colors blending just right, the composition balanced. Then there’s the pressure, the pressure to perform. Each assignment feels like a test of my worth. Did I choose the right topic for my thesis? Am I taking the right approach? My professor is so intimidating. Sometimes I think she can see right through me, like she knows I’m utterly terrified of failure. I can feel the anxiety bubbling to the surface. Breathe in, breathe out. Just get through today, that’s what I tell myself. But really, I do find beauty in creativity. I love how art and science can intertwine, the mix of logical and abstract. I want to create something meaningful, make connections that matter. I can’t shake the thought that maybe there’s something more out there for me. A different way to channel my neuroticism into something positive, maybe even useful. In the next couple of years, what happens if I find my passion and it’s too late? I shouldn’t have spent so much time worrying about the future. I should focus on now. Letting go is hard. I’m constantly preparing for potential outcomes that I can’t even predict. I open social media and scroll. Everyone’s thriving, or at least they pretend to be. The highlight reel of their lives. I should shut this off. Okay, back to work. I can do this. Just focus. One task at a time. I can handle it. If only I could find my footing, gain a sense of belonging, I might just be okay. The future is still unwritten. I just have to keep moving forward even when my mind tells me to retreat. Maybe today I’ll just start with one sentence. That’s progress.

Alright, so I just saw this video of a guy doing parkour off buildings and it made me think about how I always wanted to try something like that but then I remember I’m terrified of heights like what even. I get that adrenaline rush just watching it though, like the music builds and they jump and it’s almost like flying but not really more like falling with style. I think climbing trees is the closest I've ever gotten to that. I used to spend hours just hanging out in trees, feeling on top of the world but, thankfully, not too high up. It's funny how I love the idea of adventure but sometimes the fear kicks in. I wonder if that’s normal. I feel like we all have these things we want to do but stop ourselves at some point. Like I have this itch to backpack across Europe someday, eat pastries in Paris, and ride a gondola in Venice. Have to remember to not bring too many shoes if I do that, too much luggage is a hassle I can already see myself getting frustrated at airports and stuff, but still it’s exciting to think about. Just imagine meeting people from everywhere and hearing their stories. I’m the type who loves talking to strangers. I went to this coffee shop last week and struck up a convo with this woman who turned out to be an artist and she told me all about her gallery and it was so fascinating, I could have listened for hours. What’s with people being so cool, right? I love meeting them and finding out what makes them tick, like everyone has a story worth telling and I'm all ears. Sometimes I feel like I just get on a roll and babble about my own stuff too much. I wonder if it's annoying, but then again, people usually seem into it, right? Like they laugh or nod along. It’s all about connecting I guess. And speaking of connection, this one time in my class, we did this group project on environmental science, and we all just clicked. We sat together for hours brainstorming and it felt so good because each person had different ideas and we built off each other’s thoughts. I love when that happens, the synergy, it’s like a flow state where everything just works. We ended up making this presentation that was super creative, it felt like we put our hearts into it. Then we nailed the presentation! I could feel the excitement in the air as we wrapped it up, all of us high-fiving because we crushed it. It’s such a rush. Makes me think about how important collaboration is, especially in college where everyone is just trying to find their place. I’ve also been stuck on this thought about how I want to travel somewhere tropical. Not the typical touristy places though. I want to find a small island where I can sit on the beach with a book and a drink and just soak it all in. This idea of sun and sand is calling my name, and I can almost feel the warmth already. There’s something freeing about the ocean, the way it stretches on forever makes me feel small but also infinite, like anything is possible. I think about how when I get out there I’ll meet locals who’ll tell me where to find the best hidden spots. I crave authenticity in experiences, not just the highlights that everyone posts on social media, but the real stuff, the conversations that linger in your memory. Those conversations often lead to deeper understanding, and I feel that’s what the world needs a bit more of. Kindness, openness, just genuine care for one another, you know? You see people rushing everywhere, in their own bubbles, but what if they paused to say hi? Or compliment a stranger? I once complimented a girl on her shoes, and she lit up like it made her day. It's so simple, but it can change someone’s mood. Kindness spreads like wildfire, I swear! I wonder if I talk too much about kindness, I hope I don’t sound preachy. I think it’s just that I’ve seen how it can really alter someone's perspective. So when I think about the future, I see a lot of beautiful moments ahead. I want to keep exploring, keep meeting new faces and embracing new cultures. The world is so rich and colorful. There’s a festival in Brazil I’ve read about, Carnival, where everyone just dances and lets loose, and I think, yes, I want to be part of that energy! It just sounds like pure happiness. I bet the music is infectious, and I’d just move with the crowd, feeling the beat pulse through me. Have to make that a priority when I get a chance. And let’s not even get started on food, my goodness! Every region has something unique to offer, and food is this universal language that just brings people together. I love trying out different cuisines, discovering these little places that serve something authentic, and sitting there with friends just enjoying each bite. That shared experience, trying something for the first time, it’s this small thrill that feels like a mini-adventure every time. I should definitely keep exploring all these thoughts, weaving them into my dreams and actions toward this big future that feels wide open, just waiting for me to run into it with arms wide.

I just thought about how I always forget where I put my keys. Like, every single time I walk out the door. It’s probably some kind of cosmic joke about my own lack of organization. But I think it's more about how my mind is always racing off into different directions instead of focusing on the mundane. I mean, who really cares where their keys are when there are so many interesting things to think about? But I do care when I’m late to class, which I often am. Time feels like a construct that doesn’t apply to me most days. There’s this strange appeal to spontaneity that I can’t shake off. I sometimes wish I could just pick up and go somewhere far away without any plans. Wouldn't it be cool? Just get in a car and drive until I hit the ocean or something. Or maybe to a small town where nobody knows my name and I could blend in with the locals, become someone else for a little while. I used to think about this a lot when I was younger. Now it’s just a flickering thought that dances in and out. Thinking about college life is weird. I see people rushing around with their coffee and backpacks, and I’m just here, observing. There’s a certain beauty in just watching things unfold, like a documentary unfolding in real-time. But then there’s a part of me that wants to dive into those conversations — but not really, like when you want to jump into a pool but you’re not sure if the water’s cold. Sometimes I think I’m too wrapped up in my own thoughts. I had an idea for a short story last night. It hit me out of nowhere, like lightning. It was about a guy who finds an old map in a library and decides to follow it. But the map leads him on this wild adventure that isn’t just physical but more psychological, like an exploration of his regrets. I like those kinds of plots. I’m drawn to characters who are quiet but have a lot going on beneath the surface. I’ve met a few like that. We never really connect fully, but there's this unspoken understanding. I once had a friend who could talk for hours. Endlessly. I appreciated her energy, but I felt like a wall sometimes. She would throw her thoughts at me, and I’d just nod, trying to keep up. But she didn’t realize that sometimes I was more interested in listening to the sounds around us, like the way the wind rustles through the trees or the distant laugh of kids playing. I guess that’s how I connect with the world. Through sounds and sights instead of words. People don't always appreciate silence, but I think it's golden. Then there’s that whole thing about group projects. Ugh. I can’t stand them. Everyone else gets all excited about collaboration and team spirit. I just want to do my part and be left alone! Why can’t we just hand in individual assignments? I feel like I’m dragging my feet while everyone else runs ahead, and it’s frustrating. It’s not that I don’t like people; I just prefer to operate solo. Sometimes I think about traveling. I never feel anxious about it, but I constantly daydream about weird places I’d like to see. I don’t care much for typical tourist spots—give me the weird art installations and off-beat museums. Those places resonate with me more than any famous monument ever could. I’m drawn to what’s not mainstream, the hidden treasures tucked away in corners of a city or tucked in the back of a thrift store. I can wander for hours. There’s an art gallery nearby I’ve been meaning to visit for ages. I love how art can evoke feelings without saying a word. Like, there’s something about a painting that can capture chaos or tranquility, and I desperately want to see how different artists interpret the world. It’s like looking through different lenses, some cracked, some pristine. But sometimes I keep putting it off as if I’m waiting for the ‘right time’ to go. What even is that? Why do we wait for perfect moments when life is pretty much a series of chaotic mishaps anyway? Okay, so…another thought. Food. I think I might explore more culinary adventures. I’ve been stuck in a rut with meals lately. Maybe I’ll try cooking something outside of my comfort zone. But then again, cooking requires focus, and focus isn’t always my strongest suit. I mean, I burnt toast yesterday, and it was just toast! How hard could it be, right? I think it’d be fun to create something after all this—maybe a zine or a blog that just captures all these random thoughts and ideas. That way, I wouldn’t have to worry about what anyone else thinks. Just publish whatever comes to mind. Freedom. I crave that freedom in expression. Oh, and my keys! I really should find them before I forget again. But then again, what if I just leave them where they are and embark on an adventure instead?

it’s 2:47 PM and I’m sitting here surrounded by a pile of books I’ve been meaning to get through but I keep getting distracted by all these other thoughts like should I reorganize my bookshelf by color or genre or author or just get rid of half of them because I’ll never get around to reading them but then again what if I do want to read that one book on behavioral economics that I bought three years ago and then there’s that one course I could take next semester that dives deep into psychology and I’m already considering how I might connect that with my current major and if it’ll actually help my future or if it’s just more academic fluff that sounds cool but has no real-world application but then I start thinking about how I’ve been watching too many documentaries and maybe I should just focus on my own projects instead of getting lost in other people’s research or ideas or whatever. my plans for the weekend are already forming in my head, solo hiking seems most appealing but the weather’s unpredictable. I can’t stand crowds, so staying in the city could drive me nuts. library or nature, those are my options. binge-watching some obscure show I stumbled upon last night was a nice distraction from my critical thinking assignments but I feel guilty about it like I should’ve been working on that paper instead but it’s hard to stay in a headspace where I can churn out 2000 words of coherent thoughts when I’ve got all this other stuff buzzing around like what’s for dinner or how this semester is moving way too fast for my taste I mean I swear my calendar is just a collection of deadlines and reminders now. also, the prof in my history class keeps mentioning this one philosopher and I feel this intense itch to explore all of his works but it’s overwhelming to think about diving into in-depth analysis for every single one. it’s like I’m stuck in this perpetual loop of wanting more knowledge and having a hard time figuring out where to start as if I need a roadmap for my brain to navigate which ideas do I chase down and which ones are just distractions. oh and coffee, how did I forget about coffee? the way it becomes this ritual in the morning and I find myself brewing a fresh pot and thinking about the day but then I start losing focus on even enjoying it because I’m already jumping ahead to this meeting at 12 and what I need to prepare for that or how I have to remember to follow up on emails – the never-ending cycle of electronic communication is exhausting, people can be so unpredictable in their responses or lack thereof and it's just this constant reminder that I thrive in structure. I like knowing what comes next. I was thinking about that time I tried to bring up a counterpoint in class and it didn’t go well and I felt like everyone just stared at me and half of them were totally zoned out and I hated that moment but not for the reasons you’d think like I wasn’t ashamed of speaking up, no it’s more that I felt like people just didn’t care to engage and that’s kind of the downside of group discussions, which is ironic because usually I’m all about intellectual debate but not when there’s no real passion or interest behind it. back to the study I’m working on—maybe I need to revisit my hypotheses. some part of me wonders why I chose this route, the endless hours of combing through data, analyzing trends, writing up findings that I hope others will find interesting or useful but it’s like pulling teeth sometimes, the pressure to make it sound brilliant and innovative when really it’s just raw information dressed up in jargon. and then there’s the possibility of getting feedback and how I dread it yet crave it at the same time, it’s like I want validation but I’m equally worried about the critique. Maybe my perfectionism gets in the way, I mean I try so hard to put together something that’s flawless and misses the mark because the process isn’t about being perfect it’s about learning and growing and yet I keep chasing this unattainable standard. okay, changing gears, I need to remind myself to take breaks, I should probably consult that self-care list I came up with last month, go for a run, meditate, draw something, that always helps clear my mind but that requires initiative that sometimes I just don’t have or wonder if I’m wasting time because every minute should be productive but what does that even mean when you're just one person in an ocean of obligations? sometimes I just want to throw it all out and start fresh. a clean slate sounds nice but every time I consider it I realize I really do enjoy the depth of my interests. it’s the contradictions that make life interesting after all.

I keep thinking about that time I forgot my best friend’s birthday. I can’t believe I did that. I swear I had it marked on my calendar but somehow it slipped my mind. Now I feel this heavy guilt like a rock in my stomach. She was so excited about it too. I should’ve just called. A simple call. I overthink everything though; what if I said the wrong thing? What if I stuttered or took too long to find the words? I wish I could be one of those people who just talk and talk without a care in the world, like my roommate. She’s always making friends, and I’m just trying to not trip over my words when I order coffee. It’s hard to connect when you’re constantly worrying about how you come off. What if I’m boring or too shy? I miss going to the bookstore. The smell of the pages and the quiet, it feels safe. I like to browse for hours, looking for hidden gems that nobody else seems to want. I wonder what someone would think if they saw me stroking the spines of old dusty books. I think maybe they wouldn’t get me. I don’t even get me sometimes. Should I even be out in public? What if I start to sweat or get that tight feeling in my chest? But then again, if I don’t go out and take some chances, what’s the point? I want to be open to the world. I read in an article once that being open-minded leads to more happiness. I crave that feeling of joy but I don’t know how to bridge that gap between dreaming and doing. I remember deciding to organize a study group last semester, thinking it would help everyone get motivated to study. I planned it so meticulously. I sent out group messages, set reminders, made a schedule. I was proud of myself for being so organized, but then when nobody showed up, I felt really embarrassed and anxious, like I was exposed. Were they waiting for my invitation? Did I pick the wrong time? I kept checking my phone, hoping for someone to text me that they were running late. But no one did. Maybe they all had their own stuff going on, I mean, life’s busy. I get that. I tried to convince myself it wasn’t personal, that I hadn’t failed anyone. But I just kept replaying things in my head. How could I have done it differently? Yesterday, I was sitting in the library, and someone was studying biology next to me. They had flashcards laid out, and I could see them mumbling to themselves, trying to memorize. I wanted to ask if they needed help, or if they could share their study tips, but instead, I just sat there staring, feeling a tight knot in my throat. It bothers me how much I hold back. I’m so agreeable, but it’s almost like I forget about my own needs and thoughts in the process. I don’t want to be pushy, but I also feel like I have valuable insights. What if they don’t value my help? What if they recoil at my suggestions? It’s a tiring cycle. Sometimes I can’t help but feel like a ghost, just floating around while life happens. My mind goes back to a conversation I had with my mom last week. She always encourages me to take risks, to step outside my comfort zone, but it’s so hard. I mean, I don't want to disappoint her, but I also feel pressure to meet society's expectations. She doesn’t understand the anxiety that creeps up sometimes when I’m faced with new situations. I felt so bad when I told her I canceled going to that party. It’s like I let her down, and then she tried to reassure me, which only made me feel worse. Shouldn’t I be able to just go to a party like everyone else? But the thought of being around so many people, the noise, the possible judgment, it all becomes this overwhelming wave crashing down. And then there’s my paper that’s due next week. I can’t get it started. I keep rewriting the thesis in my head, but nothing feels right. I keep imagining how it will sound, if it’s too boring or not academic enough. The pressure feels like a weight on my shoulders. I keep trying to remind myself to just write, just get something down, but I find myself reading the same paragraph over and over again and the words just blur together. I know I need to focus, but every thought pulls me away from what I’m supposed to do. I like being thorough and conscientious, but I want to also be free enough to just let ideas flow. Why is it such a battle? Okay, I need to breathe. I really just need some quiet time with my thoughts. Maybe I’ll take a walk later, or just sit in that favorite corner of the library. Maybe that will help me reset. I wish I could find that balance between engaging with the world and retreating into my own thoughts. It’s a hard dance to learn, but I think maybe I’ll get there eventually.

Okay so the clock is ticking and I can feel that rush, that pressure of time. My phone buzzed earlier, something about a meme that a friend sent me and I laughed out loud, it was so ridiculous, but I forgot to reply because then I saw another notification. How many notifications can one person handle in a day? I love it and hate it at the same time. People always saying to turn them off, “It's good for your mental health.” But why would I want to miss out on the latest gossip? Like my friend Karen just broke up with her boyfriend, I feel so bad for her but also part of me is like “Yay more time to hang out with her!” Who doesn’t love a good breakup story, right? It’s like watching a TV drama unfold in real life and I can be the supportive friend but also what if I can't help her? I get anxious just thinking about it, like what if I make it worse? I tend to overthink everything. Then there’s that group project due soon. I’m really not that into it, I mean I just want to show up and let others do the heavy lifting. But of course, we all know that doesn’t end well, right? I should probably do my part, but I just can’t help but hope that someone else takes charge and I get all the glory without actually doing much work. I mean isn’t that how it’s supposed to work? I get so distracted. Last week I spent an hour just scrolling through TikTok instead of reading those boring articles. The “For You” page gets me every time. Those dance challenges make me want to join in but then I remember I can't dance. My rhythm is terrible, but that doesn’t stop me from trying to figure out how to sway my hips like in those videos. It would be so fun to hang out with friends and just dance like nobody's watching. Speaking of friends, I just miss the huge crowds sometimes. I thrive in those situations, you know? The laughter, the noise, the connections—it’s electric. But then it gets overwhelming too. Like too much talking, too much energy from everyone, and my head spins. I start to feel anxious, like I have to keep up with everyone’s mood. I wish I could just relax and not care so much about what they think. It’s exhausting. Still, missing out gives me anxiety too. I hate not being invited, but then when I am invited, I sometimes dread the social exhaustion afterward. Such a damn conundrum. Why can't I just be like everyone else who goes and has a good time without thinking about it? It makes me crazy. And then I think about my grades. They are so not where I want them to be. I feel like I could be studying harder, but there's this part of me that just wants to keep chatting and having fun. I end up on some random YouTube rabbit hole instead of cracking open those textbooks. The panic sets in when I see my professor’s email saying we need to discuss my grades. What do they even want to talk about? It's always the same thing—“You have potential.” Yeah, right. How do I balance fun and responsibility? I’m still trying to figure that out, like all those TikTok hacks that promise to make you productive, but I'm just not buying into them. I want to go out instead. Party, chat, meet people, and go through life like it’s one big group project where everyone just gets along. I also feel bad because I know deep down I’m like the person who tries to please everyone. If someone asks me to do something, even if I don’t want to, I find it hard to say no, and then I’m stuck feeling drained and overwhelmed. Being agreeable is a double-edged sword. Shouldn’t I just stand up for myself? But I think about how I’d hurt their feelings or how I’d come off as rude and I can’t handle the thought of them being upset. It spirals. It’s all about keeping the peace and being the fun one, right? I feel like I’m living in a sitcom where the laugh track plays every time someone tells a joke and I’m just there with a smile, giving high-fives, but inside I’m like, “What am I doing?” Oh, and there’s this thing with friends picking where to go eat. It’s like a never-ending cycle of indecision. I’m not picky. I’ll eat whatever, but seriously how hard is it for a group to agree on pizza or sushi? But no, everyone has their weird preferences and then it turns into a debate that could ignite World War III. People are so intense about food! I mean, I get that it’s important but come on! Just pick something! Man, I need to keep this flow going. It’s almost like therapy, just laying it all out without any filters. I can’t wait to hit that send button. Hope it makes sense.

Okay so I was just thinking about how I can never sit still for too long, like I’m always bouncing around from one thing to the next, way too many interests, there’s just so much to explore and learn about! I can't help it I need stimulation, like my phone, it's constantly buzzing with notifications, but that’s fine, I love it, the adrenaline rush of answering messages, scrolling through social media, posting photos, connecting, like how did I end up in this digital age where I can literally talk to people across the world? Sometimes I catch myself getting lost in conversations late at night. I wonder if that means anything. My mind races from one idea to the next, just like my conversations, I can be in the middle of one topic and then suddenly switch to something completely random and off-the-wall. The weather, then dogs, then politics, then what did that guy on TikTok say about aliens? It’s like my brain is an internet browser with too many tabs open, all the time. I should really work on focusing more but I just get bored. But then again, being bored is a choice, right? I mean, I could pick up a book, but a lot of the time I choose to scroll through memes instead. Memes, they’re a whole cultural phenomenon, aren’t they? It’s wild thinking how they can explode overnight and be the language of an entire generation. I love how they can encapsulate feelings and ideas in such a succinct way. Creativity at its finest. Speaking of creativity, I need to get back to painting; I picked up a brush a few months ago for the first time after a long break and wow what a rush it was! I can’t believe I ever stopped. The smell of paint, the colors swirling together, the freedom to just create something out of nothing. I’ve got so many ideas for pieces just floating around in my mind and it feels like they need to be manifested, like I need to be in a studio with music blasting, splattering paint, dancing around with a brush in my hand, just vibing with the canvas. I really hope my friend is free this weekend. We’ve been talking about checking out that new art gallery downtown. I heard they have this insane installation that changes based on crowd interaction. How cool is that? I can already picture myself walking through it, feeling the energy, taking in all the sights and sounds, capturing it with my phone to share with everyone. I just love sharing experiences, it’s like I’m constantly curating this living scrapbook of my life. Every moment is a photo opportunity, I see beauty everywhere, especially in the weird stuff. Like, I don’t just stop and smell the roses, I’m out there smelling everything, literally. There’s a whole world out there, every corner of every street has its own story and I want to hear them all. I wish I could be a travel blogger or something, hopping from country to country, collecting experiences and sharing them. But planning trips is exhausting and there’s so much to consider. I’m drawn to spontaneity too though, like that time I bought a last-minute ticket to a concert in another city. It was one of the best nights ever! Just me and a bunch of strangers singing together, no inhibitions, just pure joy. I love that feeling of being completely immersed in an experience, the energy of a crowd is electric. It’s like I thrive in those situations, absorbing everything around me. But then again, I sometimes feel trapped by expectations and social norms. Like, why is it that we must act a certain way or follow the same path as everyone else? I mean, I respect traditions but c’mon let’s shake things up a bit! I went to this random open mic night last week and there were people sharing their poetry and music and it was raw and real and beautiful. I thought about getting up there, but then chickened out. I hate when that happens. But maybe next time, maybe I will. Going outside my comfort zone is good for growth, right? Okay, shifting gears; food! What’s a good place to eat this week? I’ve been craving ramen, like deep, rich broth, slurping noodles, and then dessert, always dessert! I’m really into trying out new restaurants, especially ones with unique items on the menu. Don’t give me the basics, I want weird flavors mixed together, I want to feel like I’m on an adventure every time I eat. And dessert is non-negotiable, it’s like the cherry on top of every experience. I’ll drive across town for a killer slice of cake, no shame in that. Maybe I should invite my friends to try a new spot, turn it into a mini food tour. It’ll be fun and chaotic, and there’s nothing better than laughter and good food shared with great people. That’s the essence of living, isn’t it? Moments made with others, laughter echoing in the air, just being present. It’s all about creating memories for me, that’s what life is about!

I just remembered that time last month when we all went to that concert and it felt like the entire world was there with us. Crazy energy. I love that feeling of being surrounded by people who are just as hyped as I am. It’s like the crowd becomes this living, breathing thing. The music was so loud I could feel it in my chest, and everybody was singing along. I wish every weekend could be like that. Sometimes I get so caught up in having fun that I forget about responsibilities, like laundry or studying. But honestly, who cares about laundry when there are good vibes to soak in? Maybe I should just embrace being a bit messy. I mean, nobody really notices if my room’s a disaster when I have friends over, right? It’s all about the laughs, the memories, and the spontaneity. I think I thrive in social situations. Like when I walk into a party and immediately feel the pulse of music mixed with laughter, it’s like a boost of energy shoots through me. I love saying hi to people, giving random hugs, striking up conversations with whoever looks interesting. I remember this one party where I ended up talking to someone for hours, just about everything and nothing. I love that connection, the way people can bond over the simplest things like shared favorite snacks or that one movie that everyone watched as kids. The nostalgia is real. Speaking of nostalgia, sometimes I think about my childhood and those endless summers. Riding bikes, playing tag, the simplicity of it all. No phones, just pure freedom. I wonder if kids today miss out on that with all the screens. I mean, I get it, technology is amazing, but I can't help but feel they’re not experiencing the same magic we did, like that feeling of running through the sprinklers or building forts in the backyard. I really enjoy just chatting with anyone, like, I could talk to a wall and maybe it would talk back if it had a personality. I think that’s why I get along with almost everyone. It’s easy for me to find common ground. I noticed that people tend to open up to me. Maybe it’s the whole smiley vibe I give off or my tendency to nod enthusiastically. I hope they realize that I genuinely care about what they have to say—a mix of interest and empathy. There’s something fulfilling about hearing stories, knowing everyone has a unique perspective. But then, sometimes I feel a bit guilty for not diving deep into more serious topics. I mean, there’s a lot of important stuff happening in the world, and I find myself avoiding heavy discussions. I think I prefer keeping it light and fun. I notice that I tend to steer conversations towards humor or entertainment. I guess that’s my way of coping; laughter is powerful. I love making my friends laugh. If I can lighten someone’s mood just by being silly, I’m all in. Even in classes, my mind tends to drift. I’m more interested in the social dynamics than grades. It’s funny how I can be surrounded by brilliant people and still feel like I’m just happy being there without the pressure of competing. I mean, I love when group projects happen because it’s less about the work and more about the interactions. I can just chat and throw ideas around without feeling the weight of deadlines. The group energy is electric when you find your groove. But sometimes, I think I really need to buckle down and deal with the boring stuff too. My friends are always telling me I should get more organized, maybe start a planner or something. I see them making lists of things to do and I’m over here with a chaotic jumble in my mind, thinking, “I’ll remember later.” Spoiler alert: I don’t. I should probably have that checklist on my phone or something, but it feels like such a chore. Maybe it’s just my nature to trust that I’ll figure it all out in the end. I don’t know, maybe it’s all about balance. I can’t be all fun and no responsibility, right? But then again, where’s the joy in being super serious all the time? I like to think the world is brighter when you can laugh at the little things, make a joke out of a bad situation. Like when you spill coffee on your shirt but end up with a funny story instead. I think that’s what makes life colorful—the unexpected moments, the laughter, the connection. That’s my jam. I just want to keep soaking it all up. I could go on about this forever, but then I’d probably lose track of time, like I always do when I’m having fun.

This pen is weird, I mean, who even chose this? My fingers are kind of slipping on it which is really annoying because I keep having to adjust my grip and I hate it when something disrupts my flow. Flow states are so crucial, I’ve read about people getting into them while painting or writing or even running. I could never run, I mean, why would anyone choose something so exhausting when you could just walk, or better yet, ride a bike. God, biking is such a rush. Wind in your hair, music blasting, this feeling of being alive, moving forward, cutting through the air. Speaking of music, I’m so into this new album I found last week. Indie rock but like with a twist, they blend so many styles. I love when bands take risks, it’s like they’re inviting you to their personal journey, and I want to scream the lyrics at the top of my lungs. But then, what if someone hears? Ugh, that would be so awkward. I hate the idea of anyone thinking I’m trying too hard or that I’m some wannabe hipster. But honestly, I just love music. It fuels the energy I need to keep going through my days, especially the stressful ones. And speaking of stress, my professor has been super intense about those deadlines.
