I mean, who even makes a syllabus with so many projects piled one after another? It’s like a creative destruction zone in my brain. I can’t even decide where to start. Should I focus on research for my paper, or am I better off drafting the design project I promised I’d finish this week? Or maybe I should tackle that group presentation that is just looming over us like a dark cloud. Oh, and group projects—they make me want to punch a wall. Honestly, why are there always those frustrating moments of total chaos? A fast-paced meeting where no one knows what they’re doing. It’s like everyone brings their hesitance to the table, and I just wanna yell, “Why don’t we just get it together?” I mean, I know I should be more patient, but it drives me insane to see so much wasted potential. I want to lead, but I also want them to just get stuff done — is that too much to ask? I know it’s not fair; I’m probably too hard on them, but I can’t help it. Then I think about relationships. Friends are complicated, aren’t they? I love being around new people. Meeting someone new is like unboxing a mystery gift. It’s electric. But then there are days when I really wish people would just stop talking. The endless small talk—it’s exhausting sometimes. Like, can we skip the pleasantries and dive into something real? Let’s talk about what really matters, please! But then, what is “real” anyway? I’ve been thinking about that lately. What do people consider genuine? I guess it varies from person to person. I love the idea of deep conversations, but oh man, they can get so heavy. What if we talk about something dark? What if I spill my truths and someone judges me? I think about all the parts of me that are messy, and I wonder if I should just keep those to myself. It’s hard to find that balance between being open and being too exposed. Dinner can be a social landmine, especially when everyone wants to do different things. Some friends are like “Let’s go for sushi!” and others are “I’m feeling pizza!” Why can’t we just agree that food is food? But then, people have their preferences, and I get that. I sometimes wish I could just go somewhere new, try something adventuresome — maybe Ethiopian food. I’ve never had that before. It would be fun to explore that world. Okay, focus. I have to remember that I have homework to do. But what if I end up procrastinating again? Ugh. I have this weird habit of diving into random things on the internet when I should be working. Remember last week I went down that rabbit hole about rare bananas? It’s an actual thing. Who knew? Bananas, people! I can tell you all about them and how they’re actually endangered or something. It’s such a weird part of life. Time’s ticking, and I’m just here rambling. I feel like I’m always doing that, just spilling out thoughts like a broken faucet. I can’t keep my mind in one place; it’s like a crowded room filled with chatter. I’m always moving, always thinking, never slow, never quiet. There’s so much to explore and discover; I just want to dive into everything. But there’s also that undercurrent of anxiety that ties my stomach in knots. What if I can’t keep up? What if it’s all too much? I guess I’ll just keep going, one thought at a time, one project at a time, because that’s what I do.

Okay so I'm sitting here and I can't help but think about that party last weekend. It was packed, like super packed, and I thrived in that chaos. All those people! I'm made for these kinds of situations. I love the loud music and the laughter and the random conversations that spark out of nowhere. Like this one guy, he was telling me about his job in sales and how he convinced a whole company to switch their whole system. I don't even care about sales, but I kept asking questions because it's just fun to see how people get excited when they talk about something they're passionate about. I like when chaos is happening around me. It’s energizing. I can't stand when things are calm and quiet for too long. I’ll go stir up some activity. I don’t really think of myself as a people-pleaser, but I guess I just find joy in bringing people together. I remember getting everyone to play that ridiculous drinking game. It was like one of those “never have I ever” type things, but more outrageous. I always think of ways to break the ice and get people laughing. It’s like I light up when I see someone’s face change from bored to engaged. I wonder how it is for people who don’t get those social highs. Then again, I notice certain things about how some folks don’t mesh well. Like that girl in the corner who looked totally overwhelmed. I don't get that. If I were her, I’d just jump in and talk to someone. I tend to drive the conversation, and I can’t help but feel a little frustrated when people don’t engage back. Why not just say what’s on your mind? I mean, it’s all about connecting, right? But then, maybe that's just me. I think it’s my conscientiousness; I like to be engaging while being organized and purposeful. I can’t stand just standing around. Then there’s that guy, he was trying to be the peacemaker, going around trying to get everyone to chill and relax. I mean, come on, dude. You gotta bring the energy, not tone it down! Someone needs to step up and bring life into the room. I guess some people get anxious in those situations. I just roll with whatever. Social gatherings are my jam. I thrive off the energy of others, the back-and-forth banter, the laughs that can turn into something deeper. I don’t mind if people can’t get along, as long as we’re all together. It doesn’t bother me if there are cliques. So I left the party feeling pumped. I was just vibing. But then there’s the next day. I wake up and I’ve got a million things on my mind. My study schedule. Classes. I feel like I’m flying through it all. I have to keep everything organized. I’ve got my planner, the color-coded sections. I’m diligent about following through with my tasks. I write to-do lists while others play games or binge-watch shows. The satisfaction of checking things off? That’s a rush on its own. I know some think it’s boring, but nah, I find it revitalizing. I think about friendships, too. I don’t invest in them lightly. I like to keep a close circle. I keep it tight and that’s it, you know? I don’t have time for drama or soft-hearted discussions. I like straightforwardness. If you’ve got something to say, just say it. All this fluff doesn’t work for me. I’d rather focus on the action. Let’s hit the gym! Go for a hike! Grab food, something solid. Also, I wonder why people are so hesitant to plan. Let’s get things done! I’m not saying we can’t chill, but what’s the point of sitting around? I like routine mixed with those spontaneous moments. Keep some structure but leave space for fun. I don’t get why people can’t see that. Maybe they overthink it? That doesn’t really come into play for me. I just do it. I get that sometimes it's hard for people to balance but sometimes I think they make it harder than it is. Just jump in, make relationships, explore, have fun! So many experiences to take advantage of, and it’s all about that energy! Why are people ok with just going through the motions? I need purpose, but I want it layered over some good times. Can't believe how many don’t prioritize that. I’m all about results, making connections. There’s so much life to live. You can't just sit and wait for it to happen. You have to make it happen.

that blue pen I always carry around it’s starting to run out of ink and I can’t decide if I want to buy a new one or just refill this one but then there’s something satisfying about a fresh pen like all the possibilities of writing something new and I think about how I’m surrounded by people all the time like at the dorms and in class I can’t imagine being alone and that makes me anxious sometimes like I want to keep people close but what if they don’t want to be close back like I really care about my friends but I can get clingy it’s just that I need that reassurance like I’m always checking in seeing how they’re doing and if they’re okay because what if they’re not okay and they just don’t tell me and my heart races thinking about that and I start to feel sweaty and what if I mess things up I have this tendency to overthink everything like I can think myself into a spiral about a text message like did I sound dumb or too excited or not excited enough and I think back to last weekend when we all went out and I had a blast but did I dominate the conversation too much I hope I didn’t but I can’t help it I get all pumped up and everyone else is laughing and I can’t stop myself and it feels good to be the center of attention but then I worry someone might feel left out and I don’t want that I just want everyone to be happy but how do I balance that like I sometimes think I need to dial it back but then I feel so alive when I’m sharing stories and jokes and getting people to join in and it’s weird thinking that it can be both thrilling and terrifying at the same time I crave connection but I also fear losing it and that’s such a contradiction and I overthink almost everything it’s exhausting but it’s like a default setting in my brain constantly analyzing everything in social situations but I need to be there like at the parties and the gatherings because that’s where I recharge my batteries and then there’s that feeling of dread the thought of spending a weekend alone even just the idea feels suffocating limited like the walls are closing in on me and I get fidgety thinking about it and yeah I know I’m a little too intense sometimes but I can’t turn off the enthusiasm it just bursts out like soda shaken up too much and I just want people to see my excitement I guess I worry about being too much but I can’t help it when I’m excited it just spills out like if I’m talking about classes or projects I’m involved in and I have to remember to tone it down sometimes but what if they find it annoying but when we talk about being stressed out or anxious it makes me feel connected too like we’re all in this together right but then I wonder if I’m too empathetic like do they really want to hear me ask how they are doing or am I just annoying them it goes back to the idea of wanting to be liked which is a big deal for me and it can feel like a heavy burden and I think about how my friends are probably much more chill than I am and that makes me feel like I have to keep it together like I can’t let them see me stressed because I want to be the happy one the one who brings lightness in the room and that can also be exhausting like the idea of being a beacon for other people when sometimes I just want to curl up and hide but I can’t I feel a responsibility to keep everyone buoyed up and then I spiral into thinking even further about how I can help them succeed and if they’re doing alright on their assignments it’s like I’m taking their worries on as well like I can’t help but feel invested in their journeys too and then there’s that nagging voice in my head questioning if I’m being genuine or just performing and I think about how my parents are so proud of my grades and they’ve always drilled into us that hard work pays off and I try to remind myself of that when I feel overwhelmed but it feels like a tightrope walking and I don’t want to fall off on either side I’m thinking about next semester and how I want to keep up with my classes and maintain my GPA but what if I slip and I can’t let that happen I have to be responsible so I jot down notes and set reminders to stay organized while wondering if I’m being too rigid but I need that structure because I can’t just float around without a plan and I need to know what the next week looks like as sure as I breathe because that gives me a sense of stability which I crave so much since everything else feels so chaotic sometimes and the noise the chatter around me can feel overwhelming at times but I don’t want silence either it’s like a constant dance between craving social interactions and yet wanting to retreat and when I’m in the middle of a crowded space I feel a buzzing excitement like I’m part of something bigger but I can also feel the anxiety creeping in and it’s like I’m on a rollercoaster of emotions every day and I just wonder if anyone else feels this whirlwind or if I’m alone in this crazy mix of high energy and low-key panic because it’s all blending together in this beautiful mess and that’s just life I guess.

the clock says 2:14 am. I still haven’t finished that paper due tomorrow. I should be working on it but my mind keeps wandering. There’s that pile of laundry staring at me from the corner. It’s like a mountain of evidence of my procrastination. I could just throw it all in the wash but do I even have enough quarters left? Oh god what if I don’t. I should check. But then again, I’d have to get up. I hate getting up. Especially now. It's cozy here. I don’t remember the last time I actually felt cozy. Everything is just too loud sometimes. People talking, laughing, the sound of traffic outside, it’s overwhelming. I wish I could just tune it all out. the thought of going out makes my stomach twist into knots. I used to think it was just anxiety but is it social fatigue too? or am I just a hermit? I’d probably prefer being a hermit. But is it really that bad? To not want to be around people? I mean I can be social sometimes, but just like sporadically I guess. I get this feeling like I need to recharge and sometimes I feel bad about it like I should be more engaged. Should I? Do I need to pretend to be interested in everyone’s small talk? or can I just nod occasionally and stare off into space without it being rude? I think I’d rather be at home reading something weird and wonderful, diving into a book, getting lost in it, rather than trying to decipher social cues every five seconds. Why is it that social interactions drain me? Like a literal energy vampire. The more I’m out, the more I feel like a wilted plant. It’s a constant battle between wanting to connect and wanting solitude. I guess that’s the neuroticism kicking in, always second-guessing, overthinking every little detail. Like that time I had to give a presentation. I can still feel the sweat trickling down my back. My voice shaking, thoughts slipping away like sand through my fingers. I wonder if anyone noticed. I mean probably not, but what if they did? What if they thought I was an idiot? Self-doubt creeps in like an unwelcome guest. Can’t they just leave me alone? Okay, back to the paper… it's about environmental impact. I should care. Everyone says I should care. But I keep thinking about how I can’t find the right sources and how all the research seems so stale and recycled. It’s the same arguments over and over. Makes me want to yell sometimes. Or just ignore it and binge-watch something instead. I should be doing that instead of this. Watch something mindless. My brain needs to switch off, but I can’t give in to that temptation. I know I’ll only end up feeling worse. But, hey, what about my dreams? what if I just want to explore the world in my own way? Is that so wrong? Some people travel to find themselves but I feel like I’d be better at finding myself in a book, in a strange world far away from here. Isolation has its charms but I’m not sure if it’s a good thing. Does it make me weird? I mean who defines weird, anyway? Is it society, or is it just me? Is being too open-minded a flaw? I’m not sure what it is but sometimes I feel like I hold too many ideas, too many thoughts swirling around. I want to challenge everything. Why don’t people question more? It’s like they’re satisfied with the mundane, and it frustrates me. It’s not about being agreeable or trying to fit in, but it’s like there’s a huge contrast between how I view the world and how everyone else does. I mean we’re all in this together, right? Or are we just pretending? I probably shouldn’t be overanalyzing things like this. Let it be. But how can I let it be? I go on rabbit holes of thoughts. I can’t help but think maybe if I express this, if I pour it all out now, it’ll make more sense later. I want to be able to be open to possibilities, to experience everything life offers, but the weight of expectation is suffocating. And I'm holding a lot of that weight. Why do I care so much about what others think? Maybe it’s the low agreeableness too. I get it, I’m not a people-pleaser. Quite the opposite really. I push back against norms. But sometimes I just want to fade into the background. to be inconspicuous. I crave the freedom to explore while still being hidden, like a chameleon. Oh god, I can’t believe I’m analogizing myself to a lizard. Time to close those tabs and focus, right? But now I’m looking at the clock again. 2:30 am. This paper is going nowhere. 2:30 am and all I’ve done is think about how I don’t want to think. I should start typing something. Anything. Anything at all.

I can't believe I forgot to text Jamie about the concert. She'll be so bummed if I'm not there, but I got so wrapped up in studying for that history exam. Ugh, I can't let my friends down, especially when they always drag me out because they know I love a good time. It's like, I live for those spontaneous adventures, like that time we went road-tripping to the beach at 2 AM just because the weather looked nice. I mean, how can you resist the call of the ocean? The salty air, the waves crashing, the thrill of just GOING. And it feels like there's so much to experience, I want to soak it all in, every single second. Speaking of soaking things in, my roommate's been so into cooking lately. She made this incredible pasta dish the other night, and it tasted like heaven in a bowl. I can't even boil an egg without burning it, but she’s always experimenting with flavors. I love watching her, it sparks my creativity too. Maybe I should try to learn something new. Potluck coming up next week, I could totally bring something, even if it’s just a dessert from that bakery down the street. The red velvet cupcakes from there are to die for. Okay, wait, what was I saying? Oh, right! Friends! I think it’s so important to keep those connections alive. I mean, the other day, I called up Sarah out of the blue, just to see how she was doing. I just had this feeling that she could use a chat. She's been going through a rough patch with her family, and I hate that she feels alone. I swear, if I could hug her through the phone, I would! It’s funny how those little moments—just a quick call or text—can really make a difference. Even though sometimes I feel so much, like I could explode with all this energy and empathy, I want to help others feel lighter too. And then there’s my psychology class. How mind-blowing is it to learn about the human mind? I feel like I want to dive deep into everything. It fascinates me how everyone has their own unique experience of life, and yet we’re all wired similarly. The brain, emotions, interactions, it’s all connected. I can’t help but get excited about the possibilities. Oh! I still need to pick up those prints from the art exhibit I went to last month. Such incredible artists—there was a piece that just blew me away. It was this huge mural about community, vibrant colors and patterns all intertwined. It felt alive, like you could step into it and be a part of the story. I love art that makes you think. It’s the same with music too. Just yesterday, I discovered this indie band. They’re so refreshing, I can’t stop listening to their sound. I think I’ll get tickets to their next show for sure. I wonder if anyone else wants to go? I should start a group chat, make it a whole outing! Sometimes I worry about being too much, too loud. But then I remember that my energy can be infectious. Like when I went to the coffee shop yesterday and just struck up a conversation with a stranger in line. He was so chill and we talked about everything from his favorite books to the best Netflix shows. Can you believe we spent a whole hour just chatting? I love how strangers can turn into friends in the most unexpected ways. It’s like the universe is full of connections waiting to happen! And with plans, I get so excited thinking about future trips. I want to go backpacking in Europe after graduation. How incredible would that be? Just wandering through the streets of Rome, trying gelato for breakfast, mingling with locals in markets, getting lost in the history—I can practically taste it! My calendar is filled with dates for potential trips, events, and hangouts. It’s like a vibrant tapestry of life and friendship. I always keep it organized, though, because I hate double-booking. I want to honor my commitments. That’s one of my biggest values, being reliable. I guess it’s part of my personality. I want to be that dependable friend, the one people count on, but I also need to make sure I leave room for spontaneity. What if I initiated some kind of monthly adventure? Like a hiking crew or a movie night where everyone brings their favorite film? That could be fun! I’m already fueled with ideas—potluck themes, escape rooms, summer picnics. I never want this energy to stop! There's just so much to do, so many people to meet, and adventures to create. I feel like I could talk forever about this. Life is a thrilling ride, and I can’t wait to see what’s around the next corner.

Okay so, I keep thinking about the group project I’m in because I really want it to go well but everyone seems to have their own ideas, and I don’t want to hurt anyone's feelings but also like we really need to get this done. I wish we could just stick to one plan sometimes! I know I can be a little intense about things because I care so much about doing a good job, and I’m a bit of a perfectionist, but it’s also really hard when people don’t seem to be as invested. Maybe they think it’s just an assignment but to me it’s like my reputation or something. I just don’t want to let anyone down, especially when we all have different schedules and it’s hard to coordinate meetings. Speaking of schedules, I need to set reminders because I keep forgetting things. Like, I know I have that exam coming up, and I should really study a lot more. The thing is though, when I sit down to study, my mind just wanders everywhere. I might start with flashcards and then suddenly think about the weird dream I had last night where I was at a music festival but it was raining, and everyone was just sitting there, not dancing. That’s strange, right? I don't even remember what kind of music was playing. Just this heavy feeling of being stuck in mud but also wanting to be happy. But anyway, I need to focus more on my classes. Oh, and I keep hearing about this new café that opened near campus, and I really want to check it out. I feel like it would be a great place to meet friends, and I’d probably get to know more people there. It’s just that when I walk in, I have this overwhelming urge to introduce myself to everyone, say hi, even though it can be super awkward sometimes. Like, I just want to be friends, but do they want to be friends with me? What if they already have their friend groups and I’m just this random person barging in? But then again, I get to know people well pretty quickly, which is nice. Those initial conversations are kind of funny, how everyone tries so hard to be polite and friendly. Then there’s my roommate. Sometimes she doesn't pick up on social cues, and it’s a little stressful because I care about her but I also need my space sometimes. She talks a lot. I mean, A LOT. I’m glad she’s so friendly and that I can talk to her about things, but there are moments when I just need quiet. I don’t know how to tell her that without sounding harsh. I guess I’m just worried about how she’ll take it, like she’ll think I don’t want to be her friend anymore but it’s not that! It's just that my brain gets so overwhelmed with everything, and I need some time to recharge. I keep thinking about that social event I’m supposed to go to this weekend. I bought a new dress for it, and I’ll probably overthink my outfit choices right up until I leave. It's so silly how I get anxious about what to wear but then I want to be the center of attention at the event, like, I want people to notice me but also not too much, please! It’s like a balancing act. I should probably just chill out about it but it’s hard. I want everything to be perfect, you know? If I show up in something that isn’t “me” then what even is the point? Sometimes I wonder if I’ll ever really settle into a groove where I can just be myself without feeling like I have to perform all the time. I love being social and making friends, and I truly enjoy my conversations with people, but the anxiety that comes with it just doesn’t let up sometimes. I keep replaying conversations in my mind, worrying that I said something dumb or weird, even when I know most people don’t notice. Then there’s the family back home. I really miss them. They make me feel grounded. I feel safe there, but it’s also hard to be away because they don’t get to see how much I’ve grown or how I’m trying to manage all this social stuff. I wish they could see me now, the version of me that’s trying to navigate college and figure things out. I keep thinking that they would be proud of me, but it’s that constant tug-of-war I feel where I want to prove myself yet I’m scared I’m doing things wrong. What’s funny is I love being around people so much that I’ll often just dive into events or hangouts even when I know I’m coming off a bit anxious. It’s like I crave the energy of social situations, and I thrive on it, but it gets tiring! I guess that’s just the way I’m wired. I feel like I could talk forever. But okay, I have to go tackle that studying eventually.

There's something about the smell of freshly printed paper that gets me every time. Like the excitement of opening a new notebook or flipping through a brand-new textbook. It’s like the potential is palpable just waiting to be unleashed. The crispness of each page, untouched by scribbles or doodles, is kind of exhilarating. I wonder if anyone else gets this rush or if it's just me being weird again. But then again, I embrace my weirdness. It's kind of my thing. Who wants to be boring, right? Speaking of boring, I can’t believe how many people just go through life on autopilot. How do they do it? I mean, waking up, going to the same job day after day, eating the same lunch. It’s like a never-ending loop of monotony. I could never live like that. I need variety, excitement, new experiences. Every day feels like an adventure waiting to happen. There's so much to explore. Traveling to new places is my favorite thing in the world. I was just thinking about that time I went backpacking through Europe. Met some incredible people, shared stories over cheap wine in a little hostel in Paris, and tried new foods—oh the street crepes were heavenly. I want to do that again. The spontaneity of it all, the freedom, the lack of constraints, it’s intoxicating. Sometimes I think about how social interactions energize me. I thrive when I'm around people; it feels like their energy fuels mine. A big group discussion in class gets my heart racing. I can’t help but jump in and share my thoughts, even if they’re a bit off-the-wall. Who cares? The worst that could happen is someone disagrees, and if they do, that just gives me something to talk about. I often wonder how other people manage to stay quiet during discussions. It’s like they forget the whole point of being in a classroom is to share ideas, to bounce thoughts around. I love a good debate, even if I don’t agree with the other side. It’s all about the exchange of ideas, and I find it fascinating. I think I appreciate differences more than most. That’s where the excitement lies. People have such varied backgrounds and perspectives. It’s like they’re all pieces of a giant puzzle; the more diverse the pieces, the more interesting the picture. I can’t stand fake niceness though. I don’t have time for that. Let’s be real. If you don’t like something, say it. No need for sugar-coating. I’d rather have honesty than a polite nod. Politeness can mask so many truths. Give me the raw stuff. I crave authentic connections, the kind where we can laugh, disagree, and learn from each other. That’s where growth happens. And why is it that some people think they need to follow a script for their lives? I see them shuffle through their day-to-day, checking off boxes. It’s baffling. I mean, that’s fine if it works for them. But what about the unexpected moments? Those times when you take a wildly different route home just because you felt like it, and you stumble upon that quirky little café with the best pastries? Those are the moments that make life beautiful! I can’t imagine missing out on experiences like that. It’s all about living fully, embracing the chaos. I’m the type to throw caution to the wind and try something new on a whim. Like that one time I decided to take up rock climbing. Totally out of my comfort zone, but amazing. Sometimes I think I put a lot of pressure on myself. I have all these plans and goals I want to achieve, and I set high standards. I don’t want to just meet expectations; I want to exceed them. It’s a weird balance between being self-driven and always wanting to push myself further. But I think it boils down to my openness. I’m always eager to learn, to grow, to expand my horizons. I can’t help but wanting to experience everything life has to offer. I’ll never understand those who are content with living in a bubble. I need to explore, break down barriers, and challenge myself. It’s like being in a constant state of evolution. I do wonder about the future sometimes, though. What will it bring? Will I stay this passionate about everything? I hope so. I can’t imagine a life where I’m not curious, where I don’t leap at new opportunities. But then again, maybe that’s the beauty of it. The unpredictability is thrilling. The possibilities are endless. And at the end of the day, that’s what keeps me going.

Okay so I’ve got this thing tomorrow, right? This big presentation in sociology and I feel really good about it. I mean, I always do with presentations. I can just stand up there and talk for hours. It’s like I was born to do this. I’ve got my notes all organized, color-coded with bullet points, and I know the subject inside out. Should have seen the look on my group members' faces when I was explaining our topic last week. They’re all nodding along while I’m just diving into the details, names of theorists, their theories, how they apply today—bam! I thrive in that moment, the spotlight’s on me, and I’m feeding off their energy, and it just feels so good. I even added a joke or two to lighten it up, like, ‘Did you guys hear the one about the sociologist who couldn’t stop analyzing everything? He ended up interpreting his lunch!' They laughed, it was great. I don’t think I'll have a problem winning over the class, at least not with my delivery. Some people are just so stressed about presentations, they’re practically shaking when they get up there but not me. I like to think people gravitate toward me. They enjoy my enthusiasm. Speaking of enthusiasm, have you ever been around someone who just sucks the energy out of a room? I can’t stand that. It’s like this invisible weight, dragging everyone down. I mean, I get it, life can be tough but c’mon. Sometimes you have to put on a smile and just push through, right? I think that’s what separates me from them. People have told me I’m an optimist. I want to be that bright spot that people remember when they walk away. Maybe that’s a bit self-centered? I don’t think that’s necessarily bad. I just end up that way because I enjoy being around people and I want them to enjoy being around me too. It’s a give and take, kinda like playing a game of tennis—back and forth, you know? Okay, but my roommate? She can be a total buzzkill. She just stays in her room all day sometimes—no interaction, nothing. When she comes out, it feels forced, like she’s got a script she’s reading from. I get that some people need their space, but there’s a limit to that, right? I want to drag her out to parties or at least to the food court. We both love a good taco Tuesday, and she keeps saying, “I’ll join you next time.” Next time turns into next week and it’s like, no, just come on! I don’t want to keep dragging people around like that. If she doesn’t want to go, she should just say so, stop pretending she’s interested. I can’t force her to be social. Ugh, can’t stand indecisiveness either, it’s just a waste of time. And then there are my classes. Honestly, I don’t get why some people just skate through. Like, why not put some effort into it? Group projects are the worst. I’m always getting stuck with slackers who don’t pull their weight, and then I have to step in and save the day. Sometimes I wonder if they think I’ll just take care of it all, which I usually do because I can’t help myself. Perfectionist tendencies, I guess. I’ve learned to keep an eye on deadlines and keep everyone accountable. I send reminders. I set up the meetings. Who knew I’d become the unofficial team leader? It’s exhausting sometimes but it’s not like I have a choice. The standards I set for myself are high. Gotta make that 4.0 GPA happen, no room for error. And then there’s social media. I can’t keep up with all the platforms. Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok—it feels like a full-time job. I stick to what I’m good at; I’ll post photos from our hangouts with captions that make people laugh. I feel like it strengthens my ‘brand’ as the fun, social one. But seriously, how do people find the time to create all this content? I’m out living life, and I just don’t have the urge to curate it all. I’d rather be living in the moment, soaking it all in. It’s a bit tiring watching people obsess over likes and comments. Sometimes I think they can’t even enjoy the experience itself because they’re too busy trying to capture it through a screen. Just enjoy the taco, jeez! I wonder if I’m missing out by not being more open to experiences. My friends keep trying to get me to try new things like skydiving or improv classes, but I just can’t find the motivation. I know I can be a little rigid in my routines, but what’s wrong with that? I like structure. I thrive in it. It’s comfortable. It makes sense. Sometimes people just need to chill with their excitement for spontaneity! I guess it’s just hard to find a balance. Maybe tomorrow, after the presentation, I’ll take a leap of faith and do something that scares me. But let’s not get ahead of ourselves; I’ve got to crush this presentation first.

Okay so I just dropped my phone in my bag, and I can’t find it, it’s like a black hole in there, I need to clean it out or something but it’s so hard to focus on that when there are so many other things to do, like I promised to meet Hannah at the coffee shop in twenty minutes and I love hanging with her but I’m not sure what I’ll wear because it’s cloudy outside, should I take a sweater or just my denim jacket? I don’t want to look too casual but also, you know, it’s just coffee and they have great pastries but the last time I had the raspberry scone, it was too sweet and I got jittery so I’ll probably just get an iced latte and sit by the window and people-watch because it’s always interesting to see who comes in, like, did you ever notice how people carry themselves differently depending on what they’re wearing? I wonder if it’s just me that thinks this way. And oh my gosh, I totally forgot to text my mom back after she sent me that long message about how her garden is coming along, she’s always so sweet and excited about planting but I’m just not into gardening even though I know it makes her happy I mean that’s what matters, right? Did I tell you about the time we planted tomatoes and they ended up being so good? Wait, I just remembered that summer before college when we spent every afternoon in the backyard, God, I miss that freedom, it feels like ages ago. But college is fun too, it’s just that some days feel like they drag on and I can’t figure out why I procrastinate so much, it’s like my brain just shuts off sometimes and I don’t even notice until the deadline is looming and my heart races because panic sets in. I gotta figure out how to be more organized but I don’t want to be boring either, what’s the fun in that? Like, I know people who have color-coded planners and it’s impressive but I just can’t wrap my head around that level of commitment, I want spontaneity and adventure but I also crave routine, like what even is that contradiction? And is it possible to enjoy both? I bet there’s a study about that somewhere. Speaking of studies, I’ve always been curious about how people perceive me, especially when I’m in a group. Sometimes I feel like I’m the one making all the jokes, and I love it, but then I wonder if that annoys people or if they’re just being polite when they laugh. Does that mean I’m funny? Or are they just being agreeable because I’m so high on extroversion? It’s just a cycle of thoughts that spirals around and around. I think I need to make more time for my friends, though. I miss Chloe, we used to hang all the time but then she got that internship and now it’s like she vanished off the face of the earth, and I don’t want to sound needy or desperate, but I do get a bit anxious when I haven’t talked to her in a while. I’m sure she’s busy, I get it, but I wish we could just grab food and laugh like we used to. I’m excited for us to catch up—do I sound too clingy? Sometimes I think I can come off too strong when I just want to connect with people. I mean, when you’re an extrovert, isn’t that your thing? Wanting to be around people and feeling like your heart would explode if you’re alone for too long? And today is one of those days where I feel like my brain is on overload, I keep thinking of all the things I want to do but my body just wants to Netflix and chill alone, but that doesn’t sound appealing at all either, like how do people find peace in that? I’d rather be out meeting new people, exploring some art exhibit or going to a concert even if I don’t know the band. I love that feeling of discovery. I wonder if I should go to that open mic night next week, I heard there’s going to be a lot of acoustic acts, and I’ve been meaning to try out some of my songs—God, that sounds terrifying but also thrilling, I hope my friends would come and support me, I bet they’d cheer me on, they always do. And what if I forgot the lyrics? Ugh, but that’s where the openness comes in handy, right? Just roll with the punches, laugh it off and keep going, it’s all about the experience! But then again, all of these thoughts are just swirling around, and I wish I could settle on one thing. Is this how all creative people feel? Or is it just me getting lost in my head? I keep thinking I should meditate or something, learn to quiet my mind, but when I try, my thoughts literally just hop from one thing to the next and I’m like, “What the heck, focus!” Okay, I really gotta go, I have ten minutes until I’m late! I’ll figure it out as I go, that’s the fun part.

It’s 2:13 PM and I’m sitting here surrounded by notes. Everything feels overwhelming. Like, where do I even start? I mean, I’ve got all these thoughts swirling in my head, but expressing them is difficult. I feel like there’s this pressure to sound smart or at least coherent, but my brain is just racing through a million different thoughts. I hate when people say they’re “open-minded.” It feels fake, like they’re just trying to sound cool or something. I mean, I know what I like, and I don’t need to pretend otherwise. People have opinions, I have mine, and sometimes I wish they would just keep them to themselves. The noise of opinions everywhere is exhausting. Social media is the worst. Every post feels like an invitation to argue or something, like people are just waiting to jump in with their judgments. I can’t even scroll through my feed without feeling anxious. It’s way too much. Just scrolling through feeds of people living their lives, perfectly curated and smiling, makes me want to disappear. I’d rather read articles on things than see who went where and did what. Like, can we stop pretending it’s all just sunshine and rainbows? I want to scream at the screen sometimes but I don’t because that’s too much effort. The effort of socializing is exhausting just thinking about it. I feel this tightness in my chest about it. Why can’t everyone just be more straightforward? No need for all the niceness. Some people can’t take honesty. I could never be that kind of person who can just go to a party and chat with everyone. Just the idea of it freaks me out. My best friend is so extroverted, she invites me to everything, tells me it’ll be fun, but I just can’t. The thought of a small talk makes me want to curl up in a ball on my bed. Like why do you need to talk about the weather? The weather is the weather, it’s boring and doesn’t change anything. Oh, I just keep thinking about the group projects coming up. Those are the worst. I hate relying on others. When you can’t control the outcome, it feels so anxiety-inducing. It’s like they all have their own agendas, and I just want to make sure everything is perfect. I need to keep everything organized, and they don’t care. They’re just coasting by, and I’m here stressing over whether we’re going to end up with a decent grade. I could be putting all this energy into other things, but I can’t stop worrying. Yesterday, I spent like three hours on a presentation. Did I overdo it? Probably. I can’t help it. I mean, the amount of time I waste doubting everything is ridiculous. Sometimes I think if I could just put all this energy that goes into worrying into something productive, I’d be unstoppable. But then I doubt that, too. Did I miss an assignment? Did I forget to follow up on something? And then it spirals. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t break free. So I double-check everything. My planner has so many checkmarks, and it feels good but also suffocating. I’ve got all these goals mapped out, but when I fall behind, I feel like I’m failing. If I don’t get the highest mark, I feel like I’ve let myself down. It’s hard to explain that, I guess. Everyone else seems so chill about it. I feel like I’m on this battlefield that nobody else sees. I can't just relax in the same way. I keep thinking about how maybe if I just didn’t care so much, I could be happier. But that feels impossible. It’s like this nagging feeling that I need to prove something to... who? Myself, I guess. But then if I fail, what does that say about me? And how do others see me? I can’t escape these judgments. It’s exhausting. I stare at the wall sometimes, wondering if people think I’m standoffish or just quiet. I’m not trying to be awful; I just can’t function like that. I need structure. I need plans. The chaos of unpredictability makes my skin crawl. Just give me a routine I can latch onto, and I’ll be fine. I mean, what happens if things don’t go perfectly? What if someone else messes up? I end up feeling responsible. I wish I could just let go. It feels like a never-ending cycle. But it’s what I know. So I keep going, worrying, planning, avoiding, and scrutinizing everything.

I was just thinking about the book I picked up last week. It’s that one about breaking down systems of thought—something about how we perceive reality. I wonder how many people actually read it cover to cover. I don’t like when people just take snippets of knowledge and use it to support their biases. Like, it drives me nuts to watch people twist facts to fit whatever narrative they've constructed in their heads. Okay, but seriously, how do they not see the contradictions? Maybe they don’t care. It’s like they’re in a bubble they made themselves and they can't even hear what anyone else is saying. I get that people have their own opinions but some just refuse to engage with real data. I think that’s why I like studying psychology. It’s all about understanding why people act the way they do. There’s so much complexity to it that sometimes I feel I’m just scratching the surface. I mean, I wonder what makes me tick too. Was that a bird? I should check if my window is open. I remember the last time I checked it was—it was fall, and I was paranoid about bugs getting into my room. They always seem to find a way in. Just like ideas can slip into your mind, sometimes unwanted. I like the silence. Just me and my thoughts. I don’t feel lonely. I feel productive, like I can make an impact. I guess that’s why I spend so much time researching. I mean, no one wants to read a poorly researched paper. I don’t care if it’s just a class assignment; it has to be solid. I can’t even wrap my head around how some students throw things together last minute. Doesn’t make sense. I wonder how they can live like that. But then I catch myself thinking about how I’m always prepared, and maybe that’s a little much. I get it—balance is key. But if I don’t put in the effort, then what’s the point? It’s like a puzzle. You can’t just shove pieces together randomly and think it’ll look good. I need clarity, structure. Just like when I tried to organize my notes last week, going all color-coded. Who knew that would become my preferred method? I guess it’s a little nerdy, but it works for me. I need the colors to separate concepts, to map out the connections. Some people would see an art project; I see a tool that helps my brain. Then there are those moments of inspiration when an idea hits me unexpectedly like a lightning bolt. I’ll be in the shower or making dinner, and suddenly there's this brilliant thought about a paper. I have to rush to write it down before it floats away like a dream. Why do the best ideas come at the most inconvenient times? It’s frustrating when I have to drop everything to keep the thought from disappearing. What if I forget it? It’s like carrying a fragile glass orb in your hands. I’m constantly juggling my intellectual curiosity with the chaos around me. Speaking of chaos, I just can’t wrap my head around those people who thrive in it. Like, how? I mean, I get why some things are chaotic; the world is a mess sometimes. With politics, social media, and just life in general, it’s overwhelming. I can’t even begin to engage with all that noise. It’s like trying to decipher a foreign language. It takes focus, and I need to conserve my energy for the things that matter. I get that social connections are important, but they drain me. It’s like an emotional sponge that can only soak so much before it’s overflowing. I’d rather dive deep into a single conversation than skim the surface of a thousand. My friends always ask me why I don’t join study groups. I just can’t stand it. Too many distractions, too many voices. I prefer solitude, where my thoughts can flow without interruption. Craving that clarity, you know? I think that’s where my openness comes in. I love exploring ideas, dissecting them. My mind is a buffet of possibilities, and I want to taste them all. Just the other day, I started a new research topic. It spiraled into a whole other avenue of inquiry. I can’t wait to see where it leads. Maybe I’ll end up writing about something entirely different! I can’t help but wonder if those who are more open-minded would benefit from a little structure. Maybe they’d see that there’s beauty in organization, a rhythm that helps creativity flourish. I like to think I’m offering something when I share my structured approach. If only more people appreciated that, but then again, maybe they’re just happy doing their own thing. Each to their own, I guess. I wonder what it would be like if we all just sat together in silence, letting thoughts intermingle without judgment. That could be a project—silent brainstorming sessions. Just one big room full of people lost in thought. Wouldn’t that be something?

Okay so I can’t believe I forgot to get that book from the library yesterday, now I have to go back today and it’s raining but maybe it’s not so bad I always feel like there’s something about being in a library when it rains, it’s cozy but also feels a little annoying because it makes things more complicated and I hate being wet but I love to read and I love being around people who are reading but then I also get distracted like I’ll see someone with a really cool book and I’ll just have to check it out or I start thinking about how everyone else is so quiet and I’m not sure I can be quiet for that long like I want to whisper and have moments of excitement but I have to hold that in until I get outside and then I can talk about all the cool new things I read. That one time in class when I had the idea for a group project and I got super excited and started talking about all the possibilities—everyone in the group looked at me like I was crazy but I couldn’t help it. The energy was flowing and I just wanted to bounce ideas off of everyone else and they were just staring at me, was it too much? I mean, who doesn’t love brainstorming? I like the sound of ideas clicking together, and it makes me feel like I’m on fire, but sometimes I see the hesitation in their eyes and it makes me feel weird like do I scare them? Or is it just that they don’t have as much enthusiasm, but that’s okay, right? I mean, not everyone has to be as into it as I am. But then if I get too into it and someone disagrees it feels like a punch to the gut, like come on, we’re all trying to do something cool here, and if you don’t see the vision are you even paying attention because I’m absolutely paying attention and I want to get it right. The professor said the ideas need to be strong and I feel like I can give that to them but then I can’t be too assertive, wait did I just raise my voice? Am I being rude? I mean people say I can be a little intense but why shouldn’t I be intense when I care about something? It’s frustrating that I care so much and it seems like some people don’t. It feels like I care more than they do and that just gets under my skin like why are you even here if you’re not going to bring it? Then there’s the anxiety of deadlines like I imagine the clock ticking down and I’m in a race against time and I can visualize every second I’m wasting just sitting there scrolling through my phone and that makes my heart race and I get the jitters because I know I should be doing something productive. Why can’t I just sit down and focus for once? I mean at least I’m conscientious about getting things done but the struggle to actually sit still with myself and just… work is huge and I feel like I’m climbing up a mountain but it’s made of pillows and I keep sinking into them when I should be like scaling the side and just getting to the top. I’ve got to be disciplined but why does discipline feel so heavy sometimes? I just wanna escape into a world where everything is done but that world doesn’t exist because I keep procrastinating like it’s some kind of second nature to me. And then there are those moments of pure creativity that just come crashing down on me like I’m riding a wave and then BAM sudden storm hits and I’m drowning in my thoughts like can I write that down can I make it happen? Ideas are coming to me in flashes like fireworks, loud and bright and vibrant, but then I lose them because I’m too busy being caught up in the “what ifs.” What if it’s not good enough? What if I don’t have a plan? What if they hate it? Why do I care so much? I want people’s approval I want validation but I also want to break free and create something that’s all mine. Am I ready for that? Can I present my work to the world and not care what they think? But also maybe that’s what makes me tick, the whirlwind of emotions and thoughts and excitement and fear, it’s just part of living my life isn’t it? I love being around people who are different who think differently because it expands my mind and sometimes I’m just in awe of what people can create when they’re not in their heads. I wish I could pull that spontaneity, making art on a whim but it feels like I’m stuck in this constant cycle. I want to play but I want to do it right, I want to follow through but I also want to have fun, I want to travel somewhere exciting and see the way people live, feeling that exhilaration of life unfolding but I’m always questioning myself and doubting why I’m not just out there living it you know? Just this need to go go go and yet sometimes I feel paralyzed in that need, caught in the in-between where I’m ready to burst but can’t find an outlet, and it all swirls inside me and I’m here writing this like some kind of stream while the rain outside keeps falling, and all I can think is I need a plan and I need to stop worrying because life is happening all around me and I want to be part of it.

Okay so I just got this really cool idea for a group project and I can’t wait to share it with the team but I hope they’re just as excited because sometimes people are so wrapped up in their own little worlds it’s hard to get them to see the bigger picture like how much fun we could have with this. I was thinking we could do something interactive, like a scavenger hunt on campus, get everyone involved and moving and laughing, those little moments when you’re running around trying to find clues, it’s such a great way to bond. Speaking of bonding, I just remembered that time we all went to that karaoke night, and wow can Sarah really belt out those high notes, I mean that girl is full of energy and totally fearless when she grabs that mic. I gotta say, I love being around people who are upbeat like that. It’s like energy is contagious, right? And then you have that moment when you’re all laughing till your stomachs hurt, and it’s like nothing else matters for those few hours, just pure joy. But sometimes I worry that I overcommit. I mean I sign up for everything, like this club and that club and then I’m like, how am I going to keep this schedule? But it’s not like I can say no because I don’t want to miss out on anything. I think I thrive off of the chaos, honestly. Like last week I ended up going to three different events in one night. I met so many people! I’m terrible with names but I can recall the vibe of each person I talked to, the coolness of that punk rock guy who loved the same bands I do, and the girl who was so passionate about environmental issues, oh my gosh we talked about biodegradable straws and composting and it was so easy to connect. Sometimes I look back at my week and think did I actually spend that much time socializing? And then I think, who cares? Socializing is my jam! I love it. It’s all about relationships, right? Life’s too short to just sit at home binge-watching series alone even though I do that sometimes too when I need a little breather. But usually, I prefer the loudness, the clinking of glasses at a bar, the way people are just sharing their lives and stories, it’s like participating in this giant web of shared experiences. I remember this one time at a coffee shop, the barista told us about this adventure she had traveling through Europe, and we were all gathered around, hanging onto her every word. It’s moments like that where you realize how connected we all are, even if we just met sometimes. And then there’s this quiet part of me that loves the arts, the museums, the galleries with walls splattered in color that speak to me without words. I visited that new installation last month, wow, it was so provocative, I could just feel the layers beneath every brushstroke. I wanted to talk to the artist and ask what they were thinking but they were in the corner with a group of people, and I just felt that wave of shyness wash over me like should I jump in or let them be? And then my mind races off to the next thing, like what if I wrote poetry? I haven’t done that since high school, and it was fun! I remember being so free with my words, spilling them all over the page without thinking too much. Just write, just express. I could try again, could find a cool spoken word night, that would be fun. Oh, food! I love food. My roommate and I had this crazy idea to try making sushi last week, it was a disaster but we laughed the entire time. Rice everywhere and we ended up with this weird sushi that looked nothing like what you’d buy at a restaurant but tasted amazing because we made it together. Cooking is such a communal thing, like it brings people together, chopping veggies and sautéing onions, it’s therapy. I think I want to host a potluck soon, get everyone to bring a dish, so many different flavors, eating together is such a beautiful bonding experience. But wait, I need to remember to reach out to everyone. The group chat! Sometimes I feel like I’m always the one initiating, and I don’t mind because I love planning things but I hope it doesn’t overwhelm anyone. I like being the glue, you know? If I didn’t reach out, I worry things would fizzle out. People get busy but if you don’t make the effort sometimes those connections slip away, and I don’t want that. Ugh, I think of this stuff all the time. What if I’m just a background character in someone else's life? I want to be the main character of my own and the friend they remember fondly, so I’ll keep making plans. Time to jump back into my chaotic, joyful world of people and possibilities. Just gotta keep that energy up!

Alright, let’s see where this goes. It’s like my brain is a bustling marketplace, so many thoughts zigzagging around. I keep thinking about that party last weekend. So many people! I loved being around them, the laughter, the noise, the vibe, but then I got high-strung, too much energy, too many faces. What was that one girl wearing? A bright pink dress, like cotton candy, it was so loud. I love color, but that one—ugh, distracting. Sometimes I wonder if I come off too strong. Did I talk too much? I always worry I overdo it. I get this surge of excitement and it’s like I can’t hold back. I mean, I want to connect, I want to share everything in my head. Do they think I’m too intense? They probably don’t even notice but my mind races. Like, should I have toned it down? No, but then what’s the point of being at a party if you can’t be yourself? But then my brain flips and goes, “What if they don’t like the real you?” Which, heck, I guess is part of being high on neuroticism. I’ve read that it can make people more sensitive to criticism but whatever. And that guy who cornered me talking about his cat—seriously, how many minutes can someone talk about a cat? Like loving pets is cool, sure, but I just wanted to talk about something more exciting! He looked at me like I was the one being rude. I guess I can come off as abrasive sometimes, or maybe I just don’t care enough to sugarcoat my responses. There’s so much going on in my head that I want to discuss! I want to dive into deep conversations, dissect life, the universe, or even the latest conspiracy theories. That stuff gets me excited! But I need to remember not everyone’s in the same zone, some are just there for a good time and I’m over here analyzing the political implications of social media on democracy. How did I even get into that ramble? Oh right, the cat guy. I wonder if he was just nervous and that’s why he latched onto a safe subject. Whatever. What’s up with the weather lately? It’s like one day it’s blazing hot, and the next it’s cold enough for a sweater. I can’t deal with this. I mean, I tried to dress cool for the party and ended up freezing in the line outside. It’s always something. Maybe I should just invest in a reliable jacket. I could get something wild with patterns, like a rainbow or something, to match my vibe. Speaking of investments, my bank account looks terrible right now. It’s alarming how quickly I drop cash on things. I’m not even mad, because I love living in the moment, but geez, I should get better at saving. I’ll just keep thinking about how to make more money while scrolling through Instagram. Which reminds me, that ad popped up about starting a podcast, and suddenly I was like: “Yes, I need to do this.” I could talk about everything! Just me, the mic, and my wild brain. I mean, wouldn’t it be great to have my own platform to spill my thoughts without anyone interrupting? But then there’s the fear again—what if nobody listens? Ugh, can’t think like that. And then there’s the coursework piling up. I can’t keep procrastinating, I need to focus, but there’s always this urge to go out, meet new people, make new friends. It's like a constant battle between doing what I know I should and what I really want to do. I had a paper due last week that I totally put off until the last moment because my friends planned this epic night out. I swear I can convince myself anything is more important than sitting down with my books. And when I finally cracked and wrote that paper, of course, it’s 2 AM and I’m slamming coffee like it’s water. I’m proud of it, though. I got a decent grade, but the stress—neuroticism strikes again! And when will I find the right balance? I want to be this go-getter with a full social calendar, but also someone who hands their assignments in on time, maybe even ahead of schedule. Is that even possible? I’ve heard there are people who live like that, how do they do it? They probably have planners and color-coded lists. That’s a thought. Maybe I should try that. I’d love to get into some sort of routine. Would it diminish my spontaneity? I don’t want to lose the thrill of being a free spirit. There has to be a middle ground. Life's all about finding that line, right? And it’s so odd that I crave novelty in everything. I want to try new foods, new experiences, new places, but then it feels overwhelming. It’s like infinite options lead to this anxiety of making the wrong choice. Okay, I think I’ve been rambling long enough now. Do I ever come up for air? Ah, the beauty of being a whirlwind of thoughts—what a ride.

So I saw this butterfly today, it was the prettiest thing, just fluttering around the park like it owned the place. It reminded me of summer, the lazy days when you’d just sit outside and nothing really mattered, you could just be. Sometimes I wonder if butterflies even know how beautiful they are or if they're just out there searching for food. But then again, beauty is subjective, right? Like, I mean, I love the way people are so different, one person might think a sunset is beautiful while someone else just sees colors fading, how weird is that? Speaking of sunsets, I really like the idea of watching the sun go down with friends but I kind of prefer to do it alone. I can sit in silence and just think about random stuff, like where I'd go if I could teleport anywhere right now. I think I’d want to go to this little cafe by the ocean. You know, the kind with the rickety chairs that look like they might fall apart any second? That would be perfect, sipping some weird coffee concoction and watching waves crash. I’d probably get lost in my head thinking about everything and nothing at all. Sometimes getting lost feels like the most productive thing I can do, like why should I worry about deadlines when I can just admire the way the clouds move? Then there's that whole thing about how everyone is always in a hurry. I don’t understand why people rush through life when it’s so full of little bits of wonder! Like yesterday, I was walking to the library and I found this old coin on the ground. I just picked it up and turned it over in my fingers, imagining how it got there. Did somebody lose it after buying a sandwich? Did it fall out of someone’s pocket? Little stories are everywhere if you look for them, and I love that. I could get lost in a moment like that for hours. I really don’t think I’m a planner. Like, my friends might say, “Let’s meet at this time!” and I’m just like, “Sounds good, I guess?” but I never really think about it until it’s happening. Sometimes I show up late or forget altogether, but they’re cool about it. I think? They always say they understand, but I wonder if that’s just what people say to be nice. I mean I wouldn’t be offended if someone was late, so maybe it’s all chill. I hope they don’t mind; I hate it when there’s tension in a group. I’d rather us just laugh about it and move on, but maybe I’m not a good judge of that because I’m too easygoing. Class was interesting today, though. We talked about art and how it grabs you without warning. I love that idea. It’s like the best art hits you right in your gut. I remember the first time I saw a Pollock painting, I felt so overwhelmed. Just splashes of paint all over the place, but I could feel something in it. Is it okay to say that I didn’t understand why it was good? But it somehow told a story to me. That’s art for you! Just a big jumbled mess that all connects in weird ways, and everyone has a different take on it. Also, why are people so obsessed with getting everything perfect? It’s like they think everything needs to be neat and tidy and I just can’t get behind that. I don’t like it. I feel so much more relaxed when things are a bit chaotic, like my room. It’s a disaster zone half the time, but it’s my disaster zone, and I know where everything is, even if others can’t see it. I mean, I don’t want to offend anyone—like I said, I’m a people person. I think I’m good at being nice, or at least that’s what I strive for. And then there are those moments when I connect with someone, even someone random like the cashier at the grocery store. Small talk is funny because it’s so pointless but also kind of comforting, right? I find it fascinating how we share these little bits of ourselves. I bet they go home and think about that two-minute conversation and it makes them smile or cringe or whatever. I hope people smile more. It’s nice to think about. I don’t know. I could just sit here lost in thoughts. It’s all like a big jigsaw puzzle and I’m just grabbing pieces at random, and it’s okay if it doesn’t fit right away.

it's 3 am and I can't sleep again. the clock is ticking loudly it's like a metronome counting down my sanity. all these thoughts rushing in, what did I say to Jim earlier? Did I offend him? I should really text him, no that’s too much, maybe he'll just forget. that's what people do right? forget awkward stuff. sometimes I feel like I’m just a balloon floating around people flitting from one conversation to another but not really connecting just bouncing and it feels empty. I hate when I say too much, then I get anxious about it. I said “that’s interesting” too many times in the last group chat. did they notice? what if they think I’m dull? ugh. I want to crawl into a hole and pull the dirt in over me sometimes. it’s not that I don’t like people, it’s just— I feel like I’m in the corner of a room watching everyone laugh and talk, and I’m smiling but I’m wondering if anyone sees me or is it all just a performance? I can never tell if I’m really present or just acting like I am. I remember that time someone asked me what music I liked and I just froze, why is that such a hard question? I like music but I can never think of the right words. I wish I could say something cool and confident but I just mumbled something vague about liking “chill stuff.” I think about it sometimes like why can’t I just say I love this album or that song without feeling like I’m being judged? maybe I should've joined a club or something? but then there's the meeting and everyone knows each other and I hate walking into rooms where everyone is already in their little groups. it feels like high school all over again, those lunch tables where you had to pick the right one or be left standing awkwardly. everyone must know this feeling right? maybe they don’t. maybe they’re all just acting like they’ve got life figured out and I’m the one lost in the weeds. it’s funny because I always want to help people, like I see someone struggling and I want to step in but then I’m terrified of what they’ll think of me. will they think I’m just trying to be a hero? or just a nuisance? I never know how to navigate that balance and I end up just wishing I could be invisible, float in the background and not have to engage. this room is too small. I should tidy up but that feels like a project. I get overwhelmed with projects. like, I have laundry piling up and the dishes in the sink are almost a small mountain at this point. but there’s also this relief in not doing it. sometimes I just sit there staring at the mess and think about how nice it would feel to have it all clean but then I don’t have the energy to start. I scroll through my phone instead and find some funny videos, those random cat videos always help for a few minutes until I remember that I have responsibilities. I should probably apply for jobs but even thinking about writing a cover letter sends me into an anxiety spiral. it’s like “who am I kidding?” I don’t have enough experience and I don’t know what I want to do anyway. everybody else seems so sure of themselves. I imagine they’re at networking events swapping stories while I’m here fretting over which internship I should’ve applied to six months ago. all of my friends seem to be moving on, internships, relationships, traveling, and I’m just stuck. I’m happy for them but it stings a little and I feel like I’m whining. it’s too easy to get into that mindset of comparing myself to others and it doesn’t do any good but it's hard to shake off. self-doubt is like a pit bull; it doesn’t let go. what’s next? graduation is looming, and my parents keep asking about plans. I don’t even know what I want for lunch tomorrow. they act like I should have it all figured out, like one day I should just wake up and be an adult— I don’t feel like one. there are days I want to scream “I don’t have the slightest clue!” but instead, I nod and say, “yeah, I’m thinking about it.” I wonder sometimes if I could just take a road trip somewhere far away and figure it out on the way, but then I think about traveling alone and just feel more anxious. too many what-ifs. what if I get lost? what if my car breaks down? what if I don’t have enough money? the endless possibilities of failure are paralyzing. even in my dreams, I catch myself worrying about trivial things and wake up exhausted. I think I just need a break from thinking. just a moment of peace where my mind isn’t racing through a million negative scenarios. if only I could hit a reset button.

Okay so like I just finished this group project and it was actually pretty amazing how well we all clicked. It’s funny how when you find the right people everything just flows. I mean, we had this brainstorming session last week and it was chaotic in the best way. Everyone throwing out ideas, building on each other's thoughts, laughing. I genuinely thrive in those environments, like when everyone’s excited and bouncing off each other’s energy. Creativity just sparks and it’s so exhilarating, like fireworks in your brain, right? Oh and speaking of brainstorming, I need to pick a topic for my next essay. Something fun but also meaningful. I was thinking about writing on the impact of art in social movements. Art really does have a way of bringing people together, doesn’t it? I love how it can express emotions that sometimes words can’t. Like that mural I saw the other day. It was massive and colorful and just spoke to the struggles and triumphs of the community. Every time I walk by that spot, it makes me stop and smile and think about how art shapes our lives. Then there's the whole social media aspect of sharing art too. It’s like the whole world can join in on creativity. I sometimes wonder if that's a good thing or a bad thing. Too much noise? But then again, it allows so many voices to be heard, which is super important. I love scrolling through my feed and seeing different perspectives and styles. The way people interpret things can be so refreshing. Sometimes I wish I could just travel everywhere to see these art forms in person. I bet the feeling of standing in front of a piece changes everything too. Right, speaking of traveling, I really need to plan a trip soon. I just want to immerse myself in new cultures, foods, and people. My friend just got back from Japan and she’s been raving about the sushi and the shrines. I can’t even imagine how beautiful it must have been. I want to walk through those busy streets, try street food, and just feel alive in a new atmosphere. There’s something about new experiences that just opens me up. I feel like I'm on a constant quest for knowledge and adventure. And food! Oh my gosh, I could talk about food for hours. I tried this new Thai place last week, and I can’t stop thinking about the pad thai. The way the flavors danced around in my mouth was just blissful. It’s so interesting how food can bring people together too, like sitting around a table sharing a meal, laughing, telling stories. I get so excited when I have friends over for dinner. Planning everything is like a little project, picking recipes, shopping, making a mess in the kitchen. It gives me joy. It’s funny how simple things like that can totally lift your mood. I feel like people don’t always realize how interconnected everything is. Everything influences everything else. Like, you go out with friends, you laugh, you have fun, and suddenly your outlook on life is brighter, which can lead you to approach tasks differently the next day. I read somewhere that positivity can be contagious. I think that's so true. It creates this ripple effect that just keeps going. But then there are days when it feels like the world is too heavy. I get it. I see it in my friends sometimes, the stress of exams or personal issues or whatever. I want to be that person who can always lift them up, but I also have to remind myself to check in on my own feelings too. I think it’s important to balance things out. I guess that’s where being conscientious comes in, making sure I take care of myself while also being there for others. Reflecting on my interactions, I’ve found that a simple smile or a compliment can truly change someone's day. The other day, I told a classmate I loved her shoes, and she lit up. It’s just so rewarding to spread little bits of joy. I think being agreeable helps with that too; I genuinely love connecting with people and finding common ground. Okay, now I’m back to the essay topic. I need to make sure I incorporate the research aspect, which means diving into historical contexts and significance. I should probably look up some case studies too, maybe find some interviews. I love putting pieces together like that; it feels like solving a puzzle. But for now, I’m letting my thoughts flow wherever they want to go. It’s kind of freeing, writing like this without a plan. Just a mix of ideas and excitement, like a kaleidoscope of thoughts. I should do this more often!

Okay so I just had this wild idea about starting a club for people who love spontaneous adventures, like exploring new neighborhoods or trying foods from different countries without really knowing what they are. I mean, how fun would that be? The thrill of stepping into a place you’ve never been, tasting something that might be amazing or totally gross, and laughing about it later. I can already imagine the conversations we’d have, sharing our best and worst food experiences. I think it would bring people together in such a cool way. Plus, everyone loves food, right? It’s like the universal language. Then again, what if no one showed up? I’d be standing there with all these snacks, feeling silly. I guess I could ask a few friends first, get the ball rolling. I don’t mind reaching out to people. Honestly, making new friends is one of my favorite things. I think it’s fascinating how connections can form over shared interests or even completely random experiences. Last week at the park, I met this girl who was painting a mural, and we ended up talking about our favorite artists for hours. I mean, how cool is that? Just a random encounter turning into a deep conversation about creativity and inspiration. And speaking of inspiration, I’ve been wanting to pick up a new hobby. Maybe pottery? I’ve always admired how tactile it is, shaping something out of nothing. I can imagine the squishy clay and the spinning wheel. I might totally suck at it but who cares? The whole process seems so liberating. Even if I end up with a bunch of misshapen bowls, it’s the experience that counts. It’s like that time I tried salsa dancing. I was awful, stumbling over my own feet, but the energy in the room was infectious. Everyone laughing and encouraging each other, it was just a blast. I’ve heard some people say they dislike social stuff like that because of fear of judgment, but honestly, as long as you’re having fun and trying, who cares? I think I prefer the chaos of being surrounded by people, their laughter, their quirks, even the awkward moments. It’s all part of the experience—so many stories unfold in those moments. Right now I’m thinking about how I want to travel more. I’ve got this itch to just go, no real plan. Visiting random cities, meeting locals, tasting the street food. There’s something about the unknown that excites me. I’ve been reading this travel blog where this couple visits a different country every month, and the stories they share just draw me in. I keep thinking how amazing it would be to immerse myself in different cultures, learn new languages, and just live in the moment. Imagine being in a market in Morocco, the spices smelling so strong, the vibrant colors everywhere, and then chatting with a vendor about his life there. I’d be all about asking questions, soaking it all up. And it’s crazy how some people are just really open to sharing their stories. Like, I once talked to this guy on a train who told me about his family’s history. He was so passionate about it, I felt like I was in a movie. I think if you approach the world with curiosity, you get so much in return. Then again, it can be overwhelming sometimes, trying to juggle all these ideas and commitments. Balancing school, work, socializing—there’s always something going on. My calendar is filled with events, deadlines, and hangouts, and I love it, but sometimes I wonder if my brain can keep up. I don’t mind the hustle, but I need to remember to breathe, to take a step back and just enjoy what’s happening right now. Okay, what else? Oh, the idea of volunteering has been on my mind. There’s a local shelter that always needs help, and I thought that could be a great way to connect with new people while giving back. It’s kind of a two-for-one deal, right? Meeting people and feeling good about making a difference. I think if everyone spent just a little time helping others, the world would be a better place. Plus, have you noticed how many interesting stories people have? Every time I volunteer, I meet someone new with a unique perspective—and it’s just so enriching. I need to wrap this up soon. But wait, did I mention my favorite coffee spot? The one with the funky chairs and plants everywhere? Love that place. It’s always buzzing with creatives working on their projects or catching up with friends. I could spend hours just sitting there, absorbing the atmosphere, people-watching, and imagining the stories behind each face I see. There’s something really special about those moments, feeling connected to the world even in the simplest ways. Anyway, I’ve got to plan that adventure club!
