Maybe later today I’ll sketch out some ideas. This is gonna be fun.

My shoes are really bright today. Like neon green and I can’t help but notice them every time I walk in the hallway. I wonder if people think they’re cool or tacky. I mean, I love attention. It’s thrilling, right? Like, when you walk into a room and everyone turns their heads, that’s me in a nutshell. But, then again, people can be judgmental which, ugh, sends shivers down my spine. I can’t even stand the thought of someone thinking something negative about me. So I walk with confidence, head up, shoes popping. It’s all part of the game. Classes are a whirlwind this semester, like a tornado of information. I feel like I’m always on the go, absorbing everything and anything. I thrive on it, the chaos, the constant interaction with people. Group projects are my jam, even though they drive me to the brink of insanity sometimes. But I can’t do it alone, well I could but I’d rather not. I need the energy of others around me, their ideas bouncing off mine. I can’t deal with the boring ones, the ones who just don’t care. It’s frustrating, and I can’t help but roll my eyes. We had this one kid in our last project who just zoned out, seriously dude? Speaking of zoning out, I had this wild dream last night, something about being chased by a giant marshmallow. Totally random. I could feel the fluffiness trying to engulf me, and I woke up in a cold sweat. I guess that’s the neurotic side of me kicking in, my brain just spinning out of control. Why do I even have dreams like that? It’s so absurd but also kind of funny when I think about it. I should probably keep a dream journal; I read somewhere that it’s good for mental clarity but who has time for that? I’m too busy managing real-life stuff, like juggling classes, friends, and the million social events I’m supposed to attend. Life is a rollercoaster, and I’m just strapped in for the ride. Oh, and social media! I love posting about everything, sharing my life like I'm some kind of influencer, but I can’t stand the toxic part of it. The constant comparisons, especially when I’m stressed, I mean, I go through phases of thinking I need to be perfect and then just throwing my hands up and saying “forget it.” It’s tough to balance, seeing everyone’s glamorous lives while you’re sitting in your room, eating cold pizza and trying to write a paper that’s due tomorrow. But I still keep scrolling. It’s like a magnetic pull, this endless feed of content. I notice how people interact online, some just sugar-coat everything, while others go all out with their drama. I'm not here for sugar, I want the realness, the chaos, the unfiltered mess of being human. And can we talk about how much I love trying new things? Every week I’m like, “Yes, new hobby!” I signed up for pottery last month, which was a disaster. I mean, clay everywhere, and my pot ended up looking like a deformed bowl. But who cares, right? It was fun, and I met some cool people, and we laughed about it. Except there was this one girl who got super upset about her projects not turning out right, and I wanted to shake her and say, “Chill! It’s just clay!” But my low agreeableness makes it tough to be gentle sometimes. I just want to be straightforward, no sugar-coating, just honesty. Anyway, brunch plans for the weekend, and I can’t decide between that new vegan place or the classic diner. I love trying new food, but I also have a soft spot for greasy breakfast with real eggs and fluffy pancakes. The dilemma! And I’ve been scrolling through TikTok for inspiration, but it makes me overthink everything. I’m always like, “Am I trendy enough? Should I be posting more? What if I’m not cool enough?” But then I remember that I’ll be me regardless, and at the end of the day, I just want to have a good time with my friends and enjoy life. I wonder when the next big party is, or if I can plan something spontaneous. I thrive in those moments where everything just comes together, friends, laughter, music. It’s like when I’m creating a vibe, and I can feel everyone’s energy in sync. But then the day after is always a mixed bag, either I’m flying high or crashing hard, especially if I drank too much. I hate hangovers, but they’re worth it for the memories made. Life is full of these ups and downs, and I just roll with it, faster than a speeding train, chaotic yet exhilarating, like my thoughts right now.

Okay so I was just thinking about how the world feels so big sometimes like even just the idea of traveling somewhere new or exploring things I’ve never seen and how that excites me. Like I could go to a museum or just wander around a different part of the city and it feels like a fresh start. I have this stack of books on my nightstand that I’ve been meaning to read but I keep getting distracted by all these ideas. I wonder why I’m like that. It’s like I want to dive deep into a million things at once but I never really finish any of them. I have this one book about philosophy that's been sitting there forever. I’m curious about it but also I don’t feel like I have the energy to focus on heavy stuff right now. Also thinking about how I get along with people. It’s funny because I often find myself in conversations that feel really deep and meaningful but I’d rather avoid small talk. Like the other day, someone asked me about the weather and I just felt this urge to talk about dreams and how they reflect our subconscious. I swear if I could just have a conversation about life’s mysteries with every person I met, I'd be so happy. But most people aren’t really into that. They just want to talk about their day or the latest show on Netflix, which feels so surface level. Not that it’s a bad thing, I guess, but I feel like there’s more to explore. Sometimes I wonder if I seem weird because I’m not more outgoing. Like, I can go to a party and I usually don’t mind being around people, but I get overwhelmed pretty quickly. I can sit there and enjoy watching others interact, and sometimes that’s more fun than participating. I just observe—and it makes me feel like I'm in a different world, like I'm in a bubble where I can take everything in without needing to be involved. It makes me think about how everybody has their way of doing things, their way of connecting, and I think I’m okay with just being in my own head for a while. And what is it with everyone being obsessed with social media? I mean, I have an account and I post occasionally, but I often forget to check it often. Like I’ll be scrolling through random things, and half the time I’m like “why am I even looking at this?” People sharing their lives, their highs and lows, and I feel like I’d rather have a meaningful conversation in person than scroll through photos of someone’s vacation. I don’t know, it just feels empty sometimes. Speaking of emptiness though, I think about how I make decisions. I wish I was more organized—my room is a mess right now and I really need to clean it but every time I try to, I just get distracted by something shiny, like an interesting article online or a random thought I want to explore. I should probably set some goals or something—like clean for 10 minutes, read for 20, and then reward myself with ice cream—but somehow that feels too structured. Rules are a bit terrifying, honestly. I like to wander without a plan. Also, I’ve been wanting to learn guitar. My friend plays and it seems so freeing to just strum along to your favorite songs. But I keep putting it off because I’m scared I’ll be terrible at it, even though I know it’s just practice and patience, but why is it so hard to just start? I think I get caught up in this loop of thinking too much, and then I just don’t do anything. But I want to try. Sometimes I think about how being agreeable might be my biggest blessing and curse. I hate conflict, and I find myself agreeing with people even when I don’t really think that way, just to keep the peace. It’s not that I don’t have opinions—believe me, I do—it’s just that I prefer harmony to disputes, and I’m always tiptoeing around the things that might rock the boat, which kind of sucks because I could be more outspoken about my views. But then, there are moments when it works out. Some of my friends have really strong personalities, and I can kind of just blend in and let them lead, which is comforting in a weird way. But I think I’d like to embrace more chaos in a way. Life is constantly changing and I want to be okay with all the unpredictability. If I can learn to enjoy the ride instead of worrying about every little detail, then I’ll probably find a way to connect with people on a deeper level. It’s all a big puzzle, and I’m just trying to find my piece in it all. Embracing uncertainty sounds liberating. I want to do that!

Okay so I was just thinking about how people can be so different from one another—like I mean when I’m in a group, I thrive. I feed off the energy, and I can’t help but laugh at the silly jokes being tossed around. Yesterday, I was at this gathering, and everyone was sharing the most outrageous stories. I told this one about the time I got lost in a city I’d never been to. Like it was ridiculous—I ended up at a taco stand. The tacos were amazing, though, so maybe it wasn’t such a disaster. I always wonder what people think about when they’re alone. Do they think about the things they've done? Or their dreams? I can’t sit still with my thoughts for too long; there's always something bubbling up inside, a need to connect, to share. It feels so good to talk, to see the smiles or the raised eyebrows when people are engaged. It’s like a game we all play, a back and forth, and when I get to be part of that, it’s like fuel for my soul. I get it, though, some people don’t want to be in the spotlight. I respect that, but I can't help but want to bring them in. I want to draw them out of that shell because there’s usually something really interesting under there. And then there’s the idea of deadlines. I actually love them. It’s like a challenge, right? Like an invitation to show up and prove I can do it, and I usually do. I set goals for myself at the start of each semester. Nothing crazy, just a mix of academic and personal stuff. Like, “I will reach out to at least five new people this month.” And guess what? It becomes a game, a little adventure. I’ve met so many amazing friends that way. Last semester, I joined a book club, and it’s been fantastic. The discussions we have are wild, ranging from what the author meant to how certain themes resonate with us. And man, everyone has such different perspectives. That openness to different ideas makes me feel alive. I’ve been thinking about travel too, because that’s something I’m dying to do more of. Each place has its own life, a pulse that you can feel if you pay close attention. When I visited Italy last summer, I got lost in Venice, and it was like wandering through a painting. I tried gelato from every stall. So many flavors! Pistachio and stracciatella are the best. I was talking to the locals, asking them about their lives, what keeps them up at night, what they dream about. They’re just so warm and welcoming. It made me realize how similar we are even when we seem so different at first glance. It’s funny, the threads of humanity that tie us all together. And in those moments, I can see my life like a tapestry woven with stories from every person I meet. Sometimes I think about how being agreeable shapes my interactions. I want to understand what others feel and need. I’ll go out of my way to help friends with assignments or listen when they need to vent. It’s like a natural instinct. I can’t help but feel that we’re all in this together, you know? Like we’re all shooting for the same thing—a bit of happiness, a sense of belonging. And it just makes sense to lift each other up. But then again, it can be tricky. I’ve seen some people take advantage of that kindness, which is frustrating. I have to remind myself that it’s okay to set boundaries. I’m getting better at it. And then there are the little joys in life. I went to this little café the other day, and they had the best pastries. There was this warm, flaky croissant waiting for me, and I swear it just melted in my mouth. It was like a hug on a plate, and I ended up chatting with the owner about recipes and baking techniques. I love sharing my opinion on food; it’s like a universal language. It’s funny how food can bring people together, isn’t it? People sitting around a table, laughing, sharing stories, just enjoying everything. I once thought about starting a blog just to share adventures like that—food, travel, and making people laugh. I should probably wrap this up soon but before I do, I’ve got to mention how much I look forward to each day. There is so much potential in everyday moments, the little things that can spark joy. The randomness of the universe just makes me feel giddy sometimes—sunshine, laughter, unexpected reunions. Life is like a vast canvas, and every day is a brushstroke contributing to the masterpiece of our experiences. Who knows what tomorrow will bring? But I know I want to greet it with open arms and a friendly smile.

Okay so I’m thinking about my plans for tonight, just got a text from Jess about the party at Ryan’s place. Everyone’s going to be there. Jess said something about a new playlist and snacks, I love snacks. I get so excited about snacks like chips and guac or those little pretzel bites really need to find out who’s bringing those best part of any gathering let’s be real. I hope there’s enough room to dance, I love dancing, although I can’t really dance dance but it doesn’t matter because it’s just fun to move around and feel the rhythm and join everyone in their crazy shapes and spins. There’s something about being together in a room full of friends that just lights me up, like when everyone’s laughing and sharing stories it all feels so connected and warm. Just realized I need to grab a drink before heading out—need to stop at the store. Should I bring soda or something stronger? I mean, it isn’t really a party without a little fun. Maybe I’ll just bring both? Double the chance for socializing. Talking about socializing, sometimes I think about how easy it is for me to make friends. I just kind of walk up to people and say hi and start talking, it’s like a habit or something. I can’t imagine being shy or stuck in a corner, like no way. People are so interesting, with their stories and their little quirks. Remember that time at Zoe’s birthday when I just plopped down next to that dude I hadn’t met before? Super casual, like, “Hey what’s up?” then we ended up chatting for an hour about our favorite movies. Who knew we both loved that weird indie film? It’s funny how those things stick in your mind, those random moments of connection. What was I saying? Right, so the party tonight, I can feel the anticipation already. I hate waiting though; I just want to show up and see everyone and yell “hey!” and hug people. Hugs are the best. Kind of funny, I think I hug more than most people I know. Like, is that too much? I don’t even care; it’s just nice to feel that warmth. You can’t be too touchy-feely, right? Good vibes only. But yeah, I should probably pick out something to wear that feels fun but also comfortable. I don’t like overthinking what to wear, simple but cool I guess. Maybe that one shirt with the bright colors? It always brightens the vibe. Or wait, should I go with the band tee? That’s a good conversation starter. Not that I need one but still. I keep thinking about how sometimes people get caught up in little things like, “Oh I didn’t talk to this person enough” or “What if they didn’t like me?” I don’t even think about that stuff. I’m just there, living in the moment, all about enjoying the company and the fun. I guess some people stress too much but I really don’t get it, life’s too short to worry. Just dance, laugh, eat chips, and everything else will fall into place. No drama, no fuss. Just fun times. Isn’t it weird how different people handle parties? Like, some will just stand back and observe while others jump right in. I wonder what goes through their minds? I mean, I can’t imagine just being a wallflower; I’d have to do something to break the ice. Maybe telling a joke or a funny story. Speaking of which, I should ask if anyone’s bringing board games. Those can break the ice too and they’re great for bonding. I love how games get people laughing and talking more naturally, way cooler than a random chat, like you can share a piece of yourself while trying to beat someone at Monopoly or whatever. How do people even decide what parties to go to? I mean, if it’s a party and friends are there then why not? There was this other thing going on across town but I didn’t even hesitate to choose Ryan’s. It’s like, friends first, always. I need that social energy, and not being around people feels weird. I kind of thrive on their energy, it’s like this awesome cycle of fun, you know? Plus, the more, the merrier right? Okay, so, I need to finish this up and get ready, thinking about making a fun playlist for the car on the way there since that’s a whole mood. Something upbeat to keep the vibes high. I can already see stepping into Ryan’s place, bright lights, laughter everywhere, music pumping, oh man, I can’t wait to squeeze everyone and just dive into the fun. Let’s go, let’s go! This is going to be epic. I’m ready for the excitement, the laughter, living in the moment and soaking it all in. Let’s do this!

What’s next on my to-do list? I don’t even have one. Keep forgetting things. I should really make a habit of writing things down, but honestly, who has time for that? Alright, focus. There was that party last weekend, right? The one where everyone was dressed as pirates and I went as Captain Jack but then ended up totally switching to a mermaid because I found this glittery tail at the thrift store and it just screamed at me. Sometimes you gotta listen to the glitter, you know? And everyone was like “Whoa, where did you get that?” and I was like “I made it!” even though I really just bought it. Whatever, it’s not like I’m lying. I’m just creatively interpreting the truth. Everybody does that in some way. It’s called storytelling. I love how parties can go from awkward silences to people dancing on tables in like five seconds. It’s like this collective energy that takes over. The music gets everyone going and then suddenly you’re in a circle doing the chicken dance with a guy dressed as a taco. Like, where did you even come from? Taco man is hilarious, but also, I’m hungry. Food at parties is always a gamble. Sometimes you get those gross chips that taste like cardboard and sometimes you score a platter of delicious buffalo wings. I always hope for the wings. But I never really stop to eat. I just keep talking to people. They’re all so interesting, right? There was this one girl last time who said she’s been to fifteen countries and I was like “What’s your favorite?” and she goes “Mongolia” and I was like “Why???” and she explains this whole nomadic lifestyle and I’m just sitting there thinking about how I need to buy a ticket to Mongolia but I don’t even know where it is on the map. I probably should look that up. Okay, back to the party. The energy was buzzing. I remember someone started a karaoke session and I just jumped in. I don’t even know if I can sing, but who cares, right? Took on ‘Livin’ on a Prayer’—classic. And everyone joined in. We were like this big, happy choir. Well, except for the few who were like “Ugh, karaoke” but seriously, lighten up! Life’s too short not to belt a power ballad. There's definitely something exciting about spontaneity. I thrive on it. It’s like if you don’t take a chance, you might miss out on great experiences. So, what’s the deal with getting older? I mean, I feel like 22 is just a weird age. I’m still figuring out my life. Everyone’s like “You should have a plan” but that sounds boring. Plans can be restricting. I prefer to go with the flow. Sure, I might forget to submit an assignment on time or lose track of days, but the thrill of spontaneous road trips with friends? Worth it. We drove to the beach last month on a whim just because someone said they wanted ice cream. We ended up building a bonfire and roasting marshmallows until 2 AM which was probably a bad idea because I had class at 8 the next day, but whatever, right? These moments matter more. I should probably think about a career, though. Everyone’s pushing for internships and practical jobs. Blah. I don’t wanna be stuck behind a desk. I want to travel, explore. What if I become a travel writer? Or maybe a photographer? Or something completely outside the box like a professional kite surfer? The idea of being tied down to a nine-to-five job feels suffocating. What’s the point of living if you’re not enjoying every second? Okay, so I’ll find a job, but only if it lets me run free. And then there's the people part. This is tough because I have these intense conversations with friends about life and purpose and they’re all like “You need stability” and I’m like “But life is an adventure!” My friends can be so risk-averse sometimes and it’s frustrating. I’d rather embrace uncertainty than live in a bubble. I thought about going skydiving last week but didn’t have anyone to go with. Next time, I just gotta go solo. Just jump right in, I guess. And who even cares about societal norms? Why don’t we just create our own paths? Life is too vibrant to be anything other than what’s true to us. I’m not sure what’s next, but I’m ready to jump into whatever comes my way and trust the universe will catch me. I just want more adventures, more people, more nights of laughter, and less planning. Who needs a map, anyway?

okay so I just saw this meme about how cats believe they own the house and I can't stop laughing because it's so true and I think about my friend Sarah’s cat which is like this furry little dictator always demanding attention like he knows he's the king of the universe or something but honestly I love that about him and all pets really because they have this way of just commanding your attention without saying a word it's like their personality shines and you can't help but smile and it reminds me of the time we tried to dress him up for Halloween but he just sat there looking completely unimpressed like “why are you doing this to me” and we laughed so hard it was priceless. Speaking of Halloween, I’m already excited for the fall I love the whole vibe of it like pumpkin spice everything and sweaters and the leaves changing colors and all that I mean there’s so much to look forward to right the spooky decorations and haunted houses always get me hyped up especially when friends plan get-togethers and everyone brings their favorite snacks and we just end up having the best time it’s like those moments where you feel so alive and connected with everyone and it makes me grateful for friendships and how they just make life so much richer and deeper. Okay but what about that one time I went to a pumpkin patch with a huge group of friends and we had the best time picking pumpkins it felt like we were kids again running around and getting competitive over who could pick the biggest one and taking pictures with these goofy props they had everywhere like hay bales and scarecrows and I just remember feeling so happy that day like laughter was just echoing around us and we ended up carving those pumpkins later and it turned into this whole ridiculous competition to see who could make the funniest faces and ours ended up looking more like aliens than anything else but it didn’t even matter because we were all in it together, having fun, sharing those ridiculous moments. And I wonder how people can seem to get stuck in routines sometimes like not wanting to try new things or meet new people I mean, life’s too short to just sit back and watch it unfold, right? I thrive on new experiences! like trying out different restaurants or attending events in the city, I just love exploring and meeting new faces it’s like every conversation could lead to something amazing like new opportunities or friendships and it feels like there’s this whole world out there waiting for me but I also get why some people might feel overwhelmed or anxious it’s easy to get caught up in your comfort zone but stepping out is where the magic happens. Random thought and this might sound silly but I really want to learn to dance like not in a professional way just enough to have fun at parties and weddings and stuff I love how when the music starts, people just lose themselves in the rhythm it’s infectious and I remember this wedding last summer where everyone was on the dance floor just going wild and it looked like everyone was having the best time and I felt this desire to just jump in and join but I kinda stood there feeling shy about my moves even though I wanted to join the fun because what if I looked ridiculous? but I’m thinking maybe if I take a class or something I can break out of that shell because who cares what others think, right? It’s all about enjoying the moment. On another note, I’ve been thinking about trying to volunteer more this year I feel like it’s important to give back and make a difference in whatever way I can like help the community in some small way it gives me such a sense of fulfillment and seeing the smiles on people’s faces is just the best feeling my friend Julia does a lot of work with local shelters and she always has the craziest stories about how she gets to meet all these people and hear their backgrounds and it just blows my mind how resilient people can be and I want to be part of something like that, it seems so meaningful and I'm sure it’d help me grow as a person too. I get such a thrill out of discovering new interests like I just picked up painting and while I’m still figuring out how to hold a brush properly I feel like this urge to express myself and let my creativity flow spilling paint everywhere but it’s not even about perfection it’s about the experience and the joy it brings and I can’t wait to share what I make with friends, see their reactions, because that’s part of the fun right? And honestly, it’s inspiring to see how everyone interprets art differently like that one exhibit we visited last month, and there was this piece that looked like a total mess to me but someone else saw beauty in it and I just love how perspective shapes everything and fuels our conversations. Life is just a beautiful puzzle and I wanna continue piecing it together one adventure at a time.

Okay so I just had this wild idea about organizing a massive study group for finals. Like, I know we’re all stressed with so much to do and everyone is scrambling to understand the material. It's so chaotic in the library right now. I can’t even focus with all the random conversations and sounds around me, but that’s not the point. The point is, I thrive when I’m around people! This could be a game changer. I have this vision of all of us sitting together, quizzing each other, sharing notes. I know there are some who wouldn’t want to do it because of personality differences and perhaps they don't want to mix with everyone else. That’s fine, but it’s like, how are you going to figure it all out if you don’t collaborate? Teamwork makes the dream work, right? Anyway, my friend Sarah is great at math but she gets super nervous about presentations, which I think is unfortunate because she’s just brilliant. So I should probably talk to her about helping out with the group or maybe even leading a session. She's very straightforward, though. I wonder if she’d be up for it. Plus, I could coordinate schedules and make sure everyone is on track! Maybe I should write up a whole plan and send it out, like a meeting invite and everything. I like being the one who gets things done. It gives me a thrill, I guess. Who needs to be agreeable when you can be effective? Sometimes you have to push people a little, and they just don’t get that! I feel like I can see the end goal more clearly than most people I know. It’s not about being nice all the time. It’s about achieving something. Sometimes people think I’m too harsh, but come on, we’ve all got deadlines. I mean, I strive for excellence, and I expect that from others. Memories from high school pop into my head. There were always these group projects, which honestly were a bit infuriating because some people just didn’t care. I remember being in a group where one person just wanted to coast through while the rest of us put in real effort. I took charge then too. It’s just so important that everyone pulls their weight. I can’t handle slackers. There’s a satisfaction in completing something successfully together, but you have to bring the right people in. One time, we had this science project and it was due the next week. We waited until the last minute, which was a huge mistake, but I ended up just grabbing everyone’s notes and doing a huge presentation myself. The teacher was impressed with how I organized everything, but I was sweating bullets the entire time. It’s like, if you’re going to do something, do it well or don’t do it at all. I wonder if other people think like this or if it’s just me being too intense. Okay, so if I’m going to put this group together for finals, I need to have specific roles. I’ll be the planner, obviously. Who else? Maybe I can bring in Tom for his quizzes because he always comes up with the best questions. I should talk to him. He’s good at calculus and I appreciate that. I might not always agree with how he thinks about things, but I respect his knowledge. And Jennifer, she’s a whiz at history! I think I can convince her. She can help with study guides. This could end up being a fun way to bond, too. It can’t just all be about work. Maybe we can have snacks or something! I’m a sucker for some good snacks. It lightens the mood a little. Everyone brings something, and we can just hang out, study, and relax. It doesn’t have to be all serious. I like being the center of attention sometimes, keeping everyone engaged, throwing in jokes about how hard life is during finals. Lightheartedness is key, right? I think I’ll jump on the group chat right after this and get the ball rolling. It feels good to take the initiative. Can’t wait to hear what they think. It’ll be a mix of personalities, absolutely, but that’s just what makes it unique, right? I’m always up for a challenge, and a study group seems like the perfect way to tackle this time of stress. I mean, we can be supportive of each other too, even if we don’t always mesh perfectly. Just need everyone to show up and give their best effort. I just can’t wait to get started!

So there's this random sock on my floor. It’s like, pastel blue with little whales on it. I have no idea how it got there. Why do socks always end up in strange places? I guess it’s just one of life's mysteries. I keep things tidy, but socks... they just wander off. And I'm okay with it, I think it’s funny. Oh, speaking of funny, I watched a documentary on octopuses the other day. They’re so smart, like way smarter than most people give them credit for. They can open jars and change colors. I wonder what else they can do that we haven’t figured out yet. Do they have feelings? It's kinda wild to think about. Now, where was I? Oh right, socks and octopuses. Imagine if they had arms to throw the sock back into the drawer. Wouldn’t that be cool? But then, maybe it wouldn't be cool if they got too smart. Like, then they'd demand a salary for helping out around the house. Who knows? I’m always daydreaming like this. Sometimes I just sit and think about how weird everything is. The other day I was thinking about how many people just go along with what everyone else thinks. Like a herd of sheep. I can't stand that. But at the same time, I totally understand why they do it. It’s easier than thinking for yourself all the time. But that’s just not me. It’s exhausting to keep pretending to fit in. Sometimes I wish I could just be like everyone else, do the small talk, the chit chat. But then I’d miss out on the interesting stuff. Like when you dive deep into topics that light up your brain. I mean, I could go on forever about space, or music, or art. There's something about the vastness of it all that gets me pumped. The possibilities. But at parties, it's all: “So, how’s school?” And I’m sitting there like who cares about school? I mean, it’s part of life but it’s not everything. I want to know what drives people, what they fear, their dreams, but no one really goes that deep. And I’m usually just standing there nodding, zoning out, planning my escape route. Ugh, I was talking to someone the other day and they asked me what I did for fun, like what kind of fun is even possible within the walls of what society deems fun? I told them I collect vinyl records, and they looked at me like I had three heads. I couldn't help but roll my eyes. The conversation was tragically short after that. It's hard to connect with people when they only scratch the surface. I get it’s easier for most, but it doesn’t have to be boring. Don't people ever wonder what else is out there? Like, go to a museum and just get lost in the history of it all. The colors, the emotions captured in paint and sculpture. There’s so much depth. Anyway, I keep thinking about how I want to travel to Japan someday. The culture, the food, the cherry blossoms, how could you not want to experience that? I love how open everything feels there, the blend of the ancient and modern. I just want to walk around and soak it all in, like I’m a sponge. What’s it like to wander through Tokyo at night? Neon lights and little ramen shops? People talking, laughing, just living their lives. I think I’d get lost and be totally okay with it. I’d wander into a tiny bookstore and spend hours there. What’s the last book I read? Oh, it was about time travel. Time travel is such a fascinating concept, right? In one world, you can fix your mistakes or meet historical figures. I mean who wouldn’t want to grab a drink with Shakespeare? I often wonder if people realize how much thinking matters. It seems so simple, but we rarely unplug and just think. I could talk your ear off about the philosophy of existence, or the universe's size. Or maybe dive into the grocery store's cereal aisle and ponder why there are so many types of Cheerios. But no one wants to dive that deep, they just want to grab the Fruity Pebbles and go. I mean, I get it, life is busy. Still, there’s a whole universe waiting to be explored in your own head. And I feel like I’m the only one sometimes, like I’m in a bubble floating through a mundane world. But that’s okay, I guess. I’ll just keep collecting thoughts and ideas, let them spill over into conversations with people who get it. Until then, I’ll keep my octopus socks and wonder about the mysteries of life.

Okay so I just remembered that time in high school when I tried to organize that fundraiser for the art club and like half the school showed up and it was this chaotic whirl of energy and I felt alive but I also felt this weird pressure like what if we don’t raise enough money what if people don’t like my ideas what if I mess everything up but you know it all turned out okay in the end. I mean it’s crazy how you can feel this blend of excitement and anxiety all at once. Like that rush of adrenaline is so addictive but then there’s this nagging voice in my head saying “What if you fail?” It’s like my brain is a party but all the guests are just anxieties wearing different outfits and they're constantly trying to drown out the fun ones. Like I love going out and meeting new people but sometimes I just feel so overwhelmed by all the expectations of it. Do I have to be funny? Do I need to be the life of the party? I mean I can be, I can force myself to be, but it gets exhausting. Speaking of parties, I just saw this TikTok trend about making the most insane charcuterie boards ever and like I want to try it but also who has the time? I love the idea of being spontaneous and trying new things, like maybe I can call a few friends and gather supplies and just make a whole night of it but then I start thinking about how my apartment is a mess and I haven’t cleaned in weeks—wait did I even do laundry? It’s this endless cycle where I want to be organized and have it all together but then there’s this other part of me that just wants to run wild and try everything. I don’t even know if I really care what people think of me but then there's that existential dread of being judged and all those judgments are magnified in my head. Then again, I enjoy being “out there,” like I crave that attention almost, but too much feedback can send me spiraling. Sometimes I analyze every little thing I say or do, like what did I even mean by that? If someone laughs at my joke is it because they genuinely find me funny or are they just being nice? I mean, I know I have a dry humor and people don’t always get it, but it’s kind of thrilling to see their reactions. I thrive on that. But when I feel like I don’t connect, it’s like the whole world is closing in and I can’t catch my breath. I'm scrolling through Instagram and everyone seems to be living their best lives. Travel pics, cozy nights in, adventurous outings with friends, and here I am just wishing I could be carefree like that. But who even knows if those moments are real? I mean maybe they’re just filtered, curated highlights of their lives. I definitely have a tendency to want to curate the best moments for social media too. Does everyone do this? I wonder how people would react if I just posted my messy apartment or the takeout I ordered for the third time this week. Would people judge me? Would they unfollow me? Why do I care? Ugh, it’s such a cycle that keeps spinning. Sometimes I think about how I put so much pressure on myself to be perfect. I don’t even know what perfect means. Do I have to be the best at everything, or is being consistently decent enough? And why is there this gap between wanting to be successful and wanting to relax? I guess I’m never quite satisfied. I think it comes with being conscientious, but who decided that’s a good thing? I’ll make lists until the end of time, but the moment I don’t check every box, I panic. It’s almost comical how I can be so focused on my goals but at the same time, feel like I’m spiraling into this chaos of thoughts and expectations. I want to be more spontaneous but I can’t help but plan every little detail. Like if a friend asks me to go out, I immediately start thinking about what to wear, how to get there, who’s likely to be there, and if I’ll enjoy myself. Why can’t I just say yes and go? I mean life’s too short, right? Maybe that’s the secret to not getting lost in my head, just saying “yes” more often. But then the anxiety creeps back in and it’s like a tug-of-war between wanting to go out and feeling the need to stay in and recharge. It’s exhausting to dissect every little part of me. And what a weird mix of social energy and internal dread. I think I need to find a balance, or a way to enjoy both sides of who I am—this outgoing person who loves to explore and connect and this anxious person who just needs a hug or a moment of silence. Can I have both? Wouldn’t that be the ultimate win?

The clock says 3:12 and I can’t help but notice how time is slipping away again. Why am I always looking at the clock? It's like a countdown to anxiety. I should be more productive, right? But I don’t feel like I can focus. I have this assignment due tomorrow but it feels like every sentence I try to write is just another reason to feel stressed. I mean why do they even take grades so seriously? It’s just a piece of paper at the end of the day. But it matters, doesn’t it? Degrees and jobs and all that crap. It’s all connected. My mind is racing, and I can’t settle. I see things on my desk—a pen, an old coffee cup, my laptop that feels like a prison sometimes. I really need to get more organized, I think about how last week I spent hours planning my schedule but still ended up doing nothing. I don’t even know why I try. Sometimes I think I should just stop trying to be perfect, it’s exhausting. But then I remember what happened last semester when I let my grades slip. A disaster. My heart raced every time the professor walked into class. I saw that disappointment in their eyes, and I told myself I’d never feel that way again. But here I am, reliving it. I can almost hear their voice, asking if I’m even trying. I feel like I’m on a rollercoaster that just won’t stop. Up and down, and mostly down. I can’t even breathe properly when I think about it. I scroll through social media and cringe at how easy it looks for everyone else. Everyone seems to have it all figured out. They’re going out, laughing, having fun, and I’m just sitting here with my thoughts that never seem to let me rest. Why am I so different? Why do I care so much? I don’t even like those parties. Crowded spaces? No thanks. I’d rather be home with a book or scrolling through Netflix for hours. But then I think maybe I should go out, push myself a little, but it never works out the way I plan. I can feel that anxiety creeping in, suffocating me, and I end up bailing last minute. I wonder if anyone even notices. The thought of making new friends seems unbearable. It’s like building a wall and then realizing it’s made of paper. One little gust of wind and everything crumbles. I did try once, or maybe twice. Small talk? Ugh. The worst. I can never think of anything to say. Conversations feel like a minefield, and I’m always afraid I’m going to step on something and explode. I see people chatting, and it looks easy, but my mind is like a train going off the tracks. Sometimes I wish I could be more like them, just breezing through life, but then I remember how shallow those connections can be, or at least that’s what I tell myself to feel better about missing out. The truth is I just can’t handle the chaos. I've put so much pressure on myself to do well in school, afraid of failure, and I can’t even think about what happens next. Grad school? Getting a job? The thought alone sends jolts of electricity through me. What if I pick the wrong path? Or what if I thrive in a certain area and then realize I hate it? What is it they say? “Choose a job you love and you’ll never work a day in your life”? Yeah, right. That sounds nice but unrealistic. I need stability, a paycheck, something that doesn’t trap me in an emotional whirlwind. What if I’m stuck forever in a job I can’t stand? I keep thinking about my family, hearing them say stuff like “You’ll make us proud” or “You can do anything you set your mind to.” Great, tell me to set my mind to something when I can't even figure out how to set my mind to laundry. I can’t meet their expectations, I can’t even meet my own. I’m the master of self-doubt. It’s like a heavy weight on my chest. I should be more spontaneous, that’s what they say. Living in the moment but it feels so foreign to me. Everything seems so calculated, almost like I’m constantly evaluating risks. Can’t even enjoy a simple walk without thinking about everything I need to do. It’s exhausting. I just want to breathe, just once without feeling like I have to be someone I’m not. It’s like I’m wearing a mask, and underneath there’s this swirling fog of anxiety and fears. And I keep thinking, maybe one day I’ll figure it all out, but for now, I’m stuck in this endless loop of overthinking everything.

the clock says 2:15 and I’m thinking about how time really just slips away sometimes like sand in an hourglass not that I have one but I’ve seen them in movies or maybe it’s a video game where you have to solve puzzles before the sand runs out and it gets all tense and stressful but I’m not stressed right now I’m more just calm and drifting through thoughts like a leaf on a river floating wherever the current takes it and today it's taking me toward the idea of food what do I want to eat later I could go for something comforting like mac n cheese but what about sushi I haven't had that in ages or taco night could be fun too maybe I’ll just order takeout though that feels lazy but whatever it’s just food it shouldn’t be a big deal but I remember that time I tried to make sushi at home it was a total mess rice everywhere and I didn’t even get the seaweed wrapped right it turned into this ugly roll that tasted fine but looked like a sad little burrito what even am I doing with my life and then I think about school and classes and how I’ve been slacking a bit maybe it’s more than a bit it’s like a dark cloud over my head when I think about deadlines for that history paper that I’ve barely started I just wish I could find the motivation but right now it feels more interesting to think about my travel plans for next summer than research the fall of the Roman Empire but can you blame me it sounds way more fun to be on a beach in Spain getting sunburnt while trying to speak Spanish with someone who knows how to help me navigate the local cuisine olive oil and fresh bread and maybe that weird fish dish everyone talks about that I still can’t remember the name of I should probably check that I think I have a book about Spanish culture somewhere on my shelf underneath all those novels I haven’t read yet it’s crazy how I tell myself I’ll get to them but they just keep stacking up and yet I can’t stop buying them it feels like collecting but I know I should just focus on what I have no wait I’m thinking about street markets and how vibrant they are in places like Barcelona or Lisbon with all the colors the spices hanging from wooden stalls and people laughing and talking it feels so alive and I always feel like I’m missing out when I see travel vlogs pop up on my feed like why can’t I be adventurous like them why am I stuck in this little bubble where everything is so predictable maybe I should join a club or something but group activities kind of weird me out and being around too many people drains me sometimes okay not always but definitely sometimes I prefer the quiet but then again I do enjoy a deep conversation with someone who just gets me if I could find one of those people at a club it could be worth it right but then again the prospect of meeting new people feels exhausting it’s a weird dichotomy I live in but I guess that’s just how it is I think about my roommate who’s super outgoing and drags me to parties and how I tend to end up in a corner talking to someone else who’s also not outgoing it works out alright we form our own little bubble in the chaos of noise and laughter it’s comforting in a way I guess I could say I’m good at that making connections with the quieter folks even if it’s a little awkward to start like that one time at a friend’s birthday party when we really connected over a shared love for that obscure band nobody else knew about I still think about that how we just bonded over music and our weird sense of humor and how I could talk for hours with someone who gets it I find myself drifting back to thoughts of those novels and characters that feel so real even though they’re just words on a page how do writers conjure that magic it blows my mind I wish I could write like that but I also think maybe it’s more about finding my own voice whatever that means because I tend to just spill everything like this just thoughts tumbling out like a cascade when I really should be focusing but it feels good to just release this jumble it’s freeing it’s like decluttering my brain which is funny because my physical space is a mess but I like it that way it’s organized chaos if that makes sense and I can always find what I need somehow even when it looks like a tornado hit my desk I need to keep it real too it’s like I appreciate the little things like the smell of coffee in the morning or the way the light hits the trees outside my window in the fall those are the moments that ground me and I don’t need to be surrounded by people to appreciate them but that doesn’t mean connection isn’t important just that it comes in different forms I think about how I want to create art or something that makes people feel something but I’ve got to start somewhere right and if I just keep pushing it back it’ll never happen so here I am typing this whole stream of consciousness trying to shake things loose and make sense of my thoughts and feelings and plans and fears as they all swirl together like the colors on a palette waiting to burst onto canvas or into the world and I just hope I figure some of it out before it’s time to order sushi or make my way to class again

I just looked at my phone and it’s almost noon. I should really get up, but the blanket is so cozy. I’ve got a half-eaten bag of chips on the floor next to me. They’re the salt and vinegar kind, which I love but my friends always say they smell bad. Can’t say I care, I enjoy them. Speaking of friends, I haven’t been social lately. I guess it doesn’t bother me, but sometimes I feel bad because they reach out and I just... don’t want to go anywhere. I often find myself thinking they probably think I’m antisocial or something, but really it’s more about finding my own space. Like my mind needs to wander. I think about how interesting it is that people are so different. I admire how outgoing some people are but it feels exhausting when they’re so much energy, always wanting to plan something or go out. I’m more about the simple pleasures, like laying in bed and just thinking. Or doodling random things in my notebook. The doodles… Ugh, I’m more of a free spirit with my thoughts sometimes than my actions. I’ll get these ideas and start drawing them out but then it doesn’t translate well. Still, it’s fun. I just let my hand move across the page without any worries about how it looks, about whether it’s good or bad. Sometimes it’s a spiral, or an eye, or just little characters that pop up in my mind. I wonder if I should submit something to that art show downtown but then I think about the pressure of it all. What if people don’t like it? But then again, I think it’s more about the experience than what others think, right? Yesterday, I was listening to this album that feels like a journey through different emotions, mostly happy but a little sad, too. It’s funny how music can transport you. It’s like a door to another world. I really get lost in those moments. I had this idea to write a story inspired by the songs but then I got distracted by a butterfly flying past my window. Such a bright yellow, like a ray of sunshine in the day. I tried to focus back on the story but my mind just drifted to how butterflies represent transformation. Then I thought about how I often feel like I go through transformations in my own life, just in tiny ways. Like how I used to hate olives but now I love them. It’s wild how tastes can change over time or how perspectives shift without you realizing it. I think about how people often expect certain things from you based on your personality or previous choices. I’m very agreeable, I’ll say yes to something because I’d rather not cause friction. I’d rather flow with the current than fight against it, but that can lead to overcommitting to things I don’t want to do. There’s a balance there that’s tough, but I try to keep in mind that saying no isn’t always a bad thing. I just don’t grasp it as often as I probably should. Still, it’s interesting how I can understand that inherently but not act on it. Maybe it’s the low conscientiousness? My mind wanders and I forget about the practicalities sometimes. Life feels too fluid to be tied down by strict schedules or plans and that feels freeing. But then there’s the messiness. Today I sat out on the balcony for a moment, staring at the clouds. They were so fluffy, like big cotton balls, floating around aimlessly. It makes me think of how we all float through life in our own way, each with our own colors and shapes. We land on different paths. Some merge, some diverge. I can’t help but wonder about all the paths I haven’t taken. Sometimes I think, “What if I had picked a different major?” or “What if I’d moved to a different city?” But there’s something calming, almost reassuring, about knowing I’m in the right place for me right now. It’s taken a while to accept that actively, though. Food is another thing I think about. I could write a whole essay on food alone! I love trying new things, especially cuisines from around the world. The flavors, the spices. I remember this Thai place I tried last month. Everything was a burst of flavor. I felt like I was somehow transported to Thailand. Food connects people. It’s a common ground. I think sharing meals is one of the best ways to bond with others. But I don’t often host, partly because it feels like too much effort, and partly because I get anxious about what people will think of my space or my cooking. I guess I need to work on that. People say they love to come over no matter what. Okay, I should probably grab a snack or something. The chips are calling my name.

the clock says 3:17pm and I can’t help but think how stagnant the air feels like it just clings to me I’ve always been a little weird about time it’s like a slow ocean wave that trickles but sometimes it crashes in I can feel myself overthinking every single tick like, what am I supposed to be doing right now? the laundry is piling up but I’ve been avoiding it maybe because I just hate folding clothes it feels like a never-ending chore and yet I know it’s part of life but oh man the thought of it sends me into this stress spiral like “do I even have enough time to sit down and breathe?” I often get lost in thoughts about details that nobody cares about but me like maybe if I sprinkle the detergent just right it’ll smell extra fresh I remember when my mom would let me pick out fabric softener at the store the choices were overwhelming and I would obsess over which scent was the best I was so anxious like, what if I picked the wrong one? it’s wild how those little things are so intertwined with my memories I feel like I’m standing at this big intersection of moments in my life where everything is tied to trivial decisions but they feel big when I’m in them like do I say yes to the third round of invitations for game night? my friends are great but getting together always sends me into this low-key panic mode like do I even have enough to say? is it okay if I sit back and listen? I never want to be overly domineering but I also want to be involved in the conversation without becoming a ghost that just floats around so I tend to just nod along and smile but then I’m worrying if I look engaged enough it’s a cycle like an infinite loop and every friendly face just makes my heart race in this familiar dance of social anxiety but also, it’s delightful sometimes when I do open up and laugh but I get so anxious if there’s a pause in the conversation because that means I have to jump in and fill it right? I can’t let it linger too long or everyone will think I’m weird I mean, I am weird but you know what I mean then again, the thought of being weird feels oddly comforting because there’s a shared bond in that feeling of not fitting into some perfect mold everyone seems to navigate so effortlessly I’ve always admired those people who can just walk into a room and light it up I wonder if they feel the pressure of expectation like I do or if it just comes naturally to them how do they do it? do they worry too? sometimes I think if I could just take off this heavy coat of anxiety I’d float right into those circles but I also don’t want to lose this part of myself that craves depth and connection like I’m all about meaningful conversations even if they take place in whispers at midnight, those are the best moments when the world is quiet and all you have is the soft warmth of a friend’s presence I can talk about dreams and fears and hopes and what ifs for hours without feeling overwhelmed but the second it becomes a large group I feel like the air gets sucked out of me and my brain starts racing like, what if I say something stupid? and then a million scenarios play out in my head like a horror movie but what if instead of that I was just totally okay with being my own self like, maybe I could embrace the oddities I do have instead of trying to mask them as if they’re something to hide but I know that sounds easier said than done because the world is built on these unwritten rules of social interaction that push us toward this ideal image but I guess that’s the beauty of art and creativity right? being able to express the chaos in unique ways I remember when I first discovered painting it felt like this release every stroke of the brush felt like a piece of my soul was being shared on the canvas like throwing my worries aside and just being in that moment like the colors dancing together in harmony or chaos but always telling a story that reflects my inner world it was liberating I often think about returning to those moments of creation but then I get overwhelmed by the idea of “what do I even want to say?” but maybe the act of creating doesn’t need to have a purpose sometimes just splashing paint around can be therapeutic maybe I should just let it flow the way thoughts do and not worry about how it looks at the end it’s like when I write too I just let my fingers do the talking and hope for the best but there’s always that little nagging thought in the back of my mind telling me, “is this good enough?” I’m learning how to silence that voice little by little like maybe every errant thought can find its place and every ounce of anxiety can just sit beside me instead of pulling me down it’s all a work in progress isn’t it?

I just remembered that time I misplaced my keys for like four hours. I looked everywhere, retracing my steps again and again. Sometimes I wonder why I even get out of bed. I think about how much easier life would be if I could just have a designated spot for everything. Maybe a little bowl by the door or something. Simple things, you know? The idea of tidiness is calming but I can never follow through because my brain always just feels like it’s in slow motion. I mean, how hard can it be to keep track of a few items? But then there's the couch, it’s too comfortable, and Netflix is too enticing. Binge-watching is serious business, like the new season of that show I can't quite remember the name of. It’s a crime show or a drama or both. I always forget the titles, so many out there! Sometimes I feel like a hermit, just me and my snacks. The last time I went out to eat was weeks ago. I remember the food was good but not great enough to make me want to go back. I’m kind of picky that way. People seem to love trying new places, new experiences, but there’s a level of comfort in familiarity that I like. Think of the burger joint down the street. I trust the fries there, and the shakes, I mean, you can’t go wrong with classic vanilla. By the way, it’s mid-afternoon and I haven’t eaten yet. Is that a problem? Probably should grab something before it gets too late. But there's this weird thing about preparing food, like it feels like too much effort sometimes. I get that it’s a basic human need, but thinking about it gives me anxiety. I like simple things, like a sandwich or yogurt. I don’t have to think too much about that. But then again, I need to remind myself to eat. The other day I got caught up doing nothing for too long, and I felt a bit dizzy. I thought, wow, that’s a weird feeling. I hate when I find myself lost in thought, and suddenly hours have passed. I get it’s bad for me but my brain just wanders. Seems like everyone else has such busy lives. Why don’t I feel that pressure? I mean, I care about people’s lives, but I don’t want to be swept up in the chaos. Oh, right, the group project is due soon. I need to check in with the others. They all seem to get along well; I just kind of float along in the background. I’m okay with that, though. Sometimes it’s easier when others take the lead. I can offer input but I get overwhelmed by all the ideas and energy bouncing around. I’d rather support than be at the forefront. I wonder how they even manage to be so social. Do they think about how draining it is? I guess they’re more extroverted; they thrive on that stuff. I wish I could adopt that energy sometimes, but it feels so forced. Just the other day someone was talking about a concert they went to and I felt an odd mix of interest and exhaustion. The vibe was great; everyone was singing along, but I can’t imagine myself in that crowd. Too loud, too many people. Too…everything. Speaking of music, I like the soothing stuff, the background noise that makes me feel relaxed. I don’t get the appeal of loud concerts or massive festivals. Give me a good playlist and my room, and I’m content. All those people bumping into each other would make me anxious. Maybe I’m missing out. Or maybe I’m just not missing much. I keep reading how people are more fulfilled when they step outside their comfort zones, but comfort zones are comfortable for a reason. I think I’d prefer a quiet walk in the park over a wild night out. Nature is reassuring. The way trees just stand there, no rush, no chaos. I think everyone should take it slow every once in a while. What’s with the rush anyway? I see people racing, racing to get to class, racing to work, as if the world might end if they take a moment to breathe. I don’t get it. Some of my friends always seem stressed about deadlines. I can see them worried; they wear it on their faces. I wish I could do more to help. Maybe I should just invite them to a movie night or bring some snacks. It’s funny how food can bring people together. Like, I don’t need to solve their problems, but I can be there. I suppose I like being a support system, something I’m good at. Just existing with calm energy. But, I guess there are so many unknowns in life and that’s what drives so many people—sometimes it’s hard for me to grasp why they chase them so fiercely. What’s next? I guess I can just take it day by day, one simple step at a time.

The clock on my phone says 3:14 PM. I can't believe I spent another hour scrolling. I really should be studying for that psychology exam but every time I think about it, my stomach turns. Why do I keep putting things off? I just can't seem to get myself to focus on anything meaningful. I can think of a million excuses, like the fact that my apartment is a mess. Clothes everywhere, pizza boxes piling up in the corner. It feels like my mind, messy and overflowing. I glance at the pile of dishes in the sink. I should really do those, but what’s the point? No one sees them but me. Who cares? Going to wash them feels like an impossible task. I cringe at the thought. Social things are overwhelming. I feel like I’m always on the outside looking in. Texting friends feels like a chore. Why is it so hard to respond? I see the notifications and just freeze. Like, what if they expect me to say something interesting? I’m not interesting. I don’t have anything to add. I think of what they might say, dissect their words in my head like I’m analyzing some deep philosophical text. And I can’t keep up. I think they all have these lives full of excitement and spontaneity, and I’m just here, stuck. It would be nice to have someone to talk to— like a friend, maybe. But how do I even start? I keep thinking, maybe if I just book a coffee date or something? But no, that sounds awful. What if they cancel? What if I cancel? I can’t put myself through the stress of planning. Just the thought makes me want to hide under my covers. Everyone seems so social, and I feel like I’d rather be alone, but I don’t want to be alone. It’s a never-ending loop. Ugh, my mind. I think I overthink everything. I’ll just stick to my usual routine. It’s easier. Just binge-watch another series. The latest one is about a group of friends who do incredible things, like travel the world and fall in love. Why can’t I be like them? They seem so effortlessly happy. I scroll through Instagram and see all the photos, everyone’s smiles, their perfect lives. Sometimes it feels like a competition, but I don’t want to compete. I just want to feel like I belong without having to put myself out there. Then I think about the last time I went out— it was awful. So much noise, too many people, and I just stood there, holding a drink and wishing I was somewhere else. Why can’t I just be comfortable in my skin? I think maybe I don’t fit in because I don’t really want to. I hate small talk. It feels empty and fake. Like, why are we pretending we care about the weather? It’s just a way to fill the silence. Why don’t people just admit that they’re awkward? I can’t stand it when people say “just be yourself.” What does that even mean? If I just be myself, I’ll be sitting in silence, over-analyzing every freaking word. And then there’s my family. Oh boy, they’re always asking when I’m going to get a real job, or why I’m still single. Like I need to justify my existence. Just let me be. What’s wrong with living in a little bubble? It’s comfortable. I can’t handle the pressure of expectations. I don’t want to have to explain myself all the time. I don’t want to deal with the judgment or the unsolicited advice. I feel on edge whenever they call. Can’t we just keep it simple? School isn’t easier. I sit in class, and my mind drifts. The teacher rambles on and I’m just there, staring at the desk. Everything feels pointless sometimes. Is a degree really worth this stress? I think about how I can’t even follow a simple syllabus. I miss deadlines, I forget assignments, and then I panic. Stress makes me sick. Is it worth it to even try? I think I might just withdraw from everything. Sometimes I fantasize about just disappearing for a bit. Just a break from it all. But even that feels risky, like what will they think if I just ghost everyone? But maybe they wouldn’t even notice. I mean, people have their own lives, right? They wouldn’t miss me. It’s hard to think that sometimes. I remember a friend saying that it’s about quality over quantity, but I don’t have either. And then I spiral. Why can’t I just get it together? I should care more, but every effort just feels like I’m setting myself up for failure. So here I am, in this cycle of anxiety and avoidance. It’s exhausting, really. And I think, maybe I should try harder to get out of it. But then I just feel so tired, like what's the point? The sun is shining, I guess, but I’m not outside. I’m just here, overthinking and missing opportunities, all while the clock ticks away. What a waste of time.

I can’t stop thinking about how strange it is that people can be so different and yet so similar at the same time. Like, I get along with just about everyone, but sometimes I would really rather just be by myself. I mean, not in a sad way, but just because there are so many things I want to explore in my brain and sometimes being around lots of people makes it hard to focus. A friend of mine keeps saying I should go out more, but I don’t know. It’s exhausting to think about planning social events or trying to keep up with conversations when my mind is racing in a million directions. Yesterday, I spent hours reading about the migration patterns of birds. They migrate so far and so often, it’s fascinating! I wonder if I could just be like them, fly away for a bit and explore places without all the human complexities. Okay, back to the birds. It’s amazing how they just know where to go. My mind sometimes feels the same way—like it’s just wandering without any real destination. I often find myself lost in a random Wikipedia hole, you know? One link leads to another, and then suddenly I’m reading about bioluminescent jellyfish. How do they even glow? The ocean is so deep and mysterious. Maybe I’ll end up there someday. Maybe I can make a trip. I’d love to dive and see that world, even if it means being deep underwater where it’s dark and quiet. That might be different. What if I get scared? Nah, I would just float. I wonder how many people have the same thoughts while they’re swimming. Do they think about the vastness of the ocean or just keep their heads down and paddle? People talk about self-care, and I think it’s interesting how that looks different for everyone. Some people need to go out and be around friends to feel good, while for me it’s more about having time to think or create. Sometimes I draw, but I’m not very good. Nothing I make feels like it captures what I see in my mind, but I keep doing it anyway. I think that’s the point, right? Just to let things flow without worrying too much about the end result. It feels nice to let the pencil run on the paper, even if it ends up being a mess of lines and colors. I’ve heard that doodling helps you concentrate, but I often wonder if it’s really helping or if I’m just distracting myself from whatever I should be focusing on. Then there’s the whole relationship thing. I like people, I do, but sometimes I just don’t want to deal with the expectations. Everyone seems to be looking for connection, and I don’t mind talking with someone when it happens, but pushing myself into social situations can feel like trying to squeeze into a pair of jeans I outgrew. Just uncomfortable. I often smile and nod when people talk about their plans for the weekend. I’m usually either content to stay home or have a spontaneous adventure that nobody else knows about. That’s the beauty of openness, I guess. I can change my plans in an instant if something interesting pops up in my mind. And then there’s food. I love trying new food because it’s like tasting new experiences. Just the other day, I tried a dish I couldn’t even pronounce. It was spicy and tangy, and I didn’t know what most of the ingredients were but it was so good! I keep thinking about how flavors can remind you of places or people. Even a simple meal can hold a story if you think about it long enough. I once had a friend who only ever wanted to eat pizza, and I’d eat pizza too, but I kept hoping we could explore more. There’s a whole world of flavors out there! In the end, I just want to gather experiences. The more I learn, the more I realize how vast everything is. Sometimes I worry I won’t have enough time to learn it all. But maybe I don’t need to know everything, just the things that make me curious. Some days feel like they drag on forever, and others slip through my fingers like sand. I want to savor them all, whether they’re chaotic or calm. I feel a tug toward adventure but in my own way, not necessarily the way everyone else thinks of it. I guess that’s what makes it interesting. And the thought of someone else maybe feeling the same way makes me smile. I’m okay with that. I’m okay with just being.

I guess I’m thinking about that presentation coming up. I should really start preparing it even though I have a good grasp on the topic. It’s just a moral philosophy thing, and I sort of like those discussions. But I prefer having my thoughts organized rather than speaking off the cuff. It’s really important to me to have everything laid out in front of me so I don’t miss any points. I can’t help but think of all the questions people might ask. I hope there are not too many interruptions; I really get flustered when I’m interrupted. Because I like to have my thoughts planned and clear, every question will throw me off course a bit, but I hope they’re not too challenging. If they are, I just want to be able to answer calmly and reasonably. Maybe I’ll write down some potential questions and what I could say to them. I know that sounds a bit over-prepared, but it’s just how my mind works. People say I overthink sometimes, but I’d rather be safe than sorry. It’s about clarity and keeping everyone on the same page. And, of course, making sure the professor thinks I’ve done enough. I know I have, but it’s still that pressure in the back of my mind. I just remembered the last time I did a presentation. It was in a group setting but I ended up doing a lot of the talking because the others didn’t feel comfortable. I don’t mind that, I really don’t, but I just felt responsible for keeping everything together. If I’m honest, I prefer working in a small group where everyone is contributing equally. But, I think people often see me as a safe bet, the stable one. I can be counted on. I worry about how I’ll come across though. Will they think I’m being patronizing for taking charge? I definitely don’t want that. I care too much about everyone’s feelings. What a messy balance between being assertive and still being polite and agreeable. I keep thinking about how I need to have everything tidied up before the actual presentation day. I can’t just wing it even if I know the material. Organization has always been a thing for me. I have this planner where I jot down everything, even the smallest tasks. It’s not just about school; it’s my life too. I have all these color-coded sections for different aspects of my life—school work, assignments, deadlines. I even write down when I need to wash my clothes. It’s a little obsessive maybe. I just like knowing that I won’t forget something important. Might be boring to some, but I can’t understand how people can just let everything float around. That feels chaotic. After the presentation, I might need to take a breather. Just go somewhere quiet, maybe grab a tea or something to settle my mind. I don’t really go out much after classes, I’d much rather be home, reading or watching documentaries. They help me unwind. It seems like every time I consider going to some social gathering, I think, what’s the point? I mean, catching up with friends can be nice, but so often it feels forced. I’d rather have a deep conversation with one person than hear the same small talk repeated in a crowded room. Evenings are typically really peaceful for me. I think about cooking dinner, trying out new recipes within my comfort zone, of course. I’m really good at making pasta, so I should stick to that for now. I like following recipes rather than experimenting. I find comfort in knowing what I should do next. Maybe garlic bread on the side, it’s one of those things that can make even a simple meal feel like a treat. Maybe I’ll call my mom while I cook. Her voice is calming, and she always has some good advice or just a funny story about her day. I like those check-ins. It's reassuring to have that connection with her. I keep thinking, is it strange to be so content with this? I feel like my peers might judge me for not going out all the time or being less adventurous, but I don’t care. It’s not worth stressing over. I know what I enjoy, and it’s enough for me. Stability over chaos. I think I’ll just keep my circle small, with genuine connections. Those superficial interactions don’t do much for me. Just more noise, more pressure, more unpredictability. I think I’m ready for this presentation. I’m structured, I’m organized, and I will show them that I care about the subject and about them too.

The clock says 2:42 PM and I’m already tired. I don’t want to do this. Should I? I should have cleaned my room instead. It’s a mess. Clothes everywhere, that half-eaten sandwich from last week just chilling on my desk like it has a right to be there. The sandwich is probably a biohazard now. I should throw it away. Do I even want to go back to the kitchen? More dishes waiting for me. Why cannot I just do the easy things? It always feels like a mountain to climb. I know this is bad but what if I just ignore it, close my eyes, hope it disappears like my motivation for doing anything. And then there are all the people. I hate being around them, especially when they’re loud. Group projects. Why do professors insist on making us work in groups? Can't they see I practically panic at the thought of speaking up? They think collaboration is key but it just feels like I’m stuck in a sea of chatter and I can’t even find a lifeboat. I always think about how I could just disappear and no one would notice. Or maybe they would, but only for a second. Then it would go back to their conversations about movies or whatever. I don’t even watch movies. I don’t know what to say when they mention the latest blockbuster. “Oh, you liked that one? I haven’t seen it.” I get tired just thinking about it. It’s exhausting. And social media? Ugh. I scroll through Twitter and see all these people living their best lives, going out, laughing, making friends. I just deleted my Instagram. I thought it would help. I felt a little lighter, like maybe I could breathe, but then I got anxious about the next time someone asked me to share my handle. “Oh, I don’t have one.” That awkward pause. Why do I even care? But I do. It’s like a compulsion to constantly measure myself against what everyone else is doing. This self-imposed standard that I never reach, so what’s the point? And that whole “be true to yourself” mantra? It’s not that easy. What does that even mean? Then there are my grades. They’re slipping. I can feel it. I used to care but now? I barely show up to classes. I should go to that lecture on psychology. It’s interesting but the professor’s voice just drones on and my mind drifts. I scribble down half-hearted notes that I’ll probably never look at again. I could forget about it, but what if it’s important? I know one day I’ll wish I had actually paid attention, but right now? It feels unbearable. I can’t focus. There are too many distractions. All I want is to crawl into bed and watch the walls. I swear that wall paint was a different color last month. It’s just so dull. My phone buzzes. Who is it? I don’t even want to look. It’s probably someone wanting to hang out or ask for a favor, and I don’t want to deal with it. Probably something like “Hey, wanna get lunch?” No, I don’t want lunch. I want to sit here and do nothing. But if I ignore it, then they think I’m rude or unfriendly. What’s wrong with me? Why can’t I just be more…chill? I see the pile of assignments stacked up on my desk. Oh god. I’ve got that paper due next week. I just need to start it. But I can’t think. What do I even say? My brain feels like molasses. I can’t organize my thoughts, I’d rather scroll again and scroll again. So stupid. I should get up. Just do it. But getting up feels like running a marathon. What’s the point really, in doing anything? The cycle just repeats. I know I’m supposed to find some meaning but all I find is the same empty feeling. Should I text my mom? She’d just say to stay positive. She doesn’t get it. She went to college back when it was different, quieter maybe. Things feel overwhelming now, and she can’t see it. I can’t put it into words for her anyway. She’d think I’m overreacting. Maybe I am. I don’t even know what reacting means anymore. I’m tired of feeling like this. I should probably see a counselor because my brain is like a tangled ball of yarn and I can’t find the end. But then again, sitting with a stranger and talking? Sounds awful. I look out the window and everything seems so normal. People walking by, laughing, chatting—living. I’m just here. Trapped in my thoughts. I should figure out how to change that, I guess. Or maybe I’ll let it go, let the mess stay. At least I don’t have to face the world, right? I think I’m just going to sit here and stare at this wall. That feels safe.
