It's just back to that cycle again. দুপুর ৩:১৭, and nothing seems different.

I just remembered that time I went to that concert last summer. The energy was electric, everyone was jumping around like crazy. I love music, it’s like a universal language. The way people come together at live shows, it’s just so beautiful. I was in the front row and it felt like I could touch the singer, that connection. I always get this rush being around people. I thrive in crowded spaces, honestly. I think it’s the laughter and the chatter, the way voices combine and create this amazing soundtrack of life. It’s like every moment is bursting with possibility, every conversation a chance to explore something new. Speaking of exploring, I’ve been thinking about traveling again. There are so many places I want to see. Italy is high on my list. The food! I could practically taste the pasta right now. And the art, oh my gosh, I’d get lost in the museums for hours. Rome, Florence, Venice. I want to sip espresso in a café and just people-watch. I love how traveling teaches you about different cultures and opens your eyes to the way other people live. Everybody has their own story, and I want to hear them all. And then there’s that quirky little shop I found downtown last week. It was filled with vintage records and funky clothes. I spent hours just browsing, listening to the owner talk about each item. People are so interesting, the way they hold onto memories and objects. I bought this bright yellow scarf—like a little piece of sunshine to carry around with me. I don’t know if I’ll wear it often, but it makes me happy just to have it. I’m thinking about how connected we are, even in these little moments. I remember chatting with the cashier there; she told me about her favorite band and how music is a thread that ties people together. Her eyes lit up when she talked about going to festivals. I feel like I could talk to anyone about anything. Sometimes, it’s like I can make friends with a brick wall if I have to. What’s life without a little laughter and connection? I’m also planning a group study session for next week. It’s so much more fun to study with friends; we bounce ideas off each other, and it’s less like studying and more like brainstorming. I’m excited to see who shows up. I hope everyone brings snacks; that’s crucial. Who can resist sugary treats while cramming for finals, right? I love how people come together over food, too. It’s like sharing a meal creates this intimate space where everyone is comfortable to share thoughts and opinions. I thrive in those spaces. I’ve been reading this book on personal growth and self-discovery. The ideas are blowing my mind. It’s about stepping out of your comfort zone and trying new things. I just signed up for a pottery class. Like, who even am I? I’m super stoked about it. I’ve always admired pottery from afar, the way it transforms the clay into something beautiful. There’s just something so grounding about working with your hands, creating something tangible. I wonder if I’ll be any good at it. Whatever, it’ll be fun learning with other people who are also stepping out of their comfort zones. It’s funny how change doesn’t scare me. I mean, I can’t even remember the last time I felt nervous about something new. I embrace it. I know I might fail or it might not go as planned, but that’s part of the adventure, right? I think being open to experiences makes life richer. I’m not afraid of failure; it’s just another step toward success. Dinner plans this weekend, too. I’m going to this new sushi place with a few friends. I’ve been craving sushi for weeks! There’s something so refreshing about it, the flavors mix in just the right way. Plus, it’s an experience in itself—sitting at the sushi bar, watching the chefs work their magic, the aesthetic of it all. I love trying new restaurants, too. Food tells a story, just like music. Sometimes I wonder how all these little moments can fit together to create a beautiful life tapestry. It’s like each experience is a thread woven into the larger picture. I just want to keep collecting moments, people, places, and feelings. I think that’s what keeps life exciting. It’s like a constant adventure, and I want to experience it all—rich, vibrant, and full of laughter. Every time I meet someone new, I think about the potential for connection, the possibility for friendships, stories, and memories waiting to unfold. The world is such a big, beautiful place, and I want to be a part of it all.

Okay so I’ve been thinking about how much energy I get from people but also how exhausting it can be. Like, social gatherings are great but they drain me sometimes, and I don’t even know how that works. I love being around friends, feeling the buzz of laughter and conversation, everyone talking over each other, but later it’s like, wow I need to go recharge. I can’t even sit still in classes sometimes. They could be fun if professors were more engaging. I’d probably love a class that’s just pure discussion. Oh, and that reminds me of this group project I did last week – total chaos in the best way. Everyone had ideas, bouncing off each other like it was a pinball machine. One person suggesting – I don’t even remember what the topic was, but it got derailed into this massive debate about who would win in a fight – a tiger or a bear? I mean, honestly, who cares? But we all cared because we were fully engaged, and that’s what I thrive on, I think. Okay, but then I’ll get home after all that excitement, and it’s like the walls are closing in on me. It’s the weirdest contradiction. I crave connection but also solitude. I’ll put on my favorite playlist, scroll through pictures of friends, but it’s not the same. I have this overwhelming urge to reach out, message someone, but then part of me says no, just chill a little. Am I too dependent on others? I think about this a lot. Sometimes I wonder if I'm annoying, like when I pop into group chats just to say hi, and I get a bunch of “hey”s back, and I feel so warm inside. I just want to keep the energy alive. Speaking of energy, I really should have gone to the gym today. I had all this pep in my step after that morning coffee, like I could conquer the world, but then I just sat on the couch and watched TikTok videos for two hours. Time slips away so fast. I can admit it, I get caught in those loops of scrolling and before I know it, I’ve watched every trending dance challenge, every cat video, which I’m totally not ashamed of honestly. Cats are just the best. I should adopt one. They’d be so therapeutic, right? Maybe I’d name it something quirky, like Sir Fluffington. Okay, but back to the study I’m supposed to be doing. I have this paper due soon and the topic is fascinating – something about societal norms and how they shape personal identity or whatever. I can totally see it. Like how people dress or talk, it fascinates me. What makes someone a hipster or a jock? How do people get put in boxes? I think everybody is more complex than their labels. Sometimes I catch myself wondering if I'm too nice to everyone. I hate conflict, and sometimes I feel like people take advantage of that. Like, I should stand up for myself more or say no occasionally instead of just agreeing all the time. I see it, but then the moment comes and I just want everyone to be happy, and then there’s me, the people pleaser, not wanting to rock the boat. The other day, my roommate and I were discussing relationships. I think I pick up on social cues really well, sometimes too well, which is probably because I’m always analyzing everything in my head. My friend's relationship is rocky right now, and I’m not sure how to help her without overstepping. I want to be there for her but also respect her space because sometimes it’s hard to know how someone wants to be supported. Speaking of support, I’ve been collaborating with this new friend I met in class. She’s so open-minded and we get along like we’ve known each other forever. It’s like an instant connection. We were working on this project and we had so many ideas that the whole thing turned into a brainstorming session rather than what we were supposed to be doing, and I loved every minute of it. We joked about creating an alternate reality where we were professional idea generators, just bouncing crazy thoughts back and forth. Anyway, I hope there’s some structure to this stream of consciousness because I feel like I’m all over the place, but that’s just me right? My brain works like a million tabs open at once, all interesting but none of them really getting any attention. I need to start organizing my thoughts – maybe a bullet journal or something. I love the idea of color coding things. Creative but also organized. I can be that person. Oh! And winter is coming, which has me excited and anxious at the same time. I want to go ice skating, drink hot chocolate, but also the holidays. They’ll be busy, and busy means lots of socializing. But I love holiday parties! I hope my friends throw one. See, this is chaos and yet, somehow, it makes sense in my head.

So I just got a message from Jenna and she wants to hang out tonight. I wonder if I should suggest a new place? That taco place with the crazy hot salsa? I bet she’ll love it. I love meeting new people. You never know who you’ll meet. It’s like every conversation can lead to a new adventure. Speaking of adventures, I remember that time we went to that random concert and ended up backstage. How did we even get in there? Oh right, I was talking to that guy who was a friend of a friend or something. I should really keep track of my connections better but it’s just too much. I get overwhelmed trying to organize everything in my head. I wish I was more like those people who just have it all together. I mean, they walk in with their planners and color-coded sticky notes and I’m just like, “What am I doing? Where’s my phone?” Like yesterday, I completely forgot about that group project due next week. I was so caught up in planning this weekend’s party. Honestly, I probably shouldn’t even be doing that right now, but it’s just so much more fun than studying. I get all these ideas and want to try everything! All those little decorations I bought are piled in the corner. Balloons, streamers. I should probably learn to have a little more structure but it just feels boring. What’s the point of life if I can’t make it colorful? I get anxious though, thinking about the mess and what people will think if it’s not "perfect." Ugh, I hate that feeling, but at the same time, I just want to invite everyone and have fun. Okay back to Jenna—who else should I invite? Mike? Oh, but he’s been in a mood lately. I don’t want anyone bringing down the vibe, seriously. It’s all about the good energy! Sometimes I feel like I’m holding too much weight. I need to stop worrying about what everyone thinks but it’s hard. You know? I want everyone to be happy, and if they aren’t, I feel like it’s my fault. I had a friend once who told me that I’m a “people pleaser.” That was something to think about. Do I need to please everyone? I guess I just don’t want anyone feeling bad or lonely. Back to Jenna, should I tell her about my crazy idea for a themed outfit for the party? Something wild like neon or maybe everybody shows up as their favorite movie character? That could totally work! I should text her! But then I’m like, what if they don’t want to do that? I overthink everything, ugh! Why can’t I just be chill about it? I wish I could breathe and just suggest things without feeling like I’m gonna unravel. It’s like a tangled ball of yarn in my head. I’m always thinking about possibilities, but then they slip away as fast as they come. And then there’s that one girl in class who always seems so put together. I can’t help but compare myself to her. She’s so confident, answers every question perfectly, and I’m just over here second-guessing myself. I mean, aren’t we all just faking it anyway? I bet she has moments where she panics inside too. Maybe she’s just better at hiding it? But I don’t know, it’s exhausting sometimes. I just wish I could talk to her without feeling weird. Is it weird? What if I just walked up to her and said “Hey, I think you’re cool, wanna grab coffee?” But I hate how my heart races just thinking about it. Ugh, anxiety is the worst. I need to calm down! It’s all in my head, right? Maybe I need to go for a walk, clear my thoughts. Or maybe a dance party in my living room? That sounds perfect! Just blast some music and let loose. Nothing better than dancing when everything feels heavy. And anyway, when am I going to be able to go on that spontaneous road trip? Driving with friends, just seeing where the road takes us. That sounds amazing. How do I convince everyone? Gas is getting more expensive, right? But you can’t put a price on fun! I wonder if I could sell some old clothes or something. I have so much stuff I don’t even wear. I mean, I’ll always keep my favorite pieces but I should stop hoarding things just because I think I might need them someday. Okay, wow. I’ve totally lost track of time. What was I even thinking about? Oh right, party planning, Jenna, spur-of-the-moment adventures. Life is so messy and chaotic but also so beautiful and full of potential. I just need to keep reminding myself to breathe, let it flow. Maybe that’s how I should approach everything, like a stream—just let it go.

It’s 3:14 PM and I’m staring at this blank space. Why is it so hard to get started? My thoughts are all over the place. I can hear someone outside playing music, the kind that makes me want to close the window. I don’t want to hear it—oh, great, now it’s catchy. I used to hate that feeling like a song is invading my brain, but sometimes it sticks. Can't focus when there’s noise. I remember a time in high school when I tried to block out sounds. I put on headphones and listened to classical, it worked for a bit, but then I started thinking about how it felt to drown in that kind of silence. What even is silence? My mind races through it all. Always thinking. Analyzing. What if I could capture the thoughts on paper but they slip through my fingers like sand? So frustrating. I get stuck in loops of overthinking, and it’s exhausting. What if this essay doesn’t turn out well? What if people think I’m dumb? Can’t I just get one thing right? I scrolled through social media this morning, and what do I feel? Jealousy, frustration, loneliness. Everyone seems to have their lives together, but I can’t even keep my room clean. I spent the afternoon just looking at the same pile of clothes on the floor. It’s just sitting there, judging me. I should probably do something about that, but I can’t get motivated. The world feels chaotic when I can’t even get my space organized. It’s like a reflection of my brain, a jumbled mess that won’t come together. Oh, and the people around me. They’re social, they thrive on interaction, and I just feel drained. I’d rather talk to a book. But then again, people are interesting creatures. I sometimes wonder if they think about their impact on others. I can’t help but analyze their every move. My friends say I’m too critical, but what’s wrong with analyzing? Someone has to do it! Everyone seems to just go along with things. Maybe they’re looking for acceptance. I don’t know. So many layers to people. It’s fascinating but exhausting. What was I saying? Right, I remembered how my roommate keeps suggesting we go out more. But each time, I feel that anxiety bubbling up. What if I embarrass myself? What if I just sit there and stare at my drink? I sometimes fantasize about being more spontaneous. I wonder if I could be different. I love the idea of traveling, exploring, doing new things, but then it hits me—there’s a weight on my chest. What if I lose my wallet? What if my phone dies? I don’t want to be lost in a strange place. Sometimes I just want to tell people, “Stop trying to change me.” It's like they think being introverted is a problem instead of a personality trait. They push too hard, and I feel myself bracing against it. My friends joke about it, telling me to live a little, but I can only take so much. I think they worry I’m too serious. Maybe I am. This pressure to conform, it’s maddening. I wish they understood. I’m different, but that’s okay! It frustrates me. I want to be open to experiences, but the thought of putting myself out there sends me into a spiral. I remember this one time I signed up for a weekend workshop about creative writing. I thought it would be awesome to meet like-minded people, and it was, until I got there. The energy was overwhelming. Everyone was laughing and exchanging ideas, while I sat in the corner, scribbling my thoughts. My brain was a whirlwind of emotions, excitement, insecurity. It’s hard to really connect when I feel so exposed. Writing is easier—there’s distance. But hearing their voices, feeling their energy? It’s a different beast entirely. Yet there’s beauty in it. People and their stories. I love reading about different experiences, and I’ve found a comfort in books. They don’t judge, don’t push. I can dive into another world. I need that escape sometimes, it calms the storm inside me. I keep reminders everywhere, quotes on sticky notes, like little anchors to reel me back in when I get lost in thought. I wish I could carry that mindset outside of my room. How do people do it? Maybe they don’t even think about it. How liberating. I can’t help but wonder about my future. Will I ever become more open? Will I find a way to balance all these messy thoughts and emotions? Or will I always feel like an outsider looking in? My mind is a constant tug-of-war. I look at the world and want to embrace it, but my instincts pull me back. A dance of wanting and fearing. The clock keeps ticking, and I’m still here, flinging thoughts like confetti, waiting for the right moment to make sense of it all.

Okay so I was thinking about how people just jump into conversations like it’s nothing, like how do they do that, like if I tried it would probably come out all jumbled or awkward, and then I’d just want to disappear, which is probably why I sit at the edges of rooms or at least not in the thick of things, it’s exhausting, social energy feels like this huge wave, and I just stand there watching it crash, water splashing everywhere and I’m like I don’t want to get soaked but then I overthink it and it feels worse, and like that one time at the party last month where I awkwardly sipped on my drink while hearing people laugh and share stories about wild adventures, and my mind was racing because I wanted to say something interesting but what if it’s not interesting, or what if they don’t care, but they all seemed like they were having so much fun with each other and I was just kind of there, like a piece of furniture. And then there’s the constant tension that I feel, like I’m on a tightrope all the time, balancing between wanting to express myself and shut myself off. What if they knew how much I overthink everything, like who would want to be friends with someone who analyzes every word that comes out of their mouth? Sometimes I think about all the stuff I could do if I just let go. I love exploring new ideas, different thoughts, but it’s like I’m stuck in this bubble that I can't break free from, like I see all the colors outside and want to jump out, but the bubble keeps pushing me back, making me second guess everything. I mean I can get lost in a book or lose track of time listening to music, and the world just morphs into this other realm, but the second it’s time to interact with real people, I feel like I’m a cardboard cutout of myself standing there. And then there’s all this chaos in my head. One minute I’m planning out a whole life strategy, like I’m gonna change everything, then suddenly I’m overwhelmed by something small, like a conversation I had last week, replaying it over and over, wondering if I sounded strange or too blunt. Why do I say things like that, like my brain works like one of those old cassette players—rewind, fast forward, play, but never really stopping. It’s like there’s this buzzing inside that just won’t quiet down. I wish I could just hit pause and sit in silence, but the silence feels too loud. Oh, and deadlines, don’t even get me started. I know I’m supposed to be responsible and get my stuff done, like, on time, but the actual act of doing it gives me this wave of anxiety. Sometimes I think about how nice it would be to just not care about the deadlines, to just float through life without worrying about grades or assignments. But then I think, what if that makes everything worse? Like I can’t not care because if I do, I’ll just spiral into madness. I’ve seen it happen to people, and it terrifies me. It’s funny, though. I see people being so nice, and I’ll just think, why are they like that? Sometimes it feels insincere, right? Like they’re just trying to please everyone, and I’m over here just trying to hold it together without losing my mind. I guess that’s why I prefer a smaller group of friends, where I can be myself without the pressure to conform or fit in with everyone else. But even then, I feel like I hold back too much, like they don’t really know me, like I’m hiding these layers of thoughts and emotions that they’d just never understand. Sometimes I wish I could just be more open like, “Hey, here’s my brain—take a look,” because there are so many new ideas I want to explore, like what if I could just travel to places that are completely different? I’ve been reading about cultures and lifestyles that are nothing like my own, and it’s exhilarating to think about experiencing it all. But then I think, what if I can’t adapt? What if I end up being the weird one again? I don’t know, I guess this constant push and pull between my head and reality makes life feel either super vibrant or way too overwhelming. It’s like being in a room filled with colorful paintings but all I can focus on are the crooked frames. It’s maddening but also kind of beautiful, you know? I’m just a hesitant explorer in a world that feels set on moving without me.

I can’t believe I forgot to bring my water bottle again. I swear I need a checklist for everything. Like, I make lists for lists and then I lose the lists. It’s just so typical of me, but whatever, it’s fine, I’ll survive. I was thinking about the group project due next week. I really like the way everyone pitches in their ideas. Nobody gets left out, and we all vibe off each other’s energy. It’s a good team, I mean, everyone’s just so agreeable. I can’t stand working with people who don’t collaborate. It’s like, come on, just be nice and share your thoughts. I thrive in those environments where everyone feels comfortable. It boosts my motivation and I can feel the creativity flowing. Oh, and the weekend is coming up. I can’t wait to hang out with friends, probably go to that new pizza place that opened downtown. Everyone’s raving about how great the crust is. I need to try it. I like to keep my weekends filled with social activities. I’ve got this party I’m headed to Saturday night too. I love meeting new people and just chatting. It’s my favorite part of being with a crowd. At parties, you can just float around from group to group, it’s like being a social butterfly. I just don’t get anxious, you know? Some people get all nervous about mingling. I just walk up and ask someone about their favorite movie or something. It works every time. I mean, who doesn't love talking about movies? Speaking of which, I really need to watch that new series everyone’s talking about. I think it’s on Netflix, but I haven’t had the time to sit down and binge it. Maybe after I finish this essay I’ll take a break and watch an episode or two. It’s so important to take breaks, right? I can’t concentrate for too long without switching things up. My mind just starts wandering if I stare at the same thing for hours. I guess that’s why I’m always in motion, looking for the next thing to do. It’s not that I lack focus, I just prefer variety. But in a way, I appreciate routine too. Like, I like waking up early, getting breakfast, and hitting the gym. I’ve got a pretty set routine that keeps me grounded. And then there’s the daily tasks, like cleaning my space. I actually enjoy organizing things. I find it calming, and it’s rewarding to see a tidy room. I can’t relax if my space is messy. I’ve seen those shows where they just dump everything on the floor and it gives me anxiety just to watch. I can’t imagine living like that. I need everything in its place. Having a clean space helps me think better. It’s all connected, I believe. Cluttered space, cluttered mind. I’ve also been thinking about volunteering more. I love helping out at the local shelter. It’s just so fulfilling to see the impact we can make. Plus, the people there are phenomenal. Everyone is so passionate about making a difference, and it’s contagious. I think that’s the thing that drives me – that collective energy of wanting to improve the community. I like contributing to something bigger than myself. Makes me feel good about my time. A win-win situation, really. What else is happening? Oh, I was planning on trying that new hiking trail with my friends. I like how active we can be outdoors. Being outside always lifts my mood. Plus, it’s a great way to bond and catch up. I love those long chats while walking, you get to know people so much better. That’s when the real conversations happen, not in the classroom or at parties. I always feel more connected when we’re just out in nature, away from distractions. I’m thinking about planning a little trip for the fall break. Nothing too extravagant, just a road trip to the mountains or the beach, something chill where we can just hang out and relax. I can do without the hustle and bustle of city life every now and then. I dream about just setting up a bonfire, roasting marshmallows, and sharing stories until the sun comes up. That sounds perfect, right? Simple pleasures. Anyway, I can’t wait to get to that pizza place. I hope they have good dessert options too. I’m all about dessert. Who doesn’t love dessert? It’s the best part of any meal. I mean, cake, pie, ice cream… the possibilities are endless. I guess I’m also a little traditional in that way. I like what I know. Like, I don’t experiment too much with food. I prefer sticking to the classics that I know I'll enjoy. I guess that’s what makes me comfortable. I really like to be comfortable, it’s safe and relaxing. Okay, I should wrap this up. Time’s ticking.

okay so I had this dream last night about meeting my favorite author like can you even imagine that feeling? I woke up buzzing like I had too much caffeine, heart racing like I actually met them but it was just a dream what a letdown but also kind of exciting right? dreams are weird like they can take you anywhere like one minute you’re in a café in Paris and the next you’re playing guitar on stage or trying to remember the words in front of a huge crowd and it feels real and terrifying and exhilarating at the same time I wish I could just bottle that feeling and carry it with me everywhere. got to thinking about how we’re just surrounded by people all the time like when I go to the dining hall it’s this constant hum of conversation, laughter, arguments over whose turn it is to do dishes, and it's like this beautiful chaotic symphony of student life but then there's that one guy who just cannot take a hint and keeps talking about his collection of vintage spoons like does anyone care about spoons? I mean good for him but sometimes you just want to eat in peace not hear about the history of culinary cutlery. still, I nod and smile because that's what you do right? being nice is key, you catch more flies with honey or whatever that saying is. but then it’s like when I’m hanging with my friends all that energy turns into something magical like we're planning a spontaneous road trip with no destination in mind just our playlists blasting and silly games to make the drive entertaining, I love how ideas just flow when we’re together like water from a fountain it's so refreshing, especially after sitting through lectures that feel like they last a century, and how do professors talk for that long without losing their breath? I mean, I could do a whole TED talk on just caffeine and why it should be its own food group, I think I’d be pretty convincing. I need to get organized this semester too or else I’m going to drown in assignments but then I think about how much I love planning stuff like schedules and color-coded calendars it’s like this satisfying puzzle I’m putting together, making sure every piece fits just right, meeting deadlines, and checking things off my list it feels like a superpower but also sometimes I get overwhelmed by it all like can I really keep this up? what if I don’t have enough time for fun and spontaneous plans? have to remind myself it’s okay to just breathe but I get so anxious about missing out on opportunities like, what if I don’t go to that party and something epic happens, or worse, what if nobody misses me? social media is a minefield, like I love sharing moments but then you see everyone’s highlight reels and it makes your own life feel so mundane like, I went to the grocery store today and felt like a superhero for finding avocados on sale that’s not a cute Instagram story like “look at my boring adult life and how I save a dollar!” but then I think about the people who love me and know that my avocado victory is worth celebrating, it’s all about perspective. I want to experience everything, so many places to see, cultures to dive into, people to meet, and how do you even begin to choose what to prioritize? studying abroad sounds amazing but also terrifying like I could totally be the person who dishes out travel advice someday or just a complete failure and there’s such a fine line between ambition and insanity, right? but I thrive on that energy, the challenge and uncertainty make it all so thrilling like trying new foods that you can't pronounce or dancing at parties where everyone has their own moves and you just jump in no fear, no shame it’s such a rush. then I think about getting older, will I still feel this fire in my belly or will it fizzle out into this bland routine of work and responsibilities? I hope I hold onto this part of me that craves adventure and connection, it's so easy to get lost in the grind of life and forget to live but I’m determined to make friendships that last and memories that sparkle just so long as I don't become that person who talks incessantly about their boring job, like please no one wants to hear about my spreadsheets or deadlines. so here’s to the moments of chaos and joy, the laughter and unexpected friendships, the way we all come together in this messy beautiful life. I want to soak it all in, the highs and lows because it’s all part of this wild ride. We’re all just trying to make sense of it, aren’t we?

Okay so I just remembered that time at that party last month, the one with the glow sticks and the pizza, and how everyone just kind of flowed in and out of conversations like it was totally normal, I wasn’t even worried about fitting in because everyone was just so friendly. I love that about people, how we can just connect over the silliest things, like how someone’s cat decided to sit in their lap the whole night or how someone brought veggie chips instead of regular ones, and I was like, why?! But then we laughed about it and they said they thought they were healthier, and it just turned into this whole debate about snacks, you know? I could talk about food forever, like really, who even knew kale could be fun to eat in a smoothie of all things, but then again, does it really need to be fun? I mean, just eat it, right? But it’s crazy how food connects people, like this greasy slice of pizza could bring a whole group together just over toppings, debate whether pineapple belongs on pizza, I say yes but only if you’re in the mood because why not just have fun with it, taste is subjective, right? Speaking of taste, I need to get some new music, I’ve been replaying the same songs for ages, maybe I should just dive into something totally different, like that indie scene I’ve heard about, so many people are discovering artists that never would’ve made it mainstream but are just genius in their own right, like it’s cool how we can find beauty in the obscure, music is such a vibe. Oh and then there’s that concert coming up next month, I really hope everyone can go, it’ll be such a blast, just the thought of being surrounded by people singing along to lyrics that mean something to us, it’s electrifying. The energy at concerts is just so contagious, it’s like everyone’s heartbeats sync up, I could never get tired of that. Randomly thinking about how my major is kind of all over the place too, like I’m really into psychology but also want to dabble in sociology and maybe even cultural studies, it’s just all so fascinating how people interact and create communities, I really want to travel and see other cultures, learn the nuances, the little things that make a community tick, I don’t get how some people can just settle and not want to explore the world, there’s so much out there! I mean, I’ve got friends who haven’t left the state since college started, and I’m like, how can you do that?! Like there’s this whole big exciting world to discover, from those tiny streets in foreign cities to the vast landscapes of national parks, each place with its own stories to tell, oh and the food, don’t get me started on food from around the world, someone suggested I try making dumplings from scratch and I’m like yes please, let’s do it. I love the idea of cooking with friends too, just laughing while flour is flying everywhere, maybe having a glass of wine or whatever, it’s just the act of being together, it’s the highlight of any day. Sometimes, I think I get a little too distracted with all these ideas, like I’ll start one thing and then my mind skips to another, gotta finish things, but it’s hard when there’s just so much excitement everywhere, it’s kind of overwhelming in the best way possible, if I had it my way I’d just gather a bunch of friends every weekend and we’d try something new, go hiking or hit up a new food truck or something, just mix it up and keep the energy alive, you know? I wonder how different life would be if everyone just embraced spontaneity a little more, like, what if we just picked a random city every month and took a road trip? Randomness can be thrilling, while some people cling to routines, I’d just rather be out discovering something new every chance I get, the thrill of the unknown is what keeps life spicy. There’s also that upcoming festival in the city, I should probably get tickets before they run out, but the thought of being surrounded by all those people, dancing and just hanging out is beyond exhilarating, I can already picture it: laughter, music, people in colorful outfits, totally vibing, it’s like a carnival but everyone’s so happy to be there and sharing moments, the atmosphere is so infectious. Do I sound like I’m daydreaming? Maybe, but isn’t that what life is about? Collecting experiences, memorable nights filled with people who share a little piece of their heart with you, that’s what makes it all worthwhile, living for those connections, it’s what I thrive on, it’s what motivates me to keep exploring and learning, so much to see, so much to laugh about, life is just a grand adventure when you think about it.

The clock says 3:12 PM and I can’t stop thinking about how my roommate will probably be back any second now. She gets so mad when I leave my stuff everywhere but I just can’t help it. My backpack is like a black hole. I mean, I put one thing in and ten things come out. I should clean it up, but who cares right now? There’s a party later. I need to get my outfit ready. What if I wear that blue top? The one that sparkles just enough to catch attention but not too much that it’s overbearing. Sparkly is good. I think I have the right jeans for it. I really want to feel good tonight, but I also hope I don’t get too anxious about everything. My heart races when I think about meeting new people and if I’ll have anything to say. What if nobody talks to me? Ugh, that’s stressful. I guess I could go with a friend, but what if they flake? Sometimes I just want to tag along solo. But then I think about how lonely that feels. You know? Being around people is the best but it’s also exhausting. I can totally picture myself laughing and dancing and then ten minutes later feeling like I just need to escape to the bathroom for a breather. Maybe I’ll find a spot on the couch to chill. Those are always good. I mean, it’s great to talk to everyone, but sometimes I just gotta recharge, especially when my brain gets all jumbled up with noise and voices. Oh, and I’ve gotta remember to text Sarah. She always goes all out and gets everyone hyped. I love her energy! Plus, I need to know if she’s wearing something cute. Like, no pressure, but I can’t be the only one showing up looking good. That would be so awkward. I want to match her vibe but also have my own flair, you know? Should I call her instead? Nah, texting is easier. I can just hit send and not deal with the back-and-forth chatter. What if I sound dumb? I always overthink it. But then I remember how easy it feels when we’re together. Okay, focus. I need to think about food. Can’t go to a party on an empty stomach, right? But then I’ll probably just snack on chips or something. I wish I was good at making appetizers. Everyone makes fun of me for my ramen noodle obsession. I mean, it’s quick and easy, but is it really the best option? I should probably try to whip up something more creative, but cooking just sounds like so much effort. Like, all those recipes online? I can get lost on that rabbit hole and forget what I was even looking for. Sometimes I think being able to cook well would be a cool skill, but then I remember how much I just want to have fun and be around people instead. Speaking of which, I really hope they play “that song” tonight. The one that just makes you want to dance and sing at the top of your lungs even if you sound bad. Everyone knows that song, right? I can’t remember who sings it, but it gives off such good vibes. It always gets the party started. I wonder if I’ll see Jake tonight. He’s so fun and I have this weird kind of crush on him but I don’t want to make things weird. We always joke around and it’s super hilarious but what if I say something cringey? I mean, what if I trip and fall in front of him? That would be embarrassing. Or what if he’s hanging out with someone else? Okay, stop overthinking. Just be chill. Also, I have this massive crush on spontaneous plans. Like I just love it when friends are like, “Let’s go here right now!” It makes me feel alive. But I should probably stop avoiding responsibilities. The whole school thing’s piling up with assignments and tests coming. I keep saying I’ll study later or tomorrow, but the days just fly by, and I’m like, “What am I even doing?” But also, my friends are my priority. They need my energy, my laughter. I can’t let them down. Okay, back to the party. I want to create good memories, feel the energy, and maybe have a bit of craziness. But ugh, it’s also nerve-wracking. What if there’s drama? Last time I was like caught in the middle, and it felt awful. I like keeping the peace, but sometimes I just want to jump in and scream, “Everybody chill!” But then I think about how it’s not my place and I can’t be that person. I guess I should just focus on having a good time and letting loose. Maybe just embrace the chaos. At least my friends will be around, and we can just laugh about everything after. That’s the best part.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how I need to organize my notes for that class I’m not even sure I like anymore. It’s a lot of pressure to keep up with everything. My friends seem to be thriving. They go out, make friends, post their happy lives all over social media, but I’m here worrying about whether I’ll finish my reading on time. It’s like they’re in a completely different world than I am. I wish I could just blend in, but instead, I feel like I’m the background character in a sitcom that nobody watches. What was I saying? Oh right, the notes. I can’t believe I procrastinated on that assignment. Why do I always do that? I had the whole week to work on it, but I just kept getting distracted by little things like scrolling through a million memes or re-organizing my desk. My desk. It’s so neat, everything has its place. It’s comforting but also kind of stifling, just like my routine. I don’t want to be rigid but I guess I feel like it helps me keep my anxiety in check but then sometimes I wonder if it makes me more anxious because I feel trapped in the same patterns. Speaking of anxiety, I had this awful dream the other night. I was in a presentation and I forgot everything I was supposed to say. The professor was staring at me, waiting for something brilliant to come out of my mouth. I wish I could’ve just said, “Hey, I’m doing my best. Just give me some time to think.” But instead, I woke up in a panic. My heart racing, feeling like I’d failed something important. I keep telling myself it’s just a dream, but I can’t shake that feeling. It lingers. Sometimes I think about how much easier it would be if I was just more extroverted, you know? People tell me it’s great to be social and outgoing, but I can’t help but feel overwhelmed when I’m around a lot of people. I mean, I can be friendly, and I try to be nice to everyone, but there’s this internal dialogue screaming for me to escape the crowd and find some quiet. The noise, oh god, it can be unbearable. And the small talk, don’t even get me started. I feel like I’m rehearsing the same conversation over and over, all these social interactions like a script I didn’t want to audition for in the first place. But then I feel bad because I want to be supportive, too. My friends come to me for advice, and I love being there for them. I really do. Helping them makes me feel useful, and that’s comforting. It gives me a sense of purpose, even if it doesn’t feel like I’m living my own life to the fullest. There’s this push and pull inside me—wanting to be a good friend and wanting to hide under my blankets in the dark. It’s exhausting. I even notice how it affects my time management. I hate being late; it gives me such anxiety. Like, what if the professor thinks I’m slacking off? Or worse, what if my friends think I don’t care? So I’ll be ten minutes early instead of just on time. I mean, I’ve literally sat in the library for an hour before class just because I thought it would be better to wait than to risk showing up late. Is that weird? Probably, but it feels safer. Then there’s that nagging feeling that I’m missing out on things. I see everyone hanging out, talking about adventures they had over the weekend, and I’m sitting there wondering if I should’ve pushed myself to go. But the thought of being in a loud, crowded space gives me hives. I think, maybe next time I’ll try. But then next time comes and the cycle starts again. I can’t shake it. I remember hearing once that if you're agreeable, it means you'll often put others' needs before your own. I guess that’s true. I’m always trying to make sure everyone is happy, even if it means I’m uncomfortable. It sometimes feels like I’m sacrificing my own happiness for theirs. I wish I could just tell someone how I really feel without worrying about offending them or letting them down. I keep thinking how nice it would be just to have a big, honest conversation about it all, but then my brain jumps to “no, that's a terrible idea.” Okay, focus. I need to get back to the notes. It’s like my brain is running in circles. I should probably just block out time and stick to a schedule, but then I have to think about how many pages I need to read and what if it’s too much? I can’t handle overwhelming. Just breathe. One thing at a time. Why is that so hard to remember?

It's weird how people expect so much from you just because you’re around. I prefer to be alone, honestly. Like, there's a certain peace to it. I organize my notebooks by subject and date, color code them. Seems necessary. I remember when I was a kid, I hated when my mom would make me go to playdates. I'd always sit in the corner, making up stories in my head while everyone else played with dolls or whatever. I had a collection of action figures lined up in perfect order, labeled and everything. Authority was comforting to me, not the chaos of random interactions. Predictability is key. Why don’t people get that? Today in class, someone asked if they could borrow my notes and I almost said no. I mean, I spend hours organizing my notes. Why would I just hand them over to someone who doesn’t care about their own? But I just shrugged and said, “Sure.” Maybe I should have said no, established some kind of boundary, but that feels so confrontational. I don’t like confrontation. I’d rather just avoid it. Speaking of boundaries, social media is a whole minefield. Sometimes I think about deleting my accounts. Just vanishing. But then I remember how useful it is for keeping track of assignments and group projects, not that I'm fond of group projects. Why can’t people just stick to the task? I hate it when they want to chit-chat instead of working. Time wasted. Just focus. I guess I’m the one who ends up doing most of the work anyway. Wouldn’t people appreciate that more? They say I’m too serious, but isn’t it just about getting it done? Efficiency seems underrated. And then I get odd looks in class when I raise my hand to correct something the professor said. It’s like, am I not allowed to point out factual inaccuracies? At least I’m paying attention, but then people look at me sideways like I’m a know-it-all or something. Just trying to contribute and keep things straight, you know? I get it, it’s annoying, but there’s a line between being friendly and being lazy. Then there’s this whole discussion about mental health lately—it’s everywhere. People talking about self-care like it’s a magic solution. But what if self-care is just a distraction from facing the hard stuff, like actually putting the work in? I mean, there’s a balance, right? A balance I try to maintain. I keep my schedule tight; wake up, go to class, hit the library, come back home and read or write. Rinse and repeat. It feels productive. Sometimes I think of what it would be like to just quit everything. Just stop stressing over grades or job prospects, but then I remember that’s not practical. My parents would freak if I dropped out, and I want them to be proud of me. They always pushed education as the number one priority, and I feel that pressure. Instilled in me like an algorithm. Okay, I just noticed I’m getting ahead of myself here. Sometimes I lose track of what I’m even talking about. I forgot to eat lunch today. Didn’t even feel hungry. Just kept working away at my research paper. I set this rule for myself: no distractions when I’m writing. No music, no snacks, just pure concentration. But then I found myself staring at the screen for too long. Need to remember to take breaks. It’s funny how sometimes I think people see me as cold or distant, but it’s not that. I just… I just don’t see the point in beating around the bush. If I want to say something, I’ll say it directly. Like, my roommate kept asking me what was wrong when I just wanted to sit in silence. “Nothing’s wrong,” I said. “I just don’t feel like talking.” But she didn’t get that. Why is that hard for people to grasp? And then there’s that random encounter with the kid from my class. I don’t even know his name, but he keeps asking me to join his study group. Why? I can’t figure out if he’s actually interested in studying or if he’s just being friendly. If it’s friendly, I can’t make sense of it. Just be efficient, right? Why does socializing have to be a whole additional task? I’ll stick to my books thanks. It’s not that I dislike people. Not at all. I just prefer fewer of them around. The ones I do like, I keep close and don’t mind investing time in. But socializing beyond that feels so draining, like sapping my energy. I just won’t entertain it. I’m fine with my routines, with my carefully planned days. I like the structure. When did it all become about being bubbly and spontaneous? It’s exhausting. Normal is just... it’s what it is. I can manage that. Just give me a good book and a quiet space. That's all I need.

I can’t believe it’s already Wednesday. Where did the week even go? I had so many plans to get ahead on my assignments but I just kept getting distracted by my friends texting me. They always know how to pull me in for spontaneous plans. It’s like, I tell myself I need to study and then I see a message pop up and BAM! I’m suddenly convinced that going out for tacos is absolutely essential to my well-being. Plus, I love tacos. How can you not love tacos? But then I come home and I’m just freaking out about that deadline that’s creeping up on me. I should have started the project last week but there were too many parties and I had to be there! Socializing is like oxygen. I can’t breathe without it. It’s so exhausting sometimes keeping up with everyone, but I thrive on the energy of friends around me. Ugh, and then there’s this feeling in my stomach every time I think about that paper. I mean, I want to do well. I really do. I care about what people think, especially my professors. I want them to see me as someone who has their life together and isn’t just a mess of last-minute studying and energy drinks. It's hard to shake off that anxiety. My palms sweat just thinking about getting that paper back. What if it’s not what they wanted? What if I totally missed the point? I can hardly think straight sometimes and that makes it worse. It would be nice to just not care sometimes, just do whatever, but I’m not wired that way. I can’t imagine blowing it off entirely, though. What if my GPA plummets? No, no, stop. Breathe. Maybe I should hit up Jenna later for some moral support. She’s in the same class and we can do group study sessions. She’s so chill and really understands me. I feel like I can go to her for anything. She knows how to make me laugh too, which is always a plus when I’m feeling anxious. I can just picture us at the library, trying to focus while also cracking jokes about the professors. That totally helps ease the pressure. If I’m with someone I can trust, I can manage my stress a little better. But at the same time, I feel bad relying on her too much because I don’t want to drag her down. I hope she knows I appreciate her being there for me. Friends are everything. Okay, so what else? Oh right, I need to figure out who’s going out this weekend. I heard there’s a party at Mark’s place and I can’t miss that! I don’t understand how people can say no to fun. Like, isn’t that what life is about? Connecting, laughing, dancing? I need that energy boost. Sometimes I can just feel the stress melting away when I’m out on the dance floor, surrounded by friends. But seriously, sometimes I wish I could just chill at home and play video games but I feel guilty about it. It’s like I’m supposed to be living my best life by going all out all the time. I wonder if I’ll ever learn to balance things. I mean, I’ve been saying I’ll start a planner, organize my week better but I never stick to it. I bought one last month, and it’s sitting on my desk — empty. It’s so ironic. I spend hours being social but can’t seem to manage my own time. The irony is painful but honestly, I don’t want to think about it. Who wants to think about growth and responsibility? Not me! I just want good times and laughter. How am I supposed to prioritize a stupid planner over a good taco night? And what about the new movie coming out? I’m definitely going to see it this weekend if I can convince my friends to go with me. I have to see it with a crowd. The energy, the excitement, the laughs, it’s all part of the experience. Alone? No thanks. I thrive off the reactions around me. It makes everything feel like a party. But what if no one wants to go? Do I go alone? Ugh, I’d hate that. I think I’d just end up scrolling through my phone the whole time, feeling weird and out of place. Okay, deep breath. I should probably jot down some ideas for that paper after all. Just some brainstorming. That can’t hurt, right? I guess it’s better to feel stressed about doing something than getting overwhelmed because I didn’t do anything. I hope my friends are around later, though. I just need that boost, but right now, the thought of diving into my notes is a little less terrifying than I thought. Just take it step by step, yeah? I can do this!

Right, so I’m thinking about my weekend plans, which are always a bit too packed, but I love it. There's this party on Saturday night at Jamie’s place, they do the best playlists. I’ve already asked who’s bringing what – I think I’m on snack duty. Definitely chips and dip, that’s a classic. It’ll be good to see everyone, it feels like ages since we were all together. I just hope that weird tension from the last gathering doesn’t come back. You know, the one with Sarah and Mike? Awkward. But whatever, it’s all in the past. I like to keep things light and fun. I can’t deal with drama, not my thing. Picking up snacks is fun because they’re easy to choose. I prefer grocery shopping alone, I can speed through the aisles, don’t need to compromise on what to grab. Sometimes I grab those mini pretzel bags too because they disappear in seconds. I'm not a fan of picky eaters; I get that everyone has their preferences, but come on, there’s gotta be something for everyone. Maybe I should just go for a veggie platter? But then there’s always that one person who complains it's not enough. Forget it, chips and dip it is. You know what’s surprising? How easy it is to plan something with a group of friends. Everyone is pretty agreeable, which makes a world of difference. I mean, I can’t even remember the last time we had a serious disagreement about something. We work together, pick a date, throw ideas around, and boom, plans made. I love it. Everyone’s so chill about it. Sometimes I think if I were hanging out with a bunch of people who were difficult or argumentative, I’d just want to go home. I'm not interested in negativity, I just want to have a good time. Speaking of good times, I’ve been thinking about trying out this escape room thing next month. I heard it can be intense, but I’m more excited than nervous. I think I can handle the pressure easily. If there’s one thing I can do, it's rally people. We’ll whip through the puzzles like pros. Honestly, I thrive in those energetic, fast-paced situations. Just give me a challenge, and I’ll take it on. I guess that’s the conscientiousness kicking in; I like tasks. I'm a little obsessed with finishing what I start. Once I grab hold of a project, you better believe I’m seeing it through to the end. And let’s talk about the people in my life. I really appreciate how everyone seems so friendly and easy-going. I’m big on helping out when I can. Like, last week I helped my roommate move into her new place, and it felt good, I mean nothing super complex, just hauling boxes and cracking jokes. We had music blasting and it didn't feel like a chore at all. Gotta keep the spirits high while working, that's the trick. The sense of community is strong among my friends— we’ve all got each other’s backs when it matters. No one’s left hanging. It makes everything feel so much more fulfilling, you know? I’ve noticed lately, though, that I tend not to dig deep into things. I stick to what I know. Not a big explorer of new ideas or concepts. It’s fine; I’m not really feeling the need to stretch beyond my comfort zone. There's comfort in routine. I’m good with my friends, the activities we do, and the conversations we have. I don’t feel the urge to challenge myself too much. I mean sure, I read the same genres over and over. I’m a sucker for a good mystery or a lighthearted romance. I see my friends diving into sci-fi and experimental novels, but I just can't bring myself to do it. I guess some would say I play it safe, but that’s just my style. I think that’s perfectly fine. I’m not going to pretend to like something I don’t. I can appreciate the skills of someone who dives into the unfamiliar, but me? I find my comfort in familiar waters. Maybe that makes me boring to some people, but honestly? I’d rather enjoy my time than push myself to try something that might be a flop. Anyway, I should probably wrap this up before I get too distracted by the next thing on my mind— what am I going to wear to that party? Something cute but comfortable, I want to be able to dance. If the music is as good as I remember, we’ll be moving all night long. So many things to plan, but at the end of the day, all I want is for everyone to have a blast together. Seriously, what’s better than a night filled with friends, good food, and great vibes?

Okay so, I’m thinking about how my friends are always planning these spontaneous trips and I just love that energy. Like last weekend—remember that? We decided last minute to drive to the beach. Packed snacks, blasting music, everyone laughing, and honestly it felt like a scene from a movie or something. I could feel the excitement bubbling up inside, and it was like we forgot about everything else. Just the ocean and the sky meeting at that perfect horizon where the colors blended into this beautiful pastel. I think I thrive in those moments of adventure, like I need that rush of being outside and with people, connecting and feeling alive, you know? And the way everyone brought something different to the experience, from beach games to playlists—it was just so vibrant. I also remember last semester when we had that group project. Everyone was like, “ugh, group projects,” but I was excited. I mean, it’s a chance to work with others and brainstorm ideas, us bouncing thoughts around like it’s a tennis match. I love collaborating. I tend to take charge, though not in a way that feels bossy, more like rallying the troops! Everyone just seemed to vibe well. By the end, we had a masterpiece, and I remember how everyone was hyped about it. We literally high-fived when we submitted it. I thrive on that energy of togetherness. Speaking of teams, I really enjoy when we meet at my place for game nights. I’m always making sure everyone has snacks and drinks, I can’t stand when people feel uncomfortable. It’s like, why not make it a cozy space? The laughter, the yelling over the board games, and the way we always end up talking about the most random stuff in between, like who’s the best character in that show we all binge-watched or some crazy conspiracy theory. I’m all for those tangents because that’s where the real connections happen. Also, sometimes I feel pressure to do something really exciting with my life, like if I’m not travelling or doing something extraordinary, am I even doing enough? I see people on social media and it’s like, “Oh here’s me hiking Mount whatever, here’s me at some festival,” and I think wow I need to get out there more. But then I remind myself it’s not about that. It's about the fun times, big or small. Last week, I had a blast just going to a local art fair with friends. It’s so cool how everyone’s so passionate about their crafts and you can almost feel the creativity in the air. I love how artists express their emotions and ideas. I think being open to trying new things, whether it's food, art, or even just viewpoints, keeps life interesting. And then there’s those moments when I have deep conversations with close friends. You know those nights when we end up talking until 3 AM about our dreams, fears, what we want to do after graduation? That’s when I feel truly connected. I remember one night specifically, where we ended up discussing mental health and how we handle stress, which was kind of eye-opening. We all approach it so differently. I’m usually the optimist, giving motivational pep talks, but it was great to see how much we all care about each other’s well-being. I can’t help but think about the things I want to experience. I want to travel to different countries, learn new languages, embrace other cultures. Oh, did I say I’ve been getting into cooking recently? I made this Thai green curry last week, and wow! I mean, it wasn’t perfect but it tasted surprisingly good. I want to try making sushi next. There’s something about experimenting in the kitchen that’s like a mini adventure. And the way food brings people together? That’s the best part, sharing meals, exchanging stories. And then, then there’s random acts of kindness. I’m all about leaving little notes for strangers or paying for someone’s coffee. Kindness can spark so much joy, right? There’s this warm feeling knowing you could make someone’s day better. I just want to spread good vibes. Life is too short for negativity. Oh, and I’ve been trying to pick up some hobbies too. I picked up painting. I’m not great at it but I love just putting colors on a canvas and letting it flow. I think it’s important to just be free with creativity. There’s this urge inside me to explore every opportunity, and who cares if I mess up? I think that’s the beauty of life. Just diving in, meeting new people, being part of something bigger. I’d rather embrace the chaos than stay safe in my bubble. It feels good to just let thoughts pour out like this. I should do it more often.

Okay so I just thought of that time at the party last week when everyone was dancing and it was so loud but also exciting because it felt like we were all connected in some weird way just moving to the same beat like a giant organism or something. I love that feeling of being part of a group, it’s like being in a warm hug even when people are sweaty and bumping into each other. I remember the music was upbeat and then someone started doing the worm and everyone was cheering, and I couldn’t help but smile because it takes guts to do that in public. Anyway, I ended up doing a little shimmy myself because why not, right? Life is too short to worry about looking silly. It’s funny how bringing people together just makes everything feel lighter. I mean, you can have serious conversations or deep chats, but throwing in a little dance breaks the ice in a whole different way. Time doesn’t even matter in those moments. I lost track of how long we were out there, and it felt like nobody cared either. Then the snacks were gone and someone found a pack of chips in the cupboard and we all crowded around the kitchen like it was a treasure chest. Simple things like that—food, music, laughter—make everything feel vibrant and exciting. I love being around people who get that. There’s this comfort in community, like I can draw off their energy, and they can draw off mine too. It drives me nuts when people isolate themselves. It’s like, hello, you’re missing out on all this fun! How can anyone not want to be part of this? And the people at the party, they were all so nice. It struck me how well everyone was getting along, practically no drama, just joy. I found myself in a circle with some of my closest friends and new faces, and I caught a glimpse of how important it is to build these connections. Some people were talking about how they plan to travel and it made me think about how little I’ve explored the world. I want to go places, meet more people, but even in sharing those ideas, I felt a tug. Planning trips seems overwhelming when you think about all the logistics, but maybe a road trip with a group could be fun? Then we’d make memories and it’d be a shared experience, which is always better, I think. I hate the idea of being alone for too long. Like I get that solitude is important for some, but nah, not for me. I thrive when I’m surrounded by people. I feel the energy shift when I walk into a room and see smiles or hear laughter. It’s like I can absorb that energy, and it fuels me. When I’m in a crowd, I just want to connect, chat, and make everyone feel welcome. That’s partially why I’ve organized so many study groups and get-togethers—because I love bringing people together. It’s satisfying to see everyone getting along and helping each other out. And then, of course, when we hit that breakthrough moment in studying and it turns into a brainstorming session instead of a boring grind. But then there are times when I think about how sometimes people just go straight to their phones, and it’s like, why are we even here? Let’s talk! I get it, social media is a way to connect, but in-person interaction has a magic that scrolling just doesn’t capture. You can feel the vibe, the excitement, or maybe even the tension, if it’s there. With phones, there’s this barrier, and I just want to yank that away sometimes. I want to see everyone’s face light up when I tell a joke or hear them laugh at something silly, not just emoji reactions on a screen. I mean, do we forget how to be present? I constantly remind myself to keep the energy up when I plan events, like cooking something fun or having games ready to go. I love games! They break the ice, they get everyone involved. It’s all about participation; if people are having fun, they’re engaged and connected. I always think about how I’d feel if I were in their shoes—would I want to be included? But on the flip side, being organized can feel a bit stifling sometimes. I guess that’s where I have to be careful. The structure is good, it keeps things moving, but I don’t want to be so rigid that I forget to enjoy the moment. I just want everyone to feel included and happy, and that drives me. It’s important. Having a sense of belonging is everything, especially in a world that sometimes feels chaotic. Wow, that flew by. I could keep going forever, but for now, I think I've just shared a lot of what makes me tick.

I can’t believe I have to go to that group study thing later. I wonder if anyone else is going to show up. Probably just the same three people who don’t say much either. Why do I even bother signing up? I hate group projects. They’re so pointless. It’s always awkward. I’ll probably just sit there and let someone else do the work. I don’t have the energy to deal with others. And what if they want to talk? I don’t wanna talk. I mean, I can’t believe it’s already getting dark. It feels like the day zipped by. Or maybe I just lost track of time again, which wouldn’t surprise me. I feel like I’m always losing track of time, like it doesn’t even matter. Days blend together. What am I even doing with my life? Should I even be in school? Sometimes it feels like I'm just wasting my time. I should be doing something more productive, but what? I don’t even know. I freak out about everything. Everybody else seems so composed. I mean, they’re laughing, and I’m over here just anxious. Maybe I should just leave school. Get a job. Is that what I really want? The thought makes me panic. Then again, I can’t imagine sitting in an office every day. It sounds terrible. I’d rather be here, even with the stress of classes. But then I get all overwhelmed thinking about exams, deadlines, everything piling up. I wish I could just freeze time and stop worrying. Okay, calm down. Breathe. I keep hearing people say that. Just breathe. Keep breathing, but how? What does that even mean? I can’t focus on my breath when I’m so busy panicking about the future. I might fail at everything. I can't believe I got that low grade on that last quiz. Why can’t I remember anything? Everyone else manages to keep it together. They all seem to have a plan, and I don't even know what I want for dinner. And I keep getting emails about internships that I can’t even think about applying for because I’m not good enough. What if they ask me questions? What if I fumble through it? I just know I would. I guess I should probably start studying for that midterm. I know it’s coming up soon, but I just can’t bring myself to open the textbook. Ugh, textbooks. It’s so heavy. I hate carrying those things around. I can’t even handle lugging my bag around. Why did I choose this major? It’s so boring. I thought it would be interesting, but sometimes I feel like I should just give up. It’s not like I’m passionate about it or anything, just picked it randomly. Just like how I picked that elective last semester. What a waste. I hate electives. They make me feel more pressured to socialize, and I don’t want to. I want to just sit in my own bubble and make it quiet. Is that wrong? Probably. The world expects us to interact, right? But I’d rather read anything other than talking to people. I think I’d rather read the instruction manual for a toaster than make small talk. Everything feels pointless anyway. Should I go to that party everyone’s raving about? Probably not. I can’t even picture that scene. Everybody dancing, all those small groups chatting, it totally freaks me out. I hate it. The loud music blaring in my ears. I can’t figure out what to say. I'd just end up standing awkwardly in a corner like a weirdo. I know they think I just don’t care, but it’s not that. I care too much; I just can’t handle the whole noise, lights, people thing. It hits me too hard. Maybe I’ll just say I’m busy. What’s wrong with that? It’s not a lie. I’ve got plenty of other things to be stressing about. Like that paper due next week. Why did I wait so long to start? I should probably just brainstorm. Brainstorming sounds productive. But knowing me, I’ll get distracted by something random like scrolling through my phone or watching bad reality TV shows. I get so lost in them, but they take my mind off things, you know? All that drama, even though it makes me feel worse somehow. I guess it’s entertaining. Ugh, I need to stop ruminating. It’s just a waste of time. I should be writing down my thoughts instead. My brain feels like static. Peace sounds good right now. But peace doesn’t stick around when you’re my age, right? It’s always chaos in your head. Endless thoughts colliding. I can’t calm it down, so I’ll probably just keep typing until my fingers hurt, or I can’t think anymore. What if I just dropped everything and moved away? To some quiet place where no one knows me. But then I’d feel lost too, wouldn’t I? Would a place really change anything? I doubt it. It’s all internal anyway. I just can’t seem to escape myself.

I wonder if anyone else feels the weight of every little interaction like I do. It’s exhausting, honestly. I find myself replaying conversations in my head, nitpicking every word, every awkward pause. Do they think I’m weird? Probably. I was at that party last week. The one I almost didn’t go to. I mean, the thought of being surrounded by all those loud, extroverted people made my stomach churn. But I went. I don’t even know why. Maybe I thought I'd force myself to be social or maybe just to prove to myself that I could handle it. But really, I just stood in the corner, nursing a drink—what was it, a blueberry vodka soda?—and felt like a ghost. Everyone was laughing, telling stories. I can’t tell a story. Or I can, but I just don’t do it well. My mind races when I try to think of something funny or interesting to say. I remember trying to jump in once, and everyone turned to look at me. The pressure felt like a weight on my chest and I just blinked. So embarrassing. Then there’s the wanting to be understood. It’s like I have this vast world in my head, full of ideas, but it’s hard to share it. I read so much, and I keep thinking that maybe if I could articulate my thoughts the way authors do, maybe it would be easier. I have a million notebooks, filled with half-formed thoughts and sketches and quotes. I mean, half the time, I can’t even remember why I wrote something down. Like this one quote about fear that I found in that book about existential philosophy. It was brilliant, but then I thought, what does it even mean to me? It’s all just there. And then I find myself drifting back to Twitter and seeing everyone’s opinions. Everyone has a hot take or a witty comeback, and here I am just... existing quietly. Thoughts just swirl sometimes, like that one time I accidentally texted my professor instead of my friend. How did I even do that? I still feel my face flush when I think about how I apologized profusely, and he probably thought I was crazy. I overthink so much, and then I end up being super rigid about certain things. Like I’ve planned out assignments, color-coded them in my planner because that makes me feel more in control, even when life feels like it’s spiraling. It’s bizarre how that structure gives me comfort but then sometimes I’ll get so overwhelmed that I just can't pick up the pencil at all. It’s like a double-edged sword. I crave order but also crave adventure and spontaneity. I want to go hiking, visit new cities, see all this art, and yet, do I have the energy to deal with all the people? Maybe. Maybe not. Those art galleries are amazing though—the quietness, the beauty, the stories each piece tells. I once spent an entire afternoon staring at a painting. I could see layers of paint, feel the artist’s emotion in every stroke. It was almost spiritual. I connected with it more than I ever have with anyone. Why do some people get it and others just don’t? I mean, isn’t that what life should be about? Feeling deeply? But then maybe I feel too much. It gets…heavy. Like, I can’t watch sad movies without feeling this pit in my stomach for days. It’s too relatable or something. I can’t escape my own head. Then there’s the worry about the future. Everyone keeps asking me what I’m going to do after graduation, and I just want to slam my head against a wall. I have no clue! Am I supposed to know? Everyone else seems so certain. It’s all just work and life and bills, and I feel this pressure to conform. Sometimes I wish I could drop everything and go traveling, just take a backpack and disappear for a while. But then I think about all the chaotic unknowns that comes with that. What if I get lost? What if I run out of money? What if I don’t even like it? What if? I should probably talk to someone, a therapist, but I never do. I’ll just muddle through it because that’s what adults do, right? And yet, I still love new experiences. I guess that’s my openness showing. I want to try every new food, read every genre, learn languages. I took French last semester, and even though I struggled with it, I loved the challenge. There’s something exhilarating about stumbling through a conversation in a different language. It’s like a puzzle. But then, when I’m alone at home, those thoughts of “What am I even doing?” creep in. It’s a vicious cycle, overthinking and wanting to break free. I guess that’s why I’m typing all this out. To make sense of it all. Sort of.

Okay so I’m thinking about how I always end up at these parties and everyone’s like hey you should chill for a minute and I’m like why would I do that? Let’s go! Let’s talk! Let’s dance! But then I get this weird pit in my stomach about it and is that anxiety or just excitement? Who even knows. Just saw a TikTok that was all about how anxiety and excitement are essentially the same physiological response and I’m like can I just pick one? Like is my brain just having a party of its own inside my head and there’s confetti everywhere and I’m sweating but it’s not from dancing it's from this chaotic laugh-cry situation that never really ends. And sometimes I get so wrapped up in my head that I can’t even hear the music anymore, it’s just this background noise that fades until I’m 100 percent aware of my breathing instead. Why is it so hard to just chill out and enjoy it? But then I remember the last time I tried to calm down at a party I ended up sitting alone, scrolling through my phone, and that was worse, I felt like a total loser. I mean I enjoy being around people but when did everything get so complicated? Relationships are messy. Friends don’t text back in time and suddenly it’s all awkwardness. It’s like one misstep and the whole thing could fall apart. But then I love people, I love the drama! I love the chaos of emotions bouncing around, the stories, the laughter, the moments that make you realize life is not just mundane daily grind stuff. Like, I thrive on that stuff! But then it triggers my neuroticism and I’m bouncing off the walls with every little text that doesn’t come through. Like why does Sam always leave me on read? I’m not even that intense am I? Or maybe I am, but they said I’m fun to be around so surely that must count for something, right? Or maybe I misread the situation. Maybe I’m too much or too loud or too opinionated. It’s all so confusing but I love it. Then there’s the whole agreeableness thing. I’m just not willing to let things go. I want to argue about everything and I’m not afraid to take a stand. What’s the point if we all just nod along and say yes? That’s boring! How do you even know what someone thinks if no one’s willing to clash ideas? I went to this debate thing last week and man, was it invigorating. Everyone’s throwing ideas back and forth, each rebuttal increasing the intensity! It’s the moments you catch the eye of a teammate and you both know what you think and you’re ready to dive into the discussion. It was electric. And then there’s the flip side where I can’t let it go when someone disagrees with me. I’m constantly defending my position, even when maybe I should just let it slide. I argue about pizza toppings! Pineapple? Yes! No? Who cares? It's an endless loop of passionate stupid but I thrive on those exchanges. And being low on conscientiousness? Wow, that’s a rollercoaster. My life is a series of spontaneous decisions and flaking on plans. I sign up for everything! Then I remember I have five assignments due next week and do I even know which week it is? I have six tabs open in my head and I’m juggling half-finished tasks and I don’t even know how to prioritize because I’m too busy being open to all these adventures! But deadlines are real and I can’t just live my life like a runaway train, but then again that feels suffocating. I just want to paint and dance and travel! I want to go to places at midnight and talk to strangers and see where life takes me. But it’s this constant battle of wanting to be responsible and wanting to just chase every shiny thing. It’s a strange paradox. To feel so deeply yet not always be nice about it. I get intense about things, like why people want to be mediocre? Shouldn’t we be striving for the best? What does it even mean to be the best? Who decides that? Is it money? Is it fame? Or just a sense of fulfillment? It’s overwhelming. And then I’m somewhere between wanting to tell someone my ideas and feeling like a total burden. But the moment I find someone who wants to hear it, I’m off! Ranting about music, or art, or life. Okay, so that’s my brain, this constant whirlwind. Up, down, round and round. Every thought seems to connect yet separate in this big chaotic mess that is me. And the best part is? I wouldn’t have it any other way. I thrive on the unpredictability. Bring it on!
