Okay so I’m thinking about how the last time I hung out with friends I felt a bit out of place like they were talking about things that just don’t interest me. It’s not that I don’t like people but there’s a limit to small talk or maybe it’s just that I prefer deeper conversations, those about ideas, art, philosophy—anything that gets past the surface level. I can’t handle superficial stuff for long. I mean, how many times can you talk about the weather or the latest Netflix show? I’d rather read a book or think about the universe or the next big idea I want to explore. I really loved that novel I read last month, something about existentialism. It’s so wild how one person’s thoughts can spark a whole new way of seeing the world, and I can’t help but feel a little disconnected when everyone else is focused on what they binge-watched last weekend. Now I’m picturing my bookshelf—stacked with books I haven't read yet, taunting me really. This immense hallway of knowledge. It’s like a treasure chest waiting to unleash all these worlds and ideas and pieces of minds that are just waiting to be explored. I wonder if everyone else feels the same weight when they see unread books. Do they feel the pull of possibility? Or do they just shrug it off? I’m always drawn to those quirky little books, maybe because they push boundaries, create weird connections between disparate ideas. I mean why not? Why shouldn't a book about mushrooms go hand in hand with a book on psychology? All those connections waiting to be made, sometimes I feel like I can see them just out of reach. And speaking of connections, the thing with my friends is that sometimes they don’t get that I’d rather sit quietly in a coffee shop than hit the loud club scene. Clubs are just chaotic and overwhelming. I keep thinking about how I’d like to dissect conversations instead of participating in the noise. It's like a giant hamster wheel, just spinning and spinning, and I’m here on the sidelines wondering if anyone else is as exhausted as I am. They seem to thrive in those environments. Good for them. I admire their energy. Maybe I just recharge differently. That's fine but I can’t pretend to enjoy it, capering around with flashing lights and pounding music. So back to the study I was supposed to participate in... but I dunno. I signed up because I’m curious about what they’d measure but then I think—do I really need someone else analyzing me? It's so easy to slip into modes of thinking that get categorized. But what if the answers don't fit? Low extraversion, low neuroticism—that sounds pretty boring right? High openness, that makes sense, and high conscientiousness, I like order and structure, which makes sense why I’d obsess over deadlines or plans. But it's a double-edged sword because I sometimes worry I’m missing out on experiences because I'm being... responsible. But is it really responsible to chase every whimsical desire? Maybe it is if that's what feeds creativity. It’s all a balancing act, isn’t it? Remember the time I tried to plan a trip with friends and they kept suggesting places that seemed dull? I had this grand vision of visiting an art installation in a different city and they wanted to hit the same bars we always do. So frustrating. I guess that’s part of low agreeableness, right? I resist conformity. It’s hard to back down when your gut tells you something is wrong. So I put my foot down and, surprisingly they jumped on board, but I wonder how many times I pushed too hard? Sometimes I think my hobbies make me appear a bit strange. Like, why does someone my age spend weekends analyzing abstract art or roaming around art galleries? I think it has to do with the layers involved, there’s always something beneath the surface. Just like people, like friendships, it's rarely straightforward. I’ve always been fascinated by the complexity of thoughts and interactions. I guess that makes me a little more open-minded and curious. I don’t mind though; I’m okay being a bit of an enigma even to myself. I wonder when the next time I’ll be able to experience that thrill of discovery, the kind that fills you with energy and ideas. Is it odd to hope that a rainy day will prompt a new insight? Because I do. Rainy days have a magic to them. Plus, they give me the perfect excuse to stay in, read, write or just think deeply about the world. It's funny how solitude can feel like a collaboration with your mind.

Okay so where do I even start? I’ve got so many thoughts racing in my head, but they all feel jumbled, like a messy drawer of random things like old receipts, a broken eraser, and that note from last semester that I never turned in. Why do I keep things I don’t need? It’s almost like I’m holding onto them just in case, like that one time I thought I’d need a shoelace but never did. I wonder if I’m too much of a planner sometimes. Like everything has to have a purpose or a reason. I get so anxious if I don’t have my calendar all filled out or a to-do list in front of me. What if I forget something important? It’s not uncommon for me to check my phone several times over, just to make sure that I’m on track. I remember that one group project last year. Everyone just tossed their ideas around like they didn’t care, and I was like "can we please stick to the plan?" I hate it when things go off course. It’s awkward because I want to be agreeable, but I also need structure. So what do I do? I just smile and nod while internally screaming. The pressure builds, and I can feel my stomach twisting into knots. Why can’t everyone just be as conscientious as I am? I mean, it’s not that hard to show up and do your part, right? There’s this constant background hum of anxiety that I can’t seem to shake. What am I even supposed to do with that? And then there’s the social stuff. Ugh. Small talk? I just can’t. Like, don’t you feel awkward too? It’s all just so forced. Sometimes I watch others chat effortlessly, laughing, sharing stories, and I’m over here like, “Do I really need to join in on this?” I’d rather do just about anything else, like organize my closet or sort my pens by color. Which is insane because I want to connect with people, I really do, but I keep worrying I won’t say the right thing, or worse, I’ll say something stupid and everyone will remember it forever. I think a lot about friendships, how I want to keep them happy and healthy, but it’s exhausting to be the one always pushing for plans. What do you do when you feel like you’re putting in all the effort? I think this one friend is a little bit flaky, like she’ll agree to hang out and then bail last minute, and then I start overanalyzing everything and I’m left wondering if it’s something I did. I hate that spiral. It’s like a trap. All I can do is ponder it over and over. I know I’m probably being too hard on myself, or maybe I’m not. Then there are those moments when I feel so overwhelmed by my thoughts that my brain just shuts down. Is that normal? Like how do people handle life without constantly being a ball of stress? I wish I could just let things go. I can’t help but think of every little detail, and how it could go wrong. It’s like I’m always preparing for the worst-case scenario. I sometimes think maybe I should try meditation or yoga or something but then I’m like "what if I can’t do it right?" Why can’t I just accept that some things are out of my control? People say that being agreeable is a good thing but sometimes it feels like a burden. I don’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings, but it also means I get pushed around a lot. I think I worry about saying no too. Keeping the peace is a priority for me, but at what cost? I just want to feel secure in my choices without feeling like I’m compromising my own needs. I guess it’s just a part of who I am, but it leaves me feeling so stretched thin at times. I tend to stick to the same routines; they bring me comfort. I have my favorite places to study, my go-to snacks, my playlists that don’t change. Change doesn’t sit well with me. I just want to know what to expect, a clear path ahead. New experiences? They terrify me so I stay in my bubble, feeling safe, but I also wonder what I’m missing out on. What if something amazing is out there but I’m too scared to reach for it? It’s frustrating. I know that stepping out of my comfort zone could potentially lead to growth but then there’s all this fear. And sometimes, being conscientious just feels like a double-edged sword. I want to achieve things, to be responsible, but it can feel like a never-ending hamster wheel. How can I balance it all? Should I just let some things slide? I don’t know how to not care, though, and that’s the problem. And in the end, I find myself back here, just spiraling again, thinking too much instead of living. If only I could break free.

i just spilled the last of my iced tea everywhere, and now i’m sitting here with a soggy paper towel, which isn't even enough, and i guess i’ll have to get up and get a new one, but i don’t want to move. why do i always do this? i mean, it’s not even good iced tea, just store-bought, but it’s the only drink i have besides water and somehow that feels even worse. i hate making small talk, and water is such a small talk drink, it’s like the most boring option i could have. also, why does my roommate leave her dirty dishes piled up in the sink? it’s disgusting. like it’s just a plate with leftover pizza crust from who knows how many days ago. why can’t she just clean it? i can’t even deal with it. it’s just sitting there with food remnant stains and i think if i ever have to touch that dish i might combust from the grossness. i could say something to her, but it always feels so awkward. what if she gets defensive? it’s not worth it. i’ll just let the crust grow into a new species of mold, and i can say i discovered something cool. why is everyone so loud all the time? like i can hear people shouting in the hallway and it annoys me so much. do they not get that some of us are trying to concentrate? maybe everyone else is just naturally outgoing and has things to talk about, but the thought of engaging in that feels exhausting. i’d rather be in my room with my laptop, looking at the same memes for the hundredth time. it’s less taxing that way. i hate the pressure of forced socializing. i have this upcoming group project that i’m already dreading. why can’t we just do everything alone? the last group project i had was a complete disaster. i don’t understand how people can be so... relaxed about it. i don’t want to negotiate who does what, it feels like a power struggle of sorts. plus, if someone drops the ball, then we all suffer. no thanks, i’ll take my own suffering, please. better that way. i just want to do my part, but if everyone else isn’t pulling their weight, or if i have to chase them down for answers, it just gets under my skin in a way that makes my stomach churn. i keep thinking about my grades too. i should be studying harder, but then again, what if it doesn’t even matter? like, the pressure of getting the grades, it’s overwhelming sometimes. i should be more motivated, but what if this is just what i’m supposed to be? beneath average? what if that’s all i’m cut out for? i’ll just end up in some dead-end job and regret that i didn’t try harder, but then again, trying hard never really felt like my style, ya know? and it’s like, if i put in all that effort and still fail... ugh it makes me want to curl up in bed and binge-watch shows instead. there’s this thing, too, about everyone around me having these grand plans for their futures. they’re all busy networking and making connections, while i'm here like, “i just want to not freak out.” but what if they’re going to leave me behind? it feels like a race, and i don’t even want to play. why can't there be a pause button on life? just for a little while? feel like everyone else is moving forward, and i’m stuck in a loop of anxiety and procrastination. makes me wonder if it’s too late for me to jump into the race, but what if i don’t even want to run? like, sometimes i think about taking a semester off to just figure myself out, but then i panic about falling behind. not that i even have a plan to fall behind from. it’s just a wild guess anyway. what if i never find out what i’m good at? what if i’m just mediocre across the board? i think about that a lot, and it just cycles in my head, driving me further into a pit of self-doubt. the silence is actually comfortable right now, which makes me think i should probably capitalize on that, but even that thought feels too much. i can’t settle on what to focus on first—and that’s going to lead to freaking out about nothing getting done. the whole idea of making a list is so overwhelming too. too many options—what if i pick the wrong thing? i don’t even know how people can handle their lives. everyone looks so put together, and i’m here just drifting. it feels heavy, like an anchor tied to my feet, dragging me down deeper into the mess of thoughts that won’t stop swirling in my head—like some twisted version of a treadmill where everyone else is sprinting and i’m just standing still.

okay so I'm just sitting here staring at the wall kind of thinking about how unmotivated I feel like I should really be doing something productive but also here I am with this overwhelming sense of disinterest in everything I should care about like school projects and group assignments no one seems to understand that sometimes you just want to exist without all the pressure to be social or work with others it’s like every time there's a group project I think about how much easier it would be to just do it alone people complicate everything and I don’t get why they talk so much in meetings it’s like we all know what we need to do so just do it and stop dragging it out with all this small talk I don’t understand the point of chatting about the weather or weekend plans I just want to know the facts get to the point why do people waste time like that but then I guess I’m supposed to care about their lives which I don’t and that’s probably why I don’t have many friends I don’t know how to fake interest I just want to do my thing without having to pretend to be excited about stuff that doesn’t interest me like some of the clubs people talk about I just can’t wrap my head around why they’d want to gather and discuss things that seem pointless I mean if it’s not useful why bother it’s like all that socializing over coffee why is it always coffee can’t we mix it up with tea or water like why does everything have to revolve around caffeine and sugar the taste is just not appealing to me sometimes I think about how I should probably be more open to experiences but then I wonder why I should if it doesn’t seem necessary everything seems to have this layer of expectation attached to it like people expect me to want to go to parties or hang out in that loud environment of music where I can barely hear myself think I mean really how can you enjoy something when it’s just noise everywhere but then there’s those times when you’re just alone at home scrolling through some random website and thinking about how strange it is that everyone else is out there while I’m here and I wonder if there’s something wrong with me for choosing to stay in like so what’s the deal with all these unwritten rules of social behavior every time I feel like I should be doing something I end up reverting back to this comfort zone of just being alone or reading something that’s not too taxing on the mind like why not just binge-watch a show instead of pretending to have fun with others those dramas always seem to drag out forever but at least I can escape into something predictable knows what’s going to happen next like that one show I just finished the character arcs were dull but it filled the void of boredom pretty well and I didn’t have to deal with people it’s weird how much time can disappear when you’re just watching things and not participating in the outside world like is this what growing up is people are always talking about maturity but if maturity means dealing with social norms that don’t make sense to me then maybe I’m just not ever going to be an adult there’s a sense of freedom in not following everyone’s lead but then I think about the future am I setting myself up for some big failure but honestly is failure even a thing like can you really fail when you’re just existing and not engaging with this relentless cycle of expectation it’s like society dictates what’s considered success but who even came up with these terms what if I just don’t want to play their game are they going to kick me out of some imaginary circle because I’m not acting how they think I should I mean it’s almost like a club you’re either in or out and I’m somewhere in between sort of floating around in my own bubble but then it’s like sometimes I wish I could just attach myself to something more meaningful but everything just seems like a distraction and I don’t want to be led by shiny things just to end up feeling empty afterwards sitting in this room with my own thoughts it’s like this endless echo of what ifs and should haves and maybe I’m fine just being here being who I am in this space without feeling bad about what others think like why is there so much pressure to conform I’d rather be alone with my books or videos or just doing nothing than pretending to fit in that seems like the bigger waste of time to me so here I am still staring at that same wall wondering if I should just get up and do something worthwhile but then I think about how worthwhile really depends on perspective and maybe I’m fine with my lack of productivity or lack of social engagement maybe this is just me and it’s okay.

I really don’t get why everyone makes such a big deal out of social gatherings. Like, why do people constantly want to be surrounded by others? It’s exhausting to even think about. I mean, awkward small talk, constant monitoring of what people are saying, worrying if I’m saying the right thing or if my face is giving the wrong impression. Ugh. I’d rather just stay home and binge-watch something or read. But then again, it’s hard when everyone is constantly pushing to hang out. It feels impossible to escape. Can’t people take a hint? Just because I’m not smiling and eager doesn’t mean I’m anti-social. Sometimes I just want to process my own thoughts without interruption. Then there's the whole group projects thing. Why do professors think that’s a good idea? It’s always the same: I end up doing most of the work while everyone else just floats by. People always say they’ll contribute, but it always falls flat. I’ll do the research, write the majority of the paper, edit like a million times while wondering if anyone else is even checking my work. I think I could get away with doing less if there was some accountability, but nope. It’s way easier to just let me handle it, I guess. Sigh. And then when I finally look at the grades, I’m left wondering if it was worth it. I should just go in solo more often. But that usually results in a “not collaborative enough” message from the prof, which isn’t fair. Just because I don’t vibe with everyone doesn’t mean my work isn’t valid. I’ve been feeling this constant pressure—like I have to be the best at everything I do. It's tiring honestly. Every assignment that comes in, there's this tight coil in my stomach, reminding me that I should be on top of it all. If I don’t get an A, it’s like a personal failure. Maybe it’s because I can’t stand the idea of people judging my work. I can just picture them whispering in the corner like, “She really thought she could do that.” Like, why even bother if that’s the endgame? I can’t deal with the anxiety that comes from it. Focusing on perfecting everything seems like a defensive strategy, like if I can just do all the right things, maybe I won’t be the target. Then there’s the idea of relationships, friendships, whatever you want to call them. They’re so complicated. I mean, do people even know how to just be straightforward anymore? If someone has a problem with me, I don’t want to play the guessing game. Just tell me. But no, everyone expects you to read their mind, and I’m left feeling like some kind of social detective. Reading body language, listening for tone shifts—it feels like an Olympic sport that I didn’t sign up for. I have enough to juggle; I don’t need a moral minefield in my life too. And don’t get me started on dating. What a nightmare. Social norms mean nothing when I’m trying to figure out if the other person is actually interested or just being polite. Is this too intense? Is there a point where I’m reading too much into things? I can never tell. Navigating the waters of human connection feels like tightrope walking at a carnival. I’ve been thinking of switching majors again. I’m in this field that seems practical but lacks the creativity I thought I wanted. But the thought of going through that process again is daunting. I can’t handle the unpredictability of it. It’s all so overwhelming—requesting transcripts, meeting with advisors, figuring out what classes I technically need. People say I should just follow my passion, but that’s such vague advice. What if I’m passionate about nothing? What if I just want structure and clear expectations? I wish someone could lay it out for me, like a checklist—“If you want to switch, do these five things, and you’re good to go.” But it’s not like that, is it? Instead, I find myself playing this endless game of second-guessing every choice. I’ve tried journaling to sort out these feelings, but sometimes I feel like the words just swirl together into a mess. Maybe I just need to vent more to people. They say talking helps, but I’m not sure. I can’t always find the right words. It’s hard. It’s all so complicated. I wonder if anyone else feels like this, or if I’m just overanalyzing again. Or maybe I’m just a little stubborn. I definitely wish my schedule was way less chaotic but it’s hard to change that. I wonder if all these emotions will ever settle down or if I’ll always be caught in this loop of doubt and anxiety. It’d be nice to find a way to break free, to just trust my intuition without overthinking everything.

Okay so I just remembered that time I was at that concert and I lost my friends for a second and my heart kind of sank because I hate being alone in those massive crowds but then I thought about how exciting it was to be surrounded by all those people. The lights, the music, the energy, oh man, and I met this girl who was just so cool and we bonded over our favorite bands, it was like instant friendship, you know? And I just want to be around people all the time but sometimes it overwhelms me, like I can feel my chest tightening a little when too many thoughts crowd my mind. I overthink everything, should I have said this, should I have done that, ugh! Neuroticism at its finest but I also think sometimes it's a little bit of a superpower, like I can read a room pretty well. I just need to remind myself that not everyone is judging me, right? But what if they are? It makes me anxious sometimes. And then there's school. So much to do, so many group projects where I end up taking the lead. I kind of like that because I like to organize things, get people on board and make sure we’re all on the same page — I thrive on teamwork! But also, I stress about deadlines all the time, even when I’m like ahead of the game. I could have a paper done a week in advance and I’ll still be like “What if I forgot something?” I keep going back and checking it. I have a planner, like a detailed one, because I feel like if I can map everything out, it won’t get too chaotic in my head. It’s like my mind is a tangled ball of yarn and I need to pull out the strands one by one without losing my sanity. And speaking of sanity, let’s talk about friendships. Some days I feel so full of love and connection, like I could hug the entire world, and other days I worry if I’m being too much or if I’m smothering my friends. I want to connect but I also don’t want to be that friend who always needs to vent or talk deep stuff, even if that’s my favorite thing to do. I appreciate how real everyone is, and I want to reciprocate that but I also wonder if they can handle my rollercoaster of emotions. Sometimes I feel like a sponge just absorbing everyone’s energy and then I’m all over the place when I get home, like I can't tell what feelings are mine and which are other people's. It's intense, but also kind of invigorating, like I'm living life on this incredible edge where every conversation and interaction feels meaningful even when it’s just casual chit-chat. And then there’s that art project I started. I have all these ideas swirling in my head and I’m practically buzzing with inspiration but then the moment I sit down to do it, I freeze. I want it to be perfect, to express everything I’m feeling, but what if it doesn’t come out right? So I keep sketching and erasing, which feels like a metaphor for my life sometimes. I see life as this vast canvas, and I want to splatter it with colors and shapes but it can be daunting. At the same time, that openness to new experiences drives me to keep trying even when I fail. I think that’s what makes me tick; I thrive on new ideas and adventures. Just the thought of traveling somewhere new makes my heart race with excitement, imagining all the people I could meet, the cultures I could experience, but also the fear of it not living up to my expectations. I can’t help but feel like I’m constantly in this state of flux, balancing the small joys of everyday life with the big dreams lurking in the back of my mind. Balance is key, right? I try to see the good in everything. I want everyone to feel valued, which is why when someone is upset, I can’t help but dive deep into comforting them even if I have my own stuff to deal with. Life is too short for people to feel isolated. I mean, we’re all in this together, aren’t we? But meanwhile, I’m grabbing snacks and planning out my meals for the week because I am all about structure and routine. How does that make sense? I love the chaos of connections but crave a degree of order in my day-to-day, is it too much? Probably. Whatever, I’m riding this wave, and I’ll keep paddling as long as I can. So many thoughts, so much life ahead.

The clock says 10:23 and my mind is racing. I can't focus. There's this loud buzzing from something in the corner, is it the refrigerator? Annoying. I’d love to rip it out and chuck it out the window. But then I’d have to deal with the mess. Why do people just leave things laying around? Just take care of it! I’m sitting here trying to study but instead I'm thinking about how chaotic everything is. My notes are scattered, I organized them, but somehow it all feels wrong. How did I even end up in this major? People said it was practical, high demand but I can’t shake this feeling of regret. Should I have chosen something else? Maybe something easier or something that would have made me happier? But happiness is overrated, isn't it? Everyone talks about it like it’s some golden ticket. Work hard, get an A, that’s how you get a job, right? Everyone else seems so confident, and here I am second-guessing every single thing. I wish I could just finish this assignment already. It has to be perfect. If it’s not perfect, it won’t be good enough. My brain keeps loop-de-looping over what I’ve written, waiting for the inevitable dread of imperfection. What if my professor hates it? What if they think I’m stupid? They probably don’t even care about my existence anyway. I’m just another face in the crowd. It’s easier to not think about it. It’s better to hide. But I can’t hide. I can’t avoid this group project either. The thought makes my stomach churn. Why do they even call it a “project”? It’s more like a punishment. I know I’ll end up doing all the work and then, they’ll just slack off. Typical. I’ll be the one who ends up staying up all night to make it look decent when everyone else just shows up for the presentation. Those presentations give me cold sweats. Standing up there, speaking to people, it’s terrifying. But nobody seems to care. They’re all just there waiting to criticize or judge. And who knows what they’re thinking? I can’t read minds. I don’t want to read minds. I know they’re just thinking about their own problems but that doesn’t help my anxiety. Sometimes I just want to scream at everyone to shut up for a moment. Let me get my thoughts in order. Can’t they see I’m struggling? But maybe it’s just me being dramatic. I’m always the one freaking out over nothing. My brain can give me a full-blown panic attack over an email draft. It’s ridiculous. I hate it. Why does everything feel overwhelming? Last week, I tried to go out. I thought maybe a little socializing would help, but it just pushed my stress levels through the roof. The noise, the laughter, it was all too much. I wanted to run away but I didn’t want to seem rude, so I sat there nodding along while my insides twisted in knots. They talked about movies I’ve never seen, music I don’t listen to. I felt like an outsider. Nothing felt right. I can never seem to find the right words. I always come off as blunt, which I guess is true, but I can’t help it. I don’t want to sugarcoat things. That's just a waste of time. But then they look at me like I’m some kind of weirdo. I get it. I’m not the life of the party. Was I ever? Probably not. I wonder what that feels like. Being so carefree and fun. I can’t remember a time when I just let go. I should probably get back to reading the textbook. I signed up for this class, and I did that for a reason. I need to remember the reason. But the material is so dry and boring. I want to learn, but it’s like pulling teeth. I can’t stop thinking about how to arrange my notes, to fit them in just right, to create a perfect study guide. I’m obsessed with it. It's not even that I think it'll help; I just can't help myself. It’s like an itch that needs to be scratched. Everything needs to be in its place. Or else it feels like everything's spiraling. I bet my roommate thinks I’m a freak. She doesn’t get it. I should talk to her more. But then again, what would I even say? I don’t have anything interesting going on in my life. I don’t want to bore her. And it’s awkward. I think I’ll just stick to my routine. Probably safer that way. Keep everything predictable. I like that. I can’t stand surprises. I hate them. Anything unexpected just makes my heart race and not in a good way. What’s the point? It’s exhausting. Who has the energy for that? Not me. I’m done for the night. Time to shut this down before I spiral again.

Okay so I just remembered how I was at that party last week and I couldn’t stop talking, like I was on fire and everyone was laughing at my jokes even when they weren’t that funny which just fueled me more and I ended up doing that ridiculous dance move where you pretend to swim in a pool but there’s no pool. It’s so nice when people get your energy, I love that buzz of being around others, even when I get anxious about what they think, it’s a constant battle in my head. I hate when my thoughts spiral, you know? Like, oh no, did I say something embarrassing? Am I too much? But then I just keep talking and they keep laughing and I ride that wave. But then the next day I replay the whole night in my mind, thinking about every little interaction. I wonder if I should’ve asked Mike about his new job instead of rambling on about my internship. It’s funny how I care so much about what others feel, but it’s also exhausting—this constant checking in with myself and others, making sure I’m not stepping on toes or overstepping boundaries. Why do I do this? Why can’t I just chill? I mean I love being close with people but sometimes I feel like I’m juggling a million balls and every time I drop one I freak out. And the way my brain works, I mean it’s like a racing car sometimes, always something new to explore, new people to meet, new ideas to discuss. I love diving into new experiences, like trying that weird fusion restaurant last weekend that served sushi tacos? Genius. I think I could eat anything if it’s wrapped in something else. I could literally talk about food forever. I have a friend who just eats to live, and I’m like how can you not enjoy the flavors? The colors? The whole experience of it? I wonder if it’s a personality thing, like the extraversion and openness make me crave those social culinary experiences. But then there’s the stress of it. Oh my god, the stress! My brain just won’t shut up sometimes. Like when I’m preparing for assignments, I write lists and notes everywhere and I worry I’ll forget something crucial. I can’t skip steps. I have to make sure everything is perfect, but why does perfect even matter? There’s so much pressure—it’s crazy. I really admire people who can just throw things together and not sweat it. There’s a certain freedom in that. It’s not me, though. I’m like, if I don’t plan this, it’s going to end in chaos, and I can’t deal with chaos. Then again, when I meet new people, I love to learn about them, where they’re from, their dreams, their quirks. It’s fascinating. I feel so connected to the world when I hear their stories. There’s this warmth that just wraps around me, even if I’m nervous about the conversation. I wonder if everyone is as invested in those little details, those nuances, or if it’s just me. Sharing little bits of ourselves makes it all feel worthwhile, right? We’re all just trying to figure it out—like that guy in class who always plays devil’s advocate, I love how he challenges everything but sometimes I want to tell him to mellow out. There’s a line between stimulating debate and just being contrarian. And speaking of class, I had a group project and I got tasked with organizing it. Of course, I was super excited at first, like, let’s do this! But then I started worrying about everyone’s schedules and trying to mesh our different ideas while keeping our goals on track. I was really trying to delegate while also making sure everyone felt heard. I don’t want to be that person who just takes charge and ends up alienating everyone. Balance, balance, balance. I’m always trying to find that sweet spot. I really wish I could bottle up my extroverted energy and share it, like here, take a swig of my enthusiasm, feel it burst in your gut and make you want to create something amazing right now. Let’s go skydiving or try rock climbing or something! But then I feel this gnawing tightness in my chest because what if they don’t want to? What if I push too hard? I’ll be devastated if someone feels pressured—ugh, the complexity of it all! And then I just want to curl up with a good book or paint something vibrant on a canvas. I love that openness, that freedom to express myself, but even then I get all tangled up with thoughts like, will this be good enough? Why do I care so much? I need to just let it out, release that energy without worry, go wild, be unapologetically me! But can I? Who even knows.

it’s funny how every time I think I have a plan for the day something comes up. like that time I really wanted to just stay in and binge-watch that show about people trying to survive in the wilderness but my roommate needed my help with a paper. I guess you could say I’m not the most social person but it’s not that I don’t want to help, it’s just that I often feel like I’m not qualified. like what do I know about writing a paper, I can barely write my own without freaking out about every single word I put down. sometimes I stare at a blank screen and it feels like it’s staring back judging me, when really it’s just a computer. sometimes I get so anxious about not doing anything that I just end up doing nothing at all. like I’ll have a million things to do but I can’t start any of them because they just feel too big or too messy or too something. last week I thought I’d clean my room and it ended up such a mess that I just walked away and made some toast instead. toast is safe you know. bread in the toaster is always a solid choice. I’m not even sure why I care so much about being "productive" anyway. like what’s the point? everyone’s just in their own head and there’s this weird pressure to be some kind of perfect human but I can’t even manage the basics most days. I mean, sure, I can get up and go to class and pretend like I’m listening while I’m actually thinking about how I should’ve just stayed in bed. because it’s comfortable there. I can scroll through my phone and ignore everything happening in real life. sometimes I wonder if other people feel like I do, but I kind of doubt it because they seem so carefree or whatever. conversations feel like a performance, and I get so tired of trying to keep up. small talk can go on forever and I just want to get back to my thoughts, which are not very conversational. like, how am I supposed to talk about the weather for more than five seconds, and when people ask about my plans for the weekend I just want to say “nothing” and be done with it. but I can’t. I have to put on this mask and say “oh I might hang out with so-and-so” when really I would give anything just to sit in silence and think about nothing for a while. today I didn’t feel like getting out of bed but here I am. it’s always the same struggle. I think about what I’m supposed to be doing versus what I actually want to do and there’s this gap that feels impossible to bridge. sometimes I make plans to hang out with someone and then I dread it the whole week leading up. I end up making excuses, and they probably think I’m rude but I’m just overwhelmed. college is supposed to be this big social thing but it’s like a pressure cooker where everyone is boiling over with expectations. I read somewhere that people are supposed to become more outgoing in college but I think it’s the opposite for me. social interactions feel like tightropes and I’m just waiting for the stumble that’ll send me crashing to the ground. I see all these groups laughing and it looks so effortless, and I wonder if they talk about the things that keep me up at night. probably not, right? they seem too busy living their best lives or whatever the phrase is. I know I should probably be more like them but the thought of it just makes me anxious. more often than not I’m stuck in my own head rehashing conversations from days ago, picking apart every word like it was a mistake. what if I said the wrong thing? what if they didn’t laugh at my joke not because it wasn’t funny but because I’m just not cool? it’s hard to shake that feeling. I envy the people who can just go with the flow. I feel like every single thing I do is calculated, weighing the pros and cons in the back of my mind. it wears me out just thinking about it. if someone asks me how I’m doing, I don’t ever feel like I can give them an honest answer without diving into all my insecurities, and even that sounds exhausting. so I put on a face and say “fine” even when I’m anything but. it’s like a weird dance, this balancing act of being real and being what everyone expects. I just wish I could be a little more at ease. I know I’m not, and it’s tiring trying to figure out how to be.

Okay so I was just thinking about how I can’t stand small talk like it just feels so pointless sometimes like why are we talking about the weather when there’s so much more interesting stuff out there? I mean, I get it, people like to connect but I’d rather dive into a weird conspiracy theory or discuss the absurdity of our society’s obsession with social media than say “nice day, huh?” For me, that’s just not engaging enough. It’s like living in color while everyone else is stuck in black and white or maybe it’s more like everyone’s happy in their little routines and I’m just this oddball looking to shake things up. Not that I mind keeping to myself, quiet spaces can be so refreshing. I wonder if that’s why I’m always drawn to avant-garde art or experimental music; it’s like all these unconventional ideas just flow and explode around me, and I can’t help but feel excited by it. And then I think about relationships and how they often revolve around these social norms but honestly, I don’t find them worth the effort really. People keep talking about loyalty and support, but I just want raw honesty or like a mind-bending conversation that leaves me questioning everything. It’s like I want someone to challenge me instead of just nodding along and saying “yeah, totally.” I guess that makes me hard to deal with sometimes but I can’t fake interest in things that don’t captivate me. It’s exhausting trying to fit into that mold, you know? Like in group projects, my friends always want to just go with the flow and get a decent grade. I’m the one thinking let's do something totally radical or off-the-wall, and they look at me like I’m crazy. I’m like, “why not?” but it doesn’t always go over well. Sometimes I wish I could be more like them, more willing to go with the flow but I just can’t, I genuinely struggle with it. Oh speaking of group projects, I remember this one where we had to create a presentation about the future of technology and I—of course—suggested we do something wild like explore the idea of sentient AI creating art and what that means for human creativity. Nobody was on board, they were all like let’s stick to the standard stuff, but I was insistent; it felt like such a rich topic. In the end, I ended up doing most of the work alone and they just kind of took credit for it. It just reinforces this notion that I’m better off by myself but then it starts to feel lonely sometimes. I wasn’t feeling down about it; just frustrated. I think some people thrive off the ‘team’ environment, feeling safe in their little circles but for me, it’s like I want to break from all that and go swimming in the deep end. I like exploring the unknown even if it means floundering sometimes. And what’s the deal with mainstream culture anyway? It feels so repetitive, like you catch the same themes over and over. I can’t even watch movies half the time because they all seem to follow the same formula. I don’t even know how people can sit through another superhero flick. Give me some weird indie film that challenges my perceptions any day. I want movies that leave me questioning my worldview until I’m a tangled mess on the couch thinking about them for days. I’m drawn to things that are offbeat or twisted, narratives that weave through your brain and leave you with more questions than answers. That feeling of being awestruck by something unexpected is what keeps me curious. And then there’s travel. Don’t get me started. I love visiting places where other people don’t usually go, where you can feel the real pulse of a city instead of tourist traps and overcrowded attractions. There’s something exhilarating about stumbling across a tiny bookstore in an alley or finding a street vendor who serves the best food without the shiny facades of commercialism. I want experiences that jolt me alive, that spark curiosity and wonder. I don’t need the predictable sightseeing tours when I can wander aimlessly and discover hidden gems. That’s where the real magic is, in the unexpected moments that make you feel alive. I’m realizing how much I value freedom. Just being able to do what I want without someone telling me what's appropriate or normal. I want to experiment with life, to throw caution to the wind and dive into experiences without worrying about fitting the mold. I mean, who says you can’t have dessert for breakfast? Why not paint your walls a bright orange? I find it absurd how people confine themselves out of fear of judgment. Those chains are so suffocating. I guess that’s part of being open, wanting to expand my horizons and embrace all the chaotic beauty that life has to offer. Okay so now what?

I just saw a picture of a really cool rock formation and now I can’t stop thinking about geology. It’s fascinating how the Earth just formed these massive structures over millions of years. I wonder what it must have been like when they first formed, like tectonic plates pushing against each other, and all this pressure creating something beautiful. I never really understood why some people don’t care about the Earth’s history. It’s so important, and there’s so much to learn. Speaking of learning, I really need to finish that research paper due next week. It’s on climate change and the impact on coastal cities. There’s so much data to sift through. Sometimes I get frustrated trying to piece everything together, but then I remember how crucial the topic is. Maybe I should take a break and go for a walk to clear my head. Fresh air helps me think. I might even bring my sketchbook. Drawing helps too. I love to sketch landscapes, mostly trees and mountains. There’s something so calming about capturing the essence of nature, even if it’s just with a pencil. Anyway, I can’t believe how quickly the semester is flying by. It makes me anxious thinking about how little time I have left to complete all my projects. I should really start organizing my schedule. A part of me loves structure, like I can’t function unless everything is planned out just right. It’s comforting, I guess. But then, I also love spontaneity, like the thrill of doing something unexpected. I’m thinking about the last-minute road trip my friend suggested. I’m not sure if I can just drop everything, though my gut tells me it could be fun. I really value time with friends, even though I’m not the most social person. I prefer those deep, meaningful conversations over small talk. I find those moments much more rewarding. I’m also thinking about how my friend gets stressed out about social gatherings while I tend to feel at ease. I wish I could make it easier for them, but sometimes I don’t know how. I want everyone to feel included and comfortable. It’s tough when you care that much, sometimes I wonder if I worry too much about others’ feelings. I guess it’s part of being agreeable. It’s that balance of wanting to help and not stepping over the line. I remember when I organized that study group last month. Everyone seemed to enjoy it. I want to do more of those, maybe even monthly if people are interested. It’s easier for me to be productive when I’m with others, but I also need my alone time to recharge. It’s a weird mix, but it works for me. I should probably plan that study group soon. It’s nice to have a communal space where people can share ideas and learn together. I feel like everyone has something unique to contribute, regardless of their major. I love that blend of perspectives. It teaches me so much more than just textbooks and lectures. I wonder what kind of projects we could come up with. Maybe focus on environmental issues? I know how passionate everyone is about sustainability. Just the other day, I read an article about how important it is to advocate for renewable energy. It really spoke to me; it’s something I deeply believe in. I hope my peers feel the same way. And what about the arts? The intersection of art and science is so interesting. Like, who says I can’t find a way to merge my love for sketching with environmental advocacy? I could create a campaign filled with visuals highlighting the importance of protecting our Earth, and that could open up conversations. Or even a mural! Just the thought of it makes my heart race. I love projects that have layers to them, where I can explore different media and bring people into the conversation. I still have to balance my own workload, though. It’s sometimes a challenge, especially with how much I enjoy taking on new tasks. I need to remind myself that it’s okay to say no. I always feel guilty when I do, even when it’s for the best. I care about producing quality work, so maybe I should evaluate what’s realistic. It’s like creating a to-do list but only with the things I know I can handle. I feel like I could benefit from more organization in that aspect. Okay, so back to rocks, if I got into geology, I wonder what kind of career I could have. I could teach others about the beauty of the Earth's formations or even work in preservation. That would be a dream job. Just think of all the places I could explore. I could get lost in the mountains, with my sketchbook and a camera. That sounds so peaceful. Why don’t I do that more often? I have to make it a point to visit more natural sites. There’s a lot we can learn just by being outdoors. I think it’s time to plan some adventures, both in learning and in life.

So I just finished my last class of the day, the one that usually drags on because the professor loves to drone about the same things over and over again. It’s like, dude, we get it. But I guess it’s better than that one class where everyone just stares at their phones and pretends to pay attention. I can’t stand when people don’t engage. Like, come on! This is why you’re here, right? To learn and discuss, not to scroll through Instagram. That’s how I see it anyway. Speaking of which, I really need to post something. My last post was ages ago and it’s not like I’m doing this for validation but it’s nice to keep up appearances. I should take a picture of my lunch. Or maybe not. I had the same boring sandwich again. Turkey and Swiss on whole grain. I need to mix it up. Maybe I should try that new place around the corner. Everyone keeps talking about it. But then again, do I really want to go somewhere new? What if it’s not as good as they say? I’ve gotten used to my routines – they keep me grounded, right? Like, I know my favorite spots, I know what to order, no surprises. Oh, speaking of surprises, that pop quiz today was ridiculous! I actually studied for it, but of course, it was all about stuff I didn’t expect. Why do professors do that? They must know everyone hates surprises. They love the power trip, I guess. Like they’re testing our ability to handle stress or something. Not sure how that benefits me in real life when I’m just trying to get my degree and move on to the next thing. And what’s with the group projects? Honestly, I’m over them. Can’t people just do their part? I end up doing most of the work every time, because who wants to deal with someone else’s procrastination? I’m the one who likes to plan things out, make sure everything is organized. It’s not like I’m a control freak, I just want to get things done efficiently. So what if I have high standards? I want to see results. It’s like that saying, “If you want something done right, you gotta do it yourself.” I’ll take it a step further – I want to be the one leading the project. If I could just be in charge of everything, that would be ideal. But then people would probably call me bossy or something. Whatever, I don’t really care. I mean, I do care about how others view me, but I’m good at keeping that under control. I’m not one to let small things bother me. People are gonna have their opinions, but most of the time, I just brush it off. I’ll engage when it matters, but if someone’s just being negative for no reason, I’m not gonna dwell on that. Life’s too short for drama, right? I’d rather spend my time making connections, networking, meeting new people – that’s where the fun is. I’m thinking about joining another club. I already belong to the debate team, which is challenging and helps me hone my skills. I love arguing and making my case. I thrive on the energy of others when we’re discussing topics passionately. But I wonder if I should branch out. Maybe a sports club? I used to play basketball in high school, but college is different. Plus, the competition is fierce. Then there are fitness classes. I saw one for kickboxing. That could be fun! Probably not too serious, just something to blow off steam. Ugh, why do I keep getting distracted? Anyway, I need to stop procrastinating on my assignments. They’re piling up and due dates are sneaking up on me. I should probably start working on that presentation I have next week. Group work again. I don’t have an issue being the one who leads the discussions, but convincing others to stay on task is a whole other story. I still need to catch up with some friends too. We used to hang out a lot, but lately, everyone’s been busy with midterms and projects. It’s like we’re all living in our own little bubbles. I guess I shouldn’t complain too much. It’s good to have a little solitude to focus, but balance is important. I miss the spontaneous outings, the late-night conversations, the laughter. Hopefully, we can plan something soon. I mean, we’re all in this together, right? That’s another thing – I thrive on collaboration, teamwork, all that jazz. I feel more energized when I’m with others who share the same goals, pushing each other to do better. So why do I feel so much pressure to lead? Maybe I just don’t trust others’ commitment levels. Need to figure that out. Okay, I think that’s enough for now. Time to tackle that presentation.

I wonder what’s for dinner tonight. Last night was spaghetti, which is okay but kind of boring. I think I have some frozen veggies in the freezer, maybe I can throw those in. I should really plan meals better but I never do. I mean, who has time for that? It feels like everything is always so chaotic. I get anxious thinking about it. I think I need to clean my room too. It’s a mess, I can barely see the floor. Why do I keep putting it off? I feel overwhelmed just looking at it. Things piling up around me that needs to get done but I don’t want to deal with it all. There’s this constant tug of war in my mind. Like I want to be productive but I also just want to curl up in bed and disappear for a bit, you know? Sometimes I feel like I don’t fit in anywhere. I go to these gatherings or events because I feel like I should, but I’m always just observing, like an outsider looking in. It’s tiring, I’d rather just stay home and read or watch a documentary. Speaking of documentaries, I watched one about the ocean last week. Super fascinating, all this life in the depths we don’t even know about. Makes me feel both small and connected to everything. It’s wild how humans have only explored like, what, five percent of it? That blows my mind. I want to go deep-sea diving but it scares me a little. What if I see something huge? What if I don’t come back up? My mind spirals into the “what if” zone, and suddenly I’m picturing a gigantic creature pulling me under. But then I think about all those beautiful bioluminescent organisms and how they could light up the darkness. So cool. I always say I want to travel more but then I think of all the planning it requires. I can never get my act together on that. I was talking to a friend the other day about how I don’t like confrontation, and she suggested I might be too agreeable. I mean, I get that I like to keep the peace but sometimes it feels like I’m losing myself a bit, you know? Like I’m bending over backward to avoid making waves. I keep thinking that if I just say “yes” to everything, people will like me more, and then it hits me that I don’t even know what I truly want half the time. I really should stand up for myself more, but then I imagine the discomfort that comes with it. It’s easier to go along with things, right? It feels safer. It just seems like life is filled with so many decisions, casually thrown at you like confetti, but instead of feeling festive, I just want to run away. There’s this part of me that craves change and new experiences but then the other part is like, “Nah, let’s just stick with what we know.” Like trying new foods, every time I go to a restaurant, part of me gets excited but I also order the same thing every time out of habit. Weird. Because I love the idea of being spontaneous and trying street food in some far-off country, but there’s that voice that says, “What if it’s gross?” or “What if I get sick?” Ugh, anxiety is such a buzzkill. I’ve started journaling about these thoughts, which helps a little. It’s like, I can pour out all these chaotic things in my head and then sort through them later. But sometimes it feels pointless too, like throwing words down a well and never hearing them echo back. I miss my childhood sometimes. Things always seemed so simple back then. You play outside, climb trees, run around without thinking about responsibilities or bills. Adults are always in such a rush to ‘grow up’ but honestly, what’s the big deal? I think about that a lot. Maybe I should just drop everything and live in a cabin somewhere. But also, I’d miss my friends. Even though I find social situations draining, I do appreciate those connections. Maybe I just need to find the right balance. There’s so much pressure to be outgoing, to fit a certain mold. I mean, people think I’m shy but I just get anxious. I’m here, just not really here, I guess. Sounds confusing, right? Like I’m stuck in this limbo that feels both comforting and suffocating. People are complex. I can’t help but think about everyone and their stories, their emotions. Everyone has things they leave unsaid. Even me. I think about the things I wish I could tell people, but it’s hard to be vulnerable. I mean, what if they judge me? What if it changes how they see me? I know I shouldn’t care so much. I should just embrace who I am but there’s always this nagging doubt. Sometimes I just wish I could let it all go. Just be.

okay so I just remembered that time I met that guy at the party last month he was super interesting like we talked for hours about everything from music to philosophy and I totally felt that spark of connection you know that feeling when you just click with someone and it’s like the world fades away and it’s just you two in this bubble of conversation it’s the best feeling ever and then I got his number and we’ve been texting but it’s funny how sometimes the energy can feel different through a screen like you can't quite capture that vibe that we had at the party and I wonder if I should just suggest hanging out again soon because it’s always nice to have that face-to-face interaction everything seems more real that way plus who doesn’t love a spontaneous adventure? and I'm always down for those, like my friends and I went on that weekend road trip last week we just packed up and drove nowhere specific just followed the signs to wherever the wind blew us and ended up at this amazing little diner that had the best milkshakes I’ve ever tasted and we took silly pictures and laughed so hard my stomach hurt honestly those moments are just what life’s about I feel like you can’t plan memories they just happen and maybe that’s why I love being around people so much because in those random moments there’s this electricity in the air you can almost reach out and touch it and I thrive on that energy like sometimes I walk into a room and I just feel it buzzing I want to know everyone’s story and share mine too it’s all connected and the more I dig into people's experiences the more I learn about myself I think oh gosh I need to go back to that diner soon for sure and it’s strange how places can hold memories but people can make them even more vivid I wonder if it’s just me or if everyone feels this way about social interactions like even just the small ones like chatting with a barista about their favorite coffee or an old friend randomly texting me to catch up I love it all I mean how could you not love the richness of human connection and then there’s this whole world of ideas and creativity that spills out from these interactions I want to explore everything like how colors make you feel or how music can transport you to different times in your life I was listening to some old songs last night and I could picture where I was the first time I heard them it’s wild how our brains work to connect experiences and feelings and I’m always wanting to share my playlists with friends like “you have to listen to this it’s so good!” because I just want to spread that joy like wildfire which is probably why I can’t imagine ever working a job that doesn’t involve people like I love teamwork and brainstorming sessions they get the creative juices flowing I thrive in those environments where everyone’s bouncing ideas around and the synergy is just so palpable can you imagine trying to work on a project alone? like just sitting there with your thoughts? no way! I’d just get lost in my head and that sounds exhausting but then again I like my thoughts they’re like a never-ending rollercoaster and I so enjoy taking people along for the ride but they can spiral sometimes too but it doesn’t bother me I think it’s neat how our brains have so many layers like an onion or something and peeling each layer back can reveal something new I’m always looking for new experiences too that’s why I signed up for that pottery class even though I’ve never done anything like it before I just want to try it I'm sure I’ll make a mess but it’s fine that’s the joy of learning right? it’s all about the process and creating something new from scratch sometimes I think about how life is like a giant canvas we’re all painting in our own unique colors and the more we connect with each other the more vibrant our masterpiece becomes and speaking of colors, I need to find a new plant for my room I was at this plant shop the other day and there were so many beautiful ones it’s exciting how something so simple can brighten up a space and remind us to nurture even the smallest things, you know? I need to get a snake plant because they’re so low maintenance and I tend to forget things sometimes like watering them but it’s okay I’m being conscientious and trying to be organized like I’ve started making lists for my day it’s oddly satisfying crossing things off and then I start thinking about how I measure success which is not just about the tasks you complete but the connections you make and how much you grow as a person and I want to grow every day so I’m going to reach out to that guy from the party it’s time for a new adventure!

okay so I'm sitting here thinking about that conversation I had with Lisa last week it was so awkward I mean she just kept talking and talking and I could barely get a word in edgewise and I was sweating because I had no idea how to steer it back to something normal and I started overthinking everything like did I nod too much? was my smile awkward? I always feel like I need to be on guard around people like social interactions are some sort of minefield and I’m waiting for the next explosion and it’s exhausting honestly just thinking about it makes me want to hide under my blanket like I can feel that wave of anxiety creeping in just thinking about having to go out again ugh but then I remind myself that I do enjoy some of it like there’s that rush when I’m in a good conversation or I find a new idea that makes me feel alive like last week I stumbled across this article about quantum physics and I got so lost in it that I forgot about the time I think I spent four hours just immersed in this whole other world but it’s weird how I can get so passionate about concepts yet have so much trouble connecting with people face-to-face like it’s a mystery to me how some folks can just charm a room and I'm stuck over here trying to remember the last time I felt comfortable in a group setting the library is my sanctuary it’s all those books lined up on the shelves just waiting for someone to read them and sometimes I pick a random one because my mind wanders and I want to explore something new I love that feeling of listing all the different genres and trying to find patterns in stories or themes or the way language evolves it’s fascinating but then it comes back to that social anxiety and wondering if I’m missing out on something crucial by being introverted but I wonder is it really missing out or is it just a different kind of existence like I think about the artists I admire who were reclusive and didn’t conform to society’s expectations and I can’t help but feel a little inspired by that but I also feel that urge to please others like someone told me once that introverts are just people who haven’t found the right environment to thrive yet and I wonder if I’m waiting for that correct moment to click like will it ever happen or am I destined to feel like this forever and I know I have my coping mechanisms I have my routines I’ve built them up carefully like a fortress around myself because relying on my own structure helps me feel grounded and when I start to veer off course it’s like the whole world tilts sideways I mean I can plan and organize my life to the minute if I have to I relish in that control and it gives me a sense of accomplishment but then I think about how much energy it takes to maintain all of that and if I leave one thing unchecked will it all come crashing down on me like those Jenga towers and I’m always wondering if I’m being too rigid or if I need to loosen up a bit but how do you find that balance and then there’s this flash of something that reminds me that the world is full of possibility and new experiences waiting for me if I just reach out but then the thought of all that effort feels insurmountable like climbing a mountain without gear because I’ve seen it done before and it looks exhilarating but what if I slip and fall right back into the pit of anxiety that weighs down on me and keeps me tethered to my fears how do I keep exploring without letting it consume me when it’s so inviting to just stay cocooned in my own thoughts where I’m free from judgment or expectation and man my brain just races like that really cool moment when I figured out how to solve a problem on my own and the joy of that discovery was incredible but then reality comes crashing back in and I realize that I can’t always live in that joy because of the way emotions can swing from elation to despair in an instant and it’s like being on a rollercoaster that I didn’t sign up for but I’m still strapped in and I can feel that little twinge of anger at the things I can’t control like when I check the weather and it basically says it’s going to rain all week and I had planned to go see that art installation that’s been buzzing around campus and it irritates me because it feels like an obstacle to my freedom to explore but then again maybe it’s a sign to find more art in the books I own and look into how perceptions shift based on experience because art doesn’t just need to be in a gallery right? it can be everywhere if I just open my eyes and pay attention but still the thought of facing the world sometimes gives me chills and I want that fear to dissipate but that urge to remain safe and tucked away pulls at me too.

So I just finished that group project, and honestly, I can’t believe how much time I spent on it. Like, who has that much time? I definitely don’t. I was just trying to get everyone on the same page, but I feel like no one cared at all. It’s always the same thing—everyone’s like "yeah let's meet up," and then they don’t show. So I took charge. Not a big deal, right? Just jumped in and started assigning tasks. I can’t deal with all the back-and-forth. Just tell me what you want, and let’s get it done. People say I’m controlling. Whatever. I just like things done right, like yesterday’s pizza was weirdly cold when I got home. I mean, how do you screw that up? It’s pizza. I can’t even handle cold pizza. Good pizza. Like the kind that should be perfect—fresh out of the oven, gooey cheese, the toppings placed just right, not all randomly thrown on like some kid was picking out stuff during a tantrum. I swear I can make better pizza. And that guy from my class always brings weird toppings—pineapple? Seriously? Sweet and savory don’t mix, it doesn’t work! Pizza is Italian and should be treated with respect. I really should try making my own. But do I need a recipe? Nah. Who needs that? Just grab some dough and go with the flow. It’s like life. Just grab and go. No rules. Sometimes I think I should just throw things in there, like whatever is in the fridge. Spinach? Sure. Leftover chicken? Why not? Oh wait, I don’t have time to cook anyway; I always end up eating out or grabbing something on the go. I feel like I’m always running to meet friends. I love hanging out. Like, seriously, if I could, I’d be out every night. Last weekend we went to that new bar downtown. It’s super loud but whatever—it’s fun. I can talk over the music, no problem. Everyone was dancing, and I was like yeah, let’s go! The DJ was alright, but I need them to pick better songs. People just want to listen to the same old stuff. Like, how many times can you hear “Don’t Stop Believin’”? I’m all about that energy, though. I feed off of it. When everyone’s buzzing, it feels good. I can convince anyone to do anything. "Hey, let’s go to that late-night taco place!" And boom, the whole crew is in. But then we sat at the bar later, and some dude tried to start a deep conversation about feelings. Nope. Not my thing. I don’t want to talk about life’s meaning after a couple of margaritas. I just want to eat. And order another round. My friends think I’m intense. Yeah, I get it. But I can’t help it. I enjoy being in charge. I think people gravitate towards me because I’m loud and confident. Or maybe they just want to be entertained? I mean, I’ve got stories. Who doesn’t love a good story? I’ll never understand people who sit quietly in a corner, observing. Why not jump in? Sometimes, I get bored at parties. Like when people start discussing politics. Ugh—why? Get me out of there. I would much rather talk about favorite movies or who’s going to win the game. I just want to have fun. I don’t get why some people need to complicate everything. They mope around, judging everyone, and that just feels wrong. If I wanted to be judged, I wouldn’t be here. I think I should just switch topics. Normally, I’d just shout over them. Sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn’t. Whatever. And being nice? I don’t know. Sometimes I just can’t do it. You know when people are too soft? The world needs a little tough love sometimes. Like don’t just say “it’s okay” when someone’s making a mess of things. Just tell them straight up. Life isn’t all rainbows and butterflies. Some days it’s messy, and you just gotta deal with it. I’m not saying I don’t want friends. I just like honesty. Even if it’s rough, it’s better than pretending everything is perfect. Life isn’t perfect, and neither am I. But hey, I’m totally fine with it. I like being straightforward. Keeps things clear. Now I’m craving tacos again. Should I go? The night’s still young. I love nights like this. Open-ended and full of potential, no limits, just a chance to enjoy being myself, being loud, being out there. Ugh, maybe I’ll get tacos and call it a night. Or hit up the bar again. Choices. So many possibilities. Let’s see where the night takes me!

the cat is sleeping on my lap and I can feel her little heartbeat it’s calming maybe I should take a nap too but then I think about the laundry piling up and how I didn’t really do anything productive today I mean I should’ve gone to the library but the couch was just too comfy it’s weird to think about how people love to hang out in big groups or go to parties I don’t get it mostly I just want to be at home in my own space and not think about all the socializing I should be doing even though I like people sometimes just not all at once I remember that time in high school when I tried to join the debate team just to see what it was like I ended up sitting in the back and not saying anything because I couldn’t think fast enough I think they all thought I was shy but it was more like I didn’t have anything useful to say it’s easier to just nod along and blend into the background sometimes especially when they start arguing about topics I can’t wrap my head around like politics or the economy I’m not really interested in that stuff I just want to know what’s for dinner my roommate is talking to someone outside the door I can hear them laughing I feel the urge to just grab my headphones and drown it all out music always helps bring me down a notch even if the world is buzzing outside my bubble I still feel like I’m floating in my own thoughts and those thoughts tend to be pretty predictable sometimes I’ll catch myself thinking about the same old memories or worries or the things I should be doing instead of just sitting around but I don’t actually want to do any of those things not right now the refrigerator hums and I wonder if I should really be eating at a specific time or just when I feel like it what if I just ignored hunger and ate only when it was convenient but that doesn’t really work does it at a certain point I just feel sluggish and forgetful I think it’s important to at least make sure I’m eating enough even if I’m not eating the healthiest like that time I survived on ramen and microwave burritos for a whole semester I think my body is still recovering from that but I’ve got to get through these exams first not like they’re the end of the world but it feels overwhelming at times and I just want to crawl into a book and disappear for a while but I don’t want to read anything too challenging just something to slip into without having to think too much maybe I’ll reread that one fantasy series I liked and get lost in the pages I know what happens but that’s the comfort of it I just want to escape sometimes and that’s a whole lot easier than trying to care about real-life responsibilities going to the grocery store is always a mission too so many choices like frozen meals or fresh produce and I can’t make up my mind I’d rather just go for the same old stuff I know I like I remember my mom always telling me to try new things and my response would just be “why?” but she meant well like when she tried to get me to go to summer camp and I had to act polite even though all I wanted was to stay home and do puzzles and watch cartoons it’s hard to explain that it’s not that I don’t like people I just prefer the quiet moments where there’s no pressure to be lively or entertaining I think a lot of my friends see me as the calm one the one who listens but it’s not like I’m a wallflower by choice it’s just how I feel most comfortable there was this group project last month it was an absolute mess everyone was throwing ideas around and I just nodded and agreed with whatever direction they wanted to go even though I had my doubts about whether it would actually work it’s exhausting to keep up with opinions and perspectives sometimes I thought maybe I should’ve spoken up but then all the possibilities started crowding my mind and I couldn’t settle on just one so I decided to stay quiet and let them figure it out while I doodled in the margin of my notebook to keep myself sane but the doodles turned into a whole art thing and now I think maybe I should try to do something with that like maybe just one painting or even a collage I don’t have to show it to anyone just for myself I think once I start I might feel freer like when you ride a bike downhill and the wind hits your face it’s just a thrill to let go just like that time I went on a spontaneous road trip with a friend who was really outgoing and I matched her energy for a few hours before I crashed back into my own head and wanted to just sit in the quiet again I wonder if I’ll ever feel different or if this is just who I am and there’s something comforting in that too I guess it’s okay to be a quiet person in a loud world I belong to my own rhythm and that’s not a bad thing even if I second guess myself sometimes I just have to remind myself that it’s enough to just be.

Yesterday was such a blast. I went to that new taco place downtown with a bunch of friends. It was so packed, like a concert, and we had to wait, but it was totally worth it. We were laughing, joking around, and the energy was just infectious, you know? I love being in a crowd. The music was a mix of pop and some old school stuff, and everyone was dancing while waiting in line. I jumped in and started making up silly dance moves, and my friends joined in, making people around us laugh. You could see the smiles everywhere, like a chain reaction. I feel like when you’re in a group like that, it just lifts everyone’s spirits. I don’t get how some people prefer to isolate themselves. I mean, social connections are everything! I ordered some spicy tacos and they were a bit hotter than I expected. So, of course, I had to share some with Jenna because she always thinks she can handle the spice. She couldn’t! It was hilarious. Her face was turning red and she was chugging down her drink. We ended up having this impromptu taste test of the other tacos we ordered. I think we tried every kind on the menu because we just got so excited about food. Friendships are built over food right? It’s like this universal truth. I don’t know how people survive without those shared experiences. Then we decided to hit up that new ice cream shop nearby after tacos. I was absolutely stuffed, like ready to burst, but ice cream is a must after tacos, right? We took a million pictures with our cones and shared them all over social media. I know some people don’t like the whole ‘posting everything’ thing, but I think it’s fun. It’s like a memory book. I want to share my happiness about that moment with everyone. It’s like a little snippet of joy. And the ice cream was so good! I got vanilla with rainbow sprinkles and just smiled the entire time. It’s funny how something as simple as ice cream can make everything feel so right. After all that, we decided to walk around the park, which was perfect because the weather was nice, warm but not too hot. I love being outside, especially with friends. We ended up playing frisbee, which I totally suck at, but it didn’t matter because we were just having fun. I threw it wrong and hit Steven in the back of the head. He turned around, laughed, and tossed it back. That’s what I love about our group. No hard feelings. Just good vibes. We also talked about what to do this weekend. I’m trying to organize a game night at my place. I have this awesome collection of board games that I want to show everyone. It’s going to be epic! I love planning things for friends. I want everyone to feel welcomed and have a great time. Sometimes I feel like the planner in the group but I kind of like it. It gives me a little spark to think about how I can make things enjoyable for everyone. We could even do snacks again because that’s critical! Speaking of snacks, I need to make sure to stock up on chips and dip. And maybe some cookies? I don’t bake but I can buy some from the bakery. No big deal. I feel like food is the centerpiece of everything. I can already see everyone laughing and having a good time, like when we played that ridiculous trivia game last time. We ended up making up our own rules and it turned into this hilarious competition. I love when that happens — when things just flow, and the laughter is nonstop. I really don’t understand people who don’t like games or socializing. I can’t imagine sitting at home all weekend, binge-watching something alone. I mean, I get the appeal of some downtime and chilling out, but I personally get energized by being around others. That feeling of connection is just so essential to me. It’s just mind-boggling when friends cancel last minute. I mean, come on! I thrive on those plans. I need that interaction. It's essential.
