Oh, and speaking of plans, I need to check my calendar because my parents are visiting next month. They’ve been so supportive, and I can’t wait to show them my normal routine here. I love sharing my life with them. It’s like showing them a part of me that they don’t get to see every day. I hope they enjoy it as much as I enjoy sharing it. I just love life. It’s all about the connections, the laughter, the experiences. I wouldn’t trade it for anything.

I can’t believe I forgot my umbrella again, it’s always the little things that get me in a twist, like why can’t I just remember to bring it when it’s cloudy, I mean is it really that hard to remember what the weather forecast is or even just look outside. I feel like everyone else just gets through their day with so much confidence and assurance, and I'm here like, oh no I’ll just take my chances and end up drenched or stuck in some awkward conversation when all I want to do is find a quiet corner. I can’t stand those awkward small talk moments, they make my stomach twist in knots. I’d rather sit in silence than try to fill the void with meaningless chatter. People seem to like to talk about the weather though, and what do I say, oh it’s raining again, so fun, what a great time to be out. I mean it’s not like I’m a hermit or anything, I just don’t see the point in pretending to be interested in what some random person thinks about the weather or what their weekend plans are. Everyone is always so upbeat and it just drains me thinking about having to match that energy when I’d rather just nod and let my mind wander to my comfy couch and a good book, or even just mindless scrolling on my phone, that’s my escape, but it often feels so shallow. And then there are the group projects, oh man, those drive me insane. I am probably the least organized person you’ll meet, I can’t hold my thoughts together long enough to make a solid plan, and then someone always steps in and takes over, and that just makes me feel even worse because I know I should help but it's like I can’t. It’s like I’m trapped inside my own head, and I care too much about what everyone thinks. I just want them to like me, but then I feel paralyzed by not being proactive enough and it spirals. I think about that time in high school when we had to do a presentation, I panicked. I forgot my notes, I didn’t contribute enough, and my partner did all the talking. Everyone kept looking at me like, come on, say something. I wanted to disappear, just fade away and not deal with it. But I smiled and nodded, delivering this shaky line at the end and I could see the pity in their eyes. I would rather avoid that than show up to yet another awkward moment. It’s just a constant battle in my head between wanting people to like me and not wanting to deal with the stress of being around them. Sometimes I wonder if it’s normal to feel this way all the time, all this anxiety bubbling under the surface like a pot about to boil over, and I don't know how to let it out without making a scene. I feel like such a burden when I try to talk about my feelings, like who wants to hear about my worries anyway. “Oh here’s Sarah again talking about how she’s feeling anxious.” But then there are days when I think, maybe I should just scream into a pillow or talk to my cat. She’s a good listener, she just purrs and looks at me with those judging eyes like she understands my struggles. Sometimes I think she must be judging me, but then she just curls up next to me and it makes me feel less alone, there’s comfort in her presence. Then there are the times I get invited to parties or social events, and every single time I think maybe this time will be different, maybe I’ll actually enjoy myself. But no, I end up standing in the corner wondering why I didn’t stay home with that book instead. I’m constantly wrestling with the idea of stepping out of my comfort zone but even the thought sends shivers down my spine. I can’t stand the idea of trying to make friends while worrying about whether they like me or not. I mean what if they find out I’m boring, or that I don’t have anything interesting to say because my life consists of just going to school, staying in my room and binge-watching shows. And then there are those moments of clarity when I realize, maybe I’m enough as I am. But that thought is fleeting, like smoke, and soon I’m back to worrying about if I’ll ever really fit in. Still, the thought of just leaving it all behind, just doing my own thing sounds so appealing, getting lost in books or Netflix, that’s my kind of adventure, but I know I can’t avoid the world forever, even if I want to. So I keep trying to figure it out, one anxious moment at a time, hoping maybe someday it’ll all make sense or at least be less overwhelming.

The clock says 2:47 PM and I guess I should be studying but my mind is just all over the place like a kid in a candy store. I mean, why do I even care about the grades at this point? They’re just letters, right? But then again, my parents would freak out if I don’t get A’s. The pressure is on. It’s like I can feel the weight of their expectations making my skin crawl. Why can’t they just chill? I went to this party last night and it was like a blast, everyone was there, and I was in my element, laughing, shouting, just being me. But then I had this moment where I thought, wow, is this it? Like, are we just going to keep doing this? Parties, drinks, everybody pretending to laugh at the same jokes? It’s fun but also so superficial, and I can’t help but wonder what it’s all for. Speaking of shallow, I just remembered that I forgot to text Jess back. She’s probably freaking out. Our conversations can swing from deeply philosophical to the latest TikTok trends in like two seconds. I like that about our friendship; it’s unpredictable, keeps me on my toes. I should probably remember to check in on her. But ugh, that requires mental energy I’m not sure I have right now. Maybe I’ll just send a meme or something, that usually does the trick. It’s like a little lifeline in the sea of boring monotony. Oh and then there’s this thing where I can’t get past the fact that the library smells like old books and bad coffee. I don’t know how people study there. It’s just so suffocating and I can’t focus. Every time I sit down to read, my mind drifts off to other things. Like how do those people just sit there for hours? Don’t they have lives? I’m the kind of person who needs to be on the move, the one who can’t sit still because life is too vibrant to just zone out and read dry textbooks. Late-night walks are my jam though. I might be a little neurotic—okay, maybe a lot—but those walks clear my head. I get to let my thoughts run wild, chase after ideas, hear the city pulsing with life. I love that. Turning down a street, random strangers passing by with their lives intersecting for just a split second, it feels magical. I could talk to anyone at that moment. Just start a conversation with a stranger about how weird pigeons are or why milkshakes don’t come in banana flavor more often. Why don’t they? It’s such an underrated flavor. I need to bring that up at my next hangout. And then there’s social media. The double-edged sword of my existence. On one hand, it connects me with all those amazing moments and people, but then the other hand just drags me down a rabbit hole of comparison. I can feel myself getting anxious scrolling past perfect lives. It’s like, how do people manage to look that good while living their lives? Do they even have lives or are they just influencers? It’s funny how my brain works. I can be at a party, surrounded by friends, and still feel like I’m on the outside looking in sometimes. But then again, who gets deep on a dance floor, right? It’s all about vibes and the beats. And sometimes, when the music hits just right, I feel invincible. Like I’m on top of the world. But then it fades, and I’m left with the crash of noise and lights, the come-down feeling makes me want to curl up and let my thoughts spiral. Maybe I should try meditation or something. I’ve heard that can help with the overthinking. But sitting still never works for me, my legs get restless, and I end up just thinking about how long I have to sit there and breathe. Speaking of which, I should probably get up and move around—sitting here is doing nothing for my energy levels. Can’t let it drown me, the anxiety. What if I just decided to be more spontaneous? Quit stressing about planning every little detail? I mean, what’s the worst that could happen? Maybe I’ll finally go skydiving or something crazy like that. Every time I think about trying something new, I get this rush of excitement. What if I could just break free from the usual? Who cares about what people think anyway? Life is too short to fit into boxes or wear masks, right? I need more wild adventures. I want to travel to that little café in Paris where they say the coffee is life-changing. I want to stand on a mountain and shout because I can. Anyway, I should probably wrap this stream of thoughts up and do something—anything. Anything but sitting in my own head.

I always find myself wondering if I should’ve gone for the other major. I mean, everyone says psychology is fascinating but sometimes it feels like there’s just too much to absorb, like trying to drink from a fire hose. And the classes overlap so much with philosophy and sociology, and I can’t help but think maybe I could have done better with something creative, like art or maybe music. I love music but I never really learned an instrument, just dabbled here and there. I remember in high school I tried to learn the guitar and got so frustrated, I put it down—would have been endless practice just to get to the fun parts, and I hate feeling bad at things. I mean, who has time for struggle when there’s so much to explore? Speaking of exploring, I find myself getting lost in podcasts. There’s something soothing about them, the way people narrate their thoughts or stories. Sometimes I zone out and just let it wash over me, like I’m part of a world that’s unfolding somewhere else. The stories about true crime can be intense though; makes me think about human nature, the darkness, but also the light. It’s a weird dichotomy - I’m kind of drawn to the complexities of people. I think it’s because I tend to care a lot about how other people feel, like, way more than I probably should. It’s just that I want everyone to be happy, and I know that’s unrealistic but I can’t help it. I see someone struggling, and my brain goes into overdrive trying to help them. It’s exhausting sometimes but also rewarding, I guess. I can’t focus on one thing too long before my mind jumps to the next thing. Just yesterday, I was trying to write a paper and it turned into this whole spiral of finding out about the theories of personality. And there’s so much overlap in the research, I got sidetracked looking at the big five personality traits and how they influence behavior. I’m low on conscientiousness but high on openness; maybe that’s why I have a hard time sticking to plans. Even when I try to keep a schedule, I’ll get distracted by something shiny, like a stray thought popping into my head or a new hobby I want to pick up. And there’s always something shiny. Like right now, I can hear someone laughing outside. It’s just nice to hear people enjoying themselves. I don’t want to be around them right now, though. I like my solitude, but I also feel this urge to connect. It’s a weird balance. Group projects are a nightmare because I want to contribute but I also want to give everyone a chance to shine. I guess that’s part of being agreeable; I often put others’ needs before my own, which is probably not the healthiest thing. I can see how people would think that makes me a pushover, but I don’t think they understand. I just want to keep the peace, you know? But I also wish I had a little more drive sometimes, just enough to rein it in and get things done on time. Then there’s all this excitement about travel—it’s like a siren song. I keep thinking I need to go somewhere new, explore different cultures, and taste new foods. I daydream about backpacks and trains and the freedom to wander. The idea of being in a city with cobblestone streets and local markets. I want to eat street food and navigate my way through strange neighborhoods. Or maybe it doesn’t even have to be that far. There are places nearby I’ve never explored. I guess that’s what being open means, right? Finding beauty in the unknown, but whether I can actually do it is another thing. I feel like I’m caught between wanting to anchor myself down and craving that adventure. Maybe it’s not so black and white. Maybe I can find excitement in the little things. Like cooking something new, trying out a recipe that looks complicated. It’s a bit of a disaster most times, but there’s that sense of joy in creation—even if it ends up kind of bad. Food can be such a personal thing, sharing meals with friends or families or strangers, even, just connecting over a plate. I often find myself reminiscing about dinners that turned into late-night talks where we spilled our secrets and laughter flowed. But then there are those times when I retreat back into my brain, hiding away when things get too loud or crowded. The quiet is nice, though. I can spend hours on end just sitting with my thoughts, reading, or diving into a new topic. I think that’s where I thrive, connecting to ideas rather than people sometimes. Ideas don't judge; they just exist. How strange is that? The world is so full of things waiting to be uncovered, the thrill of learning never diminishes. I want to know more, be more.

Why do people always want to categorize everything? I mean it’s like, there’s a box for everything and everyone. I hate boxes. I feel like I don’t fit into just one. It’s like ordering at a restaurant when you want everything, but you can only choose one entrée. There are too many options. Last week I was out with friends and we ended up at that new Thai place. Honestly, should've gone somewhere else but part of me loves to try new places even when it’s a total bust. I’ll get a Pad Thai but then I also want to try the Tom Yum soup and the green curry because why not? It’s fun! But my friends just want to split everything and stick to the safe choices. So boring. I never understood that. So anyways, back to the restaurant, I’m looking at the menu, and there’s this huge wall of photos of the food and I’m just thinking: “I want to eat all of this today!” But then time rolls in and we order and there’s just no time to focus on the details, and then the food arrives and it’s chaotic. Everyone’s grabbing for something, and there’s too much energy, too many voices, everyone’s laughing, and I’m shouting over the clattering dishes. I love that energy but we all have that friend who keeps saying, “calm down,” and I’m over here like, “Dude, it’s a party! Let’s just enjoy it!” And oh god, then there are those moments when I'm in a corner just feeling like I can’t keep up with the conversation but I want to chip in. It’s like my brain is running on a loop of thoughts but my mouth is lagging behind and I think, "Why does it take me so long to say things?” Meanwhile, someone else is interrupting, and I’m like, “Hey! That was my thought! I think it’s brilliant!” I don’t really know how to explain my brain. It feels like there’s a million tabs open, frantically refreshing, at least that’s how it feels when I’m talking to people. I can’t stop wondering if they think I’m weird, if they’re annoyed with me. I get all these hypothetical situations playing out in my head. Like, what if I say something awkward and they all just stare at me? What if I miss my chance to make my brilliantly witty comment? Or more likely, what if I say something, and it totally bombs? So yeah, a bit neurotic there; it’s exhausting to care so much about what people think, and I know I shouldn’t but I can’t help it. And oh god, don’t even get me started on group projects. Seriously, worst part of college. You'd think people would be more motivated to work together, but nope. Every time it’s like herding cats with these students who just want to coast by. And here I am, trying to draw out the best ideas, and it’s like pulling teeth. I mean, come on, step it up! And they look at me like I’m the bad guy for wanting to make it something awesome. But then again, I do get bored easily. That’s a part of me too. Can’t sit still. Can’t focus on a single task for too long. Yes, I know we’re supposed to be diligent and finish assignments, but sometimes I distract myself with research articles about random stuff, like how the secret to happiness might be in the way you eat spaghetti? Or how to tell if your cat loves you—who even thinks about this stuff? It’s fascinating! And then I think, “Why are we not putting energy into these life mysteries?” Then I find myself getting excited about some obscure indie band that only five people have heard of, and I want to tell everyone about it. But nobody seems interested, and what do I do with that? I can’t help but wonder if I’m too much or too intense for the average person. I don’t even know if that’s a thing. But I love sharing what I love, and if they don’t get it or if I come on too strong, then I get insecure. It’s such a frustrating cycle of wanting to connect but feeling like I’m scaring people away. Sometimes I think maybe I just need to tone it down, but then the idea of being less me sounds horrible. I want to be big, and loud, and proud! But why does being me mean I should worry about how I come off to others? Why can’t we all just be excited about things without feeling like we’ve got to measure our reactions? And the truth is, when you feel things intensely, it can be like a double-edged sword. I feel joy more deeply, but I also feel anxiety and frustration, and it all gets so tangled up. I guess I just have to keep reminding myself that it’s okay to be a lot. Sometimes it’s okay to sit back and let others join the conversation, and sometimes it’s okay to be the one holding the mic and singing every word to the catchy chorus.

So the clock says 11:42 AM, and I should really be studying for that exam next week but I just can’t seem to concentrate. It’s like my brain is on a loop of worries. What if I forget everything? I can’t handle the thought of failing. My friends keep saying it’s just an exam, but they don’t get it. I can’t just brush it off like they do. What if I disappoint my family? They have such high hopes for me and I can’t take the pressure sometimes. I keep thinking about my grades. They need to be perfect. Always perfect. I hate when people say, “Just do your best!” because what does that even mean? I feel like my best isn’t good enough, and that terrifies me. I wish I could just relax. I see people out there being social and it feels so alien to me. My roommate is out with friends again. She’s always out there laughing, enjoying life, and I’m sitting here wondering if I should have gone. But then I think about the small talk – which I’m horrible at – and I get anxious just imagining it. It’s like, “What if I say something dumb?” I mean, it’s ridiculous, I shouldn’t care so much, right? But I do. I care way too much about what others think of me. I want them to like me, but every time I open my mouth, my mind goes blank. I’ve always been this way. Whenever there’s a group project, I’m the one who ends up doing all the work. It stresses me out to let others have too much responsibility because I’m convinced they won’t care as much as I do. That’s the thing about being conscientious. I can’t just let things slide like some people do. It’s not in me. But why do I take on so much? I mean, I like helping others, I really do, but then I get overwhelmed, stuck under this mountain of tasks I’ve imposed on myself. And then there’s that social media stuff. I scroll through Instagram seeing everyone’s perfect lives and it makes me tense. They all look so happy, and here I am, stuck in my small bubble, just hoping to get through the day without spiraling into a panic. Sometimes I think about deleting my accounts but then I feel like I’d be missing out on something, like maybe there’s some important connection I’m supposed to make. That FOMO kicks in, and then I’m back on there, looking at more perfect faces. Do I want to go out? I mean, I feel like I should want to, but thinking about it makes my stomach turn. There’s that pressure to be entertaining, to fit in, and what if they don’t laugh at my jokes, and what if I say something awkward? I know my roommate would say, “Just be yourself!” but it’s so much easier said than done. Sometimes I don’t even know who that is, the “me” who can be free and easygoing. I look around my room. It’s a mess. I cleaned it last week, but it’s already cluttered again. I should really start organizing better. Maybe that would help me feel less anxious, but then I feel overwhelmed just thinking about where to start. It’s like I have this mental checklist of things I need to do — fold laundry, study, clean, eat something decent — but then it just becomes this heavy fog that I don’t want to engage with. I wish I could just tackle one thing at a time without feeling crushed under the weight of it all. And I’ve noticed when I get really focused on something – like studying or organizing my notes – I lose track of time. I wonder if other people do that too or if I’m just in my own little world. That sounds so cliché. I think about how people talk about mindfulness and how they say to live in the moment, but how do I do that when I'm so anxious about the future? I keep worrying about where I’m headed, what job I’ll get after college, if I’ll even find a job. What if I’m stuck in a dead-end role? The thought makes my heart race. I need to breathe. I need to remind myself that I’m doing okay. I have a family that supports me, friends who care – even if I don’t hang out with them as much – and that I’m making progress even when it feels like I’m not. I should probably just make a plan, write out what I need to do, and stick to it. That feels safer. That feels like something I can control. Control is good. Why is it so hard to just be? Maybe I need to let go a little. But how? Ugh. I look at the clock. Where did the time go? I’m still not sure I feel any better.

So I’m thinking about that time I was at that party last month and how I basically stood in the corner the whole time. Everyone was around the—what do you call it, an island?—the kitchen island talking and laughing and I just felt so out of it. Like why do people get so excited to be around each other? I mean, it’s kind of nice, but also so overwhelming. The noise, the music, the people drinking and having fun, and I just wanted to disappear into my phone. I kept refreshing Instagram, hoping to see something interesting but it was just the same people living their lives, and I thought: is that it? I can’t remember what I did at that party aside from sip my soda quietly. Did I say anything? I probably just nodded and smiled. I always smile. Maybe I’m too agreeable. I don’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings. But sometimes I think I should just be honest, say what I really feel—oh but what if that’s rude? What if they don’t like me? I get anxious about these things. Anyway, I got home and felt so relieved. I couldn't wait to just lay down and breathe without the noise. Sometimes I wonder if other people feel this way. I mean, do all those extroverted people really enjoy being with others all the time? Like, what do they get out of it? Maybe I’m missing the point. When I’m by myself, it’s quiet. I love quiet. My room is like a sanctuary. It’s not that messy—okay, maybe it's a bit cluttered. Why do I keep so much stuff? I have clothes everywhere, old books I forgot I owned, half-finished snacks—why do I have those? I get distracted easily. It’s annoying. I tell myself I’ll clean, I’ll organize, but then I forget or I just don’t feel like it. I procrastinate a lot. It’s exhausting, I know, but what if I just don't care enough about the mess? Is that part of being low on conscientiousness? Probably. I remember this one time in class when I was supposed to present, and I got so anxious. My palms were sweaty. I could feel my heart racing, and I thought maybe I could just sit quietly and not say anything. But no, I had to talk! It’s a requirement! So I just stood there, mumbling my way through and wishing for it to be over. Everyone looked so focused, and I was just wanting to crawl under my desk. Why do I do this to myself? I could have prepared more, but then again, preparing makes me anxious too. What if I prepare and still don’t do well? It feels like a trap. Overthinking is my hobby. And then there are my friends. They’re so nice, so patient. Sometimes I feel bad. Like I’m dragging them down with me. They’re all so lively and fun, and I’m just... me. Do they notice? I hope they don’t think I don’t like them. I really do. It’s just that I can’t match their energy. I don’t know how they do it. They’re always planning things—hangouts, trips, movie nights. I want to say yes, but then I think about all the people and the sounds and the potential awkwardness. What if I say something stupid? What if they get annoyed at my silence? I value their friendship too much to risk it. Then there’s social media. It’s as if I can't escape it. I scroll mindlessly, getting sucked into the endless loop of photos and captions. Everyone seems to be doing amazing things and I’m here, just watching it all unfold through my screen. I think I should post more, show people my life, but what if nobody cares? Or what if my life is too boring? I have playlists on Spotify that are just collections of songs I like. Do people care? I know I don’t want to brag. I’d rather fade into the background, really. Okay, wait, what was I talking about? It spirals, right? This whole conversation with myself. I should keep it somewhat linear, but that’s hard for me. You know how it is, like thoughts just bounce around in my head, and I want to grab onto something but it slips away before I can hold it. So back to my sanctuary. I do love my space. I love reading in bed, just me and my thoughts without anyone intruding. Sometimes I can get lost in a book for hours, transported to other worlds where people aren’t watching me, judging me. But the loneliness creeps in sometimes, doesn’t it? I know I’m not alone, but why does it feel like that? Do I put up walls so high that I can’t see outside? I just want to get out of my head and live more freely, maybe even enjoy those gatherings. Or will I always go home wishing I could’ve been a little more spontaneous? Can I change how I feel about it, the noise, the people? I guess I just have to keep trying.

I just thought about how weird it is that people have such different tastes in music. Like, I was listening to this obscure indie band last night, and it hit me how much I love finding these nuggets that no one else seems to care about while everyone else raves about the same pop hits. It’s all so mainstream. I don’t know, the lyrics were raw and unfiltered, and I just feel like in an age of overproduced everything, it feels like a breath of fresh air. Then there's the whole thing about connections, like, why do people feel the need to be in huge groups all the time? I mean, sure, it’s nice to have a few close friends, but I prefer my quiet afternoons with a book or a new record playing in the background. It’s surprising to think how much energy socializing takes—so much small talk, so many ‘how’s the weather’ convos that go nowhere. Speaking of books, I just finished this one on existentialism. It made me think about how life is just a series of choices and how people often get stuck in routines and don’t even question them. Free will or determinism? Do we even have control? It’s kind of liberating, the thought that you can just get up and change things. I want to hike more. Or maybe take up painting since I’ve always admired abstract art but never tried it myself. The canvas is just sitting there in the corner of my room, waiting for some impulse to strike me. Maybe I’ll just dive in one day, no planning, just start splattering colors. That could feel freeing, like a small rebellion against being orderly. I don’t believe in rules in creativity anyway. If I can mix shades that clash, why not? And then there’s food. I hate the idea that I have to conform to diet trends. I mean, why would I eat only kale when I really want pizza? But I also love trying new dishes from different cultures. Yesterday, I tried making a Moroccan tagine recipe I found online, and it was a mess, but it tasted amazing. Spices everywhere, and I think I might've used way too much cinnamon but whatever. It’s all about the experience, right? I started wondering about the stories behind the cuisine—how did people come up with these flavor combinations? Where did they even get their ingredients? There’s just so much history in a single meal. Then I think about how mundane most people's lives seem, getting up, going to work, coming home, repeat. I get it, you need stability, but where’s the adventure in that? I sometimes feel like an outsider. I mean, I know people have their own struggles, life can get heavy with responsibilities, but why not take one day and just drive to the coast? Or maybe it’s just me wanting to escape, the thrill of spontaneity calls to me. I sometimes dream about dropping everything and just going on a road trip—no map, just fuel and music. Would I even care if I got lost? Probably. Oh, and I’ve been thinking about travel as a way to experience life. The thought of colors and smells in a different country excites me. I keep a running list of places I want to go—Japan, Brazil, the markets in Istanbul. I want to be enveloped in chaos and newness, to see people living differently, to see how art is created in other cultures. I don’t get the allure of just sitting on a beach every year in the same spot. I want to collect experiences, not sunburns. Let’s pivot to tech. I have a love-hate relationship with social media. It’s fascinating to see how connected we all are and yet how isolated we can feel. It feels like a constant stream of curated lives, and it’s hard to know what’s real. I prefer face-to-face conversations, but I wonder if I’m missing out on some deep connections I could be making online. There’s a weird balance to strike, and it honestly feels overwhelming sometimes. I think about unplugging for a while, see how that changes my perspective, but then I think about how I’d miss out on some of the interesting ideas floating around. And then there’s the idea of learning. A continuous process, right? I love open learning. Free courses online. I can learn anything from coding to art history if I want to. I dabble in a lot of things but never stick to one. I think that’s okay, though. Who made the rule that you have to specialize? It’s exciting to explore. I think the world needs more of that—less rigidity and more experimentation. Why not mix disciplines? Art and science, like those lab experiments where the artist creates chemical reactions. That could be something. Honestly, this stream of thoughts feels like a mishmash of who I am, a glimpse into my brain, the chaos of ideas and reflections. It’s a bit messy, like life. But maybe that’s what makes it beautiful too.

I was just thinking about how I really enjoy the little things like the smell of old books and how, sometimes, I wish I could dive deeper into those stories. Like, I have this book on my shelf that I’ve read like three times but I never finish it because I get distracted by another one. This is why I have ten books started and none finished. It’s probably a bit chaotic, but it feels good. I love the feeling of new pages but also the comfort of familiar words. I wonder how many people have the same issue with books. I mean, it’s not a problem but more of a habit or a quirk. Then there are my plants. The little green guys that are like my friends but don’t talk back, you know? They thrive on my attention and care, and I think that's why I’m drawn to them. I can forget my worries when I’m watering them or repotting them. Each time I see them grow a little bit more, it fills me with a sense of pride. I talk to them sometimes, which makes me feel a bit silly, but it’s just relaxing. It’s nice to have living things around that appreciate my company without judgment. I wonder if that’s a bit weird. People might think it’s strange but I find it soothing. Also, I get along with pretty much everyone. I had this roommate last year who was going through a rough time and I just made it a point to listen. I think people really appreciate being heard. I try to create an atmosphere of comfort. It’s funny because I can be around people but not need to be the star of the show. It’s relaxing to help others. I don’t need the spotlight, but I often like to support the ones who do. Like, I’ll help with their presentations or take notes for them. I feel like there’s a lot of satisfaction in being useful. Sometimes, when I’m at a gathering, I’ll just quietly help out with dishes or offer to drive people home. I’m good at that. It’s a way for me to be social without the pressure of small talk. Speaking of small talk, I find it exhausting. I mean, it serves a purpose but it’s just so surface-level. I’d much rather dive into deeper discussions about what makes people tick or what they’re passionate about. I love hearing about people’s lives and experiences. It’s fascinating. But I guess that’s why I’m drawn to the majors I’m in – psychology and philosophy. I love thinking about how we connect and why we do the things we do. It’s almost like a puzzle, trying to figure out what’s going on in someone else’s head. I wonder about their motivations and struggles. It’s not invasive; it’s just curiosity. I’m also really organized with my notes and my schedules. Sometimes I feel like I’m way too planned out, but it helps me stay on track. I have this planner, and it feels like I’m mapping my life as if I’m in control of it, even though I know life can sometimes throw curveballs. I think I take pride in those little color-coded systems. They bring me a sense of order amidst chaos. I like how a clean desk can start a productive day. I can’t work if things are scattered everywhere. It’s like my mind can’t focus unless I have a clear space. Then there's music. I love discovering new genres and artists. It’s a sensory experience that takes me places. I can lose hours on Spotify just exploring different playlists. Sometimes I find songs that just hit me in a way that resonates deeply, and I can’t stop replaying them. It’s like this little discovery process. I found this artist the other day who mixes jazz with electronic beats and it’s mind-blowing. I wish I could learn an instrument, maybe the piano. There’s something about the way music connects people that’s just beautiful. Food, oh wow, where do I even start? Cooking is a form of creativity for me. I love trying out new recipes and infusing flavors together. My friends always say they enjoy my cooking – I guess that’s just another way to show my care. I often bake whenever I feel like I need to lift my mood or someone else’s. There’s something so charming about sharing something homemade. It’s a little piece of me that I give away. I guess I just want to show love in all the quirky ways I can. And I like how cooking allows me to explore different cultures through food. There’s always something new to try, some dish from a far-off place that opens my mind even more to the world. It’s like traveling through a plate. And there’s so much more to think about, about how I fit into all this. I feel like the world is just this big tapestry of experiences and connections, and I’m just one little thread woven in. Sometimes it’s overwhelming but mostly it’s exciting.

Okay so, I just can’t stop thinking about how many parties I’ve been to this semester and how many people I’ve met. It’s wild. I mean, I thrive on that energy, right? Every time I walk into a crowded room, it’s like I can feel the pulse of excitement, it just fills me up and I want to talk to everyone, I try to remember names but it’s hard when they blur together. Ugh, I can’t get over what happened last weekend. This girl, Sarah, she wore the most outrageous metallic dress and I was like, YES, QUEEN, but then she tripped over her own feet and spilled her drink all over herself. It was chaotic but also sort of funny? I can’t decide if I should’ve helped or just laughed it off, I mean, I did laugh it off, but I felt bad afterward. Plus, I never see her again until the next big party. And then there’s the anxiety of seeing the same people over and over, like do they remember what I said last time? I always worry they think I’m annoying. And then I wonder if they’ve noticed the way I sometimes zone out when people are talking. Like, I’m listening, truly! But my mind races to a million different thoughts at once. Like I could be at a bar, and someone’s telling me their life story, and I’m thinking about this project that’s due next week and did I actually finish that paper? I really have a lot of things on my plate, but then I feel guilty for zoning out. Can they tell? I hope not. Sometimes, I think I should just be more engaged and nod more, but my brain just flips gears. Oh, and that thing with Kevin? He texted me about grabbing dinner and I was like, yes! But then I spent the whole day thinking about what to wear and I checked the weather like three times because I can’t stand being cold and trying to look cute at the same time. It’s exhausting. I finally picked this outfit that makes me feel good, but then I started stressing about if he’d think I was trying too hard. And what if I have something stuck in my teeth? What if I talk too fast and he doesn’t understand me? I hate that feeling of being awkward, and I run the risk of it happening every time—I can feel my heart race just thinking about it. And speaking of heart racing, running has been my outlet lately. I can block out the noise in my head when I’m pounding the pavement, it’s like everything else dims and all I focus on is my breath and the rhythm of my feet hitting the ground. Sometimes I feel so free, but then I get paranoid I’m not fast enough or I’m going to pull a muscle. I know, not the most rational thought, but that’s the way my brain works. I’ll freak out over small things—it’s like I’m constantly on alert, ready for something to go wrong. Classes are piling up too, and I can’t shake the feeling that I should be more organized. I’ve got notes everywhere. I wish I could just stick to one system. Half the time, I’m juggling different notebooks and sometimes I forget where I wrote certain things down. Like, did I note that crucial fact in my chem notebook or my history one? It’s all a blur. I have to keep it together, though, and I tell myself I do for the most part. I mean, grades matter, right? Like, I want to be proud of what I accomplish, and I worry about my future. It’s daunting. And oh, should I mention how every time I’m out, I can feel that burning need for validation? Not in a desperate way, just, sometimes it’s nice to hear someone say they like my outfit or think I’m funny. I crave that social interaction, and because I feel so deeply, it’s like I ride a high from it but then drop hard when I’m alone again. My roommates are great, but still, sometimes I can’t sit in silence, it’s too much. I need noise, chatter, the buzz of life around me. If I could just bottle that energy and carry it with me, it would be perfect. And then I realize I might just piss off a few people with my loud opinions—sort of a catch-22 since I love the debate but don’t like losing friends over it. I can tear into a topic, and I’m passionate about it especially when it’s something I care about, but I have to hold back sometimes. There’s a balance to be struck. I’ll just keep navigating through it all, I guess. That’s life, right? Just the constant sway between highs and lows. Who knows where it’ll all lead?

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how my plans just changed again and I really hate when that happens but also I feel like it’s kind of exciting? Like the unexpected is just part of life and I have this constant urge to do something new or meet someone new but then I just get anxious about what might happen or if I'll say something dumb, which I probably will because I always do but somehow that ends up being okay because people laugh and it’s like we’re all in this awkward, messy life together. I remember last week, I was at this party and I totally mispronounced someone's name, how embarrassing right? And everyone kind of stopped for a second then just burst out laughing and I just went with it like, “Hey, I’m the name butcher here!" And it felt good to just roll with the punches. I thrive on that – the energy of the moment, the rush of connecting with others. It’s like a high, I get this rush from being with people, from their laughter, their voices, and I can’t help but feel that life is so colorful in those moments. I wonder if they feel the same? Do they see the same colors? Or are they just being polite? I sometimes think I talk too much but then again, silence is just so heavy sometimes, like a weight pressing down and I could break it but what if no one wants to talk to me? But also, who cares? Because sometimes a random comment can spark a whole conversation and that could lead to a new friendship or at least a funny story to tell later. And then I think about my classes, I've been so organized this semester. I have a planner and I color code everything, it’s honestly kind of calming to see all the things I have to do laid out like that. I feel accomplished even before I’ve done anything! But then I get these moments of panic like, “What if I forget something?” What if I’m missing an assignment? What if I disappoint my professors? They have such high hopes for me and I can’t let them down! But then I take a deep breath and remind myself that I can handle it. I’ve handled a lot of things before, like that time I helped organize the charity event. I remember everyone was freaking out about the venue and the schedule and I was just like, “Let’s take it one step at a time!” And somehow we pulled it off, and I think that taught me a lot about the power of teamwork. Teamwork is crucial, it’s like each person brings a puzzle piece to the table and you can't see the full picture until everyone adds their piece, that’s how relationships work, right? Like every little thing matters, every interaction shapes us, molds us into who we are, and it’s fascinating and terrifying at the same time. And speaking of relationships, I was texting Emily today. She’s one of my best friends, and I get this warmth in my chest just thinking about her. She’s so open-minded and just gets me, you know? We share everything – our hopes, fears, the embarrassing things that we would never admit to anyone else. I love that openness. But sometimes I worry that I’m too much for her or anyone else, like my emotions are a roller coaster and I’m just dragging everyone along for the ride. But then again, isn’t that life? A chaotic roller coaster? I think we’re all just doing our best to hold on tight, and the moments of joy and laughter make it worth the ride. I keep thinking about how I want to travel and experience new cultures – it’s like a fire inside me that I can’t extinguish. I want to try all the foods, meet all the people, hear their stories and share mine. I’ve got this urge to understand everything, to soak it all in, but sometimes that neurotic side kicks in and I’m like, “What if I can’t handle it?” What if I get lost? What if I can’t communicate? But then the adventurous side says, “But what if it’s amazing? What if you find a piece of yourself you didn’t even know was missing?” I think that’s the balance I try to find in life, to push my limits while also recognizing my boundaries. It makes me wonder how others process these feelings. Do they always feel the weight of their choices? Or do they just glide through life? I want to glide sometimes, just let go of the worries and trust that everything will work out. Maybe that’s the secret to being happy – finding a way to embrace both the chaos and the beauty of it all. It’s exhausting, though. I wouldn’t trade it for anything, though. All of it is part of the adventure. And I’m here for it, every colorful, messy moment.

Okay so I can’t stop thinking about that wild party last week, people everywhere, music blasting, and I just dove right in like I always do. Someone brought a karaoke machine, and I ended up singing "Don't Stop Believin'" in a group of total strangers who were somehow my best friends for the night. I never thought I'd belt out Journey like that, but the adrenaline was pumping and everyone was cheering. I love how spontaneous life can be in those moments. But then there was that one guy, the one who thought he was better than everyone else, you know? I can’t stand people who act like they’re cooler than the room. Like, dude, you’re here too, just chill. Why ruin the vibe? Let's just have fun, right? Maybe I should’ve told him to lighten up but whatever. I tend to shake off negative vibes like it’s water off a duck. Keeping it positive is my style, but I also don't have time for anyone trying to rain on my parade. I was just reflecting on my classes too. They’re all over the place, my schedule looks like a rainbow exploded. I keep jumping from sociology to philosophy, and now I’m deep into some art history that I didn’t even know I’d enjoy. I love how every class feels like a portal to another world. Yesterday we talked about the Impressionists and how they broke all the rules, and I just lit up—like, that’s me, breaking the rules in life. Who needs a plan? I mean, sure, a little structure would probably help, but where's the fun in that? Speaking of plans, I still haven’t gotten around to scheduling my next trip. I need to just go somewhere, anywhere. I have a serious case of wanderlust. Maybe a road trip with friends, or just backpacking somewhere spontaneous. I’d love to hit up the mountains or maybe some beach, the thought of just lying in the sun with a cold drink makes me smile. I can see myself with a group, laughing, exploring new places—maybe hitting the weird roadside attractions. I live for those quirky spots. Why have a boring vacation when you can see the world’s largest ball of yarn or some funky museum? And oh, I keep thinking about how badly I want to learn the ukulele. I mean, why not? It’s the perfect travel instrument. It’s lightweight, fits in a backpack, and you can play like, the happiest songs ever. Every time I see someone strumming a ukulele, it just looks like pure joy radiating from them. My roommate has one, and she plays it all the time. It’s infectious. I tried to practice but ended up just jamming to whatever was on Spotify instead which, honestly, is kind of my jam. I can’t get enough of music. If I’m not listening to it, I’m moving to it or catching live shows. Then there’s this whole thing with social media. It's such a double-edged sword, right? On one hand, it connects people and gives us the chance to share our lives, but then there’s the fake stuff, and it's annoying. I scroll through and see the curated perfection, and I’m just like, c’mon. I’d rather post real moments, the silly stuff, the mess-ups. That’s where the laughter is, in the raw, unfiltered moments. Back to life stuff, I need to get my act together on the whole adulting front. Bills, rent, all that responsibility looming over me and making me want to run in the opposite direction. Like, why can’t we just live freely and without these obligations? But then again, that freedom sometimes gets wrapped up in chaos, and chaos can be fun! But still, I should probably at least pretend to plan for a future or something. There’s a particular book I’ve been eyeing, something about taking risks and living boldly. I feel like that’s a mantra I already live by. Sometimes I make these impulsive decisions about hanging out, or trying something new. Why overthink everything? Go with the flow. The universe seems to reward that kind of energy, giving me all these experiences that just light my heart on fire. I guess it’s about balance—having fun, enjoying life’s adventures, and leaving room for the unexpected. I don’t want to be that person who gets stuck in a rut, you know? I crave the excitement that life offers and the chance to meet all these new people along the way. So anyway, what's next? A spontaneous trip, a ukulele in hand, and maybe I’ll finally tell that guy at the next gathering to chill out. Life's too short for drama, let’s keep it light and full of good vibes. Cheers to that!

okay so I just found this old pen on my desk it’s one of those fancy ones with the fancy grip and it probably doesn’t even work anymore but I can't bring myself to throw it away because who knows when I might need it even though I rarely write anything down anymore it's all typing or texting or whatever but nostalgia or maybe just an obsession with objects that have no real value like I have a hard time letting go of things people say it’s clutter but I see it as memory like this pen brings me back to some random class I took a long time ago when I was actually kind of interested in the material or maybe I just like the way the professor spoke it’s odd how some voices can just pull you in - or not like that one class with the guy who was always monotone and made accounting sound like the most boring thing on earth and then I’d just zone out and think about how my life was spiraling in some different direction that uses all the creativity I have pent up but where do you even go with that when life keeps throwing bills and assignments at you. I remember last week I walked into this cafe to study and it was packed but there was this corner table that looked like it hadn't been cleaned in days but I sat there anyway like a hermit in my own little world and just watched everyone around me talking and laughing like they belonged to some secret club while I sipped my coffee trying to focus on the textbook in front of me but the words kept blurring together I think the caffeine was making me jittery but I couldn’t stop thinking about how everyone else seemed so carefree and I was drowning in thoughts that felt way too much like a heavy blanket suffocating me I thought about texting someone but then I just froze because what would I even say hey want to chat while I have a panic attack or something I don’t think I’d make friends that way but casual conversations feel so shallow these days like why bother with the surface stuff when we’re all just trying to survive. everybody keeps saying to be “authentic” but what does that even mean when my version of authentic is just me being overwhelmed and not knowing how to articulate it properly I remember being at a party last month where I desperately clung to the wall hoping nobody would notice me but the music was loud and the people were dancing like they were all in sync and I was just standing there feeling like an imposter in my own skin like how do people do that how do they just throw themselves into the chaos maybe it’s the alcohol or maybe they have some secret manual that I missed out on in life where’s my manual like I can't help but feel this constant tug-of-war between wanting to express all these ideas I have swirling in my head and just feeling so fundamentally disconnected from everyone. I think about traveling so much but then I don’t go because the planning stresses me out and then there’s the financial aspect and what if everything goes wrong and I wind up lost or stranded or worse what if I don’t fit in anywhere I could have some grand adventure but in reality, my idea of adventure is scrolling through travel blogs for hours only to close the laptop feeling even more trapped in my own routine it’s a cycle I can’t seem to break and I think about how I write these long emails to friends but never send them because no one wants to read all that emotional baggage I guess it’s not like they ask for it. there’s something freeing about writing this though it’s like a secret escape from all the pressures to be interesting or fun or social I mean I can be fun but it’s usually when I’m just alone in my room singing off-key to my favorite songs or painting something without any expectation like art just flows out of me even when I feel like the world’s falling apart outside my window I just get lost in colors and shapes for hours sometimes I forget time exists and I wonder if I’m more of a dreamer than a doer but isn’t dreaming where it all begins even if I have all these anxious thoughts spiraling around like will I get a job after graduation and what if I fail and why can't I just relax and enjoy the journey. so back to the pen it’s still here and it’s just a thing but it feels heavy with potential like if I just picked it up and wrote something anything it could possibly lead to some revelation or at least a distraction from whatever this is but do I even want to put that kind of pressure on something so simple why can’t I just let it be a pen and not some symbol of my tangled mind and all these unfulfilled expectations and oh my god does anyone else feel this way or am I just talking into a void that no one lives in except me it’s exhausting trying to make sense of everything when everything feels so out of reach somedays I’m just hoping to make it through the next hour without spiraling again and maybe I’ll take this pen and actually write something meaningful or just doodle little hearts and stars and breathe for a minute.

I can’t believe I spilled my drink again, why do I always do this? It's like my hands have a mind of their own, or maybe it’s just me not paying attention. Focus! I should focus. It’s just chaotic in my head. My phone just buzzed again, another notification, probably from that group chat I can’t get away from. Everyone wants to hang out but I don’t always feel like it. It’s exhausting, the back and forth, the planning, I sometimes just want to hide. But then, what if I miss out on something, that FOMO creeps in, it’s annoying. I can’t take it. Like when everyone went to that concert last week. I went, though. Bad idea. I was so overwhelmed by the crowd. The noise, the lights. I thought I’d have fun, but I ended up feeling trapped, like a sardine in a can. But I did get to see my favorite band, right? Can’t complain too much about that. Still, I can’t shake that feeling of wanting to be alone but also craving the buzz of social interaction. How contradictory. My friends are always texting me, “Coem out! We miss you!” and I’m like… do you really? Or is it just the idea of me being there? It’s tiring trying to figure out what people really mean sometimes. I also feel like I say the wrong things. I’m too blunt, maybe? A lot of my friends say I can come off as rude. I don’t mean to. I just... say what I think. It’s who I am, I guess, but sometimes I wish I could just go with the flow without sinking into anxiety. I mean, I hate small talk. Small talk just feels so fake sometimes, like we’re all playing a role. “How’s the weather?” Ugh, just say you want to talk about something more meaningful. But what does that even mean? I feel like I’m not deep enough for deep conversations, but I don’t want to pretend either. Makes me feel bad. Then there’s that discomfort when the conversation hits a lull. Awkward silences drive me nuts. I’d rather someone just say, “Hey, you’re being awkward, change the subject.” But I can’t say that, right? That would be a disaster. Maybe it’s just me. I don’t know. High energy but high anxiety. I keep thinking about that time I bombed that presentation last semester. So many eyes on me, I swear I could feel the judgment radiating from the room. It was just a simple group thing but I completely blanked. All my points went out the window, poof! And my group was like, “We’re so embarrassed.” I can’t handle that kind of pressure. I overthink everything, especially when I’m in front of people. I thought I’d go into communications, but what do I know? Maybe I should’ve gone for something easier like accounting or something. But – ugh – that sounds boring. What if I just quit college? Seriously, just walk away and travel the world. But then I’d miss out on my friends. There’s the dilemma. But I could never live in one place forever. I’ve thought about that a lot. But then there’s my fear of being alone. People say travel is about self-discovery, but I don’t like discovering myself alone. I like being around people, even if it drives me nuts. Maybe I’m too needy? Oh and my roommate, we’re like oil and water. She’s super organized, always has her stuff in order. I can’t even find my keys half the time. She keeps telling me to get my life together but it’s not that easy. I wish I could stay focused! I keep saying every week I’ll get organized and every week it’s the same mess. I don’t think I’m a bad person but am I a bad roommate? She just rolls her eyes when I’m messy. Like, come on, it’s not that serious. It’s just clothes on the floor but I get anxiety every time I see her neat side of the room. It reminds me that I’m a mess. I get tired of seeing how easy it is for her while I constantly feel like I’m running on a hamster wheel. And then there’s my family calling me, they say I should just find a serious path, settle down already. I’m like… no thanks! I’m too young for all that! But I can’t help feeling restless. I need to figure it out, but I’m not even sure what “it” is. Maybe I need a vacation. I’m always torn between wanting to go out and wanting to disappear.

Okay so I was thinking about breakfast. What’s the point anyway? It’s just a meal to fuel the day. I could skip it or just eat whatever. Like last week with those leftover tacos. Who cares if it’s 8 AM? Tacos are versatile, right? But then someone might judge that choice. It’s funny how food can bring out opinions. I could eat cereal like a good college student, but what does that even mean? It’s bland. Who decided breakfast should be so rigid? Just give me donuts and I’ll call it a day. I wonder if there’s a donut shop nearby that’s open at this hour. I should look it up, but nah, I probably wouldn’t even go. I’d just think about it and then get distracted by TikToks or something. Those little videos just spiral into hours of brain candy. I love the creativity. Some people are so talented editing their lives and making them look amazing. I envy that a little, but like, in a way that makes me want to create my own but then I just don’t. That makes me think of the last time I tried to paint. I bought a huge canvas, like those ones that make you feel professional, but I just stood there staring at the blank space for hours. I ended up accidentally using the paint as a coaster for my drink instead of actually painting. Academic pressure doesn’t help either. All these deadlines looming and sometimes I just feel like making art instead. Wouldn’t it be nice to be one of those people who can just drop everything and travel the world? I think of Paris, the food, the people, the art. But then I remember I don’t like crowds. I’d be that person in the corner of the café sketching the Eiffel Tower for hours while everyone else is taking selfies. How do people stay in giant groups and enjoy themselves? I’d rather have a small group of friends or just sit with a book. Books have always been my escape. Fiction, especially. It’s like walking through someone else’s world. Last night I finished this book about an alternate reality where people can talk to animals. So cool, I wish that was real! Imagine chatting with your pet. Would my cat be snarky or affectionate? Probably snarky. There’s something about the way he stares at me sometimes, like he knows more than he lets on. I get lost in my head thinking about the stories behind others. Everyone’s got a narrative; it’s just buried under the surface. That’s why meeting new people is so intriguing. I’m not great at it, though. I get anxious and tend to mumble. Small talk is the worst. What’s the point? I’d rather dive into deep conversations right away, but I guess that’s not how social norms work. People want to ease into it, but I’m not great at that. Maybe it’s just my low agreeableness. I don’t know, but I’d prefer to explore ideas rather than chit-chat about the weather or how class is going. Like, is anyone else bored of that? I mean, snow is pretty but it’s cold, why talk about it when there’s so much more interesting stuff to discuss? I could talk for hours about philosophy or art theory or just random stuff that pops into my head. I mean, that’s what makes life exciting, right? The unexpected connections that come from random thoughts. What do you think goes through a squirrel’s mind? They seem so frantic yet focused. There’s a sense of purpose in their chaos. And how do they decide which nut to bury? If I were a squirrel, I’d have a vault of hidden treasures. Maybe a stash of shiny things. People seem to think that’s weird, but I get it. A shiny object can tell a story. I’d collect bottle caps because they’re colorful and hold memories. Should I start a collection? I can imagine the conversation that would stem from that. A quirky thing to show off. But then I think about how much space that would take. I’m minimalistic, or at least I try to be. Less clutter, less stress. But what if I just focused on collecting stories instead? Just conversations with random people and making those memories. I keep saying I want to go to that coffee shop that hosts local poets and musicians. I wonder if they’re any good. Do I even want to go? I could sit in the corner and observe instead. Writing down snippets of what I see and hear, capturing that energy without having to engage too much. That sounds nice. I love the buzz of creativity in the air, even if I’m not the one directly participating. I could doodle in my notebook while everyone else performs. Or maybe I could surprise myself and share something. But then again, what if I bomb? Ugh, anxiety. But then I think what’s the worst that could happen? A bad night? That’s not the end of the world. I imagine I’ll probably just leave and laugh about it later. I need to get out of my head more. The loud thoughts can be exhausting. Life is short and there’s so much to see and do, yet I get caught in my routine of avoiding all the possible awkwardness. Just gotta get out there and embrace it, right?

I just finished that book I was reading. It was boring. I didn’t even want to finish it but I hate leaving things undone, which is odd because I don’t care about most things. The characters were flat. The plot predictable. I mean how many times can someone save the world from an impending disaster without it feeling stale? I rolled my eyes more times than I can count. I could've put it down, but I think I was waiting for something good to happen. It never did. Why do I even bother? Maybe I just like to complain about books. I see people talk about books on social media so passionately. I don’t get it. Do they even read the same stories as I do? And now I have this heap of laundry at home that’s like a mountain. I think I should do it, but then again, I really don't care. It’ll still be there tomorrow, I guess. It’s just clothes. They don’t talk back. I should have just bought more socks and T-shirts instead of doing laundry. Less hassle that way. Simple math, really. But then again, that pile is a reminder of responsibilities I don’t want to deal with. It’s ridiculous. Everything feels like a chore. Why does everything feel like a chore? I just want to exist without all these pointless distractions, you know? I should just buy all new clothes and toss the old ones. Easy. Just drop them off somewhere, let someone else deal with them. I think about food. I should probably eat something besides chips. But what? Cooking is too much work, and takeout is expensive. I don't feel like spending money on food anyway. Instant noodles are so cheap but they make me feel gross afterward. It’s a cycle I get stuck in. Sometimes I think about meal prepping, but that sounds like too much planning. Planning is overrated. I want things to just work out without me having to make decisions about them. Decisions are too much sometimes. Like what flavor of ice cream to buy. Why is there so much choice? I just want vanilla. Everything else is too complicated. The weather is changing, I guess. It’s getting colder. I don’t mind the cold but everyone complains about it. I don’t get why. It’s just a season. It’s just a temperature. You layer up and deal with it. People make it sound like a catastrophe. Just wear a jacket, right? I think the world would be better if we all just accepted it instead of whining about it. Every time it snows, it’s like everyone has forgotten how to drive. Just slow down, it’s not rocket science. I don’t drive much anymore anyway. Fewer idiots on transit, for sure. It’s kind of peaceful sitting quietly and just watching the world move around you. I don’t get why people feel the need to chat on the bus. Just sit there and enjoy the ride, right? Sometimes I wonder if I’m missing out by not being more social. I don’t even know what “social” means in this context. I’ve never been into parties or events. Boring. Small talk sucks. I hate pretending to care about what someone did over the weekend. Honestly, I don’t care. And why do people ask about the weekend? What if I just sat in my room all weekend? Is that not allowed? I think about how everyone always says you need to build connections for the future. Future what? I just want to get through today. Maybe tomorrow will be better or maybe it will just be the same. Who knows? The academic pressure is mounting, but I feel strangely indifferent about it. It’s just grades. Everyone around me is so stressed, and I just don’t feel it. I keep hearing people say they want to impress their professors or get into grad school. Why? Is grad school worth it? It sounds exhausting. More homework, more stress. I’m fine with my undergrad, whatever it means at this point. I’ll finish and then what? I can’t even think that far ahead. I can’t imagine sitting in a classroom again. I think I prefer isolation. At least I can binge-watch shows without interruption. No one bothers me, no one asks me about my plans. Just me and my screen. And here's a thought: do people really even care about my opinions? I mean, do I care about theirs? Probably not. It’s all just noise. I don’t need everyone to agree with me, but I like it better when they don’t talk at all. Some silence would be nice. Just existing in space without all this chatter and chaos. That sounds good. I should find a place where there’s less noise. Somewhere quiet. Or maybe just stay home. Home is nice. I like home.

This pen is really annoying. I keep clicking it and it’s almost out of ink. Can’t even write clearly with it. I should just get a new one but whatever. This paper seems pointless anyway, not like anyone’s going to care about my thoughts. I mean, who actually cares what I think? Not a lot of people, I guess. Just me, thinking about how things are pretty much the same every day. Wake up, do the same routine. I never change it because why? It works, I guess. Routine is safe. I feel like jumping into something different would be a hassle I really don’t want. People talk about stepping out of their comfort zones, but that sounds exhausting. Like, why should I bother? They always say you grow from that stuff, but I feel fine just being me. Friends keep suggesting I try new things, go to parties, meet new people but I don't see how that could be enjoyable. Wouldn’t it just be awkward? Small talk is the worst. Why do people think they need to fill the silence? Just let it be silent. Silence is underrated, honestly. That one roommate I had last year, really liked to chat. I mean, I don’t mind talking but it was non-stop. Dude, chill. It was like he didn’t know when to stop. He would talk about the most random stuff too. Like, what’s the point of discussing the latest video game if I haven’t played it? I sat there nodding while internally wanting to escape. He thought I was interested because I didn’t interrupt. I just don’t feel like getting into it. I’m better at listening, honestly. That’s just my style. Thinking about that class where the prof wanted us to partner up for a project. I wanted to go solo but everyone was like “No, teamwork is essential.” But why? Why can't I just handle it myself? Group projects never go smoothly anyway; someone always slacks off. I know I’d end up doing most of the work. Why stress over someone else’s laziness when I could just manage my own. Teamwork is overrated. I remember those times in high school. Group projects were hell. I’d always end up with people who didn’t do anything. It’s like they just coasted along while I carried the weight. It still gets under my skin thinking about it. They acted like we were just supposed to be friends, but it was always transactional. Work together, then act like we were buddies. Fake. I just want to do my work in peace. Is that so hard to ask? Friends. What even are they? Sometimes I wonder if I really have any. More like acquaintances, really. We don’t hang out outside of class much. They send a text here and there, but that’s about it. I don’t feel the urge to reach out either. They’re fine and all, but it’s just a lot easier to keep to myself. Less hassle that way. It’s funny how people complain about loneliness, but I’m downright okay with it. I hate crowded places. So much noise and movement. It makes me anxious. Why would I willingly put myself through that? Every time I walk into a crowded area, it’s like I can feel everyone’s eyes on me. The pressure is suffocating. I’d rather be at home, scrolling through things online. Much more comfortable. Phones exist for a reason, right? People keep posting their lives online, like they’re super happy and have it all figured out. It’s all a facade. Nobody’s life is that perfect. I can’t relate to their joy, their experiences seem hollow. But it’s not worth it to engage in all that. I prefer my own space. No need for fake smiles or forced laughter. Winter is coming. I hate it. Cold, gray, and wet. Why do people love it? It’s just miserable. Summer is better. Even though it gets hot, at least I can just sit in my room with a fan. But winter means bundling up, shoveling snow, and the eternal struggle of trying to warm up. Just can’t stand it. Anyway, I’ve got this paper looming in the background of my mind. I keep telling myself I’ll work on it later but who needs deadlines? Probably not me. They just add pressure. I can procrastinate all I want and still make it through. I’m just not the type to stress over grades. It’s just a piece of paper, after all. Life goes on regardless. I wonder if I’ll ever find something that excites me. It feels like I’m just floating through at this point. But that’s okay. Stability can be boring, but it’s also familiarity. Familiarity is comforting. Maybe that’s enough for now.

Okay so I just remembered that time I lost my phone at that party last month. I was so distracted, I was dancing like a maniac and somehow slipped it out of my pocket. The music was pumping, people were laughing, and I was in my element, but then it hit me—where’s my phone? That panic was a rush, like all those thoughts racing in my head, what if someone took it? What if I can’t call anyone? I mean, I really rely on my phone for everything. It’s like the ultimate lifeline, my connection to the world, my calendar, my social media, all the memes! Speaking of memes, there was this one going around last week about how you can’t trust people who don’t post about their vacation, but honestly, who cares? I post whatever I feel like it, selfies, ridiculous filters, anything that captures the moment. People are so uptight about social media, huh? Like, yeah, I get that it’s not all real life, but it's fun! I once posted a video of me trying to do a TikTok dance challenge and I fell flat on my face. Everyone loved it though! I got tons of likes and comments. I thrive on that attention because it means I'm connecting with people, even if it’s just through a screen, right? Social interactions fuel me. But then there are also those times when I feel like I’m too much. Like, can you relax just a little? I don’t mind being the loudest one in the group, but am I annoying them? I hope not. People just need to loosen up sometimes! But let’s be real, it’s hard not to stress over the little things. I mean, I can be super chill one minute, then caught in a spiral over something as trivial as a missed text reply. Why didn’t they respond? Did I say something wrong? I should’ve said something cooler! Ugh! And it’s not just about texting; it spills into everything, my grades, my friendships, I never know if I’m overthinking or just paying attention. My professors probably think I’m too carefree to care about my grades, but I do! I just choose not to act like a robot about it. I can’t fit into that mold of perfection. Conscientiousness? Nah, too boring for me. I live for spontaneity, for new experiences—the places I’ve traveled, the people I’ve met! There was this road trip last summer with some friends. We were supposed to hit three states but ended up in a completely different one because we took a wrong turn. It was wild! I was the one driving and maybe I ignored the GPS a little because we saw this roadside diner with the best pie ever. Like, the universe was just telling us to stop. You would think once we found out we were lost, the others would flip out, but instead, we just laughed and enjoyed our detour. I love that kind of unpredictability. Life is too short to be serious all the time. But then I think about how messy that can get. I get it, boundaries make sense. I’m always pushing for deeper conversations, wanting to know everything about everyone, but do they want that? Or am I crossing some line? I don’t mean to be rude, but why play nice when you can have real talk? My friends probably think I’m too intense. I just don’t get the point of holding back; that’s where the good stuff is. But at the same time, I don’t want to scare people off. Balancing it all is like walking a tightrope. I mean, I can be pretty abrasive. I’ve been told I don’t always consider how my words might affect someone else. It’s a balancing act, right? I care deeply but sometimes it comes out wrong, you know? I want to connect, but there’s this barrier of me pushing too hard, and then I freak out thinking I might lose friendships because of it. That thought keeps me up at night more than I’d like to admit. Overthinking is my second nature. Sometimes I like to just throw caution to the wind on spontaneous outings, and then I feel amazing, like I’ve cracked a code to life. Then I think back on my decisions and wonder if I should’ve been more cautious, like should I have actually studied for that exam instead of going out? But that’s life, isn’t it? You can’t have experiences without a little chaos in the mix. It’s invigorating to just embrace it all! Even if it means having those late-night meltdowns where I spiral about every little thing or explode with laughter over something silly. I wouldn’t change a thing. I’m just living—messy, loud, and maybe a tad neurotic, but who isn’t? That’s what makes us human, right?

okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how everything is just so noisy in my head like why can’t I just be normal sometimes I mean I guess who is normal but I feel like I’m constantly questioning everything and it’s exhausting like my thoughts just spiral and I can only think about the things I didn’t do right or the things I should be doing but I’m sitting here doing nothing which is its own kind of exhausting in a way you know like I should be studying or doing something productive but here I am just staring at the wall thinking about how I should be studying and that makes me feel worse but I can’t help it it’s like my brain is a hamster wheel and I can’t get off it’s just going around and around all these thoughts like why did I say that thing in class last week I can’t believe I said that everyone must think I’m so weird and I hate that why can’t I just blend in like why can’t I just be invisible sometimes but then again do I want to be invisible because sometimes I wish people would talk to me but then I get all anxious because talking just feels like a minefield like what if I say the wrong thing and people look at me funny I hate being judged but I judge other people too and that makes me feel bad but it’s hard not to because I notice everything like I’m too aware of everything everyone does and sometimes it makes me feel so isolated because I don’t want to be in groups but being alone feels bad too like what is the balance why can’t I find the balance it’s like wanting to eat ice cream but also not wanting to gain weight so I just don’t eat it at all which doesn’t make sense because it’s just ice cream but everything feels heavy and serious all the time it’s like I can’t relax ever and I wonder how people just seem to have fun without thinking about all the consequences like is it just me or is everyone else pretending all the time I think the world is full of fakes hiding behind smiles and I can’t trust anyone really but I also want to open up to someone it’s exhausting but I just feel like there’s a wall around me and I don’t know how to break it down because even if I wanted to let someone in what if they’re just going to hurt me why do I think like that but I can’t help it it’s like I see the worst in everything maybe I should see a therapist but then I think what if they don’t understand me or what if they think I’m crazy but maybe I am crazy is that even a word we should use what is crazy anyway feel like it’s a label that gets thrown around too easily but I don’t know how to articulate any of this because when I try it just comes out all jumbled like I don’t even know how to express myself I just want to scream sometimes but no one would listen anyway why would they who cares about my problems I mean they’ve got their own issues why would they want to hear me go on about how I can’t find a job or how I hate group projects in class because I honestly can’t stand working with others even when I know I need to it’s like inherently wrong to want to be alone but then I feel guilty about it I feel bad for not being social but really am I more socially anxious or just antisocial it feels like a blurry line somewhere in between I don’t want to offend anyone I don’t want to hurt anyone but then I also feel myself getting touchy and defensive and I don’t want to let that out because what if I say something that pushes someone away but maybe that’s inevitable because people leave everyone leaves or they just stop caring it’s so frustrating to deal with and I think I should just deal with it because that’s life right but I can’t seem to just push through it like everybody says because it feels like the weight is just getting heavier and heavier like literally climbing up a steep hill and I keep
