slipping back down and I don’t know if I’m ever going to make it to the top and there’s a part of me that thinks that’s okay because maybe I’m not meant to be happy maybe that’s just not how life works and it’s a sad thought but it’s also sort of comforting in a weird way like embracing the darkness means I can’t get disappointed anymore right but then I think what if I’m wrong what if I’m missing out on something really great and it just makes me overthink things even more like why can’t I just take it easy and chill out I don’t know it’s like my brain doesn’t know how to chill out I wish I could just turn it off sometimes it’s like I’m tired of hearing my own thoughts like I just want a break from me which feels impossible because I can’t escape myself and that feels super claustrophobic you ever feel like that like no matter where you go there’s just this loud buzzing in your head that won’t stop and it just gets worse every time I try to think about something else it’s like my brain has a mind of its own and it just wants to keep going on and on and I have to keep up with it even though I don’t want to it’s a wild ride I never asked for.

Okay so I just got up from a really weird conversation with my roommate about the nature of reality and if dreams are actually just windows to alternate universes or something like that. I mean, have you ever thought about the possibility of sliding into another life while you sleep? It’s wild. I can totally see it, so much craziness out there, and here I am just living in my tiny little world, surrounded by my chaotic room full of clothes, books, energy drink cans, and a plant that is somehow still alive despite my neglect. I can’t believe it’s still green. I bought it on a whim, thought, I should have a plant, it’ll make me feel grounded, but really it’s just another thing I have to remember to water, and I barely remember to water myself most days. I’ve been thinking about going out tonight. Got all these friends who want to hit the bar and I know it’ll be a crazy good time but also there’s this nagging thing in the back of my head like what if I say something stupid or trip on the way there or it’s just one of those nights where nothing goes right but then again, isn’t that part of the fun? The chaos of it all? I like being surrounded by people—love it, actually. The noise, the laughter, the energy, it’s electric and I can’t get enough of it. But then I come home and I feel that crash, like kinetic energy just dying away into the silence of my empty room. That’s where the neuroticism kicks in, I guess. I start overthinking every conversation I had, like did I really say that? Am I too loud? Did that person roll their eyes at me? I can’t handle being misunderstood. It eats at me. I remember this one party I went to last month. I was feeling it, you know? The music was thumping, drinks were flowing, and I was just vibing hard with my friend Laura. We were telling stories and laughing so loudly, I think we turned heads. I love that, love being the center of attention! But then suddenly it hit me, everyone was staring and I felt this wave of self-doubt wash over me. Like maybe I was too much, and what if they thought I was annoying? And then I caught myself in the mirror, hair all wild and messy, tank top that was probably too tight, and this little voice in my head was like you need to calm down, girl. But I pushed it aside and just kept going. I probably overdid it with the shots, though, because the next thing I know I’m spilling my drink all over someone’s shoes and they looked furious and I just apologized too many times and then I left the party feeling like an idiot. Got to love the combination of high extraversion and low agreeableness—always saying what’s on my mind without a filter, not caring if it ruffles feathers, but then regretting it later when it hits me that maybe I could tone it down a notch. But really, who wants to live life on the sidelines? Not me, not when there’s so much out there to explore, so many experiences waiting to be had. I always crave new things—food, music, people, places. Last week I tried this new Thai place that opened up downtown and WOW, I could not get enough of it. Spicy, tangy, just a rush of flavors that made my head spin. Why do I forget to take chances sometimes? It’s like I have this constant push-and-pull situation happening in my brain. I want adventure! But then this anxiety creeps in, and I end up sitting at home watching whatever show is trending on Netflix like I’m too scared to venture outside. What does that even mean? I keep thinking about how I need to find a balance. I mean, who defines balance anyway, right? Is it just a made-up concept to keep us from going too crazy? I could easily see myself living out of a backpack, bouncing from city to city, chasing experiences, making friends in random places, and getting lost in vibrant cultures but also, what if I screw up somehow and end up broke or stuck somewhere? I don’t even have a solid plan for next week let alone months down the road. Feeling restless. Maybe tonight I won’t worry about the aftermath of the bar. Just let it flow. I keep telling myself that the best moments happen when you stop worrying about what could go wrong. I need to stop letting anxiety dictate my fun. I just want to live, breathe, and enjoy every second without analyzing it to death. Okay, so that’s the plan. Let go, feel the night, and just be.

I should probably make a list of things to do today, but I keep getting distracted by all the fun stuff I want to do instead. Like yesterday, I was planning on studying for that big sociology test, but then my roommate started playing this awesome playlist. We ended up dancing around the living room like we were at a party. It was one of those moments that just feel so right, you know? Music always lifts my mood. I love how it brings people together, makes everything feel a little more vibrant. I wonder what’s in that playlist. I should ask her for it. Okay, back to the list. I need to finish that project for marketing class. The group is depending on me, but I get so energized when we all meet up to brainstorm. Everyone has such great ideas. So many creative minds in one space. But then again, I’d rather just organize our notes and put everything together neatly. I thrive on structure. I get a kick out of making sure everything is just right and everyone’s on the same page. Ugh, I can’t stand when things don’t flow. Last week we met at the coffee shop and it was pure chaos. Everyone talking over each other, coffee spilling, laptops open, it felt like a circus! I did my best to keep it all together, directing the conversation from one point to another. Thank goodness for me! I think I helped steer us back on track. It’s just fun to help others and make sure we’re all on the same wavelength. Group projects can be a nightmare, but I thrive in those situations. I should probably suggest a more structured meeting next time—like an agenda. But I don’t want to sound like a know-it-all. Speaking of group projects, I’m excited about that upcoming trip with friends. We’re going hiking, and I can already picture us at the summit, taking tons of selfies. I can’t wait to shout from the top of that mountain. You get to feel alive up there! It’s like every worry falls away. Nature is so grounding. I love hiking with my crew; they’re the best, always joking, making everything feel lighter. I might be the one leading the way, but honestly, they boost my energy. There’s something about shared experiences that makes life feel richer. What’s for dinner tonight? I should probably invite everyone over to my place. I love hosting! I could make those tacos everyone loves, but maybe I’ll try a new recipe. I don’t know why I always stick to the same thing, but it works. People love it. Maybe I’ll make a big salad on the side. Everyone loves that too! I’m known for my salads, which is hilarious because they’re pretty simple. Kale, cherry tomatoes, some feta, and my homemade vinaigrette. Easy, but it’s all about presentation. I should decorate the table nicely too. Maybe some candles? I’ve got those fairy lights that I can string up around the room. So cozy! I love when everyone comes over, the laughter, the stories. I remember last time we laughed for hours about silly things, like that time Susan tripped on a sidewalk crack and almost fell into a bush. Hilarious! She was mortified and we all couldn’t stop laughing. Good memories! And just like that, I can see how important our friendships are, building those little moments that spark joy. Ugh, I have to get back on track with my exercise routine. I started going to the gym with Mike, but then classes got heavier and I put it off. I love working out; it feels like a release, and we chat while we lift weights. There’s this great community vibe in our gym, people are so friendly. I miss that energy. I need to carve out some time, maybe fit a quick session before dinner tonight. I can totally make it happen. Oh, and I should check my messages. I’ve been meaning to reply to everyone. I get so distracted by the little things that chatting gets pushed to the back of my mind. I hate to leave people hanging. I genuinely want to connect, hear about their lives. I’m always ready to listen, help if I can. My friends tell me I’m a good listener, which is nice to hear, but sometimes I wish I could offer more than just words. I’m thinking about my future again. It feels so close yet so far. I want to finish this degree strong, maybe even join a marketing firm that prioritizes teamwork. Something where I can be hands-on and collaborative. I just want to be in a role where I can make a difference, where my attention to detail shines. I guess that’s also why I’m so hyper-focused on relationships. They matter. I thrive on human connection. I think I’m meant to help others while being part of something larger. A big warm community where everyone supports each other. That’s the life I want. I should reach out to more people today, maybe even check in on that friend who’s been quiet lately. I can’t let anyone feel alone.

So, I was thinking about how I keep a journal that I never look back at. Isn’t that weird? I just write stuff down, and it feels good in the moment, like I’m letting thoughts tumble out of my brain. But then later, it’s all just forgotten, more like a scribble on the back of my brain than any kind of memory I’d want to revisit. I wonder what the point is sometimes. Shouldn't I be reflecting on it? Maybe I should just record ideas but not actually write them down. But what if the act of writing is the only thing keeping me from losing them altogether, like that time I forgot the name of my favorite band for three weeks straight until I heard one of their songs in a store. It’s like my brain wants to keep things secret from me. Social media is another weird trap. I scroll through endless posts, mostly just food and pets—super cute but also, like, wow, a whole world out there that I’m not in at all. I feel fine with that though. I don’t need to be liked. I don’t post much, just the occasional photo of a book I’m reading or a landscape that catches my eye. Nature—what a concept. You ever just sit in a park and feel like you’re the only person there? Birds flying overhead, leaves rustling, like I could just blend into it and nobody would notice. That’s my jam, the quiet, the stillness, so much going on in nature and my little bubble of silence. Just last week, I went to the library, which is sort of a sanctuary, tons of books stacked up like they’re whispering secrets to each other. I could stay there for hours just absorbing whatever I can grab off the shelves. I love the smell of old paper, the dust motes in the air like little time travelers. I picked up a book on philosophy. It was a mixed bag. Some was just nonsense, but some really struck a chord. Maybe I’m too open-minded? They say that you can’t take everything to heart, but maybe that’s the fun part. Being able to consider so many perspectives but not having to agree with any of them. Hmm... I’m thinking about the last group project I did. Turns out I’m pretty terrible at teamwork—too many opinions clashing. I just want to get the information out there; I don’t want to go back and forth about whose idea is better. It’s like, let’s just put the best of what we have on the table and call it a day. I guess that rubs people the wrong way. They call me cold. I’m not cold—just practical. I get it, though. Being agreeable is important, but it’s like wearing a mask that I can’t be bothered with. I’d rather sit back and let the stronger personalities take the lead. I respect that. I’m not into emotional drama, either. I can feel it when people are upset or anxious, but I won’t dive into that pool. It’s overwhelming. My own emotional landscape feels so much calmer—I can’t take on someone else’s chaos. I remember my roommate crying over something trivial, and I felt bad, but I had no idea how to help. Asking me to console you is like asking a cat to swim, I just don’t know what to say. I hate that feeling of leaving someone hanging. It’s easier to say, “I’m here if you need anything,” and keep going about my day. But I guess that makes me distant? Who knows. It’s just my comfort zone, I suppose. Isn’t it wild how different everyone’s comfort zones are? Some people thrive in crowded clubs, dancing, being the life of the party. Not me, I would rather dissect a thought in solitude. I could probably fill a room with that, just thoughts bouncing around, all the things I’ve read and absorbed boiling over into ideas that might not even make sense but excite me. It’s like they say, knowledge is power, but what does it mean if you can’t share it? I should really pick up painting again. There’s something freeing about throwing paint onto a canvas and letting it turn into whatever it wants. It’s like the purest expression of thought, no filters or opinions. Just color. I enjoy color. Colors clash and complement in ways I sometimes wish life would. The world is so gray sometimes. Maybe I should fill it with more color. But then again…keeping it simple is nice too. I don’t need to please anyone but myself. I’m good with that. Exploring ideas, bending them into something new, like a pretzel of thought. Who wants to fit in a box anyway? Just be yourself, which sounds so cliché but rings true to me. Whatever “yourself” turns out to be, I guess that’s the journey, huh? I’m just here figuring it out one thought at a time.

Okay so I was just thinking about how much I love spontaneous plans like when random people text me and say hey wanna go do something right now and I’m like yes I'm in! I can’t stand when people are indecisive though like just pick a place and let’s go already. I mean life’s too short to be sitting around debating tacos versus sushi when there’s an amazing taco truck two blocks away. Speaking of food, I’m just so hungry right now but I don’t want to go to this place down the street that’s popular with the college crowd because it’s SO overrated. They think their avocado toast is the best thing ever and honestly I’m not impressed. I miss trying new places, new foods, new experiences. If I could just hop on a plane to say, I don’t know, Thailand or Mexico right now, I would totally do it. I heard the street food there is insane. I keep thinking about that last trip I took, it was to New Orleans and wow, just wow. The music everywhere, the energy, the vibe! I made friends with these two guys from Canada because we all ended up waiting for the same jazz band to start playing in some random bar. We just started talking about life, traveling, music, and then they invited me to hang out afterward. We were walking through the French Quarter at midnight and it was like a scene from a movie. The streets were alive with people eating, laughing, and dancing. I just love how you can meet the most interesting people in the most random situations. I thrive on interactions, the thrill of meeting new personalities. You can learn so much from just a conversation right? Like these guys, they were so different from me but we bonded over music and travel and that connection was so real. Now I’m thinking about how some people get really anxious about social situations. I won’t lie, I’ve seen it happen where they just freeze up or overthink things, and I can’t understand it sometimes. Why stress about saying the wrong thing? Just speak your mind! I mean, I don’t really filter myself. Sometimes I end up saying things that might not be tactful but it’s just my nature to speak up and say what I think. It gets me into trouble sometimes, but isn’t it better than bottling things up? I just saw this meme about how people should stop sugarcoating everything and I feel that deep down. Though there’s a fine line, I guess. I don’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings, but also, if you don’t want my opinion, why ask for it? Oh, and then there’s the whole idea of being responsible. Ugh. Responsibility is a drag. I mean, I get it, I have school and jobs but I can’t help but think that I’d rather be out living life than worrying about deadlines or cleaning my room. I mean I can’t even remember the last time I organized my desk and honestly, it’s a mess but I can’t find the energy to care. I just want to live in the moment, you know? People always talk about planning for the future but isn’t the present what really matters? Sure, I should probably get my act together and maybe it would help, but sometimes I just look at my friends who are so meticulously organized and they’re honestly stressed out all the time. And I’m just doing my thing, living free, and I feel like I might actually be happier. Then again, it’s hard for me to remain in one place for too long. I’m always looking for the next adventure. I think travel is a huge part of that. There’s so much to see! Today I was reading about Iceland and the Northern Lights and now I’m just daydreaming about standing under those colors in the sky. It sounds magical. I mean, who wouldn’t want to experience nature like that? If I had the chance, I’d take the first flight. And then there’s the thought of all the incredible festivals around the world, the culture, the celebrations. I don’t want to wait until I retire to explore. I want to do it now when I can really enjoy it. Sometimes, I wonder if my friends get tired of my energy. I can get a little intense, you know? Like let’s go out, let’s dance, let’s meet new people. But I just can’t help it, I thrive on connections and pushing myself out of my comfort zone. Onward and upward, that’s my motto! If something doesn’t challenge me, it doesn’t excite me. I’m always ready for something new. There's so much to experience! I can’t wait for my next adventure.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about all the things I have to do and it’s overwhelming because I have a million things on my list. I should really start organizing my time better but then I get anxious about not doing it perfectly and I end up procrastinating. It’s like this cycle, you know? I want to be productive but then there’s this nagging feeling in the back of my mind that I’m just one step away from failing at everything. I should probably look up some tips on time management or something but then that makes me feel like I’m just avoiding the problem even more. Why are these thoughts so loud? It's hard to quiet them down. And then there's the whole social aspect of college. I’m not really the most outgoing person. Group projects? Ugh, they stress me out. I can work well with others when there’s a clear task, but the small talk and the social bonding that everyone seems to thrive on? I just freeze up. It’s like my brain just stops functioning. I remember this one time in class, we had to do these icebreakers and I just sat there wishing the ground would swallow me. People were sharing fun facts about themselves like they were on a game show and I was just thinking, “What’s my fun fact? I once spent an entire weekend binging a series?” Which isn’t really fun at all, but it does sound relatable to a certain extent. Does that even count? It's so strange how others can just jump in and share memories or experiences without second-guessing themselves. I mean, I’m curious about people and love hearing their stories, but I can’t help but overthink my own. What do they think of me? Am I boring? Do I have anything interesting to say? Sometimes I think I’d rather observe than participate. I love people-watching. The way they interact, their little quirks, the emotions on display. Like that girl at the coffee shop with the pink hair and piercings who’s always deep in a book. I wonder what she’s reading and if it’s good. Or that guy who always sits alone but seems perfectly content with his laptop and headphones. Does he ever feel lonely? I hope he doesn’t. And then there’s my family. They’re supportive but I worry I’m letting them down. They want me to be happy. I want to be happy. It’s just, what does that even mean? I sometimes feel like my happiness is so closely tied to their expectations that I forget what I want. Like all those conversations about what my degree can lead to. It’s hard to explain that I want to be open to all possibilities, that I don't want to pigeonhole myself into a specific job title or career path. Maybe I just want to explore a little before I settle down into something routine. But then, does that make me irresponsible? I don’t think it does, but then I think maybe I’m just making excuses. Ugh, my brain won’t stop churning. It's like a washing machine, spinning and spinning but never actually rinsing. I should probably try meditation or yoga or something to calm my mind down but then I think, will that really help? I can see myself sitting there, trying to breathe and the thoughts just keep rushing in, "Did I forget to respond to that email?" "What’s for dinner tonight?" "Am I going to be able to pay for groceries next week?" And on and on. I also think a lot about how I can be more giving, how I want to help others. Volunteering sounds great but sometimes I feel like I’m not doing enough, like whatever I do will never be enough and that’s paralyzing. I see people involved in charity work or activism and I want to join but then I think about the commitment and what if I can’t handle it? My emotions are like a roller coaster—one minute I’m all in and want to change the world, and the next I’m worried I can’t even change my own life. I keep a journal, you know? Writing helps. It’s like emptying my brain onto paper. I think back to the days when I’d write poetry in high school. I always felt free when I wrote because it was just me and my thoughts without judgment. Sometimes I try to recapture that feeling, but it’s not always easy. And then there’s the future. What will happen? I’m both excited and terrified. I want to discover more of what the world has to offer, travel, meet people, learn new things. I wish I could just step outside of my comfort zone without all the anxiety wrapping around me. Why is it so hard to balance all these feelings? It’s exhausting. But maybe it’s okay to not have everything figured out right now. That thought lingers in my mind. I think I’ll just take it one step at a time.

I really hope I don’t mess this up. Okay, so I’m thinking about my friends and how much I love being around them. They all bring different things to the table. There’s Jess, who’s always so upbeat and has that infectious laugh. I can’t imagine life without her energy in the group. We were at that party last week, and I thought I’d be overwhelmed but honestly, it turned out to be a blast. Everyone was talking, and I was in my element, cracking jokes, telling stories, feeling like the life of the party, you know? It’s weird how I thrive in those situations but then later I'm just anxious about how I came off, like did I say something dumb? Did I make a fool of myself? Like what if Alex didn’t find my joke funny? I replay it in my mind and get all worked up. Social outings are fun but they really drain me later, it’s like I have to recharge. I’m always texting everyone afterward, making sure they had a good time too. It’s just my thing, I guess. I like keeping the vibes up, making sure everyone feels included. I just want everyone to be happy. Maybe that’s why I worry so much, because I want to lighten the mood, but then I start to overthink all the time. Like, what if they don’t really want to talk to me? What if I’m just being annoying? Ugh, that thought makes me cringe. I need to stop going there in my head. Then I think about school. I’ve got this project due, and of course I’ve left it to the last minute because I just can’t say no to all the outings and hangouts. I don’t know how to balance it all. I keep saying I’m going to manage my time better but when the weekend comes and Jess says we’re going to karaoke, I just can’t resist. I mean, who can, right? I love karaoke! Standing in front of everyone, belting out songs, it’s exhilarating. But then the next day I’m behind in my readings and my group project is staring at me like a ticking time bomb. Plus, I get nervous because I don’t want to let my teammates down. I can’t help it; I just feel this pressure to be the reliable one, and I don’t want to be the one who messes things up. It’s also funny how I have this need for everything to be perfect. My notes are color-coded, and I keep my planner neat. I spend hours organizing everything. I can’t stand clutter or chaos. If my desk is messy, I can’t focus. I start to feel all twitchy inside. But then, I’ll be out, and the night will be all crazy and messy, and I love that. I love being spontaneous in social situations but when I come home, I need everything to be in its place. It calms my mind. It’s like I have these two sides battling all the time. Party planner extraordinaire but also a meticulous organizer. Also, I think about my family a lot. They’re great but sometimes I feel like I’m suffocating because they expect so much from me. Like, I want to make them proud but at the same time, I’m just me. They don’t really get the whole social butterfly thing. They’re more traditional, like, “Why can’t you just focus on your studies?” And I get it, I do. But school isn't everything, right? Speaking of studies, I’ve got a test coming up. I’m trying to force myself to study, but I feel the urge to just text my friends instead. Like, “Let’s go grab food.” It’s tough when your mind is racing and you just want to be out there living life, but then there’s that nagging side of me that goes, "You should be responsible." Sometimes I just need to push through the discomfort. But then again, I can’t focus if I’m stressed out. And what about the future? That’s a whole other whirlwind. I feel the pressure to figure everything out, like what career path to take. I want to go into a field that allows me to connect with people but also feels secure. I keep hearing about internships and opportunities but then my anxiety spikes and I think, “What if I fail?” It’s exhausting, like I’m always on this tightrope. Balance is key, but how do you find it when your mind is always racing? I hope I can figure it out soon, but I know I’ll probably be here stressing over it for a while. Okay, I should probably wrap this up. I could go on forever, but I need to think about the test. And maybe get ready to hang out later. I think we’re doing dinner and that’s comforting. Ah, the joys of my life, it’s a whirlwind sometimes, but I wouldn’t trade my friends for anything.

I keep thinking about how loud everything is outside. This stupid construction noise that never ends. It distracts me from studying, from focusing on what I need to do for class. I have to finish that paper that's due next week. The one I’ve turned over in my mind a dozen times. Why is it so hard to organize my thoughts? I wish I could just sit down and type everything out without second-guessing every sentence like it’s some kind of test. It feels like there’s always something lurking just below the surface, like I’m not doing enough or I’m going to mess it all up. I wonder if everyone else feels this way or if it’s just me. Do they sit there and think about deadlines all the time or do they just go with the flow? I can't go with the flow. It’s funny how people say they thrive under pressure. I just get more anxious. My brain races in circles. Did I turn in that assignment? Did I remember to email the professor about that question I had? It’s like my mind is a hamster wheel, and I can’t find the off switch. I should probably write a to-do list, but I can’t even decide what to put on it because I start second-guessing what’s really important. Should I review for that exam next month or start on the presentation? Maybe I should just do both? No, that doesn’t make sense. I can only handle one thing at a time. It’s like I’m juggling but the balls are all on fire. The way people talk so easily makes me a bit envious. I hear them laughing and discussing their weekends. I can’t relate to the whole casual chit-chat thing. I’d rather be sitting somewhere quietly, just by myself or with a book. I don’t want to join in on the small talk. I don’t even know what to say. What’s the point, really? It’s the same loop of questions and polite nods. I can’t muster the energy to feign interest in things that don’t matter to me. It feels tiring. Sometimes I wonder how those outgoing people do it. Where do they get their energy? It’s all so exhausting to be “on” all the time. I don’t want to be in a big group or even a small group unless I know everyone really well. I’ve spent too many awkward moments at parties just standing there, unsure of what to do. Why did I even go to that one last month? It’s a blur now, just faces and noise, and that one friend who kept trying to pull me into conversations. Thank God for the bathroom excuse. I just wanted to get out of there, breathe, and think by myself for a minute. My brain runs a million calculations about conversations I missed, things I should have said, people I should have approached. It’s just a never-ending cycle of what-ifs. And then there’s the social media thing. Everyone is posting these pictures having the best time while I’m just sitting at home staring at my laptop. Like, am I missing out? I mean, I get it, we all have our lives and I can’t pretend to enjoy what I don’t. But sometimes I scroll through and it’s like a reminder of how different I am. Maybe it’s just a matter of preference. Maybe I’m not really missing anything. I hate feeling judged by those images, like they’re a reflection of what I should be doing. But still, I feel it. My anxiety doesn’t just come from social stuff, though. It’s everything. I have this constant feeling that I need to be doing more, achieving more, proving myself. I can’t help but think, what if I’m not enough? And the thing is, I know I can produce good work when I focus. I’m gritty. I know how to buckle down and get things done. But I get stuck in my head, worrying if it’s really good enough or if it will be what my professors expect. I can be my own worst critic. I never let myself enjoy that little burst of satisfaction after hitting that “submit” button. Oh, I’ll breathe a sigh of relief, but there’s always that nagging thought behind it. I wonder how many other people feel this way. Are they out there acting like everything is fine while they’re just as tangled inside? I wish I could find someone to share this with, but I struggle with that too. What would I even say? I’d probably just end up turning it into another monologue in my mind, dissecting it until it loses meaning. I bet I’m overthinking this again. Why can't I just let things go? Maybe it’s time to try a different approach, but then again, what would that look like? I’m not really one for change. Everything seems overwhelming. I’ll just stick to my routine and hope I figure it out along the way. I really need to get back to that paper.

okay so I keep thinking about how people are always rushing everywhere it’s like can’t we just take a minute to breathe I mean what’s the hurry I see it on campus everyone’s hustling to class or coffee or who knows where and I feel like I could just sit on a bench and watch them all day long it’s oddly calming like people are in their own little bubbles not really noticing each other but I’m noticing them and I’m okay with that it’s nice to be a spectator sometimes like the world is a movie and I’m just the quiet person in the back row holding popcorn which I hate eating in public because the sound of crunching feels too loud but I like the taste so what do you do anyway I’ve been thinking about joining a club or something they say to get involved but I don’t really know how to start it’s like there’s this big sign that says “welcome” and I’m standing outside shyly like do I even want to go in like do I want to meet new people or just stick to my small group of friends who are nice and safe but also kind of boring sometimes but that’s okay right because I don’t need excitement I don’t need what they call adventure that sounds tiring I just want to have a few good chats where we can laugh and talk about the same old things without any pressure, pressure is the worst I mean I don’t like it when my phone buzzes and it’s someone asking for something urgent it’s like can’t we just relax and take our time but I guess that’s the world today everything is fast-paced everyone’s connected but also disconnected how ironic is that and sometimes I think about how I prefer just texting instead of calling or seeing someone in person because it’s just less fuss less pressure I hate the awkward silence that sometimes comes when you’re in a group and no one knows what to say next it’s like come on someone just say something even if it’s silly but then I think maybe that’s why I don’t initiate things much I don’t want to be that person who throws out a random comment and it just flops like a fish out of water it’s painful to witness so I stick to laughing at other people’s jokes or just nodding along my friend was telling me about a thing that happened in class today and I was really trying to listen but my mind drifted thinking about lunch which is funny because I just had breakfast but I’m always thinking about food I don’t know it’s comforting maybe because it’s something I can control and you can always count on food to be there right or at least most of the time I mean sometimes I think about how nice it would be to just eat without thinking like just enjoy what’s in front of me but then I start thinking about the calories and the healthy options and what will my friends think when they see me eat that and it spirals into this thing like maybe that’s why I don’t go out to eat much because how do you choose I mean you want to pick a place that everyone likes and then there’s the menu and you feel pressured to make the right choice even though it’s just food and in the end everyone’s going to eat it anyway and I wonder why I care so much and then I think about avoiding it altogether just meal prepping and eating at home alone and that sounds kind of lonely but also nice like I could listen to music and not worry which I really like but then there’s this whole social side of eating that I’m missing out on and I wonder if it’s too late to change that but deep down I almost don’t want to because my routine is cozy and nice my tiny room with books stacked up everywhere sometimes I forget to put them back on the shelf and they just pile up on my desk like little towers and I like to think of them as my fortress of solitude even if they’re just textbooks and novels and papers from last semester that I need to recycle but I haven’t yet because I think maybe I’ll need them for something which I probably won’t but it’s like my brain doesn’t want to let go and I’m okay with that it’s just how I function my slow and steady way of doing things works for me I mean at least I’m not stressing over it or anything but then I think about how sometimes I’m missing out on opportunities maybe but there’s no real urgency is there who says I have to do what everyone else is doing don’t get me wrong I appreciate those who reach out and want to include me in plans but I just don’t have the energy for spontaneity I’m more of a “let’s plan this out” type of person but sometimes not planning is a good thing it leads to something unexpected but I just can’t shake that feeling of wanting to know what’s coming next like what’s for lunch what’s for dinner the schedule for the next week is there a quiz coming up I need to be prepared so yeah I suppose I’m stuck in my own little world that’s comfortable like a pair of old shoes but sometimes I wonder if there are better shoes out there waiting for me to slip into them.

So I found a sock in my backpack I could’ve sworn I lost like three weeks ago. Why do I always have mismatched socks? It's annoying. I can't focus on this study for some reason. I don't know if it's the weather or the sound of people talking outside my window. Everything just feels a bit too much. I want to crawl back into bed and pull the covers over my head. My roommate is probably out doing something social right now. I never really get the urge to just hang out with people. It’s like I feel that pressure building when someone asks me to go out. Like, what if I say no? I really hate disappointing people but I also don’t want to go, it’s such a struggle. It’s easier to say yes sometimes but then I end up regretting it. I’ve been thinking about how much I dislike parties. The noise, the lights, and everyone trying to engage in small talk. Just sitting there awkwardly smiling while trying to think of something appropriate to say. I’ll just nod and agree with whatever they say. It’s a good tactic, right? You just blend in. Everyone seems so comfortable, and I’m just there overthinking every little thing. I keep remembering that one time I went to this huge gathering and stood in the corner for an hour sipping on soda. I felt like I was invisible. I don’t know how people can just jump from one conversation to another. I feel like the more I think about it the less I want to engage. It’s almost paralyzing. Plus, I don’t have anything interesting to say anyway. I mean, I do things, but they’re pretty dull, and I really hate being judged on them. Ugh, and then sometimes people try to pull me into conversations when I’m not in the mood. Like, why can’t they take a hint? I know I’m smiling, but that doesn’t mean I want to chat about their weekend trip or whatever. I already feel overwhelmed thinking about it. And then there’s me trying to act calm and chill, but inside my head is racing. I can hear the clock ticking, I can hear the cars outside, I can feel my heart racing, and I just want to escape. I keep, like, second-guessing myself. I came across some article that said being agreeable might lead to better relationships, but what if I’m just a doormat? I feel so stressed out trying to please everyone all the time. I mean, I know I want to be nice and kind, but sometimes it feels like I'm getting steamrolled by people who don’t even care. I always end up helping friends with their problems and then feel drained afterward. What do I get out of it? I hate how much I worry about whether I’m doing enough or if I’m being a good friend. It’s exhausting. I know I should set boundaries, but when I think about saying no, my palms get sweaty. Also, I think I might have forgotten to reply to a text. I hope they don’t think I’m ignoring them. I get so worried about that. It just goes to show how much I’m constantly analyzing everything. Then I get stuck on things. Like, if I missed an assignment deadline or forgot to study for a test, it’ll haunt me for days. I’ll replay it over and over. And even small things—if I forget to say thank you or to smile properly, it eats at me. I wish I could just shake it off. Some people just move on, but I’m over here in my head full of regrets and what-ifs. The other day I thought I said something dumb in class and it just looped in my mind for hours. It was so embarrassing. Why am I like that? I can be sociable one minute and then just shut down completely. Maybe I just prefer my own company. I mean, when I’m at home, I can binge-watch those shows without feeling guilty, and I don’t have to put on this mask of being happy or social. I’ve developed this habit of checking my phone constantly. It’s like a safety net but also a trap. I scroll through social media, and I get upset seeing everyone’s perfect lives. I know it’s not real but it still makes me feel crummy. Everyone’s smiling and out there living their best lives, and I’m just here hoping I remember to buy groceries later and not let the milk go bad. What if I really am missing out on life? I get restless seeing everyone so carefree. I really wish I could open up more, but it’s like I want to keep everything inside. I get the sense of wanting to connect but then when it happens, I panic. I just want to be settled but it feels impossible sometimes. Why is it all so complicated? I suppose I’ll just stick to what I know for now, but this whole thing is giving me a headache.

Okay so I was thinking about how I really need to start planning out my week better, like I have all these study sessions and group meetups this week and I can't seem to find a good balance with my own time. It stresses me out, like I want to be there for everyone and make sure things go smoothly. I think I said yes to too many things again. I really enjoy being around people and helping them, but then I worry about not having enough time for myself. I don't want to let anyone down, but sometimes I feel like I'm being pulled in so many directions, like a plate juggler, and I'm about to drop a few. I should probably set boundaries but I hate the thought of someone thinking I'm not committed or willing to help. It makes me anxious just imagining that. Yesterday, we had this group project meeting, and everyone was so enthusiastic, which is good but also overwhelming. I was trying to keep track of all the ideas and suggestions but my mind was racing. I felt like I had to contribute something, anything, even if it wasn't fully formed just to keep the energy up. Everyone was so nice and supportive, not that I would expect any less, but still, it’s comforting to know. I felt the pressure though, like if I didn't say something smart or impactful, what would they think? Probably that I’m not good enough or something, and I hate that feeling. But then I remember that everyone else probably feels the same way, and we are all in the same boat, so why do I let this get to me so much? I can't stop thinking about the next social event, though. It's this mixer on Friday and I love those. I love meeting new people, seeing familiar faces, and just chatting with everyone. But then I think about what I’ll wear, and it seems silly but I always stress about that too. What if I look too plain? What if I'm overdressed? What if nobody wants to talk to me? It’s like my brain makes up all these scenarios that just spiral into feeling like I can't breathe. I know it can be fun. I want to dance and laugh and have a good time, but there's always this nagging worry in my mind. And don’t get me started on the group chats. I like being in them, really, but sometimes I feel flooded with notifications. It's hard to keep up with everything everyone is saying. Sometimes I wish I could just pop in and pop out without feeling like I need to respond immediately. But then I think if I step back, it'll seem like I don’t care, and I really do care. So I keep scrolling, trying to catch up on conversations and feeling my heart race a bit more. I know they all appreciate my input, and sometimes I dive in and share ideas, but what if it’s not enough? What if it’s just noise? Talking to my friends always helps. They know how to ease my mind, how to distract me. We’ve talked about going on more hikes or doing some outdoor activities. I'm always up for new things but sometimes I feel like it's a lot of pressure to plan those things out perfectly. What if it rains? What if someone backs out? What if we don’t have fun? I want everyone to have fun, to feel good. I guess that’s why I tend to overthink things. If I can’t control every little detail, I get anxious. I want everything to be just right. This leadership role I’ve taken on is exciting but so nerve-wracking. I like being able to help others but I’m constantly second-guessing myself. Am I being too pushy? Am I being effective? I want to be supportive and help others shine but I don’t want to overshadow anyone. That balance is tough, but I find myself almost obsessively checking in with team members, making sure they feel included. I hope I’m doing okay. I just want to be liked, I guess? I know I have good qualities, but the mistakes I’ve made in the past always echo in my mind. Like that one time I talked too much in a discussion and maybe didn’t let someone else finish, and I still think about that. I mean, everyone makes mistakes, right? So why can’t I just let it go? It hangs over me like a cloud, making me feel like I have to be extra careful in everything I do. I just want everyone around me to be happy, to feel valued, and I think I get too caught up in making sure that happens. I guess that’s just part of who I am.

I can't believe how loud that guy is in the next room. Do people really not understand how to keep their voices down when they're on the phone? It’s like they think they’re the only ones who exist in this universe. I guess I shouldn’t be surprised, people are so self-absorbed these days. I should probably do something about my own noise levels. I shouldn’t have played that music so loudly last night. I really don’t care what anyone thinks about my taste in music, but still. It’s just a constant battle. Anxiety is a part of life I guess. Nobody wants to hear that, though. They all want you to be this cheerful, happy person. What even is happiness? I don’t know. Just a facade. I can’t shake the feeling that I could fail at any moment. Like in class, when they call on you and you’re just praying they don’t pick you. This panic starts creeping in, and then it’s like my brain short-circuits. I see the professor’s mouth moving, and all I can think is please don’t let them say my name. My stomach drops when that happens. Sometimes I just want to hide. Social gatherings make me feel like I’m suffocating. Like, who decided mingling is a thing? I much prefer the quiet comfort of my own room with my books and my phone, scrolling through social media, absorbing all that pressure from everyone else’s perfect lives. It’s weird how you can feel so alone in a crowd. I don’t get it when people say they find joy in new experiences. Why? Why would I want to jump into a situation where I have no idea what’s going to happen? I’m fine with my routine. I know what’s going to happen next. I don’t want to try new foods or meet new people—I mean, what’s the point? It’s just more potential for disappointment. And oh, what if they don’t like me? What if I say something stupid? I can’t help but overthink every single detail. My brain is this constant cycle of self-doubt and dread. Friends say to relax, it’s not that deep. But it is! It is! Where are all the good shows on TV? I swear it’s all just reality stuff now. Who needs another season of dating shows? It drives me nuts thinking about how fake it is. Everybody’s just playing a role, trying to be someone they’re not, and then they act surprised when things don’t go as planned. If I could just watch a show that doesn’t make me cringe or feel like I’m going through social torture, that would be nice. I mean, do people actually find this entertaining? They just sit there, glued to their screens, absorbing all this nonsense. I just want a good plot. What happened to storytelling? I started reading this book last week and the characters are so boring. I can’t believe I wasted time on it. It’s like the author had no idea what people are actually like. I just want some authenticity. Is that too much to ask? Probably. People will shove whatever they want down your throat, art or not. And then if you voice your opinion, they look at you like you’re the villain. It’s infuriating. I’m not trying to hurt feelings; I just want to be honest. But who even cares about honesty these days? Eating alone is also one of those things that doesn’t bother me but people try to make it an issue. Why is it considered sad? I don’t need a social event every meal. Who decided that sharing food meant you’re happy? I like eating my cereal in peace, staring at the wall or my phone. But I guess if you don’t have someone to share your meal with, it means you’re lonely. I don’t feel lonely. Not all the time. Just sometimes when I see couples or friends laughing, sharing half-eaten fries, and it makes me think about things I'd rather not think about. Grades stress me out. I don’t care what my professors think about my potential because I’m pretty sure they’re all just judging. It’s like they’ve formed this little club and I’m just outside looking in. But whatever, people disappear into their own worlds, so why should I care what they think? I keep telling myself that if I just keep my head down and get through this semester, I can escape eventually. I hate group projects. I hate working with others. I want to do things my own way and not be dragged down by someone else’s inability to pull their weight. It’s always a disaster when you rely on someone else. Trust is a dangerous thing. Well, this is probably enough rambling for today. Just a lot of chaos. I wish things were simpler. I wish I didn't worry so much. Time to just dive back into my comfort zone—cereal and a good show, whatever that even means anymore.

Okay so I just remembered that pizza place downtown that has those amazing toppings I really love. I can’t believe I haven’t gone there in like a month, which is weird because I used to go every week with my friends after class. We would sit in that bright red booth by the window and just talk about everything, classes, or the latest Netflix obsession, or that crazy night out last weekend. I really miss those hangouts. There’s just something about sharing a pizza that feels so communal, so warm. And the way the cheese stretches when you pull a slice away – it’s just so satisfying. I wonder what toppings they have now. Did they come up with anything new? Sometimes it’s the little things that really make life feel special, like a good slice of pizza. Speaking of Netflix, I really need to catch up on everything I’ve missed. Everyone is talking about this show that’s based on some book, and I’ve read the book! I love the way stories come alive on screen, and seeing characters you’ve imagined turned into real people is such a trip. It’s fascinating how different directors interpret stories, like when they add new elements or twist the plot. I’m really open to all kinds of genres. I can flip from a rom-com to a thriller in a heartbeat and still be entertained. I remember when I watched that scary movie with my roommate; we were both screaming and laughing at the same time. That’s what I love about experiences with friends. Those little shared moments become cherished memories. What was I talking about? Ah yes, pizza. I think the last time I had it was when we ordered in during that study session for finals. I do love group study sessions, even if they end up being more social than productive. But sometimes just being around people helps me focus better. I think it’s because I enjoy the vibe of working together, the energy of everyone hustling to get things done. It’s motivating when everybody is engaged, even if we end up reminiscing about high school or that unforgettable road trip last summer. I can’t believe we managed to fit five people in that tiny car, it was so cramped but that made it fun. The music was blasting and we were all singing off-key. I love those spontaneous moments of joy. Then there’s the coffee we always drink during these sessions. I swear, coffee brings a whole new level of energy. I enjoy trying new brews, like when we went to that artisanal café that has the best lattes. The barista was super friendly and explained all the different beans and roasting processes. It’s nice when people share their passions like that, it makes everything feel more connected. I think I could talk to anyone about anything, really. Everyone has stories, experiences, and I love hearing about them. I guess that’s why I enjoy being around people so much. Oh, and that outdoor concert in the park last month! That was such a blast. It was a perfect day, everyone was vibing, the music was live, and I met so many new people there. I struck up a conversation with this girl who was really into indie bands, and we ended up swapping playlists. I love discovering new music through other people. It’s like expanding your own world, you know? Music is a universal language, it brings people together, and everyone can find something they connect with. I wonder if they’re having another concert soon. I should check the schedule, get a group together and go again. What’s on my to-do list? I need to remember to send that email to my professor about my project. I want to get his feedback, and I’m really excited about it. I put in a lot of effort into that research, and it feels rewarding to dive deep into something I’m interested in. It’s fascinating how much we can learn when we just explore topics that pique our curiosity. I could get lost in research for hours and hours. I have this unquenchable thirst for knowledge, and I want everyone to feel that too. I can’t stand when someone feels stuck or trapped in their own bubble. Everyone should be encouraged to explore their interests. I think I’m getting sidetracked again. But isn’t that part of the fun of these thoughts? Just letting them flow wherever they want to go? I love that sense of freedom in writing like this. I just feel energized thinking about all these interactions, the experiences, and how they weave together into this rich tapestry. That’s life, isn’t it? A bunch of moments, some sweet, some crazy, all adding up to something beautiful. It’s all so interconnected, and I find joy in exploring those connections. I need to get back to planning my week. There’s so much to do! But first, maybe a slice of pizza. Or two.

I keep thinking about how messy my room is but like I just can't muster the energy to clean it up I mean there's that pile of clothes on the chair that I swear gets taller every day and I forgot I even had some of them it's like shopping without spending money annoying I know but instead of cleaning I just sit here staring at my laptop screen scrolling through social media like an idiot everyone is posting their happy moments and I just feel like I don't belong to any of it especially when they post their group pictures I wonder if they don’t notice me or they just don’t invite me on purpose well probably both but honestly who wants to be in photos anyway I can’t stand how I look half the time and when I'm with people I feel like I just blend into the background like a ghost or something I wish I could just disappear but then I remember the last time I tried to socialize and it was a disaster I laughed too loudly at a joke that wasn’t even that funny and I felt all the eyes on me like I was on a stage completely exposed and everyone knew I was just awkward I hate being the center of attention it makes my skin crawl and then there are those people who are just so good at talking they can carry a conversation like it’s nothing how do they do that I can't even think of something to say without feeling like I’m overthinking every single word I can’t even keep up with what they’re saying sometimes my mind wanders and then I’m lost in my head trying to figure out what to say next and then I end up saying something stupid but I don’t even know if they noticed or if they just pretend to listen while they scroll through their phones I really hate when I’m trying to connect and they’re not there with me and it’s like talking to a wall what’s the point of even trying I think I’d rather stay home where it’s quieter and I can wear my pajamas all day without anyone judging me but then I feel guilty for not trying to go out and be social like everyone keeps telling me to do maybe I should just try harder but it’s exhausting and I feel like I’m pretending to be someone I’m not and who wants to do that for the rest of their life I’d rather be alone than put on a fake smile and pretend I’m having a good time when really I just want to crawl into bed with my headphones on and forget the world exists sometimes I get this overwhelming sense of dread like I’m missing out on something super important but when I think about going out it just sounds like the worst ordeal ever I can usually down a few shots and loosen up but then I end up saying things I regret and making a fool of myself which is even worse I don’t want to have to rely on alcohol to feel comfortable in my own skin but it’s either that or just watching everyone else have fun while I’m stuck in my own head just overanalyzing every little thing they say and do it’s such a drag I hate how I’m wired to worry about everything like how did I get this way I try to meditate and breathe but my mind is just a spinning wheel of anxious thoughts and it never shuts up oh and then there's school don’t even get me started on classes I can’t focus on anything half the time I just zone out and the lecture becomes a blur and the assignments pile up like a monster I can’t escape I can’t even figure out which deadline is coming up next and I just sit there staring at the syllabus like it’s written in a different language how do they expect us to manage all this it just feels like a setup for failure and I’m not even sure if I care anymore like does any of this really matter in the long run who cares about grades I mean I want to pass but it’s not like it’s the end of the world if I fail right but at the same time I can’t shake off that nagging fear that I’m wasting my life away and there’s no going back I should be doing more but what is more even is it joining a club or going to parties or just having normal conversations like normal people maybe I should just try being more open but I have no idea how to even start that feels way too risky what if I put myself out there and it blows up in my face and everyone laughs at me or worse they just ignore me completely like I’m invisible it’s scary to think about sometimes I wish I could just hit reset and start over but I guess there’s no magic button for that I just keep existing day by day trying to make sense of it all and hoping something will change but what if it doesn’t and I just stay in this rut forever it’s maddening.

I just remembered that time when I went to that concert last summer, it was so epic, my friends and I dancing like nobody was watching, I mean, that’s what it’s all about, right? There’s something about live music that just brings everyone together, right? Like we were all strangers before but then we were just one big happy group, singing along in unison. I love that feeling, being part of something bigger. It’s like an electric connection, and I totally thrive on that energy. I can feel it now just thinking about it. Anyway, I need to make a list of things to do today. I’ve been trying to stay organized because I always want to make the most of my time. I don’t want to waste a minute. Gotta hit the gym today too, I love working out with my friends, it’s a whole different vibe than going alone. Especially when they motivate me to push harder, and I just can’t imagine not having that support. I mean, who wants to face the treadmill alone when you can gossip and laugh while pretending to be serious about those last few reps? What’s on my agenda? Oh, I need to finish that group project. I volunteered to organize the presentation because I like making things look good. A little creativity can make a boring topic fun, and everyone is so grateful when I take the lead. I wonder how they’d feel about an infographic, something colorful and engaging. Not too many graphs though; those can be snooze fests. I'll suggest it in our group chat later. I love brainstorming with them. Everybody brings something different to the table, and I like seeing how ideas evolve, it’s like a mini magic show every time. Oh! And I should probably call my grandma. I haven’t talked to her in a while, she loves hearing about my life, and I love hearing her laugh. She always has the best stories from when she was younger. That generation really knew how to have fun without all the tech we have now. I can’t imagine a time without instant messaging or streaming. Also, I wonder if I should visit her soon; maybe I could surprise her with a little gift. Something simple, like her favorite tea or a new puzzle. She’s such a sweetheart and always worries about me, I mean, I’m not a kid anymore but I guess she can’t help it. Speaking of gifts, I’ve been thinking about my friend’s birthday coming up. I have to start planning now, I like to be really thoughtful with presents. She loves painting, so maybe I could find a cool art kit or take her to that pottery class we mentioned a while ago. The one where you can get messy and just create something, anything really. I think she’d love that. And I’d enjoy it too! I’d probably just make a bunch of weird bowls or something, but who cares, right? The joy of creating is what matters. Have I mentioned how much I love trying new things? I feel like there’s always something out there waiting for me to discover it. Like food! Ooh, I should try that new Ethiopian restaurant that opened downtown. I’ve heard fantastic things about injera and the communal dining experience! I’m all for sharing food. It's like an invitation for conversation, and I could totally use a good chat with some friends over some delicious dishes! And then there’s that trip everyone keeps talking about for spring break. I’m so in! Road trip vibes, blasting music, singing along, spontaneous stops? Sign me up. We should just throw some bags in a car, get snacks, and see where the road takes us. I love planning adventures like that. There’s a thrill in not knowing exactly what’s ahead, but knowing it’ll be fun. Just the thought of new experiences makes me excited! I should start a group chat to get everyone hyped about it. But before all that, I need to finish my readings for class. I can’t let myself get too distracted. I don’t mind studying, though. I like learning new things, especially if the subject is interesting. I’m not one to shy away from a big textbook either; I’ll highlight and jot down notes while wandering off into the margins with my thoughts. I’m so curious about everything; it’s like there’s a world of information waiting for me. Okay, I should focus. I’ve been rambling for what feels like hours. Maybe it’s the music in my head or the thrill of the day ahead, whatever it is, it’s nice just letting thoughts spill out. There’s so much to look forward to, and I hope I get to share it all with people I care about. That’s the best part, sharing experiences and making memories. Life is better when it’s filled with laughter and connection, the little moments that feel big, you know?

Okay so I just left that group meeting and honestly, I never understand why people act like they’re afraid to just say what they mean. I mean, it’s a waste of time to beat around the bush when we could be getting stuff done. People just want to avoid conflict, I guess, but I'm all about clarity and getting things resolved. I think it's better to be direct than tiptoe around everyone’s feelings. Life is too short for that. Like, just tell me what you need and I’ll figure it out, I’m good at that. The meeting felt so drawn out; we spent way too long discussing that one point that I thought was pretty straightforward. Just make a decision and move on, right? We could’ve spent that time brainstorming ideas for the next event instead. Which reminds me, I need to find a venue for the spring showcase. I’ve got some solid contacts, so I’ll reach out tomorrow maybe first thing in the morning. Gotta stay organized. The planner I got is really nice; it has sections for goals and lists which I love because I like things neat. I can’t stand messy. Anyway, after the meeting, Claire mentioned something about going out this weekend and I’m like, ugh can we not? I mean, I get it, socializing is good and all but sometimes it feels exhausting. I like to save my energy for things that matter, like, I’d rather work on my projects than go to a party where everyone’s going to pretend they’re interested in small talk. Plus, it’s not like I’m missing anything massive; it feels repetitive. There’s always that one person who tells the same story again and again. What’s with that? Do they think it gets funnier every time? I’m all for social engagement, but c’mon, if it’s not interesting the first time, it definitely isn’t interesting on the tenth. Okay, focus. I need to figure out how I’m going to manage my time better this week because I’ve got a ton of assignments due. I can’t believe I left that paper until the last minute. Really should’ve started earlier, but I work better under pressure anyway so I’m not too worried. I just need to crack down on my schedule. Gotta prioritize the stuff that actually contributes to my grades. College can feel so chaotic sometimes but I thrive in that chaos; it brings out my productivity. I should probably start getting into study groups; they can be useful if everyone is on the same page, but man, some people just don't pull their weight. I hate relying on others for my grades. Speaking of grades... I really don’t get how some people can blow off their responsibilities and still end up with decent grades. I’m over here, grinding away, and their approach is like, “Oh, I’ll do it later.” How do you even “do it later”? I don't know, but I guess that’s their choice. I’m definitely not going to let someone else’s laziness affect me, that’s for sure. I remember this one class last year, everyone was just waiting for the midterm to come around and I was already studying weeks in advance, making flashcards and writing notes. It felt great to see my hard work pay off with the top grade in the class. It’s all about the hustle, right? I enjoy competing with myself, aiming to be better and more efficient. It’s satisfying in a way, like, I get to tick things off my list and see progress. But then there are times I wish I could just chill for a moment. Sometimes I look at my friends as they relax and it makes me wonder if I’m missing out, you know? But then I think about the fact that they’re stressed about their assignments while I’m cruising through my to-do list like a boss. I guess it’s a trade-off. I’d rather keep my head down and achieve my goals than deal with the consequences of procrastination. It’s so easy to let things slip when you're not focused. I can’t wait until the finals are over, though. When I can finally breathe. I like to plan a little trip or something to reward myself after a tough academic push. Maybe head up to the mountains or something. Get some fresh air, go hiking, clear my head a bit. I need to recharge so I’m ready for the next batch of classes. Yeah, that sounds good. Outdoor time will be worth it, just need to keep reminding myself why I’m doing this in the first place. Everything is a stepping stone, right? I just have to keep checking off those boxes and everything will fall into place.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how I need to be more organized but then again who really has time for that right? There's so much happening around me, like my phone buzzing with messages. My friends are always asking to go out, and honestly, I love it. I thrive in those chaotic environments, laughter bouncing off the walls, the energy palpable. The music, the chatter, just feels alive. What was I even saying? Oh yeah, organization. I could probably benefit from a planner but what’s more fun is just rolling with it, seeing where the night takes me. I mean, I find spontaneity a thrill—who needs a plan? And then there’s this nagging sense of worry that creeps in sometimes. Like when I said yes to three different social events this weekend and now I’m like how am I going to juggle them? I’ve gotta be there for everyone, right? My friends rely on me to keep the vibe up and if I’m feeling stressed that’s not going to work. But what if I don’t go to all of them and then they think I don’t care? It’s like this internal battle, this back and forth of wanting to be the fun one, the reliable one, but also wanting to be in my room binge-watching some random show. Why do I care so much what they think? It’s exhausting, sometimes I wish I could just turn it all off. By the way, speaking of shows, I started a new one last night and it’s kind of wild. I can’t even remember the name. Some dystopian thing with people fighting for food or whatever. I’m really into that stuff, dystopian futures, alternate realities, they fascinate me. I just love the endless possibilities of what could be. But then again, I have to remind myself to temper my expectations. Too high and it becomes disappointing. That’s what I was thinking when I went out with Josh last week. He was all hyped about this new restaurant, and I was like yes let’s go, but then it turned out to be kind of mediocre? The tacos were soggy, but we laughed about it, right? I mean that’s the whole point of going out, enjoying time with friends even when the food sucks. Oh, and I can’t forget about the time I tripped in front of everyone, I was trying to impress this girl, trying to seem cool. I was just talking a big game about how I can dance and then boom—down I go. The laughter was contagious though. I think that’s why I can’t help but keep putting myself out there, even with my neurotic tendencies. I’m always worried but then in those moments of embarrassment, I find it’s okay to be goofy. Everyone has their moments, right? It’s funny, I can be super chill about certain things but then the smallest stuff can set me off. Like if someone cancels last minute, I spiral. I don’t know why—maybe it’s the fear of missing out? I hate being the last one to know what’s happening, the last one invited. It’s like a punch to the gut. I mean I understand people have lives, but can’t they at least keep me in the loop? I just wanna be part of the crew, you know? I guess being agreeable is a double-edged sword. I want to be liked so much that I sometimes forget to stand up for myself when I should. Just thought about how my semester is going too. Classes feel overwhelming sometimes. Like, I’m enrolled in this history class and the professor is so passionate but it feels like a lot of information. I get lost sometimes but then I remember that I can always study with friends. Study groups are my jam, I love being around people. But my friends might be getting annoyed with my distractibility, it's tough keeping focused when you get a group of talkative people together. Oh man, I’ve got so many ideas swirling around my head, half-formed plans, dreams, and lots of what-ifs. I wanna travel, go to new places, experience everything life has to offer, but there’s always that voice saying what if it doesn’t work out. What if I get lost or miss something important? I mean fear can be a bit paralyzing but then I remind myself that life is too short to let that dictate what I do. I admire those who just pack their bags and go; they inspire me. I love nights that turn into mornings, where we end up talking about life under streetlights, the city quieting down. Those conversations are the best. I'm all about those deep talks, sharing thoughts and feelings, and trying to understand the chaos of life. Yet here I am with my swirling thoughts, trying to pen it down, and all that comes out is this mess of emotions and stories. Life is a big chaotic mix, and I wouldn’t have it any other way, well maybe just a little more planning sometimes.

why can’t I just get it right just once that’d be nice so I guess I’m gonna try to stop worrying for a second just breathe and focus on one thing at a time I can do this I just have to start Okay so I’m here just typing away because that’s what I’m supposed to do right now not that I’m complaining because I actually love the sound of the keyboard under my fingers it’s almost like a song and I can feel the urge to express everything and everyone all at once which is kind of overwhelming but also exhilarating like when I walk into a crowded place and I can feel all the energy buzzing around me I love that I thrive on the connection with people even though sometimes I feel too much and it’s like the world is too loud and I can’t settle down into my thoughts like wait when was the last time I had a moment of stillness oh right during that meditation app I tried last week but I only lasted five minutes before I started thinking about that big project due soon it’s funny how I get all these ideas racing in my mind like a million conversations happening at once and I want to crowd-source my thoughts but I don’t always have the time to reach out to everyone I know and there’s so many of them I have friends everywhere and I rarely feel lonely but when I do it’s like a creeping shadow and I have to push it out and focus on what I can do right now like that time when I almost texted Sarah to talk about our plans for next weekend but then I got distracted by the thought of whether or not I should buy that dress for the party that I have no idea what to wear to I mean the options are endless and sometimes I wonder if I’m trying too hard to make people happy or if I’m genuinely just excited to see them again and it’s probably both but do they feel the nervous energy I have bouncing around inside me like a pinball machine I mean maybe they do maybe that’s why our conversations flow so easily because I can read them and they can read me and sometimes I just want everyone to be happy but then I think about my own happiness and that whole question of how I balance that out do I always need to be the one reaching out or can I sit back and let someone else take charge oh that reminds me of that movie I watched last week with my friends and how one of them took charge of planning the whole thing and they were a little stressed out about it and I just wanted to tell them it’s okay you don’t have to worry about making things perfect but then I worried about whether I’d come off as condescending and I don’t want to be that person I want to be supportive but also genuine so I’ve got to find that balance how do I even figure that out it’s like a puzzle and sometimes I feel like a walking contradiction because I can be so outgoing but then I also want to curl up with some quiet time and a good book because the constant noise of the world around me can be draining but then I think about all the adventures I missed out on when I just stayed in even if it’s not that big of a deal like that time I skipped a spontaneous road trip because I had a paper due and then everyone was posting about how much fun they had and I got that sinking feeling and I have to remind myself it’s okay to take care of my responsibilities but man the FOMO is real and I wish I could clone myself sometimes like when I want to be at three different events all at once and meet all the new people while keeping my old friends close but it’s like juggling and I feel like I could drop one of those balls at any moment it’s harrowing but exciting and oh I keep thinking about my next vacation the beach maybe or a big city like New York or Tokyo because those places make me feel alive and I want to explore all the cultures and the food is an adventure in itself I mean what if I find a new favorite dish that changes my entire perspective on food and life in general like that time I tried sushi for the first time and it blew my mind or how I discovered boba tea and suddenly I’m obsessed and it’s like the world keeps opening up new doors and I feel this rush of gratitude that I can experience that and connect with people who feel the same way so I wonder if this ever ends like does it get quieter or do I always have to lean in and feel everything as intensely as I do or is that what makes me, me and there’s a certain beauty in that chaos I guess even in the moments when my heart races and my palms sweat as I push myself to meet new people or try new things and maybe that’s okay maybe that’s just life and I want to embrace it fully because each laugh and each tear adds to the tapestry of who I am and yeah I’m here for it.

I can’t believe I forgot to submit that assignment last night. I mean it was right there in my to-do list. But I didn’t remember until I was already in bed. And now it’s like the weight of it is crushing me. What if I fail the course? I know that’s an extreme thought but it feels true right now. It’s always the little things that spiral into these massive catastrophes in my head. I can’t help it. I obsess over everything. I’m pretty sure I’ll spend the whole day worrying about how to fix it instead of actually fixing it. Even have a headache just thinking about it. It’s such a helpless feeling when things get out of your control. I am definitely better at planning than executing. Then there’s the group project due next week. There are these people in my group who don’t seem to take it seriously at all. It’s like I’m the only one who cares. Maybe I am too uptight? No. I can’t be. I don’t want to be. I want to get a good grade, and they’re just doing whatever. I mean, we need to come together and work this out. Everyone always says “let’s meet up,” and I just can’t. I don’t want to. So awkward. Why do people think these things are fun? I’d rather work alone, get it done myself, and not have to deal with everyone else’s quirks and personalities. People always tell me I should “just lighten up” like it’s easy. I like order and control. Chaos is the enemy. And have they seen the current state of the world? It’s chaotic enough without adding social situations to the mix. I hate being put on the spot. In class when professors ask for volunteers, I just sink lower in my seat like an ostrich trying to hide its head. And then everyone else just raises their hands, all eager, and I’m stuck in my own head thinking about how I could never just blurt something out like that. Sometimes I wish I could just be more like them, you know? Why can’t I just switch off the part of my brain that’s always monitoring my every move? Finding time to study seems impossible too. I can’t concentrate if there’s noise. People talking nearby, music, anything. Why do people think it’s okay to invade my space? Sometimes I think I must be the only person in the library who wishes for absolute silence. I see groups laughing or chatting, and I want to be part of that but at the same time, I just feel annoyed. I just want to be left alone to get my work done. Why is it so hard to have a decent environment without other people’s energy bleeding into mine? I see a bunch of people studying together but for me, that just sounds like a disaster waiting to happen. And social media, too. It’s all so draining.
