There’s this pressure to post and engage, but I just scroll through without saying anything most days. I think about how everyone else seems to connect effortlessly and wonder if I’m just missing something crucial. Maybe I’m just not cut out for all this openness. It's exhausting. Every time I think about joining a new group or club, I talk myself out of it. Why would I want to put myself through that stress? It’s funny how I always feel so exhausted after social situations, even brief ones. I sometimes wonder if people think I’m rude when I don’t talk much, but it’s just that my brain is running a marathon of thoughts, overanalyzing every single detail. Did I say the right thing? Was I awkward? Do they think I'm unfriendly? It’s all this mental clutter that I can’t just switch off. Then I think about my future. What kind of job will I have? I don’t want a job that involves constant interaction. That terrifies me. I need structure and predictability. I know I should probably network or something, but just the thought of reaching out to people makes me sweaty. And what if I mess up? I’d die. It’s the fear of failure that drives my perfectionism, and I hate it. Things need to be planned out perfectly. What’s the worst thing that could happen? I can’t even think about that. I feel like I can’t relax until I’ve accomplished the next task or two or three. Why is it that some people just glide through life? So carefree. I wish I could find that balance. Maybe I overthink, but it feels necessary. I don’t want to fall behind, and I don’t want to disappoint anyone or myself. The clock is ticking and each tick makes my heart race, reminding me of everything that needs to be done, all at once, all the time. I’ll just keep pushing forward, keeping my head down, and staying focused, even if I feel a little out of place in the world.

I’m staring at my notebook now and I can't help but think how important neat handwriting is. It’s like if the mess on the page reflects the mess in my brain. I always try to keep it organized, every letter uniform, and the thought of scribbling something down feels chaotic. I like order. Maybe that's my conscientiousness kicking in? Keeping track of everything. It's exhausting though. What if I mess something up? What if I forget to write something down that I was supposed to remember? I hate that feeling—like the time I forgot to turn in that assignment and I was so anxious in class. Everyone else seemed so put together, chatting, laughing, and I was just sitting there, nervously tapping my pen, feeling my heart race like it's a race I didn’t sign up for. Then there are group projects. Ugh. The worst. It’s like everyone wants to be involved and I just want to hide in the corner with my notes. Why can’t we just work independently? It would be so much easier. I always end up doing more work than I should. I mean, I’m not complaining about doing my part, I just wish people would take it seriously. I’ve seen people laugh at deadlines and it drives me insane. What if I’m reliable and they bail? I feel the anxiety creeping in, keeping me up at night over a project that’s weeks away. I'm stressed just thinking about it now. Then there are those times when I’m just minding my own business, maybe in a library or a quiet cafe, and someone approaches me. Why do they always seem to think I'm up for a chat? Just because I’m nice doesn’t mean I want to talk about the weather or whatever small talk nonsense they have in mind. It’s awkward. I can feel myself getting red just recalling those moments. I’ll nod and smile, and part of me is really just longing for them to leave me alone. It’s ridiculous. So many people thrive on social situations, and I just want to run away. I wonder why I’m like this, why the thought of chatting feels so heavy. And then there are the moments where I see something on social media. I scroll through my feed, feeling this strange mix of curiosity and dread. What are people thinking? They seem so carefree and happy. Am I too serious? I worry about what everyone is doing, where they are going, if they’re having fun. It can feel isolating. I’ll compare myself to their perfect pictures. It gets unhealthy. Someone will post about a party and I think, should I go? But then the idea of being around so many people makes me feel queasy. What if I say the wrong thing? What if I trip and fall? What if I don’t know anyone? Sometimes I’ll convince myself to go, but I end up just standing back, watching everyone else. I also think about my friends. I love them but sometimes their extroverted ways wear me down. They want to plan outings, and I get the feeling that they don’t really get why I hesitate. It’s not that I don’t want to see them; I just can’t muster the energy all the time. I feel bad saying no but I also have to protect my peace, right? Oh, the guilt! It’s like a tightrope walk balancing my needs against theirs, always trying to ensure that they’re happy and I’m not crumbling because of it. Things like movies, like how everyone else is talking about the latest blockbuster while I’m still stuck in my comfort zone, rewatching something that feels safe. I don’t want to dive into something new; it feels too risky. I mean, what if I don’t like it? I’d rather just stick to what I know. Maybe that’s why I struggle with creativity, with trying new things. It feels overwhelming to even think about trying to be open to new experiences. And with all this anxiety swirling around, I feel like I have to distract myself with lists, planning every little detail, writing them out so that I can check them off. That’s how I find comfort. If I have a plan, it makes me feel ready. I have sticky notes everywhere, reminders scribbled in margins, even my phone pings at me to stay on track. I’m managing, I guess. Just trying to keep it all together, piece by piece, even if my mind feels like one giant puzzle missing half the pieces. And I think that’s just how it is sometimes, right? Just hanging on while everyone feels free to soar.

I just remembered that time I went to that party last month, so many people, overwhelming but in a good way, you know? Everyone laughing and dancing, and I was just in the middle of it all, soaking up the energy like a sponge. People pulling me in every direction, saying “come over here” or “let’s take a picture,” and I loved it. That’s the best part, the connections. I can’t understand why some people prefer staying home alone. I mean, what’s the fun in that? The thrill of being with friends, new friends, old friends, strangers who will become friends, it’s all so exciting! And the music, oh my god, the music was bumping! I don’t even remember what songs were playing but it doesn’t matter, everyone was just there to vibe, dance, and be present. I feel like I could talk to anyone about anything. Like, even the weird guy in the corner. I remember I met this one guy who kept talking about his lizard collection. Who collects lizards? But I just smiled and nodded and asked questions because he was passionate about it. That's what people want; they want someone to listen. I don’t get why people can’t just be open and share their weirdness. I mean, I’m out here sharing my life and my random thoughts all the time! Then there was this girl, so bubbly, with bright red hair and a laugh that could probably break glass. We ended up chatting about nothing particular but I could tell she was just as high on life as I was. It’s like we were feeding off each other’s energy, just laughing and smiling. We even talked about planning a trip together, which if I’m honest, I probably won’t follow through with. I mean, it sounds fun but life is just so… busy. But who knows, spontaneous trips are the best kind, right? You just grab your friends and hit the road. I wish everyone saw the beauty in just letting go sometimes instead of worrying about the little things. God, speaking of little things, this semester is flying by. I should probably be studying, but who really wants to do that? I mean, I’ve got deadlines creeping up and yet here I am thinking of the next party or the next hangout. My friends are planning a game night this weekend and I’m already thinking about the snacks. You can’t have a game night without pizza and way too much soda. I really need to make sure I get some snacks that everyone likes though, can’t have anyone left out, that’s the worst! I hate it when someone feels excluded; it bothers me so much. Sometimes I worry that I don’t take things seriously enough. I mean, my professors constantly talking about deadlines and responsibilities are giving me anxiety. But at the same time, I just can’t help but think that life is too short to be all business. I want to enjoy this time. It’s fleeting! I could sit and study but then I’d miss out on making memories. I’d miss out on sharing laughs with friends. I’ve always felt that people matter more than grades, you know? Maybe that’s a bit reckless, but I like to think it’s just living in the moment. And then there’s the balance with classmates, networking, making connections for later. Group projects can be so annoying sometimes. Teamwork sounds great in theory but not when you’re the one doing all the planning and delegating. I just want everyone to get along. Can’t we all just be friends? I mean, isn’t that the point? I’ve always tried to see the best in people. It can be exhausting because not everyone is like me. Some people are so caught up in their own heads but that’s fine. I get it. I just wish everyone could be a little more chill and friendly, like let’s just have a good time together! Okay, talking about good times makes me think of summer. What’s the plan? Beach trips, barbecues, late-night bonfires with friends, just hanging out telling stories. I love those nights when we sit around and someone brings out a guitar, and suddenly we’re all singing along, even if we don’t know the lyrics. Just that feeling of togetherness, that warm vibe. I want to soak it all in. But I know school will kick into high gear again soon and who knows, I might not even have time for anything fun. Ugh, reality check, right? Anyway, I really need to figure out how to balance things better. It’s all about finding that middle ground. But for now, I’ll keep living in the moment and enjoying my friends and the laughter. I’ll deal with the rest later.

Okay so, I'm just sitting here thinking about how much I love meeting new people. Like, every time I walk into a classroom or a party, my heart races a little bit in anticipation of all the cool conversations I could have. It’s wild how many different perspectives are out there, and I just want to soak them all in. Yesterday, I bumped into Sam from my sociology class at the coffee shop, and we ended up talking for two hours about our favorite documentaries. I never knew he was so into true crime, which honestly I could talk about for hours. The way people can get so passionate about their hobbies or interests is just fascinating to me. I could do a whole podcast on that, inviting different guests to share what they love. Wouldn’t that be fun? Then there’s that project I have coming up with the group. It’s due next week, and I think we have a solid idea. I could really see us doing well on it. I mean, we all clicked right away, and I just love how easy it is to brainstorm together. Normally, I feel like nobody knows what they want to say, but this group just bounces ideas off one another. I wonder if I should suggest we meet for pizza this weekend to wrap things up. It’s a good way to avoid the stress of finalizing everything in a cramped library. And honestly, who doesn’t like pizza? Plus, it gives everyone a chance to relax and really vibe well before we hit the panic button for the final edits. Speaking of stress, I can’t believe how little it affects me. I mean sure, I get nervous before exams, but it’s more like an excited energy than anything else. I’ll walk into that exam room, and it’s like my brain switches on, and I can just flow. And then I see classmates with their eyes wide, tapping their pencils on the desks because they’re freaking out, and I just want to remind them to breathe. I wonder if I could start a study group to help others. Just some chill sessions where we could go over material together and maybe throw in some fun games to lighten the mood. Everyone could use a little less anxiety, right? I should probably get my calendar out and start organizing things more. I have a planner, and I color-code everything to stay on top of due dates and appointments. It seems a bit extra, I guess, but I love it. It feels satisfying to look at all the colors. My friends tease me about it, but it's not like I just plan boring stuff! I include fun events too. Last week I wrote down “Hiking with the crew” in bright green ink. I can’t wait for that—it’s going to be epic! Fresh air, beautiful views, and snacks. Did I mention snacks? Always got to have good snacks. I should bring those protein bars I love. Oh, and I need to check in with my roommate. She’s been a bit quiet lately, and I’m not sure if it’s school stress or something else. I really want her to feel like she can talk to me. We’ve always had an open-door policy, and I think it’s important to keep that line of communication clear. I’m sure we’ll go for some ice cream soon and have a longer talk. Ice cream makes everything better, right? No one can resist ice cream. Then there’s this upcoming concert I’m excited about. Everyone’s gonna be there, and I can’t wait to just lose myself in the music with friends. There’s something about live music that hits different. You know? The way the crowd sways, sings along, and even the vibe of the venue adds to it. Plus, I might meet new people in the crowd! I should probably look up the setlist beforehand, though. It’s always great to know a few songs, so I can sing along. I wonder how many people show up just for the Instagram photos, but honestly, I’m not even mad if they do. Everyone experiences things differently. I love how spontaneity can lead to amazing moments. A couple of weeks ago, I impulsively joined a salsa class with a friend, and it was so much fun! I didn’t think I’d be into it, but the energy in that room was electric. Everyone was laughing, and we were all stepping on each other’s toes, which made it even funnier. And at the end of the session, we all paired up for a mini dance-off. It felt great to let loose, and I think I’ll definitely go back next week. I’m kind of rambling now. Is this too much? Whatever. I guess this is how my mind works—just jumping from one thought to another without any brakes. I think I’ll try to write more like this. It feels liberating, to just let it spill out. More of it, less worry. What’s next? I have no idea, but it’ll be fun finding out!

Okay so I always think about how weird it is that most people love small talk but it feels like such a waste of time like I’d rather have a deep conversation about the meaning of life or the latest theory in astrophysics but everyone just wants to chat about the weather like can we skip that and dive into something real. I can’t stand the superficial interactions at parties. They seem pointless. I mean, I want to connect, but not like that. If I ever go to a party, it’s like I watch everyone else work through the social dynamics and I’m just standing in the corner thinking about how interesting it is that people can change their personalities depending on who they’re around. Why can’t they just be themselves? I’d rather talk about the universe or something totally random like conspiracy theories, which I find fascinating even if they’re outlandish. Sometimes I think about how much I love learning new things. So there’s this book I’ve been meaning to read it’s all about ancient civilizations and how they were connected through trade routes like did you know that the Silk Road didn’t just move goods but ideas too? People were exchanging concepts, philosophies, religions I mean that’s just so exciting and it’s like we still do that but just in different forms like Twitter and TikTok are like the modern Silk Road and there’s this huge exchange of ideas and memes which are sort of like our new folklore can you imagine? Also, I went to this lecture last week that was all about the impact of technology on modern relationships and wow it got me thinking like do we really connect or are we just creating illusions of connection through screens? I can’t help but wonder if these digital interactions are any less meaningful than the face-to-face ones that we had before smartphones disrupted everything. I guess it depends on the person and how deep they want to go. Do people even want to explore that? Sometimes it feels like they don’t they just scroll through their feeds, liking photos of food and babies and puppies and I mean who doesn’t love puppies but come on we’re talking about the future of humanity here. I’ve tried to engage some friends in these discussions and they get uncomfortable like do they not see the deeper implications of where we’re headed? We’re always online and yet somehow more disconnected than ever. I think it’s important to recognize these shifts and try to adapt while still maintaining some authenticity. Speaking of authenticity, I once read that being truly yourself is the hardest thing to do but it’s also the most rewarding. I sometimes feel like I’m on this journey to figure out what my true self is or maybe it’s more about embracing all these different aspects of myself and not boxing anyone into one identity. I don’t know if that’s even possible. Then there are those times when I get tired of all the heavy stuff and I just want to zone out and watch some mindless TV show. Lately, I’ve been binging this absurd reality show where they put a bunch of strangers on an island and watch them try to survive and why do we find that entertaining? But there’s something about seeing people struggle and act like it’s real life that just pulls me in. Like they really believe they’re in danger, but are they really? And speaking of reality, I’ve been thinking about traveling more because I feel like I haven’t seen nearly enough of the world. Every time I look at a photo from a different culture or landscape I just feel this immense pull to pack my bags and go explore. I mean there’s so much out there, so many different ways of life and I can’t help but be curious. How do people in Japan experience life compared to those in Brazil or Iceland or Egypt? If I could just hop on a plane right now I think I would. My friends don’t always get it. They’re so comfortable in their routines and their little circles and I’m just here like let’s expand our horizons and break out of the norm. I think if we travel together we’d learn so much more about each other but there’s always that fear of the unknown like what if we get lost or what if we don’t get along? But I think conflict is part of life. It’s healthy to argue about things sometimes. Anyway, I guess at the end of the day I just want to have experiences that challenge me and push me to think and grow and be okay with the discomfort of not knowing everything. Maybe that’s why I struggle with conforming because I crave that openness to new ideas and experiences without necessarily needing to fit into a box. What’s the point of living in a box anyway?

I’ve been thinking about that weird conversation last week where I tried to explain the concept of existential dread to my roommate and they were just looking at me like I had three heads. I want to know if it’s just me or if a lot of people feel this way—like, life is just a series of moments and we’re chasing after something that doesn’t exist. I wonder if we overcomplicate things, or if that’s just the human condition. Like why can’t we just be happy? Is happiness even a thing or just a construct? Anyway, I started reading this book about minimalism. It’s fascinating how people can just decide to live with less stuff, get rid of everything that doesn’t spark joy—what does that even mean? Does it mean I should throw out my collection of old magazines? But what if I want to flip through them someday? There’s something comforting about keeping things around. I’m a bit of a hoarder in that sense but I like to think I curate my collection, you know? The right amount of nostalgia mixed with practicality. Random thought: what if I’d chosen a different major? I’m in psychology, and I like it, but sometimes I wonder if I should’ve gone for something like philosophy or sociology. They both seem more abstract, and I love abstract things. Thinking outside the box is my jam, like I've always been the kid in school who would go off on weird tangents during class discussions and the teachers would just nod, probably thinking, “where is this going?” I never really cared too much about fitting in. It's amusing how people are so hung up on being liked. I mean, do I really care if someone finds me agreeable? Not really, as long as I do my thing and keep my standards high. Speaking of standards, I’ve noticed that my friends are more laid-back about deadlines. I am so rigid about it. It’s not a control thing, it’s just how I work. I got that paper in a week early, and they were like “Why?” and I was just like, "Why not?" I feel stressed even thinking about it being left until the last minute. I guess that’s where that conscientiousness really shows. I can’t help it. It’s ingrained. Even if there’s some part of me that wants to break free from that societal structure and just go wild. But no, I can only imagine how chaotic that would be. I can picture it — papers everywhere, unfinished projects piling up, and just a general sense of chaos that would really do my head in. I need some level of organization, but I like to keep my options open. I can’t be too constricted. And then there’s the idea of travel. The places I want to visit are all so different. One moment it’s Tokyo with all its neon lights and bustling energy, the next it’s some quiet village in the countryside of Italy where I can just absorb the art and history. I want to experience it all, but is it really possible to do everything? I feel like I’m just scratching the surface sometimes. It’s like I’m open to new experiences but also cautious about them, weighing the pros and cons in my mind before I leap. I could probably spend entire weekends just planning hypothetical trips. Could it be overwhelming? Yeah, but there’s a thrill in not knowing what’s next. Something else that puzzles me is social media. Sometimes I feel like it’s a trap, like I’m watching people live their lives so colorfully, and I’m here in my structured bubble just thinking about it. Do I even want to present myself to the world like that? I mean, I share things here and there, but I’m not about to pour myself out like some open book. Maybe it’s because I’m cynical about it; I see through the curated images and perfect captions. People have depth, but we only show a slice. I get it, though. Who wants to post their messy kitchen or their existential crisis? But I can’t help but think that there’s beauty in the mess, and the chaos of life is what binds us all together. Wow, speaking of messes, I really need to clean my room. It's become this weird battlefield of old books, laundry, and snack wrappers. I get distracted by so many things, like I’ll sit down with the goal of cleaning and then suddenly I’m five chapters deep into a book I didn’t even plan to read. I can’t focus long enough which is the opposite of how I am with academic work. I guess it’s the freedom of my personal space; it’s not subject to the same rules. Oh, and that brings me back to the study I read about—how creativity often thrives in chaos. Maybe I should embrace the mess? It’s an interesting thought. I could make it a sort of laboratory for my ideas. Anyway, I just hope whatever I end up creating reflects that openness I feel inside. I want people to see that there's more than meets the eye. I guess that’s it. What even is the answer to all these questions?

Yesterday was wild, I met up with Sam and Jamie at that new taco place. Honestly, the vibe was great, super colorful, music pumping, just what I needed. Tacos were huge, and we were laughing about everything—school, life, that one time Jamie tried to make a spicy salsa and nearly burned his tongue off. We tend to do that, just jump from one silly story to another, weaving in and out like a chaotic dance. It's hilarious, though I sometimes wonder if we really listen all the time or if we’re just waiting for our turn to talk. I think we all get each other which is cool. Then, halfway through our meal, this guy at the table next to us spilled his drink. Serious disaster. He was embarrassed, but honestly, everyone was cracking up. I mean, it’s just a drink, right? We started making up the wildest backstories for him, and I couldn’t help but wonder how dramatic could someone’s life be to turn a simple spill into a cinematic moment? Probably not that dramatic, but fun to think about. I’m just glad nobody was mad, you know? It’s just a drink and we’re all here to enjoy good food with good friends. After we finished eating, we just wandered around the area. There was a small street fair going on, booths selling handmade crafts, little food stands—I felt this surge of excitement just walking through the crowd. The energy was contagious, people were just smiling everywhere. I couldn’t help but chat with some random strangers selling jewelry that looked a little quirky but fun. I picked up a bracelet that looked like a bunch of colorful gummy bears. I mean, who wouldn’t want to wear that? Totally silly, but it made me laugh. I don’t even wear bracelets often but I had to have it. It was five bucks, a steal for a little piece of whimsy. So, then we spotted this balloon artist—he was incredible! I swear he was making balloon animals really fast, like a machine. I started talking to him, asking how he got into that. He told me some story about how he started doing it at kids’ parties but then realized he loved the crowd. That made me smile. It’s like, why not? Who wouldn’t want to be at the center of that joy? Everyone wants to laugh and have fun, and if you can make that happen with balloons, more power to you, man. I would totally be that person if I could. Imagine going to parties and just bringing balloons to make everyone happy. I wonder what other talents I could develop. I drifted off thinking about how people in our lives could use more joy. Like, I have this friend who always seems to see the downside of everything, and it's exhausting sometimes. I want to cheer him up but I have no idea how. It’s like he wears this heavy cloud over his head. I mean, I get it, life has its stresses, and school is tough, but come on. I can’t help but think that just getting out and talking to people could help. I feel like we all need a little more social connection, even if it’s just silly small talk with strangers. On the other hand, I can’t be the one to fix everyone’s problems. That’s not how this works, right? I can keep inviting him out, but if he doesn’t want to join, there’s nothing I can do about it. I feel for him. At the same time, I have to live my life too. I mean, why should I worry to the point I stop enjoying things? Sometimes I think I live in a bubble of joy that’s a little too buoyant compared to the reality of others. But I like my bubble, it feels nice and warm. Right now, I’m thinking about that project due next week. I really should get started on it, but honestly, the thought of organizing all of that makes my head spin. I can’t seem to sit still long enough to write papers anymore. I want to be out with friends, living it up, not stuck typing away in my room. I’ll get to it. I promise. Or maybe I’ll just pull another all-nighter, right? I can do this. I’ll figure it out, I usually do. I guess all I need is a little time, maybe some music. Music always helps. Okay, I need to wrap this up, I can hear my roommates getting back. I love when we all just hang out and talk. It’s a wild mix of conversations, sometimes I wonder how we all ended up together. But it works, and that's what matters. Alright, let’s go!

All right, so I’m thinking about this weekend coming up. It’s going to be great. My friends and I are planning a big get-together, and I just love those. There's this awesome potluck happening. I really need to remember to bring that pasta salad recipe my mom gave me. It’s so good, and everyone loves it. I mean, who doesn’t love pasta and creamy dressing? Plus, I’ll get to catch up with everyone; it feels like we haven’t all been together in ages. It’s so important to maintain those connections. I’ve been doing a lot of texting, like checking in on everyone, seeing how they’re doing. I want to make sure everyone feels included. And speaking of connections, I need to get that group chat going again for our class project. We’re working on this presentation, and I can tell that some people are a bit more laid-back about it, while others are freaking out. I love everyone on the team, but I just want to make sure we nail it. I can’t stand the feeling of being unprepared, you know? Maybe I should take the lead and set a timeline for us—like deadlines for when we need to finish each part. I think that would help. I like organizing things. It makes me feel accomplished when everything falls into place. I thrive in those situations! I really hope the weather stays nice for the potluck. It’s so much better to be outdoors. The vibe is just more fun. We can grill, play some music, maybe even get the frisbee out. I need to remember to bring my speaker too. It’s portable and sounds fantastic. I should make a playlist—everyone likes a bit of variety. Can’t leave out the classic throwbacks, right? Those always get people moving. I’m so ready for the dancing and laughter, it rejuvenates me. I live for moments like that. It’s like every bit of stress just fades away when I’m surrounded by good friends. Then there’s also finals coming up. Not as excited about that. But I feel pretty good about my classes. I’ve been studying, staying ahead of the game. I made this really detailed study schedule. I don’t want to get caught off guard. I’m not worried at all. I mean, I’m confident that I’ll do well. I have a good grasp on the materials, and I’ve always found that if you break things down into manageable chunks, it’s much less overwhelming. I think my teachers appreciate my diligence, too. They’re always seeing me at office hours or sending emails with questions. It’s important to show you care about your learning. Sometimes I wonder if I should mix things up a bit, try some new clubs or activities. It’s just that I’ve got my go-to ones, and they’re all great. The volunteering with the local animal shelter is so rewarding. I love helping out there. Those dogs and cats need all the love they can get. Plus, I’ve made friends with the staff, and we always have the best chats while we’re cleaning or playing with the animals. And honestly, who wouldn’t want to be surrounded by cute puppies all day? It brings me joy just thinking about it. Plus, I’ve got my fitness routine to stick to. I’ve been going to the gym regularly and attending group classes. Working out with others is such a motivator. We cheer each other on, and it just feels good to be part of that energy. I love the classes; the instructors always have new ideas to keep things interesting. I’m definitely more of a planner with my workouts, though. I have a set schedule that I try to stick to. Makes it easier to stay on track. Okay, so let’s talk about food again because that’s always on my mind. I’ve been meal prepping like crazy lately. It’s all about the balance, right? I love making healthy meals, but I can’t resist a good dessert either. I’ve tried to find recipes that satisfy my sweet tooth without going overboard. I mean, moderation is key! I also like sharing what I make with my roommates. They’re always impressed. I just enjoy being able to contribute in little ways. Makes the living situation feel more communal. I should also probably start thinking about summer plans. What are my friends doing? I know a few are going home, but others want to travel. I think it’d be awesome to go somewhere new, but I’m also that person to keep things simple. Like, maybe just a road trip to a beach or something. That sounds fun too! I love the ocean. And I can picture us blasting music, just laughing and bonding in the car. Those moments always create the best memories. I can’t wait. Oh, and I should probably wrap things up here. I feel like I’m so scattered, but that’s honestly how my brain works sometimes. Just jumping from one thought to another, but that’s life, right? So many things to look forward to, and I know I’ll make the most of it all!

Okay so this is happening. I was just thinking about how I always end up in the craziest situations like the time I joined that improv group on a whim. I remember just walking by and hearing laughter and people shouting out silly prompts and I had to join. I mean who doesn’t love a good laugh? It’s like the best social glue ever. There’s this rush of creativity, like everyone just lets go of their inhibitions and becomes this wild version of themselves. I totally thrive in that environment, you know? Just bouncing off ideas, no judgment. It’s freeing. Speaking of improv, I just saw this meme about how “yes, and” is basically the key to life. Like, yes, and I want to try that! Can you imagine? Just saying yes to everything? It’s kind of scary but also super exhilarating. I mean, what if someone said, “Let’s go skydiving” or “Let’s start a band”? A band would be fun but I can barely play the ukulele. Maybe just some tambourine shakes? But why not? I could be the tambourine queen. The world could use a little more musical chaos. And then there’s travel. I need to travel more. I’ve got this itch to hop on a plane and land somewhere exotic. Like, take me to the beaches of Thailand or to the markets in Marrakech. I want to experience new cultures and meet interesting people who have stories that could blow my mind. I can imagine walking through the streets, trying new foods, tasting spices that tingle your taste buds in a way you never knew was possible. The street food appeals to me—grab a skewer and chat with a vendor. People are so fascinating. Everyone has a story, right? That makes me think about human connection and how it’s this intricate web of shared experiences. I can just walk up to someone and say, “What’s the most ridiculous job you’ve ever had?” and BOOM—instant conversation. I love it. It’s like peeling back the layers of someone’s life and discovering these little nuggets of humor and hardship. I met this guy once who worked as a clown at kids’ parties but his fear of balloons was unreal. Who knew? A clown scared of balloons! The irony makes me laugh just thinking about it. I also think about how people can be so different. Like, my roommate is super organized and has color-coded everything—her planner is practically a work of art. I’m always like, “What’s the plan?” and she pulls out this regimented schedule while I’m over here just riding the wave of spontaneity. I feel bad for missing deadlines sometimes, but then I’ll have this burst of creativity and whip up something amazing at the last moment. Usually, it works out, but I’m definitely not winning any “Most Reliable” awards for that. But I love the unpredictability of life. It’s like a box of chocolates or something cliché like that. I thrive on it. One night could be dancing the night away at a club and the next could be a quiet dinner with friends, talking about our wild dreams and schemes. We could end up planning a mini-road trip on a drive with random stops at quirky roadside attractions. The world needs more randomness, less planning! Getting lost could lead to the best adventures. Let’s forget the map and just drive. Then there’s that feeling of being around people and the energy vibrating in the air. I pick up on everyone’s mood. If someone’s down, I genuinely want to know why. I want to be that person who can lift someone’s spirits with just a joke or two. Laughter is the best medicine, right? Sometimes I ramble on with the most absurd jokes and they either land perfectly or flop miserably, but I can never resist trying. Who doesn’t love a bit of chaos at a gathering? Let’s turn everything into a game! Quirky charades? Yes, please. I’ll impersonate a llama if it gets everyone laughing. And how can I forget about the importance of kindness? I can’t even imagine a world where we don’t just help each other out. I hold the door open for strangers and smile at people as we pass by. A simple “how’s your day?” can make a difference. This one time, I paid for a stranger’s coffee because, why not? Their face lit up as if I’d changed their whole day. Isn’t that the best feeling ever? I love creating those little moments of happiness everywhere I go. Sometimes I feel like a cheerleader, always rooting for others to shine. Okay, wow, time flies when you’re just spilling thoughts onto the screen. I could go on for hours, really, but I guess I’ll wrap this up. There’s so much more to explore and think about. Life is a grand adventure, and I’m all in, ready to soak it up like a sponge.

okay so i was just thinking about how many plans I have for this weekend which is kind of insane but also so exciting I mean I love having things to do like back to back to back I thrive on that energy you know? I have a brunch with friends like a big group—maybe ten or twelve of us—it's always a blast, tons of laughter and the best part is the different foods everyone brings I mean you think pancakes are simple but you haven't had a pancake until it's covered in homemade strawberry syrup right? I need to look up that recipe again because I had one at this little diner last month that was just out of this world and now I want to recreate it for the brunch but I'm also thinking what if I just bring mimosas instead because who doesn’t love a good mimosa especially when you can mix and match different juices like peach and orange and it just feels so festive makes me wonder how many people will actually show up because the last time we tried to do a big brunch like this we ended up with like half the crowd but then again those who come they really come to enjoy it I mean I don't like half-assing anything it's all or nothing right I think that’s how I operate in everything like I can't really understand how people can just do things half-heartedly like why would you bother but then again not everyone is like me and maybe that’s what makes life interesting like we all have these different quirks that lead to wild combinations but my brain is racing thinking about the next week already like I have a presentation in sociology that I’ve barely started on and I need to come up with a concept that’s both unique and relevant and I’m feeling a bit of pressure but I thrive on that pressure so it’s kind of like a double-edged sword and helps me to focus at least I hope so I have a bunch of ideas swirling around in my head what if I throw in a comparison between different cultures around the world and how they view community and connection that could tie in nicely with my own experiences living in this crazy global society today wow I just got really excited about that idea because I can really turn that discussion into something engaging I mean nobody wants to sit through a boring lecture right? People love stories and connections—things that really make life pop, and then I can intersperse it with anecdotes like the time I volunteered at that community center last summer and how different it was from my own upbringing like the kids were so eager to learn but also so full of life I wonder if I’ll have enough time to flesh that out before everything else starts piling on because after class I need to hit the gym I’ve been trying to stay consistent with my workouts because it helps me clear my mind I feel like when I don't hit the gym I get all anxious and jittery I mean maybe it’s the high energy I have just needs an outlet you know? But I need to adjust my schedule for the gym because I might have to switch it to mornings but I’m not a morning person like at all I wish I could be one of those people who jumps out of bed ready to seize the day but I need like a solid hour to wake up or maybe I could just set a few playlists and call it a day like pump myself up before working out but also it’s the social aspect that makes it fun like chatting with friends after a class or joining a new group that meets up which is such a good way to meet new people and I love that aspect of being social like how people connect and share experiences but sometimes I just want to do things on my own and go to different places because there's a whole world out there and I want to explore it but I also feel so settled here at school that it’s hard to pull away it's like a balance between adventure and routine and routines can be comforting but at the same time I crave novelty like trying that new restaurant downtown that serves fusion tacos I love the idea of blending cultures through food like that’s so creative and maybe I can write about that for a class project or my blog or whatever but I get so caught up in what I should do sometimes I forget to just live in the moment like I was hanging out with some friends last week and we were just talking and laughing until the sun went down and it reminded me how vital those moments are you know? It’s the memories that we build that matter most and while I’m trying to keep up with everything sometimes I feel like I’m going to burst from all the excitement and stress and I wouldn’t have it any other way I thrive on that because it reminds me I’m alive and engaged in this chaotic world and I wouldn’t trade it for anything.

Just came back from the gym and I’m pumped. I love the energy in there. Everyone's sweating, pushing themselves, and it feels like this shared experience. I can’t just sit still. I had that new playlist blasting, and I swear it makes all the difference. The bass drops hit just right when I’m sprinting on the treadmill. Friends keep asking me how I stay motivated, but really, it’s just the vibe. I thrive off that group energy. It's contagious! I can’t believe how much I enjoy being surrounded by people who are enthusiastic about their goals, even if it’s just for a workout. Speaking of energy, I’ve got plans tonight with my friends. We’re going to this new place downtown, supposed to have amazing burgers. I’ve heard mixed reviews but honestly, it’s about the company. I just love spending time with my friends. I’m more excited about catching up than the food itself, but hey, good food is always a plus. I hope they have some good shakes too. I’ll probably go with chocolate. Classic. I’ve tried being adventurous and ordering something out of my comfort zone, but I always go back to what I know. Consistency is key, right? I want to know what everyone’s been up to since we last hung out. There's usually one friend who has a million stories, and it’s so entertaining to hear them. They’ve always got the craziest experiences, and it’s like a live show whenever they start talking. I end up laughing so hard it feels like workout cardio. Then there's the friend who’s always planning the next trip. We should really just go somewhere. A weekend getaway sounds amazing! I’ve been thinking some beach vibes would do us good. Lots of sun, good food, and just relaxing. Okay, so my phone is buzzing with messages. It’s my mom asking if I got my laundry done. I did it last night, actually, she really knows how to pick her moments. I think she worries about me because I’m probably not the most organized when it comes to chores. Still, I appreciate her looking out for me. I should call her later. I like hearing her voice, and we have these random chats about everything, which I enjoy more than I’d care to admit. She’s always so supportive. I can share anything with her, and she gets it, like she’s been there. It makes me feel connected. On the topic of connection, my roommates are cool but sometimes I feel like I need to keep the peace. Everyone has their quirks, and I’m good at navigating that because I just like to keep the harmony. When someone’s in a bad mood, I can’t help but try to lift their spirits. Crack a joke or play some upbeat music. I thrive off good vibes. I’ve noticed that I can sometimes be a little too accommodating, but I genuinely want everyone to feel good and get along. It just feels right. Oh, the other day, I tried to organize a game night. Everyone seemed to like the idea, but then when it came to picking a game, it became chaos. Some wanted card games, others wanted board games, and I just wanted something simple that everyone could jump into. I had to steer the conversation back to what I thought would be fun. Eventually, we settled on charades, which is always a hit. People got competitive in the best way. I mean, who could resist doing silly actions for a laugh? It’s like my dream scenario; just good friends, lots of laughter, and a little bit of friendly competition. Also, I’ve been thinking about joining this volunteer group. It’s supposed to be fun and a great way to meet new people while doing good. I care about helping out and just being around those who are also passionate about making a difference. It feels rewarding to be part of something bigger. Plus, I think it’ll be good to meet people I wouldn’t normally cross paths with. I’m all about building a sense of community. Getting involved like that seems like a perfect way to spend my time. I keep bouncing between ideas and things, it’s hard to focus on just one! So many thoughts are racing — like what show I want to binge next. Probably something lighthearted. I’m not into heavy stuff right now. I like to keep my mood up. Maybe a sitcom? They always make me laugh. Or I might try to read a little. I should probably pick up that book gathering dust on my shelf. One day, I’ll get around to it. There’s always tomorrow, right? Anyway, I can feel my energy winding down after all this thinking. It’s time to get ready for tonight. I need to freshen up, pick an outfit, probably just something casual. I’m mostly focused on just having fun. Getting excited just thinking about it! Who knows what stories will come out of tonight? I can’t wait!

Okay so I’m just sitting here trying to think of something to say. My phone is buzzing again, probably another group chat that I don’t really care about. Group chats, they just get overwhelming sometimes, too many notifications, too many opinions. Can’t people just text one-on-one if they actually have something to say? I don’t even know half the people in those chats anyway. Why do we have to pretend we’re all friends? It's so tiring. I don’t feel like participating in forced socializing. Just let me sit in my room and watch Netflix. Why is that so hard for everyone to understand? I mean, I guess some people feel left out and that sucks, but I’m fine not being included. I just wish everyone would stop acting like they care about each other. Speaking of Netflix, I started watching this weird show. It has like this convoluted storyline that somehow makes no sense. It’s probably some trendy thing, but I’m just watching it to see how ridiculous it gets. The characters are overdramatic, like, chill out, your whole life isn’t that exciting. Why do they act like they need to impress each other all the time? Not everyone needs to be the center of attention. I’ve got my interests, thank you very much. Why can't other people just keep to their own? Does everyone have to post every little detail of their lives online? It’s obnoxious. I do think about how people are always talking about self-care and mental wellness and all that, but honestly, do they even know what it means? I mean, isn’t self-care just doing whatever makes you happy? Then again, I don’t need all those fancy bath bombs and bubble baths. Just give me a good sandwich and let me binge a series without interruption. Why are people obsessed with aromatherapy? It’s all just scents and feels. Give me a break. Let’s talk about real things. Like I don't understand why we have to make everything an event like “self-care Sunday.” It’s just another way to post on Instagram. And how come everyone suddenly has a side hustle? Like is it really necessary to turn every hobby into a business? Can’t a person just enjoy making stuff without it having to pay off? I made some art last week. It’s not great but it’s mine. I didn’t post it on social media, obviously. I don’t need that validation or whatever people are looking for. But I can’t say I wasn’t tempted. Maybe it’s the human instinct to share but ugh, just imagine the comments. I can’t handle the “oh wow, so talented” or the “is this supposed to be art?” Like, just let me create in peace. People can be so judgmental without even realizing it. School’s another thing. Classes are whatever, I go, I sit there, I listen, I take notes—if I feel like it. Not exactly a thrill ride. Just give me my degree, please. It’s wild how many people take it so seriously. Everyone’s stressing over grades like it’s the end of the world. I just want to pass and get out, not break myself over GPA. I don’t see why that needs to be the focus. Let’s be real, most of this stuff I won’t even use. They say college is for the experience, but what experience? Crowded rooms, awkward group projects, endless assignments, it feels pointless most days. I’d rather just learn things on my own. Things feel so pressed for time, like there are deadlines for everything. What’s the rush? Just let us take our time and figure it out. I know that’s not how life works, but I swear every adult acts like they have it all figured out. Like yes, please tell me your secret to happiness because all I see are people who look worn out. I mean I’m sure they have good moments but can’t they just chill? Sitting outside in silence sounds more appealing than running around like a chicken with its head cut off trying to keep up with everyone else. I don’t know. I guess I just prefer to operate on my own level. Not really interested in what everyone else is doing or how they feel about things. Couldn’t care less, honestly. Just pass the time, keep to myself, and let the world spin without me. It’s not that deep. Some people think life is some grand adventure or something, but it’s just day after day of the same routines. If I have to listen to someone else rant about how their day was “crazy” one more time, I might just scream. But whatever, I’ll just keep doing my own thing. Who needs all the noise?

Okay so I was just thinking about how everything feels so heavy sometimes like I can’t shake off the weight of all these thoughts. I see people laughing and talking and I just want to curl up in a corner. It’s like, do they even know what it’s like to feel like they’re stuck inside their own head? I hate small talk it feels so pointless. Like, why ask how someone’s day was? I mean, does it really matter? I can’t even remember what I said. I guess I should care more but it’s just so exhausting pretending that I care. But then again, I get worried that people will think I’m rude or something. It’s a fine line. I remember this one time in class, just sitting there, listening to everyone chatter and I just wanted to disappear. The professor asked a question and everyone raised their hands, but I just sat there because I didn’t have anything to say. I don’t know if it’s because I don’t like talking or just afraid of being wrong. Fear of judgment can really keep you quiet. I wanted to disappear under the table or something. And then when I finally spoke up, my voice was shaky and I felt like everyone was staring at me. I hate that feeling. I wish I could be like those people who just jump in and say whatever. And then there’s that social situation I avoided last weekend. That party at Sarah’s place. I had every excuse ready. Homework? Check, but really I just didn’t want to interact with a bunch of people. I heard it was packed and loud, the kind of thing that makes my heart race just thinking about it. I think that’s why I don’t get invited to things often. People probably think I’m a bore. I’d rather binge-watch my shows or read, even though my attention span isn’t great. Thought of “what if” keeps swirling but once I realize I wouldn’t have fun, it’s just easier to stay home. Why do I feel guilty for not going out? Is that weird? I can’t help but feel like I’m going to miss out but the thought of being around too many people just makes me anxious. I keep checking my phone too, it’s like a reflex now. What if someone texts me? But they never do, and that hurts a little. I mean, do people even think about me or am I just this ghost floating around, unseen, unheard? Sometimes, I obsess over friendships. I don’t want to be too needy, but I care so much about the people in my life. I can’t stand the thought of hurting someone or being the cause of their sadness. I always end up overthinking messages before I send them. Did I say the right thing? What if they think I’m boring? What if they never reply? It’s always a cycle. I wish my brain would just turn off sometimes. I wish I didn’t think too much about every little thing, like how I should have said something differently or how I laughed too loud during that one awkward moment. Ugh, why do I even care? Oh, and the other day I ran into Julia, she seemed fine. I was surprised though because I heard she was going through stuff. She asked me if I was okay and I just blurted that I was but kind of felt guilty for not asking her how she really is. But I didn’t want to pry. It’s such a delicate balance between being friendly and overstepping. I don’t want to be that person who over-commits and gets too involved. Still, why can’t I just be more open and ask, just be there? I also wonder if my lack of planning is really causing issues. I mean, I’ll start working on something and then just get lost in thought or distracted. I have this pile of reading to get through but I keep pushing it. Procrastination feels like a close friend. I mean, deadlines are approaching but I can't find the motivation to do anything except scroll through my phone. Guilt, again. Sometimes I feel like I’m not meant to be here, like everyone else knows something I don’t. I wish I could be more assertive, but then I think, what if I fail? I hate failure. I can’t even remember what I wanted to be when I grew up. I think I wanted to help people but how can I help if I can’t even help myself? These thoughts just spin in my head, like a hamster on a wheel, trapped in its cage, going nowhere. And then I think of how life just keeps moving. I can’t stop it. I just wish I could pause and breathe. I’m walking on this tightrope of anxiety and it feels like I’m one step away from falling off. But then again, what if I don’t? It’s hard to tell where all this goes.

The clock says 2:15 PM and I’m still thinking about that email I sent. Did I sound too abrupt? Was it too formal? Maybe I should’ve added a ‘thank you’ at the end. I always worry too much about how others perceive me but I can’t help it. The anxiety creeps in, gnawing at my stomach. I can’t focus on anything else until I know the response. I hate waiting. Why does it take people so long to reply? It’s a simple question. Just answer it, please. My mind spirals into worst-case scenarios, what if they think I’m rude? What if they decide not to work with me because I can’t communicate properly? I mean, I overthink everything, like those times where I replay conversations in my head, over and over, just dissecting every word. It’s exhausting. Then again, I prefer to do things on my own. Group projects are a nightmare. I mean, I like when things are done right and it seems like no one cares as much as I do about the details. I can’t stand it when people don’t pull their weight. It drives me nuts. So much wasted time. It’s always me having to pick up the slack. Somehow, I end up being the one who organizes everything, which I know has to be done, because if I don’t do it, no one else will. So, I take charge but my mind races like a hamster on a wheel that just won’t stop. Then there’s the social aspect. I go to the clubs and events but I just stand there, observing, hoping to blend in, or just wanting to be invisible. Small talk is painful. I can’t do it. I’m not good at reading the room, so I smile and nod but inside I want to scream. I’d rather be working on my assignments than navigating another round of “Where are you from?” or “What are you studying?” The questions seem so shallow and repetitive. If someone would ask me something real, something with depth, maybe I could engage, but it’s always the same like a loop on repeat and I just want to escape, because I don’t have the energy for these fake connections. Often I retreat back to my room, where I find comfort in familiar routines, my own little bubble of control. Routine is my savior. Everything has its place, its order. I have a planner and stick to it religiously. Each hour mapped out. I can’t just wing it. I can’t go with the flow. It drives me insane when my plans get disrupted. I feel unmoored, like I’m floating with no anchor. The chaos of spontaneity is terrifying. I think that’s why I often find solace in solitary activities like reading or writing, because I know I can control those outcomes. The characters don’t let me down like people do. They follow the storyline I want them to follow. Sometimes I wonder if other people feel the weight of the world like I do. I guess that’s why I don’t connect easily. Everyone else seems so relaxed and carefree. They laugh and joke like it’s the easiest thing in the world. I feel like I’m trying to fit into a puzzle where none of the pieces align. It’s like I’m a different shape. I often think I should try harder, put myself out there, but it feels completely exhausting. Then my brain gets stuck on the thought ‘Why try? It won’t work anyway.’ The what-ifs suffocate me. What if I do meet someone I like and then they don’t like me back? Not that I'm looking for anything serious, I mean, I can’t even handle texting someone regularly. The idea of commitment is, well, terrifying. I’d rather just be alone with my books, my games, my thoughts. Even friendships are complicated. It’s tough to keep up with everyone. What if I say something wrong? Or they talk behind my back? My head races with all the worst-case scenarios. Even if someone seems nice, my brain is always on guard, running simulations on possible betrayals. Then sometimes something shifts and I find myself debating whether I should push myself to be more social. But then I recoil at the thought. It all feels so uncomfortable, like stepping into an ice-cold pool. I envy people who just jump in without a second thought. How do they do it? I overthink everything, so everything becomes a mountain of anxiety rather than a hill I could easily climb. It’s a constant battle in my mind, back and forth like some twisted tennis match. Focus, focus, I tell myself. I’ve got to finish that assignment due next week. But even now, I can feel those threads pulling me into a labyrinth of thoughts that have nothing to do with my work, just spiraling ever inward, searching for a way to untangle it all.

Okay so I keep thinking about that article I read about introverts and how they recharge by being alone. It's so true. I mean, I can’t even imagine going to a party. Just the thought of mingling with people makes my stomach twist into knots. Small talk is the worst. I’d rather have a root canal than ask someone about their weekend plans. I feel like I’d rather just dive into my thoughts, into the depths, you know? But then again, do I want to be alone all the time? It gets lonely. But when I go out, I feel like there’s this pressure, this invisible weight to connect. Ugh, just thinking about it makes me anxious. And the way people expect you to act—the smiling, the laughing, I mean, can’t we just skip all that and get to the real stuff? I remember this one time I was at a birthday party, and everyone was so loud. I could hardly hear myself think. I sat in the corner, sipping my drink, just watching. People are so unpredictable. I mean, one moment they’re giggling like school kids, and the next they’re deep in some philosophical debate, which is actually kind of interesting if you can sift through the noise. Sometimes I wish I had the courage to just say, “Hey, what do you really think about life? What’s the point?” But then I think, would anybody even care? Maybe I care too much about what others think. Or not enough? I don’t know. My brain is always juggling these thoughts, and it’s exhausting. I feel like I’m on a carousel, round and round with the same worries. How do people function like this? With normal feelings? Stability seems like a fairy tale. I mean, even with my own thoughts—it’s like I’m always searching for something deeper, something more meaningful. Speaking of depth, I wonder what the world would be like if everyone was open to ideas, all the time. Like, we’d live in a continuous brainstorm. How cool would that be? But then, I get paranoid thinking about how people can twist your words around. Like, suddenly you’re the villain in someone’s narrative. I have enough struggles without adding that to the heap. Oh yeah, that makes it ten times worse when you’re neurotic. Every little criticism I take so to heart. It’s like I wear my insecurities on my sleeve, and I’m constantly scanning for anyone to point that out. Sometimes I think about traveling. I mean really traveling, not just sitting in a café somewhere scrolling through Instagram. Like, immersing myself into different cultures, tasting food that makes my taste buds explode, or maybe even picking up a new language. But then I start to spiral. What if the airport is too crowded? What if I can’t figure out the logistics? What if I get lost? I don’t do well with disorganization. And it’s almost like my brain works harder to find all the possible ways it could go wrong. I should probably work on that. I should probably work on a lot of things, like my own personal chaos. I mean, I don’t have the best study habits. I could be organized and have a planner or something, but I never stick to it. Maybe that’s a symptom of being low on conscientiousness? I often think it’s just more fun to embrace the chaos and see where it takes me. But I wonder if that’s just another excuse for my procrastination. So many ideas, so little motivation. I feel like my thoughts are like clouds—always shifting, never settling, and I forget my original point half the time. And can we talk about the stifling feeling of expectation? It’s everywhere. From family to friends to society. "You should do this," or "You should be like that." I hate it. It makes me want to rebel, but then again, I don’t even know what I want to rebel against. That sounds dramatic, doesn’t it? Maybe I’m overthinking this again. Maybe I just need a hug. Or maybe I just need to retreat into my thoughts and let the world blur out. What if there was a place where everyone just shared their weirdest thoughts without judgment? That would be my paradise. I’d walk in and say, “I think about the meaning of life, but I can’t even find my keys half the time.” And everyone would nod, because at least I’m being real. The fake smiles, the awkward laughter, it all just feels like a distraction from what’s actually going on inside. But in the end, I guess I’m just trying to figure out how to find peace in the chaos. It’s like a puzzle that has no picture to guide you. Just pieces everywhere, and you don’t know where they fit. I’ll figure it out eventually, maybe. Or maybe I’ll just keep typing away my thoughts until something clicks.

okay so I just remembered this party last weekend I was super excited about like I got all dressed up and thought I looked amazing but then I spilled red wine all over my favorite shirt and it was the one I was actually really looking forward to wearing I think my friend was a little embarrassed for me but I just laughed it off because that’s what you do right everyone was having a good time dancing and laughing I don’t really dance well but I figure just jumping around is better than sitting there feeling awkward I get anxious in big crowds like sometimes I can feel it creeping up on me like a shadow and I have to shake it away but not this time I felt really social like I wanted to talk to everyone so I just kept approaching people and telling them funny stories even if they didn’t really respond the way I hoped it’s like I can’t help myself sometimes I just need to be in the spotlight I know it’s a little much but I got a lot of energy and it’s not like I want to annoy people I just want to feel connected but then I started thinking about how exhausting it is to keep up that energy especially when I have so much going on with school right now and all these assignments piled up in the corner of my mind and I can't decide which one I should start on first should I even start on anything or just go hang out with friends more and let them distract me from my responsibilities I wish they would just give me a break I know I need to get it together but honestly, it's hard to not feel overwhelmed everything is just swirling around and I can’t concentrate I think it’s hilarious how my roommate always has her stuff so organized and I feel like such a mess next to her I mean she always has her papers in order and her study schedule made out and I never have any of that I’m more of a “fly by the seat of my pants” kind of person but she seems to manage to balance it all and I’m over here like what am I doing with my life sometimes I feel like I’m just floating around with no direction and then other times I’m like full steam ahead like I want to meet new people and try new things but I realize I don’t really want to try anything that’s too different or challenging which is odd because I love being around other people who are daring and adventurous but for me? I guess I just prefer the comfort of familiar things doesn’t mean I don’t enjoy a good spontaneous night out it’s just I know I won’t be like those thrill-seekers climbing mountains and skydiving or something I’ll probably sit there on the ground and take pictures like that’s my contribution I’m okay with that though because it gets me out and I get to meet interesting people even if it’s just a passing conversation that makes me smile for a minute or two Ugh and then there’s social media don’t even get me started on that I love scrolling through Instagram and seeing what everyone else is up to but then I start comparing my life to theirs it’s like a rabbit hole that just sucks me in every time and I tell myself to stop but then I keep scrolling just one more post just one more and then I realize an hour has gone by and I haven't done anything productive like wow isn’t this just the best way to waste time but also it feels so good to connect and see the faces of people I care about even if it’s all just a highlight reel and I know it isn't real life but it still makes me feel something and then I start worrying about what they think of me like am I living up to their expectations or do they even care and why do I even care what they think it’s a circular cycle and I just keep getting caught in it Maybe I should go for a walk or something clear my head but then I think about how cold it is outside and I just don’t want to move I need to just focus for once and get something done but that thought makes me feel so anxious like how can I even sit still when I’ve got this buzzing energy inside of me waiting to burst out everywhere I feel like I’m just a bundle of nerves and excitement all at once sometimes it’s great like I can make friends easily and laugh and just be in the moment but then there are times like now I feel like I’m on the verge of freaking out over nothing and I think why can’t I just chill out I hate feeling like this But then I remember that amazing taco place my friends and I went to last week they had the best spicy salsa I think my tongue is still tingling from it I love food and how it brings people together like every time we go out it’s just such a vibe and I can’t wait to go again but I want to enjoy my food and not feel rushed to get back to studying because that’s where my mind goes always back to the responsibilities I keep pushing aside for the shine of social interactions and laughter but do I ever really feel fulfilled?

I really need to stop overthinking everything. It’s like a constant loop in my head that just doesn't quit. I can hear my thoughts buzzing like an old fridge that needs to be unplugged. People say you should embrace the chaos but I can't help feeling like chaos is a bad idea, it makes my stomach twist. I mean, do people really enjoy small talk? I’d rather dive headfirst into a discussion about quantum mechanics than have to ask someone about the weather. I find it all so superficial, and yet I end up trapped in these conversations weekly, maybe daily if I venture out. But what’s the point? I don't even care about their vacation or their dog’s latest trick. Just thinking about social events makes my heart race. The anticipation of awkward silences or the pressure of keeping up with group dynamics freaks me out. I’d rather stay in my room with a good book or maybe start a new project. I love learning new things, like why certain herbs can actually boost mood or how ancient civilizations influenced modern democracy. That’s the stuff that keeps me awake at night, not how to make polite jokes about the latest trends in TV shows. And yet, I feel like an outsider, looking in on these social settings where everyone seems so much more at ease. It’s like an invisible wall, and I’m stuck on the other side. Maybe my low agreeableness makes it harder to connect — I just can’t fake the niceties. It feels wrong, like putting on a poorly fitting suit. I get frustrated with myself too, like why can’t I just go with the flow? What's the point in dissecting every interaction? It gets exhausting. I wonder if I could ever be more extroverted but then I catch myself thinking maybe that’s just not me? Do I even want to change? Change is exhausting, and I think I like who I am most days. But I also feel like I’m missing out. There’s balance somewhere in there I haven’t found yet. I crave deeper conversations, though that might be asking a lot from people who just want surface-level chit-chat. I could talk about philosophy for hours, get lost in discussions about moral dilemmas or art movements. But no one else seems to want to play that game. There must be a perfect circle of friends out there who want to dive into deep waters, but instead I'm floating around in these shallow pools. I keep my commitments tight, though. My conscientiousness makes it hard to let things slide. I have this planner with color-coded sections for different subjects and daily tasks; it’s like my brain organized into neat boxes. The satisfaction of ticking off those to-do lists gets me through the day. It’s a strange juxtaposition — I’m stressed out about whether I’m doing anything worthwhile, but I also thrive on schedules. Why can’t my social life fit into that structure? Like "Wednesday: attend networking event 6-8 PM" with a quick debrief scheduled right after for mental recovery. But those spontaneous plans that involve people just... they paralyze me somewhat. Speaking of spontaneity, what about those who just jump into things? I envy them, I truly do. They must experience life so differently. Maybe that’s the risk-taker in me trying to speak up — but then my neurotic mind comes crashing in, spinning tales of what-ifs and worst-case scenarios. Like if I tried dancing at a party, I would trip over my own feet and everyone would laugh. And guess what? That fear becomes a solid wall to actually going out. It’s like a vicious cycle and I can’t break through. Then again, I also find beauty in being a deep thinker. I love exploring ideas, dissecting complex theories, even writing about things that make no sense to others but are enlightening to me. I could ramble on about the latest in genetic research, or how virtual reality might change education — and that excites me. But sharing those thoughts feels so vulnerable, like opening myself up to judgment. It’s safer to keep them in my head. Maybe that’s why I score so high on openness. I think about all the possibilities that exist in thoughts. Traveling through ideas feels more exhilarating than actual travel sometimes. But then, am I just hiding? It’s like I want to jump into the world and experience everything, but my feet are glued to this safe little territory. The contradictions swirl around in my head, creating a storm that I can never quite calm. It’s exhausting. I just wish for a manual sometimes, but they don’t make those for life choices, do they?

Okay so I was just thinking about how interesting it is that some people can just sit in silence and be okay with it while I’m over here like, where’s the noise where’s the conversation? I mean it’s not that I can’t appreciate a peaceful moment, but my mind just races with thoughts and questions and things I want to share with people. I had this incredible conversation with a friend the other day about how we both feel the same way about connecting with people. There’s something exhilarating about meeting new people, learning their stories, hearing their laughter. I swear every time I go to a party or a social gathering, I have so many ideas bouncing around in my head. Like, what if I tried to learn about a hobby I never thought about before, like pottery or salsa dancing? Salsa dancing sounds so fun! I mean, imagine all the rhythm. I’ve always wanted to learn how to move like that, but I just end up gravitating toward activities where I can meet others. My roommate always laughs and says I have the “party radar” because I know where all the cool stuff is happening. But then I think about studying and how I sometimes get distracted by the thought of what everyone else is doing. I can't sit down and read for hours on end without my mind drifting off thinking about what I could be doing instead. Don't get me wrong, I love learning, and I always want to do well in school, but it can be tough to balance all these activities. Still, when I get on a roll working on projects, I feel this rush of motivation. Like that time I stayed up late with a group working on a presentation, and even though I was tired, we were all so excited about the topic that we couldn’t stop discussing it. Those moments bring a sense of fulfillment. I love collaborating and bouncing ideas off each other. I swear, they just amplify the energy in the room. Speaking of energy, I can’t help but think of how much I appreciate my friends. The best thing is being able to just talk about anything and everything without any judgment. One of my friends is going through a rough patch, and we had a long heart-to-heart about it. I was trying to be there for her, offering support and listening to her worries. I mean, I can’t help but feel things deeply when it comes to others. It's hard not to get invested in people’s lives. Sometimes, I wonder if it’s too much, but I just care so much! I love seeing them happy, and I always want to uplift them. Then, I think of all the organizations I’m involved in and how they give me the opportunity to meet so many different people. I joined the student council last semester, and it’s been a whirlwind of planning and events. The thought of organizing a charity event gets me fired up. I've always believed in giving back and being part of something bigger. Teamwork makes it all more rewarding! I could talk about the different projects we’re planning for hours. Fundraising usually taps into this creativity I didn’t know I had. And after each event, seeing everyone come together is just magical. Oh, and the travel plans! There’s this idea floating around about a group trip to a national park next month and I can’t get it out of my head. Nature is this whole other world waiting to be explored. I could sit and watch the stars for hours, just reveling in their beauty. And hiking? Don’t even get me started. Standing at the top of a mountain after a long hike gives me this sense of accomplishment and it’s like, wow, I did that! But I also wonder how all this connects back to my future career. What do I want to do with my life? I think I can see myself in a role that involves working with people, like community outreach or event planning. Something that fosters connections and brings people together. I want to make a difference, you know? It's like, there’s this drive inside me to inspire others and be part of their journey. I think a career that taps into that would just be perfect for me. It motivates me to keep exploring, ever-curious about the possibilities that lie ahead. But then again, I find myself daydreaming about all the random things I could try, like learning a new language or diving into photography. How cool would it be to capture those spontaneous moments of joy? Or even writing a book filled with stories of people I’ve met? I can already imagine flipping through pages filled with memories and experiences. Life is so rich with experiences that I almost feel like there’s never enough time to soak it all in. But I’m going to try my best to savor every moment!

Okay so I keep thinking about how I should probably be working on that project for my sociology class but I can’t stop worrying about how everyone else is managing their time because sometimes it feels like I’m the only one struggling and I just don’t want to fall behind. It’s like I’m always analyzing other people’s schedules in my head. Like I see them in the library all day and I’m wondering how they can focus for so long. I wish I could do that. But then there’s this nagging feeling of dread like what if I can’t? I just keep going back to my to-do lists, trying to find some semblance of order in this chaos. I really like order. It’s comforting. I check off the little boxes and feel a rush of satisfaction but it’s never enough. I feel like I have to do more because what if I missed something? What if I forget to study that one chapter and it ends up being on the test? Anxiety is exhausting, honestly. And then there's that group project, the one where I volunteered to take the lead because it’s easier to be in control than to rely on other people. But that means I have to keep chasing everyone down for their parts and that really stresses me out because what if they don’t come through? I can’t assume they’ll be dependable, right? Some people just don’t care as much and I can’t handle that feeling of letting the team down. I feel like I'm the one holding it all together and what if I’m not doing it right? I have to email them again and nudge them but what if that makes me look pushy? I don’t want to be pushy but I also don’t want to fail. Sometimes I think about how hard it is to meet new people. I mean I don’t hate people or anything, but small talk is just the worst. It feels so forced.
