Like last week at that party—everyone was laughing and dancing and I was just standing there with my drink trying to find a way to break into the conversation. I felt like such a wallflower. Why can’t I just be more outgoing? I mean it shouldn’t be that hard. I wish I could just dive in, make jokes, and be funny like my roommate does but I panic thinking what if I say the wrong thing and everyone looks at me like I'm strange? Then there’s the internal dialogue, like I’m critiquing every word. Did I laugh too loud? Did I look stupid? Thinking about the future makes me anxious too. What if I pick the wrong career? I keep overthinking it all like should I go into something stable like accounting or something more creative like marketing but what if I’m not good enough at either of those and then I disappoint everyone? My parents have these expectations and I don’t want to disappoint them; they always talk about security and how important it is to have a steady job. But what is steady? What if I’m supposed to take risks? I feel like I’m stuck in this loop of analysis paralysis and I don’t know how to break out. I read those articles about following your passion and they sound nice but what if my passion leads me nowhere? I keep scrolling through social media, seeing everyone else’s lives and thinking they have it all figured out. Their travels, their dinners, their spontaneous fun. I can barely get myself to leave my apartment most days. I wish I could just be content with what I have. But instead, I’m over here worrying about what I’m not doing or who I’m not being. I guess I worry a lot. I try to be agreeable, try to fit in, don’t want to ruffle feathers but that also means I can’t express my own needs, right? People always assume I’m fine because I’m so agreeable but inside I’m screaming sometimes. It’s a strange dichotomy. Shouldn’t I just say what I want? I have those thoughts, but I don’t want to be a burden. No, better to carry it all myself. And what about my relationships? I feel like I’m always apologizing for things that don’t need an apology. Why do I do that? I think it’s easier to be nice than to deal with potential conflict. I have that one friend who’s always shouting over everyone and I secretly envy their confidence. I don’t want to be them, but why do they get to be so loud and free? I just want to be part of it without feeling so exposed all the time. Is it a crime to want to be liked and accepted? I don’t want to be just a face in the background, but how do I step out? Sometimes, in the stillness of my own thoughts, I think of how exhausting it is to juggle all these expectations—I can’t help but wonder if there’s a way to find some peace in that chaos.

It’s weird how people jump into conversations and I’m just there like a deer in headlights, okay so I just want to blend into the wall, invisibility sounds fabulous. The group project thing in class is coming up and my heart sinks. Color me thrilled, right? I don’t even know how I’m going to handle it. I mean, group work is the bane of my existence. I dislike the fact that people expect you to coordinate schedules and come up with something cohesive. I’d rather just dive into my own ideas, you know? There’s so much I have to say but it feels like it gets lost in translation when I try to communicate with others. Frustrating. I wonder if they even see the beauty in things the way I do. The world is so rich and layered. Why can’t they just appreciate the chaos like I do? The other day I was staring at this painting in the gallery, it was just splashes of color everywhere and it resonated with me. Maybe it’s because I feel like I’m just kind of splattered across life, trying to find the right mix to make sense of it all. I love that it’s random but intentional. Like, I connect with chaos. There’s something so freeing about it. I don’t even know what the artist was thinking but I could feel everything—frustration, happiness, confusion—all of it just swirling around. If I could capture that in a bottle, I’d carry it around everywhere, just open it up and let the feelings waft out. But then I think about how no one really gets that. Like, people just float through life looking for answers, for direction, for the right pathway to success. But what if success is just embracing the mess? I almost prefer to dissect the pieces than to put them together and feel boxed in by some societal standard that I never agreed to. Can people see how one-dimensional their lives can be when they just toe the line? I mean seriously, no one questions things like the way I do. I want to rip the seams and explore the contents but then I sit back and feel like an outsider. I guess that’s normal for someone like me, right? And then there’s social media. Ugh. I can’t handle all the curated perfection. It's maddening. I scroll through and see the highlight reels of everyone's supposedly perfect lives. But imagine if they shared the 3 p.m. crying session or the existential dread that bubbles up like an old soda can. I’d like to see that. I want authenticity, not this glossy façade. The oversharing often seems phony, and the hidden truths are even more troubling. It feels like a masquerade ball where everyone’s in disguise, pretending to be someone else while I sit in the corner, peeking through the cracks. Sometimes I think about how people, especially extroverts, have it all figured out, they walk into a room and just own it, and I’m here, overthinking every word that might leave my mouth, my palms sweaty even thinking about it. I wonder what it feels like to say something without worrying about the repercussions. They pull me in like a gravitational force but I’m the black hole, sucking in every bit of energy and turning it inward and it drains me. Social settings are like marathons where I’m only left panting at the finish line. I’m honestly so tired of pretending to enjoy things I don’t. Festivals, networking events, it’s like a circus I never wanted to join. But then again, there are moments when I’m sitting alone, just lost in thought, and that’s where the ideas flow. I can dive deep into philosophy, art, or any abstract concept without distractions. There’s a peculiar joy in solitude, even if it’s tinged with loneliness. I’m free in my head. I can spiral into an idea and unravel it thread by thread, but when it comes to expressing it to others? Ha! Not so much. The pressure to put it into words is nauseating. And when they don’t get it, or when I’m met with that blank stare? Yikes. I envy those who seem to glide effortlessly through life and I kind of resent it too. My mind races with thoughts while theirs seem to flow smoothly, like they’re tuned to a different frequency. Maybe it’s just easier to sit back and think of the beautiful, chaotic mess that life is, even if nobody else sees it that way. I could get lost in that. But that’s the irony, isn’t it? Here I am, ready to embrace the messy, while I feel stuck between a rock and a hard place, grappling with my internal storms and social seas that I just can’t navigate.

Okay so, I just saw this post about the weirdest food combinations, like pickles and peanut butter, which made me think about the time I tried chocolate-covered bacon at that food truck festival. It was surprisingly good—sweet and salty, the best of both worlds, like how I always crave something spicy after a sweet dessert like that weird banana pudding that had crushed cookies in it nobody really needed. Food is such an exciting thing, a world within itself, and I love exploring it. I want to taste everything, try all the combinations, and I wonder if that says something about my personality or just my complete lack of shame when trying new things. Like, sometimes I feel like I’m a human tornado, just running around, picking up new experiences and tossing aside the boring stuff. I mean, who wants to stay in their lane? I hated that monotonous routine in high school, waking up then going to classes where everyone was just trying to fit in. Not me. I want to break the mold, explore the edges, and sometimes I feel like I’m too much for people who don’t get it. I meet someone, and they’re all about their safety zone, and I’m just like, “what if we went hiking at midnight instead?” or “have you ever tried Zumba in the rain?” I love that thrill, that spontaneity. Speaking of spontaneity, last week my friend convinced me to go to this underground dance party. Totally not my usual scene, but I was glad I went. The vibe was electric—everyone moving, lights flashing everywhere, and I love how people were just letting loose. Dancing like nobody’s watching, which, duh, is what everyone should be doing all the time. I jumped right in and started doing my ridiculous moves. I swear some folks were just staring at me like I’d lost my mind, but whatever. I was having fun. Fun is all that matters, right? It's almost like an unspoken rule—if you’re not having fun, why are you even there? I tend to think that way about most things. Oh, and then the DJ played this mashup of 90s pop and hip hop. I lost it. I literally lost my mind and started yelling the lyrics to “Wannabe.” It’s like, seriously, who doesn’t love a good throwback? The energy was wild, and it felt like freedom. I’m still buzzing from that night. Next week, I’m thinking of organizing a little gathering at my place, maybe a karaoke night. That could be fun! I already know I’ll be the one belting out “Total Eclipse of the Heart” like there’s no tomorrow. I mean, why not? Sometimes I think about how I interact with people. My friends tease me about how I don’t always read the room. It’s like I walk in, and I’m just ready to pounce on whatever crazy idea I have, while they sometimes hold back or plan ahead. But I don’t want to plan. Planning sounds so dull! I could never understand that need for structure. So rigid. Each time I make a plan, it falls apart anyway. Instead, I see the world more like a playground. There are swings and slides and all these climbing walls you can explore. Why not just jump in and figure it out? That said, sometimes I also feel bad because I can be a little harsh. I don’t always think through how my words might affect people. Especially when they get too caught up in their own heads. I get it; life can be heavy. I just want to tell them that there’s so much out there! Lighten up! I know some folks can’t handle it when I throw in the truth with a twist of sarcasm, but I honestly think that’s what makes life colorful. Yeah, maybe I don’t play nice all the time, but does that mean I’m a bad person? Nah, I just see things differently, and I don’t feel the need to sugarcoat it. Another weird thought just popped in my head: what would happen if all my friends just decided to drop their day jobs and all we did was travel the world? It’d be a mess, I know it. But imagine the adventures! Traveling to random countries, tasting street food, getting lost in markets, dancing with locals. Each place a new chapter, stories waiting to unfold. Honestly, I think I’d thrive in that chaos. The concept of having a ‘normal’ job always made me feel trapped, like I was in a cage, so suffocating. I want the world to experience my energy sometimes—where the excitement propels you into just living every second to its fullest. I guess that’s how I process life, what gets me through. Adventure, spontaneity, and a whole lot of crazy antics. Sometimes I wonder how I’ll channel all of this into something meaningful, something that sticks and lasts beyond just me. But for now, it’s just living in the moment and diving headfirst into anything. And that, I think, is just fine with me.

I just thought about how I’ve got this weird knack for meeting people, you know? Like last week, I bumped into this guy at the grocery store, and we fell into this deep convo about the best pizza toppings. Pineapple? Love it. Anyway, he was adamant about mushrooms and we debated for like twenty minutes which is better. That’s always me, right? Just chatting away with strangers like they’re old friends. No filter. Totally unpolished. I mean, some people give me weird looks, but who cares? Life is too short to worry about that stuff. I feel like being open just makes things more fun. Like the time I decided on a whim to join a salsa dancing class. First class was a disaster! But it was a blast. Everyone was laughing, and I was stepping on toes but who hasn’t? It's all about the experience, the thrill. The music blasting, feeling your heart racing. It’s a rush when you throw yourself into new things. I love that. That’s better than scrolling endlessly through boring social media feeds. What do I even want in life? I guess adventure? New experiences? Yeah, that sounds about right. I could sit in class and pretend to take notes, but honestly, I’d rather be out exploring. I went hiking last month with some friends and we ended up lost but it was so cool! We just found this random waterfall, and we jumped in. The water was freezing but the laughter was warm. Those moments are everything to me, you know? I can’t stand the thought of being cooped up inside. Even right now, I’m itching to run outside and see what’s happening, find some people, make some noise. Make a joke, make connections, laugh hard. I guess that’s why my grades are all over the place. I’ll blow off studying for a night out with friends. I can’t help it if the night is alive! Just realized I forgot to grab lunch again. Great. Maybe I’ll just grab a snack later. What’s a sandwich? Forget the bread, just give me the filling. I’m fine with a granola bar or something. I think about food differently. Like it’s just fuel, right? Or like a social thing. All those foodie posts on Instagram? Blah. I’m here for the experience, not the aesthetics. And I can’t believe how many people won’t try street food because it looks sketchy. I tried this taco truck one time and honestly it was the best thing ever. I could talk about food for hours. The smell of tacos reminds me of last summer. Spontaneous road trip with friends to this music festival and we lived off food trucks. I swear, I met so many amazing people there. Like this girl who was all about kombucha and fermented things. We got into this debate about gut health and all that. I’m not even that into health but it was fascinating hearing her perspective. And then we ended up dancing to some random band under the stars. Just living in the moment, you know? Speaking of moments, I can’t stop thinking about how quickly time flies. It’s like, I can’t believe it’s already October. Wasn’t it just summer? I need to plan something big soon. Maybe another road trip? Or even a camping trip. But it needs to be spontaneous! That’s the key. I can’t stand over-planning everything. That’s when things get dull. I guess that's part of my personality—always seeking that next thrill. But what does that mean for relationships? I wonder if I scare people off with my spontaneity. Gosh, I hope not. I can be intense sometimes. Like when I get really excited about something, I just go off. People tell me to dial it back, but what’s the fun in that? It’s way more fun to be loud and unapologetic about things. You only live once. I think I’m drawn to people who are just as wild as me, the ones who can match my energy. But then there are those moments in a group when I can feel I’m too much. Like they’re all looking at me waiting for me to chill out. It’s funny how I can read the room and still not care. I just keep going. It’s exhilarating. But maybe part of me questions if that's too much for some people. And would they just roll their eyes or walk away? I’ll just keep being me. I’ve got a big party to plan for next week. Just a get-together with everyone. I have all these ideas buzzing in my head but I should really write them down. Or not. I could just wing it. Getting everyone together sounds like a blast. People love the excitement, the music, the chaos. And I can’t wait to see what happens!

The clock says 2:15 PM. That reminds me I have to remember to grab lunch before I head to the library. Speaking of which, I really like the library. It’s quiet, but not in a way that feels lonely. The smell of books is kind of comforting. I wonder if that’s a thing people talk about, the smell of old books. It’s like a mixture of paper and ink and a hint of dust. It’s weird how you can love something like that. I can lose track of time browsing through the shelves, sometimes. I lose myself in the titles. There’s this one book I read that had an amazing take on existentialism, I should try to find it again. But I forget the title—classic me! I should keep a list of books I want to reread and ones that I want to read. There’s something calming about being organized. My room is mostly organized, except for that pile of clean laundry I haven’t folded yet. I mean, it’s not a huge pile but it’s there staring at me. It’s funny how one little thing can make a space feel cluttered even if everything else is put away. I should really take care of that this weekend. Or maybe I’ll do it later today? Nah, I’ll probably get distracted. I get distracted easily. Especially when I start to think of something interesting, like how people interact. It’s fascinating to watch people and their dynamics. Social interactions can be so complex—a simple exchange can have so many layers. I remember a conversation I had with a classmate last week about empathy, and it made me think about how I connect with people. I tend to be a good listener, I think. I like to understand where people are coming from. It’s interesting how being agreeable can sometimes lead to misunderstandings. Not in a bad way but like, I find it so easy to agree with someone’s feelings that I might not ask enough questions. I don’t want to push anyone away you know? I don’t mind being a sounding board. I wonder if that’s something people take for granted. Knowing that you have someone who really hears you, even if they don’t have all the answers. I don’t always have answers and that’s okay, I’m okay with silence sometimes. Silence isn’t always awkward. Doing group projects is both a blessing and a curse. I like working as a team, I find it super rewarding. But then there are those moments when someone isn’t pulling their weight, and even if I feel frustrated I don’t say anything because I don’t want to cause conflict. I think that’s my agreeableness kicking in. Maybe I should be more assertive? I wonder if I should practice that. It would be nice to voice my opinions more clearly without feeling like I’m going to hurt someone’s feelings. Still, I always seem to find common ground with others. I guess that’s a silver lining? I keep thinking about what it means to be conscientious. I like to plan things, but I also enjoy the spontaneous moments. There’s this balance between having a structure and allowing for unpredictability. I remember one time my friends and I decided to just drive somewhere and ended up at a beach we’d never been to. It was one of the best days ever, just the sun and the waves. I think we even ended up having a picnic with snacks from a gas station. We laughed about it later—nothing beats the spontaneity of that moment, even with the structure of us deciding to drive somewhere. Sometimes I think about how people label themselves as introverts or extroverts—like it’s a binary. But it’s really more of a spectrum, right? I mean, can’t someone be an introverted extrovert or extroverted introvert? I guess that’s where the openness comes in. I love exploring different ideas or perspectives. It’s exciting to learn about new cultures or philosophies. There’s something about travel that calls to me. I want to visit places that feel different from where I am now. I want to walk through streets filled with history and taste food I’ve never had. There’s a yearning in me to understand the world more deeply. I wonder where I’d go first. Maybe somewhere in Europe? Or Japan? The way they blend tradition and modernity is so striking. I should take a class on world history next semester. That’d probably deepen my understanding of everything. Plus, I like to write about what I learn. I’m not great at it yet, but I guess practice makes perfect. I’m starting to realize how much these thoughts connect back to my values. It’s all about understanding myself and the people around me. Life is this collection of experiences, relationships, and reflections. I wonder if I’m overthinking it. Maybe I should just grab that lunch and stop pondering so much. But then again, these thoughts keep swirling around like a dance in my brain. It’s okay to get lost in them sometimes, I think. It’s part of being human.

Okay so I was thinking about how much I love meeting new people and just the energy that comes from a crowd. Like, the other day at the campus event, everyone was everywhere and the music was blasting. I can't even remember the last time I felt so alive. It’s like this rush you get, right? The conversations flowed, and I was bouncing from one group to another, learning names and hearing stories. I met this girl who’s studying marine biology and she was so passionate about it. I could see her eyes light up when she talked about coral reefs. Made me want to dive into the ocean myself—though I’d probably just float there clueless. Then there was this guy who was just incredibly funny, he had this energy that grabbed everyone’s attention, and I was laughing so hard my stomach hurt. We started talking about our favorite movies, and somehow we ended up debating the best superhero films. I lost track of time, which is ridiculous since I had that paper due the next day. But honestly, who cares about a paper when you can chat about Captain America and the Avengers? After that, I ended up volunteering to help with the next event. I don’t even know what I was thinking, but it just felt right. I love the idea of being part of something bigger, you know? And it’s more than just helping out; it’s meeting more people and making connections. It’s like I thrive on that, the networking, the shared experiences. It’s kind of cool how you can get close to someone in just a few hours, right? Like, all it takes is some shared laughter and a few inside jokes and now they’re practically friends, or at least acquaintances who feel like friends. But then I remember my roommate, she's so meticulous about everything. I mean, her notes are color-coded and organized in a way that makes me feel a little chaotic. But I respect that. It’s impressive, and I can’t help but admire how dedicated she is to her studies. I should probably take a page out of her book and get my act together for that paper. Maybe even color-code my notes? I don’t know if I could do that but it might be worth a shot. Oh, and speaking of organization, I’ve been meaning to declutter my side of the room. It’s a mess! I can’t even see the floor right now. Every time I try to clean, I get distracted by something. Is that normal? Like I’ll pick up an old concert ticket and suddenly I’m reminiscing about that night and the friends I went with. Just thinking about how much fun we had dancing, laughing until we cried. Good times. I like how open I am to experiences, but sometimes I think it overwhelms me. There’s always something to do, something to explore. Yesterday I went on a spontaneous hike with some friends, and it was amazing. The view from the top was breathtaking, and we were just sitting there, sharing stories and snacks. I brought those weird flavored chips—like dill pickle or something—and everyone acted like I was insane, but they ended up loving them. It’s pretty neat how food can bring people together, even if it’s a bizarre flavor. And then there’s the late-night study sessions. They always turn into these hilarious debates about random topics. The other night we were arguing about whether aliens exist. I mean, who doesn’t love a good conspiracy theory? I can’t even explain how we got there, but we were so into it, and then someone mentioned the multiverse, and everything got super deep. At one point, we were all looking at each other like, “Are we really debating parallel universes at 2 AM right now?” It’s those kinds of moments that remind me how wonderful it is to connect with others. What am I even going to do when school’s over? I want to keep this energy. Maybe travel somewhere completely different. Somewhere that’s not just a tourist spot but a place where I can meet locals and really soak in the culture. I think I’d thrive in that kind of environment. But whatever happens, I just want to keep embracing life. I love the spontaneous road trips with friends, the laughter that never stops, and the conversations that go deep into the night. Every little moment matters, and I can’t wait for all the adventures yet to come. There’s a whole world out there, and I’m ready to explore it—one new friend at a time.

Okay so I just remembered that time I accidentally signed up for a baking class thinking it was a cooking class. I was so excited to meet new people, and then I found myself surrounded by all these flour-covered individuals who actually knew what they were doing. I mean, I barely make toast without burning it, but somehow I was there trying to follow along with an instructor who was way too enthusiastic about bread. I think I made a cake that ended up looking like a pancake. It was a disaster, but everyone was so friendly about it, and we all laughed. I love that about groups, everyone is supportive, and it makes the whole experience fun even when things don’t go right. Everyone just wanted to have a good time, and they kept giving me tips on how to improve my cake game. I still think of that day fondly even if I now steer clear of baking. And speaking of groups, I can’t stand being cooped up alone for too long. Like, even when I'm studying, I need to be around people, even just the buzz of a coffee shop nearby. It’s so motivating to see everyone else working, the energy is contagious. I can’t do the whole studying alone thing for long stretches. It gets boring and my mind starts to wander thinking about random stuff instead of focusing on the exam I have coming up. I think I need that social interaction to keep my brain engaged. Oh, and team projects! They are the worst and the best. I mean, I love working with others, but sometimes you get that one person who just won’t pull their weight. Like, dude, we are all in this together, let’s just put in the effort and ace this. I always end up taking the lead because I just can’t help myself. I get so invested, it’s like, if I’m going to be part of a team, I want us to succeed together. It can be frustrating though because I wish everyone was on the same page. But then again, I’m always the one trying to encourage everyone, so usually we pull through. Community vibes, right? Oh gosh, I just remembered that one time I organized a study group and everybody showed up and it was amazing! The snacks I brought were a hit, too. I made sure to pile on the chips and cookies. Everyone is happier when they have food, right? It became this little tradition where I’d always bring something sweet because who can study without a cookie or two? Honestly, I think those snacks were the real reason everyone kept coming back. But it’s all about the connections we made too, we shared notes, laughed about how clueless we all were about a specific topic, and by the time finals rolled around, we felt like a little family. Isn’t that the best? Yeah, we’re all so different in that group too. Some of them were super chill about grades while others were freaking out, and I found myself being this mediator, trying to keep everyone calm. It’s funny, I love that role, but sometimes I wish I could be a little more laid back about it all. I mean sure, I care about my grades too, but there’s more to life than just numbers, right? I’d much rather have a good laugh over a shared disaster than stress myself out over a B. But then again, I guess we’re all motivated in our own ways. Some people dive into studying super hard and others ease into it. I remember one of my friends who was always so chill about everything, like she’d roll in 10 minutes before class and still somehow ace the exam. How? I think I need to take a leaf out of her book sometimes. But I guess that’s not me, I’m always planning and thinking ahead, trying to make sure everything's in order. I have like this planner obsession—color-coded and everything. I think it helps keep my life from turning into chaos. I definitely don’t mind that structure. It keeps me grounded, helps me know where I need to be and when I need to be there. My friends joke about it, but they also use my planner sometimes to figure things out. I guess sometimes it's my organized chaos they rely on. Whoa, I just had a flashback to middle school where I was always the one organizing the school events. Class parties, charity drives, you name it, I was probably involved. The thrill of getting everyone together is just too much fun. I can’t imagine life without that sense of community—being around people, sharing laughter, working together, it’s everything. Anyway, I should probably get back to studying. Maybe I’ll grab a snack to motivate me first. Snacks help, right? Plus, I wonder how my friends are doing with their study sessions. I should text them and see if we can meet up later. I just love that synergy—it keeps me going!

I’m sitting here thinking about how other people seem to have it all figured out. Like, they can navigate group projects with ease and share ideas like it's second nature. It’s such a strange concept, this idea of small talk. I can’t even muster up the courage to comment when someone says something I find kind of silly, but I get it. People need connection. I just wish it didn’t feel so forced. I prefer direct conversations. If someone would ask me straightforwardly what I think about something, that would be easier. Like, “What’s your opinion on this assignment?” which is actually just a stressor because it’s tied to grades, and if I say the wrong thing, I might come off as incompetent, which is not ideal. I know I should probably try harder to engage with people, but it’s exhausting. Sometimes I just want to sit in a corner and watch everything happen. I mean, there’s a certain charm in just observing, right? It’s like having a window view into the lives of others. There's Sam in class, being all chatty and pulling everyone into his orbit. I almost find it amusing how he just talks and talks without any visible effort. It’s interesting to see how he lights up the room, but I can’t help but think about how fake it all feels. There's something genuine about silence too, isn't there? Food. I like food. I should probably eat something soon. Oh, I got that leftover pasta from last week in the fridge, which probably isn’t the best idea but whatever. It’s filling and tastes decent enough. Do I really want to cook anything? Nah. I’d have to clean, and cleaning just sounds like a hassle. I prefer the leftover situation. It's comfy in its own way. I should think about going grocery shopping though I dread the crowds. I hate being in those aisles where people suddenly stop and block the way. Just move, you know? Back to the class thing, it’s funny how you can kind of read people based on their body language. Like there's this girl in the front who nods at everything the professor says – is she just trying to impress, or does she genuinely care? I don't think I’m that interested in making an impression, but I do like to please others. I guess that’s where the agreeableness comes in—it’s nice to not want to step on anyone’s toes, but then sometimes I feel like I’m just going with the flow too much. Like, if everyone says we should do this project one way, I just go along, even if I think there might be a better approach. Then I remember we had that group presentation last week, and we all agreed on a direction but halfway through, I realized it was off-track, and I didn’t say anything. Why didn’t I say anything? I wonder if that’s a weakness or just how I function. And if we got a bad grade, would anyone even care? Probably not. Just move on to the next thing. Should I text someone? But who? The one person I think about isn’t really a good text buddy. They’re busy with their life, and I don’t want to interrupt. I don’t need to keep seeking out people to fill a void. I mean, I have friends, but hanging out with them feels like a lot of work sometimes. What a contradiction—I enjoy their company but also wish to be alone. Is there a balance? Or do I just not have what it takes to find one? There’s so much pressure. The weather’s changing. I find that the cooler air makes me feel calmer. Does that even make sense? It’s like the chill wraps around me and muffles all the noise. I can just exist in that moment. But winter is long, isn’t it? At least that’s what I’ve heard. I dread those dark, cold months. That’s when people get moody. I start feeling bad for everyone else too, even though I’m fine. I can’t stand it when people are sad. Like, why can’t they just be okay? I’m okay. I hope they know that they can talk to me if they need to, but do I really want them to? Okay, so thoughts are all over the place. I’m thinking about how I need to keep it together for my next class. I should review my notes or at least skim through the readings. But then again, it’s just sociology—nothing too groundbreaking. I feel like I’m on autopilot through lectures. Just nodding along, taking notes, but my mind drifts. People are so animated while debating theories, and I’m here, just hoping the class ends before I zone out completely. But at least I’m nice about it. I smile and nod. Everyone needs a pleasant face in the room, even if it's totally blank inside.

I can’t believe how fast this week went by. Feels like just yesterday I was packing for the weekend trip and now it’s already Thursday. Time really flies when you’re around people all the time. I love being surrounded by friends. All the laughter, the shared jokes, even the silly moments that probably don’t make any sense to anyone else but us. Like that time Sarah tried to order a tall drink and accidentally asked for a “small grande” and we just couldn’t stop laughing. I can’t help it; I thrive on these interactions. I wonder if that makes me a bit predictable. I mean, I know what I like, and I stick with it. I have my favorite cafes, the ones where they know my order by heart. I don’t like trying new things much unless it’s with a group. I have a friend who’s super spontaneous and always wants to try out new places, and I get it, but it’s just not really me. I like my routine! It makes me feel secure, like I know what I’m doing, which might be why I keep getting involved in group projects. Okay, back to the weekend. We’re planning to hit the beach! I can’t wait to soak up some sun, play beach volleyball, and just hang out. I love the whole vibe of it. Everyone’s all smiles, and it’s like a mini vacation every time. I always make it a point to organize stuff. I’ll probably end up planning the food and making sure we have enough drinks. I think I enjoy the actual planning almost as much as the beach day. Seeing everything come together is so satisfying. The way everyone pitches in, the teamwork… It’s like we’re all part of this little community. I wonder if other people feel this way too. It’s great to see everyone contribute. Sometimes, when I see someone just sitting there, not really engaging, it kind of gets to me. My favorite part is when we start playing games. I get super competitive, but it’s all in good fun, of course. I remember last summer we played frisbee until sunset, and someone accidentally threw it too far and it landed in a tree. I ended up climbing it because I just couldn’t let the day end without that frisbee! The whole crew was cheering me on, and it felt awesome. Little moments like that really make a friendship stronger—shared laughter, shared struggles. Speaking of friendships, I have this ongoing group chat. We talk about everything from funny TikToks to serious life stuff, like studying for exams or someone’s breakup. It’s wild how much we all lean on each other. It’s kind of like having a safety net. I can’t imagine not having people around to share life with. I mean, I do love my alone time, too, but it’s never really for long. Maybe an hour or two to recharge before I’m itching to get back out there. I think it’s important to connect with people rather than just being on my own—there’s too much joy to be had. Do I ever think about why I’m so social? Maybe it’s the way I grew up. Family dinners every night, always having to share stories about our day. I can still hear my mom’s laughter and my dad’s corny dad jokes. They’d always encourage us to make friends and be kind, which I think stuck with me. I want to be the type of person that others rely on. Helping others feels good. It’s just so rewarding to lend a hand or even just be there to listen. Sometimes I fear that I might be too agreeable. There are these moments when I just go with the flow, even if I have a different opinion. I think about this especially in group settings. I want everyone to feel respected and heard. But then it kicks in, and I’m like, “Am I sacrificing too much?” It’s just hard to know when to stand firm and when to go along with the group choices. Just the other day, we were choosing movies, and I ended up saying yes to something I didn’t want just to keep the mood going. I guess there’s a balance in there somewhere. I mean, I could sit here all day writing about friendships and social life. At the end of the day, I just want to keep building those connections, keep creating those moments that make us smile. It’s all about the memories, right? That’s what counts. Even if it’s just a silly inside joke about a “small grande” going down in history as a legendary phrase among friends. It’s life! And it’s great!

So I have this paper due in a couple of days. I should probably get started on that but I keep thinking about all the things I need to do instead. My roommate keeps asking me if I want to go out, like, it's her thing to just drag me along, but I really don't feel like it, I’d rather stay in and read or maybe just watch something familiar. I like familiar things. It’s comforting. I don’t like surprises when I’m in the middle of something I enjoy. I guess that sounds boring but it’s just how I am. Maybe I could just tell her no again. I don’t know if that would hurt her feelings or make her think I’m antisocial. I'm not antisocial, I just prefer quiet nights with my thoughts. Right, so back to this paper. I need to figure out how to organize my points. I usually write outlines and just follow them; it keeps me on track. I wonder if I’m missing something by not being more spontaneous like some of my friends who just go with the flow and don’t seem to worry at all about the details. It’s kind of nice to let loose, I guess, but I just don’t see the point if it’s going to mess up my plans. Like chaos isn’t my thing at all. I prefer to know what’s coming next and how things are going to unfold. I think it helps me stay calm. Calmness is good but maybe too much calmness? I feel like I should spice my life up a bit. But how? My idea of spicing things up is trying a new recipe or rearranging my room. I just thought about the last time I did something different. I tried that new Thai place with my roommate. It was okay. The food was spicy, which I didn’t expect. I had to drink three glasses of water just to cool down my tongue. My roommate thought it was hilarious. I felt slightly embarrassed but that’s okay I suppose; she laughed it off. Sometimes it’s weird how we find joy in each other’s misfortunes. I kind of like how she brings that lightness to situations. I don’t think I’m good at that. I don’t think I’m good at making light of things. I prefer to stick to the facts and get things done. I always help her with her assignments too, like when she struggles with math. She’s really good at history but math? Not so much. I’m glad I’m good at it. It makes me feel useful, and I like being useful. Think I need to start considering my future too. Grad school? Sounds daunting. Could I manage that? I should probably visit the career center or look into some workshops. That seems important. Am I making the right choices? I don't always feel sure. I’ve never been one to take risks, and that makes everything feel a bit heavier sometimes. I guess that’s why I like to plan everything. It gives me a sense of control. The thought of stepping outside my comfort zone makes my stomach twist. I mean, I’d like to think I could be brave, but there’s comfort in predictability. I admire people who can try new things without worrying about outcomes. There’s something so free in that, so… alive? I wonder how they do it. Okay so, back to that paper. I think it’s on climate change. I have a lot of information; I just need to narrow it down. Why am I so good at gathering information but not so great at expressing it? I’ll read a million articles and feel like I understand everything but when I sit down to write, it’s like I forget all of it. Where do those ideas go? I just hope I can meet the requirements and get a decent grade. I really stress over grades sometimes, not in a panicked way, just in a ‘I want to do well’ way. I know it’s a bit much but the idea of disappointing my parents is really not an option. They worked hard to get me here, and I want to make them proud. It’s funny how sometimes I wonder if they think I’m too focused and serious. Like when I told my mom I wanted to do something responsible with my life, she sighed and said I should have some fun too. Fun. What is fun to me? I think it’s satisfying work. If I can finish that paper and know I’ve done my best, that’s fun in its own right. I guess that’s the joy of living pretty simply. Just focus, and everything will fall into place. That’s just how it works, right? But I should also get out more. Balance, that's what I need – a mix of work and a dash of going with the flow. Or maybe just a sprinkle. Not too much chaos.

Okay so, I was just thinking about how much I love talking to people. I mean, like, really love it, you know? When I see someone in the hallway, my brain is just like, “Say hi! Make a connection!” I thrive on those interactions. Sometimes it feels like my entire day revolves around how many people I can share a smile with. But then there are days when I feel this wave of anxiety crashing down on me, like what if they don’t want to talk, what if they’re busy? And I start overthinking everything, like what if I said the wrong thing last time we spoke? Ugh, it’s exhausting but also so exciting. I just can’t help it! Have you ever gotten that rush? Walking into a room full of people, and you just light up? It’s like an electric charge runs through me. I love that high-energy vibe. Sometimes I wonder if it’s too much for other people, though. Maybe they think I’m too intense or something? I want everyone to feel welcomed and included—like, who wouldn’t want to be in on the fun, right? But then I worry because I’m also sensitive to other people’s feelings. Like, if I feel someone pulling away or getting uncomfortable, I instantly think it’s my fault. That’s the neuroticism kicking in. I mean, why can’t I just chill? But then again, I feel like that’s what makes me who I am. I care way too much sometimes, but isn’t it nice to care? I’m super conscientious, which means every detail matters to me. I can’t just throw together a paper or a project—I have to outline it, research it, and make sure it’s perfect. But the openness kicks in because I also love exploring new ideas and perspectives. It’s like a balancing act between wanting to be thorough and wanting to try something unconventional. Maybe that’s why I love group assignments so much! I get to hear other people’s viewpoints, and it makes me feel alive, but I also want things to be organized. I can’t stand a chaotic group dynamic where people don’t contribute equally. It drives me nuts. And then I think about how much I enjoy being spontaneous, like going out on a whim with friends or taking a last-minute road trip. But at the same time, I make these elaborate lists for what to pack! It’s funny—I want the adventure, but I also can’t let go of my planner. Balancing that spontaneity with my need to be prepared is a constant tug-of-war in my life. Like, one minute I'm planning a big event, and the next, I'm texting my friend, "Hey, let’s just drive to the beach right now!" But then I’m like, “Shoot, what if I don’t have my sunscreen?” It’s ridiculous, but it’s just me. Oh, and I can’t get over how much I love trying new foods! There's this place downtown with the most bizarre fusion menu—the kind of stuff you never knew existed, and I always drag my friends along to experience it with me. I live for those moments where we’re all trying to figure out what the heck we’re eating and laughing because it’s so weird. But deep down, I'm always worrying if they’ll like it, you know? What if they think it’s gross? What if the flavors clash too much? Do they think I'm a weirdo for suggesting this? But it’s the thrill of trying something different that excites me; it’s a blend of adventure and anxiety, and it’s honestly like a rollercoaster I can’t resist. And I’m always thinking about how to improve myself. I read self-help books like they’re novels. There’s something so motivating about learning how to be a better version of myself. I’ll jot down notes, set goals, and start new routines. Then, I’ll hit a snag, and it’s like my brain flips out because I feel like I’m failing. It’s an exhausting cycle where I’m simultaneously pushing myself to grow while also being hyperaware of every little setback. It’s maddening but also rewarding when I manage to achieve something, like finishing a big project or making a new friend. There was this one time I reached out to someone who seemed shy at a party. I just started chatting and was so nervous they’d be unresponsive. But they totally opened up, and it was like magic! We ended up talking for hours, and I thought, “Wow, I’m so glad I did that.” But then I spent the next week worrying if I’d overstepped or made them uncomfortable. I guess that’s just the neuroticism again, mixed with the excitement of connection. It’s such a whirlwind—but maybe that’s what makes life feel vibrant and worthwhile. I wouldn’t trade it for anything, I think, even the chaos.

I can’t believe I forgot to bring my notes for that class, I had them all organized and now they’re sitting on my desk. I need to get a better system for that, maybe a digital organizer or something. Who even uses paper anymore? Everything’s online, but I still want to write things down sometimes. It helps me remember better, I think. I wonder how effective flashcards really are. I see everyone using them but it seems like a lot to constantly review. I’d probably just end up losing them in a pile of stuff. Man, I really hate clutter. I like things straightforward and tidy. It’s just easier to focus; I can’t stand it when my desk is messy. I should probably clean it up again, but I really want to finish this assignment first. It’s due tomorrow and I’m almost done. I work best under pressure, but I also like to be ahead of the game. It’s a weird mix but it keeps me motivated. I’m not great at small talk though, which is ironic because everyone expects you to be more lively and animated if you’re organized and hardworking. I can’t believe how many group projects I’ve had to do this semester. It’s like they think everyone loves collaborating and sharing ideas. I just want to do my own thing. I get it, some people thrive on that energy, but I’m just like let’s get down to business. This is why online classes can be appealing. I can read and absorb everything without people interrupting me. But then again, isolation can get to be too much sometimes. I have friends, I do—just not the kind that feels the need to text every day. I prefer deeper conversations, but they almost never happen in a casual setting. It’s frustrating when you’re surrounded by chatter but none of it feels significant. I miss those late-night conversations in the dorms though. Deep stuff, philosophical ideas, existential questions. Like what even is consciousness? If everything is just a series of chemical reactions, does that mean our thoughts don’t really matter? Can I just sit here with my mind spinning in circles for too long? Sometimes I feel like my brain is just too full of ideas and questions. Like I can’t sort through them all when I’m trying to focus on one thing. Lately, I’ve been reading about creativity and how it works, the brain activity involved, and how different people process ideas. It's fascinating, but it also makes me wonder if I’m doing it right. Is there a standardized way to be creative? I guess that’s why I’m always trying to find various angles to understand things; it feels like a puzzle I need to solve. Depending on the situation, I like to jump from thinking analytically to thinking creatively. It’s exhausting, but fulfilling in a way too. Speaking of puzzles, I really enjoy crosswords. They’re like little brain teasers and I can get lost in them for hours. I think it’s important to keep challenging myself mentally. That’s probably why I end up seeking out new experiences so often. Even just trying out a new cuisine feels exciting. Recently, I tried this Vietnamese place and the flavors were totally different from what I expected. How can people live in the same bubble their whole lives? It’s like so much of the world is out there, waiting to be discovered. I’ve been contemplating traveling a lot lately. I want to see new cultures, meet new people—though that latter part is challenging. I know I wouldn’t want to do solo traveling, not really, but being in a group can be so draining. What do I want from this? I think I just want to break out of my routine, that I can feel it creeping in like a fog. I thrive on novelty, but there’s a part of me that values structure too. How do people balance that? I guess it’s about finding the right outlets. I’ve thought about joining a club, maybe something like hiking or art. But then I wonder if I’d just get annoyed by group dynamics. It’s a strange push and pull I have going on inside me. I want to embrace spontaneity but keep everything organized too. It’s like wanting to paint with wild strokes while staying inside the lines. My notebook is getting too full of half-baked ideas and sketches. I should probably take some time to read through it. There are things that spark joy and others that just seem like a waste of time. I need to figure out which is which. I want to create something that matters without getting lost in my own thoughts. Okay, I feel like I’m just rambling now but there’s something calming about putting it all out there, just letting it flow without worrying about the end product. Who knows, maybe this is all part of the process, figuring it all out one thought at a time.

Okay so I just remembered that time I convinced a whole group to go on a spontaneous road trip to the beach just because it was a nice day. I mean it was one of those random Wednesdays that felt like summer. It’s amazing how people can just flip their plans, right? Like one moment we’re trapped in our routines, and the next we’re barreling down the highway with the windows down and music blasting. I love that feeling of freedom. The thrill of driving fast with friends laughing, yelling over the music. Why don’t we do that more often? Work, school, responsibilities always get in the way. Just yesterday I had a long chat with a friend about how we should really just drop everything and travel for a month or at least a weekend. I wonder if that’s feasible. Sometimes I feel like most people are so wrapped up in their own heads. Like why spend so much time worrying about what others think? I mean who even cares? I definitely don’t always fit in the mold of what others expect of me, but I find that energizing. It’s like a puzzle I get to solve every day just by being me. I remember in high school when I joined every club possible, from drama to the debate team, just so I could meet new people and explore new things. I can’t believe I was such an overachiever. But it was worth it. I met some of my best friends that way. I feel like some people make it such a big deal to find their “people,” but honestly, I just throw myself into the situation and figure it out as I go. But then again, I can also be pretty focused when I want to be—like when there’s a project due. I can buckle down and get things done. My roommate always jokes that I’m a total perfectionist. I mean, I have my own system for everything. I have my planner, my color-coded notes. There’s something satisfying about ticking off boxes. Just yesterday I planned my week down to the hour. Balanced study time with a few spontaneous hangouts. Gotta keep life fresh, right? Sometimes my friends laugh when I say I can’t go out because I need to finish a paper. But then they’ll see me later at a party and think I’m the life of it. I thrive in those settings. Oh, speaking of parties, I really can’t stand when people are too passive-aggressive. Like if you have a problem, just say it. It’s so easy to talk it out. I’m not afraid of confrontation. In fact, I kind of love it. I’m direct, and I think that helps ease the tension. I guess that goes hand in hand with my low agreeableness, huh? I mean, I’m not trying to be mean or anything, just honest. I don’t see the point in sugar-coating things. Like once someone said I was too blunt, and I was just like, “Well that’s who I am.” I think you get further that way. It’s refreshing to just lay everything out on the table. No games. Just the facts. And I have to say, I have such a curiosity about the world. I mean, I can’t get enough of new experiences. I’ll try any food once. From the weirdest street taco to sushi to Thai food I can’t even pronounce. Just last month I finally tried that durian fruit everyone talks about. The smell was so strong when I walked into the market, but I needed to know what it tasted like. It was fascinating. Really strong but so unique. It’s like every bite is an adventure. I even take odd classes because I want to broaden my horizons. My current classes are a mix of psychology, philosophy, and art history. It’s amazing how connected all those fields can be. I love making those connections. The world is more intertwined than people think, and I guess I’m always on the lookout for those threads. I sometimes wish I could just snap my fingers and get people to see the beauty in differences. It’s okay if someone doesn’t think like me or doesn’t want to go out every weekend. I mean, I enjoy those moments but I also love having deep conversations about the universe. Like, what’s out there, all the possibilities? The stars always pull me in. I find myself gazing up at them often. It’s humbling and exciting at the same time. The night sky holds so many secrets. Oh, last summer I went to this astronomy camp with a bunch of strangers and we just laid on the grass and stargazed. It felt like connecting with something bigger than ourselves. Crazy, right? I wish I could find more opportunities like that, just dive into the unknown. I think it’s the unpredictability of life that gets my adrenaline pumping. I thrive on it, and I can’t wait for the next adventure, whatever it is.

I can see my phone screen lighting up again, notifications. Probably another group chat thing I don’t care about. I don’t understand why people feel the need to chat all the time. Conversations are exhausting. I’d rather just scroll through my feed, let the world happen without me. It's just noise. Everyone expects me to engage, but I can’t. Like, I’d rather be alone with my thoughts. It's safer that way. Social situations make my heart race. I don't know if I should respond to the message. They probably won't care if I don’t. What was the point of my last class? I can’t even remember what we discussed. Just random facts that I won’t need next week. I should study, but it’s hard to focus when everything feels so overwhelming. I know I should have taken better notes or something, but it’s too much effort and I don’t really want to. I can’t be bothered to organize my notes. It’s a mess, a big jumbled mess, and that’s just how it is. I can’t even find the motivation to clean my room. Clothes everywhere, dishes piling up. It makes me anxious. I see it and think about how I need to fix it, but then I end up just staring, doing nothing. I wonder if I’m the only one who feels this way. Probably not, but I bet no one talks about it. Everyone wants to seem perfect, put together. I can’t do that. I just want to crawl into bed and forget about everything. My mind races with thoughts, and I can’t turn it off. I think about what I should be doing, what I’m not doing, how far behind I am. They tell me to breathe, to take it one step at a time, but that just seems like trivial advice. I can't do one step when there’s a mountain in front of me. I just want to scream sometimes, but I can’t. The world is so demanding. Why can’t people just chill out? They don’t understand me, and I don’t understand them. Why do they care about the football game? Or what’s happening in the lounge? Who cares about the latest drama? I don’t even know what people are talking about half the time. I feel like I’m in my own bubble, and it’s not the same as everyone else's. I just wish they would leave me alone. I hate the small talk. “How’s your day?” Who cares? Just let me exist. I didn’t sign up for all this socializing. Ugh, I need to figure out a way to automate my responses so I can be less involved. Sometimes it’s quiet, like when everyone’s out doing their thing, but then the noise comes back. My roommate blaring the music or talking about something dumb. I can’t even focus on my own stuff. It’s like she doesn’t realize I just want silence. The chaos drives me crazy. I know I should probably talk to her about it, but I hate confrontation. Just nodding along and pretending like everything is fine is so much easier. I don’t want to hurt feelings. Or maybe her feelings don't matter? I don’t know. I think about it too much. I keep looking at my to-do list. It feels like an eternity of tasks, but then I think about all the things I’ve said I would do and didn’t do. It just keeps growing. I should be applying for internships, planning for next semester, but nope, just sitting here staring at my computer screen. It makes me feel like a failure. Am I the only one who has these moments? I keep worrying that everyone else is figuring it out while I’m just here, struggling to keep my head above water. What’s the point of it all anyway? Grades? Jobs? I’m not even sure if I care about my major. I just picked it because it seemed okay at the time, and now I’m stuck in this loop of just trying to make it through. I keep scrolling through old assignments, wishing I had done them differently, but what’s the use? I rarely get feedback, and when I do, it’s just a number that makes no sense to me. I’d rather not know, honestly. It’s hard to escape this feeling of being trapped. Like I’m running in circles and not going anywhere. Everyone talks about growth and opportunities, but I feel stuck—frozen in place. Maybe I’m overthinking everything again. That's what they say, right? I think I should just take it easy, but how do you do that? It’s like a constant battle with my own mind. At the end of the day, I just want to be left alone with my thoughts, but life keeps intruding. I want control. I want to just breathe... but then again, here comes another notification.

okay so I'm thinking about all the plans I have for this weekend and it just makes me excited like there's so much happening and I love being around people it's energizing I mean what’s better than a gathering with friends good food laughs maybe a game night or a movie marathon I really hope someone brings snacks I’m a sucker for popcorn especially when it’s buttery and salted maybe a little caramel to mix it up too like who doesn’t love that sweet and salty combo? I also want to invite a few new people from my class I met them last week and they seemed super fun I like meeting new people and discovering what makes them tick everyone has a story right and I want to hear it and tell mine because isn’t it so cool all the unique experiences we each have it just expands my perspective and that’s something I value so much like trying to understand different viewpoints and cultures it’s just fascinating honestly I could spend hours just chatting about random topics with someone I barely know and just vibe with them. Speaking of which I really need to get better at keeping in touch with all my friends I’ve got this big circle of amazing people and sometimes I feel like life gets in the way and I forget to check in but I try to remember to send texts or funny memes because laughter is the best bond sometimes I wish I could just clone myself and hang out with everyone at once that would be wild right? we’d have like a massive party but also chill sessions where we just talk about our hopes and dreams and what we want to do with our lives and it’s so cool hearing everyone’s ambitions like it makes me feel motivated to chase my own dreams too. The other day one of my friends was talking about starting a podcast and I thought that was a brilliant idea it’s such a creative outlet and it would be a fun way to share interests with others I’ve considered starting one too but then I think about what I would even talk about for an hour straight without losing my mind which is a challenge because my mind jumps from one topic to another so quickly like if I start thinking about music I’ll get lost in how much I love discovering new artists and genres and then before I know it I’m listening to some obscure band from a completely different country that I never knew existed and I’m digging through their discography just to find that one song that speaks to me and it’s exhilarating to think of how much music is out there! I really appreciate how music connects people too like I went to this local concert last month and there were people from all walks of life just grooving together sharing this experience and it just felt magical like the energy in the room was palpable and we were all feeling the same thing and I remember thinking that I wish every day could feel like that like just pure joy and connection but then I realize it’s the little moments in between those big experiences that matter too like having a deep talk with a friend over coffee or just walking through the park and taking in the beauty of nature. I also want to travel more there's so much out there to explore I daydream about hopping on a plane and just landing somewhere new not knowing what to expect but ready for the adventure and all the cool people I’ll meet along the way I’ve always believed that travel teaches you so much about yourself and the world and each time I go somewhere new I feel like I grow a little bit more because you have to adapt and learn and it gives you a fresh perspective on life and the struggles others face and it’s all interconnected like when I visited that small village last summer and met those locals sharing meals with them and hearing their stories I realized how easy it is to forget the simplicity of life when you’re wrapped up in your own bubble. And speaking of bubbles I want to pop out of mine more often like trying new things cooking exotic dishes or taking classes that push my boundaries like I randomly signed up for a dance class and I’m terrible but it’s so fun just letting loose and not caring about being perfect it’s about the experience right? the joy of moving and expressing yourself is what counts I think everyone should try dancing at least once in their lives because even if you feel silly it’s about getting lost in the rhythm and just being free. oh look at the time! nearly twenty minutes it’s so easy to get lost in thoughts and feel those connections whirl around me I think I’m ready to take on the weekend and all the exciting moments ahead!

I really should start organizing my notes for that class but I keep thinking about how annoying it is when people don’t pull their weight in group projects. Like, seriously, I always end up doing everything because I can't trust anyone to do it right. Why can’t they just follow the guidelines? It's really not that hard. I mean, I get it I’m a bit more uptight about these things but hey if I’m going to spend my time doing something, I want it done properly. And then I think about all those other times I've had to clean up someone else's mess. Ugh. That last project was a nightmare. Did I end up doing most of the presentation too? Yeah, definitely. If you want something done right, you have to do it yourself. And speaking of presentations, why does everyone get so nervous? I think it’s silly to be scared of speaking in front of people. Just say what you need to say and get it over with. I’m really good at it. It feels like a game to me, just keep talking until the time’s up or the audience stops paying attention, whatever comes first. But then again, maybe it’s not fair that I’m the one who gets to enjoy that while others are sweating bullets up there. They should just learn to embrace it, like I do. I’ve had some feedback saying I come across a bit too harsh sometimes. But if you can’t take the heat, right? You can’t get mad about what I say if you can’t keep up. Sometimes I worry if I’m a bit too much. It’s kind of exhausting to be me honestly. The constant buzzing in my head, the thoughts ricocheting off the walls, and then I get all worked up over little things like my roommate not doing the dishes after I explicitly asked her – seriously, how hard is it to just be considerate? Then I start spiraling. It’s like I can’t help it, the worries pile on top of each other and I’m just shouting at myself in my own head. Did I mess something up today? Did I forget to respond to that email? Why didn’t I sign up for that club? I should probably go to more events, meet more people, and how do people find the energy? It’s like juggling fire while running a marathon. My brain is like a vending machine stuck on a repeat cycle of anxiety. What if I miss a deadline? Or what if I never get a job after I graduate? There’s this constant pressure to excel, and I put that on myself too, and when someone else doesn’t contribute? Like, I can’t even! It just adds to this obsessive need for everything to be perfect in my life. It's so annoying really, and I wonder sometimes if I’m doing this all wrong. Like, should I lighten up? Should I stop caring so much? But then I think: no, it’s important to take things seriously. Oh, and why do people always want to share their secrets? I don’t care about your breakup or how you failed a test. Keep that to yourself. I mean, can’t they see that I’m already juggling my own issues? But then they come to me looking for support, and I try my best to be there, but honestly, I don’t have the patience for their drama. I roll my eyes at yet another story of how someone sent a text to the wrong person. Like, grow up. Texting isn’t that hard, and you should know better by now. But I’ll listen because I feel like I should, I guess? It’s complicated. When I think about weekends, I guess I can’t help but feel a bit tormented between wanting to socialize and just needing to recharge. I always end up going out, even when I plan to stay in and chill. My friends are relentless sometimes; they’re like “let’s go,” and I can’t say no. I’ll get caught up in the excitement, and then the next day I’m just a ball of nerves because of all the socializing. Like, I had the best time, but now I’m an anxious wreck trying to piece myself back together. And then my phone beeps with all these messages about the next hangout and I’m like can I seriously do this again? It’s all about balancing the need to be around people and the overwhelming feeling of wanting to just have my own space. How can it be both? But I guess that’s life, isn’t it? Just a constant back and forth, wanting connection but feeling like the pressure is too much sometimes. I don’t know how to find a middle ground. Should I just pick a side and go hard on that? That seems easier. So many thoughts, and none of them really answer the big questions. Do I even have questions? Or just noise? I should really check my email.

Midnight snacks are the best. I don’t know why I always crave them. It’s like my brain knows it’s late and just decides, hey, you deserve some ice cream. I always have those little cups in the freezer. Chocolate chip cookie dough is my jam. I could eat a tub of it and somehow justify it as a “reward” for being productive today. Productivity, ha! Who decided that 8 hours of sitting in front of a computer counts as productive? Sometimes I wonder if I should switch my major to something more hands-on. Like, I don’t know, pottery? But then again, the mess. The clay under my nails. Not my vibe. But still, it seems fun and unpredictable. I love unpredictable things. Like, surprise parties for friends or spontaneous road trips. I once drove all the way to the beach just because a friend texted “let’s go.” Packed my bag in ten minutes. Hit the road blasting my playlist, feeling the excitement surge through me. Speaking of playlists, I have way too many. I make them for everything. Study sessions, workouts, chill evenings, or just melodies that remind me of summer. I swear that music can change your mood in an instant. I could be feeling down but pop on a happy tune, and suddenly I’m dancing around my room like a maniac. My roommate probably thinks I’m nuts. She’s the quiet type, more introverted. I like having her around though, it balances my energy. But sometimes, I wonder if I overwhelm her. I talk a lot. Like, a lot. I can go on for hours about the random thoughts swirling in my head. Imagine a ping-pong ball bouncing everywhere. That’s my brain. Jumping from one topic to another. Right now, I’m thinking about how I really need to clean my room. There are clothes everywhere, and I swear I can’t find half my socks. Where do they even go? I probably have a sock monster living in my closet. That would be a fun story to tell, right? “So, I discovered a sock monster in my closet.” But then again, who would believe me? I need to start writing things down. I have ideas that fly away, and I can't catch them fast enough. Someone told me that if you write your thoughts, they stick better. Planning is key, I guess. But I like spontaneity. That’s the beauty of life. I wish I could travel more. There’s so much to see. I’ve never been out of the country. I should really change that. I hear the food is amazing in Italy. Pasta and pizza, my two favorite food groups. I could live on that. And the art! Art ignites something in me. The colors, the emotions captured in a moment. It makes me want to create, to try new things, to explore new mediums. I should take a painting class. I remember trying watercolor once. Not the best, but it was so liberating. Slapping those colors on the page, mixing them, seeing what happens. I should probably talk to my advisor about my classes next semester. I’m thinking about adding a minor, maybe something that complements my major. I go back and forth a lot. I can’t settle on one thing, and sometimes that feels exhausting. But I thrive on new experiences. I don’t want to miss out on anything exciting. FOMO is real! I even joined a few clubs this year. Book club is a blast, we read all sorts of genres. But then there’s the debate club too. It’s hilarious watching people get passionate over seemingly silly topics. And I can get fiery—interests collide in my head, and I just have to share my opinion. Okay, why do we even have grades? I mean, some people are naturally good at tests, and others aren’t. It feels like a flawed system. I’d rather have projects that reflect our creativity and ideas. Who actually remembers what they crammed for an exam? It’s the projects that stick with me. The group work is so much better. You get to collaborate, bounce ideas off each other, and create something meaningful. I really need to stop procrastinating. I keep putting off that one paper that’s due next week. I know I’ll regret it later. But here I am, typing all these thoughts instead of doing it. It’s like my brain says, “Let’s do anything but that!” Maybe I can reward myself with some ice cream after I finish. Speaking of rewards, I should do something nice for my friends. Have a little get-together. Could we call it a party? Yeah, let’s do it. Good vibes, good music, maybe some games. It’ll remind us that we can still have fun amidst all the chaos. I love bringing people together. We make such great memories. That’s what life is about—collecting moments, laughter, and friendships along the way. I should go to bed soon, but these thoughts won’t let me. There’s just so much to think about, so much to do. And tomorrow is another chance for adventure.

I just remembered that time at the beach with my friends last summer. We built this massive sandcastle—like seriously, it had towers, a moat, and everything. I can’t believe how many people stopped to take pictures of it. I mean, it was just sand, right? But there’s something about creating something with friends that just makes it feel special. And then we all ran into the ocean and started splashing each other, laughing so hard. I love how spontaneous things can lead to the best memories. It’s like you can plan a trip down to the hour, but sometimes it's the unplanned moments that make everything sparkle. I can’t wait for summer again. Thinking about summer, it also makes me think about all the music festivals. I went to that one last year with a few friends, and it was unreal. The energy was electric, and everyone was just so free and happy. I like to think that music brings people together in a way nothing else can. Even if you don’t know the lyrics, you can sway to the beat and feel connected to the crowd, to the moment, to those around you. I met this boy there, he had neon hair and the biggest smile. We ended up talking for hours, sharing stories, just vibing. His name was Max. I really hope we stay in touch. It’s funny how a single day can lead to these tiny connections that can grow or fade away. Speaking of connections, I really enjoy meeting new people. I think it’s the best part of being in college. There are always new faces and new stories, and each person adds a different shade to my experience. I joined that club because I heard they were doing volunteer work, and immediately I thought, “Yes! Count me in!” Helping out always brings me joy, plus I met so many cool people there. One girl named Lily is amazing; she’s been to like eight countries already and has the best travel stories. I can’t wait to hear more about her adventures. I want to travel more, experience different cultures—just dive into everything life has to offer. There’s so much food to try! Food, oh man! Cooking is a new hobby I picked up. I started with simple things like pasta and moved to baking bread, which was a total challenge but so rewarding. The smell of fresh bread is unbeatable. I keep trying to convince my roommates to join me, and sometimes they do but other times they just stare at me like I’m crazy. But the other day we made homemade tacos, and they loved it! The laughter, the spilled ingredients everywhere, it became chaos—but delicious chaos. I’ve learned that food can be a little bit of a metaphor for life. You throw in different ingredients, you mix and match, and sometimes it’s a mess, but usually, it ends up tasting pretty good as long as you’re enjoying the process. I just remembered that book I read last week; it was about personal growth and the importance of stepping out of your comfort zone. I love grabbing books that shake up my perspective. I don’t like to settle into a routine too much; I thrive on new experiences and challenges. There’s just so much out there waiting, right? I want to explore everything. I’ve got that itch to try rock climbing, and I’m dragging a few friends with me next week. The idea of being up high and looking down gives me a rush—plus, it’s great to conquer fears, or at least try. I've heard it's a great team effort, too, which makes it even more appealing. Then there’s my classes. They are kind of a whirlwind but in the best way possible. I love engaging in discussions and hearing diverse opinions. Sometimes, the debates get heated, but in a good way, like everyone respects each other despite the differing views, and I think that’s what makes it worthwhile. I appreciate when people want to share their thoughts. It’s nice to see different perspectives. It reminds me of what Lily said about travel—you grow by seeing things through others' eyes, and I can’t agree more. Then there’s that part of me that has this longing to create something impactful. I often think about starting a project, maybe something involving those stories and experiences I’m collecting from everyone. It could be a blog or a podcast. Sharing what I’ve learned from people, their journeys, their laughter, their struggles, and weaving it all together sounds exciting. I haven’t figured it all out yet, but it’s fun to think about. I feel like we’re all in this together, and I want to be a part of that, somehow fostering more connections, more understanding. And it’s wild to think about how different everything can be in just a few months or years. So much can change, and yet I feel like I’m ready for anything—I guess that’s the beauty of being this age. Every moment is a chance, every day a possibility. Here’s to embracing it!

Okay so I just can’t stop thinking about how terrible my group project is going. Everyone is supposed to pitch in and it’s just me doing all the work. I mean, why do I even bother, you know? I get it, not everyone is as passionate about this topic as I am but if I’m going to get a good grade, I can’t just let them slack off. I need to pull this together. But what if they don’t appreciate it? What if I’m doing all this and they just ride on my coattails? It's beyond frustrating. I can’t even focus on my other classes because I'm so stressed about this one project. It makes me wonder why I even take on these roles in the first place. I don’t need the stress but maybe I like the adrenaline rush of being in control? I just can't stand it when things don't go right. It's this constant worry that if I let my guard down, everything will fall apart. Every time I walk into that classroom, I see the same faces, some of them already glued to their phones. It's infuriating. I don’t get how they can be so nonchalant. I want to shout, hey, this is important! But I can't come off too harsh—I'm not getting into trouble over this. So I just keep my mouth shut and grind my teeth every time I hear someone else making a half-hearted suggestion. Why do they think it's okay to show up unprepared? Am I just surrounded by slackers? I swear, if it wasn't for my sense of responsibility, I wouldn't even bother. But then there's that nagging feeling that I just can't let it go.
