I have to keep pushing forward, making sure everything is perfect. But what is perfect anyway? And why do I have to feel so anxious about everything? Like seriously, I can’t step into a social event without thinking what I'm going to say, what people will think of me, just the whole cycle of overthinking. I can’t let loose because I’m so busy analyzing every little interaction. God, I want to be more chill, more open to new experiences. But it’s like my brain is just working against me. Every time I think I’ve got my emotions in check, bam! Something happens, and I spiral. It could be something small, like a rude comment from a classmate that I just can’t shake off. Or maybe I’ll think about that time I mispronounced a word during a presentation. Everyone laughed, but I couldn’t take it. I replay it in my mind like a broken record. Just stop, right? But I can’t. Then I’ll catch myself obsessing over things that happened days ago – how could I have said that? Did I sound stupid? It’s like I’m my own worst critic. Why can’t I be more like those people who just go with the flow? I’m so rigid sometimes, but it’s because I have a standard, right? Is that a bad thing? I think it’s a good thing. But then you have these moments where you realize you’re isolated because nobody wants to hear Mr. Perfect all the time. Maybe that’s why I’m not good at making friends. I’m too straightforward, too harsh, people can’t handle me. They look at me like I’m some kind of robot. I mean, can't they appreciate that I just want things to be done right? It’s not like I’m being a jerk on purpose. And when I do meet someone who seems cool, I freeze up. I don’t know how to approach them without feeling nervous. I wish I could just walk up and say, “Hey, let’s grab a coffee!” But instead, I run through a checklist in my mind, analyzing every angle of the conversation before it even starts. Ugh, it makes me mad just thinking about it. Maybe I should just try to be more spontaneous, just go for it, but my brain keeps pulling me back. It’s exhausting. I think about how I want to travel, see new places, but then the planning kicks in. It has to be perfect. What if I don’t have the right clothes? What if I don’t know what to do when I get there? Questions piling on questions until I convince myself it’s too much work. But I really want to see Paris, just once. I can picture the Eiffel Tower and all that. But would I just overthink the entire trip instead of enjoying it? Probably. I just have to find a balance, but how? Maybe I should practice small talk more, put myself out there. Or maybe just take a break from trying to please everyone. Who knows? Wouldn’t that be something? Just let go. But here I am. Overthinking again.

Okay so I’m sitting here, and everything feels kind of hazy. I don’t know if it’s the lighting or if I’m just tired again. I really should have gotten more sleep last night but I stayed up watching random videos online. Why do I do that? I always say I’ll go to bed early and then I just end up scrolling and scrolling. There’s something so distracting about screens. It pulls me in like a black hole. I can’t even remember half the things I watched. Just mindless stuff, probably. Something about cats or that weird guy who makes elaborate meals in a tiny kitchen. I shouldn’t care about any of it but I do. Just lose track of time. Then there’s this endless pressure of things I have to do. Like I should be studying but do I feel like it? Not really. I mean, I know I have to, but it’s like this voice in my head says “nah, better to binge-watch that series I’ve already seen five times instead.” And I keep thinking about deadlines I can't shake off. They hang over me like a cloud but I'm not doing anything about it. It’s frustrating to feel that way. I’ve got so many assignments piled up, but every time I sit down to work, it feels like I hit a wall. I don’t know why, but it’s like my brain shuts off. I should just get it done. I can’t believe I agreed to go out with those classmates next weekend. It’s supposed to be a party or something. I’m already dreading it. I mean, small talk with people I barely know? Why would I put myself through that? It’s not that I dislike them, it’s just that the thought of trying to keep a conversation alive feels exhausting. I can already envision standing in a corner, feeling out of place while everyone else seems to be having fun. And what’s even worse? The music will probably be so loud that I won’t even hear what they’re saying. Why do people enjoy that chaos? It feels like a trap. Here’s the thing: I know I can’t avoid social situations completely. But I do what I can. I sometimes wonder what’s wrong with me. Why can’t I just be normal and enjoy gatherings like everyone else seems to? It’s almost suffocating when I think about it too long. There’s always this nagging feeling of not being good enough. I see other people chatting and laughing easily, and then there’s me, standing there looking awkward. Maybe I just don’t care enough or maybe I care too much? It’s confusing. Thoughts bounce around like they’re in a pinball machine. Focus is not my strong suit. Every time I try, something pulls me away. And geez, don’t even get me started on my social media. The more I scroll, the worse it gets. Everything looks so polished and perfect, and I’m sitting here wondering why I can’t achieve that level of “life.” My feed is a mix of influencers and friends showing off their adventures, and it makes me feel like I’m trapped in my own bubble. I get it, I know it’s not real, but it feels real. Like, there’s a weight to it that makes me feel so small. I should just unfollow everyone but then I feel bad. Like they would notice. I can’t decide if I should call my family this week. I mean, I love them and all, but sometimes it’s so draining. My mom always wants to know about everything, and then she starts comparing my life to my cousin’s. I get it, he’s doing well and I should be too according to her. It leaves me feeling kind of bitter. I don’t think it’s fair. Just because he fits a certain mold doesn’t mean I should fall into that too. Then there’s this anxiety creeping in. It’s like a shadow and it follows me everywhere. I try to ignore it, but it’s always there, lurking. I can’t pinpoint what exactly causes it. Sometimes it’s just the thought of stepping outside or facing people. Other times it’s the overwhelming feeling of being unaccomplished. Do I even care about what I’m studying? I kind of wish I could just take a break from everything. Maybe go away somewhere quiet where I wouldn’t have to deal with any of this noise. Like, I could just pack a bag and go. But then reality hits me. I have responsibilities and expectations, and that thought just buries me deeper into my chair. I should really just get back to my assignments. Just start somewhere instead of letting it all pile up. But the cycle restarts again. I’ll probably end up scrolling again instead. Just like always.

Tim’s wearing that ridiculous hat again. The one that looks like a mix between a beanie and a wizard’s cap. Why do people do that? Fashion is so subjective and weird. I can’t believe how much we pay to look like we just rolled out of bed. I mean, it’s almost 2024, and we’re still stuck in this cycle of weird trends. Anyway, I’ve been thinking about this study I’m supposed to be working on. I hope the results come out okay. I don’t know why I signed up for it; probably because I want to help but also because I’m curious about the whole thing. It’s like a mixed bag of motivation. Speaking of curiosity, I read something interesting about how people interpret art differently. Art is subjective, but what if it’s more than that? What if our interpretations say more about us than the artist? Maybe that’s where my openness comes into play. I can see a painting and think about the stories behind it, while others might just see colors and shapes. Part of me wants to dive deeper, to analyze everything, but I can’t help but also feel overwhelmed. The world is just so messy, and I like order. It’s a weird conflict. A text message just popped up. It’s from Sarah. She’s asking if I want to go out tonight. No chance. I know she means well, but my idea of a fun night is usually curled up with a good book or binge-watching that new show everyone’s been raving about. I hate the small talk that comes with gatherings—do they even care what I have to say? I often feel like I’m just there to fill the silence. But saying no feels awkward too. Why is it always so complicated? Sometimes I wonder if I just get in my own way. Like, I overthink everything. It’s exhausting. Why do I need to dissect every little interaction? Like why I said that one thing that felt dumb in retrospect. And the anxiety rises like a tidal wave. I guess that’s where the neuroticism kicks in. I wish I could just chill out, let things happen. But no, instead, I have to replay all the moments in my head until I’m too tired to care. Then I can’t sleep because I’m remembering every embarrassing thing I’ve ever done. It would be easier if I could trust people more but then again, why would I? I’ve seen people betray trust for the silliest reasons. My roommate, Lisa, said she would study with me last week, but she bailed to go party. I mean, it’s not like I’m mad at her, but it’s just disappointing. I guess I expect too much or something. I see the world through this lens where if I’m giving my time and energy, I expect the same in return. But maybe that’s not how it works. Maybe I need to let go of that expectation. I saw an article the other day about how to be more agreeable. It felt like a slap in the face. Why should I change who I am just to please others? If I don’t want to agree with something, I shouldn’t have to. But then again, I do wonder if I’m too rigid. Is it really that bad to have strong opinions? Sometimes I feel like I’m stuck between wanting authenticity and being too blunt for my own good. I should probably work on that. I need to go grocery shopping too. I keep putting it off. The fridge is practically empty except for a questionable jar of pickles and some expired yogurt. Do I even want to deal with that? Grocery stores are always a hassle. People milling about without a care in the world while I’m just trying to navigate the aisles without having a panic attack. Social anxiety is the worst. I bet no one else even notices how many times I check the time or scan the place, feeling overwhelmed. Then again, there’s this little part of me that thrives on discovery. I love finding new ingredients or trying new recipes. It’s therapeutic. I can’t escape the feeling that there’s something cathartic about chopping vegetables and watching them transform into something delicious. And cooking at home is so much better than takeout. The last time I ate that gross Chinese food from the corner place, I swore I’d never go back. Yet here I am, debating whether I should just order in again tonight. Tempting. The clock is ticking, and I haven’t even gotten to the study I’m doing. I need to focus more but it’s hard when my mind is racing everywhere. I wonder what it would be like if I could just embrace spontaneity for once. Go out with Sarah just because. Or try something new. There’s this huge festival coming up next weekend, and I’m debating whether to go. But my gut says no while my brain says yes and who knows which one is the right one? I should decide before I end up overthinking that too. I’m probably just going to end up staying home and binge-watching that show instead. Maybe I’ll even make some popcorn, watch a movie and just for once not worry about what everyone else thinks. That sounds like a plan, right?

I can’t believe I forgot to buy more coffee again. That’s just classic me. It’s almost like my brain files things away and then throws the papers everywhere when I need them. I wonder why I’m so forgetful about these little things. Maybe I get lost in thought too easily? I mean I was trying to focus on that sociology paper but then I started thinking about how interesting social dynamics are and how they could apply to my relationships. Like, do people, including me, really understand how our interactions create the world around us? Anyway, I got so sidetracked. I should probably write that down somewhere so I don’t forget. Ugh, I hate it when I lose track like that. Focus. Okay, so the paper. I have all these notes but they’re scattered like my thoughts, and I just keep wanting to dive into different tangents. Like what if I analyze empathy in groups? That could be cool. I find that so fascinating—how being kind and open can really shift the vibe in a room. But then I think, I should probably stick to the topic. But then again, I don’t want to suffocate my ideas. I hate feeling rigid. It’s like when I work out and try to stick to a routine but then I see a new exercise that looks interesting and I just have to try it. It’s like my mind is always just... exploring, you know? Being open to experiences really makes life richer. I love that about myself. It’s like I can appreciate detail in everything. Yesterday, I spent an hour just looking up different artist techniques. I mean, I don’t even paint, but it’s so cool to think about all the ways people can express themselves. I should try painting again. It was such a calming experience when I did it last summer. The colors, the textures, the way the brush moves—everything just flows and blends together. Maybe that’s what I need more of lately—just some quiet time to create without pressure. But then I remember I have that big exam coming up, and I start to feel a little pressure creeping in. It’s not anxiety, thank goodness. I feel pretty chill about it, but it’s that classic reminder of looming responsibility. Maybe I’ll put on some lo-fi music and get lost in those studies. I really enjoy studying better when I have some background noise. It's like it helps filter out distractions while still letting my mind wander, if that makes sense. I’ve always been kind of a studier—structured and organized. I thrive on a calendar and lists. My friends tease me about it, but I think it's a good thing. Keeps me on track. But I also wonder if it limits me sometimes—like I follow the path too diligently instead of veering off to explore unknowns. What's so interesting about that, though, is how I can balance this need for structure with my openness to new ideas. It’s all about finding that sweet spot, I guess. Like with my friends—whenever we plan stuff, I love that they trust me to suggest fun things but also open to their spontaneous ideas. The last time we went to that new Thai place, I almost didn’t want to go because I was trying to stick to my usual routines, but I went and it was so worth it! Why is it so hard to break old habits sometimes? It’s like my brain has this safe zone it wants to stay in, but the outside world calls to me with its colorful options. I should remind myself to embrace that more often. I mean, how else will I discover unique food, or movies, or ideas if I don’t step outside my usual choices? But I have to admit, sometimes saying “yes” feels exhausting too. I want to be agreeable and supportive, but there’s also that fine line between being there for others and losing touch with what I want. I think that's why I prefer smaller gatherings—like intimate dinners with close friends rather than huge parties. I find that I can engage in deeper conversations that way. Social situations can feel so draining, but with the right people, it’s exhilarating. I wonder about those big gatherings where everyone feels pressured to perform. I grasp the importance of connection. I crave those deep conversations, the ones that linger in your mind after they end. There’s so much to learn from others, and I genuinely love listening to their stories. I feel like I understand people better when they share their lives, their thoughts. And I think that’s where my agreeableness comes into play because I care. I really do. It's such a rewarding feeling, being there for people when they need it. I wouldn’t trade that for anything. Okay, I think I’ve strayed far enough. Time to get back on track. I’ll just... grab that coffee when I can and focus on my paper after this. Just keep moving forward, one step at a time, maybe with a little bit of adventure mixed in. Yeah, that sounds good.

I don’t know why I’m staring at this wall. It feels like it’s mocking me, like it knows I’m trying to come up with something meaningful. Why do I even care? I could just as easily get up and leave but then again, where would I go? The same places I always go, which is nowhere sometimes and everywhere at others. I hate small talk. I dread it, actually. It’s like when someone asks how your day is going, and all you want to respond with is “do you really want to know?” because honestly nobody cares. Or maybe they do, but it feels so forced. People treat social interaction like it’s a chore, like it has some rules and somewhere there is a handbook that I’m not privy to. I think about that a lot. Handbooks for life. I’d definitely want to skim through the “how to deal with social situations” chapter and underline the parts that make no sense to me. Do I even want to fit in? What’s fitting in anyway? It’s exhausting to think about. I wonder if I’m doing this all wrong. Like I’m here in this world trying to be a person, but being a person seems so emotional. Like do I need to be emotional? I get annoyed easily. Just the other day, someone said something that I can’t even remember but it rubbed me the wrong way. Just a casual comment, something about the weather or whatever, but I suddenly felt like everyone in the room was judging me. I know that’s irrational, but still, there it is. Anxiety tied in a bow. Sometimes I think I’m one of those people who’s perpetually anxious. It’s like a second skin. Then there’s the idea of doing things. I mean, I have all these ideas swimming around in my head, so I’m open, right? But then I don’t actually do anything with them. It’s like I’m capable of a million things but also incapable of getting myself to do any of them. Procrastination? I practically invented that concept—grab a snack instead of reading those assignments. Is that why I have low conscientiousness? I just get impatient and distracted. I’ll try to focus, really, but then I start thinking about something random like the number of books I’ve read this year or my favorite childhood cartoon, which now feels like it illustrates just how bizarre life is. Life is bizarre! Why do I have to know the weather when I could just decide it doesn’t matter? Or why do we even have to have routines? Those feel stifling. Like everyone is on some schedule that doesn’t make sense to me. But how do you break out of that? What’s life like without the mundane? And why do people care? People seem to care a lot about fitting in; it’s like a behaviorist experiment you didn’t sign up for. It’s strange. Like, I’ll see people laughing together and I’m so tempted to just butt in and blurt something out, but my brain gets hung up on the “what if they think I’m weird?” question that never seems to dissipate. I’ve always found that odd. I can be open to experiences, yet I get so caught up in my own head about what others think. I wish I could disengage from that. What if I just said whatever came to mind? What if I decided to embrace the awkward silence that follows? But then, what’s the point? It feels like I keep reaching for something just out of reach. It’s infuriating. Yet, there are these little moments when I feel like I’m really alive, when I stumble upon an interesting fact or when a song strikes a chord. Those bursts of creativity are exhilarating, the beauty of an idea just lighting up my brain. Sometimes, I wonder if I’m in the wrong field. What if I’m supposed to be more creative? But then I think about the pressure of that and it terrifies me. What if I can’t deliver? What if my ideas aren’t good enough? And there’s that cycle again—openness and creativity strangled by anxiety and dread. I remember once thinking about how I’d love to go to art galleries and just immerse myself in the colors and the expressions, but then the thought comes crashing down: Who would I go with? And does it even matter—do I want to share that with someone? It’s all just a jumble. I think it’d be better alone sometimes, but then I spiral into this self-reflective abyss. How do I balance that freedom of thought with this overwhelming urge to stay hidden? The paradox of wanting solitude while also craving connection whispers in my ear. I can’t quite figure it out. Maybe it’s just part of being who I am. Wouldn’t it be nice if I could just be?

okay so I was just thinking about how the weekend is coming up and it’s always so exciting like what should we do this time everyone always wants to hang out and that's great but sometimes I just want to chill too like is that selfish? I don't think so it's just balance right? I mean we can do so many things like go out dancing or have a movie night or hit up that new restaurant everyone’s raving about but then there's the whole decision-making process which can get kind of overwhelming like what if they don't want to do what I want to do which is totally fine but I want everyone to be happy but I also want to be happy and what if we don't find a place to eat that everyone likes? It’s like the ultimate social game and sometimes I feel like I’m way too invested like it’s so important to me to keep the peace but then again isn’t that part of who I am? I remember this one time last summer we went to the lake and it was just perfect the weather was warm but not too hot and everyone just vibed you know the music, the laughter, the jokes, I love making people laugh it brings me so much joy and I realized I have this almost innate ability to lift the mood wherever I go just walk in and it’s like a light switch and people smile and I’m all about that energy like if your energy is high I want to be around you and if you’re down I wanna help you out like let’s talk, eat ice cream, go for a walk, or just sit quietly together that’s okay too. Sometimes I think about how hard it is to find that balance with people though like some friends are so different like one will text me like “what’s up?” and I will immediately think “let’s plan something big and awesome” while another is like “let’s just hang” and sometimes I don’t know how to adjust my high energy to match their low and that can get tricky but it’s all part of learning about people right? It’s like a game of chess where you have to think ahead, know when to be aggressive, and when to hold back. I don’t know maybe I overthink it a bit but I think it’s cool how personalities mix and clash like a vibrant palette or something. Speaking of colors, I can’t help but think about that mural downtown that everyone talks about - you know the one with the giant flowers and the bright blues and pinks? I can’t wait to go take photos with friends there again I’m thinking it will make for a perfect social media moment we can all strike fun poses like “look how artsy we are!” but really it’s just a backdrop to the fun we’re having together i love capturing moments and memories I keep thinking about how photos mean so much more than just pixels they tell stories of who we are and where we’ve been. I should probably print some out and make a scrapbook that could be something nice to do on our next rainy day. And don’t get me started on food I could talk about food all day long like when did brunch become such a big thing? People go nuts for avocado toast but honestly I’m a waffle girl myself like give me a stack with some fresh berries and whipped cream and I am set for the day. It’s fascinating how food brings people together we had that potluck last month and everything from tacos to pasta was laid out on the table and we all just dove in it felt so nice to share not just the food but the love and the time spent together breaking bread like a family. I feel lucky to have people in my life who want to connect. Then there’s travel oh man I love traveling what’s better than exploring new places talking to new people? The thrill of stepping off a plane in a new city and seeing what they have to offer it’s like opening a present you never know what you’re going to get. Last year I went to Europe and it was just magical everything was so different and beautiful all the streets, the history, the culture I just want to keep doing that finding new spots and experiencing things from different viewpoints but I also gotta remember to save money and not break the bank because then I can’t hang out with my friends which is super important. But okay so back to friendships I’m so grateful I have such a diverse group of people around me like I have friends from different majors, cultures, backgrounds it’s a treasure trove of ideas and perspectives and it’s just so refreshing to have those conversations that challenge my thinking but also expand it I love when someone says “hey have you thought about it this way?” and it’s like BAM my mind opens up to a whole new world I want to learn so much and grow and I want my friends to grow too we should always be learning together and pushing each other toward greatness. Time just flies when I’m thinking about all of this it’s so interesting to see how interconnected everything is how one little thought can lead you down a rabbit hole of memories and ideas and dreams. I think the key is just to keep moving forward to embrace the chaos of life and all the joy that comes with it.

Okay so I just saw a video on social media about this new trend where people are painting their kitchens bright colors like neon green or hot pink and I can’t decide if that’s genius or just totally insane. Like do people really want to cook in a place that looks like a rave? I mean, it’s kind of cool and I love bright colors but also can you imagine how distracting that would be? I'd want to dance around instead of chop onions but maybe that’s just me I’ve always been more of a “let’s have fun” kind of person. I like to get everyone involved. I wonder what my friend Sarah would think of it. She’s all about those minimalist vibes, white everything, so boring! But then again she does have the best avocado toast recipe. Ugh can we talk about food? I could go on for hours. Yesterday I went to this new taco place downtown and I had the weirdest combination of flavors, there was some sort of mango salsa or something, and it was just the perfect mix of sweet and savory like I could eat that every day for the rest of my life. And then there was this spicy sauce that made my mouth feel like it was on fire, but in a good way! How do they even come up with this stuff? I want to try making it at home but what if it doesn’t taste anywhere near as good? I can't even keep track of all the cooking shows I've watched where chefs just whip up these amazing dishes like it's nothing. I want to be like that, I mean maybe hosting a dinner party would be fun, I love having people over. My apartment’s kind of small but I could rearrange the furniture to make it feel more open. That might be a task, but I can make it happen. I always start with this great plan in my head and then I get distracted halfway through because I found a random old book I forgot I had and now I’m lost in this story about a secret society of librarians hunting for a hidden treasure. Like, who comes up with this stuff? It’s wild! Oh, and speaking of wild, I was out at this bar last weekend and I ended up having this deep conversation with a stranger about life goals and dreams and it was so refreshing. It’s like there’s this whole world of people out there and we’re just waiting for the opportunity to connect and share ideas, but sometimes it takes a few drinks to break down that barrier. I mean, I’m pretty outgoing but even I sometimes find it tough to just approach someone and say “Hey, what’s your life philosophy?” But maybe it’s just how I roll. I thrive on those unexpected moments and conversations. On the other hand, I think about how some people get overwhelmed by social situations. My roommate is like that. I sometimes forget because I’m always ready for the next party or the next big adventure, and she’s just content at home with her cat binging on Netflix. I mean, there's nothing wrong with that, but I always feel like we’re missing out on something. I guess we just see the world in different ways. It kind of balances out though. I can get things done and she keeps me calm, like a perfect team. Speaking of teams, I’m excited about the upcoming soccer season but also a bit nervous. I’ve always loved being active but there’s this pressure to perform well, especially since we’re all competitive. But the camaraderie makes it worth it. I need to remember to stay positive and just enjoy the game instead of getting caught up in my own head about performance. It’s just a game, right? But I love running around, feeling the wind on my face, and the thrill of scoring a goal! Sometimes I wonder if I should try something new like rock climbing or salsa dancing. Could you imagine? I’m not the best dancer but I think I’d have a blast just flailing my arms around and laughing at myself. There’s something freeing about just letting go of the need to be perfect. A good friend of mine is really into hiking but I’ve never been that great with nature. I always seem to get lost, but maybe that’s part of the adventure? Sometimes I think about just getting in the car and driving somewhere completely random, just feeling the wind and the music, and letting the road take me wherever. Then I go back to thinking about my future. What’s next? Should I take that leap into entrepreneurship? Part of me wants to jump into the unknown and start something completely fresh, maybe an event planning business. I’m always coming up with new ideas for parties and gatherings. I think it could be super fun if I just took the plunge. There’s so much to explore, and I want to embrace that, be creative and make it happen! Time is ticking, and life’s too short to stick to the same routine forever. I want to live! I want to create! I want to challenge myself and push my boundaries!

It’s weird how you can just sit in a room and feel like the walls are closing in. There’s this low hum in my head, like a fridge that won’t stop running, and it makes everything feel annoying. God, why can’t I just focus on one thing? The pile of laundry is staring at me like it has eyes. I should really do something about that. My phone buzzes again, probably a notification I won’t check or maybe I will but I’ll regret it because it’s always some group chat that feels like a giant weight. The “hey, what’s everyone up to” message pops up and my brain goes into a spiral—what am I supposed to say, what do they want from me? I mean they want to hang out but I don’t want to, I don’t even know why they insist on it. It’s exhausting just thinking about it. I’d rather stay here in my bubble, my fortress of solitude, which is really just my messy room packed with half-finished projects and crumb-covered plates. I remember in high school everyone made plans for after graduation and I was like, “You guys do you, I’ll be over here.” I’ve always been that way, like stuck in the middle of a crowded party, watching everyone else enjoy themselves while I’m just trying to find a corner to breathe. Why do people like socializing? Anxiety creeps up whenever I think about it, like I can't keep up with the small talk. I can’t even figure out if I have anything interesting to say, and it’s such a relief when the plans fall apart. It’s sad but also true. I’d probably rather binge-watch another series I’ve seen five times than deal with the pressure of forced conversation. Like, right now, there’s this show I watched last month; something about a couple going through therapy and it’s supposed to be relatable? But it just seems exaggerated. I mean, every time they dig into their past, I’m thinking about how unnecessary all that drama feels. It’s like, don’t people realize that everybody has issues? I’m sitting there, silently judging how they’re processing things and wishing they’d just stop talking to each other and deal with their lives on their own. Maybe that’s just me being cynical. My stomach feels weird, like it’s twisting into knots just thinking about people. I really need to buy groceries but the thought of walking into a crowded store makes me feel sick. I swear I can hear the cart wheels and people mumbling constantly, and it makes me want to turn around and leave. Why can’t groceries just magically appear at my door? Or maybe I could just stockpile enough food for a year and never go out again. Wouldn’t that be a dream? Just enough chips and soda to sustain me for, like, forever. Sounds ridiculous but it’s not like anyone really checks in on me anyway. I think about sending a few texts, but then I stop myself. What do I even say? “Hey, I’m alive?” That makes me sound like I’m fishing for pity or something. I don’t want anybody’s pity, but then again, isn’t that what you’re supposed to do? Stay in touch with people? Act like you care? Of course, I care about some people, but it’s too much sometimes. They don’t get it. I don’t want to feel obligated to message someone back just because they asked how I’m doing. I mean, I could tell them, I’m doing fine. But inside, it’s a different story. So here I am, resenting simple conversations, and, you know, social norms. Why can’t everyone just agree to leave each other alone? I know that sounds harsh, but it’s what I want. I’m tired of pretending it’s all sunshine and rainbows when I can’t even figure out my own thoughts half the time. Trapped—like in this mental labyrinth where every turn leads back to overwhelming feelings. It’s like a vicious cycle. I wish I could just send a text that says “Let’s never talk again” and have that be the end of it. How liberated would that feel—if friendships could just end with a click? But then I think about how lonely that would actually be. Like, I need someone to talk to sometimes. Just not all the time, right? Maybe I can send a message later, some half-hearted “let’s hang out sometime,” just so they know I’m not completely off the grid. Another half-assed attempt at connection that I’ll probably forget about five minutes after I hit send. Okay, let’s just stare at this pile of clothes instead. It’s not going to do itself, but the worst part is, I’m not even sure where I’d start. There’s just something about laundry, like you have to sort it out first. Categories: darks, lights, delicates, whatever. It’s like sorting through my life, then remembering that I barely keep track of the small things, let alone relationships.

It’s 3:47 PM and I’m staring at the ceiling. Got this weird ceiling fan that needs a good cleaning. Dust bunnies everywhere. What’s the deal with dust anyway? I mean, it just sits around and collects. Are we just too lazy to clean? Probably. I should vacuum, but I won’t. There are better things to do. Like scrolling through my phone. That’s basically all I need. Social media is just noise. Who even cares what anyone's doing? Maybe I care sometimes, but not really. It’s just a habit, I guess. I have this friend who posts constantly, like every meal, every day, and I just can’t even. The world is not your diary. They say it’s good to connect or whatever, but nah. I’m fine being alone. Why do people act like being alone is a disease? It's peaceful. Commercials interrupt everything. Why do they exist? They’re loud. I mute the TV during commercials. Is that why there’s so much streaming now? Everyone got tired of commercials? Makes sense. I’m supposed to be studying but all these distractions keep popping up. Here I am, typing my thoughts instead of reading. I thought I’d write about something important but really, I don’t care. I should care more about my grades, but they don’t even feel important right now. School feels like a series of hoops. Jump through this one, jump through that one. It gets old. Classes are fine if they don’t drag on. But most of them do. Why can't they just get to the point? I don’t know, maybe I’m overthinking it. It’s just all a waste of time, isn’t it? I keep staring at my bookshelf across the room. Books I haven’t read. Thick ones that look intimidating. Why did I even buy those? Probably some recommendation I saw online. I didn’t even know what I was getting into. I can’t focus long enough to read. I go in circles. I start and then I lose interest and put it down. Who cares about finishing a book? Maybe it’s okay not to finish things. Why is that a thing? You don’t always have to see things through. It’s like they say, “don’t start what you can’t finish.” But then what about all the unfinished projects? The half-eaten meals? The weird crafts I started but never touched again? Reality is messy, and that's fine. The world is wild, and I don’t even want to be part of that chaos sometimes. Listening to music is nice, but then it gets repetitive. I can’t even find songs I really like anymore. Everything sounds the same. More beats, less thought. No one seems to care about lyrics, but that’s what I used to love. Now it’s all about streams and likes and whatever is trending. If it was even worth it, I’d feel differently, but it’s just noise filling up my brain. Relationships seem overrated too. Everyone's so obsessed with finding their “people.” If I wanted people around me, I’d just get a dog. At least dogs don’t talk back or have a bunch of emotional baggage. They just need snacks and walks. Simple, clean cut. I think about dogs while I scroll through my phone. I should volunteer at a shelter. That might be nice. But then I think about the people I’d have to deal with and eh, maybe not. My phone buzzes. A message. I’ll check it later. It’s probably not important. I’m not the type to reply right away. A little mystery never hurt anyone, right? Plus, I don’t always have something smart to say anyway. It’s just tiny white words on a screen. The world is built on communication, and everyone just tosses words around like they don’t mean anything. I don’t want to waste my time responding unless it’s something worth talking about. It’s all small talk nonsense. The weather, shows, school. Who really cares? Ok, so I’m contemplating making frozen pizza. It’s always in the back of my mind when I’m stuck in thought. That’s a given. I can always count on pizza to save the day. Easy, no cooking skills required. Just pop it in the oven and wait for it to do its thing. A reliable friend. Then there’s that satisfaction of slicing it into perfect triangles. It’s oddly therapeutic. But then, eating alone feels lonely sometimes. Not that I want to eat with anyone else—too much noise, too many questions. I could just stare at the wall while I chew. Silence is key to surviving. It’s like my own bubble. I can sit there with my thoughts swirling all around, but that’s fine. It’s just me. There’s something about being content in your own space. So maybe I’m a loner, and that’s okay. I don’t feel the need to explain myself. I don’t even really want to go out unless it’s absolutely necessary. I’m fine with my pizza and my thoughts.

Okay so here we go, just typing away without thinking too much about it. The clock just ticked over to 3:15 PM and I’m wondering what’s for dinner later. Probably the same thing I always have, something easy. I hate trying new recipes and always end up going back to the same few things. It’s just easier that way. I don’t get the appeal of adventurous eating or travel for that matter. I see other people sharing their “foodie experiences” on social media, but it just seems like a lot of effort for a meal. I’d rather just grab something familiar and go back to my usual routine. Thinking about how I just don’t understand how people make friends so easily. I don’t get it. Like, what’s the point of hanging out? Small talk drives me nuts. I never know what to say. It’s awkward all around. People just talk about the weather or some new movie they saw. Also, the movies these days feel so recycled. I mean how many superhero movies can one franchise produce? It’s exhausting. I’d rather just stick to my interests, which might mean binging another documentary or something nobody else seems to care about. I was scrolling through some social media feed earlier, and I just don't get the fascination with influencers. The whole concept is bizarre. I mean, who cares about their daily life? Who even are these people? What makes them interesting? They all seem the same to me. Having millions of followers does not equal substance or worth. It’s almost funny how people idolize them like they’re some kind of royalty. Then again, I guess everyone has their own thing. People seem to need to connect with something or someone, but I just don’t see the value in it. If I’m being honest, it’s simpler to just be alone. More quiet, less hassle. I’m okay with it. The constant need for social validation is baffling. Why can’t people just be comfortable with themselves? They want opinions and likes and comments. Just feels draining. Sometimes I think about how I should probably try to care more about these things. Like I know it’s important to have friends or keep in touch with family but honestly, it feels like a chore. People ask me when I’ll settle down or go out with them, and I just shrug it off. I don’t feel any urgency. Maybe I would if they offered to pay for my dinner or something. That’s a fair trade. I just don’t believe in wasting my time on things that don’t interest me. I hate the idea of forced interactions. Like you have to engage in “team-building” activities. Gross. Can’t I just be left to my own devices? I don’t need to bond over trust falls or whatever nonsense. Just let me do my own thing. And then there’s work. I don’t get why people love their jobs so much. It’s just work. It pays the bills. I go in, do what I’m supposed to, and leave. Simple. No need for all that passion nonsense. I see coworkers talking about “finding purpose” and I just roll my eyes. Do they not realize everyone is here for the paycheck? I think about school a lot too. University can be such a drag. The repetitive nature of classes. All the required readings. Half the time I wonder why I’m even here. Just to get a piece of paper that says I can do something. The lectures are often boring, and nobody seems to want to get to the point. I don’t see the need for group projects either. Why can’t I just do my own work? I like to control what I produce. I don’t have time to deal with other people’s nonsense. Then there's this looming project due next week. I should probably get started, but I don’t see the urgency. I feel like I can just wing it, hit the bare minimum, and call it a day. They say it's important to be thorough and engaged, but why? It’s just a grade. I don’t need to pour my soul into it. Oh, and why does everyone care so much about the latest trends? I don't get it. Clothing styles change so rapidly. I’m perfectly fine wearing the same old thing. It saves time and effort. I don’t feel the need to impress anyone. Lastly, I’ve noticed how very few people seem to appreciate silence. It’s like a golden space. Just being alone with my thoughts is refreshing. Why do we need constant noise and chatter? I don’t get how some people thrive on chaos. I guess that’s just the way things go. Different strokes for different folks, I suppose. But for me, it’s nice just being this way, even if it’s a little unconventional to others.

I just realized my backpack is still sitting in the corner, it’s messy as usual. I need to organize it, but it’s so much effort. Like, I mean I have all these pens rolled at the bottom, and papers I forgot about stacked up. I used to keep it neat, but I don’t know what happened. Maybe it’s the end of the semester. No, I shouldn’t make excuses. It’s always about priorities and time management. Why does everyone want to talk about feelings all the time? I don’t get that. I prefer facts or just straightforward plans. My roommate is always like, “Let’s share how we feel about our day” and I just nod. What’s the point? Just tell me what’s due tomorrow. I like lists. I live by them. Grocery, assignments, things to do. Simple stuff. I keep things in order so nothing slips through the cracks. I can’t stand chaos. Like when I walk into the kitchen and see dishes piled up. It makes me anxious. I have to clean it up, but then I’m like, why should I? It’s not my mess. But the thought of leaving it makes my skin crawl. Okay, focus. I should probably start studying for that exam next week. Biochemistry is brutal this semester. Why did I think taking that class was a good idea? It’s all memorization and pathways that make my head spin. I need to focus on the Krebs cycle again. I have a diagram somewhere. I hope I saved that in my files. I can’t remember if I did. Ugh, I should have labeled it better. I hate when I can’t find files. Why can’t everyone just organize their stuff? I’m a bit of a control freak about it, but it’s annoying when people just toss things around, like my notes. They don’t seem to understand how relaxing order is. I think I’d rather clean than socialize. People stress me out. They’re unpredictable. I like knowing what to expect. Like the library hours. They are consistent. I can plan my studying around that. I don’t want surprises. Should I even go to that group project meeting? It feels pointless. I mean, I know I would do better without them but whatever. We need to get it done, and I need a good grade. I’ll go, but I’ll just sit and listen. I’ll probably end up doing most of the work anyway. It always happens. People talk a lot, but they don’t contribute, and then I’m stuck formatting everything. I don’t mind the work itself, but I get annoyed when no one cares. Why do they even sign up for group projects if they don’t want to do anything? I should just take charge, but they don’t seem to like that either. I hate this weird balance of trying to get the work done while also trying to be mindful of everyone’s feelings. Why is it always so complicated? The weather has been weird lately. It rains like every other day. I keep forgetting to bring an umbrella. I hate being wet. It’s so uncomfortable. I really want to build a routine where I check the weather every morning. That would make everything easier. Planning ahead makes me feel secure. But then I look at my planner, and it’s just filled with appointments and deadlines. Not enough free time. I need to schedule some time for myself in there, but then I feel guilty for taking a break. I feel like I’m not accomplishing enough. When I relax, I think about everything I have to do and then I end up stressing out. I don’t know how to unwind properly. I saw a friend post about yoga the other day and honestly, I just rolled my eyes. It isn’t for me. I’d rather go for a run or lift weights, something that gets my heart pumping and is structured. Yoga feels too freeform. I can’t just sit there and stretch. I need to be moving with a purpose. And then the whole mindfulness thing? What a load. Just focus on your goals, grind it out, and achieve. That’s what life is about. No time to waste on deep breathing. Who’s got time for that when there are grades to maintain? They always say to open up, but it’s exhausting. Why can’t people just focus on the task at hand? I like my space, my quiet time. I’m totally fine with living my life with a clear boundary. I should take a break from socials again, at least until I get through finals. Less noise. But then there’s always the fear of missing out. What if they have fun without me? Not that I want to join. But it’s silly to think like that. Just stay focused. Who cares what they’re doing? It’s not my problem. Just pass the semester first, then think about everything else.

I can't believe Sarah canceled again, I was really looking forward to that movie night. We were supposed to watch that new thriller everyone's talking about. It’s like people don’t understand the value of plans, you know? It gets me a bit anxious when things change at the last minute. What am I supposed to do now? Just stay home with my dog? Not that I mind, he's a great companion, but sometimes I crave the social interaction. I should probably text Jenna and see if she wants to hang out but I don’t want to bother her, she’s been so busy with her new job. Everyone’s getting busy, then there’s me, always wanting to connect. It’s kind of exhausting trying to keep everyone together. I feel like I’m the glue, or at least I want to be the glue. But sometimes it feels like no one else cares as much to stick around. It’s almost 6 PM, oh my god, I need to figure out dinner soon. It's always like this, I forget to plan meals ahead and then I just want to order takeout, but I feel guilty spending money on that when I could just make a sandwich. But sandwiches don’t feel satisfying enough. Maybe I’ll make pasta. Pasta’s safe, you can’t really mess that up, right? It’s comforting too. I could throw in some veggies to make it seem healthier. I hope I have garlic in the fridge. I can’t stand the thought of bland food. It needs flavor. But who has time to chop things? I should just stick to frozen meals, that’s easier. But then, do I really want that? Maybe I should invite Rachel over, she's always a good time and provides that right amount of chaos. But I’m not sure if she’ll bring along that guy she’s dating. He can be a bit much sometimes and I don't want it to be awkward. Social dynamics are tricky, I feel like there’s an unspoken set of rules that I never quite grasp. I wish I could just not care and invite whoever without overthinking it but there’s that nagging voice in my head. What if she doesn’t want to come if he comes? What if she feels pressured to bring him? Uncertainty is the worst, like a weight on my chest. Okay, let’s figure this out. What if I text Rachel and say, hey, it’s a chill night, no pressure if you want to bring him or not? That could work. But then I get worried about the potential for rejection. She could say, no, I’m busy, or maybe she’ll say she’s coming but is just humoring me. Would that mean I’m annoying? It’s too much to unpack, but I’m sure she’ll be cool about it. She usually is. I just want to feel like things are set in stone sometimes. But then again, I think maybe it’s good to be flexible, but I’m not really good at that. Do I need another hobby? I have the time, sort of, but it sounds exhausting. What about painting? I think it would be fun, but then I’m thinking “what if I’m terrible and mess it up?” I can’t stand the thought of a failed project sitting there reminding me that I tried and it didn’t work out. I guess I’m too self-conscious for something like that. It's funny how I can socialize with a room full of people but can’t handle a simple painting that might not look good. But that also makes me want to be better at it, to prove I can do things well. But then I go back to thinking maybe it’s a waste of time to pick something I know I might not enjoy. And then, there’s the group project looming next week. Ugh, I can’t even think about that right now. I need to coordinate with everyone, but sometimes people don’t get back to you and I need to make sure everything is organized. I just don’t want to be the one to lose points if they don’t pull their weight. So I have to be the one to double-check everything. It’s like I have a radar for responsibility, and I can’t ignore it. But what if we don’t even get a good grade? All this effort, for nothing. Or worse, I’ll get stuck with someone who doesn’t care and I’ll just be frustrated again, and it’ll haunt me. I’m usually the one organizing the meetings, sending the reminders. Ooh, I should definitely plan a weekend trip for us, like a getaway or something! Beach, hiking, maybe a fun camping trip? I could start with a group chat and see who’s in. Maybe I’ll mention it to Rachel and gauge her interest first. If she’s excited, that might just convince others. But what if no one else wants to go? What if I’m the only one that cares about these little adventures? It’s hard not to think about who’s really in for the long run. That thought just dances around my mind, exhausting but pushing me forward into social plans, because I can’t just curl up into my shell forever. I think I’d miss out on too much.

okay so I’m just staring at this empty page and thinking about how much I love organizing things like my bookshelf or my closet even if it’s not messy but there’s always room for improvement like maybe I could color code it or arrange by genre and how satisfying that is it’s like a little triumph every time I place that perfect book in the perfect spot and each time I do that I feel this little rush of achievement but then I think about how much I hate wasting time like I should be studying or writing that essay that’s due next week but it feels so far away I’m not even stressed about it which is nice but then again maybe I should be a little bit more stressed like a little anxiety could help push me to actually do something productive and then I remember how my friend always says deadlines are like motivation in disguise because it’s like a little push to get things done but that doesn’t really apply to me because I can usually get things done ahead of time I guess that’s the conscientiousness in me like I’m not saying I’m perfect but I really do enjoy checking things off a list seriously is there anything more satisfying than that sound of a pen scratching that line through a task like it’s this little victory against procrastination oh and I really should do laundry soon it’s piling up but I did manage to clean my room last weekend which felt like a monumental feat it was like a whole new space like I could breathe in there it’s funny how a tidy space can really change your headspace like it’s comforting and calming I sometimes think how people underestimate the power of environment and how it impacts mood and productivity and how living in a clean space makes me feel like I’m in control of everything even though life is chaotic sometimes and I like that reassuring feeling it’s hard being out of control all the time I remember last semester I was so stressed about group projects like the kind where you have to deal with everyone’s different working styles and attitudes and some people just don’t care as much as I do which makes me want to just take over and do everything myself but then I remember how important teamwork is and how it teaches patience and communication but still it’s frustrating because I can’t help but want to achieve the best result possible and then I think about how I can be a little too accommodating sometimes like I want everyone to be happy and it’s a fine line because I can’t please everyone and I don’t want to be a doormat but it’s tough when you want to maintain harmony and balance but then sometimes you need to stand up for what you believe in like when that girl in class was just dismissing everyone’s opinions in the discussion and I had to interrupt and just say hey that’s not fair but it made my heart race and I hate that pressure of speaking up but it felt good afterward even if it was uncomfortable in the moment and I think about how in those moments I feel so alive like taking risks like when I tried that new food recently it was some spicy ramen I wasn’t sure if I’d like it but I went for it and wow my mouth was on fire but in a good way like pushing boundaries and exploring new flavors it’s like stepping into adventure without leaving my comfort zone and I guess that’s what being open means like trying new things and learning about different cultures even if I don’t have much exposure because I love reading about them which makes me think about how literature can transport you to other worlds and how amazing it is that there are people out there living those lives and then I get this hunger to soak it all in maybe I should take a creative writing class or something I wonder if I’d be good at it probably not since I’m so critical of myself but who knows I could discover a hidden talent or just have a blast with it writing can be freeing sometimes unless you get stuck in your head about it and then the words just drain away and I hate that feeling of being blocked it’s like going blank in class when the professor asks a question and you just freeze because your mind goes completely blank and then you feel everyone’s eyes on you and it’s the worst feeling ever why is that so common but then I think about how everyone has those moments and we’re all in the same boat we’re just students trying to figure it out but then again some people seem to have it all together and how does that happen but I guess it’s all a facade and everyone has their struggles and maybe that’s what connects us that vulnerability but then I think of how easy it is to fall into that comparison trap and I don’t want to do that it's just draining but I find myself doing it anyway and then I have to remind myself that we’re all on our own paths and that’s okay so I’ll just keep planning and organizing and learning and growing.

I can't believe I forgot to call Sarah back—where did the time go? Oh, I'm thinking about that party last week. Everybody was there, like a swarm of bees buzzing around, and I just thrived in it, even when I felt a little anxious because of the crowd and all the small talk. I wonder if it’s weird how excited I get being around so many people but also how my mind races with worries like whether I said the right thing or if they think I'm over the top. Maybe I was too loud? But then again, who cares, right? I think it makes it more fun for people, having someone to amplify the energy. Speaking of which, I need to text Sarah about brunch this weekend, it's weird how I can't stand the idea of missing out on fun stuff even if I’m just a little frazzled. Is it neurotic to stress about these things? Maybe, but I can always rely on my friends to pull me out of my head. That feels nice. But do I worry too much—like, what if they get tired of me? But then I think nah, they love my energy, right? What was I saying? Oh, yeah, brunch! I could suggest that new place that just opened up. Everyone says it’s great, and I’m so open to new experiences, especially when food’s involved. I wonder what their special is—something with avocado, probably, everyone is obsessed with avocado. Boring! I want something wild like a breakfast taco or a dragon fruit smoothie or something that just makes you feel alive. Why does food have to be so predictable? I really want to try making smoothies at home—like smoothie bowls with all the toppings, but I don't even have the blender at my place. Ugh, I just remember my roommate borrowed it and never brought it back. I should just buy a new one, I mean why not? What if I threw a smoothie-making party? That could be fun. Everyone in the kitchen, bright colors, talking, sharing recipes. I love that. Now that I think about it, do I ever do things half-heartedly? I mean, yeah, that’s probably true, like my studies. I should probably be stressing more about my classes but I keep telling myself I’ll get it done eventually. It’ll work out, right? Everyone around me seems to be so focused and diligent, but that just makes me feel a bit shaky inside like I’m not enough. But I’m friendly! I can make connections, and I thrive on those. Maybe I could just network my way through school? I mean, that has to count for something in the long run. Speaking of connections, I was scrolling through social media earlier, and wow, the number of travel photos everyone posts is insane. Like, how do they afford that? I mean, I want to travel, I want to go anywhere else, Asia, Australia, Europe! But for now, it’s about other experiences, like going out in the city, like that concert I went to last month. The energy was electric, and I forgot about everything else; all the anxiety just disappeared when I was singing along with strangers, jumping up and down. Music just gets me in a place where I feel alive. I texted the group chat about going to another concert next week, but I didn’t get many responses. Did they forget? That’s a bummer. Okay, back to brunch, I really need to get on that. I want to pull everyone together, but I don’t want to feel like I'm putting too much pressure on others. I hope they want to have fun, too. Why does it always feel so daunting? Like, I’m just going to send the text. Worst case, they are busy—best case, they’re down for pancakes and mimosas and laughs. You know, I just want to create those moments that make life feel a little less chaotic. What if I also suggested a game night? Maybe that’d get my friends in—a game of Cards Against Humanity or something where we can just go wild and laugh at the dumbest jokes. I have this image in my head of us all crowded around the table, drinks spilling, laughter spilling, and I can feel my heart racing just thinking about it. Okay, I need to shift my focus. What about that presentation coming up? I’ve got to start working on that. Ugh, but I don’t want to! I’m so not in the mood. I should just wing it, but I know I’ll get all anxious thinking about it last minute. But hey, I can just talk and make it lively, throw in some jokes, keep it entertaining, right? I’ll just need to figure out a good hook. Alright, time to wrap this up. Time flies when you’re just spilling thoughts. It’s funny how my mind darts from thing to thing. I guess I’ll figure it all out somehow. Life’s too short to stress too much anyway.

Okay so I'm sitting here thinking about all the things I want to do this weekend. I mean the world is just full of possibilities, right? Like I could totally go explore that new art exhibit downtown or hit up a food festival. Food festivals are the best! So many flavors and people to meet. I love how everyone is just there to enjoy and share their love for food. It’s like a combination of all my favorite things – meeting people, trying new things, enjoying life. That reminds me, did I ever tell you about that time I tried octopus tacos? Mind-blowing, I swear. Totally unexpected. Speaking of unexpected, I’ve been thinking about jumping on a last-minute flight somewhere. I know I can just reach out to a couple of friends and we’d be off to who knows where. Spontaneity is where it's at. Life’s too short to plan every single detail. Like, I can't be bothered with checking every box and making sure I’ve followed a strict schedule. I mean, who needs a plan when you have friends who are up for anything? And it’s so much fun just being in the moment, feeling the energy of whatever city we land in. I love how traveling opens your mind, makes you appreciate different cultures, and especially food. Food is the gateway to everything. I keep thinking about how people sometimes get so caught up in social media and how curated everything looks. Sometimes I just want to shove it all aside and go have a real experience, you know? Like, I want to be at a beach bonfire, laughing with friends, sharing stories with random people who just joined in, and the warmth of the fire is contrasted by the chill of the ocean breeze. Last time I went to a bonfire, we ended up doing karaoke on the beach. It was so random, but everyone joined in and we all sounded terrible but no one cared, and then we just made up our songs. It was hilarious. That’s the kind of stuff I live for. It’s funny how my mind jumps from one thing to another. I can see the whole night happening in my head. Beach, fire, laughter. Wait, I need to remember to bring marshmallows next time. And s'mores! How did I forget about s'mores? Chocolate, graham crackers, and marshmallow gooeyness all perfectly combined. Sometimes I wonder what life would be like if I were more serious. Like, what if I were a super serious business person? No way I could do that. I mean, I love life and people too much. I’d be miserable in an office from 9 to 5. Oh, and speaking of people, this semester I’ve met some amazing folks in class. We have this group project, and it’s kind of chaotic, but it works because we all have different ideas and perspectives. I love brainstorming sessions where no idea is too wild. More often than not, the weirdest ideas become the best ones. It's like a mesh of creativity. Plus, I always end up making new friends – it’s like this endless cycle of connecting, and getting to know them is like digging for gems. Everyone has a story and I want to hear them all. My phone just buzzed. Who could that be? Probably one of my friends asking if I want to grab lunch. I should check. Lunch is another thing – it’s never just about the food, it’s all about the experience. I mean, it can be at a quick diner or a fancy restaurant, but if you’re with good people, it makes everything taste better. There’s something about deep conversations over a plate of pasta that feels so fulfilling. I could talk for hours about anything and everything. I’ve been thinking about my next big adventure someday, maybe backpacking through Europe or going hiking in the Rockies. The thought of waking up in a different place every day excites me. Meeting locals, hearing their stories, sharing laughter in broken languages. I want to dive into it all without worrying about the little details. Life’s too beautiful for that. Okay, now I’m thinking I should really start planning something tangible instead of daydreaming. But then again, there’s this freedom in not planning too much. Maybe it’s better to just keep things open. Just like the unexpected joy of a spontaneous road trip. Just hop in the car, roll down the windows, and let the music play. I should wrap this up now. But honestly, isn’t it great to just let your thoughts flow? I love how the brain works. It’s funny, wild, unpredictable, and most of all, free.

Why do people always seem to be in such a rush? I mean, I get it, life can be hectic, but sometimes slowing down a bit could help. They might miss things, like little moments of beauty or interesting interactions. Like, I regularly find myself analyzing how everyone is moving around me in the library. The way someone flips their book pages, or the tapping sound of their pen against the table. I wonder if they even notice the soft rustling of the paper or if they’re just focused on cramming knowledge into their brains. It's like a dance, everyone's in their own rhythm, but it doesn't quite match with anyone else's. I prefer a more methodical approach, like studying with a clear plan, setting specific times to go over the material. There's something satisfying about checking off boxes—I love lists. My planner is color-coded, and I feel accomplished when I tick things off. Food. I’m always thinking about what I’ll eat next. The dining hall has this weird thing where they serve food at strange hours. I wonder about the logic behind those hours. Like why do they close at 7 p.m. on weekdays but stay open until 9 on weekends? What if I’m hungry at 7:30? Do they not realize that college students often eat late? I guess they expect us to manage our time better. I try to eat healthy, but then the other day I caved and had that pizza. It was so cheesy and gooey. I felt guilty after, though. Balance. I need balance in everything. It’s hard sometimes, especially when everyone else seems to be living life on a whim. I admire that, but I don’t think I could ever do it. Too stressful. I think it’s more peaceful having a routine. Yesterday, I helped a classmate with their project. I kind of enjoy that. It feels good to help someone understand something they’re struggling with. I can explain things clearly. It just comes naturally to me. She was really thankful too, which made me feel warm inside. I mean, it’s nice being appreciated, but it’s more about the satisfaction of making a difference. I hope I can keep helping others in some capacity when I graduate. I feel like that’s important. Making the world a little better, one small act at a time. I can’t remember the last time I went to a party. Parties are not my scene. I tried once, and it was overwhelming. So loud, so many people talking at once. Everyone was laughing, but I couldn’t join in. I just stood there holding my drink, quietly observing. I left after an hour. I mean, it’s nice when people invite me, but I just can’t get excited about it. I’d rather have a small get-together or just a coffee with a friend. Deep conversations, connecting over shared interests, that feels right. Sometimes I wonder if I’m missing out on something by not being more open to experiences. I hear my friends talk about spontaneous road trips, wild adventures, and I just nod. It sounds thrilling, but to me, the thought of packing and planning is exhausting. The idea of being in a car for hours feels daunting. I mean, I can appreciate the beauty of the world through pictures and stories. I keep my focus on the things I know I enjoy. Baking, reading mystery novels, organizing my space. It’s grounding. I’m also a bit of a planner when it comes to my future. I have goals—clear, tangible goals. I want to finish my degree, get a decent job, find stability. I’ve seen too many people struggle without direction. It scares me, and that’s why I put so much effort into my studies. It’s comforting knowing I’m on the right path. I keep my grades up, and I have a backup plan for everything—internships lined up, potential job applications ready in case. It’s good to be prepared. I think about my family too. They’re supportive. Always cheering me on, reminding me to take care of myself. It’s sweet knowing they believe in me. Makes me want to do well, not just for myself but for them. I hope I can make them proud. Sometimes I think about how to translate their support into gratitude. A thoughtful gift, a heartfelt message. Keeping close ties matters a lot to me. I value those relationships. Anyway, I’m glad I have this time to reflect. It helps to let everything out. I don’t have to worry about how it sounds or whether I’m saying the right things. Just getting my thoughts down feels freeing. I should do this more often. It’s nice to just think and type without being self-conscious. It’s all part of finding my way through this crazy college experience, right?

The clock says 3:47 p.m. I should probably be studying, but my mind keeps wandering. I wonder about the concept of time—it's so weird how we measure it. Days, hours, minutes, but does it really matter? Everything feels so finite yet infinite. Like, my to-do list is my own personal black hole. Is it even possible to finish everything? The thought of it makes me anxious, but then again, I thrive on lists. I love crossing things off. It gives me a sense of accomplishment, even if it’s just a small thing like watering the plants or organizing my notes. Speaking of notes, I should really get back to those chem notes. Organic chemistry is like this massive puzzle that I can’t seem to solve. My friends make it look so easy, like they just get it. Meanwhile, I’m sitting there furrowing my brow, trying to figure out reaction mechanisms that sound like some alien language. I might have to break out my flashcards again. I know I’ll feel better once I have things organized. But the thought of studying with all those diagrams is just so draining. Why do I even put that pressure on myself? Because if I don’t do well, then what? I’d just be another statistic of a college dropout, and I can’t imagine that. I want to make my parents proud. They work so hard, and I feel this weight on my shoulders. Okay, focus. Breathe. Maybe I should think about something else. What’s for dinner? I hope there’s enough leftover pasta in the fridge. I really don’t feel like cooking. I could go out, but that involves so many social interactions, and that’s exhausting. I mean, I like being around people, but sometimes, I just need to retreat and recharge. Why is it so hard to explain that to others? They just expect you to be “on” all the time, like I’m a walking party or something. Yesterday, I overheard some classmates discussing weekend plans, and they seemed so excited. Good for them. But I just couldn't muster the same enthusiasm. I don’t want to be rude and say no to invitations, but it’s like an emotional rollercoaster every time. The anticipation builds, then I step into the room, and panic sets in when I see so many people. It’s louder than I expected. I end up standing by the snack table, pretending to be interested in chips or something. I can’t even remember what they were talking about. I zone out so easily, especially when there are so many conversations happening around me. Just give me a good book and a cozy corner, and I’m set. Still, I feel guilty about not joining in. Like, am I missing out? Is everyone else having fun while I sit alone? But also, those moments of solitude are so precious. I can think, explore new ideas in my mind—like, what if I started sketching again? I used to love drawing, but somehow, I lost that spark. Maybe I can tap into that creativity again. It sounds liberating, but also, what if I’m not good anymore? What’s the point of creating if it’s not perfect? Perfectionism is a trap. It's just like with my studying. I keep trying to be the best version of myself, but who knows what that even looks like? And that leads me back to social media. Everyone curates their lives perfectly on there, and it’s overwhelming. I scroll through and think, “why can’t I be more like that?” But then again, I want to be genuine. I want to express myself authentically, but what does that even mean? Sometimes it feels impossible to balance being true to myself and fitting into societal expectations. It’s like I can’t win. Maybe I should just take a break from it. I mean, would anyone even notice? I’m slipping back into self-doubt again. I need to shake it off. Focus again. Deep breath. I’ve got to remember the goals I have. I want to travel someday too, explore different cultures. I’ve always been fascinated by the world—there’s just so much out there. I want to try different foods, meet new people, learn their stories. That’s the kind of connection I crave, not just passing small talk at parties. But how do I get there? It feels so far away sometimes. Anyway, I need to get back to my studies. I should put all this energy into learning rather than mulling things over. I have the tools, the motivation, and at least a little bit of drive. If I can just focus… I'll make it work. I can always find a path forward.

Okay so I’m thinking about how people really frustrate me sometimes like I know I’m supposed to be sociable and fun and energetic and whatever but honestly it’s exhausting. Why can’t people just get to the point? There’s always this small talk, this endless chit chat that feels so pointless like just tell me what you need or want from me and let’s move on. I mean I thrive in large groups, I love the energy, the buzz of a party or a gathering but when someone starts talking about their cat or the weather I just want to roll my eyes and walk away. I can’t even help it. I get these intense feelings all the time and it’s like an emotional rollercoaster because one moment I’m feeling on top of the world and then someone says something trivial and it just flips me. It’s so annoying. I can't believe how people get hung up on the small stuff while I’m trying to have a good time. And then there’s the organization part. I love having everything in its place, like my planner is color-coded and if a meeting gets rescheduled I literally can’t sleep until it’s updated. I get that it’s kind of a control thing but I think it makes life easier. Why can’t everyone just be a little more structured? The chaos just drives me nuts! I wish people understood that I’m not trying to be mean when I correct them or point out how disorganized they are. I just need things to go smoothly; it’s how I function. If I’m in a group project and someone wants to wing it? Absolutely not. I’ll take charge and assign roles and deadlines and make sure we knock it out of the park. I don’t understand why that would bother anyone. Like get with the program! Right now, I have this group assignment due next week and I’m already stressed about it. Why do people wait until the last minute? This is exactly why I stay ahead of the game. I guess I am a bit of a control freak but it’s necessary. My roommate keeps saying I need to relax more, but I don’t think she gets how much pressure there is sometimes. I see deadlines as these ticking time bombs and I can’t just pretend they’re not there. And oh God, I can feel my heart racing just thinking about it. I should text them and make sure we’re on track. I hate when people are flaky; it just makes everything harder. Then there’s the social media aspect of everything. I post a lot because I’m proud of what I do and who I am but sometimes I can’t help but think it’s all too much. I see all these perfectly curated feeds and I’m like, do people really live their lives like that? I mean I want to show my achievements but at the same time I don’t want to be fake about it. Still, I can’t deny it feels great when someone likes my post or comments something nice. I really thrive on those little bursts of affirmation. It’s like a high but it doesn’t last long. Then I’m back to feeling all these intense emotions and I have to find something else to distract myself. Conflicts really get me worked up too. Just yesterday I had this argument with a classmate who was so condescending about my idea for a project and I just snapped. It wasn’t pretty. I felt so angry and upset that they couldn’t see my perspective. Sometimes I wish I could just let things go, be chill about it, but that’s just not who I am. I can feel the heat rising in my face and then the impulse to just say whatever is on my mind without filtering it. And I know it won’t help but sometimes I can’t stop myself. It’s like this tidal wave of emotions and I’m just drowning in it. Sometimes I think about what it would be like to just go with the flow, you know? To be more open to different ideas and perspectives but then I get anxious because what if it doesn’t work out? I like things to be predictable and structured, so jumping into the unknown just scares me. I guess that’s why I sometimes feel alienated from others who are more laid back about things. It’s frustrating. I really do love being around people but it’s so draining sometimes, like I have to recharge after social events. I can talk endlessly if I'm fired up about something, but the moment someone gets too emotional or messy I’m just like, oh great here we go again. I like having my own space, I need that to recenter. Even if it means missing out on that next party, I’d rather have peace of mind. That's all I crave sometimes, just a little tranquility amidst all the chaos of life and people.

Okay so I was just thinking about how weekends are the best time to recharge and hang out with everyone. I mean, there’s something so thrilling about knowing that Friday night is coming and plans are in the air. Last weekend was fun, we all went bowling and I totally got the best score in my group. It’s not like I’m super competitive but you know, it just felt good to have a little triumph. Plus, it helped that everyone brought their A-game too.
