Another day trapped in the cycle of anxiety and excitement. I want to live big but sometimes I feel so small. It’s exhausting.

Okay so I was thinking about how different people approach weekends. Some people go out, party, meet up, socialize, and I’m just like, could I stay home and read a book or watch a documentary? That sounds amazing. I don’t even think about FOMO that much, like I'm aware of it but it feels distant, like a concept for other people. I love the quiet of my own space. I guess it’s my happy place. I mean, I enjoy being around friends, but I really need my alone time to recharge. Finding balance is key, I think. Sometimes I wonder if other introverts feel the same. I’ve read about the energy drain of socializing; it’s like charging a phone—some days I’m at a hundred percent after hanging out, others I’m at ten percent trying to keep up with social cues and chit-chat. Ugh, small talk can be so draining. It's funny how people can be so good at it, effortlessly sliding from one topic to another, while I’m over here sweating bullets trying to think of something interesting to say. I mean, I genuinely care about what people share, so it’s interesting to hear their thoughts. Just not super comfortable mixing in my own opinions sometimes, unless it’s a topic I really connect with, like the environment or art or something. Maybe I’m too agreeable for my own good? But I guess that also means I’m open to listening. Speaking of art, I’ve been thinking a lot about that photography exhibit I went to last month. There was something so raw and human about the way the artist captured everyday moments. Like, even a simple street corner came alive through their lens. I wish I could see the world through that perspective. It’s inspiring how one person’s view can shift the way others see reality. I hope when I create things, people feel that connection too. I want to express ideas, and emotions, even if the medium is just a pen and paper. I’ve been journaling a lot more—mostly random thoughts, snippets of conversations, ideas for projects—and it’s freeing. Sometimes I consider taking a creative writing class, but then I think, what if I don’t fit in? What if everyone has so much more experience and I just feel weird? But I kind of remind myself that new experiences can be good, right? Maybe that’s the openness in me, pushing me to try new things even when I feel hesitant. Plus, I’d like to think everyone has something unique to bring to the table, even if they don’t believe it. It’s a comforting thought. I often wonder about that—how do people see themselves? Are they really aware of the value they bring? The struggles with self-image seem universal, and it’s wild how we all tiptoe around those feelings and yet, there’s shared experience in that discomfort. Sometimes, I get caught in a cycle of thinking how we should prioritize kindness—like, it’s so easy to forget. I mean, when someone’s rude or dismissive, it’s hard not to internalize that, but I try to remember they might be having a rough day. We never know what others are dealing with. I want to make it a point to spread more kindness, even just in the little things. Holding the door for the person behind me or complimenting someone’s outfit, it seems minor, but it could really shift someone’s day. Like that saying, "be the change you wish to see." Sounds cliché but there’s a truth to it. And staying conscientious about it seems like a worthwhile endeavor. I also think about how I can be more patient with myself. I tend to set these high standards—responsible, organized, always striving to do my best. But sometimes I push too hard and forget to ease up. Perfectionism can be a double-edged sword. I want to achieve great things, but I have to remind myself that all progress isn’t linear. It’s okay to fail sometimes. Those failures often lead to the most growth. I’m learning to be kinder to myself in those moments of uncertainty and mistakes. It’s a journey, for sure. And then there’s all this talk about sustainability and climate change. It feels overwhelming, especially with the news cycling through heartbreaking stories of disasters and loss. I find myself wanting to do more, whether that’s choosing eco-friendly products or advocating for change, but I wonder if I’m making enough of a difference. I guess every little bit counts, right? Maybe I should get involved with a local organization focused on environmental issues. That sounds fulfilling and something I’d love. I just want to dive deep into the things that matter while still keeping that inner balance I cherish. This endless cycle of thoughts—sometimes exhausting, but mostly invigorating.

I can’t believe I left my earbuds at home again how do I keep doing this every time it’s like I’m cursed or something and the music is the only thing that keeps me sane so I guess I’m gonna have to listen to whatever’s playing in the cafeteria and hope it’s not that awful pop they keep blasting it drives me nuts but whatever I’ll survive. I think I’m craving something sweet today maybe I’ll hit up that new donut place on Maple Street it’s supposed to be amazing I mean, donuts? Who doesn’t love donuts, right? I should probably try to find a buddy to go with me because it’s more fun that way but I don’t want to drag someone away from their studying even though I think we could use a break. I wonder what kind of donuts they have. Chocolate glazed, maybe? Or the maple bacon one might be good I don’t know who thought of that combination but it’s genius I mean bacon with sweetness it just works I guess. I should really focus on my assignments but they bore me to death. It’s all about theories and research and all that stuff that makes my head spin. Why can’t universities just embrace the chaos of life? Everything’s so structured and predictable it’s suffocating sometimes. Let’s just throw caution to the wind, I say, let’s embrace spontaneity. Speaking of spontaneity, I think I might just text Jess to see if she’s down for donuts she’s always up for an adventure but what if she’s busy with her own thing, there’s this fine line between being overly persistent and just enthusiastic and I’m not sure how to navigate that sometimes. Ugh, who cares? Life is too short to worry about social rules I think I’ll just shoot her a message anyway. Oh! And I can’t forget the music festival next weekend! I’m so excited I mean, it’s going to be wild and crowded but that’s part of the fun I think I thrive in that kind of energy and it’s so freeing to dance with strangers and not care about what anyone thinks. Just lose yourself in the rhythm and those bright lights blinking all around like it’s a totally different world. The smells, the sounds, the vibes it’s intoxicating. I hope the weather holds up because it would suck to have rain ruin it but honestly even if it does that’s just another layer of craziness to experience. How can people be introverted? I understand needing time alone sometimes but I thrive off energy from others. It’s like a buzz in the air – if I’m in a crowd I feel alive, that’s where the magic happens, and I always end up meeting new people, making new friends. Last time at a concert, I met this girl who was just as obsessed with that band as I was and we ended up talking for hours about everything from our favorite songs to the most ridiculous things we’ve done in our lives. I love finding those connections that seem so random but then just click. I should probably be more organized but to be honest I can’t stand rules and regulations. The world is so much bigger than fitting into boxes. Whenever I try to follow a schedule, my brain goes crazy like an animal trapped in a cage, so I just wing it most days. If I end up late for a class, whatever I catch up later, or maybe I don’t and that’s cool too. I mean, who needs to follow a 9-to-5 structure in college anyway? I want to explore all the weird corners of campus, meet people, go to spontaneous parties, and just live fully. I remember that one night we ended up in that art gallery opening – I had no idea there was a gallery there but it was one of the best nights ever. Just wander, discover, be surprised. What are the odds I could persuade Jess to join me for that? I wonder if she’d be into it or if she’d give me that look like ‘really?’ but hey, I mean nothing ventured, nothing gained, right? I could convince her how fun it would be and feed off her enthusiasm while pushing her limits a little. I sometimes think about how easy it is to pull people outside their comfort zones, especially the ones who are a bit rigid I can’t help but poke at that. Life is too vibrant to live by a set of rules. And what’s the deal with how everyone is so serious about everything in academia? Like yeah, sure, knowledge is important but don’t forget to have fun too. There has to be balance. I want to make the kind of memories that make my heart race, stories I can tell years later – “Oh remember that time we sneaked into that event?” and everyone laughs, and I remember every detail, every face, every moment. What’s on the horizon today? Who knows? But I’m ready to find out. Donuts first, then who knows maybe an impromptu dance party? Yes please.

Okay so what was I saying about that party last week? I mean, it was wild, like, just chaotic energy everywhere, people bouncing off the walls. I thrive in those situations, you know? But then there were times I just needed to step back and breathe because the noise was a lot. I could feel my heart racing, but I couldn’t help but jump into everything. Friends dragged me into this crazy game of beer pong, and I knocked over my drink and spilled everywhere. Embarrassing? Yeah, but whatever, I own it. That’s life, right? Just ride that wave. And I can’t stop thinking about their dog. So cute, like rolling on the floor, fluffy little thing, with that tongue out and eyes wide open. I tried to give it a snack but it was one of those picky eaters. Animals are weird, you can never tell what they like. Why do I find that so amusing? Just think about it—people claim they’re the best judges of character, yet look at how they treat dogs. That tells you everything. Then there’s that guy, what’s his name? Jack? He was so loud, fighting with everyone about politics. Like, we get it, you read the news. Calm down. But then I found myself getting sucked into it and it was like a rollercoaster of emotions. I can’t believe I wasted so much time arguing. It’s like, what’s the point? And I’m so not the type to back down, so it ended with me yelling across the room about freedom of speech or something, going all in. My friends were just cringing over there, but I didn’t care. The adrenaline of those moments keeps me alive. Still, is it crazy that I can feel the anxiety creeping in right after? Like, did I say too much? Did I just blow my chance with that cute girl? Who even knows if she liked me back—probably not, but whatever, I’m not gonna fade away quietly. The clock's ticking though, I need to find a new way to balance this out. I can’t keep doing this life of chaos and then spiraling into worry afterward. I can feel everything, like, I feel angry and happy and sad all at once and it’s exhausting but also exhilarating. Why can’t I just be chill about everything, like some people do? Like, my roommate, she’s so laid-back, too agreeable, always letting things roll off her back like water. I can’t do that! I flag down the ambivalence, grasp it tight until it can’t breathe. But I do crave connection. I want to meet everyone. I want to learn their stories and their dreams and their failures, what they think about. It's fascinating, the variety in people. It’s like a buffet of personalities. I get so giddy, trying to piece together stories and motivations like a puzzle. It feeds that openness. I go to places I’ve never been—art shows, underground music venues, farmers' markets—everything! That rush is unreal, but then again, what if I don’t connect? What if I’m too much or too blunt? Then I spiral again. Ugh. I got an email from my professor about group projects. Aren’t we done with that? I mean, I get it’s for collaboration and all, but I just want to dive deep into my own ideas without having to compromise. ‘Oh, let’s all gather around and share thoughts!’ Shut up, I just want to do my thing! That’s probably why I don’t work well with others, but honestly, I don’t feel the need to hide that either. Just let me do me. On the other hand, there’s that fear of missing out. Like, what if I don’t join? Then they’re all bonding without me and I’m sitting alone with a book again by myself, not that I’m against it, but sometimes I wonder if I’m just avoiding what’s right in front of me. Weird, right? I mean, my brain’s always going, spinning, even in the quieter moments. I think about traveling, exploring, trying to meet new people and learn new things—but am I just doing that to fill a void? Sometimes I think about my life choices and question if I’m even going in the right direction. But who really knows? Oh, wait, I remember the sushi place we went to last month—the chef was this older guy who tells the best stories while making his rolls. I sat there wide-eyed, staring as he explained the origins of each ingredient, how it shapes the flavor, and I could hear that passion in his voice. I see that and I think, wow! I want that kind of drive, that kind of zest for something! Yet, here I am, flitting from one thing to the next, trying to figure it all out. But can you blame a wanderer for being a little lost? It's just part of the journey.

Okay so I just remembered that time in high school when I painted my entire bedroom a bright green because I thought it would make me feel happy or something and it did for like a week but then it just felt too much like a lime popsicle and I hated it but I couldn’t bring myself to repaint it so I just lived with it. I never really liked living in boxes. I wonder if that was the start of my aversion to conformity, like why should I live in a space that doesn’t reflect who I am? I’d rather have an explosion of creativity around me than beige walls but here I am in this little apartment with white walls that feel so sterile but I guess it’s easy to cover them with posters and drawings from friends, all these little snapshots of life, the moments that remind me of better days. It’s funny how I treasure those things way more than the furniture I’ve bought. People keep saying you need a ‘work-life balance’ but isn’t that just a fancy way of saying you should pretend to be someone you’re not? I mean, I can sit for hours looking at books and art online, looking for inspiration. Oh, there’s this really cool artist I found on Instagram who makes paintings out of discarded materials and I’m just in awe. But then I think about starting something like that and the looming question is always about how I won’t do it properly. Perfectionism isn’t really in my genetics but I guess expectations from outside pressures creep in sometimes. Why do I worry about that? I hate rules and constraints, but then I put them on myself. I’d rather try a million things than stick to a routine. Why are people obsessed with routines anyway? There's something comforting about the chaos, like how art can come from something messy and unplanned. I often get lost in doodling and I don’t know if I’ll ever get the nerve to actually show anyone what I create. It’s just for me, I guess? That feels selfish but then again it’s not like anyone’s clamoring to see it. Was I ever meant to share? It’s tough though. Art feels like a conversation even when you’re alone. Sometimes, I hear music that resonates with me so much and I think it must be what it feels like to connect with someone on a different level without saying a word. I wish more people thought like that. Why aren’t there more deep conversations? Everything feels so superficial sometimes. But maybe I’m just too sensitive about it. Then there’s that time I tried to join a club. I like the idea of meeting people but the socializing part? Not so much. I mean everyone seemed so loud and excited about getting together, but when I walked in, I just felt this overwhelming urge to fade into the background. I ended up sitting in the corner doodling in my notebook while they all chatted boisterously about mundane things. It’s not that I don’t enjoy socializing; I just feel more at home in my head than out in the world. My brain is like a treasure chest of ideas that I can't always express. I admire extroverted people but the thought of trying to keep up with their energy is exhausting. I do love hearing people talk, their voices weaving stories that feel alive, but my contributions are rarely the loudest. And speaking of stories, have you seen how many different interpretations there are of the same book or movie? It’s mind-blowing! When I read or watch something I always have my own take on it, and then someone else’s perspective flips it on its head. I love how open-ended everything is. No clear answers. Just layers and layers of meaning and feelings. I could talk about this stuff for days. Not that I would; I’d much rather write about it. My fingers just move over the keys and spill whatever thoughts come to mind. Kind of like a therapy session without actually talking to someone. But I can’t help thinking about how that therapist would probably preach about balancing my thoughts with reality and I’m just here like is it even possible to have a fixed ‘reality’ when everything feels so subjective? Maybe this is why I wonder about perspectives so much. Being agreeable is nice for me because I want everyone to feel okay, I really do. But then I feel like I get steamrolled sometimes, like my opinions don’t matter because I’m too laid back. But I don’t want to be confrontational. I’m okay with people disagreeing with me, but it still stings a bit when you think you’re sharing your heart and they just don’t see it. I guess it’s all about learning to let go and not hold on too tightly to what doesn’t belong to me. If only life came with a manual that had the answers clearly laid out like a syllabus.

Five minutes past midnight, and I’m lying here, staring at my ceiling, wondering if I should’ve just gone to bed earlier, but then again, I never really feel tired until it’s super late. The worry creeps in about my grades, there’s that class I didn’t participate in, and I can’t stop thinking, Was it that obvious I didn’t do the reading? My group project is due in a week, and I haven’t even opened the file yet. I should really reach out to my partner. But what if they’re annoyed that I’m behind? I hate bothering people. What if I let them down? It’s so easy to spiral. Last week, I had a panic about a presentation and ended up convincing myself I’d flop even before I stood up to speak. Everyone else looked so confident. Why can’t I feel that way? It’s exhausting. I think about my friends—wait, do I even have friends? No, they’re more like acquaintances, really. A couple of people I hang out with sometimes. I’m okay with that, I guess. Partying or going out? No way. I’d rather be at home, but then I feel like I’m missing out, especially when they post about it online. Social media just feeds into my insecurities. FOMO, that’s what everyone talks about, but I don’t want to be around a crowd. Getting invited feels good, but then I freeze up when I’m actually there. It’s like, what do I even say? Do people just want me to agree with them? That’s all I’m good at; I get nervous when I actually have to make a point. Speaking of making points, I need to remember to bring that book back to the library. I borrowed it like three months ago, I think? I’m going to have to pay a fine if I don’t, but even thinking about interaction with the librarian stresses me out. I can picture them looking at me with that disappointed expression, like I’ve let the whole library down. But I can’t help it! I tend to forget these things. I wish I could just say, “Hey, I’m a mess” and have it be okay, but it feels like everyone else has it figured out—how do they manage to keep track of everything? I should write a list, but then, what if I lose the list? It’s just a cycle of stress. Tomorrow there’s an event on campus. I got the email, but I can’t imagine going. Too many people. Too many conversations. Small talk is the bane of my existence. I dread the whole thing. What if someone asks about my major? I’m not even sure about it anymore, and I don’t want to look clueless. I feel like I should change it, but what if I pick something worse? Every choice seems to have the potential for disaster. What if I don’t fit in anywhere? Community is nice in theory, but in practice, it’s like walking through molasses. A struggle. My roommate is great—super outgoing, always invites me to join her. I appreciate it, but it’s also like “why can’t I be like you?” If I try to be more social, I end up feeling drained. I feel like I owe it to her to at least be a little more adventurous. But then I panic. Do I risk it? What if it’s a disaster? I remember the last time I tried something new—a club meeting that I thought might be fun. I ended up standing by the door, heart racing, feeling stupid for even showing up. No one talked to me. I slipped out after 10 minutes. Worst feeling ever. I hate feeling invisible, but I also hate having attention on me. It’s confusing. Talking about feelings and emotions is hard. I don’t know how to articulate it. It’s always easier to agree with someone else’s feelings than to express my own. I feel like I’m always in the background, agreeing or nodding, but never jumping into the conversation. Then later I’re stuck replaying every moment in my head, critiquing myself. Should I have said this? What did they think of me? I just want validation, but then I feel guilty for wanting it. Ugh, it’s a mess. I’m spiraling again. Sometimes I wish I could just hit pause on my brain. I want to breathe without judgment, but every deep breath feels like a weight. Sleep would be better. But insomnia is my friend. Sleep is where I get attacked by all the thoughts. It’s like my brain doesn’t believe I should get a break. Maybe next week will be better. Or the week after that. I can't wait for the semester to be over, but then what? Summer feels like a ticking clock. The more I think about it, the more anxious I get. Just keep swimming, I guess.

I just remembered this time in high school when we all went to the beach during spring break and the energy was just electric. I mean everyone was there, it felt like such a community, you know? The sun was shining, and we all just ran into the water like kids. I was laughing so hard because someone kept losing their sunglasses and diving to find them and we were all screaming and splashing around like it was the best day ever. It was chaotic but in a good way. I love that feeling of pure joy, that collective excitement. Then someone brought out a portable speaker, and suddenly it was like a mini party. I live for that vibe, that connection with everyone. It's funny how the simplest moments can stick with you. I wonder if my friends remember it the same way. I should ask them sometime. Or like when we spontaneously went to that concert downtown last summer! I got a text that said “Hey, there are tickets left, let’s go!” And I just jumped up without thinking, texted back “I’m in!” and then off we went. The music was unbelievable, and I didn’t care that we were waiting in line for ages. I made friends with some random people behind us, we were trading stories about our favorite songs and it felt like none of us were strangers at all. I thrive in those moments where everything just clicks, it’s like the world opens up and you can breathe deeply, you know? Okay, but sometimes I think about my room. It’s always a mess. Like a total whirlwind of clothes, books, and snack wrappers. I mean, if anyone walked in, they’d probably think I was a total disaster. But here’s the thing—I know where everything is. I just… I don’t stress about it. I’d much rather spend that time planning a new adventure or figuring out where my friends want to eat next. I swear food is my love language. I get so excited about trying new restaurants or flavors, and I’m always on the lookout for the next best thing. I have this insane list of places to try, and it feels like a treasure map. I could go anywhere and eat anything. I’ve never been one to stick to just one cuisine, why would I when the world has so much to offer? I think that’s part of being open, right? Embracing all the flavors of life, meeting new people, seeing new places. Speaking of new places, I’ve got to plan that camping trip with my friends. I can’t believe none of us have gone yet! We keep saying we will but never actually do it. We’ve got to, like, just pick a weekend and go. I can’t imagine how fun it will be, sitting around the fire, telling ghost stories, roasting marshmallows, and maybe even getting lost in the woods. Just thinking about that freedom makes me giddy. I mean, it’s a little bit of unpredictability, and that’s where the best stories come from. And I want to make memories, not just live in this safe bubble you know? Life’s way too short for that. But oh man, what if it rains? I don’t even care, I’ll get soaked and still have a blast. There’s something exhilarating about being outdoors when a storm rolls in. It’s wild. I know some people freak out, but I’m like “bring it on.” I remember this one time at a festival when it poured, and instead of sulking, we just started dancing in the rain with strangers. We made a giant puddle and had a blast! Instagram pictures were everywhere the next day, everyone was smiling and happy. That’s the kind of energy I love—the spontaneous joy that bubbles up when you least expect it. I think that's what life is about, right? Embracing the chaos, finding joy in little moments, connecting with people. I don’t understand how some people can be so rigid, so strict about plans. I mean, sure, having a schedule is nice but what happens when something unexpected comes up? You just let it slide? Nah, I want to dive into it. I want to be open to anything. The best experiences are often the unplanned ones. Just the other day, I went to grab coffee and ended up at this open mic night instead. I had the best conversations with strangers who I hope I’ll run into again. I even picked up that little guitar riff… maybe I should learn an instrument. But honestly, I don’t know how to focus enough to practice. I’m happy just bouncing around from one thing to another, living in the moment and feeling everything around me. I love the thrill of meeting new people, switching from one topic to another, laughing about the dumbest things. Life is so colorful, and I want to soak it all in.

Okay so I’m thinking about how I really don’t want to go to that party this weekend but everyone keeps texting me about it. Like why can’t they just leave me alone for once? I don’t want to deal with small talk and pretending to be excited about the things people are saying when really I just want to sit at home and scroll through my phone. Why is that such a bad thing? I mean, parties are too loud anyway and I can't stand it when people get too close to my face when they talk. Just back off a little, right? I don’t want to smell someone’s cologne or hear them chew or laugh too hard like their whole life depends on it. But then there’s that fear of missing out. If I don’t go will they talk about me? Will they think I’m boring and lame? I hate feeling like I’m missing out on something because that makes my head race—what am I missing? People sharing laughs, new connections, maybe some deep conversations but like, who even has those? They just say, “What’s up?” and then it’s downhill from there. I can’t stand it when people talk about things I don’t care about or don’t understand—like someone the other day started talking about upgrading their gaming setup and I’m just standing there nodding my head like I know the difference between a GTX and a RTX? No way! But I can’t say that, I have to keep nodding and smiling through it which makes my stomach twist. Ugh, and social media is a mess too. I scroll through and see everyone posting their best moments and I wonder if I’m missing out on something grand. Maybe I should be a part of that picture-perfect life? But then I think about how annoying it is to maintain all that positivity online when in reality, I can’t even keep track of my laundry. Why do I care if someone else is hiking or being adventurous? I don’t even like hiking! It’s sweaty and there are bugs and who really enjoys sweating while walking uphill? I’d much rather be playing video games in my room, maybe with takeout—definitely with takeout. And as much as I can't stand the idea of going out sometimes I just feel this pull to go and be with others because it’s exhausting to be alone but also exhausting to be around others. I feel like I’m being torn in two and it drives me nuts. I just wish I could be in my own bubble without everyone expecting me to join them in their social agendas. But then again, if I don’t go, am I the only one not there? It’s like a giant anxiety bomb just waiting to go off, and I feel it in my chest. Why does everyone have to be so cheerful and friendly all the time? I don’t really get how people can just be so agreeable. Like, I can’t pretend that I think everything is perfect when it’s not. I see people walk around with smiles on their faces like they’ve got it all figured out and I just want to yell at them—“How are you this happy? What’s your secret?” But I guess there’s a lot of pressure to be socially acceptable and I’m just not capable of faking it for too long. I do try to engage in conversations but after a few minutes I just get drained and need to escape. I can’t even think about it too much without feeling my nerves rise again. And the thing is, when I do finally drag myself to socialize it turns into this chaotic whirlwind of talking too much and then overthinking everything I’ve said. Did I make a fool of myself? Did I interrupt someone again? I can’t help but feel like I’m this huge inconvenience in other people's lives. Just want to live with my own thoughts and not have to please anyone else. I should probably talk to someone about this, but nah, therapy feels like a waste of time. I mean, who’s got time to sit and hash out feelings? I could just be home gaming or doing literally anything else. Right now, I’m thinking I’ll probably end up going to that party because everyone’s gonna keep bugging me and I’ll feel guilty for not showing up. Maybe I can find a corner to hide in, or just keep checking my phone? I can pretend I’m busy or something. Or maybe I can just go and only stay for a little bit. The whole idea of having to engage feels heavy and I just want to scream sometimes. People need to relax and let me be. Just let me chill! But somehow I always end up in these situations where I’m expected to be all in. Maybe I’ll bring a book next time, just in case I need to escape the chaos for a bit. That’s a plan. Or maybe not. I don’t really care!

The clock is ticking, and I’m staring at this empty page, which is somehow more intimidating than any exam. I feel the anxiety creeping in already. What if I don’t have anything to say? But I always have something to say, just scattered thoughts. It’s like I have a hundred tabs open in my brain but none of them connect. My mind jumps all over the place. I wonder how other people manage to be so confident in social situations. I feel like an alien observing everyone else. Okay, so there’s this old coffee cup on my desk. The remnants of who-knows-what because I never clean up after myself. It’s just sitting there, like a monument to my procrastination. And then I think of how I hate clutter, but I just can’t bring myself to do anything about it. I read somewhere that people with high openness are more creative, but I don’t feel creative. I feel chaotic. Am I even an artist or just an excuse for messiness? And speaking of plans, I told myself I’d study two chapters today, but that’s out the window now. Overthinking every detail, should I outline what I want to say? But then I’ll just get stuck in analysis paralysis. I feel like a snail with a heavy shell, moving too slow to keep up with the world outside. Everyone seems to be running past me, laughing, sharing ideas, and I’m just standing there feeling overwhelmed. Why do people make it look so easy? I wish I had a manual on how to just be normal. I find myself in those crowded parties—everyone’s there chatting, smiling, and I’m just clinging to the wall. I mean, I can’t even fake it well enough to fit in. I’d rather not engage in small talk. What even is small talk? “Nice weather we’re having” is like a special kind of torture. I remember this one time, I tried to socialize, maybe I was feeling adventurous? No, not adventurous. Curious? Or just bored? I went to this art exhibit, and of course, I got lost in the pieces. A giant painting with swirls of color. I wanted to talk about it. I approached a group of people. I didn’t know how to jump in. They were discussing techniques, prices, and art schools. I had so many thoughts, but my tongue felt heavy. Why am I like this? They probably thought I was a weirdo just standing there. It’s so hard to get out of my head. Then I think about the time I went hiking alone. I love nature. It calms me. I can escape. But I also worry about getting lost. It's a double-edged sword. The whispering trees and the rustling leaves contrast with this internal shouting inside of me. “What if you fall, what if you trip?" But there’s beauty in letting go, isn’t there? I could just stand there for hours, watching shadows dance across the ground. But then I remember the anxiety of someone passing by, the fear they’d see how awkward I am. I’ve got all these wild ideas bouncing around in my head. I could start a blog? But what would I even write about? I think about that famous quote, “write what you know” but what do I know? I know I can think deeply about things that don’t seem to matter. I can dissect my feelings and dive into profound existential crises. The world feels too big and overwhelming, and I’m one small dot in a sea of chaos. Friends, what are friends? I don’t know how to be a friend. I worry too much. I don’t think I’m agreeable enough. I’m not nice enough. I want to be but there’s this barrier. I can’t get past the instinct to critique or throw shade. What if I started doing something totally off the wall? Like, pick a random book from the library and just lose myself in the pages? That could lead to a million train of thoughts! But then I’ll end up overthinking even that. I want to be in awe of ideas, but self-doubt sneaks in like an unwelcome guest, ruining the fun. I want freedom to explore but feel tethered to this anxiety. It’s like I’m in a glass box and everyone else is outside looking in. I just want genuine connection but can’t figure out how to break through this glass. Deep breath. I’m just going to keep typing until the timer goes off. No stopping, no deleting. That’s the plan, right? Just let it all out. The thoughts are endless, and I’m just riding the wave as it crashes, never really catching a break. So much to say, but the words feel jumbled. What if I just take a leap? What if I stop holding back? That would be something. But even that feels daunting. What if it’s a disaster? But what if it’s not? What if it’s beautiful? Maybe one day I’ll figure it out. I guess I’ll just keep trying.

Okay so I was thinking about how weird it is that every time I go into a group setting, I get this rush of energy like I'm on a caffeine high even if I haven’t had coffee that day. I mean, I can make friends with just about anyone. Like last week at that party, I ended up talking to somebody about their pet iguana for almost an hour, and I don't even like reptiles. But the passion they had for it was infectious. I love hearing what makes people tick and sharing stories. That connection is everything. Also, just realized the other day how fun it is to introduce people to one another, like playing matchmaker but not romantically, just connecting brains and hearts. And then there’s my roommate who is super introverted – she seems to get drained after social events while I’m bouncing off the walls afterwards. I try to be mindful of how she feels, like asking if she wants to join in on something or just chill by herself. But sometimes, I wish she would come to the parties. It’s so much fun when everyone’s laughing and just vibing. I think she secretly likes it too. Maybe she just needs a little push. Speaking of which, there was this time when I dragged her to karaoke, and she ended up being the star of the night! That’s the magic of a little nudge. I’ve been thinking about how many interests I have that keep branching out. I started with photography in high school, but now I’m into hiking and trying out new foods. It's like every step I take leads me to more adventures. I went rock climbing last week. My hands were sore for days after, but I felt accomplished. I even got to encourage others who were trying it for the first time, showing them the ropes – literally. The thrill of seeing someone conquer their fear is incomparable. It’s this shared experience that makes it all worthwhile. Sometimes I scroll through my camera roll and just smile at all those memories. I took this picture of a sunset at the beach, and you can see everyone in the shot – some playing frisbee, others just lounging and laughing. It makes me appreciate how important those small moments are. It’s about being present. Oh, and that hiking trip last month, where we got lost but found this hidden waterfall instead? Epic! We jumped in fully clothed, and let me tell you, that cold water was a shock to the system but felt amazing. There's a freedom in spontaneity. It’s a thrill. On the topic of spontaneity, I want to try traveling more. I don’t even care where; I just want to experience new cultures. It's wild how different people live and what they find normal. This summer, my goal is to go abroad, maybe somewhere in Europe or Southeast Asia. All those street markets and local cuisines, it feels like this endless buffet of experiences. I crave that! I keep thinking about learning a new language too, which sounds daunting but exciting at the same time. Oh, and I heard about an art class that combines painting with mindfulness. That's something I might want to sign up for. I’ve never been great at painting, but it feels like such an adventure. The thought of splashing colors around and being free with it is very appealing. Art has a way of connecting people too. I went to a gallery opening last month, and meeting the artists was incredible. They were so passionate about their work. It was like they breathed life into whatever they created. Also, I’ve been trying to read more. I used to love reading back in school but then life got all busy, you know? But now I’m diving into all sorts of genres. The other night I started a sci-fi novel, and wow, the imagination these authors have is mind-boggling. It’s so cool to think about alternate realities and possibilities. I often end up daydreaming about those worlds, which is a nice escape from the everyday grind of assignments and exams. Speaking of assignments, I really need to get on top of my readings for class. I’m good at planning, though – I usually manage my deadlines well despite all the fun stuff I get into. Balancing things out is key. I just wish everything could be fun! Who wants to sit in front of a computer writing essays? But I guess if I keep making it a social thing – like bringing friends in to help each other study – it won’t feel like a chore. And then there are the little things like what tunes to hear while working out. I’ve become obsessed with creating playlists. It’s like curating a vibe for every moment. Upbeat songs, slow jams, you name it. Music brings everyone together, especially at the gym or the beach. I think I’ll plan a beach day soon. Maybe invite everyone! The idea of it just fills my heart with excitement. Let's go!

okay so here I am just tapping away and thinking about how sometimes I talk too much in class like why can't I just chill out for like two seconds and let others get a word in edgewise but then I think no that's part of my charm right or at least that's what I tell myself I mean I can’t help it if I’m excited about the lecture on social behavior and the prof is super engaging I just want to throw out ideas and get everyone hyped up even though I know deep down nobody is *really* as hyped about this as I am it’s funny and maybe annoying but whatever that's life the whole thing just makes me want to go out and grab some pizza with friends and maybe hit up that new bowling alley everyone's raving about like seriously why not I’ve got the energy for it and the night is young is that a cliche I’m totally okay with cliches they’re kind of like the backbone of conversation right? speaking of conversation lately I’ve been thinking about how easy it is for me to make friends but this whole ‘agreeableness’ thing that they talk about in psychology is just not me like I don’t feel the need to tiptoe around feelings or hold back when I think someone’s just being stupid or wrong and that can end up being a double-edged sword I guess but it feels good to say what’s on my mind I mean if you don’t tell someone their joke was lame then how will they ever learn right but I can tell sometimes people are like okay yeah you’re kind of harsh and I’m like dude chill it’s just my honest opinion but they don’t see it that way and I’m thinking do I really need to sugarcoat everything just to keep folks happy? nah that seems exhausting it would make me anxious and I’m really not an anxious person I just go for it I dive headfirst into new experiences and even if I mess up so what? everyone has a laugh, especially me and it usually makes for a great story later on but then I think do other people even want to hear my stories? does it matter? I could sit and ponder that all night or I could just go grab some ice cream right now because ice cream is life obviously so what’s the deal with trying new things? I know everyone says you should be open to new experiences but that sounds like a trap like do I really want to try that exotic food everyone’s raving about? I mean, it could be good but what if it’s not? no thanks I’ll stick to my pizza and fries instead of spending money on something I might regret which probably accounts for my low openness right? doesn’t matter though I’m happy chilling in my comfort zone and dragging my friends along for the ride they can try the new stuff if they want but I’m not in recently moved into this apartment with some roommates who were supposed to be chill but honestly they’re a little much with their need for everything to be spotless like chill guys we’re college students not a hotel but whatever I can’t deal with their passive-aggressive vibes I might just start blasting music when they get *too* intense about dishes or trash it’s like come on we’re not living on a reality TV show here just a bunch of students trying to survive and I’m doing my best to avoid conflict but I know at some point I’ll probably snap but that’s just me I’m like a balloon filled with air you can only blow so much before it just pops right? I wonder what they compare me to behind my back is it bad that I don’t care? oh and how about that party last weekend? hilarious! everyone was dancing and having a great time and I just went all out, of course. I’ve got the moves even if no one comments but that’s not why I do it I do it because it feels good to just let loose and be ridiculous plus it’s a great way to blow off steam after a long week of classes and tests I was practically a one-person dance party and people actually started crowding around me like it was a show or something when really I was just having fun being myself but there’s always that guy who thinks he can outdance me and honestly I welcome the challenge but also like chill it’s not a competition I’m just trying to have a good time thinking about how I love being around people but sometimes it gets a little overwhelming especially when everyone is trying to talk over each other and I’m like why can’t we just take turns? oh man, now I’m sounding like a mom or something but seriously listening is just as important as talking but nobody seems to get that and it leads to these circular conversations where you can’t even remember what the point was in the first place I just want to have fun and connect but there’s always a fine line between how much energy I can give and how much is expected back and do I really care? probably not let’s just roll with it and see where the night takes us because that’s what it’s all about right?

Okay so I woke up today and the first thing I thought was about that project due next week. I need to make a schedule. I work best with a plan. I can’t just dive in. That’s chaos. Speaking of chaos, I saw this guy walking his dog last night. I mean why can’t people just keep their dogs on a leash? It's like, come on, don’t you know your dog could run off at any second? I wanted to say something but then I thought, “let him live” or whatever but still, I don’t get it. I like things orderly. I ran into Sarah after class yesterday. She was all over the place talking about her weekend plans and how it’s going to be this epic adventure. I mean, I can’t really understand those spontaneous people. Just pick a place and stay there for a bit, right? But no, she’s like, “We’ll drive here, then there, and we’ll see this, and then I might just meet some random person and go to some random place.” It stresses me out thinking about it. I like my weekends predictable; I find comfort in routines. I’ve got my gym schedule, and then I’ll meal prep on Sundays. That’s how the world should work. And yeah, the gym. Can’t believe I actually like going there. It’s a bit social but in a structured way. I’m not one of those people who likes to chat while I work out but I see familiar faces and we nod at each other. That’s cool. The other day, I noticed that guy who lifts more than I do—totally intimidating but also motivating? I mean, I can’t let him outdo me forever, right? So small goals. And I read somewhere that writing down goals helps, so I’m going to jot them down later. It’s a whole system; I have it in my head. I want to increase my weights, then run an extra half mile. The routine drives me. I hate the feeling of falling behind. Then there’s the thing with group projects, ugh. I just don’t understand why people can’t be on the same page. There’s always that one person who’s like, “Let’s just wing it!” Wings? No thanks. I wanna know who’s doing what and when. Just give me the tasks, I’ll handle it, don’t worry. The others are all like “Sure, let’s just go with the flow.” Flow? What is flow? I don’t trust flow. Flow leads to chaos, and chaos leads to disaster. I remember one time I was assigned with a group that just didn’t get the concept of deadlines. I mean, who does that? How do you not take deadlines seriously? It's a basic life skill and you’re in college! Ended up having to take charge and fix everything last minute. I was stressed but I nailed it in the end, as always. Winning in the end is what it’s about. So many people complain about their roommates, but I honestly like mine. They’re okay. Not too intrusive, which is perfect because I can’t deal with constant noise. There’s this balance, y’know? They keep to themselves, and I stay on my side doing my own thing, which is just the way I like it. Peace is nice. But sometimes I just want to blast music because my playlist is fire, but I can’t do that when they’re home, right? It’s fine though. I’ve got my headphones, I’ll live. I think if I had to pick a roommate, it’d be someone who’s focused, no drama, no mess. Then there’s the whole dating thing. Everyone is swiping left and right like it’s a sport. I don’t know. I don’t think it’s for me. I see guys out there looking for their next adventure but I want something solid. A little predictable, you know? I feel like it’s too much work moving through the endless options. I’d rather meet someone through friends or at a gathering that has some structure. What’s wrong with that? You get to know them, it’s more… genuine. But dating apps? It’s just a swipe, how is that a real connection? My phone buzzes, and I get a message from a class group chat about a study session. Perfect! Structure! Groups work better when they have a clear agenda, right? I can lay out the topics, and we can tackle them one by one. That’s where I thrive. I’ll be the leader, assign tasks, get things rolling. I love it. I can’t imagine just showing up and figuring it out as we go. That sounds awful. Like an improv class for life. No thanks. I’ll stick to my plans and systems. That’s where I feel most alive.

there’s this pencil on my desk it’s yellow and the eraser is worn down maybe I should get a new one but I never seem to lose it even though it’s all I need sometimes I think about how things can be simple like that even the rubber around the eraser kind of crumbles and I catch myself thinking why am I even caring about a pencil right now when I have a study coming up but hey it’s the little things right pencils make me feel grounded I’ve always preferred writing by hand because typing just seems to rush me like my thoughts can’t keep up with my fingers and then I lose track of what I want to say and it frustrates me I’m the kind of person who likes sticking to a plan if I make a to-do list I have to check every box it’s satisfying in a weird way but I guess that’s just how I am some would say I’m rigid for it but it works for me I know what to expect and I don’t have to deal with uncertainty too much I don’t know why I want things structured like that maybe it’s a control thing I just feel better when everything is organized nothing worse than chaos or clutter just makes me anxious I mean I don’t even understand the appeal of living like that my roommate loves spontaneous trips and just taking off with no plan I can’t fathom it I like my routine I hate when something disrupts it and I feel like I’m swimming in the deep end I’ve realized I’d probably be perfectly okay with a life where nothing ever changes too much the same friends the same activities things that just work and keep grinding and I really don’t think that’s boring I’ve had friends who love to explore but it seems so exhausting after a while all these new experiences it’s too much information to process I guess that’s where my low openness comes in but I mean it’s fine it feels good to know what I want and I don’t have to pretend to be open to things I don’t care about like new restaurants or trendy events I’m great with my old favorites I could eat the same food every week really and I’m not even tired of it why complicate things there are things that matter in life and then there are things that don’t and I choose to focus on what matters to me how could anyone not want that it’s funny how people say you should embrace change I feel like I’ve embraced enough just dealing with things as they come I don’t need more change than life already throws at me I mean the semester just started and I’m already overwhelmed by deadlines and exams looming ahead it’s like they come in waves there’s this rhythm to it all and I just have to keep my head above water even when people are busy chatting about weekend plans I just nod along as if I care but really I just want to finish my readings and hit the gym to stick to my routine it’s all about discipline isn’t it I thrive on it I think it’s all I have is the structure of my tasks like a safety net and those stupid group projects they drive me insane I don’t trust other people's work ethic and that makes them think I’m uptight but I know when I do things I do them right and it’s just so hard to let go if I took a step back I’d worry that everything would fall apart I can’t tolerate sloppiness it just feels messy not that I hate people just don’t ask me to be all sweet and agreeable all the time i can’t fake it I look at people and think how do they keep everything so light and easy and I feel like they just go on whims while I’m here plotting everything out on a spreadsheet like it’s my life’s work I need to know what’s next it’s like the world is on this constant shift and I’m just standing still trying to figure out the moving parts someone told me it’s about balance but what does that even mean waiting for the right moment to act I’ve never been good at that I just like to take the reins and steer the path myself sometimes I envy them—how carefree they seem I wonder if they ever feel overwhelmed the way I do probably not since they just look so put together and calm but here I am stressing about deadlines and planning for next month and wondering if I should go to that party I’m invited to or just stay home and finish my project because what do I really get from socializing anyway just small talk and obligatory laughter that feels empty sometimes I think I’m better off alone but sometimes it feels a bit lonely too like am I missing out on connection but then I remember that I’m okay with being on my own I can read a book or binge a show or just think about my next move without having to worry about syncing up with someone else’s vibe just focus on my responsibilities and keep everything in line that’s the plan really just gotta keep pushing through so many thoughts racing but I feel like it’s fine to just keep my life simple in this crazy world.

Okay so I was just thinking about how exhausting social situations are and how I just want to avoid them because I get so anxious and like my brain is just firing off a million thoughts and they’re all terrible. I mean, what if I say something stupid or what if people don’t like me or what if nobody talks to me and then I’m standing there like an idiot. The idea of being in a group makes my stomach turn and I hate how everyone seems to be so effortlessly engaging with each other while I’m over here just trying to figure out how to exist without being awkward. And then there’s the noise, too much noise all at once it’s like a chaotic mess and I’m just trying to keep it together in my head. I don’t even want to think about group projects, ugh. I’d rather just do my work alone but then again my work is never really done because I don’t finish things. I have this mountain of assignments sitting on my desk and I’m like “yeah I’ll get to it” but instead I just scroll on my phone or binge-watch another show for the third time because it’s comforting to know what’s coming next. Why do I even keep watching the same shows, I mean, I could totally start something new but what if I don’t like it and then I wasted time when I could’ve been doing something familiar? And then I think about how my grades are slipping because I just can’t find the motivation to do what needs to be done, it’s all so overwhelming. I feel like everyone is judging me all the time, even when I don’t know them and haven’t spoken a word. It’s like I can read their thoughts even if it’s just my imagination running wild. They must think I’m such a freak for sitting alone in the corner instead of mingling or whatever. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not meant to fit in anywhere, like I’m this puzzle piece that fell from a box that doesn’t exist anymore. It’s hard to connect with people and I think it’s because I just don’t get why people do what they do. I mean, why does everyone care so much about small talk? It feels pointless to me. I’d rather talk about the deeper stuff right away but that’s a bit intense, even for me. Why am I like this? It’s frustrating. And then I think about how different I am from the people who seem to glide through life. I can’t imagine just being breezy and chill about everything. I have this constant low-grade anxiety that spikes for no reason sometimes. Like my heart will race out of nowhere and I’ll just be sitting there, minding my own business, and boom. Overthinking everything, like I’m stuck in my own head. It’s a trap, I can’t escape it. Sometimes I’ll lay in bed at night and my mind is racing with thoughts about everything I did that day or what I didn’t do. Like why did I have to make that dumb face when I was talking? Or why didn’t I just say something interesting? But no, I just smile awkwardly and nod like an idiot. That leads me to friendships or whatever you call them. I don’t really have many and the ones I have feel fragile. Like, what if they find someone better? Or what if I say the wrong thing and ruin it? I just don’t trust that people won’t leave when they find something better to do. It’s this weird spiral of doubt that just eats away at every interaction. And I feel bad being negative but being positive feels so fake. I can’t do small talk and I can’t fake positivity. It’s exhausting trying to keep up appearances when I feel so distant. I just want to be honest but being honest feels like a risk. Sometimes I imagine being completely alone, like off the grid or something. Just me and my stuff, no pressure to be social or anything. I could read or write or do whatever it is that I feel like doing without interruptions. The pressure to conform to social norms is just so draining. But I also think that’s not realistic because I’d probably just end up feeling lonely after a while. It’s a weird paradox where I crave solitude but then I remember how terrible it can feel when you’re really alone. But honestly who needs friends anyway? They just bring complications and expectations. And then I look at social media and see all these happy faces and think how it’s all fake. Everyone is putting on this performance and I can’t do that. I can’t pretend everything is great when it’s not. I wish I could find a way to just be okay with being alone without feeling like a total loser all the time. It’s a balance that feels impossible to achieve. All I want is some peace and quiet in this noisy chaotic world, but the more I think about it, the more trapped I feel.

Okay so I’m just sitting here and thinking about how I always avoid the group projects. They stress me out so much. People are so unpredictable. I’d rather just do it all myself, but then I get frustrated because it takes longer and I can’t really change that about myself. I know I should try to be more flexible but it’s just hard. My roommate is always like “you need to chill” but that’s not how my brain works. Everything feels like a ticking time bomb and I’m just waiting for it to explode. I hate surprises. Like the time when the professor changed the exam date without any notice. I had a whole plan and suddenly it was all thrown out the window. My heart raced, I felt like I couldn’t breathe. Why can’t they just stick to the schedule? I mean, it’s not that hard, right? Speaking of schedules, I’ve got my planner right here, packed with color-coded notes. I use different colors for classes, assignments, and appointments. It’s a bit excessive I guess but it keeps me organized. I can’t stand clutter. Not just physical clutter but mental clutter too. People say to “clear your mind” but it’s like trying to shove a million thoughts into a box and then the box just explodes. I’m always worried about everything. What if I mess up my presentation? What if I forget to cite something? What if I trip in front of the whole class? My friend says I need to practice self-affirmations but that feels like talking to a wall. Sometimes it feels like everyone else gets it except me. I scroll through social media and see those perfect lives – it just reinforces that feeling like I’m not doing well enough. Ugh, speaking of social media, I can’t deal with it half the time. I tried TikTok but it’s so loud and chaotic. I can’t focus on anything. And these challenges? No thanks. Then everyone acts like you’re the odd one out if you don’t participate. It’s exhausting. I’d rather read a book or anything that lets me be in my own bubble. But then there’s that nagging voice saying “are you missing out?” I don’t know, maybe I am. It’s like a cycle that never ends and it just makes me feel more anxious. And then there are the people in class who just interrupt each other like it’s normal. I can’t stand it. Maybe it’s just my low agreeableness, but I think it’s rude and disruptive. Like just let me finish my thought, you know? But I know I should probably say something, but I don’t want to stir the pot. If you speak up too much, you’re labeled as “difficult.” That’s the last thing I want. I want to get through this without being that person everyone rolls their eyes at. I just want to blend in, do my work, and go home. The fewer interactions, the better. Food is my safe zone. I love meal prepping on Sundays. It’s like a ritual that calms me down. I make the same things every week, chicken and broccoli or maybe quinoa if I’m feeling wild. It’s comforting in its predictability. I find it strange when people talk about going out to try new restaurants. That sounds terrifying – trying new foods? What if I hate it? What if I accidentally order something super spicy? I don’t want to embarrass myself in front of strangers. I’d rather stick to my tried and true meals. Safe. Simple. My friend once said it’s boring, but I just think it’s practical. But then there’s this weird push from society to be more open and adventurous, and it gnaws at me sometimes. Like am I missing some core experience because I’m afraid to step outside my comfort zone? People keep pushing me to be different and challenge myself. I can practically hear them saying “just try it!” But what if I don’t want to? What if I’m perfectly fine just the way I am? It’s frustrating. People are always telling me to relax, to just be myself, but honestly, how do they even define that? What does being yourself look like? I can’t understand how others seem to flow through life so easily while I’m here picking every little thing apart. Overthinking is exhausting. I worry I’ll never be satisfied. The future seems so foggy, and I keep thinking about what job I’ll have, and how I’m supposed to present myself in interviews. What if I say the wrong thing? Deep breath. Okay, I need to take a break from this train of thought. I just wish things felt less overwhelming. Can I just rewind a bit, get back to that safe bubble? I think I’m ready to retreat into my planner, find some quiet. I guess that’s what makes sense to me.

Okay so I was just thinking about how conversations can totally shift the way I feel about a day, like I can be in a funk one minute and then boom, I’m chatting with a friend about our weekend plans and everything’s bright again. It’s wild how energy bounces from person to person, like I feel so alive when I talk to someone who’s excited about something. That’s why I love group projects. I mean, sometimes they can be a lot to handle, but when everyone contributes and there’s a flow of ideas, it’s like we’re creating this awesome energy together and it just makes everything better. Oh, and memories of college events pop into my head, like that one time we had a dance party in the student lounge. Everyone was just letting loose, I think we were all so tired from classes and assignments but then someone brought a speaker and suddenly we were all dancing like we hadn’t a care in the world. I can still hear that playlist in my mind. Speaking of playlists, I was actually going through my Spotify today and I got sidetracked listening to all these throwback songs that remind me of high school, like those campfire nights where we’d sit around and sing, well try to sing, but it was mostly just laughing and badly attempting to harmonize. I miss those simpler times, but I also love where I am. There’s so many new experiences here, like just last week I went to a workshop on sustainable living. The energy in that room was insane! Everyone was so passionate. It gave me all these ideas on how I can incorporate more eco-friendly habits into my day-to-day. Like, it was just a little thing but it made me excited to change my single-use habits. Then, I went home and dug through my kitchen for reusable alternatives. It’s amazing how just one conversation can spark a whole new direction for you. And then there’s the people I meet on campus. I swear, I can strike up a conversation with anyone. Just last night, I shared a table in the cafeteria with someone I’d never seen before. We started talking about food and I found out he’s a foodie too! We ended up sharing our favorite local spots. He told me about this hole-in-the-wall pizza place that serves the best slices. Pizza is definitely one of my favorite foods, so now I need to check that out. Food just brings everyone together, right? It’s like this universal language. Oh wow, I remember the potluck we had last semester. Everyone brought a dish and it was this beautiful mishmash of cultures and flavors. I could taste the love in every bite. How did we even survive before social media to coordinate all that? I think I would at least have a better time being super connected and not having to worry about missing out on anyone’s plans. But isn’t it interesting how some people can be so closed off? I get it, I really do, everyone has their reasons. But I want to know their stories. Like what got them to be that way? I approach everyone with this openness, like what if there’s a great friendship or collaboration just waiting to happen? I think about how life is too short for weird barriers between people. I want to break those down! And it’s funny how sometimes I find myself in a situation where I feel like I’m in a room full of potential friends but nobody’s talking. Like, come on! Let’s share a laugh, let’s dive into a conversation about anything! Like the weather, or that weird cat video that’s going around. Who doesn’t love a good cat meme? Oh that reminds me, I still need to send that one to my friend who loves cats. And then there’s the times I sit down to study and I get way too distracted by ideas! Today, I mean, just thinking about how studying actually can be fun if I make it interactive, like I can grab colorful markers and write notes on big paper. I did that last week for my biology class and it was so much more interesting. I was even singing the terms in my head, like I literally made a song for the cell cycle and it was both ridiculous and effective. I’ve got to remember to incorporate more creativity into my study sessions. And how about when I’m hanging out with friends and we end up talking about our dreams for the future? It’s like, the possibilities are endless! I want to travel, I want to write, I want to help people, all these ambitions swirling around in my head. The world feels so big and full of opportunities and I want to dive into every single one of them. There’s so much to learn from each experience, wow, I just love that feeling of being alive and engaged with everything happening around me. It’s exhilarating!

okay so I’ve been thinking about how people always bring up personality tests and like it’s supposed to tell you about yourself but honestly it feels more confusing sometimes like I don’t know how much I believe in them like I get it but then again do I really fit in any category it’s just strange to think about and I wonder what it means to be low on extraversion maybe it just means I don’t like big crowds or loud places like parties make me anxious but in a subtle way because I’m not the kind of person who freaks out or anything I just don’t enjoy small talk like I would rather be home sometimes with a book or watching a movie and that feels more fulfilling you know? but then again maybe people think that’s weird like why wouldn’t you want to go out and socialize and I get the whole ‘make connections’ thing like I want friends but it's just tiring to be around so many people at once and it takes so much energy maybe that’s why I score low on this scale? It’s just easier to be agreeable than to put myself out there and risk rejection or something. I remember this one time in high school when I was invited to this huge party and I didn’t really know anyone but one person and they were busy most of the night and I felt like I was stuck there awkwardly standing in the corner sipping on lemonade wishing I had brought a book or something to keep myself occupied or maybe even a journal to jot down my thoughts instead of just standing there looking lost like a misplaced puzzle piece and I think about how those moments are just so uncomfortable but nobody really talks about them anyone ever checks in with the quiet ones? And when I think about being low on neuroticism I feel like that means I handle stress okay I mean I have my days when I worry but it’s not like I spiral or anything it just happens and then I get over it usually pretty fast I guess I just take things as they come which is nice but also not great sometimes like I could be more motivated to do a lot of things but I don’t think that’s a bad thing just a different approach like I don’t mind if things go wrong I know they will sometimes and that doesn’t mean I can’t still be friendly with everyone I meet even if I’m not super chatty like I like finding common ground with people it’s just easier that way and I think everyone appreciates a little kindness right? I often end up smiling at people instead of engaging in long conversations it’s like a shortcut to connection without having to get caught up in the noise and speeches that don’t mean much and I hate confrontation so I just go with the flow most of the time but it makes me think that maybe my agreeableness is tied to that because I want everyone to feel good but sometimes maybe that’s to a fault like I don’t want to disappoint anyone so I’ll say yes to things I don’t want to do because I don’t want to hurt feelings it’s a mess sometimes and that probably reflects a bit of low conscientiousness like I forget to plan or stay organized all the time my room is a disaster and sometimes I leave homework until the last minute but it’s not out of fear of failure just more like I get sidetracked easily or distracted by so many things around maybe it’s a blessing and a curse because I stumble upon interesting stuff especially online but then I lose track of time and suddenly it’s 2 a.m. and where did my evening go? And then there’s the low openness part I guess I like routine because it feels secure and change is kinda scary like I don’t really experiment with clothes or food or any of those wild things people do like trying new hobbies or traveling without a solid plan it’s probably why I still haven’t really left the state much or explored what’s beyond familiar areas I think about how some people thrive on spontaneity while I just look for the next comfortable spot and I wonder if that’s mundane or just who I am but I don’t mind being me really maybe being low on that openness means there’s a comfort in predictability like I know what to expect and that feels safe. I just want to find my spot in the world while balancing between knowing I don’t have to be the loudest person in the room to be worthy of being there and understanding that sometimes it’s okay to just blend in though sometimes I wish I could break free of my patterns but it feels like swimming upstream against a strong current and I wonder if that’s what everyone else feels like too or if it’s just me navigating through my own world quietly smiling at the edges of chaos while staying out of the spotlight where I’m comfortable just watching the play unfold.

Okay so I just saw a squirrel outside my window and it reminded me of the stupid argument I had with my roommate last week over keeping the windows open. I mean, who wants to suffocate in a stuffy apartment? I can’t even think when it smells bad like that. But he was like, “What if a bird flies in?” and I was like “Dude, that’s not gonna happen,” but he got all paranoid about it. I just wanted fresh air! Anyway, squirrels are a mess, always running around like they’re late for an appointment or something. They don't care about anything, just go and grab what they can find. Wish I could be like that sometimes, just do whatever without caring what anyone thinks. But I can't. I always care way too much. I feel like I'm constantly on edge. It’s exhausting. I could really use a break. Last week was crazy. Too many assignments, too many people asking me for stuff. Everyone thinks I'm social and can handle it but it makes me want to scream. I can't say no, like ever. I hate saying no. It sounds harsh. I don't want to hurt anyone's feelings even if I feel like just curling up and disappearing. It's like a rollercoaster ride I didn't want to get on in the first place. My high-energy self just can’t say no when someone’s like, “Hey, can you help with this project?” and “Can you go out tonight?” I could totally be a hermit if it didn’t mean disappointing everyone. I mean, staying in feels so much better than putting on a happy face and pretending I'm enjoying myself. Who even enjoys parties? They’re so loud, and I have to be “on” the whole time. Speaking of parties, there was this one last month where I accidentally spilled a drink on some guy's shoes. Total accident but you’d thought I had set his shoes on fire. He got all dramatic, and I was like chill, dude, it’s not the end of the world. But I apologized, because that's what you do, but inside I was thinking, "Wow, get over yourself" but I couldn't say that out loud, because what if he told someone else and it got back to me? Ugh, people are so sensitive these days. Like can’t we all just relax? But then again, it’s not like I’m an angel. I definitely have my moments when I snap at people too. I think I just overshare sometimes when I want to vent. Like, if something bothers me, it comes out and then I instantly regret it because I know how judgmental people can be. Totally need to learn to filter myself better. Why is everyone obsessed with social media? I mean, I get it, you want to share your life, but it’s all so fake. Everyone curating this idea of perfection, and I’m over here scrolling thinking, “Why can’t I have that?” I should probably just delete it, but then I’d miss out on the gossip and memes. Ugh, it's a trap. I get jealous, I hate that. I want to be happy for everyone but then I start comparing everything. My life is like a trash can compared to theirs. It drives me nuts. Like, how do they manage to always look perfect? I never feel perfect. I feel like a walking disaster most days. And then there’s the whole “finding yourself” thing. People constantly preach about self-discovery and I’m like, can we just have a map? It feels like a scavenger hunt without clues. Everyone’s out there living their best lives while I’m here juggling deadlines and trying not to freak out. I thought college was supposed to be fun but honestly, it’s just high school with a bigger budget. Same petty dramas, same cliques. I’m too old for this but somehow I can’t let go. This place is like quicksand. It pulls you in even when you don’t want it to. I keep feeling like I need to fit in but fitting in feels like a chore. Sometimes I think about just packing my bags and moving to a small town. But then I remember I actually like the chaos, in a weird way, even if it stresses me out. The energy of the city buzzes around me and it’s addictive. But then again, I want to scream and hide all at once. I can’t find the right balance. Why does living feel like this constant tug-of-war? Maybe if I just sat down, breathed, and tried not to think, things could get easier. But who am I kidding? I can’t stop thinking. Okay, wow, did I just write all that? I’m tired.

Okay so I just remembered that time last summer when we had that huge barbecue and everyone came over. The smell of grilled burgers was amazing, and I ended up eating way too much potato salad. Who even brought that? Was it Jenna? I think it was Jenna. She always makes the best potato salad. I don’t want to think about calories. I feel like calories don’t really exist when you’re having fun with friends, right? I can’t believe I thought I could escape doing all those summer assignments when all I wanted was to hang out with everyone instead. But that’s fine, I sometimes just push that stuff to the back of my brain and enjoy the moment. I’m really good at enjoying the moment. I mean I thrive on it, nothing like a good laugh, especially when someone tells a hilarious story about something dumb they did like that time Tim got locked out of his apartment in his pajamas. I can’t help but laugh just thinking about it. Speaking of laughter, I hope we do another barbecue soon. I love when everyone is just sitting around, telling stories, and it feels like nothing else matters. It’s kind of like a mini-vacation. I wish I could just pack up life into a weekend like that every week, where everything is easy and fun and nobody's stressing about papers or exams or boring responsibilities. Just the sun and good food and those silly games we play, like cornhole. What a weird name for a game, honestly. Cornhole. It’s like it was named by a five-year-old. But it’s fun, and I’m really competitive, even if I don’t have the skills to back it up, I just love trying to throw those bags and show off a little, you know? It’s exhilarating. Right now my phone is buzzing with notifications, which probably means another group chat is blowing up. It’s either about the next get-together or just random memes that make absolutely no sense and yet still crack me up. I feel like we spend so much time sharing memes, it’s like our own language. I can’t keep up with all the trends but I love that people send me things that make me laugh, even if I have no idea who these characters are or why they’re doing silly dances. I mean, as long as I get a good chuckle, I’m in! I wonder if it’s weird to think about memes like that? Probably, but whatever. I’m having fun, and isn’t that what really matters? Oh, and I was thinking about Halloween coming up, which is basically my favorite holiday. I love costumes, and just being ridiculous with friends. I don’t even care about the candy that much; it’s all about dressing up! Last year I went as a taco, and it was the best choice ever. Everyone was cracking up and taking pictures. I really enjoy the spotlight, I guess. It’s just so freeing to get a laugh and wear something outrageous. Like, who cares if I’m not the best at making sure every detail is perfect? I just want it to be memorable and fun. Costumes are all about having a good time, right? I can’t stand when people get too serious about everything. They miss out on life! Honestly, I feel like I see the world differently. Like, what’s the point of stressing out over grades when I can just make friends and laugh my way through college? Sure, I want to graduate, but not if it means I have to miss out on all these good times. I can totally cram for finals, but I’ll take those memories with me. That’s the stuff that matters. I think about that sometimes, how life is so short. It’s just a bunch of moments strung together, and I just want mine to be filled with laughter and joy.
