I know I should probably be focusing on my resume or internships or whatever, but man, give me a break! That gets boring, and I just want to hang out and forget about all that for a little while. I also love spontaneous adventures. Like when we drove to that random beach because someone saw the ocean on Instagram and decided we needed to go. It was almost midnight. Who cares? We jumped in the car, totally unprepared. It was almost chaotic, but that’s what made it awesome. I love that feeling of being alive and just going for it. I mean, who has the time to be super organized all the time? Not me, that’s for sure. I’m all about the experience, the thrill of just doing things and breaking the routine. I feel like my friends get me, and we all agree—life is too short to stress about being perfect or doing the right thing all the time. Let’s just eat good food, hang out, and laugh until our stomachs hurt.

Okay so I was just thinking about how the sunlit days make everything feel so alive, even in winter when everything is gray I swear the tiniest bit of sun just makes me feel like bursting into song or something. There’s this vibrant energy that just pulses through the air, especially when I go out with friends, there’s something incredibly magnetic about the way laughter echoes through a crowd, it creates these little waves of connection. I really love how spontaneous plans can turn into the best memories, like last week we decided on a whim to hit that new taco place, the one with the funky decor and the giant cactus outside, oh my god the tacos were out of this world. And then, I want to remember the way the evening unfolded, we ended up wandering through the streets, listening to a street performer playing this jazzy tune that just made me want to dance right there on the sidewalk, but I held back a little, I didn’t want to embarrass myself in front of strangers and yet the urge is so strong sometimes, to just let the rhythm take over. Why do we care so much about what others think, right? I mean, I can talk to basically anyone, and that’s great, but when it comes to dancing, it’s like a wall goes up. Sometimes I wonder how people can be so uptight about things like dancing or singing too loudly in public, it’s all just fun, right? People get so stressed out about the smallest things, but I choose to focus on the laughter and the joy, I thrive on the friendships and the shared experiences, it fuels me in ways I can't even describe. Like that time we all got together for karaoke, and yeah, I'm not a great singer but who cares? The way we laughed and cheered each other on just felt like magic. Speaking of magic, I cannot believe how quickly the semester is flying by. It’s almost like life has this sneaky way of getting away from you when you’re too busy enjoying it, like you blink and suddenly it’s finals week. I mean, studying is an afterthought for me sometimes, especially when there are so many fun things happening. And don’t get me wrong, I care about my grades, but I also want to live these moments fully. It’s this constant balance, like, do I dive into textbooks or do I dive into life? And friendships! Oh my goodness, they’re like the coolest adventure ever. I love how easy it is to connect with people. I met this girl in class, she has this quirky style, bright hair and smiles that light up the room, and we just clicked instantly. We were chatting about books and art, and before I knew it, we had plans to check out this gallery opening next week, so spontaneous. It’s crazy how these little moments can turn into big friendships, isn’t it? And then there are the late-night talks about dreams and aspirations. I love hearing about what inspires people, what makes them tick, it’s like unlocking this treasure chest of creativity. And yet, I find myself sometimes losing track of what I’m supposed to do. Like, I keep telling myself I’ll get that assignment done, but then I’m out with friends, or I’m getting lost in daydreams about travel, like where would I go if I had the chance? I’ve always wanted to backpack through Europe, experience the culture, the art, the food, oh the food! Each country has something unique, and I just want to soak it all up, meet people from all walks of life, hear their stories. Traveling feels like it would be an endless adventure, and I crave that. There’s this great big world outside my window just waiting to be explored, and the thought of staying put feels suffocating sometimes. I want to experience everything, every little nook and cranny filled with history, culture, laughter, and stories waiting to be shared. It’s exhilarating to think about! Oh, and then there are those times when I sit alone and reflect, maybe a little too deeply sometimes, but it’s all good. I think about friendships and how they evolve, how some stick while others just drift apart, and it’s not a bad thing honestly, just the ebb and flow of life. There’s so much beauty in that impermanence, the way people come and go, how new connections blossom. And hey, maybe I’ll just grab my backpack and go on an adventure someday soon. Maybe I’ll even end up in a foreign land with friends, or make new ones along the way and just let the experience wash over me, I’m all in for whatever comes next.

I keep thinking about the party last night. Everyone was there, even people I didn’t expect. It’s funny how social energy just builds and everyone seems to turn into this weird version of themselves when they’re having fun. I thought I saw someone from my history class but wouldn’t have expected them to show up. They were dancing on the table, and honestly, who thought that was a good idea? But it was hilarious, I live for that kind of chaotic energy. Jumping from one conversation to another, I mean why stick to one group when there’s so much going on? I remember talking about everything from philosophy to what we think about pineapple on pizza, which is an absolute crime against humanity by the way. But other people think it’s amazing, I guess that’s just how opinions go. There’s this constant buzz in the air, people laughing, music blasting, it’s like a medley of life itself. I think I even spilled my drink on someone’s shoes, but they just laughed it off like it was nothing. People are so interesting when they let their guard down. I love observing, but I also love being right in the center of it all. I wonder what it feels like to be someone who would rather stay home and watch Netflix. Boring? I can’t imagine missing out on all this energy floating around. Like, how do you even resist the pull of a vibrant crowd, especially when there’s music you can’t help but dance to even if you can’t dance? Who cares if I look ridiculous? I must be the most uncoordinated dancer on the planet, but it feels freeing. Movement is life. Oh, and speaking of the music, there was this one DJ whose set was just mind-blowing. I wish I could have that kind of talent to mix beats like they do. It’s like they know just how to lift the crowd. I heard glimpses of techno, some hip-hop, and even that one pop song I can’t get out of my head. It’s the sixth time I’ve played it today, the chorus just loops in my mind. And now it’s stuck, great. I’m singing it in the shower and probably irritating my roommate. But whatever, I can’t help it if I like to blast my playlist. There was a moment when I found myself on the porch, just taking it all in, the smoke from somebody’s cigarette drifting up into the night sky. The cool air felt amazing after being packed in with all those people, and the stars were actually pretty clear for a city night. I thought about how rarely we actually look up. So much is happening around us, but we forget to simply breathe it all in. I tried to convince a couple of friends to join me for a late-night stroll after the party, but they were too wrapped up in their own conversations. I should’ve just gone by myself, but there’s this social pressure to stick together. I love spontaneity, but sometimes I feel like I’m the only one who does. The hardest part about being this social butterfly is when people want to plan everything and have set times. Who wants to live life on a schedule? It’s like, let’s just get in the car and drive somewhere new. Last month, we just took off to the coast at midnight. No destination, just vibes and the open road. We cranked up the music, blasted through the twists and turns. That feeling of not knowing what’s next is exhilarating. I wonder what it would be like to just keep going forever, like a road trip that never ends. A random thought—what’s the deal with those “life hacks” people post online? I mean they’re either too good to be true or just pointless. I find it funny how people live their lives waiting for some magical trick to make everything easier. Sometimes it feels better to just mess up and learn from it. Like that one time I tried to make pancakes and burned them to a crisp. I laughed about it because it was ridiculous. Or how I once almost missed an exam because I thought it started an hour later—thankfully I made it but my head was spinning. But then there's this urge to try everything—food, experiences, new people. I just can’t stick to one thing for too long. I’m always on the lookout for what’s new and exciting. I remember this spontaneous decision to try out that salsa dance class. I was so out of my league, but jumping right in was such a thrill. Everyone was so welcoming—people were smiling, clapping, and giving each other high-fives. That’s so much of what life should be about—connection, laughter, and those fleeting moments that just stick in your mind. I don’t ever want to stop exploring, meeting, experiencing. There’s just no time to waste.

I just remembered that time I went to that amazing concert with friends last summer. Oh man, the energy in that place was insane. Everyone was singing along and just vibing hard. I love how music brings people together. There's something about the shared experience of being surrounded by a crowd, all of us moving to the beat. It’s electric. Speaking of electricity, my phone battery is at 30% but I’ve got my charger in my bag luckily because I can’t stand when my phone dies. I use it for everything! It’s like a lifeline. You know what’s hilarious? I once freaked out because I thought I lost it, and I found it in the fridge. How does that even happen? Oh right, my friend Sarah texted me about that new café opening downtown. I’m so excited to check it out! I mean, I love trying new foods and drinks. It’s like a little adventure every time. I wonder if they have matcha lattes. I’ve been obsessed with those lately. The whole process of making them is like a little ritual for me now, whisking the powder just right. I should invite some friends to come along. They’ll love it. I always want people to be included. Remember that art exhibit I went to last month? It was mind-blowing. I still can’t get over the giant installation made of recycled materials. Talk about creativity. I really appreciate when artists think outside the box and challenge conventional ideas. It makes me want to dive into my own creative projects, but then I get sidetracked by all the things on my to-do list. Speaking of which, I need to set aside some time to actually start painting again. I've been putting it off for ages. I could just do it on a whim, blast some tunes, and see where it takes me. That freedom of expression is just exhilarating. Where was I? Right, my to-do list. Sometimes I think I might be a little too organized, but I like it that way. Making lists gives me a sense of control, even if chaos is just a step away. I swear I thrive in the middle of it though, bouncing from one task to another, chatting with people, finding something new to get excited about. I can’t imagine being all cooped up in my room without human interaction. I crave those connections. I’ll probably text my buddy Dave later to see how he’s doing. We always have the best conversations, like we’ll get into deep discussions about life, and then switch to the silliest topics and just laugh until our stomachs hurt. Oh! I need to remember to buy those concert tickets for next month. It’s for that band I’ve been obsessing over. I can’t believe they’re coming to town! The moment the tickets go on sale, I’m going to race to grab them. I bet it’ll sell out fast. I could plan a whole night around it, dinner, drinks, the whole shebang. I love planning those kinds of outings. It gives me a sense of purpose, keeps everything moving and exciting. Maybe I could ask a bunch of people to join, like a mini road trip vibe. Road trips are the best, right? The music playing, the snacks, and the laughs. It’s all about the journey and the random places we stop along the way. I might need to get my car checked though. It’s been making this weird noise lately. Like, it’s probably nothing but who knows? I’m the type that likes to address things before they become a problem. It’s probably that conscientiousness kicking in. Just part of me wanting to manage everything efficiently. Gotta stay on top of that stuff, or I’ll drive myself nuts. And I hate feeling that anxiety creeping in, which is why I generally try to stay positive and proactive. I can’t help but smile when I think about my friends and how we support each other through everything. Also, I should probably start doing more outdoor activities. I’ve been saying that forever. Hiking, biking, playing volleyball at the park—so many things to enjoy outside with friends. I miss those days! Nature is just so refreshing, and every time I'm out there, I can’t help but soak it all in—the sights, sounds, the fresh air. It’s like a reset button. Did I just say reset button? I sound like an app or something. Anyway, I love spontaneous plans where we just grab a frisbee and head to the park. Life should be fun and unpredictable. Okay, focus! I really need to finish that assignment due next week. It’s not that hard, but I’ve been procrastinating. I guess I’m more motivated when there’s a deadline looming over me. That’s when the creativity really kicks in! I just get excited about things and dive right in. I can do this! I’ll wrap things up here and get back to studying. The brain gets buzzing with all the ideas and that’s when the true magic happens.

Okay so I was just thinking about how people can be so weird sometimes like today in class this guy just kept tapping his pencil and I couldn't focus on the lecture and I kept giving him the side-eye like can you not but then I thought maybe he was just really into whatever we were discussing which is kind of cool I guess but then again it’s just a pencil like how hard is it to just chill for one hour? And it's like sometimes I wonder if people realize how distracting they are when they can just zone out and do their own thing or if they’re just so in their own heads that they don’t even notice the world around them, makes me think about how I get lost in my thoughts all the time too. I wonder if my friends think I’m annoying because I can’t sit still like why is it always me who wants to do something spontaneous, like hey let’s go get ice cream at midnight or go on a random road trip to the coast. People are always saying no, it’s too late, or it’s too far but last week we drove six hours to see that concert and I had a blast even if we got lost twice, that was part of the fun right? I mean if you’re not living life on the edge, what are you doing? But I also get anxious all the time, like I can’t help but overthink every little thing I say or do, and I won’t lie, it messes with my head. I’ll replay conversations in my mind and cringe like, did I sound stupid? Did they think I was trying too hard? I should have just said what I actually felt, but no, I always go for the laughs when the situation calls for something more serious. And then I worry. What if I’m ruining my friendships? I have this friend from high school who I haven’t talked to in ages and I keep meaning to reach out but then I think, what if I’m not interesting enough? What if they’ve changed so much that we have nothing in common anymore? But also I miss them, so it’s a mess in my head. I’m always overcommitting to social events too, like I signed up for three clubs this semester and I’m already regretting it because now I have meetings every other night but I just love being around people and connecting, it fuels me, it’s what I thrive on. Then again, I don’t know if they can handle my energy. Sometimes I feel like I’m just bouncing off the walls and they’re all there like, “Chill out a little,” but I can’t help it, it’s almost like I can’t turn it off. And it’s wild too because when I feel like things are going well, I’m on a high, like life is amazing, but then the smallest thing can trigger me and I spiral down to thinking everything is going wrong and I start doubting myself and everything I do. Like last week I submitted that paper and I thought I did a pretty good job but then I kept worrying about it, imagining the professor just shaking their head at my writing, and then I didn’t sleep well and was exhausted for days, but at least I survived the group project without losing my mind. It’s amazing how much is happening all the time, like I’m constantly meeting new people, going to parties, but also freaking out about every decision I make and letting myself get tangled in my own thoughts. I saw this meme the other day about how introverts recharge by being alone while extroverts recharge by being with people and I totally get that but sometimes it feels like I can’t find the balance. There’s this constant push and pull between wanting to be out there and not wanting to overexert myself to the point where I crash and burn. But it makes me feel alive when I’m out dancing or laughing with friends, even if it’s just at some random bar or a backyard BBQ. That high of connection and spontaneity is addicting. I could never be that person who just stays in every weekend, like the FOMO would eat me alive, but then I get home and crash and just lay there thinking about everything, and my mind won’t stop racing. It’s exhausting yet exhilarating at the same time. I think I’m just wired this way. I guess I wouldn’t change it either because when I’m in the moment, it feels incredible even if the aftermath is a whirlwind of anxiety and sleepless nights. I just hope I can figure out how to manage it all before next semester! Maybe I need to find some kind of grounding technique? Or should I just embrace the chaos? Life is such a crazy ride, isn’t it?

Okay so I just thought about how funny it is that I can be in a room full of people and still feel like I’m on a different planet, like everyone’s talking about their weekend plans and I’m just listening and imagining how wild it would be if we all decided to do a spontaneous road trip instead, like pack up some snacks, crank up the music, and just drive until we feel like stopping. I wonder where we’d go. I’ve always wanted to see the Grand Canyon. It seems massive and surreal, I can almost picture myself standing at the edge, feeling the earth’s roar beneath my feet. Sometimes I think about how open the world is and how many different people there are, each with their own stories that I would love to hear. Speaking of stories, my friend Sarah just got back from her trip abroad and I can’t wait to hear every little detail. Then again, I also love how stories morph and twist when you share them with different people. Like, if I told my wild idea about the road trip to my buddy Dave, he’d probably turn it into some epic adventure with dragons and treasure hunts, and I’m just like, yes, please! The more I think about it, the more I want to grab some friends and just start driving. The thought gets me buzzing with excitement. Road trips are the ultimate freedom, right? Anyway, last week I was volunteering at the animal shelter and it’s just the best feeling. I love getting my hands dirty cleaning the kennels and caring for the pups. They have so much energy and they just want to be loved, you know what I mean? I always end up bringing home little gifts, like a collar or a toy, even when I promised myself I wouldn’t. But how can you resist that little wagging tail? It’s a bit chaotic but the energy is contagious. I feel like everyone should work with animals at some point, just for the joy it brings. Oh and speaking of joy, the other day I tried out this new taco joint down the street with a couple of my friends after class and it was one of those places where you just know the food’s gonna be good just by looking at the colorful decor and the smell wafting through the air. We spent hours laughing and sharing our favorite food stories. I love how food can bring people together like that. We ordered way too many tacos, but seriously, can you ever have too many tacos? No way. And then there was this dessert, churros with hot chocolate sauce, and I could feel my heart do a little dance. I love moments like that. Moments where you’re just being and there’s no pressure to perform or act a certain way. Just existing, and everyone’s in the same vibe. And then I remember when I took that trip to the beach last summer with those same friends, how we stayed up late talking under the stars and sharing dreams. Somebody mentioned wanting to travel to Europe and then we all started planning, even though we didn’t have any real plans. I think sometimes we create these huge ideas in our minds, like setting dates and making it all official, but really, it’s just the thought of adventure that pulls us together. Sometimes I feel like I have this never-ending list of things I want to do and I don’t know how to prioritize them. Learning to cook more, trying to pick up a new language, maybe that art class I was eyeing. I can get so caught up in the excitement of new experiences. But there’s something comforting about routine too, like how I always wake up and end up making the same coffee, it’s like a little hug in a mug. Speaking of hugs, I feel like we need more of them in the world right now. Like, I just want to grab random people and give them a squeeze because life is too short for distance. You ever just see someone who looks like they’re having a rough day and you’re tempted to go over and say, “Hey, I hope your day gets better”? Sometimes I do that, and it’s amazing how people respond. I love how kindness can ripple outwards in unexpected ways. Then there are days when I just want to retreat into my own little world, grab a book, and get lost in pages filled with people and places I’ve never known but want to understand. Books are a doorway, aren’t they? They let you experience lives you’ll never lead and see things through different eyes. And don’t get me started on the possibilities of creativity – writing, drawing, creating music. I just want to dive into all of it, every single corner of existence. There’s so much to explore and I’m here for it all!

The clock says 3:47 PM and I’m just sitting here trying to focus. Why is it so hard to concentrate on one thing? My mind is racing like a hamster on a wheel. I can’t stop thinking about how I forgot to respond to that email and how my laundry is still in the washer. What am I even doing? I should probably get up, but I don’t want to face the social interaction of the world outside this room. I mean, I like people and all, but sometimes I just can't deal with their energy, especially when I’m in a zone. I think being agreeable is great in theory. I want everyone to be happy, but then there’s always that nagging voice in my head saying, “What if they don’t like you?” or “Are you really saying what you mean?” I wish I could just float through life without worrying so much. Maybe I’ll just be a hermit. It seems easier. Then again, the world is so vast and interesting. I love learning random facts about different cultures. The way they interact, their customs, the variety of food. Food! Why haven’t I eaten yet? I’m starving but also kind of paralyzed by choice. I have leftovers but they don’t sound that appealing. Maybe I should order something, but then I think about how much money I’m spending. I’m so bad with money. Like, really bad. I always say I’ll save, but then I see a cool book or some art supplies and it’s like my mind goes blank, the urge takes over. I can justify it. “Oh, but I need this for my creativity!” That’s what I say to myself, but deep down I’m just losing control of my financial life. I want to be responsible. I really do. But later. Right now, I want to jump into painting or maybe even write a poem. I really like poetry. Something about expressing feelings in a raw, messy way speaks to my soul. But then there’s that voice again, judging me. What if it’s bad? What if no one understands? I don’t care, right? But I do care. I care too much. I feel like a sponge soaking up everyone’s emotions. Maybe it’s because I empathize. I see someone down or stressed, and it sticks to me like glue. I wish I could help everyone, make it all better. But then I get overwhelmed and I feel anxious because I can’t fix everything. It’s like I’m in this constant push and pull between doing what I want and worrying about how it'll affect others. And then there’s creativity, the endless well of possibilities. I love how it can be an escape. Sometimes I think about traveling, just packing a bag and hitting the road. Maybe to some little town where they have quirky shops and galleries. I’d love to explore those places, meet the locals, and hear their stories. I always feel like they have deeper insights into life. That open-mindedness sparks so much joy in me. But then, going back to the social stuff, even the thought of meeting new people makes me anxious. What if they don’t like me? What if I freeze up when I try to talk? And then I remember that one time at that party when I couldn’t find a conversation, and I just stood there awkwardly until someone rescued me. It was mortifying. The weird thing is, I crave connection but then the anxiety kicks in. I can’t help but think about it. It’s this loop. I wish I could be one of those people who effortlessly mingles, but here I am, tying myself in knots over the idea of having a casual conversation. I can spend hours thinking about the “what-ifs” and imagining every possible scenario. Sometimes I think about how much better life would be if more people were open to understanding each other. It’s hard to find that balance though, living in a world that often feels shallow and disconnected. I want to make things meaningful. Every experience has a story, every person has a background that shapes who they are, and I want to dive into that. But every deep conversation seems to stretch me thin sometimes. I’m all about the depth, but it can be exhausting. How do I find a way to express myself without fear? How do I navigate this swirling mix of emotions and creativity and societal expectations? I keep coming back to the idea that it’s all about the journey. I mean, emotion is messy, and I’m messy, we’re all messy, and that’s okay. It’s just about finding the beauty in it, right? So, I’ll keep exploring, keep feeling. And maybe one day, I’ll find a way to share that without all the fear.

Okay so I just saw this really cool video about the ocean and like how deep it is and all the weird creatures down there that we don’t even know about. I can never get over how vast the ocean is and how there might be entire species we’ve never even seen. Like imagine one day they pull this giant squid out of the water that’s like the size of a bus and it’s just been chillin’ down there for centuries. Makes you think about how much we don’t understand about the earth or ourselves. Sometimes I want to dive into all of that, just explore, but I also get this twinge of anxiety about what I might find. It’s like, can you be curious and terrified at the same time? Because I totally can! It’s like when I go to a party and I’m excited to see everyone but also feel this tight knot in my stomach because what if no one talks to me or worse, what if I say something super awkward? I mean, people say I’m friendly and I try to be, but what if they’re just saying that to be nice? There’s a part of me that’s always thinking about everyone’s opinions, like how will this come off, did I seem too eager or not eager enough? Why can’t I just be chill? Ah, it’s exhausting sometimes because I care so much about making everyone feel good but then I’m so tightly wound up about my own performance. I think that’s what drives my friends crazy sometimes, they say I stress too much over the little things but I can’t help it! Then there’s the whole thing about goals and getting things done. I really do love making plans and lists. It gives me such a sense of accomplishment when I check things off. Yesterday I wrote down “grocery shopping” and felt amazing when I got everything I needed. I even organized my fridge when I got home! Like, I totally separated the veggies from the fruits and put the sauces on the door. I mean, who does that? But it just looks so good and makes cooking feel way more fun, you know? But then I think about all the things I still have left to do and I feel this rush of pressure. Like there’s this looming cloud of deadlines and responsibilities that just hangs over me. It’s not even just academic stuff, like I’m thinking about my friends too. I want to be there for them, but there’s so many of them! I can’t keep track of everyone’s problems and joys. Social media makes it even harder because I see everyone doing these amazing things and I want to cheer for them or reach out but it’s like, how do I even know they need me? What if they’re just putting up a front? I try to be that person who supports them through everything, sending memes or texts just to say “hey, I’m here” because I really do care but sometimes it feels like I’m juggling too many balls in the air. It’s a fine line between being helpful and being overwhelming. And speaking of overwhelming, group projects are the worst! I mean, I love collaborating but also, why can’t everyone just step it up and do their part? I’m always the one who ends up organizing meetings and making sure everyone’s involved and I think it’s because I can’t stand the thought of leaving someone out. It’s like, are we all in this together or not? But then I panic about getting too bossy or pushy and it’s such a mess in my head, wondering if I’m being too much or if they actually appreciate it. But there’s something about putting myself out there that I really thrive on too, like I get a high from meeting new people and trying new things. I went to this art exhibit last week and just fell in love with the colors and the way each piece felt like a piece of someone’s soul. I ended up talking to the artist and who knew they were just as passionate about their work as I was about my studies? It’s always those little connections that light me up! I keep wanting to travel more and see new cultures, and I think about all the food I’d get to try! Ugh, all the food! Street tacos in Mexico or fresh pastries in Paris, oh my gosh I could just cry thinking about how delicious that would be. I get all fluttery inside at the thought of it, but then my stress brain kicks in and I think about the logistics, the money, the time away from school, and I get all tangled up again. Okay, breathe. I want to embrace the freedom of being open to experiences and not overthinking every little thing. I just need to find that balance between being caring and staying true to myself, and maybe being a little more adventurous. I think I can figure it out, it’s all just part of the journey, right?

that notebook on my desk is full of to-do lists I never finish and they drive me nuts. I thrive on order but somehow I still can't get myself to do the things I plan. Why is that? I mean, I know there's a method to my madness. A word doc with bullet points but then I end up staring at my screen thinking about how that one assignment isn't going to do itself. At least I haven't lost any friends over it, not that I had many to begin with. It's just easier to avoid all the small talk, y'know? I mean what’s even the point of chatting about the weather or the latest Netflix series when there’s a stack of papers needing my attention. I think about how it’s totally fine to be this way. I don’t need validation from others, and sometimes the noise of engagement is just too much. I remember that one time in class where we had to do icebreakers and everyone was supposed to share something interesting. I just sat there because what was interesting about my life? I spend weekends organizing my notes and reviewing textbooks—how exciting—so I just shared that I like to keep my files in alphabetical order. That got me some weird looks and someone joked about being a librarian. I mean, I might as well be. It’s not like I’d be handing out candy or chatting about my social life like most people do. But that was fine. I prefer being alone to being surrounded by noise. They should really understand. I can focus so much better without distractions like loud music or annoying chatter. Been trying to stick to my cleaning schedule lately. I make a plan and then sometimes I don’t even stick to it and it’s frustrating as hell but I feel like it’s a reflection of my commitment. I need structure. If the kitchen’s not clean, how can I concentrate? Everything needs to be in its place. Even my thoughts feel jumbled when there’s stuff scattered everywhere. So I put a sticky note on my laptop that says “clean kitchen” and it stares at me like a nagging parent. But it’s okay because I will get to it. Got this weird feeling about spontaneity. Like why would I go and do something unplanned when there’s a perfectly good schedule to stick to? I just want to know what’s going to happen next instead of winging it, it feels like a waste of time. And people are all about going with the flow, I see those Instagram stories of friends at a party or road trip and I can’t understand how they don’t lose their minds. I’d rather plan out a day trip that includes scenic routes, rest stops, and a satisfying lunch at the same diner where I know the service is quick and the coffee is strong. Seriously, who decided life was better without a plan? They need structure, too. Then there's that study group I reluctantly joined. It’s not that I dislike them; they just get too distracted too easily. We started out focused on our project, but then someone brought up their weekend plans and it spiraled into a whole hour of tangents. I mean, I get that socializing can be fun for some, but I felt like I was wasting my time. I was there for productive discussions on theories and methodologies, not to hear about someone’s cat. Cats are cute, sure, but that’s not what I showed up for. I don’t understand why it’s so hard for people to stay on topic. Maybe I’m just too rigid or something, but I think if we all sat down for a hard hour of focused work, we’d get a lot more done. Now I've got that question in my head—what will I do after graduation? Everyone's so excited about joining the workforce, but the idea of a 9-to-5 feels like settling into a routine that could be suffocating. What if I end up in a job that doesn’t let me work alone? I don't want to be micromanaged or surrounded by people who don't understand why I prefer silence. I mean, I can play the game. I can smile and nod and be polite. I just don’t get the thrill of it all. My parents always said I needed to be more social but they just don’t get it. I’m fine. I don’t need to pour my soul out to anyone or have a crowd around me to feel good. I can find contentment in my quiet corners, piecing together facts and organizing my thoughts. It’s a comforting solitude. And honestly, sometimes I think they’re the ones who need to step back and see it’s okay to be different. There’s this weird pressure to be outgoing and bubbly when sometimes I just want to be me, which is never a bad thing. People should learn to appreciate the quiet, the focus, and the planning. That’s where the magic is.

Okay so I’ve been thinking about that organization project coming up and how I want to make sure everything is perfect. I can’t stand when things aren’t organized; it just feels wrong, like why would you want to have a cluttered desk or messy notes. Also, I find it really rewarding to check things off a list. Like that feeling when you finish a task, it’s just so satisfying, right? Makes me feel accomplished. Like I did something meaningful, even if it’s just a small task. I feel like a lot of people don’t understand that but whatever. I don’t want to judge. I guess there are just different ways to feel fulfilled. Speaking of fulfillment, my roommate keeps talking about going out to parties or social gatherings. I just can’t imagine what’s so appealing about that. Loud music, crowded spaces, the pressure to socialize. It’s exhausting just thinking about it. I’d rather stay in and read a book or maybe finish up some assignments. I like knowing what to expect. I like my routine. A good breakfast in the morning, maybe oatmeal with some fruit. Then classes and study sessions. Then wind down with a nice show or a podcast. Simplicity is key. Too much stimulation gives me a headache, honestly. Also, it’s funny how people perceive me sometimes. They see me as this calm, easy-going person but really, I just like to avoid conflict. I mean who needs that drama in their life? If someone disagrees with me, I’ll just nod and smile and let it slide. It’s a relief to just go with the flow. That’s probably why I’m friends with so many different kinds of people. I get along with almost anyone. I listen, I support, I’m respectful. It feels like the right thing to do. Why create friction when you can have harmony? But still, sometimes I wonder if I’m being too agreeable. Maybe I should stand up for myself more? But then again, what’s the point in arguing? Ugh I need to remember to get groceries. I’ll just pick up the same things I always get. Chicken, rice, some veggies, and eggs. No need to try anything new, right? I like what I know. It’s comforting. This week’s meal prep should be simple, nothing fancy. I prefer knowing exactly what I’m getting into. I saw my friend trying a new recipe the other day, and it looked complicated. All those ingredients and steps. No thanks. I’ll stick to my routine. There’s something satisfying about that predictability. And what’s up with people posting their entire lives online? It’s like, do you really need to document every single moment? I mean, I get wanting to share things, but it’s overwhelming. I think I’d feel drained if I had to keep up with that. I prefer the quiet moments, just me and my thoughts. I don’t want to be constantly comparing myself to others or feeling pressured to fit in with whatever trend is going on. It’s just not worth it. Oh, I’ve also been thinking about the study I’m participating in. It’s interesting to see how they categorize personality traits and how that correlates with behaviors. I’m definitely low on neuroticism, which I guess is good because I don’t really worry much. Other people seem so stressed out all the time about things that seem trivial. I just don’t see the point in panicking over small stuff. Just breathe. Things usually work out, right? I like being reliable, being that person others can count on. I feel like it’s my role, and I embrace it. It’s nice to be there for friends and family. But then again, sometimes I catch myself thinking that maybe I should be a bit more adventurous. Like, what’s stopping me? I guess I don’t have that desire to explore new experiences. Maybe it’s a lack of curiosity, or just comfort in staying where I am. I think I’m okay with that. Anyway, I’ve got deadlines coming up and I want to stay on top of everything. I like to plan ahead. I have a calendar and everything is color-coded. It helps me keep track. It’s soothing to look at, really. And then I ponder about what I want to do after graduation. There’s so much pressure to have everything figured out, but I just want to find a job that makes sense and allows me to be organized and productive. I could see myself in a supportive role, helping others achieve their goals. That would make me feel good about my work. I can picture that and it feels right. Okay back to studying. Got to focus. Can't let any distractions seep in. Let’s get this done!
