Okay so I was just thinking about how I really don’t get social gatherings. I mean, people always want to hang out, and I’m like, why? It’s just exhausting. I’d rather finish my assignments and maybe grab some snacks at home. Snacks are great. The other day I found a box of popcorn in the pantry that I forgot about, and that was like digging for treasure. Focus. This study is due soon and I keep thinking about how to organize my notes. Notes are essential because without good notes, what do you have? Just a jumble of thoughts that don’t get you anywhere. Why are people so fascinated by spontaneity? I can’t wrap my head around it. I have a plan for everything. I’m a planner. I like knowing what comes next. It’s comforting. I don’t understand why people find comfort in chaos. Some friends of mine just throw things together last minute and it’s like, what is happening? They’re okay with that but I have to think ahead or I just feel this rising tide of anxiety. Yeah, anxiety from not being organized or prepared for unexpected events. But I don't feel that often. I just think it’s common sense. So I was cleaning out my closet yesterday. Noticed I still have some old clothes from high school. Decided to donate them. But it’s hard to part with clothes, you know? They carry memories, like that one shirt I wore on my first day of college. I remember thinking, “I hope I don’t embarrass myself.” Spoiler alert: I didn’t, but some people did. And it’s funny how that stays with you. Other people making mistakes while you’re in the background just watching. I don’t mind some mistakes; I can laugh at them, I guess. And then there's the whole thing about group projects. I hate group projects. They're like no one's getting anything done but everyone feels like they have to contribute or everyone’s just quiet, and it’s awkward. I would much rather do it alone. Like, I know what I’m doing. I can manage. But then other people are there, and they want to have input and I’m just thinking, why? I mean, I can write a ten-page paper in a night if I have to. I just don’t want to deal with anyone else’s ideas getting in my head. And then there’s deadlines. I always have things ready a week in advance. I just can’t handle last-minute stress. It gives me this sort of discomfort, like nails on a chalkboard. Then there are the times when people ask me to do something outside my usual routine. I’ll be like, “That’s not in my calendar.” And they’ll laugh, thinking I’m joking. But I’m not. I’m serious. Calendar is life. I have color-coded segments and everything, like, here’s time for studying, here’s time for gym, and right here is time for eating and watching that new show everyone’s raving about but I can never seem to find the time to watch it so I just stick to what I know, like rewatching things I’ve already seen. It’s reliable. Super comfortable. Friendships can get tricky. I know I’m not the most agreeable person. I can come off as blunt but hey, isn’t honesty the best policy? The real kicker is knowing you’re a little too honest sometimes. People don’t get that sometimes you just say what you mean and they take it to heart. I’ve had to apologize a few times because I said something that was a little too real. It’s not my fault everyone is so sensitive. I think emotional awareness is overrated. I’m just here to get my degree and then go figure things out on my own. Speaking of which, I really need to get back to my studies. There’s a long reading list I promised myself I’d finish. I made a list of what to read first, obviously. Each book has a shelf date, and they’re all lined up just waiting for me. I can’t help but feel satisfied by it. Structure is important. It leads to success. I just wish the world had more structure too—things would be smoother that way. I have this vision in my head of a well-run society—everybody doing their tasks efficiently, no procrastination, everyone on the same page. But then it’ll hit me: life isn’t like that, and it’s kind of frustrating. Other people just drift through life and it doesn’t seem to bother them. I wish I could be like that sometimes, but then I think about the chaos it would bring and nah, hard pass. I’m good with my organized life, thanks. It’s predictable, and I prefer predictable. That popcorn I found? Perfectly predictable. Popcorn isn't like people. Popcorn is always good.

Okay so I just had the best slice of pizza ever and I can’t stop thinking about it. It was a pepperoni but like the pepperonis were those thick cup ones that get crispy around the edges and hold little pools of grease and I know that sounds gross but it was so good. I think food just makes everything better. Like how they say “food is love” or whatever. I really love sharing food too, it makes me feel really connected to people. I mean, it's more than just eating, it’s the whole experience—going to the place, picking it out together, laughing, that whole deal. I can't imagine being one of those people who just eats alone all the time. Speaking of shared experiences, I should definitely call my friends this weekend. We always have a blast when we get together. Last time we played that trivia game, who knew I could remember so much random junk about celebrities’ kids? I mean, I can be pretty competitive sometimes, but in a fun way, you know? Like, we all just want to win but we’re also there to have fun. I wonder if we should try something new this time, like a board game or something? Or maybe karaoke? I might have to be careful with that, though, because my singing is not exactly amazing. But it would be hilarious, and we would laugh, and that’s what matters, right? Okay, so I also need to get on planning for the group project due next week. I can’t remember what the topic is exactly. Something about social media, I think? Honestly, I’m not super interested in it but I know how to make it work. Teamwork is key! I generally like getting stuff done ahead of time, especially when it involves other people. I don’t want anyone feeling stressed or overwhelmed, it’s all about keeping it smooth. I guess that’s part of being conscientious—it drives me to help keep everything organized. Last semester we did a group project on environmental issues and I took charge of the whole thing because I really wanted to get a good grade. I made a timeline, assigned tasks, kept communication open. Everyone appreciated it, and we ended up getting an A. It felt really rewarding. I like being in a position where I can help others navigate through things. I mean, not in a control freak way, but more like being the glue, you know? I think that's why I get along well with everyone—people tend to gravitate towards me. But then there are days when I think about all the decisions I made in college. I never went the party route too much. I mean, I’ve been to some parties, sure, but they always seemed so chaotic. Everyone getting drunk and shouting over each other, I mean, what’s the point? And I like being able to have meaningful conversations. Like last week, I had coffee with a couple of my classmates and we just talked about the future, what our plans are, and how we want to make an impact. It was so refreshing. I’m definitely not that open to just anything, so I guess that’s why I tend to gravitate toward familiar routines and habits. I find comfort in it. I wonder if that’s a good thing or bad thing. Should I shake it up more? I don’t know if I want to go skydiving or travel to some super far-off place by myself. I honestly like the idea of being homey more than anything. I mean, don't get me wrong, I appreciate a good adventure, but I love my routine. Maybe it’s boring to some. Maybe I should try exploring new hobbies? I’ve thought about joining a painting class or something, but I can’t really see myself doing that. Maybe I’ve been too rigid in my thinking. Wait, oh! I just remembered my parents are coming to visit next month. I’m excited! I’ve got to take them to my favorite diner. They love breakfast food as much as I do. I swear we could survive on just pancakes. I hope Mom’s not trying to get me to try something new again like that time she made me eat sushi. Ugh. Bad memories. Not that I won’t eat sushi ever again, but it’s more like “let’s stick to the classics.” I just love how family gatherings bring everyone together, with laughter and stories, sharing old memories. I feel like I have a lot of good things ahead. I want to keep building relationships and just keep pushing forward with school and life. I value the people around me and just want to create the best experiences possible. I guess that’s what life’s all about anyway, making connections and being there for each other. Alright, this pizza thought is still hanging around in my mind, I think I might just grab another slice.

Okay so I’m thinking about last weekend and how I went to that party even though I really only wanted to stay home and binge-watch some show I’ve been into lately but my friends convinced me to go and I’m glad they did because it turned out to be super fun. I just love being around people, you know? Like, the energy is different when there are a lot of people around, I can’t help but feel a rush. But then sometimes, I get this anxious feeling in my stomach, like what if no one talks to me? What if I say something stupid? But at the same time, I can't resist the excitement of seeing everyone. I wore that bright red top I bought last month, which I thought was fabulous but also a little daring, but who cares, right? I mean, I want to stand out, but not too much, because then I might attract too much attention and that can be overwhelming. The music was so loud, and I could hardly think over it, but I loved dancing like an idiot. Nobody judged, or maybe they did and I just didn’t care at that moment. So freeing. But then that whole thing with Alex happened. He spilled a drink on my shoe! I mean, come on. I know it was an accident, but still. I get really worked up over small things like that. It didn't help that I was wearing my favorite shoes, which now have this weird sticky residue on them. I started laughing it off because that’s what you’re supposed to do in these situations, right? Just laugh it off? But inside I felt that familiar tightening in my chest, how can you be so careless, I thought? Maybe I overreacted, but I can’t help it. I feel everything so intensely and sometimes it’s really exhausting. Then later in the night, I ended up sitting on the couch talking with Melissa about our classes. She’s so easy to chat with. I always think how much I admire how she seems to be so comfortable in her own skin. We started talking about how weird it is that nobody ever really feels like they know what they’re doing, not even the professors. Like, how do they even decide what to teach us? It’s all just guessing, really. I could never be a professor, there’s too much pressure. I don’t have the conscientiousness for that. I can barely keep up with my own assignments. I’ve been late on a couple of things recently because I just lose track of time. I guess I just get so wrapped up in the moment or in trying to be social that I forget to turn in my homework. I mean, who cares about grades anyway? But then again, I do care because I want to please my parents. They expect so much from me. I’m really close with my family, even if I do feel a bit suffocated sometimes. My mom just wants me to succeed, and I get that. She worked so hard to get us where we are. It’s just that sometimes I feel like I can’t breathe under the pressure of it all. Like, I want to be out doing fun things but also I need to show up for them too. The balancing act is exhausting. And I wonder if everyone else feels like this? Or if I’m just a mess? It’s just a lot sometimes. And then there are those moments of pure joy, like when I got that positive feedback from my group project. It felt so great! Everyone said I’m such a good team player, and I love that. But then again, I start to worry, am I just being agreeable? Do I even have my own opinions? I feel like I just want to make everyone happy but at what cost? I keep folding into what others want and it sometimes feels like I’m losing my own identity along the way. I think about it too much, and it spirals. Also, I’ve been eating way too many snacks lately. Chips, cookies, you name it. The guilt creeps in, but then I eat more because it’s comforting, right? It's a cycle. I think I need to get a grip, but I don’t really want to go to the gym either. I hate working out! I just want to live my life, enjoy the fun times, the food, the conversations. But then there’s always that voice in the back of my head reminding me of what I should be doing. Ugh, I don’t know how to stop overthinking everything. It’s just who I am, I suppose. I want to explore but I also feel stuck in my own head sometimes. There’s so much I want to do but it feels overwhelming. I need to remember that it’s okay to just be. To embrace the chaotic mess that is my life and to enjoy the laughter with friends, even when it’s messy.

Okay so there’s this plant in my room that's been sitting there for ages and I don't even know what it is. It's one of those things you buy on a whim at the farmer’s market because it looks cool but then you forget the name. I think it's like a pothos or something, or maybe it’s a philodendron? Both of those names sound right but who knows. You just water it whenever you remember, and it seems fine, I mean it’s not dead or anything. Plants are weird, you water them, they grow, but you never really know what's going on under the soil. I wonder if it feels lonely being a plant. Do they get lonely? I bet they do, they probably want to chat with other plants but they can't. Poor things. I'm more of a quiet person myself, I like being alone sometimes. But then again, I like the chatter of the wind and the rustling leaves, not that they talk to you back, but it’s nice to feel like you're surrounded by something living. And speaking of living things, I went for a walk yesterday. I felt like I was in a David Attenborough documentary, just observing everything. There were these squirrels chasing each other, doing their little epic battles for dominance or whatever it is that they do. They’re so frenetic—there’s a part of me that wishes I could tap into that energy but then I remember how much I enjoy just meandering quietly, no rush, just taking in the sights and sounds, the way that the sunlight catches the leaves, how it filters through the trees. I don’t need to be sprinting everywhere. But then again, there’s that group project due soon, and I kind of forgot to check in with my teammates. It’s funny how we get paired together, like it’s some arbitrary lottery. I’m not the best at coordinating, but I always end up being the one who says, “Let’s just go with the flow.” Which is probably why I don’t take charge, like I’m low on that conscientiousness scale. I just want everyone to be happy, you know? Even if it means sacrificing some structure. It’s not a bad thing to be agreeable, or is it? Sometimes I think I should be more assertive, take a stand on my ideas. I just want to vibe but it’s hard to vibe when nobody’s on the same wavelength. There's this coffee shop near campus that has the best pastries, like flaky croissants that make my heart sing. The smell of coffee wafting through the air is magical too. I could sit there all day, just soaking it in, doodling in my notebook, not thinking much about what anyone else thinks. But then sometimes I do look around and see everyone else being so... intense? It’s like, why are we all stressed out? Life is short, man. There are bigger things to worry about, and here we are, stressing over assignments. I wonder if anyone else feels this way. Today, I suddenly thought about how I used to love drawing when I was younger. I can’t seem to find the time anymore but I still get random urges to sketch things. Maybe a doodle here and there? I’d like to illustrate the squirrels from yesterday, but I’d probably just doodle them in a surreal way, giving them glasses and talking in a tea party setting. That sounds fun. Maybe I could do a comic or something, just a slice of life with funky animals being their goofy selves. Where should I even start? I remember my art teacher in high school telling me that all it takes is a pencil and some paper. Easy enough, but then I think, what if it doesn't come out how I want? But maybe that’s okay. I mean, who cares? It’s just for me, right? Creatively, I feel like I could go anywhere. I want to explore different mediums, like watercolor or even digital art, but it seems overwhelming. Why do I keep putting limits on what I can do? Food is another thing that occupies my mind. I love trying new dishes, especially cuisines from different cultures. There’s something so exciting about experimenting in the kitchen. Like, I tried making homemade sushi the other day. What a mess! Rice everywhere, but it was hilarious. If only I had a camera to capture my flailing hands trying to roll rice and seaweed. But then I think about how cooking is a lot like art too. Just mixing flavors, balancing tastes, and sometimes it turns out weird, but that’s part of the fun. I’m rambling, aren’t I? But that’s okay. It’s nice to let thoughts tumble out without any editing. Like writing in a diary, but I never really keep one. Just let it flow, like water. Everything just flows together. Life is just this beautiful, chaotic mixture of moments. Just gotta ride the wave, right?

Okay so I just remembered that time I went to that concert with my friends, it was such a blast. The energy was insane, I love being around people who are just as excited as I am. Like, the whole crowd was jumping, singing along, it was the perfect kind of chaos. The vibe was electric and you could feel the bass in your chest, the kind of thing that makes you forget about everything else. I really miss those moments, just losing myself in the music and the people. I wonder what it’s like for the bands up there, seeing thousands of faces and knowing they’re the reason everyone is having the time of their lives. I mean, I can’t even imagine that level of connection, like you’re totally in sync with everyone around you. Speaking of connections, I met this really interesting guy in line for tacos afterward. He had this wild hair and really cool tattoos, we were talking about everything from music to travel to our favorite TV shows. I love when conversations just flow like that, it’s always refreshing to meet someone new who shares similar interests but has different perspectives. People are so fascinating! It’s like every new person has a whole world inside them, just waiting to be explored. Sometimes I wonder if I should be taking notes on everything we talked about, but then again, it’s way more fun to just live in the moment and experience it all. Oh, and what’s with everyone being so serious all the time? I mean, life is meant to be enjoyed! The other day, my roommate was stressing out about finals, and I tried to lighten the mood by suggesting we have a dance party in our living room. She didn’t even want to at first but then, when the music started playing, she couldn’t help but laugh. I feel like sometimes you just need to put aside the stress and do something ridiculous, like dance like no one’s watching, even if they kind of are. I guess I just really believe in the power of joy and spontaneity. And then there are those late-night talks we have, where everything flows freely and the world feels so big and so small at the same time. We end up talking about life, dreams, fears, and all these deep topics that make you feel alive. I love those moments when it’s just me and my friends, sharing and laughing and forgetting about everything else. Why should we ever take ourselves too seriously? Life is short, let’s make the most of it! I can’t help but want to experience everything life has to offer. Ugh, I really hate being confined to schedules though. I mean, why do we have to stick to such rigid timetables? Like classes, 9 AM to 5 PM. Why not mix it up? Why not have a class in a park or a field trip to a museum with snow cones afterward? I think if we could just be a little more flexible with plans, everyone would be happier. I wonder if more schools will do that in the future. I’d love to lead a class like that, just wandering around and soaking in all the cool stuff. I can’t believe I just said “wandering around” like it’s a legit learning experience. But honestly, it is! The world has so much to teach us if we just pay attention. I’d much rather be out there than cooped up in a classroom, staring at some PowerPoint. I think I’d like to travel more though, explore new cultures and cuisines. I’ve been wanting to backpack through Europe or maybe even South America. Everyone says it’s life-changing, experiencing how others live. And I just crave that kind of adventure! I got super inspired from my friend’s travel blog last week. She’s visiting a new country every month, literally. I mean, what even? I’m so envious! I really should take the plunge and start planning something, but what if it just flops? Ugh, who cares! I mean, life’s too short for “what ifs.” Isn’t that the advice everyone gives? It’s funny how sometimes when I’m feeling a little anxious about something, I just need to talk it out with someone. It makes all the difference. I guess that’s what I’m good at—being there for people, listening, bouncing ideas around. I just love being social, meeting new folks, hearing their stories. I often find that most people are just waiting for someone to ask how they’re doing. Like, really ask. I want to be that person who makes someone’s day better by simply being there, you know? That’s what it’s all about, forming those connections and creating moments worth remembering. Ah! This is exciting! There’s just so much to think about and so much to do. I can’t wait to get out there again!

I can't believe I forgot to bring my notebook to class today. It’s just such a hassle when I’m thrown off like that, like I can’t even think straight. I should really just keep a backup somewhere but then what if I have too many notebooks and they all make me feel overwhelmed? I tend to overthink that sort of thing. Like, is it better to have one good notebook or a bunch of random ones? I think it’s the good one but what defines good? The cover? The pages? It’s not like I’ll write something amazing just because I have a fancy notebook. It’s just paper in the end. Then again, there’s a weight to writing in nice paper, but I always fear I’ll waste it. Speaking of wasting things, my roommate has this plant that’s dying and I feel bad but it’s just sitting there, like a silent judgment on my being. Must I intervene? What if it’s too late? I read somewhere that talking to plants helps them grow. Is that true? The logic is kind of sound, right? Plants responding to positive energy? I might try it but what if people walk by and see me talking to a dying plant, that’s just... odd. I wonder if it would be better if I did it under my breath or if anyone would care. I mean, do people really notice those things? Maybe not. I don’t notice when other people do strange things. When I go out, I feel like I’m always the one standing next to the wall, like the wall is my comfort zone. There’s a whole world of social interactions happening out there, but I just don’t know how to jump in without feeling like I’m intruding. What if I ruin the vibe? Or worse... what if they don’t like me? It’s just easier to stay back and observe. I see other people laughing and chatting and I wonder if they’re just faking it, but then again, I have to remind myself they probably aren’t. They probably feel like they belong. I kind of want to talk to them but what if they reject me? That thought kind of strings me along like a yo-yo. And then there's that conversation I overheard last week. I’m still thinking about it. It was trivial, really—just some students discussing their weekend plans. But how did they sound so easy-going and carefree? I wish I could just tumble into conversation like that. Instead, I hold onto words like they're precious jewels, trying to find the right moment to present them, but by the time I do, the moment has passed. I always worry about sounding stupid, or worse, boring. So I choose silence. Oh, and that thing with my professor grading last week’s paper. I thought I nailed it, but I got a lower grade than I expected. I mean, I spent so much time researching! I triple-checked citations and then to see that, honestly, it felt like a slap. Did I misunderstand the prompt? I’ve been wondering if I just don’t have a grasp on what they want from me. Maybe I should have asked more questions during office hours. I always think about it afterwards, like what can I do differently next time? I need to remember to stop analyzing every little detail. It’s exhausting. Then there’s the never-ending inner dialogue. I suppose it’s part of being open, right? Always questioning, exploring ideas in my head that seem to multiply like those little bunny rabbits I read about. And oh, that time I felt so passionate about sustainability! I signed up for that club but have I made the most of it? I feel like I let my interest fade, like not doing enough is a form of being untruthful. What if I’m not living my values? Can values be lived half-heartedly? The other day, I started doodling the trees I saw while studying outside. They seemed so majestic. I wish I could draw; I think it would help release some of this tension. I can feel it building in my chest sometimes, this weight that makes me want to scream but I can’t. I mean, who can I scream to? No one wants to hear that. I’d just make it awkward, ruin a good mood. I don’t want to be a burden, you know? It’s like, people are busy with their own lives and I get that. I don’t want to add to their worries because I feel like I’m struggling. So I keep it to myself. Keep my feelings bottled up. But that makes me feel lonely, doesn’t it? And it’s a cycle. I want to reach out but feel unworthy. What a paradox, honestly. Sometimes I think about long-term goals, like if I should travel more or save to settle down somewhere—or is that just pressure? Everyone seems to be in a rush to get somewhere. I guess I should figure out my path, but everything feels so unclear. A million choices. I just want to be happy but how do you define that anyway? So many questions, so little clarity.

I can’t help but think about how exhausting social events are. Like the last party I went to where I stood in the corner sipping my drink, barely speaking to anyone. Everyone was laughing, it felt like there was this energy bouncing around that I just couldn’t tap into. I kept thinking about how I could’ve stayed in my room, curled up with a book or a documentary. Why is the pressure to socialize so high? It’s almost like people forget not everyone thrives in those chaotic settings. The noise, the small talk, the forced conversations about the weather or whatever. I mean, come on, it’s always the same stuff. Why can’t we dive into the real issues? Climate change, politics, the absurdity of TikTok trends – anything deeper than fake laughter. I swear, if I have to go through another “how’s school going?” question, I might lose it. School’s just this endless cycle of stress, and I feel like I’m on a treadmill that keeps speeding up. Sometimes I wonder if it’s even worth it. I read somewhere that college is supposed to be the best years of your life, but what if they’re not? What if they’re just an illusion that we’re all chasing? I mean, I’m trying to figure out what I want to do with my life, and sometimes it feels like everyone around me has it all figured out. I don’t even know what my major is supposed to be yet. One minute I’m fascinated by psychology, the next minute I’m losing interest in it completely. It’s like a rollercoaster of indecision, a never-ending loop of “What if?” I love learning though, that’s the thing. I’m always diving deep into topics online, exploring the weirdest corners of the internet. Some days I’ll watch documentaries about cults, and the next day it’s the philosophy of existentialism. I think that’s part of my problem too. I get so wrapped up in all this information that I can’t seem to focus on any one thing long enough to turn it into something practical. And then I feel guilty about it, because shouldn’t I be more productive? But then I think about the world outside my window, how things are always changing and moving, and how that terrifies me. I like to think about art and ideas without worrying about deadlines or grades or how it all fits into some larger picture. Speaking of art, I have all these projects I want to work on. I buy supplies, tons of paints and sketchbooks, and then I never actually start. The blank page starts to feel like a weight pressing down on my chest. I hate that feeling of disappointment when I can’t bring my ideas to life. I try to remind myself that I can create without any expectations, but there’s always this nagging voice in my head saying, “You should be doing better.” If I could just silence that voice for once, maybe I could find the freedom to experiment without the pressure. But then there’s that whole thing about connection. I crave it and I resist it at the same time. There’s this part of me that wants someone to understand my thoughts, to sit with me in silence and just be. I wish I could just tell people what I really think without worrying about how they’ll react. I throw out sarcasm to deflect, but it’s not always fun when no one gets the joke. Sometimes I wonder if my lack of agreeableness is what keeps me from forming deeper bonds. Or maybe it’s the neuroticism that has me second-guessing everything. Social anxiety, I think they call it. I start to sweat at the thought of trying to make small talk with someone I don’t know. And then there are days when I’m just so tired of overthinking everything. I’ll be lying in bed, staring at the ceiling, and it’s like my mind’s running a marathon. Why can’t I just relax? Why does my heart race when I think of texting that one person I met once? There’s a disconnect between my thoughts and my actions, and it frustrates me. I want to be spontaneous, to just live in the moment without feeling the weight of the world on my shoulders. I think about traveling, going somewhere completely foreign, but then I wonder if I could handle being in a different place with different people, different customs. It’s scary but exhilarating. Sometimes I think I’d rather sit with my thoughts than engage with the chaos of the outside world. But maybe that’s just my way of hiding. Is searching for meaning in art or philosophy a form of escapism? Who decides what’s real and what’s not anyway? I wish I could figure out how to balance my love of ideas with the often suffocating reality of life. And who knows, maybe someday I’ll break free from these chains of anxiety and self-doubt.

okay so I just remembered that I forgot to send that email to my professor about my group project like I really need to get that done but also have to study for my midterms that are coming in like a week and that’s stressing me out like my mind is racing but I can’t focus on one thing it’s like I’m juggling a hundred tasks in my head and just dropping them all like I hate this feeling. sometimes I think I’m too involved in everything like I volunteer for everything just to keep busy and make sure everyone is happy but then I don’t have time for myself and I end up feeling overwhelmed because I can't say no to people it’s like I don't want to let them down even if I’m drowning in my to-do list. I remember last semester when I had that panic attack before finals because everyone was counting on me to help study and I wanted to be there for them but I ended up cramming and then feeling like I was about to explode and honestly why do we do this to ourselves? I just want to please everyone but it’s exhausting. I’m always worried that if I mess up one little thing it’ll make things worse for everyone else and I can’t stand that feeling. I guess that’s where the neuroticism comes in like what if I forget to print the slides for the presentation and everyone is going to look at me and I’ll feel so embarrassed. Also, yesterday was such a whirlwind, I had class all day and then I had to rush to that meeting for the club I joined which is great because I love meeting new people but also it’s another thing on my plate. I feel like my social calendar is packed to the brim with events, meetings, and study sessions and I can’t keep up. It’s Saturday but I’m already thinking about the week ahead and it stresses me out. I want to enjoy my weekends but I find myself planning for the next week, scheduling everything down to the minute. Talking about plans, I’m supposed to go to that party tonight but part of me is wondering if I should just skip it and spend some quiet time at home and catch up on reading or like organizing my notes because they’re all scattered around my room in piles and it drives me crazy but I know if I don’t go then I’ll feel bad for not socializing and what if everyone else has fun and I end up missing out? It’s such a dilemma like why does it have to be so hard to choose? I wish I could just be okay with being alone for a bit but then the other part of my brain is like but you’ll miss out and everyone will talk about it tomorrow and you’ll feel left out and it’ll hit you and then you’ll spiral and overthink everything. And I do tend to overthink everything like I could be having a perfectly good time but then I start analyzing everyone’s reactions and questioning if I said something wrong or if they’re really having fun or if they’re just being polite and pretending to enjoy my presence. Ugh, it’s so annoying because I know I shouldn’t care so much but it’s like I can’t help it. Oh, and there’s a huge difference between my perception and reality It can’t be that bad when people say they like my company but I start to doubt myself every time I see someone laugh and I can’t tell if it’s with me or at me. I am always the one trying to lighten the mood or make a joke but then I trip over my words or accidentally say something awkward and I’m like great now they think I’m an idiot. I guess that’s why I make an effort to be nice and polite and agree with everyone because I just want to keep the peace. I don’t like confrontation at all. I would rather agree with someone to avoid a fight than actually express my opinion. Sometimes I think I should stand my ground more often but what if it causes issues? I see friends get into heated debates about trivial things and I just sit there smiling, nodding like yeah that’s totally fine, but in my head, I’m screaming to just move on. And I know it sounds weird but I find myself cleaning up after others like they leave their cups on my table and I just can’t relax until everything is organized and neat. Sometimes I wish I could just chill and go with the flow but it feels unnatural to me like I need a certain level of control. My planner is my lifeline, and everything has a place and a time, that’s the only way I can function. I guess it helps me manage the neuroticism to have everything so structured but man, it’s exhausting to think about all the time. Anyway, I hope I can get through this week without any major breakdowns or embarrassing moments. Just keep smiling and nodding. It’ll be okay, right?

I can't believe I actually have a midterm tomorrow. Why do I do this to myself? Procrastination, I guess. I could have studied last week, but I was out with Jenna and Chris, and we ended up at that new taco place. They have these crazy spicy tacos that I thought I could handle but wow, my mouth was on fire. It was so ridiculous. And then we started talking about that party last month, the one where Luke spilled beer all over Emily’s shoes. Classic Luke. I love parties; everyone’s so alive, just bouncing off the walls. But then sometimes I can't help but notice how chaotic they get, and everyone's laughing, having fun, and I feel like half of me is riding that wave and the other half is like "what are you doing?" like a little nagging voice in the back of my head that won’t shut up. Seriously, I know I need to calm down sometimes, but what’s the point of life if you’re not out living it, right? The energy is infectious. And oh, the cute guy from lit class was at the party, and I swear I caught him looking at me a few times. I don't know if it's my vivid imagination or what, but every time I think about it, my stomach flips. It’s like I’m buzzing with possibilities, but then I start overthinking everything. What if I misread those looks? What if he just doesn’t like me at all? Does he even know my name? I mean, I'm sure he does. I can't even remember when I met him, but I think I was talking about Hemingway or something because I tend to just go on and on when I get excited about literature. Sometimes I annoy myself with it. Okay, focus, midterm. Why do I keep circling back to it? I’ve got a ton of notes scattered everywhere. I know, I know, I should just sit down and go through them, but I keep getting distracted. I might as well just start throwing everything in the air and see what lands in my brain. Why can't I just be one of those chill people who read their books and be done? No, my mind is like a super highway, racing through thoughts and ideas. I can't help it! Each new thought is like a little firework promising something exciting. Like that time I went skydiving last summer. Adrenaline rush, pure excitement, I felt so alive! But then my heart was racing out of panic too, like—is the parachute going to open? I don’t trust gravity that much. And food, oh man. Food is such a big deal for me. I always have to be thinking about what’s next. Eating out or cooking, I love it. The flavors, the textures—it’s like an experience. I can whip up a mean spaghetti, just ask anyone who’s been to my apartment. But then again, I had that weird microwave pizza the other day, that wasn’t a high point. I thought I was hungry enough, but then I took a bite and, ew, never again! Makes me think of all the bad decisions we make in the name of cravings. I could probably spend hours talking to people about food, and what makes it even better is that I can see them light up when they talk about their favorite meals. I feel charged up just hearing about it. And then other people, they don’t get the same thrill, and it's just like, how can you live your life without diving into all this wonderful stuff? It's mind-boggling! But, wow, I get so frustrated when people don’t share my enthusiasm. And what’s the deal with people who can sit quietly and just... wait? Like they’re not anxious at all about the silence? I'm not one of those people. Silence for me is like an empty void where my thoughts spiral into a cyclone. I need to fill space with sound, chatter, anything. Is that why I keep jumping from topic to topic? It’s exhausting, and yet invigorating. I would never want to change it, though. I want to go beyond every limit, you know? Open up new horizons. Speaking of horizons, I’ve been thinking about traveling. Europe has been on my mind a lot. The sights, the art, the food—imagine drinking espresso while strolling through cobbled streets in Paris! I wonder if I’ll ever actually go. I need to save up money first. But how boring is saving? I barely have the patience to stick to a budget. I know I should because that’s what responsible adults do, but then what’s so wrong with living a little in the moment? I guess that’s the tug-of-war in my head. I keep wanting to dive into everything while simultaneously trying to be practical. When did life become this complicated? Maybe I need a magic wand to just make everything better. That would be nice. Or at least make studying less of a chore. Okay, back to the midterm. Focus. Time's running out. Why is this laptop so slow? Why is everything so slow? Ugh. I need to get my notes back out. Enough distractions!

Three o’clock. Why does time seem to drag here? I’m staring at my phone, scrolling through social media, but it’s the same stuff over and over. There’s a cute cat video. Oh look, another influencer posting about self-care. What even is self-care, really? Is it just buying products? I hate that I see those ads everywhere. They know me too well. I can’t even escape it when I’m trying to escape it. The irony of scrolling to find a break and then ending up more stressed. I mean, why are people so obsessed with these perfect curated lives? That's not real. I can’t get into that. Everyone’s always so happy and free-spirited. What is that even? I remember last week at that party. I tried to blend in, but it felt like I was just floating in the corner. People laughing, music blaring, and I’m there, in my head, analyzing every face, every interaction. Do they even notice me? Probably not. A blur, right? So I stood by the snacks. I could’ve left, but I didn’t. No, there were chips. Those spicy ones. Why do I always end up in these situations where I’m just a bystander? It’s exhausting, trying to make conversation. What do you say? “Hi, I exist”? Or “What do you think about the weather”? Generic small talk is the worst. My brain is a mess of thoughts, but I can’t seem to articulate anything coherent. I wonder if I’m broken somehow. What makes someone like me not fit in? The social cues are like a foreign language. And the silence that follows when I don’t respond quickly enough? Terrifying. It’s not that I don’t want to talk, but the pressure is suffocating. I’d rather escape into my head than deal with that. But sometimes, I want to scream at the world to notice the beautiful chaos inside me. So many ideas swirling around, but I can’t get them out. Can’t get them out. Writing feels easier. I can just let the words flow. I had this idea for a project. Something about how we all pretend to be okay, yet deep down we’re struggling. Everyone hides behind their smiles, posting sunsets on Instagram when they’re crumbling inside. I want to capture that realness – the struggle. But how? I’m not sure I’m the right person to do it. Can I even make something meaningful? Where do I even start? There’s so much pressure, and yet I want to do it so badly that it makes me anxious. It’s a beautiful contradiction, isn’t it? It’s frustrating. Do I start with poetry? Maybe something raw and emotional, but who am I to share that? Are people even open to hearing it? A thought hits me. Why am I so reluctant to push myself? Maybe it’s the fear of failure. I can’t stand the thought of putting something out there and having it ignored. What if nobody cares? But then again, if nobody cares, why should I be reserved? It’s exhausting carrying that burden of expectation around. I can feel my heart racing just thinking about it. Oh, and that guy in my class. He’s so confident. He just raises his hand and speaks up like it’s nothing. Meanwhile, I can hardly keep my thoughts together long enough to form a question. I want to ask him how he does it, but the thought of engaging makes my stomach twist. Why is he so at ease? Is there a secret I’m missing? I’m stuck here, feeling insecure. It’s infuriating. Part of me wants to disconnect entirely, but I can’t help but crave connection. What a mess! I’m scrolling again. Another post about travel, adventures across Europe. How do people have the guts to just go? I want to see the world, but there’s too much uncertainty. What if I get lost? What if I can’t find a place to stay? What if it all goes wrong? But the thought of sitting still is maddening. I want to break free. I want to explore those hidden paths, the ones that lead to secret gardens, away from all this noise. Yet here I am, stuck in my own anxiety, my own mind. Dinner tonight is going to be leftover pizza. I should probably eat something healthier but whatever. I’ll make a salad one day. But really, sometimes I can't deal with that kind of responsibility. Who am I kidding? I just want to zone out. Binge-watch another show that sparks my imagination, lose myself in someone else's story for a while. It’s comforting, but I know I should be doing more. Yet when the moment comes, I freeze. Why is life so complicated? Just heavy and thick with thoughts. How do people make it look easy?

I can’t believe I ate that whole bag of chips last night, I mean seriously who does that but they were just so good and spicy like I can still taste the flavor explosion in my mouth and how crispy they were, okay what was I saying? Oh right, I have that study coming up and I still need to prepare. Am I really prepared? I love being around people but I get so anxious thinking about how I come across, what if I say something weird during the study? But also I want everyone to like me, like what if they think that I’m too much or too loud? But I just can’t help it, I love to talk and share ideas and energy but then sometimes I feel like I overshare or like I dominate conversations and I’m not even sure if I’m doing it on purpose, it just happens. And I want those people in the study to see the real me because being fake is exhausting but is this really the real me or just a version of me that I show when I’m nervous? Okay, focus, focus on the study, what are the questions again? I need to remember to breathe. Oh, did I say hi to everyone in my group this morning? I hope I didn’t forget anyone, it’s so important to me that everyone feels included and valued. I love how we all just gel together, it’s kind of like this beautiful chaotic friendship that I never knew I needed, and it’s so comforting but also sometimes I just want to explode with energy and go out and dance or something spontaneous but everyone always looks so busy like they have their own lives to handle and what if they don't want to hang out with me? But that’s not true, right? Everybody wants to be liked and to have good friends and it’s so hard to think about rejection even if I know that most people are kind and lovely and I’m just overthinking it as usual because my brain never turns off and, like, how come nobody talks about how exhausting it is being so friendly all the time? Gosh, I just want to be understood. I remember this one time in high school when I tried out for the school play just because my friend wanted to and I ended up getting the lead role, but I was so nervous and I still remember the butterflies in my stomach when I went on stage. But it was exhilarating, and the applause felt like a warm hug, like all those people were cheering for me, but also I was so scared of forgetting my lines or looking silly. I just wanted everyone to enjoy it and maybe to think I was cool, how silly is that? But I think that’s just how I’m wired, wanting everyone to be happy and wanting to shine a light on everyone else’s happiness too. I love that feeling of spontaneity, like jumping into a road trip on a whim. The wind in my hair, loud music blaring, laughing with friends. Oh but then I also worry about planning everything out like what if we don’t have enough snacks or stops planned? I have this like internal checklist running all the time, I want things to be perfect. The last trip we took, I made a whole spreadsheet, color-coded and everything, and they found it hilarious but also I think they appreciated having the caffeine and cake stops mapped out and wow did we have fun, except maybe that one time when someone got lost but it was an adventure, right? Now I’m thinking about all the food I want to eat in my life. I’ve always been curious about trying new things, like different cuisines and flavors and wow, I could write a novel about my obsession with street food, especially those little carts you find in cities with the best spicy tacos or dumplings. I just want to travel and eat everything, meet local people, hear their stories, and just soak everything in. Is that too much to ask? But it seems so beautiful to embrace life like that, tasting culture, and I feel like my heart is bursting with excitement just thinking about it but then I also feel this tight knot of panic like will I ever get to do all these things? I need to figure out my future! Sometimes I wish I could just hit pause and enjoy right now instead of getting lost in all those thoughts about tomorrow. But then again, what if the tomorrow is amazing? What if I keep meeting interesting people and learning and growing? Is it possible to just live fully, embrace all these feelings, and still be kind and loving and thoughtful? I want to be that person who connects with others but also does good in the world, and sometimes I just want to scream because there’s this overflow of emotion that comes with being me. I want to help and be present but also be free. It’s this constant balancing act between wanting to fly high above the clouds and wanting to stay grounded with everyone I care about.

Okay so I need to focus. I really want to get this done but I keep thinking about all the other things I have to do. There's that paper for psychology due next week and I should start it but I don’t want to start it too early because what if I change my mind about my thesis. I mean it’s not like I’m good at brainstorming anyway, so the sooner I start, the more time I might waste rewriting it. But then again, the professor does grade us on how early we turn things in. It’s so annoying how much pressure there is to be on time with everything. I wonder if there’s a point where it's just too much and you just have to go for it, submit it, and let it be. I can’t imagine handing in a rough draft. But no, that’s not right. I can’t risk it. I have standards, not like some people I see slacking off. I don’t get it, how can you just not care? I mean, where’s the ambition? The desire to succeed? Then again, I have friends who keep saying, “You’re too uptight,” and I think, am I? Maybe a tiny bit, but isn’t that better than being all over the place? They’re usually late to everything, and I can’t stand it when we try to make plans. I always have to remind them about the time, not in a condescending way, but still, it feels like they just don’t take anything seriously. I want everyone to do well, you know? But they need to put in the effort too, otherwise what good is it? We’re all in this together but we’re also not. I like knowing that I can rely on myself. When I’m working on group projects, it really stresses me out because I can’t control what they do and it’s always the same. They leave their parts to the last minute and I end up picking up the slack. It’s exhausting. Why can’t we just be responsible? Sometimes I think I should just stay quiet about it. I mean, what good does it do to complain? It just creates tension, and I don’t want that. I don’t want to be that person who’s always whining about group dynamics or other people’s messes. But I can’t help it, that’s just how it is. I prefer working alone anyway. Less hassle, fewer discussions. Just focus and get it done. I mean, I know it sounds isolating or whatever, but honestly, I’m really okay with it. I like my own company. A random thought: what’s with people who constantly post on social media? I don’t get it. It’s like they need validation from strangers. I don't want to share my life like that. I don’t need likes or comments. It feels pointless, like shouting into a void. I like keeping my thoughts to myself. I don’t want anyone judging my every move. There's this pressure to be part of something, to connect, but I’m fine being a spectator. I think sometimes I’m supposed to feel lonely or something, but I just don’t. I like my routine. I wake up, I study, I go to the gym. Straightforward. No drama. I wonder why people would want to complicate that. Then I think about how I’m probably missing out on things. There are opportunities I could be taking if I just loosened up a bit. But I don’t want to force myself into social situations that make me uncomfortable. It seems pointless. I wonder if I’ll look back and regret not trying harder. But then again, what if I try and it’s just a disaster? Better to stick to what works. I have a calendar with everything planned out. It’s color-coded, even. Green for deadlines, blue for study sessions, red for meetings, and I feel like it keeps me grounded. I can visualize my week without feeling lost. I hate the idea of waking up and having no idea what I'm doing for the day. That feels chaotic. I think I’m okay with being a planner. Some might call it boring but it keeps me focused. I can’t handle unpredictability very well. And then there’s the weather. I hate when it’s unpredictable. I wish it would just stay consistent. Like, sunny or rainy but not both in the same day. I can deal with that. I dislike when it’s all over the place. I’m a firm believer in routines and predictability. What’s so fun about surprises anyway? I can’t stand the thought of being caught off guard. I like knowing what’s next. And as much as I might be overthinking things, I think it’s just part of being me. It’s not a problem, just how I operate. It’s methodical. Is that such a bad thing? I think it keeps me organized and on track, and I like it that way.

Okay so I was just thinking about how I can never seem to make a decision without asking like a million people what they think. Like, I can’t just pick a restaurant or a movie you know? I get this rush of anxiety when someone asks me where I wanna go. I want everyone to be happy so I’ll ask everyone first, but then that just leads to more confusion and everyone has a different opinion and it’s like I want to please everyone but also I want to just be spontaneous and say let’s just go for pizza or something but then I think about the vegan options and who likes what and it spirals. It’s exhausting. Like the other day my friends suggested going to that new taco place but I hadn’t heard great things. This one guy said they uses too many spices and it was bad for his stomach. But then I think, well, maybe he was just being picky, right? So I ask my other friends, and then someone else chimes in about that food truck downtown that serves the best curry fries and it suddenly becomes this whole debate. I mean, who am I to dictate the mood? It’s kind of funny because I can talk to anyone about anything but deciding where to eat makes me feel like I’m on trial. That’s where the neuroticism kicks in. It’s like I’m constantly worried about making the wrong choice or someone being upset with me. My mind races. I start imagining the worst-case scenarios, like if we choose the taco place and it really does suck, I’ll feel bad and I’ll replay it in my head for days. Why can’t I just be more laid-back? I get everyone’s concerned about their own tastes but can't we just go for something fun? I think about how I need to be more flexible, more “go with the flow.” Anyway, speaking of food, I just found out my best friend is allergic to peanuts and I feel like that changes everything. I mean we have always gone to Thai restaurants and now I feel like I can’t just suggest it anymore. I’m overthinking it again. It’s such a random thing to think about. It's like my mind is a tangled web of worries and experiences, always trying to connect different dots. Sometimes I wonder if it helps to care so much about what everyone thinks. I mean who wouldn’t want everyone to get along right? And then there are those moments where I just dive into something completely new. I signed up for that pottery class. Who knew I’d enjoy molding clay so much? I’m horrible at it, but it’s freeing, you know? Just me and some friends, and the instructor is this cool woman who just throws random ideas at us. I love being so open to different experiences. Plus, I met a guy who was super friendly. We started talking about our favorite 90s movies and suddenly we’re best friends. That’s the magic of being extroverted. I can connect with anyone, even if it’s silly. But then I think about how I forget deadlines. Like schoolwork slips my mind all the time because I’m too busy planning social events or thinking of new adventures. I remember last week, I completely forgot that big paper due. I had to stay up late cramming, which made me such a mess. I can’t believe I did that, but it feels like I’m constantly juggling plates, and while I’m great at being social, I just drop the ball on responsibilities. It’s frustrating how those personality traits can clash. I guess it’s like living in a constant state of excitement and then suddenly crashing when I realize I have obligations. I remember this one time I was so pumped about going to a concert, but I forgot to study for my exam. I was on cloud nine one minute, and then I flunked the test and was just...ugh. Really? Why can’t I balance my highs and lows better? I met this girl who’s super organized – she’s amazing. She writes down everything she has to do in her planner, and I’m just over here scribbling notes on napkins or my phone. It works until it doesn’t, you know? There’s a beauty in spontaneity and the chaos it brings, yet I think about how nice it would be to have a little more structure. But then again, I’d probably resist it. I’ve tried planners and they just sit unopened. So, in the end, I just roll with it. I’ll keep making friends and trying new things, albeit with a little anxiety in my pocket. I guess that’s just who I am – the adventurous social butterfly who flits around but sometimes forgets to land. So here's to embracing the chaos, as messy and nerve-wracking as it might be.

Okay so I just walked by a group of people who looked like they were having the time of their lives, laughing, throwing popcorn at each other, and it made me think about how being around others can be so energizing it's almost addictive like I get this rush when I’m with friends, especially when they’re people who just get me and I can be my wild, goofy self without any worries about judgment the way I can be on my own sometimes like yeah I might say something totally random but it’s usually received with laughter and that makes it all worthwhile. It’s like, how can I resist those flavors? Ugh, speaking of studying, I really should get back to that relentless pile of readings for psychology I promised myself I'd tackle but after talking to Jen earlier about our plans for the weekend, I honestly just want to dive into those adventures instead like we were brainstorming this road trip to the coast and I can already feel the ocean breeze and hear the waves crashing, the sun setting in all those pinks and oranges, and the music blasting because you can’t go on a trip without creating a killer playlist I mean that’s practically a law at this point right? But then again, there’s always that nagging voice in my head reminding me of deadlines and responsibilities but maybe I can just push that aside for a bit longer like what’s the harm in one more day of fun before the chaos of finals hits I’m sure I can squeeze it in because I like to connect with people and hear their stories that’s the best part and I want to soak up those moments because they’re fleeting and life is way too short and when else am I gonna have this kind of freedom? Every time I scroll through social media, I see someone else posting pictures of their travels or the cool things they’re doing, and it makes me realize how many experiences I want to pack into my life it’s exhilarating and scary at the same time, knowing that the whole world is out there waiting for me to step into it like I could try skydiving or backpack through Europe or learn how to dance salsa it’s just endless possibilities and I can’t help but get excited about it and I really should make a list of all the places I want to go because I love making lists, even though I never follow them to the letter because as much as I like to plan, I also crave spontaneity like just throwing caution to the wind and seeing where the night takes me that's where the best stories come from. I love meeting new people, too, especially in classes where everyone’s so different and brings their own perspectives to the table I can get lost in those discussions for hours it's just so engaging to hear someone else's view and I think that’s why group work doesn’t bother me as much as it does other people I don’t mind sharing the load or bouncing ideas around because collaboration can lead to something incredible, I believe that. Also, I can’t help but feel that there’s something beautiful about the messiness of life like things don’t always go according to plan and that’s okay it just means we’re human and it leads to growth and learning so I choose to embrace the chaos instead of shying away from it like how I’m constantly open to new experiences and trying random food or signing up for workshops that seem interesting even if I know I might suck at it I just want to soak it all in. I mean, who cares if I didn't do well in that art class? At least I tried and got to meet a bunch of creative souls who were just as passionate about their craft maybe even more than I was and I love that energy it’s contagious and it makes me feel alive I can’t imagine living any other way like why would anyone want to stay locked away in their comfort zone when there’s a whole world outside waiting to be explored? Okay so back to studying and figuring out how to fit all this fun in my schedule but at least I know I have the rest of my life to play and adventure so I might as well enjoy the ride while I can.

The crumpled piece of paper in my pocket reminds me of unfinished thoughts. I should really clean out my pockets more often. It's like a physical manifestation of my brain – all these ideas crammed in there but never fully developed. What was I even thinking about? Right, social plans. They sound exhausting. I can never seem to enjoy them. Other people just seem so effortlessly social, and here I am, like this anxious observer in the corner of the room, thinking about how to escape. I overthink everything. Like, do they really want me there? Or are they just being polite? I’ve read somewhere that small talk is an art, but it feels like torture most days. I can barely keep it going for five minutes without wanting to bolt. Then again, there are times when I'm hit with this wave of curiosity about everything—like why do we even have to socialize? It’s a question that lingers. I mean, can we just exist in our own bubbles, observing like scientists? The more I think about it, the more appealing it sounds. It’s bizarre how I crave depth and complexity but run away from simple interactions. Conversations about the weather get old fast. I want ideas, theories, brave new worlds—why aren’t more people focused on that? The universe is so vast, and we’re stuck in these tight little boxes of small talk. And then, this weird thought pops up – what if aliens are observing us and judging our social skills? How would we even explain our awkwardness and tension? They’d probably think, “why aren’t you exploring the depths of the ocean or going to Mars instead of talking about the latest Netflix show.” I mean, sure, Netflix is cool, but I want the big picture! I’d rather get lost in a book about quantum physics than to be asked about the latest trending meme. My mind races. Is my anxiety a blessing in disguise? I don’t know. It sometimes helps me to pick apart things, to dig into the messy layers beneath the surface of life. I find meaning in the chaos, I suppose? The thrill of learning something new ignites my curiosity, but then I get stuck. Everything feels like a double-edged sword. Anxiety keeps me awake at night, obsessing over things I said or didn’t say. Did I offend that stranger at the grocery store? Did I smile enough? What does it mean when I didn’t accept an invitation? It’s all so convoluted. Deep down I know I’m drawn to experiences, the unpredictable nature of life. I want to travel, see new cultures, try new foods, meet weird people. There’s this exhilarating rush just thinking about it, but I know I won’t go because… what if the plane crashes? Or what if they don’t serve food I like? It’s all so irrational but feels so real. Fear of the unknown feels like a brick wall sometimes. In class yesterday, they discussed how emotions can dictate our decision-making. I just sat there, nodding, thinking about how my mind is a labyrinth where panic reigns supreme. Every choice feels like a whirlpool that pulls me under if I’m not careful. How do people even make plans? I can barely wrap my head around my next meal, let alone future plans that require social engagement. And then there’s this battle I have with procrastination. I can sit down to study, but my mind wanders off down this rabbit hole of YouTube videos or random Wikipedia articles. I think studying is like that one step too far into social territory where I have to interact with the knowledge that’s expected from me. It’s overwhelming. I want to scream out into the void, “I want to learn, but I’m too scared to show my face among the others.” It’s easier to admire from afar, hanging out in the cyber corner of the internet where nobody can judge my awkwardness. I wish I could find that balance between craving knowledge and dodging the burden of social norms. Then again, who even defines what normal is? Is it the person who talks too much at parties, naming every small talk topic and keeping the energy up, or is it the quiet thinker in the back who can’t find anything to say? I’m not sure I really care much for labels, but being labeled as “the quiet one” stinks sometimes. I wish there was a handbook for living authentically or maybe a guide to help navigate the treacherous waters of social interaction. But then… you know what? Maybe it’s okay to not know what I’m doing. Maybe there’s freedom in confusion. Life is just one big question mark following another. I’ll stumble, I’ll fumble, and I’ll drink more coffee than I should, but at least I’m here, wondering, questioning, opening my mind to what’s out there, even if I’m terrified of stepping out of my comfort zone. And maybe—just maybe—that’s enough.

Okay so I can't stop thinking about how hectic my life feels right now. There's just always something. Like, I don't even know what I want to do sometimes. Is it the weekend yet? I should probably plan something but planning is such a drag. I like to just go out and see what happens, like a spontaneous adventure but then again it’s also exhausting because too many options stress me out. All those people. I love being around people. Love it—like I thrive on it but sometimes I can’t even deal with how loud it gets. Too much laughter can be overwhelming. And then there’s the small talk, God don’t get me started on small talk. I can only hear “How’s school?” so many times before I scream. Like—does anyone really care? I guess some do but it feels fake sometimes. I mean, sometimes I just want to say, “My life is a mess and I love it” but who wants to hear that? Speaking of mess, I really need to clean my room. It’s like a tornado hit it. Clothes everywhere, I can’t even find my favorite shirt. I think it’s under a pile of junk I’ve been meaning to sort out but sorting is too much for me. I wanna just shove it all into a corner and forget about it. I’d probably do that if my roommate didn’t have a panic attack every time she sees the mess. She’s the opposite of me—so tidy, so organized. I mean, I get it, life is tough enough without throwing clutter into the mix but I just don’t care enough about it to change. Cleaning up feels like such a waste of time. Who cares, really? My friends might if they see it but they know me well enough. I’m unpredictable but I can’t be too unpredictable around her I guess. She’ll judge, and judging makes me feel all weird inside. I guess I’m just a mess in general. But not the cute, quirky mess. More like the overwhelming type that just drains you. I’m craving pizza again. I always crave pizza. And how is it that I can devour a whole pizza by myself and still feel hungry? Pizza is like magic food. Maybe I should go out and get some. But then I have to put on pants and decide which place to go to. Are they gonna have the toppings I want? What if they run out? That stresses me out so bad. I wanna be impulsive but my brain flips out about every tiny thing and I end up just scrolling through my phone instead. TikTok is a black hole. I just watch these videos and lose track of time and then I don’t get anything done. I know I should be studying or working on that essay that’s due soon but it’s like my brain is rebelling against the idea of being productive. Why can’t I just get it together? I don’t think I’m ever going to get life figured out. Ever. Everyone’s so passionate about their majors and future careers and I’m just floating through it all. I mean, I can talk your ear off about my favorite band or how that random girl in class irritates me but when it comes to serious stuff, I don’t have the energy. I think I should care more, but I just don’t. Who cares about the future anyway? Those people who are all about five-year plans are just so annoying. How can they be so sure? Life’s too crazy to plan. I wish I could just binge-watch my favorite show for a week straight and forget about everything. But after a while, I’ll start feeling guilty and anxious for wasting time. I need to go grab that pizza soon. The thought of it is just lingering in my head. If I were in charge right now, I would definitely be throwing pizza parties every week. That would be my dream life. Everyone can just come, bring their favorite toppings, we eat pizza, laugh, take spontaneous pictures, and then I might start feeling overwhelmed by all the noise. I mean, it’s hard to stay chill when everyone is trying to talk over each other. Why can’t we just take turns? But then I wouldn’t be able to handle the silence of that either. Ugh, I’m a mess. I hate being stuck in a loop of thoughts, I feel like I’m just running in circles. There should be a way to turn it off, like a light switch. I wish I could just be in the moment and stop worrying. It feels like it’s almost time to get that pizza but it’s also almost time to panic about the essay I’ve yet to start. I guess I should probably figure out some way to balance the fun and the stress. But who am I kidding? Balancing is overrated.

The clock says 11:03 PM, kind of late to be writing but whatever. I just finished my third cup of chamomile tea—never could stand the taste of coffee, too bitter. Speaking of bitter, why do people have to argue all the time about politics? Why can’t we just talk about what we have in common? That would be way more fun. I remember that one time in class when we had to discuss our favorite books. It was so cool hearing everyone’s picks; I love how books can connect people. Maybe that’s why I’m still in the library. I always like finding quiet spots among the stacks, just me and the pages, no interruptions, no pressure to say anything or be anyone. Some people crave social interaction like it’s oxygen. I don’t get that. I mean, I’m okay with it but it’s like, not the priority. I could get lost in thoughts about the last book I read—this surreal science fiction one about alternate realities. I was fascinated by how the author crafted different lives for the same character. It makes me wonder about my own life, the choices I made, the paths not taken. What if I’d chosen a different major? What if I never adopted that stupid cat? Wait, I love my cat. She’s the best. So soft, her fur looks like a cloud. Every time I pet her, it’s like a little spark of joy. I should probably be doing something productive instead of this, like studying for that exam next week or catching up on assignments. But the idea of being super organized makes me feel a bit stifled. Is it bad that I forget deadlines sometimes? Probably. But I’m always amazed by art and creativity. Like the time I stumbled across this tiny gallery with the weirdest sculptures—surreal stuff made from recycled materials. That opened up a whole world of creativity for me—like what I can do with everyday junk. I often think about how much potential there is when we just let our imaginations run wild. That’s something I really value; personal expression. I’d love to try painting but I’m a bit scared it will suck, and then I’ll feel awkward about it. Okay, but why do people think painting has to be serious? Art should be fun! I saw a video of someone making a gigantic mural out of chalk. It was colorful and messy, and it felt liberating to watch. Just doodling on a wall that makes me feel alive. And then I think about how sometimes I feel kind of like a ghost in my own life, floating along. I’m so chill about things, but there’s this side that wants to scream at the world to notice me, to take action. But then I remember that I don’t really mind being low-key. High school was weird. Everyone was so obsessed with being popular. I was just there, hanging with my group, talking about our latest obsessions, which were usually books or old movies. I didn’t care about going to parties but my friends pressured me to join. I went once. It was loud and chaotic, people everywhere, and I felt like I was drowning. At the time, I thought that was just how it was supposed to be, like everyone was in a race to be seen. But then I realized it’s fine to step back. Sometimes I feel guilty for not being more involved, like when I scroll through social media and see people living their best lives—traveling and posting pictures of food I can’t even pronounce. There’s this nagging thought that maybe I should be out there, making memories, but then I remember all the amazing conversations I have in quieter settings. Those are the ones that really stick with me, the meaningful stuff, like shared laughter over inside jokes. I can’t shake the feeling that there’s just so much to explore. Traveling seems fascinating, but it’s also overwhelming. I wonder where I’d go if given a chance—like, would I backpack through Europe or soak up the sun on an island? Or maybe I’d prefer a quiet cottage in the mountains, writing or painting. The simplicity of nature, no noise, just serenity. It’s funny how I can picture it yet feel too shy to make it happen. I should get out more. Anyway, focusing on the little moments feels so much better than getting all worked up over grand plans. I find joy in small things—like the clock ticking softly in the background, my cat purring, and the faint smell of tea lingering in the air. It’s peaceful. I probably will regret this procrastination tomorrow, but right now, it feels just right to indulge in this quiet chaos of thoughts swirling in my mind. No rules, no structure—just me being me.

Wow, I just remembered that time at the party when everyone was doing karaoke and I hopped on stage to sing “Livin’ on a Prayer.” That was wild. The whole crowd was into it, and I just felt electric. I love those moments when everyone’s cheering and singing along. It’s the best feeling in the world. I mean, people are so fun when they’re all together. That energy is something I thrive on. But then, like, I don’t even remember half the songs I sang that night. Who cares though? It’s all about the vibe and the experience. Okay, so speaking of vibes, I went out for tacos with friends last night. We had this spontaneous idea to hit up that new place in town. Super crowded but whatever, that’s where the good food is right? I didn’t mind standing in line because the chatter and laughter around me was just so enjoyable. I think I could talk to anyone about anything and we’d end up laughing. Like that couple in front of us, I overheard them arguing playfully about who ordered the guac. That’s hilarious—who cares, it’s guac! You should always share guac anyway. The food came, and wow, those tacos were juicy. We ended up sitting outside, and it was chilly but I didn’t even care because we were just cracking jokes. I told them that I could totally do stand-up at this point. They laughed and dared me to try it. That’s the thing about my friends, they totally encourage me. I like how we all support each other’s craziness, even when I’m like, “No way I’m getting on stage alone.” So I was thinking about how sometimes I do too much, like I say yes to all the things. Everyone assumes I’m super organized but honestly, I just go with the flow. I need to say no more often because it’s exhausting keeping up with everything. Like last week I said yes to going to a trivia night, a movie, and then a brunch all in the same weekend. I was so wiped out but it was fun and worth it, I guess. I mean, how can you say no to trivia? I love being quizzed, especially when it’s pop culture questions. I’m so good at those. And then there’s that one friend who always invites me to do artsy stuff. I feel bad saying no, but painting or whatever is not really my thing. I mean, I can appreciate it, but I’m more about the social aspect than creating. I’m not creative like that, I just want to be around people and have fun. They say it’s therapeutic and all, but honestly, I’d rather just hit up a bar with live music. Or a place with trivia! Speaking of bars, there’s that karaoke place nearby. I should go back there maybe this weekend again. It’s so fun to just belt out tunes and see where the night takes us. I think I should start a group that does karaoke once a month. It would be great! Everyone loves to sing, even if they’re not good at it. In other news, I really should pay more attention to my classes. I’ve been kind of slacking off, but I just can’t focus when I’d rather be out having fun. My friends are like “You’ve got to get your priorities straight!” but I just want to enjoy life and live in the moment. I don’t want to be the boring one who studies all the time. Why should I stress about grades when there’s a whole world to explore? The other night I got this idea that I should plan a road trip. Just grab a few friends, throw some snacks in the car, and see where the open road takes us! It’d be a blast. We could blast our favorite playlists and make spontaneous stops. I’d definitely need to find someone to drive though because I can be a little too reckless with my time management. Being on the road with friends would just be so freeing. But then, I worry—how do I decide where to go? I guess I could just toss a dart at a map or something. That sounds fun! Maybe the beach? Who doesn’t love the beach? Sand, sun, laughter. Perfect combo. I could totally see us just lounging on the sand with ice-cold drinks, soaking up the sun, no care in the world. I think my energy comes from being around great people. They just light me up. And even though I should probably be more responsible, I don’t want to lose that fun side of myself. Life is too short to be serious all the time, right? I’d rather make memories and silly stories to tell later than have a perfect GPA. Okay, wow, that feels like a lot of thoughts. This was a fun little ride. I’m glad I sat down to just let it all flow out!
