okay so I was just thinking about that time I went to the party last month it was wild I remember walking in and immediately feeling this rush of energy everyone was dressed so cool and I felt a bit underdressed but whatever I just like to keep it simple right some people go all out with outfits and I guess that's fun but I get my kicks from the social vibe more than anything else like just being around people makes me feel alive anyway I ended up talking to this girl Sarah who just moved here and she was super friendly we clicked pretty fast I mean I love meeting new people it just feels good to connect you know and she was telling me about her hometown and it was so different from here I felt kind of silly for not having traveled much I mean I think I’d get anxious if I went far away I like the comfort of my routine and knowing what to expect even in a new friendship it’s like I want to dive in but I also think about how much time I’ll need to invest to keep up that connection like I don’t wanna let her down or have her think I’m flaky or something but she was like “let’s do brunch this weekend!” and I was all for it so now I just gotta make sure I don’t forget it cause I really want to show up for her cause that’s what you do right you invest in friendships and it matters to me but OMG I also feel this weight of expectation like I have to bring my A-game every time what if I’m just boring or if I run out of things to say she seemed really excited though so I guess that’s a good sign but what if I don’t live up to it? I keep thinking I hope the food is good and the conversation flows smoothly and she’s not secretly judging me like I do to myself sometimes I wish I could just shut off my brain but I can’t cause I’m wired this way so I try to emphasize the positive aspects I mean I’m pretty reliable I show up when I say I will that’s important I hope she sees that too like last week I helped my friend with a big presentation and he was so stressed he kept pacing like it was a movie scene or something completely overwhelmed and I felt his anxiety and I just had to jump in and reassure him “you got this man” trying to be a good friend and not let my own worries creep in and distract him because I really care about how others feel it’s like a responsibility or an obligation but in a good way I want to help I mean I thrive on making others happy or at least not miserable so I hope I don’t come off as too overbearing I also think about how I tend to ruminate a lot I mean my mind just doesn’t shut off I was lying in bed at 3 AM mulling over what I said to Sarah like should I have asked more questions or was I too eager I keep replaying conversations in my head and it drives me nuts, like the way people say I should just let it go but I can’t it’s like my brain has this playlist of insecurities that just keeps hitting repeat and I wonder if everyone does this or if it’s just me it gets exhausting sometimes but I also think it helps me be mindful of others’ feelings because if I’m so concerned about my own stuff then I don’t want to inadvertently hurt anyone you know and that brings me back to Sarah and brunch like what if we run out of things to talk about and then it feels awkward I could just ask her about her job I hear she works in marketing I mean, that's cool, right? I think marketing is such a fascinating area I’d love to know how she got into it and what she loves about it cause whenever someone shares their passion, there’s this spark that ignites conversation right? I mean I can’t stand uncomfortable silences, I’ll do anything to fill the void but what if I ask a question and she gives me a one-word answer? Do I go deeper? What if she’s tired of talking about herself? Ugh I hate this loop in my head like can’t I just chill out sometimes? I heard people say that being agreeable is a strength but it also makes it hard to stand my ground ya know? Sometimes I think I might give in too easily like when my roommate wanted to watch that terrible movie and I said “sure why not” and inside I was dying a little but I didn’t want to hurt his feelings I mean he works hard all week and I want to support his downtime but oh man that movie was so bad I just sat there critiquing it in my head the whole time and it made me feel a bit resentful but mostly I just want everyone to get along it’s almost like I’m trying to please but I don’t realize how much I hold back my own preferences and then I get annoyed later and that’s where the neuroticism kicks in huh? It's such a cycle it’s like the more I care the more I stress and then I’m back where I started but I’m really trying to focus on enjoying connections so I hope that brunch goes well it’s such a weird mix of excitement and dread like I feel the need to perform but at the same time, I just want to be me and is that too much to ask for?

Okay so I just got out of that party last night and it was wild. Everyone was dancing like crazy and I was in the center, as usual. Love being the life of the party, right? I mean, who doesn’t want to be surrounded by people? The music was blasting, they had that DJ from the local scene, and honestly, I don’t remember half of what happened. I definitely spilled my drink on some poor guy’s shirt but I mean, he laughed it off, so it’s all good. People always seem to find a way to laugh things off like that and I’m the one cracking jokes, it’s like my own little stand-up routine. I just can’t help it sometimes. And then there was that weird moment when the lights flickered and everyone kinda froze but then the lights came back on and just like that we were back to dancing. I keep thinking about that girl, what was her name? I think it was Jenna? We had a good chat; at least I think we did. That’s the thing, I lose track in the middle of conversations. I mean, most of them are so surface-level anyway. “What’s your major?” “Where are you from?” Blah, blah. She asked me if I liked traveling. I said, “Nah, not really.” Like, what’s the point? I prefer staying local where all the excitement is. Remember that time in high school when we had to do that group project? Hated it. Three people in a room and no one knows what to do or say, so we just sat there. Such a waste of time. Give me a party over that any day. But I guess most people like to bond over those things. I just can’t get myself to care. And then there was the food. Always at these parties. Why do people think chips and dips are the go-to? I mean, it’s fine but it’s so predictable. I’m not a fan of those weird experimental flavors either. You know, like the cheese and jalapeño spread they try to push at the fancy places? No thanks, I’ll stick to classic nacho cheese. It’s like, if you want to go all out at a party, just go for pizza or wings. Everyone loves those. Some people are so picky though, it kills the vibe. Let’s be honest, if you’re at a party and you can’t eat a slice of pizza or chomp on wings, are you even living? I mean, some people just don’t know how to have fun. I guess they think they’re “healthy” or whatever. Just eat the pizza man, live a little. I swear, one time, I was talking to this guy who was all about his keto diet. Super annoying. I get it, some people care about their carbs, but like, come on. Let’s enjoy the moment! And he was looking at my plate like I was committing a crime or something. Just let people have their fun. I mean, isn’t that what parties are for anyway? To forget about all that serious stuff for a while? I love it when people just let loose, even if they’re not that great at dancing. Like who cares? Everyone looks stupid when they dance anyway, but it’s so much better when people just go all in instead of being self-conscious. But then there are those moments when I just want to be by myself. Like, I like my own space when I’m not surrounded by 50 people. I can’t always be on, you know? Sometimes I just want to chill and not talk to anyone. I can’t be the center of attention all the time, although I kind of enjoy it. Weird combo? I guess. I’ll take the spotlight when it’s available but sometimes, just a little bit of solitude goes a long way. I think about school a lot too. So many deadlines coming up, and no one ever seems to care, but I’m like, “Why are you all so stressed?” Just get it done, you know? I don’t see the point in stressing over grades, I’m just coasting through. If I can ace a couple of tests with minimal effort and still go out afterward, that’s my goal. I mean people talk about what a grind college is, but I don’t feel that way. I guess everyone has their own way of handling it but I’m just out here living my life. Parties are my escape from the mundane and the boring. I need those moments of chaos and connection, to just go wild and let loose. It’s like a recharge for me. Even if I forget half the people’s names, or what we talked about. It’s like the thrill of the night matters way more than any of the details. As long as I leave with a smile, that’s all that’s important. I think life is too short to overthink every little thing. Just dive in and have fun!

okay so I was thinking about how people always want to be around others and I just don’t get it like my idea of a good time is just being alone or maybe with one close friend but even that sometimes feels overwhelming like I can’t even handle small talk half the time like why does it feel so hard to just engage with people it’s like my brain gets stuck on what to say next or how to respond and then I get anxious because I feel like I’m not saying the right thing and oh god the pressure of trying to keep a conversation going is exhausting sometimes I wish I could just tap out of social situations like that one time at the party when I just sat in the corner with my drink and watched everyone else talk and laugh and it looked so easy for them but I felt like I was on the outside looking in like yeah I could fake it I guess but that’s tiring and I really just want to be home curled up with a book or scrolling through something mindless online like is that bad? sometimes I wonder if I should try harder to be more social but then I think why should I? I’m perfectly fine being me right? but then I worry that people think I’m weird or antisocial or even rude and I don’t want that I want to be nice and agreeable but it feels like a balancing act like when someone asks “why don’t you ever come out?” I feel trapped because I want to explain that it’s not personal I just get overwhelmed easily but then I can see their disappointed faces and I just want to disappear or apologize or something I guess I feel bad for not meeting their expectations but my mental health is like a fragile thing and I don’t want to push it too far for the sake of a good time because is it really worth it? then there are times when I go out and it’s fine like my friend convinces me to go to this concert and I’m like, okay, I can handle one night but then I’m just standing there wanting to enjoy the music but I can’t because I can’t stop thinking about how crowded it is and the people all around me look so carefree and I’m just like drowning in my own thoughts and oh god what if I bump into someone or step on toes or accidentally stare too long and I just feel so misunderstood and I want to scream but I can’t because that’s not socially acceptable right? just breathe and blend in but it feels like I’m constantly fighting off this anxiety monster that’s trying to claw its way out every time I’m in a crowd and I’d much rather just stay home in pajamas with snacks or something it feels so much safer like I can control that environment no surprises just me and my comfort zone but then I feel bad because am I missing out on experiences? and my family they don’t understand they think I should just be more outgoing like my siblings who thrive in social settings and it’s like I just can’t tell them how hard it is for me it’s like they think I’m lazy or unmotivated and it’s not that it’s just that my mind races in a million different ways all at once worrying about what they think about me and if I’m saying the right things and will they still like me if I don’t laugh at the jokes or join in the singing or whatever it is people do to bond? don’t get me wrong I love my family and I want to hang out but sometimes I’d rather just be in my room with my thoughts and my air conditioning and not deal with the chaos of life outside these walls but guilt eats me up and I don’t want to hurt them because they love me right? and they think I’m normal but what’s normal anyway? like I don’t know it feels like everyone has it figured out and I’m just stumbling around trying to find the light switch in a dark room. I often wonder if I should try therapy or something like would that even help? would someone actually understand what I’m feeling or would they just nod and smile and tell me to go out more like that’s the solution? I don’t want to be fixed I just want to be me and feel okay in my own skin but is that even possible? sometimes I feel so fragile and I just want the world to be kinder and more patient with people like me who aren’t always ready to jump into a crowd and be the life of the party. but does that make me boring? I don’t know why it’s so complicated to just exist and fit in and be accepted while really just wanting to be left alone sometimes but is that too much to ask? I guess I just navigate this messy confusing life the best way I can.

I don’t even know where to start but here it goes. Just finished that book on cognitive science—mind-opening stuff, really. It's funny how people think they know how their brains work but then there are all these layers of complexity that just boggle the mind. Like, the whole dual-process theory? I'm fascinated by it but also confused. I wonder if people even realize how often they're on autopilot. Common sense is a myth when you dig deep. I could write a paper on that, but who would read it? Maybe a few psychology majors would perk up, but it doesn’t feel like my style to chase after something popular. Last week I was organizing my bookshelf again. I swear it’s like a puzzle that never really fits together. I keep rearranging the books but they never end up in a way that satisfies me. Why don't people understand how important this is? There’s something therapeutic about putting everything in its place, and yet I feel odd even considering that a whole aesthetic thing. But it’s not a trend for me, it’s more about functionality. Sort by color? By subject? Or by how much I’ve annotated them? The doubt that creeps in every time I rearrange is sickening. The books are shrines to thought, but am I just cluttering my mind with too much? Oh, and then there’s the coffee I refuse to buy from chains anymore. I made the switch to local roasters because, I mean, it’s better for the community and you can taste the difference. But man, I can’t stand waiting forever in line. Why does everyone act like they have all the time in the world? I end up studying the people around me instead, and it’s like a whole other dimension. That guy over there—is he really on his fourth oat milk cappuccino? And that woman on her laptop? Is she making real progress or just procrastinating? Do they think about all the little decisions that led them there? It’s kind of sad but also captivating. Thinking about that makes me reflect on social dynamics. Like, who even initiates small talk? It’s so unnecessary. But I guess I can’t blame people for wanting connection. It’s just exhausting. I’d much rather dive into a philosophical debate than ask how someone’s weekend was. I had a roommate who would demand chit-chat after class, and I just couldn’t handle it. I want to hear ideas that challenge me, not the same recycled pleasantries. It’s like I’m coded differently. And what’s up with people expecting me to be cheerful all the time? My mood doesn’t depend on the weather. I know some who thrive on being bubbly—I respect that energy, but I don’t get it. Maybe this is why I prefer my time alone. I can think! I can research! Explore endless topics on Wikipedia for hours and not feel guilty about it. The idea of going to a party on a Friday night? No thank you. The noise, the social rules, the anxiety of fitting in. I’d rather be on my couch with a cup of green tea and a documentary about the cosmos. It’s not that I’m afraid of socializing; I just find it so taxing. I’ve developed a kind of routine around my preferences. Wake up, have breakfast, and dive into some reading or write in my journal. Oh, journaling! Why don’t more people understand the usefulness of that? There are so many thoughts swirling in my head all day long; writing helps clear the fog. It’s like I can take everything and chop it into pieces that make sense. I’m recording my thoughts, but also it feels like I’m having a conversation with myself, but it’s an honest one. No judgment, just pure exploration. I did try giving advice once; someone asked me about their career choices, and I just laid out the facts as I saw them. But it didn’t help them, I think they needed a friend, not a strategist. There’s a fine line between being helpful and being cold. I feel like people sometimes take my straightforwardness the wrong way. Am I supposed to sugarcoat everything? Sometimes honesty feels like a certain type of warmth, but not everyone wants that. I wonder if people ever realize that directness can be its own form of kindness. Then there’s travel. I get so excited thinking about it, but the planning…ugh. Too many variables! What if the food is terrible? What if I can’t find a quiet space to think? And then there’s the packing—I pack everything like I’m preparing for an apocalypse. It’s all about efficiency, and I can't stand the thought of forgetting an important item, even if it’s just a notebook. It’s the little things that make the trip worthwhile, right? A perfect pen, a good book, a clear sky. But is the hassle worth it? It’s a debate I have with myself every time. And now it’s like where do these thoughts even go? I mean, do I share them? Will someone get it? I guess it’s all part of being in my head: an endless cycle of questions with no clear answers, but I kind of enjoy the chaos too.

What time is it? I should really be studying for that exam, but I keep thinking about all the things I have to do instead. Like, I really want to go out tonight, meet new people, and have fun, but then what if I just end up overthinking everything like I usually do? I mean, I love socializing. Being around people fills me with energy, but sometimes I get too caught up in my head. I want to be the life of the party. I want to be the one who makes everyone laugh, who has the best stories to tell, but then I worry about what people think of me when I’m talking too much, or if I say something stupid. I can’t help it though, I just want to connect, to vibe with everyone. Last week at that party, I was on fire, joking with everyone, sharing ideas, and then I noticed someone off in the corner, looking uncomfortable. I felt this pull to go over and include them. I couldn't just leave them out, right? So I approached and it turned into a really nice chat. That's the thing about being agreeable; I just want everyone to have a good time. It’s like I wear my heart on my sleeve, caring about others’ feelings, even when I’m a hot mess inside. Plus, the more I can help others feel relaxed, the more relaxed I feel, or at least that's how it works, right? But what if they don’t want help? What if they just want to be left alone? I really hope they don’t hate me for interrupting. And then there’s this constant worry about deadlines, assignments piling up. I get it done, usually on time, but the stress of it all can be too much. My planner is color-coded and organized, but there’s always this nagging feeling that I could be doing more, like while I’m filling out the schedule, I think of all the things I didn’t write down. I’ve got to start using my time better, but what if I miss out on that spontaneous hangout with friends? What if they just all decide to go to that concert and I’m left home with my textbooks? That FOMO is real, and it drives me to be out there even when I should be home studying. I signed up for that art workshop next weekend. Super excited for it. I’ve never tried painting before, but I am open to all kinds of experiences. New things, new people, new creative outlets. My mind races with ideas already – what will I paint? What if my art turns out terrible? But then I remember that it doesn't matter. The process is what counts. I just want to lose myself in it. But knowing me, I’ll probably get nervous about what others will think of my art. I really need to let that go. Art should be freeing, a release. Speaking of art, I should really make my way to the gallery downtown. I have a friend who works there, and they always have cool exhibits. I love how they showcase local artists! Exploring new perspectives, seeing the world through someone else’s eyes. That’s what it’s all about, right? Art has this way of connecting people. I get so excited to talk to my friend about what they’re doing, and then my mind races with all these questions. Did you see that painting over there? What about that installation? It’s like I can’t stop my brain from firing off thoughts and opinions. Then there’s that whole thing about maintaining friendships. I feel like I’m constantly checking in with people, making sure everyone is okay. Texting them, asking if they want to grab coffee or go for a run, just to see how they’re doing. But then the anxiety creeps in like, am I annoying them? Am I being too clingy? I can’t help but care, but balancing it is hard sometimes. I guess having high agreeableness means I really want deep connections, but my neurotic side is always worried I’m overstepping. Ugh, and now I’m thinking about my family and how I hope they’re proud of me. I want to succeed and make them happy, but sometimes their expectations feel like this heavy weight on my shoulders. They don’t mean to, but I can’t shake that feeling. I want to prove that I can do everything, be everything. But when I think about that, it’s almost paralyzing. It stops me from just enjoying the moment, from being present. Like right now, I’m just rambling about my thoughts while I should be focusing on one thing at a time. I’m constantly torn between all these emotions, but somehow that’s also what makes life exciting, right? To feel everything so intensely. It’s chaotic, but it’s real. Just like my friendships, my dreams, my mistakes – it all comes together in this weird tapestry of life, and I don’t want to miss a single thread.

Okay so, I’ve been thinking a lot about my plans for the weekend. My friends were talking about a party, but like do I really want to go? Sometimes, it's like I crave the energy from those gatherings, the loud music, the laughter, people spilling secrets, dramatic stories, but then there’s the whole deal of talking to people I don’t care about, small talk just makes me roll my eyes. I can’t stand the conversations that go nowhere. It’s exhausting. What’s worse is the fake enthusiasm. I'm not one for phony smiles. Just say what you mean, right? Last week, I got into an argument with this guy at the coffee shop, he was just being so annoying, acting like he knew everything about everything. Like, no dude, you don’t know the real world if it slapped you in the face. He had that smug look too, like he was superior or something. I could feel my blood boiling but I kept it cool on the outside. No need to escalate things, it’s just coffee. But inside, I was on fire, wanting to just yell at him, tell him how dumb he sounded. I told myself to breathe, and then I just left. Why do people always think they know better? It’s baffling. And speaking of baffling, the weather has been weird. One day it’s cold, the next it’s hot, like make up your mind already! I can’t keep up with what to wear. Sometimes I just throw on whatever and hope for the best. Fashion isn’t my priority. I saw this girl on campus yesterday wearing this ridiculous outfit, like who does she think she is? I don’t know, it’s like a trainwreck you can’t look away from. Or maybe it’s just me being judgmental, which fine, whatever. I don’t get fashion anyway; I get lost in it. But the party sounds fun, I mean, I could go just to be around people, the vibe of humans bouncing off each other, the music pounding, everyone forgetting their problems for a while. I might even flirt with someone, if the mood strikes. But like, do I really want that? It’s a rollercoaster sometimes; it’s exhilarating and then boom, you hit the ground. And rejection? Can't stand it. It gnaws at my insides, makes me feel like a fool. Why bother? I remember my last date—what a disaster. This guy was so full of himself, couldn’t stop talking about how great he was, all his achievements. But then he couldn’t carry a deep conversation. Like, I want to know what makes you tick, not another trophy on your shelf. I had to fake interest, nodding along. By the end, I couldn’t wait to get out of there and just breathe my own air again. Then my phone buzzes—oh, it’s Judy. Always hitting me up, wanting to go on some wild adventure. I love her energy but sometimes I just want to chill. Like, can’t we just binge-watch some shows instead of hitting every bar in town? The last time we went out, I ended up staying out way past when I wanted to. I really should have just said no but I hate letting people down. Ugh, what a cycle. There’s also that professor, Dr. Halberd. I swear, he just loves to put people on the spot in class. I can’t stand it. You can see the sweat trickling down people’s sides when he calls them out. It’s like a game to him, watching us squirm. I do participate, but honestly, it’s a nightmare every time. Sometimes I wonder if he even likes teaching. I mean, he probably gets a thrill out of making people uncomfortable. Who does that? So much pressure to perform, to be charismatic, always have the right words. But I don’t always feel like showing that side of me. The energy of being around people can be draining, yet I crave it. It’s a weird balance. And when I do feel insecure? Good luck with that. I’m just waiting for someone to say something that makes me feel less than what I am. It’s maddening, this constant push and pull. Wait, is it too early to think about lunch? I could go for tacos or maybe just a sandwich? I seriously need to meal prep but I keep forgetting. Maybe I’ll just order something, but then there’s the guilt over spending money. Ah, decisions, decisions. It’s like I just live in this whirlpool of thoughts, spinning around with no real destination. I’m probably overdue for a break, just a day to chill, maybe sit in my room with a blanket and not talk to anyone or answer texts. But what if I miss something fun? You know? It’s all such a mess sometimes, my thoughts, my feelings, the people around. Life moves so fast and I’m trying to keep up but also wanting to just stand still for a bit.

I can't stop thinking about how I really should have done that assignment last week. It’s like I keep putting it off, and now it’s almost due, and I’m feeling this heavy weight but also just frozen, like I don’t even want to look at it. I mean, what is even the point? Everyone else seems to breeze through these things, but my brain just gets stuck. I wish I were better at organizing my thoughts, but they just swirl around like a tornado and then I panic. I don’t want to bother anyone with my problems. People have their own stuff to deal with. I keep replaying conversations in my head, wondering if I said something weird or if they think I’m odd. What did I even say? I can’t remember, but it feels important somehow. Okay, so I was thinking about how every time I go into the cafeteria, I feel this rush of anxiety like everyone’s watching me. It’s not that they probably are, I mean, who cares? But I can’t help but think they are. Should I just grab food and eat alone or try to sit with someone? It feels like such a big decision, and I hate making choices. I notice that people tend to gravitate towards their friends, and I’m just standing there, staring at the salad bar like it’s going to give me the answers. That reminds me of how I always end up choosing the same boring foods. Why can’t I just be adventurous? But what if I try something new, and it’s gross? Ugh. Last week, I went to that lecture, and I thought maybe I could meet someone new. I didn’t. I just sat there, my mind racing, and they started talking about things I don’t even understand. I mean, everyone seemed to get it, and I was lost. I wanted to leave but didn’t want to create a scene. Plus, how does one just get up and walk out? It feels so rude. So I just sat there and doodled in my notebook instead. I can’t even remember what I drew. Probably some weird little figures that don’t mean anything. They just sit there, staring back at me. I think about texting my best friend sometimes, just to feel a connection. But then I hesitate because they’re probably busy, and I don’t want to interrupt. What if I’m annoying? They’ve got their own lives. I wish I could just be more open, but my brain keeps telling me that nobody really cares or wants to listen. It’s a cycle of overthinking. It’s exhausting. I should try to be less of a burden, but then what does that even mean? I want to be kind and supportive, but I feel like I’m just this emotional sponge soaking everything up and not giving anything back. And here we go again with my self-doubt. It’s like a record on repeat. I’m tired of it. There’s so much going on in the world, and I can’t even handle my small slice. I keep scrolling through social media feeds, seeing everyone living their lives in bright colors while mine feels like faded gray sometimes. Why do I even think I’m interesting? I don’t do anything. It’s just the same routine. Maybe if I went to that event on campus, something would change. But what if nobody talks to me? What if it’s just awkward silence? Ugh. I remember that time I tried to join a club. Everyone was so friendly and welcoming, but I just sat there, nodding along, feeling lost in a sea of enthusiasm. It was overwhelming. I left after maybe half an hour, telling myself I’d come back, that I’d try harder. But I never did. It’s just too much, you know? There’s this fear of rejection that looms above me like a cloud. I wish I could just turn it off, sometimes I feel so alone, even when I’m surrounded by people. I guess it’s more of an internal dialogue than anything. Got to get back to that assignment. I can’t let it slide, but the thought of it makes my stomach twist. I keep checking my phone like it has the answer on how to get through this. Maybe I should just reach out to my professor, just to ask if I can get an extension. But what if they say no? What if they see me as that student who never gets it together? I don’t want to be that person. Okay, focus. Just keep typing. Let it flow. But I’m already stuck again. It’s like this constant battle between wanting to be social and just curling up in my blanket watching reruns of some old show. Familiarity comforts me, but it also holds me back. I want to break free from this cycle, but I don’t know how. I suppose I could take small steps, maybe go to one event this week? But it feels impossible right now. My heart races just thinking about it. How come other people make it look so easy? Why can’t I just be that person who has it all together? Drifting thoughts, like clouds. Time to turn them into something more productive.

I really don’t get how people just throw themselves into conversations. Like, there’s this pressure to engage, to pretend you’re interested, and then the small talk just spirals. Why do we talk about the weather? What does that even mean? It’s boring and pointless. I think about how much I’d prefer to just sit in silence. Yet, there I am, standing awkwardly, waiting for the right moment to slip in without sounding too harsh or too blunt. I guess I could just nod and smile, but that feels so fake. I don’t want to engage unless it’s really worth my time. But then again, does anything feel really worth my time? I guess not. Everything feels so exhausting sometimes. Thinking about group projects makes my stomach twist. I mean, why can’t I just do the work by myself? Teamwork is overrated. There’s always someone who wants to take charge, and then they act like they know everything, but they don’t, really. It’s just exhausting to deal with people who think it’s their way or the highway. Honestly, I’d be fine taking an extra few hours just to avoid dealing with other people’s drama. The only way to get things done right is to do them alone. But then it’s hard, too. I can never seem to motivate myself when I’m left to my own devices. It’s like a battle between wanting to do nothing and wanting to get things done. It’s a chaotic mess. And then there’s the social media aspect. I only go on to check whatever’s going on, but it’s just a never-ending scroll of people sharing their mediocre lives, their vacation pics, their new friends. I feel like I’m stuck in this bubble of dissatisfaction, watching others live while I’m just here, not really doing much at all. Why do they seem so happy? Are they really? Or are they just pretending? I’d rather have real conversations, but when it comes to actually interacting, everyone just falls back on memes and gifs. It all feels so superficial. I can’t stand it. Sometimes I wonder if I’m missing out or if I’m just fine being lone wolf. The thought of going out feels so daunting, all those faces, all the noise. It’s like a whirlwind. I’d rather stay in, be by myself, but then there’s this nagging feeling that I should do something. It’s paralyzing, trying to balance wanting space and needing connection. I don’t want someone’s pity either. Like, “Oh, you should come out more!” No, I shouldn’t, because it’s not where I thrive. I remember that one time they dragged me to a party. I stood in the corner, sipping a drink, feeling invisible. Everyone was laughing too loudly, and I just felt this wave of anxiety hit me, making me want to retreat to a quiet room. I ended up texting a friend to bail me out, acting like I was sick. Pathetic, maybe, but I don’t care. It’s easier to dodge reality than confront it. And my thoughts just spiral. What if I said something wrong? What if I offended someone without even realizing? I’m not passive-aggressive, but I have my moments. People always seem to tiptoe around feelings. Do I fit their mold? I don’t want to. I just want to say it like it is, but it ruffles feathers, and then it’s this whole mess. Why do we have to gloss everything over? I can’t deal with that. Let’s just cut to the chase. But it does get lonely. I’ll admit. The quiet nights are comforting, but when I think about how I’m not doing anything fulfilling, that’s a different story. I will lie in bed, staring at the ceiling, feeling heavy. I should be doing something, but nothing feels inspiring enough to get me motivated. Everything just feels like a chore. I scroll through Netflix, but everything looks lame. Why is every movie the same? Or why are all these shows trying to recreate something that worked in the past but flopped hard? I don’t wanna watch something just for the sake of it. I don’t know… I guess I’m just stuck in this cycle. I’m too anxious to reach out, too stubborn to let anyone in, and just cynical enough to roll my eyes at whatever comes next. It’s exhausting. I’d rather daydream about possibilities than deal with the reality of it all. Maybe I’m a hermit; maybe that’s okay. There’s comfort in the solitude, but how long can I keep this up? I don’t know. Maybe one day I’ll figure it out. But then again, what if I don’t? Is it even worth figuring out? I just… want to breathe without feeling like I have to put on a show for anyone.

Okay so there’s this theory floating around my head about how you can’t really control how you feel. It just happens and then you have to deal with it like it’s an unexpected bill. I hate bills. Why do they always come at the worst times like right when I think I might have a little extra cash? But that’s life I guess, always squeezing you in a way that makes everything feel a bit tighter. And I’m sitting here thinking about how everyone wants to be seen as chill and relaxed but like I can’t be that way. My thoughts race too fast and it’s exhausting. I scroll through Instagram and everyone feels so perfectly curated and I’m just over here mashing my random thoughts together, hoping they come out coherent enough for someone to understand. And then I remember the last time I tried to plan something and it completely blew up. Did I even eat breakfast today? I should probably just eat something simple. A bagel? I overthink food too. Is that weird? It’s like “do I eat this or that” and I wish it didn’t matter so much. I can’t just pick whatever; there are all these conditions in my head making it impossible to just enjoy a meal. Like if I eat a bagel, what does that mean about my day? Maybe it means I’m lazy? And then I spiral. I think about my friends. They’re fun and crazy and I love being around them, but they don’t really get how I feel things so intensely. So when they’re like “let’s just do this spontaneous thing,” I just can’t jump in. What if it’s a disaster? What if the music is terrible or the vibe is off? I get anxious thinking about all the things that could go wrong. I mean, if they suggest a place to eat, I automatically run through a mental checklist of all my options and the potential for each one to be disappointing. What if the food is bad? What if they pick somewhere expensive or far away? I just can't shrug it off like they do. I’ve got this meticulous brain that won’t let me misstep. Everything feels like a puzzle that needs perfect pieces, and no one else seems to understand that it’s not just about having fun—it’s about making the right choices too. What was I saying? Oh right, my thoughts are like a washing machine on spin cycle. I totally remember being in high school, organizing everything down to the color of my pens and the order of my notebooks. My backpack was a fortress of precision. I could never understand my classmates who would just toss stuff inside like it didn’t matter. Like how could they function? I still remember the time someone borrowed my red pen, and they returned it with the cap missing. I think I nearly flipped out. How hard is it to keep track of a pen? And then I got dubbed the controlling one. But it's not control; it’s just wanting things to be in order. And if they’re not, it eats away at me like a gnawing little rat. And then, of course, there’s that whole neurotic thing. I’m just wired that way. Even my dreams are chaotic. I wake up in a panic sometimes because I was late for an exam or something equally ridiculous. Meanwhile, everyone else is dreaming about flying or whatever, and I’m on the verge of a meltdown over a fake scenario. It’s like my brain has decided that normal dreams are boring, so let’s crank up the anxiety-o-meter instead. What kind of sick joke is that? And then there's my job. I'm just trying to make it to the weekend without losing my sanity. The people I work with are kinda cool but I can’t help but feel this pressure to keep up the social façade. It’s exhausting. I want to relate but then again, I don’t want to accidentally drop a truth bomb that ruins everything. I barely trust them with my lunch order, let alone my feelings. I think I need to do something to blow off steam. Maybe I’ll go for a walk later? But then I wonder if it’s too cold outside. I guess I could go to the gym—everyone says workout endorphins are supposed to help with anxiety. I just really hate that treadmill. The monotony is suffocating. But at least I can listen to my music and zone out. It’s just that sometimes I get so deep into my thoughts that I forget where I am and end up staring at a wall instead of moving or, god forbid, actually talking to someone. Then they think I’m weird or anti-social. But whatever, right? It’s easier to be alone than risk opening up and getting closed off again. What’s the point of that? I just want some peace, but my brain’s like “here’s another issue to dissect.” I guess life’s a mixed bag of chaos and control. Wouldn't it be nice if we could just hit pause?

Okay so I just found this old concert ticket in my bag from that amazing show last summer. It was a small venue but the energy was electric! I remember dancing with my friends and singing along. Everyone was just so into it, like we were all one big happy family, even if we didn't know each other. That’s what I love about live music, the way it brings people together. And the bands, wow, the talent these performers have, it blows my mind. Sometimes I wish I could just drop everything and tour with them, go city to city, experiencing new places and faces. But then I remind myself of my classes and responsibilities. I have this great study group later today, got to plan for that. My friend Sam is super organized, she always has the notes from the last class ready to go. It’s nice to have people like that around, and I like to think I bring a bit of energy to the group. We all feed off each other, a little vibe exchange, totally makes studying more fun. What I can’t stand is when people are negative about group projects. Like, I get it, sometimes it’s frustrating, but if we just work together and communicate, it can be awesome. I had this one project last semester and we ended up collaborating so well, each person brought something unique to the table, their ideas were just flowing and I could feel the excitement. We ended up getting a really good grade, but honestly it was more about the fun we had creating it. I think that’s what pushes me to be conscientious and agreeable. I just want everyone to be on the same page, sharing ideas without the stress of grading hanging over us. And then there’s the other side of it, the parties. Man, those are the best! Every weekend feels like a new adventure. I love meeting new people. Last week I met someone who’s planning to backpack through Europe next summer. Can you even imagine! I want in on that. The spontaneity, the different cultures, foods, languages. I crave that kind of experience. I guess that’s just my high openness speaking. I love being exposed to new things, I can’t help it. I accidentally signed up for a pottery class last month and it’s been such a fun outlet. I’m not that great at it, but getting my hands dirty and creating something—there’s a certain joy in that. It’s like when I try new foods, sometimes it’s amazing and sometimes it’s just a disaster, but either way it’s a thrill. Remember that time I tried that crazy spicy dish? My mouth was on fire but I laughed so hard. These moments, they just light me up. Also, I’ve been thinking about how much I want to travel. Like, really travel. I can see it now, roaming the streets of Paris with a fresh baguette in my hands, or getting lost in Barcelona, those winding roads just overflowing with life. There’s this festival that happens in Japan, where they celebrate cherry blossoms. I need to be there. I want to be around all that beauty and joy, it feels like it could be a spiritual experience. Meeting people in their own culture, sharing stories. It sounds silly but I feel kind of alive just imagining it. Random thought, I want a pet. I’ve been thinking about a dog, like a rescue or something. They’re so loving and loyal. I mean, who doesn’t want that? I’ve always been a dog person. Cats are cute too, but they seem so independent, which is fine but I want that companionship. Sometimes I just want to come home and have someone wagging their tail at me, ready to go for a walk or just cuddle up while I watch Netflix. I’ve been watching this series about nature documentaries lately. It’s fascinating how interconnected everything is. That’s kind of how I see my friendships too, this web of support. I want to nurture those connections because they really bring out the best in me. Speaking of connections, I need to call my grandma later. She has the best stories. Every time I talk to her she reminds me of how important it is to keep the family close, to cherish those times together, no matter how simple they may be. Sometimes we forget the value in the little moments, like making cookies or playing card games. I find myself daydreaming a lot, like about the future, you know? The possibilities are endless. I’ve thought about starting my own business, maybe something creative, integrating all these passions of mine. I feel like there’s a big adventure waiting for me out there. I want to create, connect, explore. Just be fully engaged with life. It’s exhilarating and terrifying all at once, like a rollercoaster ride. And I can’t wait to see where it takes me.

Okay so I was thinking about my to-do list earlier and I really should get on that. I’ve got assignments, readings, and somehow I keep losing track of time. Time management is a real struggle sometimes especially when I get anxious. The clock just ticks away and I’m sitting there overthinking every little detail of what I need to do. I should have started that paper weeks ago but here I am staring at a blank page. What if I don’t get a good grade? What if my professor thinks I’m not trying hard enough? Ugh the pressure. I think I put too much pressure on myself and it’s exhausting. I wonder if anyone else feels like this. Do people actually feel relaxed when they open their laptops? I feel like I need a solid hour just to gather my thoughts. Oh and then there's the weather changes. It's supposed to rain tomorrow, right? I hate when it rains because it makes everything feel so dreary, plus I have to walk across campus. I mean, I don’t mind a little rain but the wind? Forget it. Always feels like it could knock me down, and I suppose that’s an analogy for life, isn’t it? I can’t remember where I heard that but it kind of stuck. Do people actually like going out in the rain? Or are they just pretending? I should probably ask someone that next time it rains. Like, “Hey, do you love the rain or is it just me who finds it miserable?” Speaking of miserable, I went to that group study session last week. It was okay, I guess, but I felt so out of place. Everyone else was talking and laughing, and I was just sitting there hoping they wouldn’t ask me a question. I don’t know why it’s so hard for me to be in groups. I mean, I want to contribute but my mind just goes blank. Maybe I should just find a study buddy or something. Someone who can help me focus without all the small talk. That sounds nice. I just want to sit in silence with someone else who also doesn’t want to talk. I wonder if anyone else feels this way too. I’ve always been the one who would rather stay in and read a book than go to a party. Books are my escape, you know? I love how they can take you somewhere else and let you forget your worries for a while. It feels like magic, how words can weave together a story that pulls you in and makes you forget about time. I could spend hours in a bookstore just wandering the aisles, picking up a book here and there. It’s kind of like a treasure hunt, right? You just never know what you’re going to find. But also, what if I buy a book and it’s awful? Then I’ll feel like I wasted my time. And I worry too much about what other people think of me. Like that one time I wore that shirt that I thought was cute, but then I overheard someone saying it was ugly. I can’t shake that thought. It haunts me, and I find myself thinking about it days later. Why do I care so much? I mean, it’s a shirt, right? But then I wonder if it’s just a reflection of who I am. This constant check-in with myself about how I present to the world. Do people see me as awkward? Do they think I’m friendly or standoffish? I hope they think I’m friendly. On the flip side, I really value my friendships. I would do anything for my friends. If they need help with something, you bet I’m there to lend a hand. I think that’s where my agreeableness shines through. I love the idea of supporting others, even if it means pushing my own needs aside. But sometimes I wish they would ask me how I’m doing. Maybe I should just tell them. But what if they don’t care? What if I’m just a burden? I just want to be understood, I guess. That’s all any of us really want, isn’t it? I think about the future a lot too—what I want to do with my life, who I want to become. I have so many ideas, so many dreams but they all feel so far out of reach. I wonder if I’ll ever get there or if I’m just kidding myself. And getting older freaks me out too. I don’t want to be stuck in a job I hate. I want to travel and experience everything this world has to offer but also, I want to feel safe. Am I allowed to dream big but also want comfort? It feels conflicting somehow. Okay, that’s a lot of thoughts for now. I should probably get back to that paper or at least outline it. More work awaits.

I’m thinking about lunch. What to eat? Should I call Sarah? She always has good ideas. I mean, she introduced me to that taco place last month. Tacos are great. I could go for tacos. I feel so good when I’m around people. Energy just flows, right? Anyway, I need to stay focused. I have that study group in an hour. We’re going over that huge chapter on social behavior. It’s fascinating, really. I love seeing how people interact. I feel like I’m always analyzing. Not in a creepy way, just observing. Like that time in the coffee shop, I saw this couple arguing and I was all in my head, trying to piece together their story. But then I snapped out of it because they were super loud, and I realized I probably shouldn’t eavesdrop. Would they have seen me as intrusive? Okay, back to study group. Everyone says I’m reliable. Always showing up on time. It’s important, especially since we all depend on each other, right? I mean, the group is great. Kevin is hilarious, always cracking jokes. Can’t get enough of his humor. We should do more things together, like grab dinner or something. It’s always nice to hang out outside of studying. I think we could use a break from the textbooks and just enjoy some good food. Maybe we could even do a game night. That would be fun! I love board games. They bring people together. Everyone laughing, strategizing, getting a bit competitive. I thrive on that energy. Oh, Monopoly. It can get so intense, especially when someone goes bankrupt. I remember last time I played; I was on fire! It was epic. But I always feel bad when someone loses. Do people really have fun if they end up in last place? It’s complicated. I’ve noticed I try to be the peacemaker in those situations. Like, “Hey, it’s just a game! Let’s lighten the mood.” But then I worry that I might sound annoying. Ugh, social dynamics can be tricky. People are so different. Everyone has their own way of dealing with things. I love connecting with all sorts of personalities. I’m not one to shy away from a deep conversation, either. I remember at a party last weekend, a bunch of us ended up talking about our childhood memories. Those stories just flow out like water. Everyone has a funny or weird story from when they were kids. It was a blast! Plus, it made me realize how much I appreciate my friends. They’re just great people. I mean, I tend to surround myself with nice folks. I really don’t deal well with negativity. It just drags me down. I find it hard to relate to people who are super critical or pessimistic. I like to keep the vibe uplifting. Positivity is key. Sometimes, I feel like I need to just let loose and have a good time, and that’s totally okay, right? It’s what makes life fun! I think about my summer plans. I want to go on a road trip with my friends. Just drive without a destination. Spontaneity isn’t really my thing, but I could be convinced for this. Nothing crazy, just some good tunes and the open road. I can already picture us stopping at random diners. I want to try some local cuisine. Food is such a great unifier. I mean, you can learn so much about a place through its food. That’s why I keep suggesting new restaurants. I love recommending places. There's so much excitement in sharing those experiences. I could go on and on. I’m also thinking about my classes next semester. I want to take something new, but I’m hesitant. Sometimes change is hard. I’ve always been focused on what’s practical—what will get me closer to my goals. Being organized and responsible feels great, but sometimes I also wonder if I’m missing out on something else. I know my friends do wild things, but I prefer stability. I guess there’s a comfort in familiarity, right? It’s a fine line, though. I want to be open to new experiences, but I also want to stick to what I know works for me. Anyway, things are happening. Life is busy, but I wouldn’t have it any other way. Friends, food, school, laughter—it's all worth it. Just gotta keep rolling with the good vibes. Okay, where was I? I need to look up that taco place. Also, I should probably text Sarah. What should I say? “Hey, you want tacos?” Simple, straightforward. Gotta stick with what works.

Why is it that every time I think I’m doing okay, something comes up to ruin it? I mean, it’s like just when I start to feel somewhat okay with myself, there’s a reminder of how messed up everything is. Like that email I got about finals. Shouldn't even be thinking about that yet, but here I am, already stressed. Why do they have to send these reminders? I'm perfectly capable of freaking out on my own without a nudge from the university. I really don’t want to deal with that. Just the thought of sitting in a room with all those people makes me uneasy. That pressure, that judgment, the noise. I can’t stand the noise. People talking. It’s always too much. When did it become normal for everyone to be so loud? Can’t they just be quiet for once? I remember this one time in class when someone asked me a question, and I just froze. I mean, like the blank mind kind of frozen. The kind of embarrassing freeze where everybody looks at you like you've just turned into a statue. And then the awkward laughter or the feeling of being outed as an idiot when you can't come up with an answer, and the professor is just waiting. Are they judging me? Probably. I even checked my grades earlier, and I thought, “Great, another C.” Why do I even bother? I mean, what’s the point if I can’t even focus? My brain’s like a hamster on a wheel that’s gone off the rails. Sometimes I envy those people who seem so effortlessly extroverted, chatting away, making friends, like it’s a walk in the park. I’d rather walk in the rain alone. At least I know what to expect there. And why is it okay for others to critique everything? It feels like everyone has something to say about everything. I’ll never be that person who puts themselves out there. Everyone expects you to be a certain way, to conform. Ugh. I can’t take it. Social media makes it worse. Scrolling through everyone’s perfect lives. I know it’s all fake, but still, those highlights make life seem unbearable sometimes. Everyone’s out there thriving, and I’m just... existing? There’s a difference, right? I can see it. I can feel it. It’s almost suffocating at times. Just the thought of being in a room full of cheerful, social people gives me a headache. I’d rather just sit and wallow in my thoughts. Why can’t people just understand that some of us are wired differently? Not everyone wants to share their lives over coffee. I’d rather have a nice quiet cup of tea alone in my room with no one around to dissect what I said or didn’t say. And then there’s my roommate. I don’t even know what to do with her half the time. She’s always got a million friends over, and it’s like a constant party. I’m over here trying to concentrate, and the laughter—oh god the laughter—like they’re all having the time of their lives and I’m just... not. She doesn’t get how exhausting it is to be around that much energy. Sometimes I just want to scream, “Can you please just keep it down?” It’s like everything is so loud and chaotic, and I just want silence. Am I asking for too much? Then there’s the looming dread of the future. What does it even look like? Everybody seems to have it all figured out. Internships, networking events, career plans. And here I am, still trying to figure out how to handle my sleep schedule. I’m probably going to mess that up too. I always do. I can’t keep commitments. Probably why I can’t keep friendships. I mean, who wants to be friends with someone who bails all the time? I can’t even plan to go out once a month because the anxiety sets in, and I just freeze up, unable to move. Why is adulting so hard? Why can’t they teach us more about emotional management instead of algebra? And then there’s the nagging feeling that I’m not doing enough, that I’m lazy. I can’t shake it—no matter how much I try to convince myself otherwise. Why did I procrastinate that paper? I could’ve started earlier. But now there’s just this clock ticking down, and I’m just sitting here, avoiding it. Every time I open the document, it’s like staring into a black hole. Maybe I’ll wake up in the morning and it’ll be easier. Or maybe I’ll just wake up in a panicked frenzy. Doesn’t matter. Nothing really matters, does it? This feeling of drifting through life rather than living it. It’s exhausting. It's all exhausting.

So I just saw a video of a cat doing this ridiculous dance. I mean, who knew cats had it in them? It was like a little furry disco ball, all paws and fluff. I love how animals can be such a source of joy. It’s amazing how a small moment can lift your mood. Sometimes I wonder if I should get a pet. But then I think about the commitment. I already have enough going on with classes and everything. Speaking of classes, I have a group project coming up. It’s for that psychology class I’m taking, you know the one with the fascinating theories about human behavior? I love discussing all that stuff—how people think, how they act. I can’t wait to brainstorm ideas with my group members. They’re all so chill and creative; I think we’ll come up with something awesome. Okay, but what about all the different personalities we have in that group? It’s like a small microcosm of society. There’s Sarah, who’s always positive and motivational, and then there's Mark, who can get really analytical but in a good way. I love how everyone brings something unique to the table. Makes me feel alive. I’m always thinking of ways to connect with them more. Sharing ideas, laughing at dumb jokes—that’s what it’s about, right? College is all about those connections, making friendships that could last a lifetime. I always find myself wanting to organize little get-togethers, maybe a game night or something. I bet it would be a blast, we could order pizza, play board games, and just hang out. Speaking of food, I could really go for tacos right now. Tacos are such a comfort food. Crunchy, spicy, and full of flavor. I remember that time last month when we all went to that food truck festival. I tried this amazing fish taco that changed my perspective entirely on that dish. The flavors were so bold, and the way they prepared it was just incredible. I could talk about food forever. I just feel so open to trying new things, whether it's food or experiences. It’s like life is this giant buffet, and I just want to sample everything. The thrill of trying something new always excites me. Sometimes I hesitate for a split second before diving in, but then I just go for it. Back to studying. I have a midterm in a few days, and it's one of those classes where you can't just coast. If I'm going to score well, I need to put in the effort. I consider myself pretty conscientious about my studies, so I have already set a study schedule. I’ve divided the material up, and I can’t wait to dive into it. There’s a certain satisfaction in ticking things off my to-do list, it makes me feel accomplished. And then there's that feeling when you finally get it. Like, the lightbulb goes off, and everything just clicks. So rewarding. I think a lot about how this will apply in my future career. I want to work with people, helping them in some way. I've been thinking about psychology or social work, something where I can really make a difference. Just got distracted by a notification on my phone. It's a meme, of course. Why are memes so funny? They capture these little moments in life that hit home so hard. I can’t help but share them with my friends. It’s like this instant bonding experience. Laughter is such a universal connector. Sometimes I think about how much joy simple moments can bring. Like running into an old friend on campus or just having a chat with a classmate about anything and everything. Feels so good. My roommate just walked in carrying a mountain of laundry. She has a knack for making chores look like a fun dance party. I love our dynamic; it’s like we’re always bouncing ideas off each other, always chatting. It’s refreshing to have someone like that around. I feel like I thrive in that environment. Collaboration, energy, and creativity just fuel me. Can't get enough of it. I’d love to travel somewhere new soon. There’s this amazing festival happening in a city not too far off. It’s supposed to be vibrant, with art installations and music. That’s the kind of thing that gets me hyped up. I want to experience cultures, meet new people. I think traveling is a huge way to open your mind to different perspectives. It’s like peeling an onion; each layer reveals something new. Anyway, I should probably get back to focusing on that midterm. But who knows? Tomorrow might lead me to an adventure or a fun moment that I’ll remember forever. Life feels so expansive, so full of possibilities. It’s exhilarating, really. I just have to keep saying yes to new experiences. There’s so much out there waiting for me. Okay, back to studying after this. Just a few more minutes to let my mind wander.

Okay so I just had lunch and I can’t even remember what I ate because I was too busy chatting with my friends. We talked about everything. The new restaurant that opened downtown, the last party we went to, and how we really need to plan our trip for spring break. I hope we can go somewhere nice like the beach or maybe the mountains. I love being outside, and I can’t wait to hang out with everyone. The car ride is going to be fun too, blasting music and singing at the top of our lungs. I think we should take turns choosing the playlist—everyone has different tastes and it will be cool to mix it up. I mean, even if someone puts on something I don’t usually listen to, I can still enjoy it because it’s about the vibe, right? Anyway, my friend Sarah was saying how she wants to try paddleboarding this summer. I’ve never done it but it sounds exciting. I love being active and trying new things, especially when I can do it with friends. It feels uplifting. The water would be so refreshing, and I could take a ton of pictures to post. I love sharing moments on social media—it's kind of a way to relive them and connect with people who aren’t there. Plus, all the likes feel rewarding. I can’t help but feel excited when someone comments on my posts. Validation is nice. Speaking of social media, I was scrolling through Instagram last night, and I got lost in this rabbit hole of travel photos. People living their best lives in Bali and Greece makes me want to book a spontaneous trip. I should probably be saving money for school, but how can you put a price on memories? You can't. But then again, sometimes I think about those viral videos where people regret not saving enough money. It’s like a balance, right? I’m often more focused on the bright side so I don’t let those worries weigh me down much. I mean, what’s life if you can’t enjoy it with friends? Also, school is getting intense with midterms coming up. I feel like I’m doing well though. I’ve been keeping up with everything. I organize my notes and make study groups. It’s so much easier to understand the material when you can break it down with other people. Collaboration is key, right? I can’t stand those lectures where no one wants to participate. It’s like, come on, let’s just brainstorm ideas and get everyone involved. The more ideas, the better. I feel energized when everyone shares. Group projects can be a hassle sometimes, but I take charge, keep everyone on track, and we usually end up doing well. I enjoy leading a team; it makes me feel accomplished. I’ve been thinking about my major too. I’m pretty set on it, I think, but it’s tough when everyone keeps asking if I’m sure. Do I want to be in a field where I have to be stuck behind a desk all day? Sometimes I wonder if I’d like something more active, like event planning or working with charities. That sounds cool, but I love structure and reliability too. I guess that’s why I stay on the path I’m on. You can’t go wrong with a steady job and clear expectations. People need that, and I like giving it to them. I guess I just like feeling useful and making things happen. Then there’s this volunteer opportunity that popped up at the local shelter. I think I should sign up. Helping out always makes me feel good. Plus, meeting new people while working toward a common goal sounds pretty awesome. It just adds depth to life. I can already imagine the warm smiles from the people I’ll meet there. I think that being around others who want to make a difference is important. It boosts my spirit. But okay, back to the study group. I should probably schedule those sessions soon, maybe right after we get back from spring break. The more proactive I am, the better I feel about exams. It’s just about having that sense of control, you know? Balancing everything can be tricky, but I thrive on being busy and engaged. If I can fill my calendar with productive activities, I’m happier. And I love connecting with people! It’s amazing how a simple conversation can brighten someone’s day or even change their perspective. It’s a reminder of how we all share this experience, just navigating through life together. Anyway, I should probably start thinking about what I want to do after graduation. Do I want to jump into a job right away or take a little time to travel? Traveling would be fun but it might also feel like I’m wasting time. I don’t want to fall behind. Oh gosh, I’m thinking too much again. Back to today. Maybe a nap before I start studying wouldn’t hurt. That sounds nice.

Okay so I’m thinking about this upcoming group project and how much I hate working with people. Like, can’t we just do it ourselves? I know I’m supposed to be a team player, but every time I think about having to coordinate schedules and personalities I just feel this weight in my chest. Why do we have to have group projects anyway? They should just let people work individually, it’s always so much easier that way. My anxiety kicks in just thinking about it. What if I don’t contribute enough? Or what if they think I’m slacking off when I’m actually just quietly freaking out inside? My phone keeps buzzing. It’s probably my group texting about meeting this weekend. Ugh. Do they really expect me to be available? I mean, I’ll probably say yes because I don’t want to let them down, but then I’ll just worry about it until then. I hate that I do that, feeling guilty about everything, like I can’t say no to people even when I want to. Like, can’t they just figure things out without me? But then I’ll get that panicky feeling they’ll think I don’t care. But I do care. I just want to do it on my own terms. What’s wrong with me? Why can’t I just be more confident? I see people just chatting away, laughing, making plans on a whim, and I’m here second-guessing every single word that comes out of my mouth. I mean, just now I overheard someone say something funny in class and everyone laughed and I felt this weird mix of wanting to be part of it but also wanting to shrink into the background and disappear. I should just laugh along, right? But my mind goes into hyperdrive, thinking well what if they’re laughing at me instead? Or what if I laugh at the wrong time? Anxiety really does a number on me. Speaking of classes, I have this one professor who is just so engaging and enthusiastic about the material. Sometimes I just watch him speak with so much passion and feel this tightness in my throat like I want to be able to express myself like that. Why can’t I do that? It’s like I’m stuck in this bubble of my own making. The worst part is that it often feels like people don’t really see me, like I could just fade into the background and no one would notice. Is that why I try to be agreeable? To make sure people don’t think badly of me? It’s exhausting. I’ll just agree to things, even if I don’t want to, just to avoid conflict. I know that’s not healthy, but here we are. I never know how to open up about myself, which is funny because I sometimes wish I could. I have thoughts, I’ve got a thousand little thoughts swirling around all the time, but they get tangled up in my head. And when I try to talk, my brain shuts down or I can’t find the words or I get all flustered. Why can’t I just express what I’m feeling? It feels like there’s some invisible barrier between me and everyone else. Social media makes it worse; I scroll and see everyone living their best lives, and I feel even more isolated. It’s like I’m watching a movie where I’m the character that no one cares about. I wish I could make plans spontaneously, like grab lunch with someone without worrying about whether they actually want to hang out or if they’re just being nice. My mind does this annoying loop of overthinking. Maybe they’d prefer to be with someone else who’s more fun or exciting? I feel like I’m the boring one in the room. It’s embarrassing to admit how much I actually enjoy just sitting with a book or binge-watching some show. No pressure. It’s like a safe little zone where I can just exist without that nagging feeling like I need to contribute or be 'on' all the time. But then I hate that I’m missing out on connection too. It’s a double-edged sword. I should probably text one of my friends, but even that feels daunting. What should I say? “Hey, wanna hang out?” Sounds so generic, right? And if they say no I’ll feel crushed. Or worse, what if they don’t reply? Instant rejection. It’s all so silly but it weighs on me. I need to learn to take life less seriously. Maybe next time I’ll just throw caution to the wind and say yes to everything. But then, that’s the neurotic part of me – always thinking about the consequences of every little decision. What did I even say before? It’s exhausting just thinking about it. Maybe I should just embrace the chaos instead of trying to control everything. But how? If only I had a manual for how to navigate life without it crushing my spirit. I guess we’ll see what this week brings, I’ll just keep holding on tight and hoping for the best.

Thinking about that awkward conversation I had yesterday. I always feel like I'm fumbling over my words. Socializing is dreadful sometimes. Why do people feel the need to fill silence with small talk? Just let it be quiet, you know? But then I can feel the pressure rising, like everyone's waiting for me to say something, anything, and I panic. My mind goes blank. I think I disassociate, like I'm watching myself from the outside, and by the time I snap back in, the moment's passed and I've lost my chance. Was it even worth it to try? Probably not. I don't care about the football scores or the latest Netflix series — I just want to talk about, I don't know, existential dread or the meaning of life or the weird dream I had last night where I was in a room full of mirrors and they all showed different versions of me, like a funhouse but deeply unsettling. That would be a way better convo starter than asking about the weather. I keep thinking everyone else has it figured out. They smile, they laugh, they effortlessly seem to connect. Meanwhile, I’m just... here. Tucked in the corner, scrolling through Twitter, absorbing all the world’s chaos like a sponge while internally I’m freaking out about how to engage. It’s a strange paradox — my mind is always racing with ideas and thoughts and what-ifs, but when it comes to voicing them, they just vanish like smoke. I like weird stuff, I do. I can get lost in documentaries about the ocean or the bizarre depths of human history, and it’s fascinating, but then I feel a bit like an alien when trying to share that excitement. People don’t want to know how many jellyfish are in the world or why we should care about the extinction of species. They want to talk about their brunch plans. It's not like I'm heartless. I do care. I care about injustice, I care about climate change, I care about how we’re all walking a tightrope called existence. But the way I express that is often... not very approachable. Like, I can think about a million ways to change the world, but I just end up sitting at home scrolling through my thoughts and avoiding eye contact with people. Maybe I’m too open? I think about everything but struggle to sort through it. I want to create, but I can’t seem to put pen to paper or fingers to keyboard unless it’s in a free-fall of inspiration, which is rare. When I do create, it’s often a mess — rambling poems or abstract paintings that only make sense to me, or maybe they don’t even make sense to me. I’m not sure if that’s good or bad. I can't help but feel envious sometimes. Not in a toxic way, just… wishing I could flip a switch and be that chill person who’s laid-back and social. I know it’s not all sunshine and rainbows on their side either. I mean, everyone has their struggles, right? But when I think about friendships, I always end up with this weird knot in my stomach. How do you maintain connections? It’s like a full-time job I didn’t sign up for. I overthink every interaction. Did I say the right thing? Did I laugh too loud? Is my face making an awkward expression? Should I text them later? Maybe I’ll just wait and see if they reach out first. I keep waiting. And then there’s the anxiety. Oh boy, that’s a fun ride, huh? It’s like living in a live horror show where I’m the main character, but I can’t figure out the script, and I’m convinced it’s all going downhill. My mind gets stuck on loops, like a record scratching. I mean, I’ll lie in bed replaying conversations or worrying about plans I made three weeks ago like they’re life-or-death situations. Breathe, I keep telling myself. Just breathe. What’s the worst that can happen? You say the wrong thing and it’s awkward? You can survive awkwardness, right? I think I just need to learn to be okay with being uncomfortable. But why is that so hard? Sometimes I wish I could just take a break from thinking. Like, what would it be like to just be present and not analyze everything? It’s exhausting. I know I’ve got this unique lens through which I view the world and yeah, I guess it can be beautiful. The colors, the stories, the hidden meanings woven into everything around me. But it’s like standing in front of a Picasso painting with my nose pressed against the glass and nobody else seems to see the chaos or beauty or depth I see. Maybe that’s why I struggle to connect — I’m feeling too much while everyone else is just trying to vibe. I guess I’ll keep navigating this maze of thoughts, feelings, and social interactions. It’s all a matter of trial and error, right? Maybe one day I’ll find my groove, or maybe I’ll just continue this awkward dance. Who knows? Just keep going, I suppose.

I really should start organizing my bookshelf again, it's a mess. I like things to be tidy, but I keep putting it off. There’s something calming about having everything in its place. I wonder if it’s the same for other people, or if they just don’t care as much. I remember when I used to rearrange my room every month, like it was some kind of ritual to keep things fresh. Even now, when I reorganize my desk and find little forgotten trinkets, it feels like I’m rediscovering pieces of myself. Speaking of discoveries, I'm really into that documentary series about the ocean. It's fascinating how much we don’t know about the depths. It’s kind of comforting, the idea that there’s still so much left to explore, even if I personally will probably never dive into the Mariana Trench. Okay, but back to my bookshelf. I should definitely finish reading that novel I started last month. I loved it at first but then got distracted by another one—classic me. I tend to pick up multiple books at once and then it’s like, which one do I even finish first? I like the feeling of having options, but sometimes it feels overwhelming. It’s like being at that enormous buffet and instead of eating, I just end up wandering around, overwhelmed by choices. I read somewhere that having too many options can actually lead to decision fatigue, which is so true. I think I might be getting better at narrowing choices down though. I guess I’ve learned a lot about prioritizing what truly interests me versus what I feel obligated to read. I’ve been thinking a lot about how I connect with people too. It’s definitely a challenge sometimes, not because I don’t want to, but because I prefer depth over breadth. Small talk feels pointless to me, and I know a lot of people thrive on it. They joke about the weather and the latest trends, but I’d rather dive into a discussion about philosophical concepts or travel experiences. There’s something so enriching about sharing pieces of life rather than surface-level details. But then, I also wonder if I come off as intimidating or overly serious to others. Am I missing out because I avoid those casual chats? Sometimes I worry about it, but I guess that’s just how I am. I don’t want to change that part of myself too much. Authenticity is important—having integrity means a lot to me. Thinking about being authentic makes me reflect on my friendships. My closest friends—aren’t those relationships just the best? I appreciate how easy we can communicate; it feels natural. We can just sit together in silence, but also dive into the deep stuff without any awkwardness. I like the way they challenge my ideas too, and we push each other to think differently. And we can share our vulnerabilities. That’s different from what I see in more casual relationships. People tend to avoid getting deep too quickly, which is fair. It takes time to build trust, but man when you do, it’s like, you can truly be yourself! I often think about how important it is to be open to new experiences. Like that time we spontaneously decided to go hiking last spring. I wasn’t feeling particularly social that day, but the fresh air and beautiful scenery were rejuvenating. Sometimes, I find that stepping out of my comfort zone—even just a tiny bit—leads to wonderful moments. But it has to feel right, like the spontaneous adventure aligns with my interests. If it doesn’t, I feel drained afterward. It’s interesting how that works. But too much planning can also feel stifling. It's a balancing act, really. When I travel, I like to have a loose itinerary but allow space for spontaneity. I think that makes the best memories—those unexpected moments, like running into a street festival or stumbling upon a quaint little café.
