I love trying new foods when I travel too—food is such an intimate experience, a way to connect with culture, isn't it? I remember once in Italy, I had that homemade pasta, and it was like nothing I had ever experienced. It made me appreciate the art and love that goes into cooking. Now that I’m reflecting on travel, I think about how important it is to embrace different cultures. Each place has its unique vibe and history. I want to learn as much as I can. I feel like there’s so much beauty in diversity, and the more I understand others, the more it enriches my own perspective. I wonder if that’s why I enjoy documentaries so much—not just the ocean ones, but anything that broadens my understanding of the world around me. There’s a sense of responsibility in it, too; it’s not just for entertainment, but an opportunity to grow. Okay, I’ve rambled long enough. Time to tackle the bookshelf—I’ll start with the top shelf. Maybe I’ll find that novel I put down and finally finish it after all.

okay so i was just thinking about how every time i see my roommate leave the apartment i wonder what they’re going to do out there like do they have plans or are they just going to grab something from the store but then they always come back with random snacks and i think we should just get groceries together it would be easier or maybe i’ll just let them keep bringing back popcorn and ice cream it’s fine i like it too but then i worry about how much junk food we have in the house sometimes i think about eating healthier but it’s so much work to figure out recipes and plus i don’t really want to force myself to cook all the time but i do want to be healthier i guess i just don’t care enough to make a big deal about it like that time i tried meal prepping and it just ended with a whole bunch of weird rubbery chicken that i don’t want to eat at all or the time i made a kale salad that tasted like grass and that put me off greens for so long just a few leaves here and there is fine i mean i eat them sometimes but it’s not as if i’m going to turn vegan or anything even if it seems like that’s what everyone else is doing now i just don’t have the energy to keep up with that friend of mine who’s always talking about her new diet fads like she’s really into that intermittent fasting thing and it’s all so confusing it’s too much pressure maybe she makes it work but i don’t want to skip breakfast or whatever they’re calling it and how do you even intermittent fast when you hang out with friends or go out to eat like i’d rather just enjoy the meal without worrying about when i can have my next one it feels like people are always focused on their bodies and their food i get it but sometimes it’s just exhausting like i went to a party the other weekend and everyone was talking about their workout routines and comparing who could do more push-ups or burpees and it was just so much i felt like the odd one out sitting there with my sparkling water like yeah i go for walks sometimes but that’s about it why do i even go to those parties i’m not really sure i think i should try to enjoy them more but it’s just a lot of small talk and it feels surface level like sure we can talk about the new movie that everyone is watching or the latest TikTok trend but it never feels deep enough and that’s what i crave sometimes you know like i want to dig into real conversations but then i don’t want to be the one to start it and risk making things awkward so i end up just nodding along like yeah that sounds cool or whatever it makes me think about how i used to have this group in high school where we’d actually discuss things that mattered like we were so idealistic and passionate about changing the world or whatever and now it’s just about who’s dating who or what’s trending on social media everyone seems more interested in likes and follows than actual friendships or something real and maybe that’s why i tend to stick to a few close friends instead of branching out i feel bad about it sometimes but the few friends i have mean a lot to me so the idea of trying to make new ones feels like climbing a mountain every time and i like just hanging out talking about random stuff without any pressure to perform i remember this one time we spent the whole day just watching old cartoons and eating pizza and it felt so perfect like we didn’t have to pretend to be anything we just laughed and enjoyed being together sometimes i wonder if i should try to push myself out of my comfort zone but then i remember every time i’ve tried it’s like jumping into cold water and i just freeze up and want to leap back out it’s not worth the anxiety of putting myself in a position to feel uncomfortable but is it really that bad if i’m missing out on new experiences i guess it can be hard to tell what’s worth it and what’s not maybe i’m the kind of person who is fine with the familiar and routine but then my friends are always saying that life is too short to play it safe and i sit there thinking maybe they’re right maybe i’m missing out on something great but what if i go to that new coffee shop and it’s just the same old coffee and a bunch of hipsters staring at their laptops like is that really something i want to deal with and then what if they start talking about their art or their projects and i have nothing to contribute like i can barely paint my nails without getting polish everywhere and it feels like there’s a barrier between us sometimes so i just stick to what i know and maybe that’s okay too i don’t have to force myself to like things that everyone else does but maybe i should try to find some balance somewhere like maybe i can be okay with my comfort zone while also allowing myself those few little adventures here and there like maybe just trying a new flavor of ice cream next time or something simple not so intimidating just small steps maybe then i can learn to be a little bit less cautious and a little more open to whatever comes next.

I can't believe I forgot to pick up that package from the post office. Ugh, I hate when I mess up simple things like that. The weekend is coming though, which means I can finally hang out with my friends again. I thrive on that energy—everyone laughing and screaming and just being loud. It's like this beautiful chaos. But then there's that nagging voice in my head, you know? What if they don’t like what I said last time? I can’t stop thinking about how I interrupted Tara when she was talking about her vacation. I feel terrible about it, and now I’m working up all these scenarios in my head about how she might be mad at me. It’s just awful. But who cares, right? I mean, life can be messy and why hold back? I want to try new things, new places, and I just want to be spontaneous, jump in a car and drive somewhere crazy like the beach or an amusement park or even just a random diner at 2 AM. I can feel the thrill, like my heart is racing just thinking about it. Eating greasy burgers in the middle of nowhere, that’s the vibe I’m talking about. I live for these moments, but then there’s that other side where I’m just overthinking everything. It’s like a roller coaster, and I love that rush but the climb just makes me scream even more. What if I just text everyone and say “road trip” and see who bites? Why do I even care what people think of me? I mean, that’s the point, isn’t it? Being around people you vibe with and just having a good time? Still, I can’t shake that thing where I worry about what they'll say when I’m not there. I have to stop being so paranoid. Like yesterday, I said something off-the-cuff about how I think pineapple on pizza is a crime against humanity and for a solid ten minutes I worried if I hurt someone’s feelings. Who even has strong opinions about pizza toppings? How did I end up in this situation? Why does everyone always want to please each other? I just want to be real. Real is hard and weird, and I like weird. I’d rather offend someone with my weirdness than pretend to be nice and bland. And speaking of weird, I was watching this documentary about people who collect bizarre stuff… like those antique medical tools or creepy dolls. It’s fascinating. They live in their own worlds and I’m here for it. I want to explore all that. I wanna go hang out with those oddball collectors and hear their stories, even if it's a little cringe. Who cares? Maybe I could find a way to make money off it, though I can barely keep my own space clean. It’s a mess in my room actually. Clothes everywhere, old takeout containers. I should clean it, but it feels so overwhelming. I can’t seem to focus long enough on any one task to actually do anything about it. Why am I like this? Maybe I just need to set a timer and blast some music. Oh, I love music! It’s my escape. I can crank up some pop or throw it back with some classic rock, singing along like I’m a superstar. I wish there was a way to make my life feel like a music video. Just give me a catchy beat and a good filming crew, and I’d be out there dancing in the street, getting people to join in. But then there’s that part of me that tells me to settle down. Maybe I should find a steady job or something. My parents always have that “what are you going to do with your life” talk. I mean, it’s a valid question but also, like, chill out. Life is unpredictable, and I crave that variety. I want to embrace whatever randomness comes my way. But I also don’t want to end up broke and living in my parents’ basement forever. I’d like to travel. So many places to see, different cultures to experience. I think about food a lot, like street food in Thailand or pastries in Paris. I just want to immerse myself in everything. But then I get stressed thinking about the logistics. Saving all that money, booking flights, planning itineraries. Maybe I’m just not cut out for it. Or maybe I’m just at the mercy of this anxiety that keeps me from being as free as I want to be. I need to stop worrying about the future, right? It’s exhausting. Just live in the moment. But I can’t help but leap to the “what ifs.” What if I miss out? What if I get left behind? What if? What if? I just need a breather and to recharge, but it feels impossible with racing thoughts. Where’s the off switch? Ah, well, whatever. I guess I’ll figure it out. Or maybe I won’t. Who knows?

I can’t believe that last week I actually said that to my professor. Just blurted it out in front of the whole class. No filter. I mean, who does that? I swear, I have to find a way to keep my mouth shut sometimes. But I can’t help it! The thoughts just come rushing out like a flood, and it feels like my brain is a crowded subway car during rush hour. Sometimes it’s exhilarating, other times it’s like, oh gosh, why did I say that. I was thinking of this movie quote that just popped into my head about being true to yourself, but then I just sounded rude instead of witty. Like if you don’t get me, maybe it’s because I’m not expressing it right. Or maybe I just need to tone it down when I’m around people who don’t get my humor or my directness. But then again, do I even want to? Who cares? There’s too much political correctness going around anyway. Seriously, let’s just tell it like it is, like, I wish people were more upfront. Why the hell do we have to tiptoe around feelings? My friend was explaining this new app she downloaded that helps people craft polite messages. Like, why can’t people just be honest? But maybe I’m too much. I always feel like I’m on the edge of chaos. I love being around people. Parties, clubs, whatever. You throw me in a crowd, and I thrive. But then my insides start twisting because what if they don’t talk to me, what if they ignore me? My heart races just thinking about it. I mean, I deep dive into social situations but I keep worrying if I’m too loud or too overbearing. Did I make someone uncomfortable? Compliments just spill out of me like I’m the world’s biggest cheerleader, but I can feel those judgmental looks sometimes and it makes my stomach turn. When I go out, I can't help but engage with everyone, but then there's this voice in my head telling me to chill out. I go from zero to a hundred, and it feels like a ride but then I crash afterward. The next day is a hangover of anxiety and regret, replaying every conversation, questioning every word. I don’t see how people can just breeze through social settings. Am I the only one who feels like I’m on a tightrope trying to balance between being friendly and not stepping on toes? I mean, I care but I don’t care. Is that even possible? It feels impossible sometimes. I throw myself into my work and projects, though. That’s where my conscientiousness kicks in. I make lists because lists make sense. But what if I forget one thing on the list? That small error sends me spiraling. I need to be the best, but the best at what? I don’t know. I have so many interests and hobbies, I’m really open to anything. I want to learn everything, but it’s overwhelming. I signed up for a pottery class and then a coding bootcamp, and I still need to learn guitar. Can you imagine trying to juggle all that? My head spins thinking about it, but I can feel that rush of excitement too. Creativity is everywhere, I love it. But then there’s that fear of missing out. If I don’t try all these things, will I regret it later? Like my friend who only focuses on her major and is always stressed. I can’t be like that. I need vibrant chaos in my life. But deadlines haunt me too. I’m constantly checking my planner, making sure I hit my marks. I’m the kind of person who would rather stay up late just to finish a project than risk handing something in that’s less than perfect. The anxiety starts creeping in again, my mind racing with “What if I fail? What if it’s not good enough? What if I can't keep up with everything I’m trying to juggle?” I even got up at 4 AM the other day to work on a project that was due next week. Why do I do that to myself? It's almost masochistic. But then that sense of accomplishment—the adrenaline rush when I hit send or submit is worth it, right? That fleeting high moment. But it evaporates so quickly. I can win an argument with anyone but then be left second-guessing my opinions afterward. I could walk into a room and light it up like a holiday party but come home alone and wonder if I really connected with anyone. Is this all just noise? Am I making a difference? Why do I even seek out these chaotic situations, knowing how they might affect me later? But I can’t resist the pull of socializing, the thrill of meeting new people, the spark of creativity that comes from engaging with life. It’s exhausting and invigorating all at once. I’m like a butterfly flapping my wings in a hurricane, and somehow, I still wouldn’t change it for the world.

That plant over there is so green it almost looks like it’s glowing. I wonder if it needs watering. I should probably check but I don't want to get up. Is it weird that I talk to my plants? I think they're great listeners. I bet they have so many stories to tell if they could talk. Maybe I should write a story from the perspective of a houseplant. There’s also that little red pen I left on my desk. I keep getting distracted by shiny objects, like that pen, and I start doodling instead of studying but doodling is kind of underrated. I mean, they say it helps with memory, right? I can remember some of my best ideas came while I was just doodling a little flower or something. Thinking about my friends, there’s this energy that buzzes when we’re all together. We get coffee and it feels like the whole world shrinks down to just us. We laugh so loud that people probably think we’re obnoxious. There’s that one time we were at that little café by campus, and I spilled a whole drink on the table. I got so embarrassed, but they laughed and made it a whole thing like “oh look, here comes the human waterfall.” It was messy but hilarious. I love how my friends can turn a simple mistake into this funny memory. I should text them after this and see if they want to meet up later. I always want to do things, explore new places like that taco truck that just opened up down the street. I’ve heard it’s the best in town, and I want to see for myself if the hype is real. Then there’s that project due next week. Ugh, group work always feels like herding cats. Everyone thinks they can lead but no one wants to take the minutes. I like organizing things and making lists, so naturally, I end up being the one who writes everything down. It’s kind of fun to align everyone’s thoughts, though. I like getting people excited about the project. Once we started talking about what we were going to do, it was like lights going off and we all found our rhythm. Teamwork can be really wonderful when everyone is on the same page. Collaboration really brings out the best ideas. I’ve been thinking about how people can be so different. Some just blend in, and others light up the room. I like talking to everyone, even the quiet ones. Everyone has something interesting to say if you bother to ask. They give me energy, and I get so excited hearing their stories. I remember chatting with this guy in my class who’s super into bird watching. I never knew how passionate people could be about birds. It's fascinating! Just the thought of spending hours with binoculars in the forest listening to them chirp, it’s peaceful in a way, and he loves it. Also, I can’t get that new song out of my head. It’s catchy but a little repetitive, but I don’t mind. Some songs just hit differently, and you can’t help but sing along. I’ve started making playlists for everything in my life. Studying, exercising, hanging out with friends, cooking, cleaning. There’s something so therapeutic about curating music. It’s like creating little snippets of memory through sound. Or sometimes when I’m feeling introspective, I listen to music that has deep lyrics and it gets me thinking. I love how music can transport you to different moments. And why is it that every time I think about going to bed at a reasonable hour, I end up scrolling through my phone for hours instead? Social media can be such a rabbit hole. I can see where my friends are traveling, see all these cute dogs and babies. The joy of dog videos is undeniable. I love sharing things with people that make them happy. Just the other day, I forwarded this funny meme to a group chat and everyone lit up. Instant happiness, right there. Oh, and cooking! I made this really strange pasta dish last week using whatever I could find in the fridge. It was a mix of random vegetables, spices, and some leftover chicken. I should have taken a picture because it looked like art. It tasted great too! They say cooking is an art, and I really believe that. You can express yourself in so many ways with the colors and flavors. Sometimes, I just throw things together based on what I’m feeling at the moment. Cooking feels like a mix of science and creativity. Alright, I think I’ve rambled on long enough. This feels like a stream of thought that just won’t stop. It’s like sitting on a rollercoaster of ideas zooming from one to the next. I love this feeling of flow where I just let everything pour out. I should write more like this, it feels freeing. I should definitely keep doing this sort of thing. There’s so much to explore in life, and it’s all around me.

The clock on the wall says 3:17 PM and I’m thinking about how time slips away from me. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to be productive but then I just end up spiraling into my own thoughts, which is kind of exhausting but also comforting in a weird way. Why does it feel easier to focus on all the things that make me anxious instead of the stuff I actually need to do? I could probably clean my room right now but then I’d get distracted by old notebooks or a random book I never finished reading. Books are funny like that. They sit there like unfinished puzzles, inviting me to take a look, but the thought of not finishing them makes my heart race. Like why didn’t I read them all the way through? Am I just bad at committing? Maybe it’s more that I’m just drawn to new things all the time, like every new idea or concept feels so exciting while the old stuff just kind of gathers dust in the back of my mind. I keep thinking about conversations I had last week. Social situations feel like a minefield sometimes, and I avoid big groups because they drain me. I mean, I’m friendly once I engage, but there’s this overwhelming fear of saying the wrong thing, feeling judged, or just not being interesting enough. I’d rather hide in a bookshop or a quiet café with my thoughts than navigate that chatter. I can still remember the awkward pause when I tried to make a joke, and nobody laughed. It’s almost like I replay that moment over and over again, like a bad song stuck in my head. So embarrassing. Why am I like this? It shouldn’t matter, and yet it feels like everything. It’s like I’m missing some social manual that everyone else has read. Also, I have this friend who’s super extroverted. She thrives in those chaotic environments and her energy is infectious, like, I admire her so much. But it makes me feel even more anxious because I’m always trying to keep up. And when I can’t, I just retreat into myself, wondering if I’m letting her down or if she’ll think less of me. I know she wouldn’t, but my mind spirals. I wish I could carry that energy with me, just a spark of it, to feel buoyant instead of this heaviness that sometimes comes with my thoughts. There’s something comforting in knowing she’s there to pull me out of my shell when I need it, even if it’s painful to admit that I need that. Sometimes I feel like I’m on this tightrope of feelings. I want to help people and be there for them. Agreeableness is supposed to be a good thing, right? But it also means I put their needs ahead of my own way too often. I have a hard time saying no. I’m not sure if I’m helping or hurting them in the long run. Is it okay to prioritize myself for once? Just last week, I agreed to help a classmate with a project even though I had my own deadlines to meet. I think I just want to be seen as helpful and nice, but it always comes back to bite me. I end up stressed and overwhelmed and really it just feels like I’m giving pieces of myself away without a second thought. My mind jumps to all these weird dreams I’ve had lately. The other night, I was trapped in this infinite library, every book was calling to me, all these stories I felt this intense need to explore but never could pick one. It’s like I want to dive into everything and also run away from it all at the same time. Talk about conflicting feelings. I woke up feeling frantic and disheartened because I couldn’t even remember the titles of those books. I wonder if that means something, like those stories are potential paths I could take, or maybe it's just my mind playing tricks. I just wish I could find a balance between taking risks and playing it safe. It’s funny how I crave new experiences and yet they terrify me at the same time. I want to travel, meet new people, try different foods, but my brain keeps throwing excuses in my face—money, time, what if I get lost? I would hate to feel out of my depth, but part of me craves that adventure, that feeling of being alive. It’s like I’m an open book bursting with ideas but stuck in a single chapter. There’s something both exhilarating and scary about that. My thoughts loop again, who am I, what do I want? Amidst all this chaos, I guess I’m just trying to figure it all out one day at a time, even if I’m a bit of a mess.

I keep thinking about that email I need to send but it’s such a hassle just to think about it. What if they think I’m annoying? I feel like every time I reach out to someone, it’s just me pushing my own agenda. Like today in class when everyone was talking about the group project, and all I could think was, “Why can’t they just get to the point?” It was just too much chatter for me. I mean who has the time to sit around and flail in discussions that go nowhere? What if I just sent a message saying “let’s do this part” instead of dragging it out? But then what if they think I’m not a team player? Ugh. Just stick to the plan, that’s what I need to do. I make lists for everything, every little detail that needs to get done. My planner is practically my lifeblood. I find comfort in it. I like seeing everything laid out, organized, it calms the storm in my head. I keep checking my phone even though I know there’s nothing there. I hate when it buzzes and it’s a useless notification. I wish I could just turn it off sometimes but then what if someone actually needs to reach me? I can’t ignore even the random text from that one guy in my class who's always asking about notes—like, I don’t take notes for your convenience. It’s irritating how some people just expect you to be available all the time. I wonder what it would feel like to just not care. To just shut off that part of my brain that feels the need to respond. But alas, here I am, an anxious wreck wondering about every single little social interaction. Oh, and that coffee shop down the street? I don’t get why it’s so popular. People crowding in, standing in line, all of them talking loudly. It’s just noise, noise, noise. I’d much rather have my coffee in silence, at home where it’s calm. I don’t have to worry about strangers. But I know I can’t just isolate myself forever. I need to get out more. Maybe join a club or something? But then what if it’s too much? What if I have to talk to people? It’s exhausting just thinking about it. I feel like everyone is so carefree, but I’m over here dissecting every word I say. It’s like I’m living in some constant state of worry. Social situations are like a minefield and I’m just trying to avoid stepping on anything that might blow up in my face. Maybe I should take a break from everything. Just focus on my studies, hit the books harder. That’s something I can control. Grades are black and white; you either do well or you don’t. It makes sense. The professors don’t care about small talk. They want results, and so do I. It’s satisfying when I cross things off my list. But then again, what if I don’t do well? What if I miss something important? I can’t help but second-guess myself. Did I study the right things? Am I prepared enough? Then there’s the whole issue of spending time with family. They keep inviting me over like it’s some sort of grand event just to sit around and talk about nothing. I love them, but I can’t stand the small talk. It’s like I’m suffocating in a bubble of forced conversation. I just want to be honest and say, “Can we skip the pleasantries?” But that wouldn’t go well. They wouldn’t understand. They think I’m just being difficult. But I’m not. I just want to get to the heart of the matter without all the fluff. Why do people like fluff? I don’t want to hear about the neighbor’s cat or how the weather is changing. Just give me real topics or give me silence. People say I should just relax and go with the flow, but how can I? Every little thing feels like an ordeal. I’m always waiting for the next shoe to drop. What if I forgot an assignment? What if everything falls apart? Sometimes I feel like I’m a cog in a machine that’s going to break at any moment. And other people just keep churning along, not a care in the world. It’s maddening, really. I wish I could just have one day where I don’t second-guess myself. Where I just wake up, hit the ground running, and don’t stop to think about what could go wrong. But that sounds too easy, doesn’t it? It’s hard to let go of that control. I feel safer when I have my lists and plans, and it’s scary to think about stepping away from that. But what’s the alternative? To keep doing this? It feels like a hamster wheel and I can’t find a way off.

Okay so I was just sitting here thinking about how weird it is that everyone keeps saying you can’t have it all but what even is “it all” anyway? I mean I wanna be spontaneous and do all the things, but sometimes I feel like I’m just this whirlwind of thoughts that never really settles down. Like the other day, I talked to a stranger in line at the coffee shop for way too long. She probably thought I was nuts but honestly, who cares? I love meeting new people, but then I get home and my brain just rides this rollercoaster of anxiety and overthinking. Why did I say that? Was that too much? Did I seem too over-the-top? Ugh, why is life like this? I scroll through social media and see everyone else living their best lives, and I’m like, am I doing enough? Why are they all so happy? And then there’s this guy I met at a party last weekend. Super outgoing, like he just lit up the whole room when he entered. He ended up chatting with me about conspiracy theories and how the moon landing was staged. I mean, I wasn’t really into that topic, but his energy was infectious. I could have listened to him forever. I laughed so hard I almost spilled my drink. I think I might message him, but what if he thinks I’m weird or too intense? But then again, isn’t that kind of charming? I think it's charming when people go for it and don’t worry. But you know what’s crazy? My brain doesn't let me just chill after those moments. I replay everything in my head. What if I looked stupid? What if he doesn't text back? What if I embarrass myself? Sometimes I think it’s exhausting to be me. I feel like I’m on a constant high, but it comes crashing down when I’m back in my room. I mean, I could go out again, but sometimes the idea of getting ready feels like climbing a mountain. I think, where’s my motivation? But I don’t want to be stuck here. I want to run around and try everything, but I also want to take a nap because my brain is like, hey, you’re too much right now. Last week I went to that art exhibit and it was insane. The colors, the vibes, everything just meshed. They had this interactive piece where you could paint on the wall, and I just threw myself into it. My hands got messy, and I didn’t care. At that moment, it was liberating. But of course, afterwards, I freaked out about who saw me being such a weirdo. What made me think I could just create art on a wall when I can barely draw a stick figure? Ah, but the energy of the place! I was floating, just being free but then came crashing down again – who’s gonna judge me? People always do. Maybe being vulnerable isn’t for me. Too much is at stake, right? Then again, wouldn’t it be awesome to travel to a place no one’s ever heard of? Like, I want to get lost in a city and meet people who are just as lost, and we can figure it out together. But then I start thinking about planning. Planning is so boring though. What if I just took off one day? But hold on, what if I miss something important back home? I mean, my friends are super supportive but sometimes I feel like I just don’t fit in. They talk about their jobs and futures and I’m just thinking about how I want to backpack through Europe – but what’s the plan? I am so not good at planning. And speaking of fitting in, I can’t stand when people are too nice. Like, just be real with me. I get that being agreeable helps with friendships and stuff, but let’s cut the niceness sometimes. If someone thinks my shirt is ugly, just say it! I can take it, or maybe I won’t. But maybe I need that honesty. I want genuine connections, not just surface-level compliments. It’s hard to find the balance. Sometimes I think I should just embrace all the chaos, like, who cares what happens if I do something crazy? I don’t want to live in a box, but I don’t want to spiral out of control either. My brain just keeps going – it never stops. It’s like a never-ending playlist of thoughts that I can’t turn off. But that’s what makes me me, right? If I didn’t have this intense energy, would I be this fun and quirky person everyone seems to like? I just want to be the loudest one in the room and also have moments where I can sit quietly and stare into space. Okay, so maybe being a whirlwind is tiring, but I can’t imagine being any other way.

Okay so I was thinking about how much I love spontaneous road trips, it’s like the best thing ever. Just grab a few friends and hit the highway, music blasting, windows down. Nothing like the wind in your hair and the open road ahead, right? I really need to plan one soon, maybe to the coast or the mountains? I love being outdoors and exploring new places. Hiking trails, beach bonfires — oh man, I could go on forever. There's something about discovering new paths that gets me super energized. And then there’s food, food is such a big part of any trip. Like we could stop at that roadside diner I saw on Instagram, the one with the huge burgers and those milkshakes that are practically a meal in themselves. What’s better than sharing a giant slice of pie with friends? Awesome conversations and laughter over something delicious. Sharing is caring, and every meal is so much more enjoyable when you can pass things around the table. Speaking of food, I was just thinking about how much I’ve been trying to cook lately. I’ve been experimenting with new recipes. Last week, I made this crazy pasta dish with spinach and sun-dried tomatoes. It turned out amazing; I was honestly shocked at my own skills. I never would’ve guessed I could cook something that good, but it felt so rewarding. I guess following those cooking videos really paid off. And the best part is inviting friends over to share it. The more the merrier, right? It’s like I can’t get enough of those positive vibes that come with gathering everyone together. Oh, and I’ve been volunteering at that local shelter, which has been so fulfilling. It feels good to give back, to show up and help people. There’s such a sense of community there, which is totally my jam. I love meeting new people and hearing their stories. Everyone comes from different backgrounds and experiences, and I think that’s so fascinating. This one guy told me about his travels through Europe, and it totally inspired me to start planning my own trip there someday. The world is so big and there’s so much to see and do. And then there’s music! I can’t even begin to explain how much I love music. It’s like an instant mood booster. I’ve been on a huge indie rock kick lately, discovering new bands and going to local shows. There’s something magical about live music, the energy in the crowd, the connection you feel with strangers just enjoying the same experience. I remember this one concert I went to last month, everyone was singing along, and it felt like we were all one big family for those couple of hours. I just live for those moments. I’m also really into trying out new activities. I joined a dance class recently — I’ve always loved to dance but never really took formal lessons. It’s been intimidating, but also so much fun. I love when we do those group activities, everyone’s cheering each other on, and it just feels safe to be yourself. I think everyone should try dancing, or something that scares them a little bit. It’s like a little push outside of your comfort zone, and it’s always worth it. Oh, did I mention I’m planning a book club? Yeah, I got a bunch of friends together, and we’re going to read a mix of fiction and nonfiction. I’m really excited about it! I love discussing ideas and themes after reading something thought-provoking. It’s like peeling back layers and getting to know deeper sides of each other. Plus, I’ve got some titles in mind that I think everyone will enjoy. It’s perfect — an excuse to sit around, drink wine, and talk about life. It’s funny how life continues to surprise me. Just when I think I’ve settled into a routine, something new pops up. Like this weekend, I just found out that there’s a festival happening nearby! I can’t wait to go check it out! All the food trucks, artisan crafts, and live performances – it feels like a little slice of joy! I’m definitely dragging my friends along. It’s a great opportunity to meet new people and just soak in the fun atmosphere. I wonder if I’ll ever settle down anywhere or if I’ll always be chasing the next adventure. Sometimes I think about that, but the thought of living in a new city excites me way more than any kind of permanence. Life is about experiences, isn’t it? Connecting with others, exploring the world, just diving into everything it has to offer. I really can’t wait to see what’s next.

The clock says 3:02 PM and I can’t believe how fast this week has gone by. I mean, seriously, it feels like I just blinked and here we are. A friend of mine texted me about going to that new taco place tonight and I’m super into it because, tacos. And I can’t say no to tacos. Food is love, right? But also there’s this whole aspect of meeting new people or reconnecting with old friends that just lights me up, like, I thrive in social situations. That buzz is just something else. I wonder how many people will be there. Last time it was just a small group but this time it seems like it’s going to be bigger. Bigger is always better, isn’t it? More food, more laughter, more stories to tell. I love hearing people’s stories; everyone has a unique experience. A canvas, really, of who they are. I’ve been thinking about how much I enjoy spontaneous plans. Like when someone just says, “Hey, let’s go do this!” And I’m like, “Okay, let’s do it!” Just the thrill of not knowing what’s going to happen next gives me a rush. Yesterday, I went hiking with a group I met at the gym. Totally last minute. Who knew those trails were so amazing? The view was breathtaking; you could see the whole valley below, the colors were vibrant like an oil painting. I’m the type who’ll stop mid-hike to take a million pictures because every angle is just so stunning. Plus, I was making new friends on the trail. It’s funny how simple things like hiking can turn into these deep conversations about life goals and dreams. Oh, and I found these cool rocks that looked like some kind of quartz or something? Everyone was interested in them. I just love sharing things I find; it feels great to connect over little discoveries. I think being open to those kinds of experiences makes life more vibrant. I mean, what’s the point if you’re not discovering new things or trying to learn from others? Speaking of which, I’ve been meaning to dive into that new fantasy series everyone’s raving about. Seriously, how do people find the time to read all those books? I get caught up in social events and gatherings, but I love getting lost in a fantasy world too. Those epic journeys, the characters, the twists, it’s like living a different life. Sometimes I wish I could just disappear into a book for a while and get lost in that imaginative realm. Back to real life though, I’m planning an art night at my place next week. Just some watercolor painting, snacks, and good vibes. I invited a mixed bag of friends and it's going to be so chill. Like, some of them are super artsy and others just want to catch up. I can’t wait to see what everyone creates. There’s something incredibly liberating about playing with colors and just letting your creativity flow without the pressure of perfection. I remember this one time, I made something that looked like a toddler’s mess and yet everyone thought it was so cool. Like, no rules, just pure fun. I can hear the laughter already as we spill paint everywhere. How does anyone live without that kind of joy? I don’t get the whole, “I prefer to stay in” mentality. I mean, staying in is great for some downtime, but there’s a world out there just bursting with connection. Human interaction is electric. I think about how being highly agreeable helps with that too. It’s like, I enjoy seeing others happy and I want to contribute to that. Just the simple act of smiling at someone can make their day, right? I can’t help but think about my classes next semester. I’m really into psychology this year, and oh my god, the connections between people’s emotions and their behaviors are fascinating. It’s like unlocking a puzzle. How cool is that? I find myself diving into discussions after class, analyzing everything as if we’re some sort of lab team trying to figure out the human condition. I could literally talk about this stuff for hours. Alright, focus back on what’s next. I have to prep for that taco night. Should I bring dessert? I think I will. Maybe brownies or something chocolaty? Everyone loves chocolate, right? I could even bake a whole batch and share, that would be fun. I wonder how that new guy from the gym will fit into the group. He seemed cool, kind of shy but I’ll make sure to pull him into the conversation. It's all about making connections and creating those memories.

That sandwich looks pretty good but I can’t believe it’s from the vending machine. They always get the ingredients wrong. I guess food is food. Speaking of food, I had cereal for breakfast but it was stale. Really stale. I wonder how long they keep that stuff in the back. Maybe it’s an industry secret. I mean, who cares, right? Cereal is just a filler anyway. Can’t decide if I like cornflakes or the chocolate ones better. Chocolate is too sweet sometimes. But, what am I saying? I eat it anyway. I eat whatever’s around. The choices get boring after a while. You ever go into a store and just get overwhelmed by choices? Like, a million types of toothpaste. It’s just mint, orange, or whatever fake flavor they make up. How do you even decide? Do you just pick a box because it has a shiny color? I think about that a lot, like how people choose things. I don’t get the obsession with trying new things. What’s the point? It’s easier to stick to what you know. I mean, my roommate is always trying to get me to go out. Everything is “Come on, just try it!” It’s infuriating. Why do I need to try a new restaurant when there’s a perfectly good burrito place down the street? It’s always packed but I guess people just love lines. I don’t get waiting for food. It’s food. It should be instant. My phone is really just a distraction half the time. I scroll through memes or random recipes I’ll never use. Who came up with those fancy food names anyway? “Gastrique”? Ugh. Just call it sauce. Sometimes I think about how people make friends nowadays. Is anyone really even friends? It’s all online. Texting feels lame. Like a chore. I barely text back. Just read the message and move on. I can’t imagine going out for drinks like everyone else does. It just feels pointless. What do you even talk about? “Oh, how’s your life? Oh, it’s exhausting” or “Work is killing me.” Like, who wants to exchange that kind of energy? And small talk? The worst. “Nice weather today,” “Yeah, I guess.” Who cares? Just get to the point. You either like someone or you don’t. There’s no need for a dance around it. Then there’s social media. Every now and then I log in just to see what’s happening and it’s the same endless loop of people pretending they’re happy. Eyebrow-raising stuff. Like a staged photo at a coffee shop but there’s a ring light involved. Come on, we all know it’s staged. Just drink the coffee. I don’t get the need for validation. But whatever, that’s them, not me. I can’t even pretend to be excited about my life. I don’t take pictures. It’s just not worth it. Life is pretty standard anyway, not exactly Instagram-ready. What’s so interesting about my day? I wake up, eat, maybe watch some stuff, maybe do homework. The cycle repeats. Not riveting content. Oh, my socks are mismatched. Wonder if I should care? They’re warm. That’s all that matters. Everyone talks about fashion like it’s a personality trait. “Oh, I’m super into this brand” or “I only wear vintage.” Who cares? If it fits and doesn’t smell, that’s good enough for me. People at school must think I’m weird. I wear whatever is comfortable and it feels good just to not think about it. Too many things to worry about. I can’t deal with group projects either. It’s always a mess. “Let’s all compromise,” and it turns into a circus. Why should I have to argue about the project? Just let me do my part and leave me alone. Less talking, more doing, that’s my motto. I’d rather take the hit for the grade than deal with the nonsense of group dynamics. I mean, do people really enjoy the process? It’s painful. Sometimes, I just want to sit and hear silence. Everyone seems to be rushing around, trying to fill the air with noise. Music in the background while doing homework, conversations everywhere, it’s exhausting. Sometimes I’d rather just sit and not engage. I can think better in the quiet. It’s like a weight lifts off when I don’t have to interact. I can breathe. Feels good to just exist without interruption. No one really gets that though. They think I’m anti-social or something. Maybe I am. But I just don’t feel the need to perform. People can do their thing, I’ll do mine. I guess that works for me.

This pen feels heavy in my hand, like a weight pulling down on my thoughts. I should probably be writing something coherent but here I am, just thinking about how I really don't like going to parties. Everyone's out there laughing and talking and I just sit in the corner wondering if they think I'm weird. Maybe I am weird. I hate small talk, hate how everyone seems to glide through conversations while I'm just stumbling over my own awkwardness. Last week I went to this gathering—forced myself, of course—and all the jokes were so surface-level. I wanted to delve into something real but I guess that's not what parties are for. Just a constant stream of “What do you do?” and “Where are you from?” Over and over. It’s exhausting. And who cares about the latest TikTok trend anyway? I don't get it. There's a pile of laundry in the corner of my room that I should really take care of. It's been there for days. Every time I walk past it, I feel like I should do something about it, then immediately decide that I really don’t want to. Just staring at it sends a wave of anxiety right through me. Why can’t I just be one of those people who hops out of bed and immediately tidies everything? I keep thinking about what it would be like if I was just a little less neurotic, a little less focused on the mess in my head. But that’s just not me. I can’t help but obsess over everything. My mind races all day, analyzing every little thing I say or do like I’m under a microscope. Why did I say that? Was it too much? Was it not enough? It’s like I’m constantly questioning whether everyone is just waiting for me to mess up. Speaking of mess-ups, I should probably be doing my assignments right now but I can't focus. I keep scrolling through social media even though I told myself I wouldn’t, and every time I see someone hanging out with a bunch of friends, I feel that familiar twist in my stomach. It’s this weird mix of loneliness and irritation. I should be happy for them, right? But why does it feel so alienating? I don't want to be in a huge group, I prefer being by myself but sometimes it gnaws at me, the idea that I’m missing out. Who cares though? I wouldn't even know what to say if I was there anyway, and the thought of trying makes me want to hide. This whole college experience is supposed to be about the friends you make but what if they all just see me as that quiet one? The one who blends into the background. Sometimes I think about how people see me and I hate it. I hate that feeling when someone looks at you like they want to engage but you just can’t muster the energy. Like I’d rather sit here and overthink things than attempt to engage in a conversation that could go nowhere. I don’t want to sound bitter but I do think most people are just out for themselves, always showing their shiny sides, all smiles and laughter, while I’m here just trying to keep myself together. Maybe they have it all figured out. I just wish I could be one of those people who feels free enough to express themselves. Talking to someone is always a gamble. Sometimes it goes well, but most times I just end up feeling more disconnected. Every word feels like a step on a tightrope. If I say the wrong thing, it’ll all crash down and that’s not something I’m ready to deal with. The idea of change feels so daunting. I’ve read all those self-help books that promise transformation but when it comes to actually doing something about myself, I just feel paralyzed. I know I “should” aim to be more agreeable, get involved, but what’s the point? It feels futile. It’s easier to stay in my own bubble than risk the discomfort. Maybe if I stayed quiet long enough, they would just leave me alone. Gosh, I can’t even imagine how my roommate must feel. I mean, living with someone who doesn’t talk much has to get boring. It almost feels like I’m doing her a disservice just by existing in the same space. I should reach out more, invite her to do things, but that would mean stepping out of my comfort zone. And if there’s one thing I hate, it’s being outside my bubble. Here I am, clock ticking, and I’m still stuck with these thoughts swirling around. What if I just stopped thinking and acted? Could I change? I really doubt it. Probably just gonna end up sitting here overthinking instead of taking that leap into the unknown. It’s safer to let life happen around me while I observe from the sidelines. But then again, what’s that doing for me? It’s just a spiral of thoughts leading nowhere.

Okay so I just remembered that weird dream I had last night where I was trying to find a library but ended up in a strange underground city. Like, it was all dimly lit and everyone there looked suspicious but also friendly in a way? I kept thinking, “Should I be scared or should I just go with it?” Dreams are so bizarre. Why do they always twist and turn in ways that don’t make sense? It’s like my brain throws everything into a blender and hits puree. I wonder if there’s a way to analyze dreams. There probably is, but I’m too lazy to look it up. I don’t know, maybe I should. Speaking of laziness, I really should clean my room. It’s a mess. Clothes everywhere, books piled up, some random snacks I forgot I had. Oh man, I hope those chips aren’t stale. I bought them during that late-night study binge last week when I convinced myself I could cram for that biology exam. Did I even study for it? I can’t remember. I guess my grades are okay, not great, but okay. Whatever. Sometimes I think I care too much about other people’s opinions. Like, what if my professor hates my writing? Or what if my friends judge me for not being more organized? But then again, I know I’m nice and everyone seems to like me, so that’s something, right? Yesterday was emotionally exhausting. I had that long conversation with Sarah about her relationship problems and I just wanted to be supportive but inside I was kind of panicking. I’m always worried about saying the wrong thing, you know? Like, what if my advice isn’t good enough? What if I make it worse? I think being agreeable is great in theory, but it makes everything so much heavier sometimes. I kept nodding and saying things like “I understand” and “That’s tough” while my mind raced through my own worries. It’s like I can’t stop myself from feeling everything intensely. I really want to travel. There are so many places I want to see. Kyoto for the cherry blossoms. Paris because everyone says it’s magical. New Zealand just for the beauty of it. But am I too anxious to go alone? It would be nice to go with friends, but then there’s the whole thing of coordinating schedules and finding places to stay. What if they change their minds? It’s so much easier to just imagine it than to actually make plans. Plus, what if something went wrong? What if I ended up lost and broke in a foreign country? But then again, the thought of exploring new cultures, tasting different foods, and meeting new people is so thrilling. I hate that I keep getting distracted thinking about my phone. Notifications popping up constantly. It’s overwhelming. Why do I care so much about social media? I want to be off the grid but I also don’t want to miss anything. I’m definitely a sucker for that “fear of missing out” thing. I scroll through everyone’s perfect lives, and it makes me feel even more neurotic. Like, I’m here lounging in my messy room while they’re out having fun. But I know it’s not real. Everyone has their struggles. I wish I could just turn it off, like, for a day or something. Then again, I can’t resist the random memes that make me laugh. They’re like little pockets of joy in a sea of noise. That one about cats judging humans? Classic. I think humor is so important—it breaks the tension, right? I’d love to go to an improv show one day. The spontaneity of it, the creativity and laughter—it sounds incredible. But what if I get called on stage? Oh God, that would terrify me. Yet at the same time, there’s a part of me that craves adventure. Speaking of adventure, I should probably start working on that art project I mentioned a month ago. I bought all these supplies but haven’t touched them. I want to make something meaningful, maybe a large canvas about how chaotic life feels sometimes. That’s always in my head. But how do you even start? The blank canvas is staring at me, judging me. I know it’s just paint and brushes, but ugh, the pressure. I feel like there’s a lot of pressure in college to have it all figured out. What am I doing with my life? Do I have to pick a major? The idea of locking myself into something sounds terrifying. I love so many things. I want to learn about everything! But what if I choose the wrong one? The more I think about it, the more I spiral into confusion. I wish I could just relax and enjoy the ride, but my brain is always in overdrive. Oh man, this stream of thoughts is all over the place. Life is weird. I guess I’m just trying to figure it all out one chaotic thought at a time.

I just remembered that time I tried to organize that party in my dorm room and totally underestimated how many people would show up. I put it on my social media, and then suddenly it was like a thousand people, okay not that many but it felt like it. I thought it would be chill, just a few close friends, maybe a couple of new people from class, but then there were like strangers everywhere. I didn’t even mind the mess, just kept laughing and dancing. Who needs rules, right? My roommate was freaking out but I just told her it was fine, and we ended up having a blast, and isn't that what life is about? Spontaneity! I don’t get why people are so hung up on planning things to the T. But like, also, sometimes I really hate small talk. Why can’t people just dive into real conversations? I mean, like, who cares about the weather when we could be talking about our dreams or that amazing concert last month, or the fact that I really want to go bungee jumping even though I probably shouldn’t because heights and all that but it’s so exhilarating, I can just imagine it! But then again, I guess it has its place. I can’t help but think about how boring it is to just ask someone what they do for fun and get “Oh, I don’t know, I like to watch Netflix” as a response. Ugh, be original! Yesterday, I met some people at this cool coffee shop, and we ended up talking about philosophy for like two hours. I swear that place was buzzing with energy, and we were all just feeding off each other’s ideas, like energy drinks for the mind. Someone brought up Nietzsche and suddenly we were off on this rabbit trail about existential dread and free will. Honestly, I love how conversations can pull you in such wild directions. I’d way rather do that than sit in some class where the professor drones on about the syllabus. It’s like, can we just talk about something interesting? I keep thinking about traveling. I can’t wait to just pack a bag and go! Anywhere really, but I’m really into Italy right now. The colors, the people, the culture! Imagine wandering the streets of Florence, eating gelato like there’s no tomorrow. The world is so big and full of experiences, how can you just sit back and not want to explore? I want to meet a million people, learn their stories, live with them in a tiny apartment in a big city, hear the laughter and arguments through the walls, the music at odd hours, the life! I hate being stuck in one place too long. It drives me nuts. I just remembered my uncle’s fishing trip last summer. Spent more time talking to the strangers on the dock than actually fishing, which I don’t even care about. I think that’s part of the reason I get along with people easily; I just jump into conversations. I met a fisherman who told me the most mind-blowing stories, how the ocean is like this living thing, and when he talked about it, I could feel the waves crashing against my thoughts. It’s like when you connect with someone, you just forget about everything else and get lost in the moment. I wish we could do more of that, less scrolling through phones and more actual face-to-face stuff. What’s the deal with people being so serious all the time? I don’t get it, lighten up! I mean, we’re all going to end up as dust in the wind anyway, might as well enjoy it while we can! I sometimes think I should write a book, just random thoughts and dialogues, but then I think who has the patience for that? Maybe I could do a podcast or some kind of crazy video series. I’d just go out and film random conversations with people in the street, ask them the most absurd questions and see what they come up with. That could be hilarious! Also, I really should try new foods more often. There's this Thai place that opened up and I keep hearing amazing things about their spicy curries. I’m getting hungry just thinking about it. I love spicy food. The heat adds this whole other layer to the experience, right? It’s like life’s too short not to try everything. And I used to be picky but now I’m like just throw it at me. I’ll figure it out! Oh, I should probably get going soon but I don’t want to. I just want to keep thinking and writing and flowing endlessly, just pouring out all the thoughts in my head like a waterfall. Time is such a weird concept. I feel like I should be more responsible or something but who even cares? I’m here for the memories, the moments, the fun, and those chaotic bursts of laughter. That’s what life is all about, right?

Okay so I just remembered that birthday party last year when I tried to organize the games and it turned into chaos, absolute chaos. It was supposed to be fun, you know? I was so pumped, like, “Let’s do something amazing!” but everyone just ended up arguing over who was supposed to keep score. I totally thought it was a good idea but then I panicked. I hate it when things don’t go as planned. I could sense the tension, the awkward glances. Why do people have to be so picky? Just have fun, right? I told myself it was fine but inside my head I was like “oh no, it’s falling apart.” The cake was good though, at least that was a highlight, everyone loved the chocolate cake but it got almost ruined because someone spilled soda on it. I hate sticky things. Like when your hands get all gooey from candy? Ugh. Anyway, I kind of lost track of that night after the cake. I remember talking a lot with Sarah and how she was just going on about her cat’s new haircut. Who cares about a cat’s haircut, but I nodded and smiled. I always feel like I have to validate people’s stories. People love to talk, and I guess I do too but sometimes I just get so anxious when they go on and on, and I’m like am I even making sense when I respond? I get this weird feeling in my stomach, like I’m going to mess up the conversation, but then something makes me jump right back in and ask more questions because I can’t stand silences. What if they don’t like me? I just want everyone to be happy. Okay, switching gears, I really need to go grocery shopping soon but I keep putting it off. I should’ve gone yesterday but I met Jen for lunch instead, and it was so much fun. I forgot about my grocery list and just went with whatever. I really need to stop doing that, but how do I say no to lunch? It’s so social, and I love hearing her stories about work. She has some wild co-workers. I wonder if I do too, sometimes I can’t tell, there’s always so much drama in the office. Makes me anxious thinking about it. Like that time Greg in marketing tried to “team build” us by making us do karaoke—oh man was that a mistake. My throat hurt for a week but people laughed, so maybe it was worth it? People loved seeing me belt it out like I was the next big star. But what if they didn’t? Ugh. Then there are the times I sign up for things, like volunteering or clubs, but then I just forget and don’t show up. Like this one time I was totally down to help out at this fundraiser but the day before I was like “What am I thinking?” I got so nervous I almost backed out but then my friend said not to. But I went anyway—and the whole time I felt out of place, all of them knew each other but I was just there floundering. Why do I do this to myself? I just want to fit in and be liked. I hate the feeling of being left out, it makes me feel sad and then I overthink everything. That’s when I spiral. I wish I could just chill sometimes, but my mind races. Oh, I’ve been meaning to text my friend about that new movie coming out. I really want to see it but it’s not like I can just go alone. I know she’ll want to join, she’s always down for a movie night. I just have to remember to reach out. I hope she’s free, I hate bothering people, though. What if she’s busy? I don’t want to interrupt her life, but then again I want to have fun. It’s annoying how much I overanalyze these things. I feel like my brain is a busy highway; it never stops. I really should get better at managing my time, like my homework and all that. But here I am procrastinating on everything, juggling social plans and trying to be there for everyone while nothing gets done. I probably should’ve finished that paper last week but I just wanted to hang out with everyone, and now here I am, feeling that pressure and it freaks me out. I keep making promises but then my mind tangles everything up and I don’t want to let anyone down. It’s so exhausting. Okay, breathe. I just want everything to be great, for my friends to stay happy, and for me to not feel like I’m drowning in all this chaos. I just need to find a way to balance it all out. Is that so hard? Maybe it is. Everything feels so overwhelming sometimes, like I’m just a piece in a giant puzzle that doesn’t quite fit. Ah, whatever. I guess I’ll figure it out. Eventually. Maybe.

Okay so I’m thinking about how I can never quite figure out what to say in conversations. Like someone will ask me about my weekend and I’ll just freeze and then mumble something about reading or staying in. I wish I could be one of those people who just has tales to tell all the time, who makes everything sound so interesting and engaging. Instead, I just kind of float in the background. It’s fine, though. I enjoy it, really, observing people’s interactions, watching how they laugh and connect. There's something soothing in that, like I’m a part of it without actually having to dive in. Social situations can feel overwhelming but also fascinating. I remember this one time at a party, I sat in a corner and just listened to this group talk about their travels. They were all animated, their voices rising and falling. I could picture everything they described—the markets, the architecture, the food. I found myself smiling just from absorbing their enthusiasm. There’s beauty in doing that, you know? The thought of exploring the world has always excited me, but at the same time, I’m content to just stay put with a book or an idea swirling in my mind. Like my favorite book, it opens up worlds without needing to leave the comfort of my room. It’s like exploring many different lives through words. Speaking of books, I just finished this biography about a scientist who was completely dedicated to her work. Her passion and determination were so inspiring, and it made me think about how I approach my own studies. I’ve always prided myself on my conscientiousness, I guess, my ability to plan and execute tasks without much fuss. But I sometimes wonder if I miss out on spontaneity. Like, should I have gone to more spontaneous parties? Maybe I’d meet people who share my love for ideas as opposed to just hanging out for the sake of it. Actually, I often feel that way about group projects. I prefer working individually, but there’s something wonderful about a team that pulls together, each person contributing their strengths. I find it rewarding to find common ground and help others see the value in collaborating. When everyone is working towards the same goal, it might seem chaotic, but there’s a thrill in creating something together. I can handle the stress, I think, as long as the communication is clear. I enjoy the structure and knowing our tasks are lined up neatly. It’s like architecture for ideas, building something solid from the ground up. But then again, I think about how much I appreciate open-mindedness. Like when someone shares a thought that challenges my own, it's invigorating. I love engaging in those conversations that push boundaries, where you can explore new concepts and viewpoints. It’s like peeling back layers of an onion, discovering new perspectives. One of my friends loves to debate, and I feel like I grow just from listening to her articulate her stance on complex issues. Even if I disagree, it’s that exchange that fuels me. I’ve been trying to embrace more moments like that, where rather than just nodding along, I can weave my own thoughts into the mix. Oh, and food! Food is a massive part of my openness to experiences. I’ve started trying new recipes—like this one Asian dish with tofu and a spicy sauce. It didn’t turn out perfect, but the process itself was enjoyable. Cooking can be such a creative outlet, and there’s something gratifying about preparing a meal from scratch. I think it’s similar to how I look at life—each day is a new chance to learn or try something different, whether that’s through food or experiences or academic pursuits. I just wish more people would connect through those little moments, sharing insights or recipes, or just chatting about books that have moved them. The world has so many stories, waiting to be shared, and sometimes it feels like people are just skimming the surface. I believe there’s so much more beneath that first impression—depth in ideas, people, experiences. Maybe that's why I can feel out of place sometimes. I see the beauty in everything but struggle to share that vision. I guess it’s all part of the challenge, finding ways to bridge my thoughts with others and help them see what I see without overpowering their own voices. I just want to create a space where everyone feels valued, where we can dive deep into conversations that matter. It keeps me grounded, focused on being compassionate and understanding, wanting to foster connections that feel meaningful. Anyway, I’ve rambled long enough. Time to reflect more on how to refine my own stories.

The clock says 3:12 PM and I’m thinking about what I want for dinner. Pizza? I guess I’ve been craving that a lot lately. But then again, the taco place down the street has those amazing nachos. Ugh, decisions. Hmm, maybe I’ll ask a friend to join me. I love the energy of sharing meals, laughs, and plans with people. Food is such a bonding experience, right? It’s like the ultimate connector. Okay, what was I saying? Right, food. I could totally use some spontaneous fun today. Can’t just sit here thinking about dinner while the day slips away. Thinking about spontaneous fun reminds me of last weekend. We went hiking. The weather was perfect, which is rare, and everyone was in such a good mood. There were about ten of us, and it felt like a mini-adventure. One of those moments where you just want to bottle the happiness up and keep it forever. We took so many pictures, and nobody cared how goofy we looked. I mean who wouldn’t want to take a jumping selfie at a scenic overlook? It's like you can feel the laughter in the air. I should really make plans to do that again. I love how easy it is to rally people together. Just shoot a quick text to the group and boom, instant adventure! I wonder if Sarah would be up for it again. She always brings that infectious energy, and I love how she can make anyone feel included. That’s what I admire about her. I like being around people who just radiate positivity. It’s a total vibe. Honestly, being around people fuels me. I thrive off those interactions. The conversations, the stories, even the debates. Just last night, we ended up talking for hours about random topics like the best ice cream flavors and the latest Netflix show we binge-watched. I can’t believe how invested we got over a chocolate chip cookie vs. brownie discussion. It’s funny how something so trivial can become a passionate debate. It makes me appreciate how we all have different tastes and opinions. It’s great! And then there's the art of listening, which I think I’m pretty good at. I love hearing what others think. I noticed how people often feel more comfortable sharing when you just nod and listen intently. It’s kind of a superpower to make someone feel heard, you know? I want to carry that energy into my studies as well. Maybe I could start a study group? I think that’s possible, but some people aren’t as down for group work. I wish they could see how much easier and more fun studying can be with buddies. Plus, we’d share ideas, and learning together just seems so much more enriching. Speaking of studies, I really need to get back to that project. The one on environmental sustainability. It’s fascinating how much impact individual choices can have. I mean, who knew that something as simple as reducing plastic use could lead to massive changes in ocean health? It’s all interconnected! I’ve been trying to implement those changes in my own life too. I hate the idea of contributing to a problem when I could help solve it. Small actions, like using reusable bags or biking instead of driving, feel empowering. Wow, I just remembered the time I organized that event for Earth Day. So satisfying to see everyone come together to plant trees. The energy was electric! People were so engaged, and seeing the smiles on their faces as they dug into the soil was priceless. I remember how we all celebrated afterward with tasty homemade cookies. Baking those felt like a true labor of love. I always feel inspired when others are passionate about similar causes. It’s like this unspoken bond, a connection that ensures we’re all on the same page. Flipping through memories, I can’t help but think about all the adventures I still want to take. I want to explore new places, meet new people, and learn new things. The world has so much to offer, and I feel like I want to soak it all in. I keep a running list of places I want to visit. Traveling is the best, especially when you can share it with friends. That energy is contagious, and getting lost in a new city is exhilarating. Time flies when you’re having fun. Who would’ve thought I’d be typing this stream of consciousness, and yet here I am. It’s strange to capture fleeting thoughts in words like this, but it feels liberating. Like I’m peeling back some layers, exposing bits of my mind. I wish more people would do this. Just let it all out without hesitation or fear of judgment. That's where the magic happens! Okay, back to that dinner decision… maybe I’ll text someone now and see if they want tacos or pizza. Why not both?

okay so I just remembered that time at the party last weekend everyone was dancing like crazy I can't believe how spontaneous some people can be it was wild like one minute we were just hanging out this organized garden party that’s what it feels like when I’m out and about just swarming around people laughing joking sometimes I think I say things too quickly without really thinking but who has yet here I am screaming my lungs out no regrets even if I wake up feeling like I've been hit by a truck next morning most times I’m still buzzing from the night before which is insane right but then “Why is that person staring at me?” I notice everything but still I keep talking what choice do I have it’s like I’m wired to engage even when I forget to breathe some days I wish I were more chill like my friends who seem to take everything in stride but then I remember the times when I embraced my wild side they make the best stories it’s the energy that makes life exciting it’s a blast to about those moments of connection yeah that’s the good stuff even if I freak out sometimes there’s still something to be learned in every crazy night and every awkward moment and I just have to keep reminding myself of that as much as I freak out it’s also about enjoying the ride

Okay so I just remembered that time we went to that concert last summer, it was absolutely insane. I love being surrounded by so many people, the energy is just electric, you can feel it in your bones. Everyone singing along, laughing, just being together, it’s like this collective heartbeat and it makes everything else just fade away. I can still hear the music ringing in my ears; it’s funny how a song can bring back so many memories. Which reminds me of my favorite playlist, the one I play when I’m getting ready for a night out. It’s got all the bangers, all the songs that make me feel alive. And speaking of nights out, I can’t believe how much I love trying new restaurants and bars. I mean, every time I go somewhere new it’s like unwrapping a gift. What will I find? New flavors, new drinks, a cool vibe. Oh, and the conversations! I could talk for hours with anyone. Just the other day, I met someone at a party who was into the same indie films as I am—so cool! So many ideas just bouncing around. I love connecting with people, hearing their stories, it makes me realize how much we’re all in this together. Makes me think about how I can always find common ground, even with someone who seems totally different. What was that book I read recently? The one about the connections between people and how those moments can change everything? I wish I could remember the title because it totally rocked my world. I want to dive into more of those. There's so much out there! I keep saying, “You have to explore!” It’s like discovering a new hidden part of yourself every time you open a new book or meet a new person. Travel is huge for me too; I’ve got this growing list of places to visit that’s practically a novel. Each spot has its own character, history, and food. Oh man, the food! I love trying street food when I travel. It’s like a flavor adventure. But then, there are those days when I just want to chill. I can’t get enough of board games with friends. Last week we had this epic game night at Jamie’s place. The laughter, the competition, everyone getting riled up—it’s such a blast! I feel like games bring out a side of people that you don’t see in normal conversation. It’s fun to see everyone’s personality shine. Plus, I always end up making new friends and strengthening old friendships, which is honestly the best part of it all. And let’s not forget about volunteering. I’ve been involved in this community garden project and it’s amazing. Getting my hands dirty for a good cause, meeting awesome people who care about the same things I do. It’s so satisfying to see things grow, both in the garden and in the community. Each time I leave, I feel this warmth in my heart like I truly made a difference. It’s that openness to learning and connecting that keeps me going. Oh! That reminds me, I really should send that email to the professor about the research project. I get so excited about ideas that I dive in without organizing my thoughts—note to self: work on that! But also, I’m just really passionate about everything I do, so it’s like a whirlwind trying to keep track of it all. I want to engage with everything, you know? The sky looks so blue today. I love sunny days. There’s something about the sunlight that just makes everything better. Everyone seems happier, more alive. Sometimes I wonder if being around people who are upbeat can rub off on you. It’s like a cycle of positivity. I think that’s why I enjoy being around my friends so much—they bring out the best in me. I’ve got to get into hiking more. I always say I will, but then life gets in the way. Nature is calling! I love being outdoors, feeling the wind, and just disconnecting for a little while. Plus, it’s a great way to bond with friends. How cool would it be to plan a weekend hike? It’s on my to-do list. And then there’s art. I’m not the best artist, but I love to draw and paint. It’s so freeing. I can just let my mind flow and create whatever comes out. Sometimes it’s a total mess, but it’s mine. It feels so good to express myself without judgment. I hope I can keep doing that as I get older. Life should be filled with all these creative outlets, right? Overall, I’m just so grateful for all the experiences I get to have—the people, the adventures, the learning. It’s all interconnected, like this beautiful web of life just waiting to be explored. I can’t wait for what’s next.

It's 3:47 PM and I just got out of class. I should probably go to the library and study for that exam next week but part of me is just so tired. It's so easy to get lost in thoughts of how unprepared I am. What am I even doing with my life? Everyone else seems to know what they’re doing, right? And I’m just here, feeling like I’m not keeping up. I don’t even like group work. I mean, I like helping people, but talking to them makes me anxious. I remember that one time in psych class when we had to do a presentation in pairs. My partner was great but I just kept thinking about how I could let her down. I hate feeling like I'm letting people down. I wish I could just focus on my own work without worrying about everything else. And then there's the roommate situation. It's like you want to be friendly, but how much is too much? I don't want to come off as standoffish but also being social can be exhausting. I feel like they want me to be this bubbly person, but I’m just not. Sometimes I think it’s easier to just stick to my books and quiet time. But then I feel guilty for not engaging enough. Should I invite them to study? Or to watch a movie? What if they say no? Ugh, I dread rejection, every time. I go online and look up tips about social anxiety. It never really helps, though. I just want to find a way to blend in without too much effort. It's like a balancing act, and I feel like I’m about to fall off the tightrope. What if I just sent a group text and said, "hey I'm studying in the common area, join me if you want"? But then I think about how they might see it as an obligation. What if they’re busy? I don't want to pressure anyone. And what if they actually come? I mean, it could be fun, but I would probably end up being quiet again, just like I did last time. And the conversation would just awkwardly fizzle out. I really should have thought about majoring in something more solitary. Like data entry or something where I can just be alone. But then there's the thought of failure, too. What if I can’t even do that? What if I just end up... I don't know, being a middle-aged barista at a coffee shop?
