Nothing wrong with that but is it what I want? I really shouldn’t be thinking about this right now. I have textbooks to read. If only I could get through just one chapter without my mind wandering. I need to focus. Right? Okay, I should try. But I can hear people laughing down the hall. It’s distracting. They sound like they’re having a good time, and I’m just here spiraling into my head. I hope it’s not too loud. I mean, it's not like I don't like fun. I do! It’s just sometimes I feel like I’m not included in what it means to be "fun." Maybe I’ll just do some online quizzes. I always end up with the same results. Communicative but reserved. Good listener. It’s funny how they describe being "agreeable" as a good thing, but sometimes I feel like it just means I’m too soft. There’s that project due next month. Ugh. The one where we have to collaborate with that group again. Should I just take charge? Or should I let someone else take the lead for a change? I’m always worried about how everyone feels. What if they don't like my ideas? I don't know if I'm any good at this. It feels like everyone else is on a different level. I don't want to overstep. I’ll probably just end up trying to pick up the slack, like I always do. My instinct is to help and support but then I’m left feeling drained. I mean, why can’t we just all agree to do everything the way I think it should be done? And then we wouldn’t have to worry about any drama or hurt feelings. That sounds so simple. And then there’s my family. They always ask about my grades and what I’m doing next. It feels like pressure. They mean well, but it sends me into a tailspin. I don’t want to disappoint them. But telling them I’m struggling feels too vulnerable. Like they wouldn’t understand. They say they love me no matter what, but what if I don’t live up to their expectations? There’s that voice in my head again. It never stops. My stomach’s in knots just thinking about it. I just want to be enough for them, for me, for everyone. I don't know if I can be.

Okay so, what’s the deal with people who just don't get anxiety? Like, okay, chill out, I get it, life is about vibes and all that but sometimes it’s just too much. I mean, I woke up this morning and immediately thought of ten things that could go wrong today. My roommate's always like “Just breathe” and I’m like “Sure, okay, let me breathe my way out of this impending doom.” Anyway, I really should have grabbed breakfast but I got distracted scrolling through TikTok. I keep finding these food hacks and I want to try them but then I think about how messy everything gets. I hate messes, but I love food. It's like, how do I reconcile those two things? Speaking of food, I really want to try that new place downtown. Everyone has been raving about it on Insta and oh my god, the pictures look amazing! I need to gather a group to go with because I can’t just eat there alone. I mean, I could, but it’s more fun with others, right? Plus, I’ll definitely be the one who keeps taking pictures of the food and everyone always rolls their eyes but they secretly enjoy it. My friends are so great at pretending they’re annoyed but they know that’s part of the deal. I hope I don’t look too anxious in the photos though; it’s embarrassing when I’m smiling and my eyes look like I’m worried about a meteor hitting the Earth or something. It’s really hard to find that balance of looking cool and effortless while also feeling like you have a million things on your mind. Like, I can be social and chatty, and I thrive in those settings, but if I'm not careful, I’ll start feeling overwhelmed and just want to disappear into my hoodie. It’s this constant push and pull - like I want to be out there and engaging with people but I also feel like I’m constantly falling apart inside. The anxiety is real, my friends. And then there's that weird thing where I just can’t say no to anything. If someone asks me to do something, even if I really don’t want to, I’ll just nod along and say “Sure! Sounds great!” And then I’m stuck in this situation I knew I didn't want to be in. I can’t help but smile and hope that the universe will make it less awkward, but honestly? It usually doesn’t. I need to learn to stand my ground, but that’s tough when all I want is to keep everyone happy. Everyone seems to be counting on me to be upbeat and fun. Okay, wait, switching gears. I really need to get my life together. I mean, like, it’s already mid-semester and I haven’t even started on that project that’s due next week. I mean, how do people plan things out? I can’t sit down and make a to-do list without feeling panicky. My friend Jill has a color-coded planner, and I look at it and feel like it’s the most beautiful thing ever and then I think about my crumpled sticky notes and just cringe. I want to be one of those people who has it all figured out but then I remember how hard it is to keep myself together. Also, my phone is buzzing like crazy. I really should check it, but what if it’s one of those weird texts that just sends my mind spiraling? I mean, I love social media and how everyone shares everything with each other but sometimes I just feel like it’s all too much. I want to be part of the hype and share my updates too, but the thought of putting myself out there is terrifying. What if people think I’m boring? And what’s up with the weather? One day it’s all sunny and I’m wearing my shorts, and the next day it feels like winter again. How do I plan an outfit when I can’t even trust Mother Nature? It’s like the sky is mocking me as I pull out layers of clothing every single day. Oh, and then I trip on my way to class because the sidewalk is uneven. Classic me. Always doing the most dramatic face plant while holding my coffee and books. I sometimes wonder if I make friends easily because I’m always blabbing about random stuff. Like, I’ll just talk and talk and somehow they’re still there, probably thinking how chaotic I am. I mean, I guess it works out in the end? It’s like, I’m so grateful for my friends who tolerate my rants and anxieties. They listen, and I sometimes feel terrible for dragging them into my emotional rollercoaster, but they still care. Okay, I should probably try to focus on something. Ugh, where's my focus? I’m just drifting here. Need to figure out a plan, but I guess for now, I’ll just enjoy the chaos of it all.

Okay so I was thinking about that time I had to organize the study group for finals and it was such a huge deal because I wanted everyone to do well but also I felt like I was stressing out more than the others like why was I always the one planning and making the schedules and sending reminders it felt like it was all on me and what if people didn’t show up? That was my biggest fear, I kept imagining myself sitting there alone just waiting for someone to arrive and nobody would I mean it didn’t happen but I was sweating bullets every time I checked my phone. I’m always checking my phone, it gives me some kind of reassurance, like if I see a message, even if it’s just a meme from my friend, it makes me feel connected, like, I’m not alone in this universe even if I feel most days like I’m juggling these big huge balls of anxiety and expectations and it gets overwhelming. But then when I see people it’s like a rush of energy, I just want to be around them but then I have this constant nagging feeling like do they even want me around or am I being too much, am I talking too loud, am I taking up too much space in this tiny café? Sometimes my heart races just thinking about how I might interrupt them or say something awkward like it just spills out of my mouth without thinking. Ugh, like that one time at the party when I accidentally called Mark by the wrong name for the third time and I could see everyone looking at me and I wanted to sink into the floor. Awkward vibes everywhere. But then again I also thought about how nice it was to just be there with those people even if I felt anxious because laughing about it later with my friends made it a little better. It’s funny how that works. I’m always trying to keep the peace, you know? Like when I see conflict, I just want to jump in and smooth things over. It makes me feel like a mediator or something, like I have this duty to help people get along. But sometimes it’s exhausting. I mean, I had to break up an argument between roommates last week and it took almost the whole evening, I felt like a therapist just sitting there listening, trying to come up with solutions that satisfied both sides. I think they were lucky I was there because I hate conflict so much, but I’m not sure if they saw that. I just wanted them to be friends again. And speaking of friends, I always make sure to check in on everyone even if they say they’re okay because what if they’re not really okay? I mean, we all have those days when we put on a brave face but deep down we’re sad or stressed or just worn out from life. Like just thinking about how many people seem fine on the outside but are struggling hits me hard, I want to help everyone but I can’t save the world. It’s like my brain is working overtime, worrying about all this stuff and wanting to please everyone. But, then again, it feels good when people reach out to me and ask how I’m doing too. It’s like an unspoken agreement that we’re all just trying to figure things out together. Oh, and then there's my classes, which always stress me out but also excite me at the same time. I want to make sure I’m doing everything right, like getting all the papers in on time and studying the right material, but I feel trapped sometimes because I don’t want to miss out on social gatherings either. Balancing act is what it feels like. I mean I want to be responsible but also be fun. Last week I had a chance to go to that concert, and I kept thinking about the homework I had to do but then I went anyway and it was the best decision ever, we danced, and I felt free for a bit, all the worries just melted away with the music. But now I’m paying for that with late-night cramming. And how do people manage to stay organized? I have a million to-do lists floating around. Sticky notes, my phone is full of reminders, I can't even keep track of it all. I want to be that person who's super organized and knows how to manage everything perfectly but it’s like every time I think I’m on top of it, I find something that slipped through the cracks. It’s frustrating, but I keep trying, rescheduling things, shifting priorities, lists upon lists. Some days I feel like a pro, and other days I’m just in a whirlpool of chaos. So, yeah, I guess I want to be liked and be productive and help people and have fun but I’m also riding this rollercoaster of emotions and it’s all mixed up inside me like a cocktail of feelings—I guess that’s just life, right?

Okay, so I was thinking about how people always say you should follow your passion, but what does that even mean? Like, how do you figure out what you’re passionate about? There are so many things I enjoy. I mean, I could spend hours talking about new tech gadgets or that obscure indie band I found last week on Spotify. I love meeting people, but not in that small talk way. I want to dive deep. What's your story? What makes you tick? I remember this one time at a party, I started chatting with a guy who worked in marine biology. I just kept asking questions. I couldn't help myself. It just flowed. So interesting! But wait, then I ended up talking to someone else about their grandma’s secret recipe for lasagna, and I was all in. I mean, who doesn’t love a good recipe? I can be pretty opinionated, though. If someone tells me a food combo is good, I might challenge it just for the thrill of debate. Pineapple on pizza? I can feel the tension rising. But I guess that comes from my low agreeableness. I just want to have fun conversations where we both push back. Now, I'm thinking about how many different hobbies I have. I try to pick up new ones every semester. Last semester was pottery. I thought it’d be a fun way to meet people, but surprise—it was super hard. Who knew shaping clay could be like a workout? But I loved it. I had this crazy idea to make a set of mugs, but they all ended up looking like lumpy aliens. Still, the laughter was worth it, and that’s what I value more than anything. I just want to connect! Maybe I’ll host a pottery party once I get the hang of it, a “bring your own wine” kind of vibe. That would totally up the fun level. It's funny how creativity feels less about the finished product and more about the process and the people you share it with, right? And oh man, speaking of processes, I’ve been thinking about starting a podcast. Everyone has one these days. I'm totally into the idea of exploring random topics, interviewing people on campus about their major, their weirdest experiences, just shooting the breeze. I want to capture those spontaneous moments. It's like capturing a firefly in a jar before letting it go. I think about how vibrant it would be. I have tech gadgets lying around that I could use. Why not? I’ll make it happen! But then I consider my schedule. I’m always packed. Classes, study groups, that part-time job, friend meetups—how do people find the time? I thrive in chaos but, like, I know I have to stay organized. I use apps to stay on top of assignments, create schedules for study sessions, and I even color-code my planner. It feels good, but sometimes I wonder if it’s too rigid. Does it stifle creativity? Maybe? But when something is on my calendar, I’m way more likely to show up and deliver. I can’t stand being the unreliable one; that just grinds my gears. Then there are those days when nothing seems to work out. Like the time I planned a group project meeting, and only two people showed up. Why even? I had snacks! I love snacks more than anything. If someone walked in with a bag of nachos, I’d probably declare them my best friend. I can’t have a meeting without snacks! But back to the meeting—I ended up just brainstorming ideas alone in my room, feeling a bit deflated, but you know—screw it! I’ll just go hard on my own ideas, right? Sometimes I question my boundaries. I mean, am I too much sometimes? I chat with random strangers at the gym, which sometimes feels awkward. But isn’t that the spice of life? That kind of spontaneity? Like, “Hey, I saw you doing squats—nice form!” A bit weird, sure, but it could lead to something cool. Maybe a new workout buddy? But oh, this is getting long. I love the thrill of new experiences but sometimes wish I could slow down. There’s something about the ticking clock that gives me energy, but too fast and I feel dizzy. Finding balance is tough, but it’s what keeps me grounded. Happy chaos is what I call it! Anyway, I’ll probably end this here. My mind is still racing with thoughts, but it feels good to just type out the randomness. I suppose that’s what makes life exciting—the unpredictable, the spontaneous, all the little things that connect us. Thank goodness for that, right?

Noodles. I think about noodles a lot. Like what type of noodle goes with which sauce. I just had some ramen last night. The rich broth, the perfectly cooked egg. I can’t stand overcooked noodles. It’s a crime. Then again, I’m a bit of a food snob. People don’t always understand how important it is to get the details right. I notice things. Little things. Like how the way someone prepares their food says something about them. Or maybe it doesn’t. Hmm. I wonder if that’s a stupid thought. Whatever. Can't please everyone anyway. I think about how I like cooking but also how chaotic it can get when I’m trying out new recipes. Messing up is part of learning, but it’s still annoying. I am super methodical about measuring, I guess it’s the conscientiousness. Once I spent an entire afternoon making pasta from scratch. It turned into an all-day affair, but the taste? Worth it. Reminds me of that time I left flour all over the kitchen. My roommate was not pleased. Ramen again. Is it weird that I keep circling back to food? I could talk about something else. Music? I listen to a lot of indie bands. They’re always under the radar and I like it that way. The mainstream stuff is just so… boring, predictable. I like the surprise of a new song that takes my brain somewhere unexpected. However, I tend not to do well with crowds at concerts. Too many people. Too much energy. I prefer small venues where the vibe is chill, where I can sit or lean back, not crowded. Being around strangers feels so draining sometimes. They’re all buzzing with conversation, having a good time, while I’m just observing. I can’t join in, I start to feel overwhelmed. Oh, and then there are social media platforms. I can’t stand them. Everyone is just shouting into a void. Their opinions, their selfies, their curated lives. It’s exhausting. I would delete my accounts if it didn't feel so necessary for staying connected even though I don’t connect in the traditional sense. I wish people would just talk about things that matter. Like why do we spend so much time on trivial stuff when there’s so much real stuff happening? All the climate change talks. It stresses me out when I think about it too much. I know what I can do, but sometimes it feels like a drop in the ocean, doesn’t it? Speaking of oceans, I should go to the beach more often. I love the waves. There’s something soothing about the rhythm of the water. I could sit for hours, just listening, watching the horizon blend into the sky. It’s calming. Sometimes I think it would be nice to just retreat there and think about life in solitude. I mean, I like solitude. It gives me space to think deeply. More people should appreciate quiet moments. I find that I understand myself better when I have that mental space. Back to crowds—when I have to go to larger gatherings, I often stick to the edges. I observe. People-watching is fascinating. They’re all interacting, but what are they really saying? Sometimes I catch snippets of conversations and feel like there’s a whole layer of communication happening that most people miss. Expressions, gestures, underlying emotions; they tell a story. I wonder if I should go back to school for something completely different. I often think about pursuing a PhD but then I’m like, what would I even study? I love learning new things, exploring new ideas. My last class on philosophy was amazing. Challenging but also eye-opening. The professors are so passionate about their subjects, diving deep into theories, nothing is too far-fetched. It excites me to think about the possibilities of thought. And books! Oh man, where do I begin? I have stacks of books just waiting to be read. An endless pile. Some of them are half-finished. I get distracted easily, especially when a new book pops up on my radar. I love exploring new genres, pushing my comfort zone. I’ll read a dense philosophical text one day, then dive into a sci-fi novel the next. I wish I could just absorb everything. Imagine if one could just download information and experiences? But then again, I think part of the learning process is the struggle, isn't it? I should probably get some exercise, but the thought of going to the gym is off-putting. All those people sweating, grunting, and crowding the machines. I’d much rather go for a run in the park. Just me, my thoughts, the rhythm of my feet on the pavement. It clears my head. I need to do that more often. Being outside, breathing fresh air is important. It rejuvenates me. Okay, did I just go on a whirlwind about food, music, people, and running? I guess that speaks to the openness. It’s all connected in a way. Life is like that, isn’t it? A bunch of random thoughts connecting here and there. I’ll have to remind myself to embrace that randomness more often.

Okay so I just had this amazing conversation with a friend earlier, we were talking about everything under the sun, like how the universe is expanding and what if aliens are out there and they’re just chilling watching us, or maybe they’re even influencing us somehow when we’re not looking. It’s wild to think about, right? And then we pivoted to food, which is always a winner. Why is it that every time I go to the grocery store I have at least three spontaneous recipes in my head and then I end up buying this waterfall of groceries that my fridge can’t even handle? I swear, there’s something magical about colors and smells and textures in the produce aisle; it pulls me in every time. I can’t even remember the last time I ate something bland. Life is too short for bland food, you know? This week I made this incredible stir-fry with like a rainbow of veggies and some sriracha, and oh my gosh, I think I’ve eaten it for three meals in a row because I just can’t get enough. I love trying new things and mixing up flavors, it’s like an adventure on a plate! And speaking of adventure, I really want to travel somewhere new soon. I have this itch to go backpacking in Europe or something, but then again, I could spend a weekend exploring local spots too because there’s something so exciting about discovering hidden gems in your own backyard. Like, last month I found this little café that serves the best chai lattes and the ambiance is so cozy, I could sit there for hours just people-watching. People are fascinating, aren't they? Everyone has their own story, and sometimes, when you get a little group together, it’s like a live performance. Just last Saturday, I went out with a bunch of friends and we ended up playing this hilarious game where we had to guess each other’s most embarrassing moments. I never knew my buddy Mark once tried to impress a girl by singing karaoke and forgot all the lyrics and just stood there awkwardly humming. The laughter was contagious, we ended up crying from laughing so hard. Those moments remind me of how important it is to keep connections strong, to just be present with each other. In fact, I feel like we should have more “no phones allowed” hangouts, where we just chat without distractions. It seems like everyone is glued to their screens nowadays, which is kind of sad, but you can’t blame them either. Social media is such a double-edged sword; it connects us but can also make you feel so alone. Sometimes I feel like I have to consciously detach from scrolling through feeds to really appreciate reality. When I’m with people, I’m all in. Sometimes it feels like I’m just a bridge between different groups of friends, bringing people together. That's one of my favorite things, playing the social butterfly and seeing how different personalities mesh. It reminds me of this workshop I attended last semester on effective communication. The instructor was incredible; she had this charisma that just pulled everyone in. I remember her talking about how crucial empathy is. It’s so simple yet profound, learning to really listen to someone before jumping in with your own thoughts. That’s something I always strive for: being a good listener while also being engaged. It’s just about finding the right balance, I guess. I’ve been thinking about getting into volunteering more too. There’s something incredibly rewarding about giving back. Last summer I helped at a local shelter and it opened my eyes to so many stories. I met this woman who had gone through so much hardship yet she still managed to smile every day. It made me realize how important it is to practice gratitude in our daily lives. Sometimes I write down three things I’m grateful for every night before bed, it really shifts my mindset, helps me focus on the positive rather than the negative. But enough about that, I’ve been itching to start a new hobby. Maybe painting? Or pottery? There’s this studio downtown that offers classes, and I feel like it could be a fun way to express myself. Plus it would be a great way to meet new people! I love the idea of getting my hands messy and creating something, especially if I can do it with others. There’s something therapeutic about art, like you can let your imagination run wild and there are no rules. Just pure creation. I really hope the semester goes well, though. I’m juggling a lot right now but honestly, I thrive on being busy. It keeps me motivated. I have this weird energy that just comes alive when I have deadlines approaching. I’m the kind of person who will start a project last minute and then it somehow turns into a masterpiece, or at least that’s how I like to think of it. Ah, maybe I’m getting ahead of myself! But it’s all part of the journey, right? Learning and growing every step of the way.

okay so I just remembered that I forgot to send that email to my professor about my grades and now I’m stressing about it what if he thinks I’m not responsible enough to handle this class what if he thinks I don’t care about my future and I really do care it just slipped my mind and now I have to double-check if the deadline is even approaching or if I missed it already oh gosh I can't handle that pressure what if he’s really upset with me I hate waiting for responses anyway like the anticipation is the worst part I wonder if it’s bad that I get so anxious over little things like this but really it's not little in my mind like a small mistake could lead to bigger consequences right like he could think I’m not dedicated and all I want to do is do well and honestly I’ve been putting in so much effort in this class but I feel like no one sees it I keep telling myself to push through and focus but then all these doubts creep in and it’s like a cycle of anxiety just spiraling down you know? there's also that group project coming up and I don’t even know how I’ll manage that because I can’t just rely on other people to get it done I need to be the one organizing everything and it’s not like I can just sit back and let them figure it out because what if they don’t care as much as I do and I end up stuck with all the work and it’s just so frustrating how some people don’t take it seriously like I can’t understand why you wouldn’t want to do your best and it’s like my brain won’t shut off it keeps going what if they don’t do their part on time and then I have to pick up the slack and then my grades suffer because of their laziness it’s so maddening how inherently unfair it is and I can’t just chalk it up to teamwork when I know I’ll be the one stuck with it in the end and I just wish I could make things the way I want them to be I wish I could just confront them but confrontation makes me feel sick to my stomach I can’t stand it what if they get defensive or angry or what if they just don’t take me seriously and I can’t stand that thought either what if they laugh at me or talk behind my back and here I am just trying to make sure everything goes well and they just don’t care it’s so hard to find people I can trust and it’s so exhausting having to figure out who’s reliable and who’s not because I don’t want to offend anyone by assuming they’re not but at the same time some people are so blatantly unreliable and ugh I just want to scream into a pillow or something like if I could just find a remote island with no responsibilities and no academic pressures that would be fantastic but then I’d probably just be anxious about not studying and falling behind and then I wouldn’t be able to enjoy the peace anyway because I’d just keep thinking about how I should be doing something productive it’s like I’m cursed to always feel this weight of obligation on my shoulders and then there’s the fact that I kind of don’t do anything social either I know I should be going out more and trying to meet people but I just don’t feel comfortable in those situations and feeling like the odd one out is just the worst like I’d rather stay home and read a book or watch a show where I know what to expect but then I see everyone else out having fun and I feel that twinge of jealousy or whatever it is like why can’t I be like them but then I think I wouldn’t even know what to say or how to contribute and my mind goes blank and it feels like I’m stuck in a loop just watching life happen around me knowing it’s passing by and I’m just here feeling so detached and it’s exhausting I want to be a part of things and not feel so lonely but the thought of actually putting myself out there scares me so much I feel like a prisoner in my own mind I just want to find my place in the world but everything feels so big and overwhelming and I can’t find a starting point no one ever really talks about how hard it can be trying to figure out who you are while dealing with all this pressure and expectations and I sometimes think that if I could just get everything organized and planned out I could handle it but life doesn’t work that way and plans always have a way of falling apart and it makes me so anxious like is there something wrong with me why can’t I just take things as they come why do I have to have a plan for everything and it feels like other people just move so smoothly through life and I’m stuck trying to keep up and it’s just tiring and I want to stop feeling like this but then what would happen if I did would I lose that drive to keep pushing myself to do better and where would that leave me?

Okay so I was just thinking about how great it feels to be around people. I mean, social gatherings are just the best. Like when everyone is laughing and sharing stories, it’s just pure energy. I can’t get enough of that buzz you get when you’re surrounded by friends. Last weekend we had that barbecue at Mark's place, and it was just epic. I still remember how everyone was gathered around the grill, and Mark was flipping burgers like a pro, and the smell! Oh man, the smell was heavenly, and then Sarah starts telling this story about her trip to the beach, and everyone is laughing so hard. Good vibes all around. I love how easily we connect during those moments. Then there’s that feeling when you meet new people. It's almost like a game to me. Who’s going to be the funniest? Who’s going to be my new best buddy? I can totally talk to anyone, and it never feels forced. Just walk up, introduce myself, and boom—instant connection. Sometimes I feel like I’m the glue in our social circle, always organizing things. Like when I suggested the group game night last month, and everyone was so pumped. I get such a kick out of planning those things. It's like my little project, and I want everyone to have fun. That’s what makes me happy, seeing my friends enjoying themselves. But then, oh man, sometimes I wonder if I’m a bit too much. Like, do people think I try too hard? I hope not. I mean I’m just being myself. I guess some people say I’m overly enthusiastic. Whatever, I love being excited about things. I get it; not everyone’s like me. I can’t stand when people are moody or negative. It’s like they suck all the fun out of the room. I just want to lift everyone up. Cheerleader mode activated. And seriously, what’s the deal with procrastination? I can’t wrap my head around it. I’m all about getting things done. When that assignment is due, it’s done yesterday, right? I can’t imagine waiting until the last minute. My friends joke about it, and I’m like, “Why would you risk that?” It gives me anxiety just thinking about it. I guess that’s because I care about doing well. It’s important to me to be reliable. People can count on me, and that feels good. I think that’s part of being agreeable, you know? Helping others and being dependable is kind of my jam. But then, I wonder if I miss out on experiences because I stick to what I know. Like, I’m not super adventurous. I don’t know, the thought of, say, skydiving? Yikes. I’d rather stay on solid ground with my friends, playing board games or watching movies. I guess that’s just my comfort zone, and I embrace it. I mean, I love planning little outings, like going to the local arcade or hitting a new restaurant. Familiar places bring joy, and there’s always something new to discover in the same old coffee shop, right? And oh, speaking of food, I had this amazing taco truck experience last week. It was crazy packed, but so worth it. Tacos are life. I was standing in line chatting with this random guy, and we bonded over how good the al pastor was. It’s moments like that that make me believe in the power of human connection. It’s just tacos, but we shared this little slice of joy, and it warms my heart. Then, of course, there are days when I just want to chill with my closest friends. Sometimes the bigger groups get overwhelming, you know? Too many voices, too much happening at once. But with my core group? That’s where the magic is. We can sit in silence, binge-watch a show, or just talk about life for hours. It’s comforting, like being wrapped in a blanket. Also, I think about how important it is to keep close relationships. I want to be there for my friends. I want to help them through tough times, lighten their load when things get rough. Last month, Emily was feeling super stressed about exams, and I told her to come over for pizza and a study session. I love being that person who steps in. And yeah, I’m pretty obsessed with planning things. I’ve got our next hangout already mapped out in my head. It’ll be outdoors, probably a picnic or something. I love how those moments strengthen our bonds. Like, it's not just about the event but about creating memories together. Totally worth the effort. So, at the end of the day, I just feel lucky to be who I am. I thrive on connection, joy, laughter, and being there for my friends. It’s a pretty good way to live, all things considered. I wouldn’t trade it for anything different.

Okay so I've been thinking about this class that I just can’t seem to get a grip on. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I just feel overwhelmed. My classmates seem to breeze through everything, laughing and chatting like they own the place, and I just want to disappear into my hoodie. I have this feeling all the time, you know? Like I’m supposed to be social, but it just drains me. It’s weird because I care about people and I want to connect but managing the energy it takes to engage feels like running a marathon. Ugh. The other day, I saw a cat poster on the wall in the common area, and it said “You’re purr-fect just the way you are.” I almost laughed but then I thought about how everyone is always trying to be perfect or fit in, and honestly, that just makes me anxious. I can’t even deal with the pressure of that expectation. What even is perfect? Maybe I should just embrace my neurotic side, like okay, I worry a lot, but I’m also super open to experiences. That’s the trade-off, right? Like I once tried painting and it was a mess but also incredibly freeing. Colors everywhere, just a chaotic explosion! Maybe I could go back to that instead of feeling trapped in my head. Speaking of chaos, I forgot to return that book to the library, the one about existential philosophy. I really wanted to read it but now I’m just like, whatever, I’ll pay the fine. My brain is such a jumble. I should be more organized but it feels like I’m fighting against this natural flow of what I want to explore. Last week I went for a walk and found this cool little bookstore, so many different sections, each one like a tiny universe. And I just got lost in there, flipping through pages, thinking about how many stories are crammed inside those walls. So many thoughts and ideas waiting to be pulled out and played with like clay. That’s what I want, to play. But then the thought hits me, what am I doing with my life? Am I just drifting? Thinking about drifting makes me think about friends and how sometimes I feel like I’m not doing enough to keep in touch, like it’s so hard to text back sometimes. I’ll read a message and stare at it for ages, just frozen, worried about saying the wrong thing or sounding stupid. What if I don’t have anything interesting to say? And then I feel guilty for not reaching out, and it spirals. It does make me appreciate friends who understand my weirdness though; if they knew how much I appreciate their presence, even when I’m quiet. I get that they’re just trying to connect, but sometimes it’s easier to find solace in silence than in chatter. And then there are those moments when I completely overthink what people think about me. Do they even notice I’m quiet, or do they find it weird? I want to scream “I’m nice! I promise I’m nice!” But it all gets caught in my throat and I just smile awkwardly instead. I really wish I could just be super chill and not get anxious about the little things. I sometimes have these dreams where I’m yelling at everyone to just stop judging me and it feels cathartic but then I wake up and realize it’s just a dream and I’m still stuck in my own head. Like, great, thanks for nothing brain. Oh, and don’t even get me started on group projects. I’m like a deer in headlights, terrified of sharing ideas. My mind races, but the exit door of my brain is locked tight. I can think of thoughts that I’d rather explore alone than present to the whole group. But then I feel bad for not contributing enough. What’s the balance? Is there a cheat code for being both involved and maintaining my sanity? I used to think about traveling a lot. How cool would it be to experience other cultures, taste different foods, see places in the world that are not just the gray campus buildings? But then I think about all the planning, and I feel paralyzed again, whirling in a cyclone of possibilities. I want to go everywhere but never leave my couch. It’s so frustrating. I could map out a trip to Nepal but what if I get there and can’t handle it? Honestly, I think I’m just in this constant tug-of-war with my own mind. Part of me is yearning for adventure while another part is hiding under a blanket, hoping it all goes away. I try to embrace those moments of openness, dive into new experiences and be a bit more spontaneous but it’s like I’m always walking on a tightrope, and one little gust of wind could send me tumbling. It’s exhausting. Still, amidst that chaos, somehow, I find beauty in the messy thoughts, the swirling ideas — and that’s what makes my world a little brighter, I guess.

Okay so I just saw a bunch of people walking by, like a parade of faces I don’t even know but it’s cool, I always find it funny how everyone’s in their own world, I mean what does that guy in the red hat think about every day? I wonder if he even thinks about what he’s wearing or if he just grabs whatever’s hanging in his closet. I never know what to wear either, but I don’t really care enough to stress over it, just throw on a t-shirt and jeans and let’s go, right? That’s how I feel about most things honestly, if it’s not fun or engaging I’m out. Why spend time worrying about details when there’s a whole world to explore? That’s the thing, I like exploring people and places and just being around noise, noise is fun. I thrive in crowds, feel alive when I’m moving from one thing to the next, not sure why it feels like that but it does and I can’t complain. My friends love to hang out but I usually end up planning things and they just tag along, it’s like herding cats really. Sometimes I can feel them dragging their feet behind, like they’d rather be binge-watching something on Netflix. I mean I get it, nice to chill but there’s a whole city to be out in. Last week we went to that new arcade place, I don’t remember the name but it had a funky vibe and tons of games. I jumped straight into the racing game, you know the one with the steering wheel and pedals? It was so much fun, I was yelling and laughing with my friends like we were kids again, no responsibilities, just pure joy. I think I won too, which is a plus. Everyone gets so serious about winning but hey if you’re having fun, does it really matter? They should have more places like that, places where you can just be loud and silly and not worry about how you come across to others. I hate that awkward tension in some spaces, that 'everyone is so serious' vibe. Why can’t we just enjoy? I feel that need for laughter and goofiness everywhere I go. It’s wild how people overthink everything, like that one friend who is so nice but overanalyzes every little thing, making it exhausting sometimes to even have a conversation. Like, come on, let’s just hang out and not dissect every word, this isn’t a class. I guess I don’t do the small talk thing either, though - too much fluff. Just get to the point, let’s have a real conversation about something that matters or just get silly. I mean, I don’t want to pretend everything is sunshine and rainbows, that’s boring, who can be that agreeable all the time? I don’t really care if someone disagrees with me either, I kind of enjoy a little debate and drama but not the heavy stuff. Just enough spice to keep things interesting. Sometimes I think about my future, what I’m going to do when I graduate. I should probably be more serious about it but honestly, the thought makes me want to run off to an amusement park and forget everything. I mean, does anyone really know what they want? Paperwork and planning, ew, I’d rather go on an adventure and see where life takes me. I hate being tied down and restricted by deadlines, not for me. I need freedom, spontaneity, and I love a good “let’s just see where this leads” attitude. My parents always want a solid plan, but life’s too unpredictable for that, right? And then there’s the constant buzzing of notifications on my phone. I can’t stand when it goes quiet for too long, that feeling of being disconnected. Gotta stay in the loop with the group chats, memes, and all that nonsense. I thrive off those moments of back-and-forth banter, it fuels me, gives me energy. Scrolling through social media can be exhausting but it’s also super entertaining, all those personalities clashing and mixing together. I mean, some people post the most ridiculous things, and it’s so relatable but also cringe at the same time, but I can’t stop laughing. How do they even think that way? Anyway, I should probably start thinking about assignments soon, but I’m just not feeling it. Feel like I’d rather go hang out or do anything else, maybe hit up that taco truck later. The world is huge with so much to see and do, and I don’t want to waste it sitting at a desk typing out essays. I don’t like being boxed in, I need to be out and about, feel that energy from other people. Just living in the moment, that’s the dream. So, whatever, let’s just see where today takes me, who needs a plan?

Okay so I just remembered that time last week when we went out for karaoke, we were all hyped up, like spontaneous and loud, and I really got into it, belting out some 90s pop song, everyone was singing along, I love that energy, makes me feel so alive. Everyone’s faces lit up and I felt like I was the star of the show, even though I can’t sing that well but who cares, right? It's about just having a good time. I wonder if the video is online, should ask the group chat, oh man, that reminds me I need to send out those pictures from the trip last month, we took so many, it was a blast. I love organizing things, like seriously, I thrive on planning events, even small get-togethers. It feels satisfying to see everything come together, everyone having a great time. Speaking of planning, I should probably start thinking about my schedule for next semester. I’m really enjoying my classes this year, so many interesting discussions in sociology, especially when we were talking about community dynamics, it’s just fascinating how people interact and connect. I mean, I can’t help but want to make sure everyone feels included and heard, sometimes I feel like the unofficial group leader when we do projects, but it’s cool, I’m good at keeping everyone on track. It’s a skill, right? Also, I’m getting really into volunteering more this year, there’s this local charity that I’ve been working with, helping kids with their homework, and it’s been one of the most fulfilling experiences ever. Seriously, every time I walk into that community center, I feel this rush of excitement, the kids are so full of energy and potential, and helping them feel supported just feels right. They look up to us, you know, and it’s like I’m making a small difference in their lives. I love being part of something bigger than myself, it makes me feel grounded. But then there’s the whole thing with my roommate, he’s been kind of mopey lately, and I want to help him but he doesn’t really talk about what’s bothering him, it’s frustrating because I’m naturally inclined to support my friends, especially when they’re down, but sometimes you just gotta give them space. I find that hard sometimes, you know? I just want to fix everything. I hope he opens up soon. I really think I could help. Reminds me of that time last semester when we threw a surprise party for Sarah’s birthday, she had no idea and it felt so great to see her reaction when she walked in. Everyone had contributed in little ways, food, decorations, music, and it was like a team effort, that’s what I love about my friend group, we just click and bounce ideas off each other effortlessly. I remember everyone just laughing and having a blast, it was one of those nights where nothing could bring us down, and when we all sang happy birthday it felt genuinely heartwarming. I sometimes wonder if I’m too focused on keeping the peace, though. Like, do I sacrifice my own opinions too much to avoid conflict? There was that one time I disagreed with the group on which restaurant to go to, but I didn’t want to stir the pot, so I just went along with the majority. Was I too agreeable? But in the end, it all worked out, the food was good, everyone was happy; still, I should probably work on speaking up a little more, I don’t want to be a pushover. Speaking of food, I really need to try cooking something new soon. I’ve been stuck in this rut of instant noodles and takeout lately. Pinterest has a million recipes, but I get overwhelmed looking at them. I tend to stick to my staples, you know? I have this delicious chicken stir fry recipe that I could make again, super easy, but it gets old fast. Maybe I’ll have a cooking night with friends, that sounds fun. I can handle the main dish and they can bring sides, teamwork makes the dream work, right? I should put that idea into action. But my calendar is filling up, tutoring, classes, work, oh and I need to check in with my family too. Gotta keep those connections strong. I always make time for my family, they mean everything to me, and I love hearing about their daily lives, it keeps me grounded. I can’t imagine life without those conversations. It makes me realize how important it is to cultivate relationships, they fill my bucket up. Ugh, why is it so hard to find time for everything? I guess I should just prioritize. Focus on the things that give me joy and make me feel productive, like my social life and supporting others. That’s what keeps me going, connecting with people, making them smile, and just being present. I can’t wait for the next big group outing or event, I just feel like that’s where I thrive the most, in the midst of laughter and connection. Alright, I’m definitely checking in with the crew about our next plans. Let’s do this!

Okay so I just realized I have a whole list of things to do and it’s kind of exciting actually. Like I love crossing things off lists, it’s so satisfying and it gives me a little rush. I think about organizing a game night this weekend. People love that, right? I can already imagine the laughter and the chaos, and it feels good to bring everyone together. Cards Against Humanity or something like that could be hilarious, or maybe we could even try a new board game. There’s this one called Codenames that I heard is really fun, it gets everyone talking and thinking creatively. Speaking of creativity, I’ve been thinking about painting again. I used to paint a lot in high school and I miss it. I painted a giant mural in my room, and it was such a vibe. Lots of colors and swirls and stuff—it felt like entering a different world every time I walked in. Maybe I’ll buy some canvases and just go for it. Just explode with colors on a Saturday afternoon while listening to my favorite playlist. I bet I could get some friends to join me too. Ugh, I also need to schedule that dentist appointment. Why do I always put that off? It’s not like it’s an awful experience, but I just dread the idea of sitting in that chair. Even thinking about it makes me feel a bit twitchy. Dental hygienists are nice, though, so at least there’s that. I feel like they always try to make it better with small talk. And then there’s the whole thing with my summer internship applications coming up. It’s exciting, but also stressful. I can’t wait to get out there and work on something real. I’ve got some cool ideas about what I want to do. I want to work in marketing, something that allows me to be creative and help businesses connect with people. Like how cool is it when a campaign resonates with you? I want to create something that makes people feel something. That could be a game-changer, right? Plus, it’s a chance to meet new people and network. I’ve always loved meeting new faces. Okay, enough about work. I could seriously talk forever about plans and projects. What was I saying? Oh, right! So, I’ve also been super into cooking lately. I’ve been trying to whip up new recipes every week. Last week it was homemade pasta, which was surprisingly fun, and the week before I tried my hand at Indian food—so spicy but so delicious! Cooking feels like such a cool mix of science and art. You get to be precise, but then you can also throw in whatever you want. I love the smells that fill the house too. It gets everyone interested in what's happening in the kitchen. I want to organize a potluck soon too—everyone can bring their favorite dish. I bet people would show up with all sorts of things and it would create such a fun atmosphere. I love how food brings people together. And, oh man, think of the Instagram stories! Everyone loves food pics. I can already envision the colorful spread: pasta, curries, maybe even some dessert. I could totally make one of those fun salad jars too—they're so visually appealing. Wow, I just remembered that time we went camping last summer. That was an adventure. We went hiking and swimming in that icy lake. It felt so liberating to disconnect from everything for a few days. I was reshooting my phone to take all those sunset pictures, and everyone was laughing at me but I didn’t care because those views were insane! It gave me a reminder of how important it is to just get away sometimes, to breathe fresh air and feel the sun on my skin. I should plan another trip soon. There’s something about being in nature that clears my mind. And I need to dive back into my reading! I’ve been slacking lately! The “To Read” pile is growing taller and taller. I have a few non-fiction books on creativity and personal development that I’ve been eager to dive into. Learning can be so thrilling, especially when it’s about topics I’m passionate about. I love expanding my mind and understanding the world better. OMG, and how could I forget about that concert coming up? I can’t wait! I’ve been waiting for these guys to come back for ages. There’s just something electric about being at a live show, surrounded by a crowd of people all vibing together, it’s like a collective energy that just lifts you up. I already have my outfit planned out, which is ridiculous but also kind of necessary. It has to be perfect, right? I really want to embrace every moment. So many possibilities ahead, and I want to soak them all in. How wonderful is it that every day can offer something new?

I just found this old notebook at the bottom of my bag. It's got pages all crumpled and some notes about classes. Why did I even keep it? I guess I thought I'd need it for something but here I am just staring at the pages and they seem so pointless now. I remember writing a whole bunch of stuff in the middle of the night and it made perfect sense then. Now, it feels like I’m just collecting dust. Why do people keep things? I'm not a hoarder but I hold on to stuff that doesn’t mean anything. The thing is, I don’t really feel the need to talk about my feelings or share stories. That whole self-disclosure thing is weird. Like, why would I tell someone about my day? It's just boring. I went to class, I sat in the back, tried to stay awake. That's it. No drama or excitement. Just sitting there, listening, or sometimes zoning out. I’d rather be by myself, honestly. I can’t deal with people crowding around me. It’s exhausting. People say that being alone is sad but I don’t feel sad. I feel fine being alone. If I need something, I can just get it myself. I don’t have to rely on anyone else to entertain me. I can scroll through my phone or read a book or something. Books are great. They don't ask me questions. They just sit there, waiting for me to open them up. I think people expect you to be this social butterfly but I don’t know how to play that game. It’s like speaking a language I don’t understand. And let’s not even start on small talk. The weather? Sports? Who cares? It’s just filler conversation. I’d rather get straight to the point. If I have something to say, I’ll say it. But it seems like everyone wants to dance around topics instead of just saying what they mean. Like, why don’t people just share their opinions without worrying about others’ feelings? It’s a waste of breath sometimes. I’m supposed to care if someone is sad or happy? I mean, I can listen but my empathy level is like non-existent. I remember someone saying I’m too blunt. They were upset because I told them their idea was stupid. But come on, if it’s dumb, it’s dumb. Why pretend? I’m not here to make everyone feel good about their ideas if they suck. Maybe it sounds harsh. It probably is but I can’t change how I think. Ugh, and group projects. They always end up being a mess. I hate being put in a situation where I have to put in effort for others. Everyone thinks they can just slack off and let me do the work. It’s like they see me as the quiet one who will take charge, but I don’t want that role. I just want to do my part and move on. It’s not like I’m going to volunteer to lead or anything. I’d rather just fade into the background. Sometimes I feel like I’m required to fit into this mold of what a good classmate or friend should be. They expect you to be supportive and encouraging, but that’s not me. I don’t have that kind of energy. I mean, I care about my grades, so I’ll do what I have to do, but you won’t catch me being all buddy-buddy with my classmates. I’d rather sit alone in the cafeteria with my phone than try to find someone to talk to. Taking tests is kind of a relief because there’s no interaction. Just me and the paper. I can zone out in a way that feels okay because no one is watching. I can focus on the questions and not have to worry about how I’m coming off to someone else in the room. It’s about numbers and answers. I don’t have to smile or nod. Just fill in the bubbles and leave. And I don’t get why people are so obsessed with knowing new things. New experiences, new ideas, everything has to be fresh and exciting. I don’t need that. I like the static things in life. I like knowing how things work. I don’t want to just jump into something that’s new and unpredictable. And then there’s all this pressure to be open-minded. Why should I accept everything? I know what I like, what I don’t like, and that’s good enough for me. It’s okay to stick to your guns. I don’t need to expand my horizons if I’m comfortable where I am. I guess at the end of the day, I’m fine just existing in my own little bubble. I don’t need to please anyone else. I’ll just keep doing my own thing, whatever that is, scribbling more in notebooks until they’re full of thoughts that are mine alone.

Okay so I was just thinking about how weird it is that some people thrive on solitude while I just can’t stand it for too long I mean I need people around me like I feed off their energy and sometimes I wonder if that’s a good or bad thing because I’ll be in a group and I love hearing everyone’s voices and laughing and sharing ideas but then there’s this creeping anxiety that maybe I’m talking too much or maybe I’m saying the wrong thing and I just want everyone to like me it’s exhausting but also exhilarating like a roller coaster but I like roller coasters but I also hate that feeling of my stomach dropping but that’s what it feels like when I’m in a social situation I don’t know if anyone else feels that way but there’s always this part of me that wants to please people and I’m always looking for that approval which is probably why I care so much about my grades and I put so much effort into everything while also being that one friend who’s always up for a spontaneous road trip or an impromptu karaoke night because who could say no to belting out some throwback hits with a bunch of friends at midnight right? It’s the best feeling when you’re all singing together and it’s so liberating and I think that’s where my openness comes in like I’m always willing to try new things and explore different ideas it’s like a whole world of possibilities is just waiting to be discovered and I can’t just sit in my room and study all day even though I know I have to sometimes because exams are coming and I can’t afford to bomb them but then I start daydreaming about all the interesting conversations I could be having or the places I could be visiting and it drives me a little nuts. I mean why do weekends fly by so fast? I can barely keep track of days and then Monday rolls around and my heart sinks because I just want to be back at the party or the beach or wherever I was laughing with friends and doing something fun instead of sitting in class trying to take notes and not zone out because that’s what I do I zone out a lot it’s frustrating but also kind of funny like I could probably write a whole book of my random thoughts and it would probably be a best-seller in the self-help genre about how to keep yourself entertained while studying or how to balance social life with academic pressure and it’s wild how many times I’ve had to remind myself that it’s okay to take a break or step back from something like I need to breathe and find my center which sounds cliché but it helps to just sit in silence sometimes and think about nothing which is hard because my brain is always racing like a hamster on a wheel just spinning and spinning never stopping and I wonder if everyone else feels this way or if I’m just a weirdo and then I get worried that maybe I’m not relatable or that my thoughts are too chaotic and no one would understand but then I remind myself that it’s just me being human and we all have our moments of doubt and vulnerability and I wish more people would talk about that because it’s not all sunshine and rainbows, right? It can't be all about the fun times and late-night hangouts because life is messy and unpredictable and that’s the beauty of it but also the struggle like sitting in the library surrounded by stacks of books and trying to focus while my mind is like “Hey remember that time we went to that concert and danced like idiots?” and I’m torn between wanting to relive that moment and needing to study for that huge paper due next week so then I go to grab a snack because snacks are life but then I end up scrolling through social media and oh my gosh everyone is posting about their perfect lives and I feel this twinge of jealousy and I think I should really just focus on my own journey but it’s so hard when you’re constantly reminded of how everyone else seems to be thriving and I’m not seriously sitting on my couch binging a show while trying to keep the anxiety at bay because what if I never measure up and then I shake my head because I know that’s a toxic way to think but it’s hard to escape that spiral and I wish I could just embrace my flaws more often and let go of the worry because there’s so much I want to do like travel to Italy and eat gelato on the streets or volunteer at a local shelter and give back because I know that feels good to help others but then my brain is like “But what about your to-do list?” and it’s like a tug-of-war in my head and what I really need is to just cut myself some slack and enjoy those moments but then there’s this pressure to have everything figured out and I know I don’t but man, life is just a mess sometimes, isn’t it?

It’s weird how some days I wake up and just feel this surge of energy, like everything is possible, and then other days I’m more subdued but still excited for what might happen next. Okay so speaking of energy, I really love how a good playlist can set the tone for the whole day. I was listening to a mix of jazz and some chill electronic stuff while I was getting ready earlier and it was just the right vibe. Really gets the creative juices flowing or whatever. I wonder how people come up with those mixes. Do they just throw random songs together? I feel like there’s an art to it. A science, maybe? Hm, I should try making one. I’ve got so many songs saved. The thrill of discovery, of finding that perfect track that just vibes with my mood. Music is like, the language of the universe, isn’t it? Everyone can connect through it even if they don’t speak the same words. Speaking of connections, I had this great conversation with a classmate last week. We were both at the coffee shop, and they had this amazing idea for a project. We ended up brainstorming together for hours. I love that. When you just bounce ideas off each other and it feels electric. Like I can just feel my brain lighting up with what-ifs and that leads to more what-ifs. It’s like a chain reaction, and the best part is how it feels like a team effort. There’s something really satisfying about collaborating. I wonder if we’ll actually turn that idea into something real. I should follow up with them. Make it happen! Right, so speaking of making things happen, I’ve been trying to get into a new routine. You know how everyone’s always talking about productivity hacks and morning rituals? I thought I’d give it a shot. So I’ve been waking up earlier. Not like crazy early but just enough to have a moment to myself before the chaos starts. I’ve been journaling too. Just random thoughts, nothing too deep, just what’s in my head when I wake up. It’s funny, some days it’s just a list of things to do, and other days I find myself writing about the color of the sky or some random thing I spotted on my walk back from class. I actually took a picture of a cat the other day, just chilling on a doorstep. It looked so content, like the world was made for it. I love that—how little things like that can make your day better. What’s next on my agenda? Oh right, I wanted to try this new recipe tonight. I've been trying to incorporate more veggies into my meals because I feel like I could be better at that. I always hear people say, “eat the rainbow!” or whatever and I just think, why not? Food is such an experience. And cooking can be such a fun adventure, especially if I get friends involved. We could have a cooking night! Just a bunch of us, and I could teach them how to make this new dish. Or we could have a themed dinner night. Everyone brings a dish from a different culture. I’ve been reading about Indian cuisine lately and it seems so rich and varied. The spices! I love cooking with spices. It transforms everything. The scent, the flavor, the colors. Ah, and speaking of colors, I need to finish that painting I started last week. I’ve been meaning to get back to it. I just really love expressing myself through art. It’s like a mix of chaos and control. I try to go abstract because I enjoy breaking the rules. I want to let the paint flow and see where it takes me. Some people don’t get it, but it’s not about the end product. It’s about the process. Just like life in a way, right? You can plan as much as you want, but the surprises are what make it interesting. I think I’m drawn to experiences that feel authentic and real, even if they’re messy. Random thought— I really want to travel more. Like everywhere. I feel like there’s so much to see and do. I’m itching for a big adventure, maybe go backpacking through Europe or exploring some hidden gems in South America. There’s just this whole world out there waiting to be explored. The cultures, the people, the food! Oh man, I can’t get enough of new experiences. I should probably get a travel buddy so that I can share it with someone. Wouldn’t that be cool? We could make memories, take lots of photos, and maybe even get lost a bit. Getting lost can lead to the best stories. Anyway, I’ve been thinking a lot about friendships lately too. How important they are. Surrounding myself with positive, supportive people makes such a difference. I like how they challenge me, how we motivate each other to grow. I think that’s what life is all about. Connections, experiences, and being open to what comes our way. Okay, I feel like I could keep going but I should probably get back to that painting. Or maybe I’ll just grab a snack first. Yeah, snacks are key to creative processes!

Okay so I was thinking about how much I love talking to people, like I can’t get enough of it, but then there's that rush of anxiety sometimes when I’m trying to keep a conversation going and I get all these thoughts racing in my head like am I saying the right thing am I making it awkward do they even want to talk to me, I just want to connect but my brain keeps spinning out all these worst-case scenarios. The other day someone told me I’m really good at making friends but I also feel like I just latch onto people too quickly, like I get super invested right away and hope they reciprocate and then it’s like this rollercoaster of emotions because if they don’t it just knocks the wind out of me. And the way I can meet someone new and feel like I’ve known them forever, it’s exhilarating! Like I meet these people at parties and we just click, and it’s all laughter and deep convos about life and dreams and then I leave, buzzing and energized. But then I’m at home and it hits me, that little voice saying what if they didn't like you as much as you liked them or what if they think you're annoying or clingy or something, and it's like a punch to the gut—why can’t I just chill? In my head, I’m thinking about all the things I should’ve said or shouldn’t have done, but then I remember how I forgot a friend's birthday last week and I felt terrible but I guess that’s just part of life, right? Friends forgive, or they don’t and then it was probably for the best if they don’t, but still, I wish I was better at keeping track of that stuff, like can’t I just be a little more organized? But also it’s just so tiring to think about all these little social rules, like why is it so complicated? I love how open and free I can be with my thoughts, and I guess that’s why I jump from one thing to another—everything just fascinates me. I’ll see a cool photo on Instagram and it sparks some random memory of a trip I took where everything was bright colors and weird street food that I look back on and think wow I can’t believe I tried that! Or there’s that feeling of nostalgia just creeping in sometimes, and I have to text someone about it because I can't hold it in. I want everyone to share in this moment with me, like remember that time when we almost got lost but ended up finding that hidden cafe? I don’t even like studying for exams or writing essays most times because I’d rather just be out connecting with people or exploring new places. Who really cares about research papers when there’s a world full of conversations to have? I mean, the world is so much bigger than just my tiny bubble of classes and assignments. My roommate’s been trying to get me to plan our time better, maybe set some schedules? But he doesn't get it; I can't commit to that stuff. It feels so limiting! I want to be spontaneous, to just grab my phone and say let’s hit the road or let’s go to that concert tonight! Who knows what kind of stories will come from that? But then there’s the flip side to all that energy and spontaneity. When I’m feeling anxious about all the things I should've done or should be doing, it’s overwhelming. Sometimes I just hide away in my room, like when I had that big fight with my best friend last month—still can’t believe it even happened. I hate conflict! My heart races thinking about it, but part of me also loves the drama of it all. It’s like every day is an emotional rollercoaster. I can go from high to low so fast, it's almost ridiculous. I know I need to work on some structure, they say being organized helps, whatever that means, but there’s something appealing about the chaos too, and I thrive in it—the unexpected turns in conversations, the late-night debates that go nowhere but feel so vital in the moment. I thrive on that rush of life, but then there are those moments where I catch myself spiraling into worry, caught up in my own head, and suddenly I’m just tired, anxious, pacing my room thinking, will I ever get it together? But then I remember all the beauty in not knowing what the next moment holds. I just want to embrace everything—friends, adventures, new experiences, because at the end of the day, what’s life without a little bit of risk, a little bit of chaos? Sure, it might drive me a bit crazy sometimes, but who doesn’t love a good story to tell?

Okay so I’m thinking about how I just love meeting new people, like every time I walk into a room full of faces I don’t know, it feels like opening a gift, you never know what you’re going to get. I mean, sometimes it’s a huge letdown, like when someone just stares blankly at you or gives you one-word answers, but other times it’s pure gold. You know, those conversations where you just click and suddenly you’re sharing your life stories and laughing until your sides hurt? Those are the best. I had one of those last week at that networking event, remember? I went thinking it would be all serious business but then I ended up chatting with this artist who makes sculptures out of recycled materials. I can’t stop thinking about it. I want to try it myself now. I’ve got a bunch of old stuff lying around that could probably become amazing things. I just need to find time in my schedule between classes and club meetings and work. Speaking of which, I really need to get my calendar organized. I have this planner that I’m obsessed with, it’s color-coded and everything. I love when things are organized. Makes me feel like I have my life together, even if I’m still piecing it together just like that artist with her recycled sculptures. What was she saying about how she feels connected to the world through her work? I get that. It’s like when I’m volunteering, spreading good vibes and all that. It feels fantastic to be part of something bigger. I mean, just the other day, we sorted food at that pantry for families in need. The smiles on their faces, the gratitude—it’s like fuel. I just want to keep doing good stuff like that. It motivates me. I was thinking about traveling again too. There’s so much of the world to see, and I want to explore different cultures. Italy has been on my mind a lot. Imagine walking through the streets of Rome, eating gelato and laughing with friends, meeting locals who tell you the best places to eat. I’ve heard the food there is heavenly, like holy levels of amazing. But also, there’s just something magical about sitting in a small café, writing in a journal, people-watching as life unfolds around me. I bet I could meet some interesting characters there too. Also, I can’t get over how much I love spontaneity. Like I’ll be hanging with friends, and someone will suggest, “Hey, let’s go to the beach!” and I’m totally in. There’s something electric about deciding to do something on a whim, like those late-night drives to nowhere, just blasting music and singing at the top of our lungs. One time we ended up at this diner at 2 AM and had the best pancakes ever. I wonder if they still serve those pancakes. Or if that diner was just a figment of my sleep-deprived imagination. Let’s talk about the music though. I can’t get enough of discovering new bands and songs. My playlist is never-ending; it’s like a soundtrack to my life. I find it super fun to curate playlists for different moods and occasions. I made this one for studying, filled with chill indie vibes, and then there’s the one for working out that gets my blood pumping. It’s almost like I can channel energy from the music and turn it into motivation. That rush after a good workout? Unbeatable. I think about the friendships I’ve made too. They mean so much to me. It’s wild how you can meet someone just once and it feels like you’ve known them your whole life. I remember running into that girl from my sociology class at a concert and we talked for hours. Now she’s one of my closest pals. We have this little group that goes out to trivia nights, and the laughter is nonstop. I swear, my life would feel dull without all these incredible people who just bring color and joy into my days. Not that I can’t enjoy my own company too. I’ve had some pretty fun solo adventures—eating at restaurants by myself, going to art galleries, living life on my own terms. Anyway, I was thinking about how every experience, every person you meet is like a thread in the tapestry of life. Just stitching together moments that become memories, good ones and bad ones, but all meaningful in some way. I mean how wild is it that we all get to live this life? It’s like we’re all on this journey together, each with our own stories but interconnected somehow. Kind of makes me feel grateful. I want to make sure I’m leaving a little positivity wherever I go, you know? I just want to be that person who brightens someone’s day, even in the simplest ways. I’m ready for more adventures. More laughter, more heartwarming moments, more connections with people. Life is just too short to sit on the sidelines. I want to embrace it all. Each moment, each conversation, each spontaneous decision. Here’s to living life out loud!

Right, so I was thinking about how wild it is that I met Sarah last week at that party. Like, she just walked up to me and started talking about her cool travel stories. I couldn't even keep track, but I just loved it, I felt so alive. It’s funny how people can click so quickly. I’ve always been one to just dive into conversations, doesn’t matter if it’s at a party or in line for coffee or whatever. But then it occurs to me, did I even ask her any questions? I was just all over the place with excitement and then she was gone, poof! And now I can’t even remember half of what she said. So much for making a meaningful connection. Oh well. Ugh, I really need to figure out this whole life thing. Like, why can’t I keep my room clean? I mean, it’s a disaster right now, clothes everywhere, but I just can’t seem to care enough to fix it. I spiral into this cycle of feeling guilty and then just completely ignoring it. Maybe if I had a cute roommate or something, I’d feel more driven to clean up. But honestly, I love living alone. It’s like this freedom bubble. I can blast my music at 2 a.m. and it’s just me and my playlists. Oh, speaking of music, that new album by that indie band is fire. I heard it on a playlist the other day and now it’s all I can listen to. I should totally tell my friends about it. They’ll love it. Do I have enough friends? That’s always a thought that sneaks in. Sometimes I feel like everyone is busy with their own lives, and I get this little twinge of panic, like what if I’m just drifting alone? But then, I don’t know, I’m always meeting people. Like that time I joined that art class because I wanted to learn how to paint but ended up just chatting with everyone instead of actually painting. I think the teacher was a bit annoyed, but hey, people! That’s what life’s about, right? Connecting? I wish I could get better at focusing though. I mean, self-discipline sounds boring, but it would help with my constant running around in my mind. What was I saying? Oh yeah, painting and art. I should definitely go back to that class, but also, what if I hate it? I never know until I try, though. Also, I keep thinking about how social media just fuels anxiety for me. I scroll through my feed, and it feels like everyone is living this flawless life, while I’m just here wondering if I should order takeout or try to cook something that probably won’t turn out right. I once burned pasta; how does that even happen? Like, is that a skill? Burnt pasta? I can laugh about it now, but in the moment, it felt like a catastrophe. And then my friend posted this gorgeous dinner plate that looked like a gourmet meal and I felt this surge of jealousy. Why can’t I be more creative in the kitchen? I mean, maybe it’s not for me. I’d rather just grab a slice of pizza and run to the next adventure. Speaking of adventures, I really want to go hiking soon! I’ve heard about this beautiful trail that goes through the woods and overlooks a lake. Nature makes me feel grounded, which is ironic because I feel everything BUT grounded half the time. I want to find a couple of buddies to go with me. The last hike I went on was hilarious. We ended up getting lost and it turned into this epic bonding experience. We all laughed more than we should have. I think it got dark before we found our way back. The stars, though! That was incredible! I could see constellations clearer than I ever have before, like all those little dots telling stories. It was like the universe was trying to say, “Chill, you’re gonna be okay.” And I don’t know if it’s just me being dramatic, but sometimes I feel so overwhelmed by life’s noise. I need these moments. Okay, but what’s with my inability to close my messages? I mean, I’ll text someone and then just keep adding more instead of hitting send. It can get so chaotic in my head; sometimes I wish I could just pour it all out to someone, but then I worry they won’t get it or think I’m too much. Is there such a thing as too much? Maybe I should keep a journal. That might help, but ugh, commitment. I’m already saying I’ll work on my room for ages now, and here we are. At least I have plans this weekend! There’s that festival downtown. I can’t wait to just roam around, try different foods, and talk to strangers. Oh, and the music! So much good music. I can feel the energy buzzing in my veins just thinking about it. People everywhere, laughter, and colors. I wish everyday could feel like that.

Yesterday feels like a distant memory already. Time is weird like that. I should probably make a to-do list because those help but I kind of know what I have to do. Just keep going through the motions and everything gets done eventually, right? I said I’d finish that chapter for psych, but I keep getting distracted. I mean how many textbooks do I have now? Four? Five? Do I even need that many? I always get them but half the time I only look at the summaries. They’re like background noise, always there but not really part of the picture. I wish I could just stick to one book, one subject, but the course load feels heavy sometimes. I guess it’s about being responsible and managing time. Gotta stay organized.
