It’s annoying when things pile up. My roommate is kind of loud. She’s nice I guess, but sometimes I wish she’d just chill. It doesn’t have to be a constant party. If I didn't know better I'd think she’s trying way too hard to impress people. Like, why? It’s exhausting just watching her. I’m still processing the fact that some people thrive on social interaction. Seems foreign. There’s an appeal to silence; it feels good. I can hear myself think. I get it, some people gain energy from being around others but I just don't get that vibe. I like my routine. I don’t want things to change too much. Stability feels crucial. The weather is changing. Fall is moving in. I can smell the leaves. That’s nice for like, two weeks but then it gets cold, and I hate being cold. The idea of winter is creeping up. Do I even want to think about that? I can't deal with it now, maybe I'll just hibernate. Just grab a blanket, curl up, and ignore everything. What’s that saying about winter? Something about waiting for spring? Yeah, not really my style. I’d rather just keep going with the tasks. Less thinking, more doing. Gotta focus on what needs to be done. What was that? Oh right, the psych paper. I should outline it. I like outlines. They’re neat and organized. Helps to see what I need to include, what I need to research. Research isn’t that bad when you get into a groove. Find a couple of peer-reviewed articles, stick to the facts, add some citations. Boom, done. And then what? Just sit back and wait for grades? Nah. No time for that. Need to keep moving. It’s almost like a game, see how much I can juggle without dropping anything. But I can’t drop anything. That would be a disaster. I hate the thought of failing. Not that it’s an option for me. I can’t remember the last time I got a C. Or maybe I just block it out because my brain prioritizes success over failure. I don’t even think I care what people think, but somehow the pressure lingers. Do others feel it as heavily as I do? I guess I can't pretend to understand everyone’s experience. I see people getting upset over little things, how do they handle those emotions? Just talking it out seems painful. I don't want to do that. I’d rather just keep everything in check. Keep emotions streamlined. Like a computer. Just run the program. No glitches, no crashes. But there's always someone who wants to vent. Venting feels like a time sink. What good is it? Just a cycle of complaints. Why waste the time when it can be spent on pragmatism? It doesn’t seem pragmatic to dwell on those things, right? It’s distracting. I should probably go work on my paper instead of ruminating. But then I remember that I need a break. A small one. Just enough time to recharge. I can pencil that in too, breaks can be strategic. I don’t need to waste time scrolling through social media, that’s another pointless excursion. It just adds noise. I don’t want distractions. Just want a clear path to the finish line. Sure, I might be missing out on ‘life’ but honestly it doesn’t sound all that great. I’d rather build my own success, my own path. I see some friends making choices that I can’t fathom. Partying too much, zero focus on anything constructive. What’s the end goal? Seems pointless. Like running in circles. I can’t grasp it. But whatever you do you, I guess? Not me though. I want my plans to align, my studies to pay off, my objectives met with precision. I keep thinking about that next step. Graduate school? I don’t know yet. Focus, don’t overthink. One step at a time. Feels right. The world can wait.

okay so I’m sitting here thinking about how the world feels so overwhelming sometimes like everything is just too loud too bright too much you know I see people walk by and they’re laughing and talking and it’s like why is that so hard for me but then I remember that the last group project I did was like pulling teeth because working with others makes me anxious I can’t help but overthink everything what if they think I’m stupid or what if I say something wrong or what if they don’t pull their weight I end up just doing everything myself and it’s so frustrating but I guess that’s what you get when you score high on conscientiousness right that need to be perfect and get things done but then it feels like a burden at the same time like I can’t escape it it’s like being stuck on a treadmill and the more I try to keep up the faster it goes it’s exhausting I wish I could just relax sometimes forget about deadlines and just chill like everyone else but nope I have to have my planner out every day mapping things out writing lists checking things off it’s so satisfying and yet so suffocating and the nagging feeling of not being good enough is always in the back of my mind so it’s hard to genuinely enjoy things like I see people enjoying spontaneous plans and I just can’t wrap my mind around it what if it messes up my carefully laid out plans what if we don’t go where I envisioned what if I end up at a place that’s too crowded or loud or just uncomfortable and don’t get me started on social media I see those perfect photos and I wonder if I’ll ever be that happy but I can’t help but feel like they’re all fake and filtered like yeah sure they’re smiling but what happens behind the scenes what’s the real story do they ever feel lonely like I do sometimes scrolling through Instagram wishing I could be that carefree but knowing I’m just not built that way I see friends hanging out and I don’t get invited and it stings even though I logically understand that it’s probably because I’m not very good at reaching out or keeping in touch that it’s my fault but really it’s hard to be the one who always makes the first move and then get shut down or ignored that just feeds the anxiety I can’t win if there’s a group text going on I’ll probably just watch from the sidelines the same way I do when I’m at parties I can feel the edges of panic creeping in the constant buzz of conversation makes me feel like I’m drowning sometimes I wish I could just yell “hello darkness my old friend” because well it feels like I’m so familiar with those feelings the anxiety the neuroticism it’s like my default setting can I ever escape it it pushes me to be better in some ways I guess I mean my grades are pretty solid but at what cost do I really have to sacrifice my happiness for good grades I don’t know sometimes I wonder if I’m too open though like I’ll see a piece of art or read something that strikes me and it just hits me right in the gut I feel so intensely like I’ll be moved to tears by a song or a story or an image and then I think about how I want to share that with someone but then I overthink it too there’s a voice in my head saying is this going to connect with them or are they going to think I’m weird or oversensitive so I just end up keeping it to myself even when I really want to share I’m great at observing but not great at sharing feelings because what’s the point sometimes I feel like I’m living in a bubble looking out at a world I can’t truly connect with that’s why I love writing though it’s like an escape I can pour everything out on the page without worrying about how it’ll be received and I can write about my anxieties my thoughts my everything without interruption it feels freeing to put everything into words even if nobody ever reads it it’s like I’m screaming in a soundproof room and just hoping someone can hear me but they never do that’s okay though because it’s mine it’s my space but then I think what if I’m just wasting my time what if none of this matters in the long run and I can’t help but spiral into thoughts of failure and regret and what am I even doing with my life is college the right choice or am I just prolonging the inevitable and is everyone else actually just pretending to have it all together while I’m over here falling apart inside it’s so hard to keep up sometimes I want to just break free or be one of those people who can go out and just enjoy life without a care in the world but that’s not me I guess and one day I hope I can be okay with that.

Okay so I just remembered that time I went to the art exhibit with my friend, the one with the weird sculptures that looked like they were melting. I didn’t really understand half of it but it was so cool to see all those colors and materials. I like art, I like how it makes me feel different things, even if I can’t explain why. Sometimes I wonder if I should have studied art instead of psychology but then I think maybe I’d just be one of those struggling artists working in a coffee shop. Not that there's anything wrong with that, but the idea of it just makes me feel a bit anxious. I don’t want to be in a position where I have to hustle for every cent. Anyway, my friend was really into this whole “interpretation” thing, like she’d grab my arm and say, “What does this one mean to you?” and I’m like, “Dude, it’s a big blob. It’s just a blob.” But she saw a whole narrative in it and I admired that. I wonder if I should have taken a painting class or something. Would I enjoy it? Or would I stress out about getting it right? Not that I’m super meticulous anyway, I mean I can be pretty messy with my spaces, my notes are all over the place, and I never remember to feed my plants. They probably hate me for that. Plants are supposed to be low-maintenance, but mine always look a bit sad. I feel like I’m failing them, like I should be more attentive. Life feels so chaotic, yet when I think about settling down... I don't know if I can. I like the idea of traveling everywhere. I want to see all the things and meet all the people. I want to experience life but in my own way, not in a touristy way. I think it would be nice to just walk through a city and see where it takes me. That whole “lost in translation” vibe sounds appealing. Maybe I’d find a cute café tucked away in a quiet street or accidentally stumble into a local music scene. I’ve always wanted to just jump into a new culture, but then there’s that voice that says, “What if you don’t fit in?” But then again, fitting in sounds boring, like everyone’s just playing a part. I can’t help but feel more aligned with the oddballs, the ones who dance to a different beat, and I appreciate how they see beauty in the bizarre. I had a class on diversity in psychology and it kind of opened my eyes, made me think about all the different ways people can view the world. I remember this one example that blew my mind, where cultures perceive colors differently. Some cultures categorize blues and greens differently than we do, and I just thought, wow, how many realities are there packed into this world? It’s a wild thought, so many interpretations and so many colors to life. But even with that chaos of perspectives, I find myself wanting to connect, like really connect with people. I can sit for hours listening to someone share their story, even if it’s a simple story. There’s just something about human experience that pulls me in, the nuances, the small details that make each tale unique. Yet, I struggle to share my own. I guess it’s that inverse relationship between openness and sharing. I could write a novel but never show it to anyone. I can doodle my thoughts but never let anyone see. I think about all the things I put off because I’m not sure they’ll resonate with anyone else. I remember this one time I started writing a poem about the sky, just the way it shifts from blue to orange to deep purple and I wrote it for a class, but then I chickened out and never turned it in. My professor would probably have been nice, but what if they just thought it was lame? It can be easier to keep things inside. I guess I’m just scared of judgment. I mean, I want to be authentic, but I don’t know if my authenticity would be accepted. It’s easier to encourage others to be their true selves, than to do it myself. Maybe one day I’ll get brave. I think about this often: courage and vulnerability go hand in hand but still, I can’t shake that fear. I want to write about feeling and art and all those random observations about life but then I get self-conscious. Self-doubt rolls in like a winter storm, unexpected and cold. I don’t want to overthink it, but here I am, stuck in my head. The world is too big to be this small. And yet my mind just keeps circling, thinking about food, like I haven’t eaten yet today. Hunger is creeping in. What am I going to have? Maybe some spaghetti? I have that leftover marinara sauce I made last week. Simple. Easy. Okay, focus. Food first, thoughts later.

okay so I was just thinking about how the leaves are starting to change colors and it’s crazy how every year it’s like this cycle of beauty but also a reminder that everything is fleeting like that one time we went hiking last fall and got lost but turned out to be the best day ever because we found that little waterfall and just sat there for hours talking about who knows what. I remember the sun was starting to set and everything was glowing like golden hour light is just the best why can’t it be like that all the time. sometimes I wonder if people realize how much beauty is in the everyday stuff like how you can find a great coffee shop just by wandering around not knowing if you’ll even like the coffee but just being open to whatever. it's funny how so many people seem to rush through life when they could just take a moment to look around and appreciate it all. I mean I used to get anxious about making plans and I still do to some extent but there’s something so liberating about just going with the flow and letting the day unfold. I don’t really care about sticking to a schedule, never have, it feels so restrictive. like when my friends plan everything down to the minute, it just exhausts me. I remember being in a group chat and they’re like, “Okay, we’re meeting at 3:00 for lunch” and I'm just thinking can we not just see what happens that day? the other day I was at the park and this little kid came up to me and asked if I wanted to play tag and I said yes without even thinking like how refreshing that is. I felt like a kid again. I mean who cares what people think when you’re running around in public playing tag with a bunch of 6-year-olds. is that even acceptable? also, I should probably think about what I'm eating more because I have this tendency to just grab whatever is convenient instead of cooking something nice. I mean I love food but cooking feels like such a chore. I wonder how people find the time to meal prep on Sunday nights. I can barely keep track of my laundry sometimes. but whatever, I guess life’s too short to worry about being perfect, right? oh speaking of being perfect this one time I got into a debate with my friend about what makes a good leader and he was all about the management skills while I'm like what about empathy and understanding because you can’t lead people if you don’t know them or at least try to. sometimes I feel like I’m like that in group projects. I could probably care less about the grade as long as we’re being kind to each other and actually getting to know one another. I always end up being the “peacekeeper” even when I don’t want to be because somehow people expect that of me based on my personality test results but it’s not like I’m a pushover or anything I just get why people feel the way they do. the other thing is music oh my God I just love music. I could listen to it all day seriously. like there’s this band I started getting into it’s all indie and feel-good vibes and I wonder why people ever get angry or sad with songs like that. it literally just lifts me up. it's strange how music can evoke all these memories like I can hear a song and suddenly be twenty again riding in a car with friends during summer blasting songs from that one playlist we made that was everything. I need that feeling again I really do. it's also wild how I have so many playlists for different moods like there’s my chill playlist, my adventure playlist, and my sad creative playlist that’s just perfect for when I feel like writing poetry or something which I really should do more of. my roommate always says I’m a dreamer which I guess is true, and I get it I can be a little all over the place when it comes to focusing on things. I have ideas for so many things but then I get distracted by something else like scrolling through TikTok or realizing I haven’t read that book I was so excited about. I need to finish that book. I really do. also, I need to remember to call my grandparents more often I think about them but then it just slips my mind. they’re getting older and I want to make sure I don’t take time for granted. my family means the world to me and sometimes I think I worry more about others than I do about myself which isn’t exactly healthy but it’s hard to switch that off sometimes. like if I could just be perfectly content helping people all the time, I totally would. but I have to find that balance. and wow I can’t believe I just wrote all this without really realizing it. I feel like I’ve just poured out a bit of my brain. it’s kind of freeing. I think I could do this more often.

Okay so I was just thinking about how I can spend hours in my head and be totally fine with it, like people are always rushing around, wanting to socialize or go out but I could sit in a quiet room with a book or some deep thoughts and be so content, it’s kind of magical, really. There’s something interesting about the idea of introverts, how we recharge by being alone, and it doesn't mean we don't care about connecting with people, it’s just a different vibe, right? I mean, sometimes I do wish I had the energy for big parties, like everyone is there laughing and shouting and it looks fun but inside my head, I’m kind of like, “Isn’t there a cool podcast I could be listening to instead?” or maybe I could be dreaming up a new project or diving into an interesting article. Speaking of projects, I’ve been brainstorming a lot lately about possibly starting a blog or even a podcast. There’s this whole world of ideas swirling around in my mind about sustainability, health, and how small personal habits can make a big difference. It’s so fascinating to dig into research and see what others are doing but then there's this nagging thought about how to communicate those ideas without overwhelming people, right? I never want to come off preachy or anything. I feel like there’s so much potential for connection through these topics and I genuinely want to share the love. And then there’s this part of me that wonders if I’m just overthinking it all. I mean, what if it doesn’t end up being perfect? Is it really worth it if I’m not delivering a flawless message? But then again, what does perfection even mean in a world that’s shifting and changing every day? I should probably push that thought away and just get things going. There’s beauty in imperfection and all that. Moments like these make me feel so open to experiences. I’ve also been thinking about how much I appreciate my friends. Like, they mean the world to me, even if I don’t see them every week or anything. There’s something about just knowing they’re out there, living their lives. We have a small group, which I like. It feels manageable and real. We can talk about anything from deep philosophical discussions to our favorite shows. Recently, we binge-watched that new series that everyone seems to be raving about, and it's just so cool to share those moments and then have discussions about the characters and what they represent. It’s a nice balance between light-hearted entertainment and deeper meaning. I love that blend and I find that so many people get that now, they care about substance being intertwined with fun. Moving on, I’ve been thinking about the environment a lot. I read this article about how our collective habits, even the small things like reducing plastic use, can really shape the future, and it’s empowering to think about the impact we can have. I remember when I first learned about composting, and I just went down this rabbit hole, wanting to know everything. Now I’m the designated compost guru among my friends, which is kind of funny because I never really thought I’d be that person. But it feels so good to help inform others about something I’m passionate about—it’s so rewarding. Speaking of passions, I do wish I had more time to be creative. I love tapping into different mediums, painting or doodling here and there; it’s like a breath of fresh air. It doesn’t have to be perfect, but it’s just the act of creating that brings me such joy. I get so excited thinking about starting some new pieces but there’s always this voice that says, “You should probably clean your room first” or “Maybe you should finish that assignment first.” But sometimes I wish I could just escape those responsibilities and let creativity flow freely without the weight of all those obligations. I also wonder about travel and where I’d go if money and time were no object. I’ve always dreamed of visiting Japan and immersing myself in the culture—the food, the art, the landscapes. There’s something about experiencing life through a different lens, that openness to new sights and sounds while also making sure to respect what’s there. I think it’s super important to understand the culture and community wherever you go. Okay, there’s so much I want to explore and learn; life is so rich and full of experiences waiting to be had. It’s a balancing act between wanting to connect with others and this deep-seated love for solitude. There’s a beauty in both. But I guess that’s just how I see it, trying to weave those threads together into something meaningful while enjoying the journey in my own quiet way. What a ride!

I just saw a squirrel outside. It’s weird how they’re always just running around like they own the place. Seriously, who gave them that kind of confidence? I wanna channel that energy. But then I think about how my life is like a series of spontaneous decisions, and I wonder if I should start a podcast about my life because people always say I have wild stories to tell. But then again, that seems like a commitment, and I'm not really into following through on things. Like that time I signed up for that painting class. It sounded great in the moment—wine and paint, right? But I only went once and just never felt the urge to go back. I should’ve just made it a whole wild night out instead. Did I ever tell you about the time I went to this obscure music festival with a group of people I barely knew? Yeah, it was one of those spur-of-the-moment decisions. We drove for hours, crammed in a tiny car, blasting whatever random music we could find on the radio. The people were cool, but I think we were all just hyped up on the idea of music and adventure. One night, we ended up in some random forest around a campfire with strangers sharing stories and laughing until we were breathless. It was like a scene out of a movie. I miss that kind of spontaneity sometimes. Why did we stop doing things like that? Oh, and speaking of music, I need a new playlist. I go through phases where I obsess over a certain genre, like right now I'm really into lo-fi. It’s chill, perfect for just zoning out while I pretend to study. But who even studies anymore? Not me. I’d rather go out dancing or find a karaoke bar. I’m so bad at singing, but it doesn’t matter because everyone’s been drinking and just having a good time. We’re all just there for the vibes, right? Someone starts belting out a song, and before you know it, there’s a whole crowd joining in like we’re all best friends. What a rush. Social media is kind of ridiculous, though. I spend way too much time scrolling. I get lost in rabbit holes of random memes and videos. Wait, one sec, let me check my phone... Okay, now I’m back. TikTok is a black hole; it sucks you in. I follow some really weird accounts. There’s a guy who just eats different types of snacks and rates them, and I’m like, “How is this entertaining?” But I can’t look away! I mean, who am I to judge? I’m here for the chaos. But then again, my friends are always trying to get me to “settle down” and think about my future. Future? Psh. What even is that? I feel like I’m in a constant loop of “what’s next?” without ever really knowing. I love the idea of travel and experiencing new cultures. Just last week, I found this cheap flight to a random beach destination, and I got so excited. The sun, the sand, the ocean... I can almost feel it on my skin. But then reality hits. I have responsibilities, I guess. Deadlines. Ugh. It’s like they suck the life out of everything fun. I don’t wanna be that person who gets bogged down by life. And here’s the thing, I’ve always got some new idea bouncing around in my head. Like, I was thinking about starting a plant-based food truck. Why not? People love food, and I love to eat. But do I really have the energy to make that happen? I can’t even remember to water my houseplants half the time. I’m always like “I should really check on them” and then it’s weeks later and they’re half-dead. But the idea of a food truck just makes me feel alive. If I could just drop everything and run away to live in a foreign country, I would. Maybe somewhere where no one knows me, and everything is new. I’d want to explore every nook and cranny. Absorb all the sights, sounds, and tastes. What would I do? Work at a local café, make friends with all the locals, get sucked into their way of life. I crave that level of immersion. I think that’s why I get restless sometimes. Life feels so mundane and routine, and I just want to shake things up. I’m the type of person who thrives on change and unpredictability. Like, surprise me! Okay, I’m getting a little too carried away. But hey, isn’t that what life is for? To follow every wild idea and have as many adventures as I can without worrying about what others think? Yeah, I think so. Bring it on, universe. I’m ready for whatever’s next!

Okay, so I just remembered that last weekend was so much fun, we went out to that new club downtown and it was packed but in a good way, like everyone was just there to have a good time, the music was blasting and I was dancing with friends, and I met this guy who works at the coffee shop down the block from me—totally cute, kind of loud but not obnoxious—a little too confident maybe, but hey, who doesn’t love a little confidence, right? What was his name? Matt? Mark? I don’t know, names are hard. Anyway, he had these crazy dance moves, and I just had to jump in and show off my own skills, like I’m not a pro or anything but I can hold my own, and the way he laughed when I made a joke about my terrible coordination was great, loved that vibe. Speaking of vibes, I can’t even keep track of how many people I met that night, it was like the whole city was out, everyone just buzzing with energy and I felt like I was at the center of it all, which is just where I like to be, I don’t get people who prefer to hang back or just chill out. Life’s too short for that, right? I mean, yeah, sometimes I probably should be a little more conscious of my surroundings or whatever but who has time for that? It’s all about living in the moment and grabbing that drink with whoever, even if it’s overpriced like come on, they could at least give us a better pour if they’re gonna charge ten bucks for a cocktail. And what is it with that one friend who always wants to leave early? I don’t get that. Like, we were having a blast and she kept checking her watch, acting like she had this big meeting or something the next morning, who does that? Commitment to responsibilities is overrated and boring. I want to live, not be tied down by work or school or whatever else people worry about. I’m just here for a good time, and honestly, I thrive in those loud, chaotic atmospheres, they energize me. I thrive way more when there are loads of people around. It’s like I can feed off their energy, I can talk to anyone, convince them to join in on the fun, it’s all good. Then there’s this guy in one of my classes. He keeps trying to be all intellectual, talking about his theories and books he’s read like that makes him better or something. I can’t with that. I mean, who cares? Not everyone has to sit around analyzing things for hours on end. Just enjoy life! He probably has a whole schedule for everything too, like he mapped out his life or something—so boring. I can’t even imagine sticking to a plan like that, it sounds exhausting. Let’s grab a drink instead of discussing whatever dark meaning there is in that book. We need some spontaneity, I’m all about the unexpected, like ditching plans to go to an amusement park instead. And don’t get me started on social media. Some people take it way too seriously; like chill out, it’s just a place to share wild times and funny moments, but then you have these influencers acting like they’re better than everyone else, posting pictures of their perfect lives, like no thanks. I’d rather be out making those memories in real life instead of scrolling through someone else’s highlight reel. I’d rather be the center of attention instead of just some follower. Maybe I don’t get how all that works, but honestly who needs to understand everything? I can do my own thing and have fun without needing a roadmap or a guide. I remember back in high school when we used to throw impromptu parties just because we felt like it, like one person would yell “who’s down for a party?” and suddenly everyone was finding snacks, cranking up the music, and just having a blast. That’s real life; that's how it should always be. No plans, no worrying about what others think—just good times and loud laughter. I really miss that energy, the way everyone would just jump on board without any hesitation. That’s when life was exciting, no filters, just living. Not saying I want to go back to high school or anything, but I think a lot of people forgot how to have fun without all the stress about fitting in or having a future. Living in the moment is where the real happiness lies. I could do this every night, just being out there, meeting new people, dancing like no one’s watching, just embracing everything without overthinking it all. Who really cares at the end of the day?

That notebook I bought last week is still sitting open on my desk, pages blank, and I can’t decide if I should just doodle in it or actually try to write something meaningful. I’m not the doodling type, though. I like structure. But sometimes, I think it would be fun to just freehand whatever's in my mind. Okay, focus. I keep thinking of the weekend. So much to look forward to! I can’t wait to hang out with my friends. We’re planning to hit that new taco place downtown. I hear they have the best salsa. My friend Sara is all about spicy food, and honestly, I’ll try anything she recommends. She’s always so enthusiastic, and it makes me feel good to be around her. She’s like my personal hype machine, and I love that energy. Makes me realize how important it is to have people who lift you up instead of bringing you down. Sometimes, I think about how lucky I am to have such a solid group of friends. We just click, you know? I remember when we all first met during orientation freshman year. It was like an instant connection. We all ended up at the same lunch table, laughing at the awkward icebreakers we had to go through. I still feel that spark when we’re together, like we can be ourselves without holding back. It’s refreshing. I don’t really have to worry about how I come across. I just know I can be my goofy self and they’ll still love me. I even organized a mini karaoke night at my apartment last month, and it was a blast. I didn’t care that I can’t hit the high notes; we were all just there for fun. It was like a mini party, and everyone was so supportive. You just can’t beat that kind of vibe. Okay, so back to the weekend plans. I need to remember to text Jake about carpooling. He usually forgets unless I remind him. He’s a great friend but sometimes his head is in the clouds. I like to think I’m the planner of our group. If I didn’t keep everyone organized, I can just see us all sitting at home, wondering what to do. I hope he doesn’t flake out this time. Last time, he got caught in traffic and missed all the fun. No stress, though. I’ll just give him a friendly nudge later. Maybe I should send him a meme or something to make it light. He loves that stuff. Speaking of traffic, I have to remember to give myself enough time before heading out. There’s always that one spot downtown that gets congested. I swear it’s worse than a slow internet connection when you’re trying to stream a movie. I guess that’s what I hate most about driving. I wish it could be as easy as just clicking a button and being there. But that wouldn’t be practical, would it? I mean, sometimes the journey is half the fun, especially with friends. We always end up blasting our favorite songs and singing along. Last time it was all about 90s hits, and we sounded ridiculous but also totally amazing. That’s the thing about my friends—nobody cares about looking cool. We’re all about having a good time. Okay, what else is on my mind? I’m kind of stressing about finals next month, but in a good way? Like, I feel prepared, and I know I just have to stay organized. I’ve been using this planner where I write down all my assignments and deadlines. If I stick to it, I should be fine. It’s just a matter of prioritizing. I mean, it’s not like I’m reinventing the wheel or anything. I understand what needs to be done, and I’m willing to put in the effort. Some people find studying boring; I actually enjoy it, especially when I can study with friends. We start quizzing each other, and it becomes a fun little competition. I think that’s the key for me—making it social. Oh, and that new club on campus! I heard they’re hosting events every week, and I’m thinking about joining. It’s all about community service, which is something I want to be more involved in. Helping others just feels right, and I love the idea of doing it with a group. I know I can make a difference, even if it’s just a little. It’s cool to think that I could be part of something bigger than myself. And who knows? Maybe I’ll meet even more awesome people there. I shouldn’t forget about my family either. I’ve been thinking of planning a family dinner soon. They always enjoy it when I cook. It’s like a little way to show them how much I appreciate them. I’ve been trying out new recipes, and I get such a kick out of it when they love what I made. I want them to know I care. I think that’s important in life, making connections and showing gratitude. I guess that’s what it’s all about, right? Just being there for people and embracing every moment. Time flies, and I’d rather be surrounded by good people than waste it being alone.

okay so I just remember this time last week hanging out with my friends at that park and it was just so spontaneous like we decided to grab lunch and then ended up at that food truck festival I’d never been before but it was surprisingly awesome like all those different smells of food wafting around and everyone laughing and chatting I love that energy so much I thrive on it seriously just being surrounded by people makes everything feel alive and vibrant and I could taste the excitement in the air haha so anyway we got these crazy tacos with mango salsa and I was just like how have I never thought of this before it’s like my taste buds are waking up or something and then of course we had to split a giant funnel cake afterwards like those things are basically a party on a plate I don’t know who came up with the idea of putting powdered sugar on everything but it’s genius and then we sat on the grass just talking and laughing about old inside jokes and it felt like time didn’t even exist for a while you know? I can’t handle it when people are in a bad mood though it kind of ruins the vibe I mean I get it life happens but sometimes I want to just throw glitter at them or something like come on let’s make a happy memory instead we even tried to cheer up this guy who was just sitting alone on a bench and at first he looked a little skeptical but by the end of our little shenanigans he was cracking a smile and I thought YES that’s what I live for, turning someone’s day around oh and then there’s this thing I started doing where I just say yes to everything like just experimenting with this idea of openness ya know? like so if someone invites me to this random event I usually just say okay why not and it’s led to some wild adventures like that midnight karaoke night last month where I belted out some classic Whitney Houston and seriously felt like a superstar even though my voice is not exactly award-winning or anything but the night was filled with laughter and in the end we all ended up taking selfies in the dim bar light and I know I’ll look back on that and be like man what a night! plus since I’m not too neurotic it’s easy for me to go with the flow even when things don’t go as planned like that time we went camping and forgot one of the tents and ended up all crammed into just one tent like sardines but we made it work and it became a bonding experience and those moments are when I feel most alive like when we shared ghost stories and roasted marshmallows under the stars but then again, sometimes I do lose track of things like I just totally forgot that I promised to help my roommate study for her chem exam because I got sidetracked with planning that upcoming dinner party which I’m really excited about! I love cooking for people and I just want to make the most delicious Italian feast ever like pasta from scratch and yeah I know it’s gonna be a lot of work but the joy in seeing everyone’s faces when they take that first bite? worth it every time also, I’m thinking of reaching out to that girl I met at the last party she had such cool vibe and I love making new friends like she was talking about her travels and I felt this instant connection just a spark of curiosity about her stories and I genuinely want to know more like where she went what her favorite places are and maybe I can convince her to join one of my spontaneous adventures one day like a weekend road trip? it’s funny how sometimes I can be kind of scatterbrained like one minute I’m planning a dinner and the next I’m daydreaming about some wild trip to a foreign country like just hopping on a plane and exploring some new city and oh man I really want to visit Japan, the food, the culture, the fashion, everything just seems so colorful and rich and I want to experience it all with people who are just as excited as I am but I’ll probably get distracted by something else before I make any real plans I guess that’s just how I am I like to keep things light and fun but sometimes I wish I could be a bit more organized like when I look at my schedule and it’s a chaotic mess I wonder if I need to work on that but then again does it matter as long as I’m surrounded by the love and laughter of friends? but yeah I really should finish that paper due Friday we’ll see how that goes with my last-minute brainstorming strategies ooo I just thought of a new idea for the party, how about a theme night? that could be fun I mean everyone loves a good theme, right? maybe we can all dress up or have a contest for the best dish or something like who doesn’t love some light-hearted competition and just creating memories with people makes all the craziness worth it.

Okay so I just saw a butterfly outside my window and it was so pretty I couldn’t help but stop and stare like wow nature is incredible right? It’s like little surprises everywhere if you just look for them, I love that. I mean I could just get lost watching butterflies all day. Speaking of things I could get lost in, I really should start thinking about what I'm going to do this weekend. My friends and I were talking about going to that new art exhibit downtown, the one with the interactive installations. It sounds so cool! I mean who doesn’t love being part of the art, right? I can already picture myself standing there, taking a million selfies, making weird faces trying to look artsy but probably just looking silly. But who cares? It’s all about the fun and just being in the moment. I can’t wait to see what kind of vibes the exhibit has. I bet we’ll meet some interesting people too. It’s funny how just being out and about can lead to the most random conversations with strangers. Like, last time I was at the park, I met this guy who was super into photography, and we ended up chatting for over an hour about everything from our favorite movies to what inspires us. It’s moments like that that just make my day. One minute I’m just wandering around, and the next, I have this whole new friend or at least a cool story to tell. Okay, but the weekend plans. I should check the group chat because I feel like something must be brewing in there. Sometimes I have to remind myself to actually read messages instead of just skimming through. I mean I can be so scatterbrained sometimes, like I don’t always connect with the facts of conversations, but my brain is just racing with ideas and possibilities and it’s like, who needs details, right? Details can be boring. I’m more about the vibes and the fun! Ooh, speaking of which, that reminds me of last week’s karaoke night. I’ll never get over how everyone just came alive when the music started. Even the shy ones were up there belting out their favorite songs. There was this one girl who was so quiet in class, and then she got up and sang Whitney Houston like she was in a concert. Amazing! I mean, it’s like people wear these masks all the time, and sometimes all it takes is a little music to peel that mask away. I just love seeing that transformation. It’s almost like it’s therapeutic, right? Sometimes I feel like we all need that release. I swear, if I could, I’d plan spontaneous karaoke nights every week. Just show up and sing whatever and create this beautiful chaos. I’m not the best singer, but who cares! It’s all about the energy! Maybe I should start organizing that. Just pop up and say, “Hey, let’s sing our hearts out!” I mean I love bringing people together like that. It’s like my superpower or something. I should probably put some of this enthusiasm into my studies, though. I mean not that I completely ignore my classes, but I can get distracted so easily. I’ll be in the middle of a reading and suddenly I’ll think about something completely unrelated. Like the other day, I was trying to finish a paper on the impact of social media, and then boom, I’m daydreaming about traveling to Japan and eating all the sushi. Totally off track! But I guess that’s just how my brain works. Always tangent after tangent like a rollercoaster. Imagine if I could travel anywhere right now. Where would I go? Definitely somewhere with lots of sunshine and colorful streets. Maybe a festival? I love festivals! Music, culture, food, people everywhere just enjoying life. It brings out the best in everyone. I feel a sort of rush just thinking about it. I wish I were the type to plan it all out meticulously, but honestly, I’d rather just grab my friends and go. No plans, just vibes and laughter. Okay, I’m really getting hungry now, what’s for lunch? I could go for something fun. Maybe sushi since I was just thinking about it or some tacos. Tacos are always a win. The crunch, the flavor! Maybe I’ll just invite some friends and make it a little fiesta since I was talking about being spontaneous earlier. Yeah! It’s all coming together! Food, laughter, friends, and good energy just flowing all around. I really hope the weather stays nice too. I love when the sun's out, and everyone seems happier. It’s like a different world. You can see people smiling more, stopping to chat, really connecting. It’s such a beautiful thing. Okay, I need to wrap this up and make some plans. But whatever happens, I just want it to be fun and memorable. Life is too short for anything else, right?

Okay so I just got the latest text from Melissa. She wants to go out, surprise, surprise. Always up for something, never knows how to chill. I mean, I love going out but sometimes it’s like can we just stay in for a night? But then again, staying in is boring. I have this craving for social energy, I thrive on it. When I hear the laughter in the distance, the music thumping from the nearby bar, it pulls me in like moth to a flame. The thing is, I know exactly how to plan a great night out. I’m organized that way, got my spreadsheets and lists for fun. It’s not a joke, I have an excel sheet of all the places we can hit and who’s hanging out at each one on which days, meticulously crafted. I think about who else I can drag along with us. Fiona always brings the wild energy but sometimes she can’t read the room. Last time she made that weird comment about Steve’s shoes. Like, lady, chill. It wasn’t even that serious. But people laughed and it got awkward. I don’t mind being the one to steer the group; someone has to keep things on track. God, I should remember to text Fiona too. I need her to stay in line. I can’t stand it when someone doesn’t get how to mesh with the vibe. I mean, if it’s party time, we need to keep it light and fun. Don’t be the downer. I’ve been the downer before, I won’t lie, but there’s a time and place for that, right? Balance is key. I prefer to think of myself as a upbeat dynamo, always ready to roll. I know I can bring people together. I’m not trying to be a people-pleaser, I just want things to flow in a way that everyone has a good time. What happens if we don’t get a good spot at the bar? Last week was a disaster; the place was packed. We had to stand around like we were in line for a concert or something. I can’t deal with that. I plan ahead, check the capacity always. There’s nothing worse than being stuck on the outside looking in. I need to be in the moment, you know? Feel the pulse of the crowd, engage with whoever’s next to me. Then there’s the matter of food. Never forget the food. I’m going all in on tacos tonight. Tacos are perfect. Quick, filling, and you can chat while eating. Can’t be eating spaghetti while trying to keep a conversation alive, that’s messy business. I think about the place on Main Street that has those killer tacos, but then I remember the last time they were out of guacamole and I almost lost it. Who runs out of guac? I need to remember to jot down a few things to bring up when I’m talking with everyone. I love discussing plans for the next big event. I’m thinking Halloween. What are we dressing up as? I’m already picturing the group costume. I’m envisioning something classic but funny, like a play on famous duos. I could be Mario and someone else could be Luigi. Or we could do something completely random, like characters from a sitcom. I could lead that too. I get impatient if people take too long to decide. I need to know everything in advance. Speaking of planning, I need to organize my schedule for next week too. Midterms are coming up soon and I need to stay on top of study sessions. I always plan out my study blocks, too. Break down the chapters, color code them, make a checklist. That’s how I roll. I can’t comprehend the idea of going into exams without a strategy. It stresses me out to think about the chaos that can ensue if I’m unprepared. Plus, my grades are important. I can’t throw those away just because I decided to wing it. I really can’t handle unpredictability. I like my life structured, with plans and back-up plans. I mean, spontaneity sounds fun but it’s also a recipe for disaster. Just the thought of going along with the winds of fate gives me the shivers. And anyway, what’s so thrilling about surprises? I’d much rather stick to my carefully crafted schedule than run the risk of unexpected awkwardness. Back to Melissa, maybe I can convince her to dial it back a notch. “Hey Melissa, how about a mellow hangout instead of the craziness?” I just want to feel connected, not stress about keeping everyone entertained. I think about my ideal evening. Friends, some drinks, tacos, and a casual laugh. That's my jam, sweet simplicity mixed with great energy. Also, Tacos. Always tacos.

okay so I was just thinking about how crazy it is that life feels like one big adventure sometimes and how every day seems to offer new possibilities like yesterday when I bumped into that old friend from high school at the farmer's market and it was such a surprise! I mean, I really didn’t expect to see them and we just started chatting like no time had passed at all, isn’t that wild? Like, we talked about everything from the crazy teachers we had to what life is like now, juggling work and barely finding time to eat what are those things called oh right health food they sell at those trendy shops, and I totally forgot about how much I enjoyed talking about that stuff. I wonder if I should plan a little reunion with a few more friends maybe we could go hiking or something outdoorsy like remember that time we went to the beach and made those ridiculous sand castles? I have this vivid memory of us just laughing and throwing sand at each other like little kids and it felt so freeing, you know? Speaking of the beach, I want to go there again before summer officially ends even though it’s like almost September already wow where did the time go? I should really grab my bathing suit and just hit up the coast one weekend. I really love the sound of the waves crashing and the way the salty air makes me feel alive! Plus, I’ve got so many ideas for photos I want to take. You know, capture the moment, live in the now, all that good stuff that makes social media worthwhile, haha. Maybe I could organize a little group trip, maybe get some friends who are just as spontaneous as me. I love being around people who are down for anything. Oh, and I’ve been reading this book about personal growth lately and it’s really inspiring me to push myself further, like with my hobbies. I’ve always been into art but I’ve never taken a class or anything. Maybe I should! I keep saying I want to expand my creativity and try new things and this could be my chance to explore it more seriously. It just sounds like so much fun to explore colors and textures! Plus, I think it would help my mental clarity too which is always a bonus. I really feel like creative expression is so important for everyone. It just brings out the best in people, don’t you think? Ugh, I hope this doesn’t sound boring or repetitive but seriously, the more I think about it, the more I want to just dive right in and take a leap of faith into something new. I’ve always been the type to want to try everything! Like even cooking! I’ve been experimenting with new recipes and I made this awesome spaghetti squash dish last week and it turned out absolutely delicious. I was like, “Wow, I really made this!” I was totally proud of myself! It’s crazy how cooking can be so therapeutic and a way to connect with others too. I love inviting friends over for dinner, sharing a meal, laughing, and making memories. It’s a simple way to show love and warmth. Plus, I get to flaunt my cooking skills! Speaking of friends, I’ve been thinking about how much I appreciate my circle lately. They’re just the best. Always up for a chat or just hanging out, and we always encourage each other. It feels good to be surrounded by positivity and support. I think that’s such an important part of life, creating a community where everyone feels valued and uplifted. I mean, I just get so excited about being with people! We can talk about deep stuff or just gossip about the latest Netflix shows or whatever and it feels so refreshing. I’ve never understood people who prefer to be alone for long periods. I get it, sometimes you need your space but oh man, I thrive in social settings! And then there’s the idea of travel, which is just so exhilarating to think about! I seriously want to visit so many places and learn about different cultures, trying new foods, meeting people from all walks of life. Like, just imagining being in a bustling market in a foreign country and sampling street food, having conversations with locals, it makes my heart race! I want to collect experiences, not things, you know? That’s the kind of wealth I want in my life. So many places on my list too! Europe, Asia, South America—ah, I need to get my passport renewed! I should also start saving up for a big trip and get serious about it. But with school and everything, it’s hard to balance sometimes. But really, what’s life without a little struggle to get to the good stuff right? It must be about learning and growing and meeting new people and spreading love! Alright, I’ve written enough for now, but I could seriously go on forever. It’s just so fun to think about all the possibilities!

okay so I was thinking about my weekend plans but then I remembered I should probably finish that paper first deadlines are so annoying but I guess having a structure helps me focus sometimes I just want to sit in my room and read but it feels like a waste of time unless it’s for class or something productive like finding a new study technique or figuring out how to manage my time better I like lists I always make lists they help me prioritize when my mind gets all jumbled up I can’t stand clutter or chaos in my workspace it drives me nuts like if there’s too much stuff on my desk I can’t think straight so I tidy up constantly just to maintain some sense of order I think that’s why I enjoy my classes because they have that clear structure and I know what’s expected of me I hate surprises I’m not really spontaneous which makes me wonder if I’m boring to other people but I’m okay with that I prefer routines and knowing what’s next I mean I can’t imagine just going out to a party without a plan like what do people do there why do they enjoy it so much I have friends who invite me to stuff but I usually decline because I feel more at peace in my own space even if it means missing out but occasionally I do go just to be polite and I try to engage but small talk feels so exhausting it’s like why can’t we just skip to the meaningful stuff and dive into deeper conversations right away but I guess that makes me seem intense to some people and I don’t want to come off as too much or overbearing I think that’s part of being agreeable I really want to keep the peace and make sure everyone feels comfortable what if someone feels awkward because I brought up a profound topic too soon I should probably reconsider my approach but it’s just how my mind works sometimes I wonder what it’s like to be more open to new experiences like traveling or trying out new foods I don’t really care for trying things that seem risky or out of my comfort zone but I respect those who do I admire their bravery maybe I just prefer to stay in my little bubble where things feel safe I mean I appreciate the little things like a warm cup of tea and a quiet afternoon or a good book that pulls me in and takes me away from reality the other day I finished this novel and when I closed it I felt kind of lost like I didn’t know what to do with myself afterward because I was so invested in those characters it’s a little silly but I get attached to fictional people way more than I do to some real-life relationships I can’t help that sometimes it’s easier to care about made-up stories than to deal with what’s right in front of me though I do have a close circle of friends that I value I like being there for them I’m the type who listens and offers help when they need it I guess I’m the go-to person when they have a dilemma which is fine but sometimes I feel like I have to hold back my own thoughts because I don’t want them to feel overwhelmed I’d rather just help them sort through their problems and it’s rewarding in a way knowing I can support them while also keeping myself grounded it’s just the right balance but then again I shouldn’t neglect my own needs I think it’s important to find time for myself but it’s hard sometimes especially when everyone else is in crisis mode school can be so stressful and I want to be there for them but man I could really use a break sometimes I wonder if I should set clearer boundaries but then I feel bad about it like I’m abandoning them or something I know that’s not the case but it’s tough to hold on to that perspective when you care so much about others I mean I want to make everyone happy but I also need to take care of myself self-care and all that which feels so cliché but it’s true it’s not easy to strike that balance but maybe I could use my planner to map out specific times for myself like a little oasis within the chaos that might help I need to remind myself that it’s okay to step back and recharge without guilt I mean I’ve got to be my own priority sometimes right and if I don’t take care of myself how can I help anyone else it’s so simple yet complex how do people find the balance I think if I had it figured out I’d feel more in control but I guess that’s a part of living and learning I’ve been thinking a lot about what it means to be conscientious too I get so caught up in the details sometimes I can lose sight of the bigger picture but I like knowing that I’m doing things right and I put in the effort even in the boring tasks like organizing my notes or planning out my week but productivity feels rewarding in its own way I should focus on that and less on being perfect I think about this often how it’s okay to feel okay and not have to be constantly striving for something better I think I’m okay with where I am right now and that’s enough.

okay so my friend just texted me about that party this weekend I’m like which one there’s always a party every weekend and I love it I can’t get enough of being around people sometimes I feel like I’m just buzzing with energy but then I remember I told myself I wouldn’t go out as much this month or whatever I feel guilty cause I’ve got assignments piling up but then again can I really resist the fun and laughter and all the chaos that comes with a packed room of people it’s just so exhilarating why do I always end up procrastinating anyway I’d probably be way less stressed if I just sat down and did my work instead of scrolling on my phone and getting pulled into all these group texts but the thing is I just feel so happy when I’m with my friends last time we went to that new bar it was like everybody was there and I was dancing and laughing and I just didn’t want the night to end and I feel like if I miss out on that I’d regret it for sure. so I was thinking about how I can be super chill with everyone but then I also get easily stressed out if things don’t go my way like what’s up with that it’s like my mind is a rollercoaster sometimes one minute I’m on top of the world and the next I’m like why did I say that why did I do this and my heart races for no reason like it’s ridiculous. I wish I could just calm down sometimes but it’s hard when you’re surrounded by so many people you have to keep smiling and make small talk and I actually love talking to people I mean who wouldn’t but then I’m also like do they really like me or are they just being nice? and that thought just spirals and I start overthinking every little thing. I feel bad for my roommate though she doesn’t get it I mean she likes her quiet time and her books and I just can’t relate like how do you want to be alone all the time without going crazy? I guess I respect that but also I’m kind of like let’s go out let’s meet people let’s explore the city but she’d rather sit at home and binge-watch some series I tried to get her to come with me last week but she was like “no thanks” and I just felt this wave of disappointment hit me like why don’t you want to have fun? it’s not like I’m forcing you but still I thought we could bond over some wild night but I get it everyone has their own vibe right and not everyone’s wired the same way I think maybe I just need to accept that. then there’s those moments where I feel super connected with people like when we’re all just sharing our thoughts and feelings over drinks and I suddenly feel this deep bond but then I doubt it later I’m like was that real? or was I just too tipsy to know what I’m saying? I don’t want to come off as fake but sometimes I feel like I relate to everyone one minute and then I can’t even remember what we talked about the next day it’s chaotic and wild but I love the thrill of not knowing what might happen next. the other day I found myself scrolling through my old group photos and just laughing at our ridiculous faces we’re all just a bunch of weirdos together and it’s comforting I don’t think I’d survive without that kind of friendship keeping me grounded even if it’s not always perfect sometimes I wonder what it would be like to just hang out with people who are super serious and focused but then I remember my friends keep me laughing and we pull each other through the tough times which is a major plus and we always have each other’s backs but who knows maybe there is a different side to things that I’m just not ready for. every time I try to get my life organized I just end up doing something fun instead I mean I should probably find a balance I definitely need to get my act together because the more I avoid my responsibilities the more my anxiety builds up and it’s just this nasty cycle speaking of which my phone is blowing up with notifications from everyone inviting me to different events and I feel this pressure to keep saying yes because I don’t want to let anyone down but it’s so draining sometimes when will I learn to say no? but I guess the fear of missing out always wins and I’m just a sucker for a good time. Gosh, I really should just focus on what matters instead of running around like a headless chicken. But the thought of being all organized and responsible is so boring it’s like securing a boring future and I’m over here just wanting to seize the moment. I guess I’ll just keep living it up and hope for the best.

okay so I just remembered this one time I went to a party and it was wild like I was in the middle of everything and there’s this loud music bumping and people were dancing but I was just standing there trying to figure out if I should join in or just get another drink I mean can I even dance I don’t know but what if people think I’m weird but then again isn’t everyone a little weird at a party or am I just overthinking it yeah probably. I remember the lights flashing and people sweating and laughing but I just felt this weird tension in my stomach like what if I mess up or spill my drink everywhere. ugh and then there’s that guy who keeps making eye contact with me and I can’t figure out if he’s into me or just looking because I’m staring at him too. I hate these social situations sometimes like you want to be the life of the party but then you also don’t want to feel stupid or like you’re not having a good time. And oh God the small talk is painful it’s like, do you really want to know what I think about the weather or do I have to pretend to care about your boring job as an accountant? and then there are those moments where I get super overwhelmed like when the music gets too loud and I just want to find a corner to breathe I don’t want to be a downer but I can feel my heart racing like I need to escape but I also don’t want to miss anything and then I’m caught in this loop of wanting to socialize but needing a break. this is why I can’t handle big crowds sometimes it’s just too much like I’ll be laughing one second and the next I’m thinking of how I can’t handle all these people. social anxiety mixed with a burst of energy is a weird mix it feels like I’m on a roller coaster that I couldn’t get off even if I wanted to. and do people even like me or are they just being polite? I don’t know if I could handle rejection right now so I just keep smiling and pretending everything is fine even when I want to scream. then you just get those moments where someone says something that just annoys you like why do people have to be so fake like just be real for once? someone walks up to me talking about their latest trip and I’m trying not to roll my eyes because who cares about your Instagram-worthy vacation when I can’t even find my keys half the time? like get a grip on reality or something. and sometimes I feel like I’m stuck in my head too much like I should just speak my mind but then I might hurt someone’s feelings because everyone seems so sensitive nowadays and it’s frustrating like can’t we just say what we're thinking without everyone getting all worked up? I guess I just don’t get the whole being nice thing like if I don’t like something I’ll just say it but then people look at me like I’m the bad guy. it’s hard to explain how I feel in social situations though like I can be super outgoing and charming but the moment I feel pushed into a corner or judged it’s like I turn into this defensive version of myself that just wants to lash out and be rude. I know it’s not right but I feel like I can’t help it sometimes. It’s a coping mechanism or something like I want to protect myself from being hurt or embarrassed so I build these walls instead but that just ends up isolating me more and then I wonder why I don’t have close friends. and speaking of friends I really suck at making them like I joke around and talk a lot but somehow I never let anyone in. it’s like a vicious cycle and I can’t find a way to break it. how do people manage to keep up friendships? I mean it’s exhausting sometimes, isn’t it? texting back and forth and making plans every week like I just want to chill. but then I’ll see photos on social media and everyone is hanging out without me and it’s like damn why didn’t I get invited? I know I shouldn’t let it get to me but it does and I start overanalyzing everything like maybe they don’t like me that much or I said something wrong last time. and it’s funny because I can easily turn on the charm at a party but the moment I’m alone it’s like the energy just zaps away and I’m hit with this wave of insecurity and doubt. alright I need to just breathe and figure out how to be okay with all of this because I can’t keep spiraling into the same patterns. I used to think being social would magically make me happy but it just confuses me more than anything. maybe all I need is to find that sweet spot between being outgoing and knowing when to take a step back. is that even possible? some days I just wish I could be invisible or something like take a break from the pressure of being social without anyone thinking it’s weird.

okay so I just remembered that time in high school when I decided to host a surprise party for my best friend’s birthday and it felt like the best idea ever but also kind of terrifying because what if nobody showed up and then I had to figure out how to distract her and make it work but it ended up being incredible and everyone who mattered came and we had cake and laughter it was such a good memory and I wonder if she remembers it like I do I hope she does because those moments are the ones that stick with you right? like how music can take you back to a specific moment in time when you felt alive and everything was perfect and I love that feeling and the way you can connect with people through shared experiences but also sometimes I think about how easy it is to connect with the wrong people too like in college you meet so many different types of people and it’s fascinating but it can also feel overwhelming because who do I let into my life and who do I keep at arm's length? but I’m usually so open and friendly it’s hard to not get swept up in conversations with just anyone especially if they have a cool vibe or a wild story to tell. I was talking to this guy the other day who has traveled everywhere and he started sharing his experiences and I was just like wow I want to do that I want to be that adventurous and I think I will be someday but then I have to remember I have to balance school and work and staying sane because sometimes the pressure of deadlines can feel like a ton of bricks but is that just me? I don’t know maybe it’s just the caffeine kicking in because I had a couple of espressos earlier and they are hitting me hard but in a good way like a rush of energy and creativity but also I think I might need to take a break and go for a walk to clear my head. I love walks, they help me think and get ideas flowing. I see so many things when I walk like this little café that I found last week and it had the best pastries and the barista was super friendly and we ended up talking about our favorite coffee blends I mean how cool is that? I feel like people are so much more interesting than we give them credit for, you just have to ask the right questions and be genuinely curious and that’s something I try to do every day. There’s always a story behind each person and why they are where they are today and it reminds me that everyone has struggles and dreams and hopes and I don’t know I just feel so lucky to be able to connect with so many unique individuals. Speaking of stories, I still need to finish that book I started last month it’s been sitting on my nightstand and I keep telling myself tonight’s the night but then I get distracted by TikTok or my roommate comes in with some random idea for a late-night snack run and I’m like yes let’s go so we end up grabbing fries and it’s the best decision ever because sometimes you just need to indulge right? and it’s those little moments of spontaneity that make life exciting like when we decided to take that random road trip last semester I swear we had no plan just a map and a playlist and that’s all you really need sometimes, that sense of freedom where you don’t worry about what’s next you just exist and have fun in the present and it’s liberating. I want more of that, more of those experiences where you throw caution to the wind and just see where the universe takes you and it’s like my mind is racing with ideas and possibilities and I can’t help but dream bigger and louder but it’s all about those small steps too right? like building a future that excites me and makes me proud and I guess that’s part of being conscientious or something because I don’t want to just float through life I want to make an impact, leave a mark, be a light for others in whatever way I can, and I think I can do that if I stay true to myself and keep working hard. Speaking of working hard, I need to get back to that group project that’s due next week we’re supposed to come up with a presentation that’s creative but also informative and I know we can do it because we have some of the brightest minds in our group but sometimes I worry about how to make it stand out I mean everyone has good ideas but how do we elevate it to the next level? my mind is buzzing with thoughts and I really want to dive back into that and see where it takes us. And okay wow I’ve been typing for what feels like forever but I could honestly keep going forever because there’s always something to think about, something to discover, and I’m so grateful for that.

I just saw someone post about a spontaneous road trip to the beach, like wait, why am I not there right now? That sounds incredible. I love the idea of just hopping in a car with friends, windows down, music blasting, it brings so much energy. There’s this freedom in the air when you’re driving to a place that’s calling your name. I could picture myself in the passenger seat, the sunroof open, probably snapping a million pictures because I want to capture every moment. But then I remember, managing my time has always been a little tricky for me. I probably should be studying for that exam next week, but can I just trade that for a sunset on the shore? Okay, so let’s talk about food because when am I not thinking about food? I could really go for tacos right now. Spicy chicken, guac on top, maybe some mango salsa if I’m feeling a bit adventurous. Why does mango salsa always make me happy? It’s like a little party in my mouth. Food is so much fun to explore, trying out new restaurants, discovering new flavors, and just sharing it with friends. I could talk about food forever, especially if it's with someone who gets as excited as I do. Sometimes my friends just roll their eyes when I go off about some new vegan joint that just opened up or that crazy dim sum place I found on Instagram. Speaking of Instagram, scrolling through it can get me into trouble. Why do I find myself looking at travel influencers and feeling that urge to book a flight? It’s ridiculous how easily I can be swayed by pretty pictures and good vibes. And just like that, I’m daydreaming about camping in the mountains or sipping cocktails on a rooftop in some exotic place. I feel like I need to plan the next few weekends to include more spontaneous outings, how about a group hike? I love the outdoors, so many hiking trails around here, and I always end up making new friends on those trails. Everyone is so chill and friendly; it really lights up my spirits when I’m out there. I remember this one time we went hiking last summer, it was such an epic day. There were a few of us and we just started shouting about how we were going to conquer the mountain, so why not shout it? When we finally reached the peak, the view was breathtaking. Like how can you stand there and not feel connected to everything else in the universe? We took a million pictures but none of them could really capture the feeling of standing there, arms wide open, embracing the moment. Afterwards, we went to this cute little diner nearby and laughed about how we thought we’d die on the way up. Back to friends, I think they are such a crucial part of my life. I thrive on those social vibes, every day feels more colorful with people around. I love talking to new folks, hearing their stories, and sharing a laugh. Sometimes I find it hard to concentrate because I’m always thinking about the next fun thing to do. It’s like my brain is wired for connection, I want to know everyone and have them all be part of my life. I should probably tone it down a bit, at least when it comes to balancing things, but who wants to be super structured anyway? Sometimes rules are made to be broken, right? Okay, so speaking of spontaneity, I just remembered my favorite memory from last summer. A friend and I decided to throw a pop-up picnic in the park. We just grabbed whatever we had in our fridges—some homemade sandwiches, chips, cookies—and just set up right in the middle of a sunny field. We invited like ten people on a whim, and everyone came with random snacks. I love it when people just click together out of nowhere. It’s the simplest moments that turn into the best memories. There was laughter, music, and even a little dance-off that broke out! Why do little spontaneous events like that always make life feel so rich? Then there are days when I’m cooped up inside and I think about all the things I could do if I just got up and went out. Like what if I called a friend right now and we just went exploring the nearby city? There’s so much to see and do, street art, food trucks, live music. I want to dive into culture and experience everything! I think the best moments come from diving into new experiences, and I want to make the most of my time. So maybe I will take some time to actually plan something fun, even if it’s just a little get-together with friends. That sounds perfect. I can just feel the energy bubbling up inside me. Let’s make some food, have some laughs, and enjoy life. Who needs to worry about the little things? Just living in the moment, that’s what it’s all about!

I just saw a squirrel outside, it’s kind of funny how they look like they have a mission all the time but really they’re just gathering nuts or something. I wonder if they have a secret life like how I think everyone has one. Like what does my roommate do when she says she’s studying but I know she’s probably scrolling through Instagram or watching TikToks, or maybe she has a side hustle I don’t know about. I wonder if I should start a side hustle too? There’s so much pressure to be productive all the time but like, what if I just want to make friendship bracelets and sell them on Etsy? Who even buys those anymore? But then again, I could see someone wanting unique gifts. I should probably look into that. What am I even saying? Sometimes I think about how people don’t even appreciate the simple things. Like the other day, I was eating this really great pizza, and it just made me realize how much food connects us, like how you can bond with someone over a shared love for cheesy bread or spicy pepperoni. But then people want to go on diets or whatever and they miss out. Why do we stress ourselves out with those? I mean life is short, you’ve got to enjoy it but then you get all this noise from everywhere telling you what isn’t good for you and I’m just like can’t we just all chill? It’s so weird how everyone’s trying to fit into these boxes. I totally don’t fit in a box, like, I’m all about experiences, trying new things, going out, meeting people but also lying on my floor eating popcorn while watching a documentary about the universe. Why is the universe so vast and why am I thinking about the universe while I’m writing this random stream-of-consciousness thing? I guess it’s just that everything connects somehow, right? Like all the people I meet, they’re all galaxies too, each with their own stories and lives and crap, and I wonder if I’m just a supernova or a black hole sometimes. Do people ever think about this stuff? Am I just too much? I have this thing where I just say what I think without holding back and some people don’t like it but then whatever, they can just deal with it. I feel like I live for the thrill of conversation, that’s what keeps me going. I love when someone gets passionate about something and their eyes light up and I can’t help but lean in to hear more about it. I had this one friend who could talk about flowers for hours, literally flowers, like did you know there’s a flower called the corpse flower that stinks like rotting flesh? Fascinating stuff. I could listen to that all day and then pivot to something totally random, like why are we so obsessed with fame? I mean look at reality TV, it’s like watching a train wreck but everyone can’t look away. I think I need to start a podcast. I have so many thoughts! I could just record myself talking about anything and people would probably listen. Right? Or they wouldn’t, but they might think it’s funny or interesting. I just need a good name, something catchy. Maybe “Thoughts from the Abyss” or “Eternal Ramblings” or something goofy. I love goofy. Goofy is underrated. You know what’s also underrated? Spontaneous road trips. I went on one last summer and it felt like freedom. Just driving for hours with no real plan, stopping at weird roadside attractions, eating at hole-in-the-wall diners. I could do that forever. Just pack a bag and go. Who needs responsibilities? But then there’s the whole student debt thing looming over me. It’s such a buzzkill. I’m supposed to care about grades or internships, but honestly, I just want to experience life and I think that’s what makes life worth living. I mean, when I’m old, I want to have stories to tell, not regrets. I want to have been everywhere, tried everything.
