I remember that time I tried whale watching, and I thought it would be boring, but I was just glued to the edge of the boat, practically dropping my phone overboard trying to get a video. And when they breached the water, it was like magic, like you can read all the stuff you want but nothing prepares you for the moment that whale shows up. I want to keep chasing those moments. Even if my room is a mess right now, I’ll get to that later. Who really cares about cleanliness when there’s a whole world out there? I mean, I could be organizing my socks or I could be chasing down an adventure. Ugh, life choices, right? But really, it’s about the connections, the laughter, the spontaneity. Sometimes I think we take it all too seriously, so I just want to keep having fun and exploring this crazy, beautiful chaos of life.

okay so I just remembered that time I went to that big concert last summer with my friends it was like a huge deal because everyone was talking about this band and the energy was wild it felt like everyone was connected like we were all in this massive wave of excitement and the music hit you right in the chest I don’t even remember what they played first but I know we were singing along like the lyrics were engraved in our brains and I was jumping up and down it’s funny how music can just bring people together like that I mean total strangers became best friends for that night sharing drinks and dancing like nobody was watching it really makes me appreciate those moments how alive you feel with people around you it’s so different from just hanging out in quieter settings sometimes I get a bit restless if there’s not enough going on around me I thrive in those crowded places where conversations overlap and laughter mixes with the beats of the music. I can't help but think about planning the next adventure because there’s always another thing coming up right around the corner like my buddy’s birthday next month he wants to go skydiving and I’m so in because who wouldn’t want to jump out of a plane and feel that rush I mean it’s like the ultimate thrill and it’s an experience we can all talk about later like a badge of honor I can already picture us all hyped up before the jump and probably making stupid jokes to ease the tension I tend to organize these things because I love getting everyone together I really enjoy bringing people along for the ride and there’s something satisfying about checking everything off the list like a little victory you know? I’m not super into change but I’m okay with it as long as it's planned I guess that’s why I prefer sticking with the familiar when it comes to my core group of friends they’re reliable and we have this unspoken understanding that makes everything easier. oh and speaking of friends, there's that one friend who always gets anxious about social things I worry about her sometimes I want her to feel included but I don’t know how to help sometimes it’s all about just staying close, being present, reminding her she can talk to me whenever she needs to I think I want to help make situations less overwhelming for her so it is nice to balance out the extroverted chaos with someone more reserved it reminds me to slow down once in a while life’s not just about going all out every second of the day but I do feel a little out of place when things are too calm or quiet I’ve tried meditating but that just doesn't hold my attention I might as well be watching paint dry or something so I stick to keeping things lively it feels like more natural to engage with the world around me. sometimes I think about how the world seems better when I’m surrounded by people it makes the heavy stuff a lot easier to ignore like responsibilities or stress or whatever there’s always something fun to distract me like planning the next game night or picking which movie to watch I love how we can just throw ideas around and then boom we’ve got a plan the spontaneity of it is kind of thrilling without getting too chaotic I put a lot of energy into making sure everyone feels good and comfortable I judge my success on how much laughter happens and how many stories we create together so I guess that’s why I don’t care much for things that are too open-ended or undefined I like structure, I like having a playbook for social situations it keeps things from spiraling too far off track. then there’s work too I don't mind taking on extra shifts to support my coworkers and make sure we all stay afloat and I see that as an extension of my traits it's nice to contribute and feel valued but sometimes it feels overwhelming when there’s too much to juggle and you start losing sight of the fun in the grind that balance is key I always think about how to make everything run smoothly putting in effort so everyone wins it’s rewarding when everyone gets to celebrate successes together I thrive on that team dynamic which is probably why I scored so high on agreeableness I just want everyone to be happy and enjoy what they do every day. I need to remember to keep things light even when they become heavy I think it’s okay to lean into joy as much as possible it energizes not just myself but everyone around me I want to create memories and experiences that make life feel vibrant and full because at the end of the day it’s those shared moments that matter the most.

The clock on the wall is ticking too loud, I really should focus on something but my mind is bouncing around like a rubber ball in an empty room. What was I even thinking about? Music, maybe? The songs in my head jump from one to the next, like an old playlist that keeps looping. I hope I don’t forget that one tune that I heard last week. It’s amazing how something can stick with you for days and then poof, it’s gone. I really should write these things down more often. Why don’t I? I tell myself to but it never happens, just like that deadline for my paper that I’ve completely ignored. I wonder if they’ll notice if I just don’t turn it in. Probably not worth the risk but sometimes I can’t help but imagine the freedom. Ditching class, hanging out in a coffee shop, but not with people—just me and my thoughts. Is that weird? I mean, who even wants to be around other people all the time? It’s exhausting figuring out what to say, how to act. I prefer the company of books. They don’t judge me for not being the social butterfly everyone expects. I saw this funny meme the other day about introverts and how we recharge. It perfectly summed up my existence. I mean, all I need is a cozy blanket, some snacks, and a good fantasy novel. Can’t beat that. I wonder if there’s a point where I should start prodding myself to be more social but then again people are… complicated. They can be nice, like when they share their fries or let you borrow a pen, which seems simple enough but sometimes I just don’t want to deal with it. Like that one time in class when a group project came up, and I thought, “great, just what I need.” It’s always the same. Someone takes charge, someone else zones out. I just want to sit in the corner and observe, but then I get sucked in, and I can feel that anxiety bubbling up. It becomes this tight knot in my stomach, and suddenly my brain is screaming “just say something.” But what? Who cares what I think about whatever topic we have to present? I don’t even care half the time. Okay, focus. I was thinking about randomness. What’s the deal with socks? Seriously, they always disappear in the wash. It’s like the washing machine is a portal to another dimension where they all go and live happily ever after. Do they form a sock society? I can see it now. A bunch of mismatched socks having wild adventures while I’m stuck here trying to find a matching set. They probably have more fun than I do. Speaking of, I really need to do laundry. But it’s such a daunting task, the loud noises of the machines and the strange smells of fabric softener. Ugh. I always get overwhelmed by that. Why is everything so much effort? It shouldn’t be. It should just be easy. Anyway, back to being open. I adore exploring. I wanted to travel Europe last summer but that did not happen. Money. Just me and my budget. I wish I could just pack a bag and go, but responsibilities weigh down on me like one of those heavy blankets. If I could teleport, I’d be gone in a heartbeat—see the Eiffel Tower or that little café where they filmed something, I can’t remember what. I hate that I can’t recall specifics; it feels like my brain is a sieve, losing all the tiny details that make life rich and meaningful. I mean, I can synthesize thoughts in a lecture right before the professor calls on me, but when it comes to personal life experiences? Blank. It’s almost funny how it works out that way. Then there’s social media. A love-hate thing for me, scrolling through endless feeds of curated lives just makes me feel more isolated. It’s like watching life happen but not really being part of it. Overthinking every post I make, is it too much? Too little? Why can’t I just type something and leave it? But no, I scroll and edit until every word feels forced. Sometimes I just want to throw my phone out of the window and vanish into the world outside. But then the outside actually has people, and people… that’s a mixed bag. Too much chaos, too much noise. And then I catch myself back with a song, trapped in this loop of thoughts, and I realize how out of touch I am sometimes. How easy it is to just drift on thoughts instead of landing somewhere solid. Like, what does it even mean to “land” somewhere? I want to explore the clouds and never come down. But then again, falling down is scary. And it’s all a balance, isn’t it? Or maybe it’s just a dance. A crazy, unpredictable dance that nobody really knows the steps to. I should probably stop typing soon. But there it is, that song again, creeping back into my mind, and I think, well, at least I’m not totally lost.

Why do I keep staring at my notes? I keep thinking about how I should have written them differently. They seemed so clear at the time but now it’s like what was I thinking? I should have organized them better or used bullet points or something. Why do I do this to myself? I mean, I get it though. It’s all about efficiency. I like having a system, a structure that makes sense. You know what you're dealing with when things are organized. Random notes everywhere just stress me out. I’m not into free-form thinking or whatever that is. It’s like if you can't plan it, why do it? Why are people so into spontaneity? It’s like they want to make chaos their lifestyle. I’ll never understand it. So, the study I’m working on. The results are interesting but not surprising. It’s always the same, isn’t it? People just want to feel validated in whatever their perspective is. It’s all about their narratives, not the data. I mean, what’s the point of gathering all this information if nobody’s going to look at it objectively? I bet the professors will argue over the interpretations. They love to do that. That’s what makes research fun, I guess? Or frustrating? I don’t know. I just want to know what the numbers say. Clear, cut, dry. No fluff. Just give me the facts. Speaking of fluff, I hate when people beat around the bush. I just want them to get straight to the point. Like in discussions or presentations or whatever. Why waste time trying to be polite or soft? Just say what you mean. I think that’s why I stick to my computer more. No small talk, just information. Just me and the screen. Why do people waste so much time on trivial conversations? It feels pointless to connect over the weather or weekend plans. Who cares? But then I think, maybe it’s just me. Maybe I’m missing out on something by shutting those interactions down. But are they really interactions if they’re superficial? It’s like we’re all acting out a script. This reminds me of that group project we did last semester. The one where everyone was just waiting for someone else to take the lead. I ended up doing the bulk of it because nobody would step up. It’s always the same—people say they want to collaborate, but when it comes to action, they just retreat into their corners. I hate that. It’s a waste of time. Teams should work like well-oiled machines but somehow they just turn into this mess of different opinions that get nowhere. I mean, that’s why I don’t like working in teams. Everyone has their own agenda. I just want to finish the project and move on. Why can’t they see the bigger picture? I think some of my classmates are just way too easy to please. They keep saying things like, “Oh, I’m just so grateful to be here.” Why? There’s nothing special about sitting in class. It’s just a requirement—show up, learn what you need, and get out. That’s the goal, right? I’m not here to make friends. I’m here to get my degree. There’s a difference. Expectations are low, it seems. Everyone seems okay with mediocrity. I can’t understand that either. Shouldn’t we be striving for more? I’ve been keeping a checklist for my assignments. Really gets me through. Cross off one thing, move on to the next. It’s therapeutic in a way. Maybe obsessive, but I like it. I need that sense of achievement. No one else seems to appreciate that kind of focus. They’re always distracted by their phones or socializing. I can’t process how they can do both. It doesn’t make sense to me. Multitasking is just dividing your attention. I like to give a task my full attention, crank it out, and then move on. That’s how it should be. Time management is crucial. Some people think deadlines are flexible. I think that’s ridiculous. Deadlines are there for a reason. It helps to keep everything organized. One of my friends said she’ll just submit stuff last minute and it’ll be fine. But what if she forgets? What if the system crashes? You can’t just rely on luck. Planning is everything. I laugh at her for it but kind of feel sorry too. She’ll learn eventually, I guess. Or maybe she won’t. Either way, it’s her choice. Just like how I choose to be rigorous and diligent. So, going back to my notes. I should probably revise them again before the next meeting. But who knows? Maybe they don’t even matter. Maybe it’ll be another one of those rounds of discussions where everyone talks over each other and nothing gets done. But that’s the point, I guess. I roll my eyes but I’ll still show up. It’s what I do. I keep going, whether it feels pointless or not, because that’s what responsibility looks like. At least that’s how I see it.

It's 2:15 PM and I need to get my laundry done but I keep thinking about how I should probably be studying for that midterm next week. It’s like my brain is juggling two things at once and neither gets the attention it deserves. That red shirt I wore last week is still in there and I really need to wash it. I should have done it earlier. Why do I always leave things until the last moment? I mean, I have everything scheduled in my planner so it’s not like I’m disorganized. Just a little too rigid maybe. My friends always say I need to "let loose" and "go with the flow." But that doesn't sound fun to me. I like having a plan. Speaking of plans, I should probably check my emails. My professor sent something important about the group project, but I just can't bring myself to open that inbox. Group projects are so stressful. I like to prepare everything and make sure everyone is on the same page, but people often have their own ideas, and that’s hard for me to deal with since I think my way is the best. Maybe I should just start sending out reminders, but then I sound bossy. That's a fine line to walk. I kind of want to just do my part and not worry too much about what others are doing. I really don’t understand why everyone goes out so often. It's exhausting. Social gatherings make me feel drained, even if I’m surrounded by nice people. I just prefer staying in, reading a book or watching that documentary I’ve been meaning to get to. There’s comfort in routine. I also find it satisfying to organize my notes or plan my week. That feeling when everything is in its neat little box? Love that. The clock ticking softly is annoying. I should probably eat something too. Haven’t eaten since breakfast and here I am, contemplating food options. Cooking sounds like a chore right now but there’s that instant ramen I keep in the cupboard. It’s easy and quick but then I feel guilty about not eating something more nutritious. I could probably chop up some vegetables to add to it, but that requires effort. Ugh, why must I think about everything in such a detailed manner? I remember last week when my roommate invited me to a movie night. I declined because I had my study schedule planned out. She was disappointed but I just can’t handle the pressure of trying to fit in with everyone else’s plans. I hear them laughing down the hall, and part of me wants to join them, but the other part feels content being here, alone, doing my thing. It’s not that I dislike spending time with my friends, I just would rather enjoy my solitude. Sometimes I wonder if I come off as distant or cold. I hope not. I really care about my friends. I always try to be supportive, listen to their problems, and offer help. I just don’t need socialization to fill my cup, I guess. I feel like I’m fine just watching them from the sidelines. They are always inviting me to things, but I usually say no. What’s wrong with me? I can’t help but reflect on how I’ve always been this way. Back in high school, I was the one who stayed home to study while everyone else went out after the football games. It got so boring hearing about their adventures while I sat there, feeling like I missed out but also proud that I did well on my exams. It’s a trade-off I guess. I always got good grades, but at what cost? A social life? Is that really so important? Maybe I should be more open to trying new things, but every time I think that, a little voice tells me to stick to what I know. I don't even like surprises. I crave order and consistency. And if someone suggests something spontaneous, I imagine all the things that could go wrong. It's funny how that works. I feel fine with the way I am, yet I also feel that pressure to change sometimes. What if I just went to that study group later? It’s strange thinking about it. I could contribute and keep everyone on task. It would mean socializing but with a purpose, which makes it easier. Plus, I wouldn’t feel so guilty about not studying enough. Ah, I just have to remind myself it’s not so bad to occasionally step out of my comfort zone, right? Maybe I’ll make a mental note to check in with them, at least. That’s manageable. Okay, laundry. I guess I should get to that soon. I need those clean clothes. Where did my mind drift again? I feel like there's so much buzzing in my head all the time, like a background hum. Focused but distracted. But that's just me, isn’t it? I don’t know if I’ll ever change completely and maybe that’s okay.

Okay so I just can’t stop thinking about that party last weekend. Everyone was there. I don’t even remember how many people were packed into that tiny apartment. My friends were running around yelling, and I was just in the mix trying to keep up, laughing, smiling, having a blast but also feeling that little nagging worry in the back of my mind like is everyone having fun? Did I say something dumb? Oh, God, I probably did, I always do. And then there was that one guy who wouldn’t stop talking about his band and I felt trapped because I didn’t want to be rude but I also didn’t care about his music at all. I mean why do people think anyone actually wants to hear about their passion project when it’s 1AM and we just want to dance? At one point, I tried to join a conversation about movies, someone said something about Tarantino and I thought, yeah I like movies but then everyone started arguing about the best one and I felt so out of it. That’s when the anxiety hit. It’s like I suddenly became hyper-aware of every little thing in the room, the music was too loud, I could barely hear myself think, and I’m thinking, okay breathe, just focus on the fun, you love this. But then there’s the one friend who keeps dragging me back to her latest drama with her boyfriend and I can’t handle the stress of her emotions while trying to be the life of the party. I just want to shake it off, try to diffuse it with some jokes but it feels like a pit of chaos so I smile, nod, and think, “this is fine.” And food! Someone had brought chips and salsa but it was gone in minutes. I wanted more. I always want more. I ended up at the fridge like how did I get here, looking for leftovers, anything to munch on—there was nothing, just a half-empty bottle of soda staring back at me like a sad reminder of my poor choices. I bet everyone thought I was weird for rummaging through their fridge but then again, maybe they didn’t care. People come and go in social settings, sometimes I feel invisible but then someone shouts my name, pulls me into a dance circle, and suddenly I’m the center of attention, which I love, but sometimes it feels overwhelming. The cool thing is that I thrive in groups, and I feel my mood lifting with the energy, but it’s that constant swing between feeling high on life and then crashing down hard because I worry I might have offended someone or the music will stop and silence will swallow me whole. But really, who even cares about all that? I mean, life’s supposed to be fun, right? So I just keep chatting and laughing but what’s exhausting is that nagging fear that crops up when I meet new people. They’re all so cool and collected and I’m just there, trying not to stutter over my words, hoping they like me, and that they’ll invite me to hang out again because I do want friends but honestly, I’m definitely not the best at keeping up with plans or organizing anything. Like, I’ll say yes to everything but then forget about it until it’s too late. I guess it’s the low conscientiousness thing. I try, but my mind is always racing to the next fun thing and I lose track. Had this weird moment at the party where I thought I spotted someone I knew but it turned out to be a total stranger. Awkward, right? I laughed it off but internally I was panicking like what if I accidentally approached someone from class and made it super weird. I realized I could never be a politician but the funny thing is I feel like I’m too agreeable sometimes. Like I’ll go along with whatever people suggest just to keep the peace, even if I’m stressed inside. Why can't I just say what I want? Oh, and the music! So good. At least that was consistent. I felt the beat take over, like I couldn’t help but move. That’s where I shine, just feeling the rhythm and losing myself instead of worrying about anything else. For a few hours, it’s just me and the dance floor, and everyone else feels like background noise and I’m free. And then I’ll breathe, close my eyes, and just dance like no one’s watching but of course they are because there’s always someone watching. I finally let loose after all those anxiety spirals and it feels amazing. But the moment you stop, it’s like the riptide drags you back and you’re back to thinking, “What am I doing? What’s next?” But all I want is to grab another drink and keep dancing. Focus on the fun, focus on the energy. I guess it’s a balancing act between feeling overwhelmed and loving life. Need to find that sweet spot.

I can’t believe how many plants I have in my room right now, it's like a little jungle but also kind of calming. The way the leaves unfurl is just fascinating, each one telling its own story. I wonder if they feel anything, like do they have some kind of communication system? I read somewhere that they can actually respond to their environment. I also could use another book about that since I’ve already gone through a few. I love books. They're like a gateway to other worlds, I can just lose myself in the pages. I should probably go to the library later, they have this collection of biographies that I've been meaning to check out. Biographies are so interesting, learning about other people's lives. It makes me feel connected to humanity in a way. But then again, sometimes it can be a bit overwhelming, I get lost in it too much and forget what I'm doing in the present. Speaking of being present, I have a short meeting with my study group today. We're usually quiet but somehow we manage to get things done. I like that everyone respects each other's input, you know? It makes the atmosphere so much more productive, even if no one is really dominating the conversation. It’s nice to have that kind of collaboration. I feel like when you’re in a group where everyone puts in the effort, it helps not just the project but also the relationships. I thought about suggesting we go for coffee after, but they might think it’s weird because hanging out after studying seems so casual. But spending time together outside of class could be fun. I enjoy getting to know people beyond just the surface level, like what they dream about or what weird hobbies they have. I feel like everyone has something unique to offer, and I want to hear those stories. Then again, I don't always know how to initiate those deeper conversations. My brain sometimes feels like it’s filled with quiet thoughts while everyone else is chattering away, and it’s hard to find the right moment to speak up. I don’t want to interrupt, and silence is so comfortable sometimes. I think it’s just this balance between wanting to connect and also being okay with being alone. I don’t mind solitude, in fact, I cherish it. I have a million thoughts running through my mind, and I often prefer to explore them on my own for a bit. I could dive into my own writing later. Journaling is kind of like therapy for me. It's a way for me to organize my feelings. It’s interesting how writing can help untangle the mess in my head. Do other people do that? I’ve heard some people loathe journaling, like it’s a chore. But I find it freeing. I’m also curious about art. I want to try painting again. I used to enjoy it but got caught up in academics and forgot how liberating it is to splash colors around. There's something about the process of creating without judgment that feels so appealing. A crazy idea pops into my head, should I host a small art night? Just a few friends, some music playing, and everyone can work on something. That could be a good way to blend my desire to be social with my love for creativity. I can picture it now, paint everywhere, people laughing, inspiration flowing. But what if nobody shows up? It’s a risk putting that out there, but maybe I just need to push past that fear. I know I can handle rejection well, it’s just more about the anticipation of it that gets to me. Thinking about it more, I realize I love the outdoors too. There's something about a hike that always rejuvenates me. I could spend hours wandering through a forest, just absorbing the tranquility and beauty. The smell of pine trees, the crunch of leaves underfoot, it’s all just so grounding. It makes everything feel so large and significant in a way that my worries seem small. I should plan a weekend nature trip. I could bring a notebook, get some fresh air, jot down thoughts in between. The idea of disconnecting from all the technology and just existing in nature is so appealing. I wonder if I could convince a couple of my introverted friends to join, it would be nice to have company out there, yet still keep the chill vibe. I really enjoy exploring new ideas and perspectives. I could dive into philosophy again, it’s like opening a door to endless questions. What if we always existed in some form? I wonder about that. It’s deep stuff, but I love the challenge of pondering the 'what ifs.' Being open to new thoughts feels vital. It’s strange how the world keeps evolving and yet some things remain constant. Like friendship, or love, or the craving for understanding. I just want to know more, experience more, enjoy the little things in life. There’s so much out there, so many stories to tell.

Okay so where to start? I think about that coffee cup sitting on the table, it's empty but I can still smell the leftover hints of vanilla latte. I want another one but I probably don’t need it. Sleep is important after all and I have that exam coming up. But then again, I love the buzz of caffeine, it just makes everything so much more exciting, right? Speaking of excitement, did you see that new exhibit downtown? The one with the interactive art? I read a review that said it was incredible. I really need to get my friends together and go check it out. I just know we would have the best time; it’s always a blast when we’re all together. Group energy is just so infectious. Sometimes I get overwhelmed with all the things to do, though. Like there’s this club meeting later, and I signed up to organize an event next month. I love planning those things—bringing people together, getting everyone hyped. It's so rewarding when things go well. The other day I accidentally set the meeting time for an hour earlier than I intended and it turned into this hilarious mix-up where half the team showed up and we ended up brainstorming ideas anyway. I love those moments of spontaneity, like, who needs a strict schedule? Let’s just roll with it! But I do have to admit I worry about my time management sometimes. Balancing schoolwork, extracurriculars, and a social life can get crazy and I definitely want to do well academically. I’m the sort of person who feels responsible for making sure everyone else is enjoying themselves too, like I take on that role naturally. My friends always say I’m the one who keeps the energy up. I’m like a cheerleader without the pom-poms! Maybe I should try cheerleading. Nah, I’d never actually do that but it sounds fun. The whole idea of jumping around and chanting—it just brings a smile to my face. Oh, and it’s not just about my friends! I’m always thinking about new ways to meet people. Just last week I went to that poetry slam and struck up a conversation with the girl sitting next to me. She was super cool and into the same indie music scene as me. We ended up exchanging numbers and now we’re going to a concert next month together. Just like that! It’s amazing how you can meet new friends in the most unexpected places, kind of like a treasure hunt. But what about the people who don’t want to chat? There’s this one classmate who seems to prefer sitting alone, and I totally respect it, but I wish I could just reach out and pull them into the conversation. I mean, I really believe everyone has something interesting to share. Sometimes I get this itch to just go over and say hi, but I don't want to pressure anyone. It’s all about reading the room, right? I think if everyone just made one new friend each week, the world would be a happier place. It’s like magic! The other day, I overheard someone say that they don't like group projects. I can’t understand that! I thrive on collaboration. There’s something so energizing about pooling everyone’s thoughts and ideas. Even when we argue a bit over decisions, I think that’s part of the process! Compromise is such an important skill, and it’s just fun to see how everyone’s brain works differently. And oh man, the travel bug. I could talk forever about traveling. Last summer I went to Europe and it was the best experience. Everything was vibrant, full of life. The people I met at hostels were such characters. We explored so many hidden gems, shared meals, and swapped stories around the table. There’s this exhilarating feeling of being in a new place and you just can't help but smile. I wish I could do it more often, just hop on a plane and go wherever, trying new foods and meeting new faces! I can’t believe how quickly this year is flying by, like—what’s next? Halloween? Thanksgiving? I love those holidays, the spirit of togetherness, the food, the laughter. I’m already thinking about what to dress up as! Last year I went as a disco ball—the outfit was a hit! I’m kind of setting myself up for high expectations this time, though. Ugh, I should probably get back to studying. But this flow of thoughts is so therapeutic, it makes me feel alive, like I’m tapping into my core. I should remember to write about this in the future, how all these connections and experiences weave together into one beautiful tapestry. That’s life, right? A series of moments strung together. Alright, back to reality! Let’s tackle that study material!

Okay so I was just thinking about how much I love meeting new people but wow sometimes it’s so overwhelming, like I went to this party last weekend and there were so many people, I couldn’t even keep track of names and faces, but I tried my best! I actually love that rush of excitement from chatting with so many different people, every conversation is like a new adventure, and I just want to connect with everyone. But then I start spiraling because I can’t remember who said what and then I think they might judge me for forgetting, which is silly right? I mean, who really remembers everything? But then my mind goes there. It’s like a hamster wheel of thoughts. Anxiety peeking through my excitement. I remember someone talking about their summer plans, and suddenly I'm feeling freaked out about my own, like should I plan a trip or just stay and work? What if I miss out on something amazing? Should I travel solo or find friends to go with? But I’m always worried about everyone else's schedules. What if I’m too demanding or too chill? That kind of balance is so hard to nail down. I want everyone to be happy and have a great time, it’s so important to me. Sometimes I can’t tell if I’m genuinely interested or just trying to be agreeable. That pressure to be liked can be suffocating. I wish I could be a cool cucumber. And the weather! It’s a like a conversation starter but this year it has been so weird. Like why is it 80 degrees in October? Global warming or just a weird climate cycle—who knows? I feel this urge to talk to people about it like let’s have a group discussion on the implications of climate change over lattes. It’s fascinating and scary and I want everyone to care about it as much as I do! It gets me so fired up and talking to people about these big issues just makes me feel alive. I love seeing their eyes light up when they’re passionate too. Then there’s school. I’m taking this class on cultural psychology and it’s completely eye-opening. I love learning how different people think and feel based on their backgrounds. It’s like a puzzle and I want to put all the pieces together. It’s fascinating to me how culture shapes individual behavior. I often find myself thinking about my friends from different backgrounds and their perspectives. Sometimes I think about how much I enjoy being around people who challenge me or make me think differently. I can’t help but want to understand their viewpoints and maybe adopt some of their values too. It’s exhausting, honestly, but worth it. But on the flip side, I sometimes feel so drained after a long week of social engagements. It’s like a rollercoaster of emotions. I thrive on social interactions but then crash and need time to recharge. Like, I’ll go out to a concert and have the time of my life, but then I’ll come home and just need to be alone for a few hours or I’ll feel like I’m going to explode from all the energy I’ve absorbed. It’s this weird push and pull—I crave connection but I also need my personal space to decompress. Finding that balance is tough! And speaking of balance, I’ve been trying to manage my time better. It’s hard because I want to be involved in everything but I also have assignments and work. Sometimes I’ll write lists or try to color-code my planner, but I always end up scribbling over things because I fill my schedule to the brim. I need to work on saying no, but saying no feels so wrong sometimes. I want to be that reliable friend who always says yes. But then I regret it when I’m running on fumes. I just want to be dependable but in a way that doesn’t burn me out. Is that even possible? I often think that I love diving into new things, trying new foods, experiencing cultures, and being spontaneous. I love that thrill of something unfamiliar. But then I need to remind myself to keep it real and not stretch myself too thin. I want to embrace new experiences but also find that inner peace in my routine. I guess that’s the challenge of being open-minded but also conscientious. Also, I think about how my friends sometimes come to me for advice, and I love it! But what if I give them bad advice? I always want them to feel supported and happy. It’s a lot of responsibility weighing on my shoulders but I wouldn’t trade it for anything. Those moments of connection, when I feel needed, those are the times I feel most fulfilled. It’s all a crazy balancing act of emotions and responsibilities.

Okay so I just remembered that time at the party where I tried to introduce myself to everyone but then somehow lost track of who I was talking to halfway through and ended up repeating myself a lot and it was kind of embarrassing but also funny because the way they all laughed made it feel okay like we’re all in this weird social experiment together you know? But then I was thinking about how much I love parties but they also make me so anxious and my heart races and I just want to know everybody’s life story and I mean what makes people tick and why they are the way they are but in like a nice friendly way where it’s not too intrusive but do people even want to share that with a stranger? Sometimes I feel like I should probably just stick to small talk but small talk feels so hollow sometimes like how do you even gauge if someone’s really into the conversation or just nodding along politely but then again I do that too because I always want to be nice and agreeable and keep the vibe good but my brain runs at a million miles an hour and I can't quite settle on one topic for too long without jumping to another. Oh! That reminds me of when I went hiking last summer with that group from class and we all ended up lost because I volunteered to be the navigator which was a terrible idea because I can’t read maps to save my life but everyone was super chill about it and we ended up singing silly songs while we walked and that helped me forget that I was totally stressed out because what if we ran out of snacks? Which made me think of how food is always such a social equalizer like you can bond over pizza or tacos or something ridiculous like who can hold the most marshmallows in their mouths. I think about food a lot actually, the way it connects people, and how everyone has a different relationship with it but I sometimes get worried people judge me for what I eat like do they think I eat too much or too little or like my chocolate obsession is weird? And seriously how is it that every single time I go to the grocery store I end up buying stuff I don’t need but then I feel guilty for spending money because I could have just made a list but I always forget and then I try to plan meals in my head but then I get distracted by shiny packaging or new flavors and what if I miss out on the best chips ever? And then there’s self-checkout where I can never figure out the machine and I feel like everyone is staring at me judging my choices like I could have picked kale instead of those fancy chips but whatever it’s just a snack, right? I think that’s why I like being around people so much I thrive on the energy of others and sometimes I can feel overwhelmed but then I just look for smiles and laughter and that makes it better except when I can’t escape the anxiety that's bubbling beneath the surface and I have to keep reminding myself to breathe and take it one step at a time. But then again, I always want to help others feel good too, like if I see someone having a tough day I just want to cheer them up and make them laugh and then I’ll tell them my embarrassing stories because if I can make them feel less alone then at least that’s something. But the other thing is, sometimes I feel so deeply and it’s like a roller coaster of emotions, which can be tiring, you know? I can go from ecstatic laughter to almost crying in a matter of seconds depending on what someone says and I wish it didn’t hit me so hard but then that’s also what makes me feel alive, like truly alive and engaged in the world. I realize I’m way too open about my feelings sometimes which probably makes people uncomfortable but I just can’t help it. Like if I get a compliment, I’ll probably overthink it and analyze every little word trying to figure out if I'm really deserving of it and then I’ll be like, “No, just accept it, they meant it!” And then there's art and creativity. I love that! I wish I could paint or draw. I can’t but I love going to galleries and seeing what people create. It opens my mind and inspires me even though I just doodle in the margins of my notebooks and my sketches look like a kindergartner’s work. I think about writing poetry too but what if it’s terrible? But then again, who cares, right? I like the idea of just putting my thoughts down however messy they are. Maybe that’s art too, just a jumble of feelings spilled onto the page. And I could talk about this all day but I also feel like I’m running out of time or space or something and suddenly all these thoughts seem jumbled up and I need to organize. But that’s fine, it’s part of who I am. So many emotions, so many ideas, and so many connections. I wouldn’t trade it for the world.

okay so I just can’t stop thinking about how awkward it is to be in a group project and you’re trying to contribute but everyone’s talking over each other and I feel like I’m just sitting there like a lump not able to get a word in edgewise and it’s so frustrating because I have ideas but I don’t want to interrupt anyone but then I worry that my ideas won’t be good enough anyway like what if I suggest something and it’s totally off base and everyone else looks at me sideways and then I end up just sitting there in silence like a weirdo and then I start thinking everyone must hate me for not being assertive enough and it spirals into this whole self-doubt thing ugh I hate that. I wish people could just take turns talking but then if I were to suggest that it’d probably come off weird like who am I to dictate how a group should function right. Anyway, I really should be studying for that exam but I keep getting sidetracked by these thoughts that just loop around in my head like a broken record. 2 days left to prepare and I feel so behind. Why can’t I focus on one thing for more than five minutes? Okay, breathe, it’ll be fine, it’s just a test, I should probably just focus on the topics I know well instead of stressing over everything. But the feeling of needing to know all the details, to be thorough, it just weighs on me, you know? Like there’s this constant pressure to be perfect. I always wonder if other people feel this way or if it’s just me. I should probably ask around but that terrifies me. What if they all give me that “what’s wrong with you” look? I can’t stand it. I want to be supportive of my friends but sometimes it feels like my own worries drown out their voices. It’s hard to be agreeable when your own anxiety is like this nagging little monster inside you poking at your brain. I mean, how do people just go about their lives so carefree? Like that girl in class always laughing and chatting, she seems so confident, I almost envy that. But then I think, is she really as happy as she seems? Maybe everyone has their own stuff going on that I just don’t see. I guess we’re all just trying to figure it out, right? But what if my way of figuring it out is just never going to be good enough? I can’t help but worry about future jobs and interviews and what if I can’t handle that pressure? I’m supposed to be responsible and conscientious but does that even mean anything if I can’t even interact in social situations? Okay back to the study material, I should probably write some key points down. Flashcards are always a good way to memorize, I used them for the last exam and it helped a lot but now there’s so much information that I feel overwhelmed. And don’t get me started on the way the professors present material. Some days, it feels like they want us to absorb everything at once and it’s just too much, my mind can’t keep up with that pace. Breathe in, breathe out. What’s the worst that could happen, right? I pass or I don’t, it’s not the end of the world, but I still can’t shake that feeling of impending doom that hangs over me. Maybe I should go for a walk to clear my head but then I think about all the things I should be doing instead like studying or doing laundry or all the little tasks piling up as if they’re mounting pressure on my chest. I can’t just ignore them and pretend they don’t exist, but I also don’t want to face them. There’s the looming deadline for that paper too, just sitting there in my mind like a dark cloud ready to spill rain all over me. Why do I do this to myself? I should be able to manage my time better but it’s like a cycle, stress leads to procrastination leads to more stress and it just keeps going. I need to snap out of it, maybe I’ll set an alarm and just study for a bit. Break everything down into chunks, one section at a time, manageable pieces. That seems less intimidating but then I think what if I still don’t understand the material? Am I smart enough? What if I need to ask for help but I hate asking for help. It feels like a failure but isn’t that what everyone says? You’re supposed to ask for help when you need it, it shows strength not weakness. But what if I can’t do it, what if I never learn to balance my life in a way that makes sense? Okay focus, breathe, think about something positive. I really like the smell of fresh books, they always make me feel better. The crisp pages, the sound of flipping them over. Maybe I should get a nice quiet space at the library for some concentrated studying. Just need to keep pushing through all this noise in my head. Just keep pushing.

So, I just saw an email about a group project due soon, and my heart sank. Group projects stress me out, like, why can’t we just do assignments individually? Too many people, too many ideas, and what if they don’t listen to me? I mean, what if they don’t take it seriously? I just want everything to be perfect, and honestly, I’d rather work alone than try to navigate the unpredictable waters of other people’s thoughts. What if someone drops the ball? It’s going to reflect poorly on everyone, especially me, because I tend to take on all the responsibility anyway. It’s like I can already see the awkward group chat messages that start with “hey guys” as if that makes it better. Why can’t they just be straightforward? Just tell me what to do, please. I wish I could just be more relaxed about it, like some people seem to be. I see others in class complaining about the workload, but they’re so chill, throwing off jokes and laughing. I always wonder if they’re really that carefree or if they just don’t care about grades as much as I do. I have this constant fear of failure lurking in my mind, almost like a shadow that follows me around. I mean, I like to think I’m a nice person. I want to help everyone, but I also want to protect myself from stress and anxiety. It feels like I’m always walking this tightrope—being agreeable and wanting to be helpful but also freaking out about things not going the way I planned. Oh, and speaking of plans, I can’t believe I’m already behind on my reading. I made a schedule, like I always do, but then life happens, and now the book is just sitting there, staring at me. I know it’s important, but I keep finding excuses. From laundry to cleaning my desk to scrolling through social media, anything to delay facing that growing pile of pages. I wonder if my friends ever feel this way, like maybe everyone else has it all together while I’m just a mess internally, overthinking everything. I shouldn’t care what they think, but then I do, and it’s exhausting. Sometimes I think about how I’m probably gonna overthink my responses in class discussions. I hate it when the professor looks directly at me, expecting an answer. The pressure builds, and I can feel my palms getting sweaty. What if I say something dumb? What if they judge me? I think it’s easier to just keep quiet and take notes. But then I see other people, and they always seem ready to jump in like it’s the easiest thing in the world. Maybe they’ve learned to not care about what others think so much. I wish I could just let it roll off my back like them. I guess I’ve always been sensitive. I can remember in high school when I’d get feedback on my presentations. I’d strive for good critiques, and when I didn’t get them, it haunted me. It’s really hard to shake off, that feeling of disappointment—like I disappointed everyone, including myself. And then there's the fear of being misunderstood. I think I’m being friendly, and I realize later that maybe I came off too intense. There’s a balance that I struggle to find, and it leaves me feeling drained. Maybe I should just surrender to the chaos of group work and try to loosen up, but the thought is terrifying. I find comfort in routines. Knowing what I’m going to do makes me feel safe. There’s some therapy about being present and breathing through the anxiety, and I try. But when I stress, I can’t focus on anything else. I go through the motions but feel like a robot. I wish I could just turn off my brain like a light switch. I guess that’s why I’m kind of strict with myself about deadlines and assignments; it helps me feel in control even if something in me just wants to run away. Just thinking about this makes me anxious too. It’s like this awful cycle—be anxious about something, then overthink it endlessly. I should probably try to find a new hobby or something to escape this constant loop in my mind. But then again, would I even stick with it or just end up dropping it like everything else I’ve tried? That’s a whole ordeal there! I’m good at following through on work, but leisure? There’s always a voice telling me it’s a waste of time. It’s silly, right? I just want to enjoy life without worrying about it. How do people find that balance? It seems like an impossible quest.

I just saw a notification pop up about the latest episode of that show everyone is talking about. I don't get why people find it so interesting. Maybe I'm missing something? I mean, it's just a bunch of people talking and doing things that don't really matter. I used to think maybe I'd watch it but what's the point? I can barely keep up with what’s going on in my own life, let alone fictional drama. There’s too much noise in the world anyway. Why would I want more? It's like when you go into those crowded places and everyone is laughing and talking, but inside, I’m just hating it. I’d rather be anywhere else. Maybe I'd just stay in my room, wrapped in a blanket, staring at the wall, thinking about all the things I haven't done. Every time I think about projects I need to finish, or emails I need to send, it just feels like too much effort. I'm sitting here, my laptop open, and there’s at least five tabs I should be looking at but I can't even remember what they are. There’s this one about an assignment due next week, I should really get on that. But then again, it’s just another thing that’s going to stress me out later, so why not just leave it alone until I absolutely have to deal with it? Deadline panic is a thing, right? Sometimes I wonder if I should talk to more people, but it’s such a hassle. And then there are all those small talk expectations. “How’s your day?” “What have you been up to?” Honestly, who cares? That’s where people get it all wrong. I don’t want to share my mundane details; I’d rather just stand there in silence. Just nodding and pretending to listen. And when they start talking about their plans for the weekend, I can’t help but feel a little envious. How do they even manage to find things to do? I'd probably just end up sitting at home anyway, scrolling through social media, which just leaves me feeling worse. And then there are the group projects. Ugh. I’m always the one who ends up doing more work than everyone else because nobody cares enough to pull their weight. I can see that it’s happening but if I say something then I come off as rude and I don't want to be that person. So I just end up absorbing all the stress. Just thinking about them makes my stomach churn. And when they ask for my opinion, I just kind of shrug it off like it doesn’t matter. Whatever I say doesn’t seem to fit into their ideas anyway. This whole idea of socializing is exhausting. There's a gathering coming up, some sort of get-together for the major, and I’m seriously considering just skipping it. I don’t know anyone well enough to want to stand around awkwardly. I can hear them now, laughing, making small talk, and I’m just there in the corner wishing I could turn invisible. Why can’t we just send emails and be done with it? Or just text? It feels like so much pressure to interact. When they come up to me and ask me questions, my mind just goes blank. I should probably study more but even thinking about that is draining. What if I just didn’t show up? I can already hear my anxiety’s voice telling me they’ll notice. “Oh, where’s so-and-so?” Who cares? That’s what I want to scream. Does anyone even remember? But then, what if they do? Then I start rethinking everything. The possibility of being looked at like an outsider makes me want to just curl up and disappear. Being around people feels like a ticking time bomb. I can hear them talking about their lives, their plans, and it’s annoying. How do they keep up? It’s all so pointless. The world outside feels too big, too bright, too loud. My thoughts are way more interesting than their chatter, I tell myself, right? The other day, I walked past this café filled with people and I wondered if they were actually enjoying themselves or just pretending. I mean, do people really find joy in that? I don’t get it. Everyone’s glued to their phones anyway, so what’s the point of having conversations? But then again, maybe I'm just bitter. It’s hard to tell. I try to avoid thinking too much about it, but it keeps creeping in. The comparisons, the judgments, that feeling of not measuring up. I hate it. My thoughts are all jumbled now, feel like a mess. Is it weird to want to just hit pause on everything? I should really get back to that assignment. Or maybe I’ll just sit here a little longer. The time is passing anyway.

Okay so I was just thinking about how the last time I went to that concert with my friends, it was such a blast! We were all jumping around, singing at the top of our lungs, and the energy was just infectious. I love that feeling of being surrounded by people, the kind of atmosphere that makes everything else fade away. Music really brings people together, doesn’t it? And then afterward, we went to that taco truck that’s always parked outside. I can't remember the last time I had something so delicious. Street food is just the best! There’s something about eating tacos at 2 AM that feels so thrilling and liberating. I mean, who cares about the calories? Speaking of calories, I really should work out more. I joined that gym last month, but I’ve only gone like three times. Maybe it’s just not my thing? I just want to be having fun and hanging out with people rather than counting reps or something. The whole structure of a workout routine seems so confining. I feel like I’d much rather do something spontaneous, like go on a hike or play frisbee at the park – something that’ll get me moving and keep the vibes high. And oh man, it’s wild how I can just lose track of time when I’m with friends. Like the other day, we were playing board games for hours. I absolute love games where everyone gets to talk and strategize together. Those were some of the best moments because everyone was just being goofy, throwing out ridiculous ideas and laughing until our sides hurt. I can't even remember what the game was, only that it had me in stitches. Maybe I should suggest a board game night again soon, those bring out the best in everyone. But then there are times I crave some deep conversations, you know? I mean, I love the light-hearted fun, but sometimes I just want to dive deep and talk about life, dreams, fears and all that jazz. Like that one time in class when my friend opened up about their family struggles, and it just made me realize how much we all have going on behind the scenes. I think that’s why I enjoy the company of so many different people. Everyone has a story to tell, and hearing those stories adds so much color to my own life. Ugh, but balancing all of it can be a lot sometimes. I get invitations to hang out all the time but I'm not always great at keeping track of them. I remember missing out on that friends’ trip because I spaced on the planning. I felt bad about it, but there’s only so much I can keep in my head, you know? Sometimes the spontaneity of being social just takes over my organized side completely, if I even have one. I mean, who even makes plans for the weekend on a Wednesday? I might as well throw a dart at a calendar and see where I land! I still remember that time I just decided to go to that random art exhibit downtown. I had no idea what to expect, and it turned out to be this mind-blowing experience. There were these giant sculptures made out of recycled materials, and it was like each piece was a conversation starter. I ended up chatting with this artist who was there, and they were super passionate about their work. We talked for almost an hour! I love moments like that. You never know who you’ll meet or what you’ll discover when you just say yes and go. And travel! Oh my gosh, I can’t wait for my next adventure. I just want to hop on a plane and see something different. It’s crazy how many amazing places there are in the world, and I feel sometimes like I’m just scratching the surface. I keep thinking about going to that carnival in Brazil. The colors, the music, the food, the people! The idea of being surrounded by that kind of energy is exhilarating. I want to dance, meet locals, try everything! Social butterflies like me thrive in that kind of atmosphere. Sometimes I wonder if I should take some time to just chill, but chilling feels wasted when there’s so much to see and do. I mean, life’s too short to sit around, right? There’s so much joy to be found in connection, in moving around, in experiencing the world firsthand. I could sit and scroll on my phone or binge-watch another series, but nothing beats the rush of real-life interaction, laughter, and spontaneity. That’s what life is all about for me: the connections, the fun, the memories made together. It's all a bit chaotic, but I thrive in that, and I wouldn’t have it any other way.

Okay so I just remembered that time I went to that concert last summer with my friends, and it was so much fun! I love live music, the energy is just electric and the crowd is all singing along and dancing even if we can't all sing well, it doesn’t even matter because the vibe is just so right. I can't believe how many new people I ended up meeting there! Each one was more interesting than the last, and we all bonded over our love for this band that played such a great show. I wonder how many friendships started at that concert that night because we were all just so ready to connect. Speaking of connections, I've been thinking about how important it is to foster relationships, like at work or in class! Actually, I’m usually the one who ends up chatting with the new people at group projects. It’s always interesting figuring out everyone's strengths, like one person is super organized, another has great ideas, and we just mesh into this perfect little team. I love that feeling of collaboration and creativity. We can brainstorm for hours and every idea that pops into our heads just builds on the last one, it’s like a chain reaction of awesomeness! Hmm, it’s funny how some people shy away from group activities. I get that some might feel overwhelmed or anxious, but I want to tell them, “Hey, just dive in!” It’s all about the experience and having a good laugh at the absurdities life throws at us. I mean, sometimes I think about how ridiculous my dance moves are, but who cares? We’re all out there just making memories, and I live for those moments. Ugh, but then there are those days when I want a break. Like, I love being around people, but there's a thin line between socializing and exhaustion, right? And then when I’m drained, it's like I need a recharge time, just a few hours to chill by myself, rewatching my favorite shows or listening to podcasts about random stuff. There’s this one podcast about unusual historical events that I just can’t get enough of! I find it fascinating how people from different eras dealt with life challenges. It really gives me perspective on what’s important, and it reminds me to appreciate the little things. Oh, and I’ve been trying to learn some new skills lately! I signed up for this pottery class and, wow, I had no idea how therapeutic it would be. There’s something so satisfying about molding the clay and creating something tangible with my hands. It’s like entering another world where it's just me and my thoughts, no distractions. The instructor is super encouraging too! I just love people like that who bring positivity into the room. It makes the learning process so much more enjoyable. Speaking of learning, I have this big presentation coming up next week for that marketing class! I’m actually kind of excited about it. I’ve already mapped out my slides and practiced a few times. Public speaking is a thrill for me! Standing in front of a crowd, sharing ideas, and connecting with people. It’s like every time I step up to speak, I'm sharing a little piece of my passion for this subject. I hope they enjoy it as much as I do! Now I’m switching gears in my head thinking about my family and how important they are to me. Every Sunday we all try to get together for dinner, no matter how busy life gets. It’s like this unwritten rule. I cherish those times where we’re all just laughing and sharing stories. My grandma always tells the best tales from her childhood, and I can see my parents' eyes light up as they listen. Then there’s the food! She makes this amazing lasagna that I swear is magic. It brings everyone together. I guess that’s what life really comes down to. It’s all about sharing experiences with the people we love, right? The laughter, the memories, and even the mundane moments. I don’t want to miss a single one. There’s so much out there to explore! From traveling to new places, trying different foods, or just having deep conversations under the stars. I want to dive headfirst into everything—life is too short to hold back. I find myself daydreaming about traveling to far-off places. I want to see everything from the streets of Paris to the beaches of Bali! Each destination is a new adventure. Imagine the stories I’d have to share and new friends I could make along the way! I really lucked out with my personality and the ability to form connections easily. Okay, I should stop daydreaming, but I can’t help it! There's just so much to do and explore!

Alright, so I’m sitting here thinking about how I really need to organize my notes for that biology class. When did I let it get so messy? It looks like a tornado hit it. Not that I mind the mess too much, but it’s just not efficient. Wow, efficiency is really my middle name, isn’t it? I like knowing where everything is. That’s such a relief. And when I do clean it up, it feels good, like I’ve accomplished something. I guess I thrive on that sense of order. The thought of facing my professor next week with these jumbled notes makes me cringe. Maybe I should just start with the easiest subject, something I can tackle without feeling overwhelmed. I remember last week we did that group project. Those other students were all over the place, chatting and laughing while I was just there trying to keep things on track. They think it’s fun to wing it, but I don’t like that at all. When you don’t have a structure, how can you really get anything done? It’s like sailing a ship without any direction – just floating around hoping to find land. That’s not me. I want to get to my destination efficiently, even if that destination is just an A on the assignment. Maybe they find it boring, but I genuinely like the feeling of ticking things off my list. Speaking of lists, I should probably update my to-do list. I’ve got everything scattered in my head, but if it’s not written down, it feels like it doesn't exist. I bought this fancy planner that I thought would help, but honestly, I just end up scribbling ideas on random scraps of paper. I should really stick to the planner. It’s so colorful and nice to look at. Maybe I’ll do that right after this. Lists give me a sense of control, don’t they? I think it’s reassuring – I know exactly what I need to do and when. But then again, socializing is like this whole other beast. All these people enjoying their weekend with parties and gatherings. It just exhausts me thinking about it. I mean, don’t get me wrong, I’m not anti-social or anything. That’s just not my go-to. I’d rather have a deep conversation with one or two people than be at the center of a huge crowd. I’m okay being alone with my thoughts, which I suppose comes from not being overly anxious about being outside of my comfort zone. I just find more peace in solitude. I think about how that one guy in my class always invites me to hang out – super friendly, extroverted type, and I appreciate that. He’s a good guy, but he just doesn’t get it. I’ll never be the person who can rattle off a dozen names of people I met last week. It’s okay, though. I think he means well. I really do enjoy connecting with people in small groups. Maybe someday I’ll surprise him and show up at one of those gatherings, but only if I know someone will be there who I can have a real conversation with. I keep noticing how everyone’s so obsessed with being open-minded and adventurous these days. I just don’t feel the need to try new things for the sake of it. I’m more of the “why fix what isn’t broken” mindset. I get why people want to travel and explore, but for me, a weekend hiking trip in the local park is just fine. I would be totally content with that. It’s familiar, and there’s nothing wrong with taking comfort in the things we know. Oh, the other day I saw a study about how people who are more agreeable tend to get along better in group settings. Duh. It’s like common sense, right? But it also got me thinking about how I value harmony, maybe too much sometimes. I don’t want to upset anyone. Like when my roommate wanted to paint the shared living room an awful shade of orange, I just nodded along instead of saying how I felt. I’ll have to bring it up later; I think there’s a compromise in there somewhere. Maybe a calmer color and we can put a fun accent piece or something. But can I really go through life not saying how I feel out of fear of upsetting someone? I’d hate to come off as passive-aggressive. I want people to feel comfortable talking to me, but I want to share my ideas too. It’s a fine balance, isn’t it? Ugh, why does being an adult have to be so complicated? By the end of the day, I just want a cozy corner and a good book, with a cup of tea in my hands. I should probably consider whether it’s okay to prioritize that once in a while.

Okay so, I’m just sitting here thinking about how overwhelming everything feels. My thoughts are all jumbled. Like, there’s this constant buzz in my head, and it’s like, "What’s the next thing I need to worry about?" I remember that time I gave a presentation and my palms were sweaty and I felt everyone’s eyes on me like lasers. I kept thinking I was going to mess it up, and what if I forgot my points? It’s exhausting just recalling it. I don’t even know how people can just chat with strangers like it’s nothing. I see people at parties, laughing, mingling, and I’m like, “No way. Not for me.” Gives me anxiety just thinking about it. Then there’s social media. I go on Instagram or Twitter, and it feels like everyone has these perfect lives—perfect outfits, perfect vacations, and I’m here in my sweatpants binge-watching some random show while eating leftover pizza. I know I should post more but what if no one cares? Or worse, what if they care too much? Like, my cousin posted this amazing travel photo, and I’m like, “I haven’t left my town in a year.” I start scrolling and it just spirals. I see this girl I went to high school with and she’s engaged, and I’m still figuring out if I want to finish my degree or take a gap year—or never leave my room. It’s 2 AM. Should I sleep? I know I should, but my thoughts tend to race at this hour. The amount of things I could’ve done differently today keeps replaying. Like, I should’ve said something more interesting when my professor asked about our favorite books. I blanked and said some generic title—I could’ve mentioned that one book I loved, but what if that made me sound weird? Ugh. I hate that feeling of overthinking every small interaction, trying to figure out if I said the right thing or if I came off too eager or too dull. It makes me just want to hide. Then again, I’m all for helping others, like if my friend asks for advice or support, I’m there, but if someone needs to make small talk? It’s like my brain just shuts down. I feel like I can’t breathe. Do people notice how uncomfortable I get? Probably. I wish it wasn’t so hard to just be normal. Normal feels out of reach. I think of my roommate who’s super outgoing and always going out. She thrives on that energy, and I just want to tell her how much I’d prefer a night in with pizza and a movie but then she looks at me like, “Why don’t you want to come?” and I feel bad. Like it’s my fault I can’t just loosen up. I wish I could be the type to just say whatever comes to mind without worrying about it. What if I did? Would I regret it? I almost envy those personalities that don’t care. I remember this one guy from class who just says whatever, he doesn’t think twice. One time he told the whole class about his awkward first date. It was hilarious, but at the same time, I was mortified for him. Would I ever be that brave? Probably not. Now I’m thinking about my future, and it’s just a mess of “what ifs.” What if I don’t get a job after graduation? What if I fail all my classes? What if I can’t keep it together when I finally face the real world? I can’t handle that kind of pressure. It’s like my brain stops working altogether. And if I keep messing up, will my family be disappointed? I can see their faces now, like, “Why aren’t you doing better?” I get it, they care, but their expectations sometimes suffocate me. Ugh. I really need to clean my room. It’s a disaster. Clothes everywhere, my desk is a mess. I should really do that, but the thought of organizing it makes me feel even more anxious. What if I can’t find stuff later? I’ll just keep avoiding it. It’s easier that way, at least for now. And then there’s my health. I think about it a lot. I worry about how much junk I eat or how little I move. I know I should exercise more, it’s on my mind constantly, but when I think about going to the gym, it feels like running a marathon just to get there. I don’t want to deal with all those fit people. What if they stare at me? I guess it’s just this constant cycle of anxiety and discomfort. It’s tiring. I know I should embrace things more, but the thought of it is paralyzing. I don’t know how to break out of it, and it just keeps looping endlessly.

okay so I'm sitting here and I can't stop thinking about how I should've gone to that party last night everyone kept texting me but I just felt so tired like super drained from the week and then I thought about the people that would be there and some of them just annoy me but then I remembered the music that would be playing and the dancing that could've happened like I thrive on that energy but I also kind of hate small talk and people asking me about my classes like ugh as if I want to explain for the hundredth time how I’m struggling with that one class that I only signed up for because my advisor said I should it’s like I can’t escape it and sometimes I just wish I could fast forward a week or two to when I don’t have to deal with all this nonsense and then there are days when I’m walking around campus and I just want to scream at the top of my lungs because it feels like everyone is just staring at me or whispering about me and sometimes I think they might actually be talking about me but then I remind myself that I’m probably just being paranoid because that’s what my brain does it just goes in circles and then I get this rush of anxiety and I can’t even focus on what I’m doing and it’s like I’m just floating through life which is like a weird metaphor but also not weird because I do feel kind of disconnected sometimes it’s funny how I can go from feeling on top of the world to spiraling down into this pit of self-doubt in like seconds and then my friends text again and they’re all excited about the plans they’re making and I want to be part of it but then I overthink whether they actually want me there or if they’re just being polite which is such a weird thought and like I can’t even enjoy myself when I do go out because I’m so preoccupied with what I’m saying and how I’m coming off and I’m just trying too hard to be that outgoing person that everyone expects me to be like if I don’t bring the energy will they still want me around and it’s exhausting sometimes like can’t a person just chill without feeling the need to perform all the time but then I also love meeting new people and hearing their stories especially when they’re passionate about something like that one time I met that artist at some random gallery opening and she had this fire in her eyes when she talked about her work I felt so drawn to her but then I got all shy and overthinked whether I should ask for her social media to stay in touch and I kind of just awkwardly walked away and wasted a good chance to connect because I was too in my own head what is it with my brain always jumping to the worst conclusions it's like hey maybe people actually like me and I don’t have to be so standoffish sometimes but then I’m also not usually one for people-pleasing which is kind of a double-edged sword because while I value honesty and being upfront about things I also sometimes come off as rude without intending to like I don’t want to be hurtful and it’s confusing because sometimes I actually do care about what people think especially friends but then I just snap back into my bubble and it feels safer that way but then that makes me feel lonely and I don’t want to be lonely because I crave connection and yet I can’t make up my mind on how to do that like it feels like I’m a puzzle piece that doesn’t quite fit anywhere and every time I think I found a spot it just turns out the picture is too complicated to navigate I keep thinking about how I want to write a story or something a vivid narrative that just spills out of me but can I even do that when I’m like half-way a mess most of the time then I think back to all the weird bits of life I’ve collected over the years like that time we went camping and the raccoons tried to steal our food or when I accidentally joined that improv group thinking it would help my confidence and I ended up just forgetting my lines and creating this awkward silence and waiting for someone to rescue me but maybe that was good for me in some way you know even if I felt like crawling under a rock at the time but I’ve got to lean into the chaos of it all because that’s where the good stuff lies right like all those emotions swirling around give life the flavor and I just want to live in technicolor I can't shake the feeling that I’m supposed to have it all figured out by now like why does everyone else look so put together and I’m still over here trying to figure out my major and my life goals and then the fear of failure creeps in because what if I make the wrong choice or waste my time on something that doesn’t even fulfill me but then again I’ve always been curious about all these different fields and what if I could combine them into something unique and exciting like a melting pot of ideas or whatever but I just hope I can make it all work without losing sight of what really matters even if that’s constantly evolving it’s just a lot to take in and I wish I could hit pause sometimes and just breathe

I can’t believe they said this paper was due tomorrow. Why does everything feel so last minute? I guess I should start but I really don’t want to. It’s like every time I sit down to write, my mind goes blank. What if I can’t find anything to say? I mean, everyone else seems to have their lives together, and I’m just here procrastinating like it’s a sport. I should probably check my notes again, but I don’t want to. It feels futile. Why can’t I just be one of those people who loves working in groups? Group projects are torture, though. I like to do things my way. How do they expect us to cooperate? I can hear my roommate talking about their weekend plans. Why do people always want to talk about their weekends? It makes me feel anxious. I didn’t even really go out last weekend. I stayed in and watched that documentary about social media. Life is way too chaotic out there in public. I can't deal with all the noise. I think it was a good watch. I just can’t stop thinking about how fake everything is. Why does everybody care so much about the number of likes? I don’t get it. I really don’t. I just want to finish this paper. If I could just focus for one second. I wonder if I should make tea or something. But would that even help? I don’t want to get distracted by tea. But then again, maybe some caffeine could get my brain moving. But what if I spill it? I’ve spilled so much stuff lately, like that coffee stain on my favorite shirt. I really need to keep things tidy. I’m constantly organizing my stuff, but it still feels messy. I can’t stand clutter. I guess I’m just neurotic about it. I counted the books on my shelf yesterday—there are exactly the right number for what I have room for. It’s comforting and infuriating at the same time. Sometimes I just want to scream and let it all out, but who do I even talk to about that? I don’t want to burden anyone with my problems. I feel like I don’t make sense half the time. Why does everyone else seem so carefree? I should probably text my parents, but then they always want to know what’s wrong, and I hate explaining myself. Maybe I should just get a pet. A fish, maybe? Lower maintenance, right? But then what if I forget to feed it and it dies? I wouldn’t be able to handle the guilt. Okay, focus. I really need to write this paper. I keep looking at the clock, and it’s ticking away. It’s just so overwhelming. I can’t think. I just keep imagining what my professor will say. What if they hate my topic?
