Or worse, what if they think I’m incompetent? I can’t even imagine. I need to pick a safe subject, something boring but acceptable. Maybe something about psychology—everyone loves psychology. So predictable. But I can’t help it. I have to be practical about it. Why can’t I just get it together? It’s like my brain is fighting against me. I see my peers typing away, and I’m sitting here frozen. I always feel like I’m going to fail, and that thought is just exhausting. I go through all these cycles in my head, like maybe if I read enough articles, I can piece something together. But then the articles just pile up, and I don’t even know what to do with them. There’s too much information. Then what if I make a mistake? What if I cite something wrong? I have to double-check everything. I can’t help being this way. I just have to make it perfect. That’s the only way it’s going to be okay. Somehow, I’ve convinced myself that if I’m diligent, it’ll all pay off. Ugh, group projects. I got paired up with that one person who never contributes. I can’t even imagine how they can be so careless. Do they not care? Do they not understand the importance of deadlines? We had to pick a topic together, and they suggested something completely off-the-wall, drama-filled nonsense about pop culture. I quickly shut that down. I don’t need that kind of distraction. Talking to them was just draining; it felt like they just wanted to waste time. Anyway, I need to focus on my own work, right? So many thoughts racing, like a hamster on a wheel. Concentrate. Deep breaths. But why do I have to care so much? Why do I feel this pressure when no one else seems affected? I should just take a break, but there’s no time for that. No time and too many overwhelming thoughts to sort through. What if I just zone out and forget everything? I really wish I could just click a button and make all this stress disappear. Why is adulthood so draining? I always thought once I made it to college, things would be easier. Why do I keep comparing myself to others? It’s exhausting.

I just can’t with the noise today, it’s like the world is amplifying everything. So many voices, so many people. I’d prefer a silent library or even my own room, just me and my thoughts. Why does everyone need to group together, laugh, and talk so loud? Maybe that’s part of being low on extraversion—I just don’t get the joy that seems to fill the air. I like solitude, long walks where I can think without interruption. It’s like I’m allergic to socializing, or maybe just hyper-aware of it. I notice every little thing—the way people smile too broadly, or how loud their laughter cuts through my thoughts. It’s like being in a room where the TV blares but you want to focus on the book in your hands. It’s distracting. Every little social interaction feels like climbing a mountain. I prefer my own peaks, the ones I can conquer alone. I remember last week when I had to present in class. The topic was exciting; I chose something about the evolution of technology and how it affects communication. That’s something I can get into. But standing up there, I felt the weight of every gaze on me. My heart raced, and I could feel the sweat building up like a storm cloud. I was prepared, I had everything mapped out, PowerPoint, notes, the whole deal but the moment I began to speak, my mind blanked. I stumbled over words like they were obstacles in my path. Why do I let anxiety win? I mean, I’m conscientious; I prepare, I study, I look at every angle, but when I’m up there, it feels like my thoughts scatter like leaves in the wind. It’s funny; I treasure openness. I love exploring new ideas, absorbing different cultures, learning about obscure theories. I can dive deep into a topic and lose track of time. But then there’s the flip side. My neuroticism kicks in and clouds my enthusiasm. Like every idea I explore has a shadow lurking, what if I’m wrong? What if I embarrass myself? I can’t just enjoy the ride; I’m always thinking several steps ahead, and honestly, it’s exhausting. I wish I could just be carefree about things. I think about how people just go out, have fun, and engage with others so effortlessly. It’s like watching a movie where the characters are free, and I’m just the observer stuck in my cerebral cinema. My low agreeableness makes me question everything, too. Group projects? Don’t even get me started. I tend to clash with people who don’t pull their weight. I can’t help it; I just can’t stand laziness. It irritates me. I want to shake them and say, “Can’t you see this matters?” but I know confrontation is not ideal. So I sit there, seething in silence, taking on more than I should. My conscientious side compels me to push through, but it also means I end up frustrated because I care about the quality of the work. I pour myself into everything I do, which makes it infuriating when others don’t seem to care. I guess I’m also a little harsh on myself because I expect the same level of commitment from myself. If I finish something, it better be perfect or else it’s not worth the effort. Perfectionism is a double-edged sword. As I walk through campus, I can’t help but feel an odd mix of curiosity and discomfort. There’s so much energy around—the undergrads clustered near the fountain, seniors rushing to class like they’re fighting against time, and I... I just slip by. My senses are heightened, and I mentally note every detail. The fragrance of the coffee shop mingling with the scent of grass, the chatter of students debating philosophy like it’s a sport. I want to engage, to jump in and give my perspective, but the walls come up. I feel like I’m in a glass box, watching life happen outside. I want to scream at the box and shatter the glass but then again… what would that change? Just more noise. Then there are the quiet moments that soothe me. Sitting alone in the corner of that coffee shop, sipping my black coffee, tapping on my laptop. It’s a little slice of paradise. I can let my mind wander. Create, think, explore. The outside world fades away. I love it when an idea strikes me like lightning, when I can scribble it down and feel alive again. The rush of creativity, the way the words come together, form something that’s mine. It’s intoxicating. I think about how my life feels like a tightrope sometimes—balancing the need for solitude with the desire to connect. The fear of failure snarling at my heels while I try to walk this line carefully. I want to embrace new experiences, new thoughts, yet I dread the vulnerabilities they bring. I know it’s all part of the journey, but honestly, I wish it didn’t feel so fraught.

Okay so here I am just trying to think of something to say but my mind is racing and I can’t help but think about the party this weekend. It’s gonna be wild. I love parties, actually live for them, the music, the people, the energy. The crowd is like this big vibrant wave and I just want to dive right in, meet everyone, chat for hours like we’re best friends already, but sometimes my anxiety kicks in. I freak out before walking in, what if no one talks to me, what if I spill my drink or say something dumb. Ugh. Those thoughts circle like vultures in my brain, but then I remember how great it feels once I’m there, how everyone just lights up when you join in. I mean, who doesn’t love a good laugh, right? I live for making people laugh. Last time I went to a party, I was on a roll, telling stories, cracking jokes, and everyone was hanging on my words. It felt amazing, but then out of nowhere my phone buzzed and it was a text from my mom asking if I had eaten. Instant stomach drop. I’m like sitting there laughing and partying and then bam! Guilt hits because I know I should have called her earlier and she worries so much. I can’t help but spiral. I start thinking about how I should definitely call her right after the party but then I’m afraid I’ll forget and then she’ll think I’m too busy to care. It messes with my mood a bit, but the moment passes when some random person starts a conversation about a movie I love. It’s like everything resets; we’re exchanging favorite scenes and the energy flows back. Speaking of movies! Oh my god, I wonder if they’ll show that new superhero flick at the cinema soon. I’m obsessed with superhero movies. Everyone says it’s the same premise but I don’t care, give me all the action, the flashy costumes, the ridiculous plots, like who cares?! It’s just fun and I love the feeling of being part of a community when it’s all happening on screen. Comic con is on my bucket list but like, do I really want to spend that much time in costume? I mean, I like dressing up but I’m also kinda lazy. What if I decide at the last minute that I don’t want to go and then I’ve wasted money on a ticket? It’s all got me thinking about how much spontaneity means to me in a way but also how I fear commitment in plans. So weird. And oh man, school is like this whole other realm. I’m in a group project that I dread. I can’t keep track of deadlines. It’s like I’m always drifting. I chat a lot, and I get along with my classmates, but when it comes to planning and being responsible, I just zone out. I mean, who cares about PowerPoint slides when you can literally be chatting about weekend plans or spontaneous hangouts?! But then the group pressures me to stay on task and I feel the weight of that, the dread of letting them down. I want to be the fun person who brings snacks and laughter but also be dependable. It’s exhausting trying to juggle those things. Then I think about my friends and how they’re all in serious relationships and I’m kind of just floating along, dating half-heartedly. I get that rush of meeting new people but commitment is like... I don’t know, it makes me nervous. I want to be there for them, to hear about their lives, their troubles, and I’m all ears but at the same time I don’t know if I should be asking for the same back. Like do I want to drag someone into my chaotic world of thoughts? They might find me too intense or too much, but I feel like that’s where the juice of friendships lies! Sharing the good and bad and okay I’m all about being there for them, but I also don’t want them to see my darker side sometimes. I mean who hasn’t freaked out about their future? Or stressed over whether their outfit makes them look good? Okay focus. Breathe. I have to chill out sometimes. I mean I’m sitting here overwhelmed just thinking about life when I should just be enjoying the moment. I guess it’s all about balance, right? Finding that middle ground between being true to myself, being a good friend, living in the moment, and managing my anxieties. Life’s a rollercoaster and I’m strapped in for the ride whether it’s scary or fun. I need to keep reminding myself that it’s okay to be a little messy, to be a lot messy sometimes. The connections I make, the laughter, the little moments of joy—that’s what really counts.

Okay so I was just thinking about how weird it is that people freak out about trying new things. Like, why not? Life’s too short to just stay in your bubble. I mean, I love meeting new people, the energy is infectious, and the stories they share are just incredible. I had this weird conversation with a guy at the bus stop yesterday. He had the craziest hair; it was like a walking piece of art. Anyway, he started talking about the places he’s been, the food he’s tried. I mean, who even thinks about trying fried crickets? But he swears it’s the best thing he’s ever tasted. I totally decided I’d try it too when I get the chance. Why not? I sometimes think about how much of life is just about experiences, right? Like, I can’t understand why people stick to the same thing every weekend. There’s a whole world to explore! I always want to know what’s behind every door, every corner. I mean, my friend invited me to this pottery class next week; sounds fun, right? I can’t wait to get my hands dirty and make something weird. Maybe I’ll create a vase that looks like a dinosaur. Who knows? Speaking of dinosaurs, I was totally into those as a kid. My room was plastered with posters of T-Rex and Velociraptors. I used to pretend my stuffed animals were on these epic dinosaur adventures. Why did I stop doing that? It’s silly how life pulls us into this serious mode. I wish more adults would embrace their inner child. And then there's the whole thing about being nice. I feel like kindness can really shift the atmosphere in a room. I remember this one time I was at the grocery store and the person in front of me in line was struggling to find change. So, I just said, “It’s all good, I got this.” I paid for their stuff, and honestly, it felt great. It’s like opening a trapdoor to happiness. I don’t know if that’s how it feels for everyone, but there’s something about connecting with others that just lights me up. Also, I can’t stand when people are rude for no reason. Like, there was this time I saw a couple yelling at a barista over a coffee order. It made me cringe. Why? It’s coffee, not a life or death situation. It’s so easy to be nice, but some people just don’t get it. I wonder what happened in their lives that made them like that. Maybe they had a rough morning? Or a bad week? It’s like this domino effect of negativity. If only they could just breathe and be like, “Hey, let’s fix this without drama.” I really think it’s important to take responsibility for our moods and actions. It’s like, if you’re feeling down, don’t project it onto others. Just bring that positive energy. I love when friends call me up and say, “Hey, let’s go out” or “Let’s just talk.” That stuff keeps me grounded. There’s something about being surrounded by people who genuinely care that makes everything feel lighter, you know? Like last weekend, I went to this open mic night, and oh my gosh, the amount of talent in that room! There was this girl who sang her heart out. You could feel her passion; it was contagious. It made me want to get up and sing my own song (which I probably wouldn’t have done, but still!). It’s like everyone was rooting for each other, and that’s beautiful. I think everyone should experience that kind of raw connection. I mean, who says I can’t just jump onto a stage one day? It’s terrifying, but at the same time, it’s exhilarating. Life is about making fools of ourselves sometimes, right? Just the thought of it makes me giddy. I can almost hear the laughter and cheers, even if they’re just imaginary supporters. I find joy in the wildest ideas. Juggling responsibilities is hard, though. I mean, school, social life, work—how do people manage it all? But I’ve found that if I just take it one step at a time, and maybe laugh a bit, it feels more manageable. I try to stay organized but, honestly, it’s the spontaneous moments that make everything feel exciting. Like planning a road trip with friends last minute—that’s where the real memories come from. And food! Can’t forget food. I’m always on the lookout for trendy new restaurants. There’s this one place that serves ramen in pineapples. I mean, who thinks of that? I gotta try it. I’m all about those unique experiences. They open your mind and create a tiny adventure out of the mundane. I hope I never lose that spark. Anyway, I guess the gist of all this is to keep exploring, stay open, and always choose kindness. Life is meant to be fun. Just dive in.

Okay so I was just thinking about how noisy everyone is in group projects and how I can’t stand it. It’s like, no one has their own ideas. They just wanna jump on the bandwagon, bring in all this fluff while I’m trying to think of the best solution or the boldest concept. I like to be lively and animated but sometimes I just wanna shut it down and say enough is enough. It’s exhausting to deal with all this mediocrity. Like, don’t you guys want to break boundaries? Maybe it’s me, maybe I’m the problem but I can’t help but feel that passion within me just bursting. I want to explore everything and talk to everyone but then why do I feel so drained by these interactions? It’s all so frustrating. Last night was wild though. We went out to this new bar that opened up downtown. It’s got that whole vintage vibe, you know? But the drinks were overpriced and the music was too loud. I mean, I love nightlife, the energy is electric and I thrive on it but sometimes I just want to actually have a conversation instead of shouting across the table. So many options to choose from! I’m always game for trying something new. Who wants the same old boring beer when there’s craft cocktails with weird flavors? I ordered some lavender-infused gin or something, tasted like a garden but I loved it. Who even thinks of these concoctions? I need to find more places like that. And then there’s the people. I thought I’d meet some interesting characters there, but most of them were just the same old. Catching glances at the guy in the corner who’s had way too many shots and is trying too hard to flirt with the bartender. Like, dude, chill. Just be yourself. Why do people feel the need to put on a show? I mean, I get it – sometimes I do that too. I can’t help it; I sometimes just wanna be the center of attention. I’m all about the hype but also, I have this anxiety bubbling underneath. What if they don’t like me? What if I fall flat? It’s like, I crave the spotlight but I’m terrified of it at the same time. Such a weird combination. Speaking of weird, have you ever thought about how strange our habits are? Like, I can’t sleep without my favorite blanket. It’s this old, ratty thing but it’s comforting. It’s like a little piece of home that I carry with me. I’ll be all over the place during the day and then BAM – curling up with that blanket feels like a hug. But then my mind leaps to all these ridiculous thoughts in the evening. I can plan my entire future in one night but then wake up feeling like I have no direction. What was that all about? Why do I dream of being a writer one night and then a world traveler the next? It’s like my brain won’t shut up and just choose one thing. I should just go for it all, right? Who says I can’t do everything? But then again, there’s that creeping fear. What if I fail miserably? Sometimes I wish I could just bottle up that enthusiasm I have for life and let it flow out on command. I can’t help but feel like I’m going 100 miles an hour while everyone else is stuck in traffic. Like, can’t we just be spontaneous? We could drive out to the coast for a sunset or hit up that late-night taco truck! But then, maybe I take it too far. I jump to plans that sound awesome and then I’m like, wait, how am I even going to pull this together? The lack of organization kicks in, and it’s like I’m just throwing caution to the wind without a parachute. That’s how I end up regretting decisions or overspending or... whatever. Oh, and the way I analyze everything. I’ll dissect conversations afterward, gnawing on every little interaction like it's a juicy piece of gossip. Did I come off too strong when I said that? What if they think I’m too intense? That thought echoes for days. I mean, people can be so unpredictable. It’s fascinating but also terrifying. I just can’t seem to stop jumping into conversations, almost drowning out the quieter ones who maybe have great ideas. Worrying about that makes me wonder if I even make a real impact or if I’m just noise in the background of life. But here’s the thing. I want to resonate. I want to be remembered, not just as a loud voice but as someone who brings something significant to the table. It’s chaotic, but that’s the thrill. I want it all – the wild, the calm, the curious and the mundane. It's about finding the balance, you know? But, how do I ever achieve that? I guess that’s the game, the constant dance between being too much and just enough. It’s kind of exciting in its own way.

Just got an alert on my phone, a reminder about that study—what’s it about again? Oh right, personality traits. Makes me think about how I fit into this whole picture. Extraversion always seems so daunting. I mean, there’s people who thrive in social settings like, I don’t know, they just seem to pulse with energy, and here I am often just wanting to melt into the background. High neuroticism, though—yeah, totally understand that. I’m the one who’s worrying about the tiniest details that most people just brush off. Did I send that email? Did I forget to attach the document? Anxiety is like a little shadow that follows me around, making me second-guess everything. Agreeableness, though, that’s a strange one. I find myself bending over backwards to keep everyone happy which sounds noble but can feel so draining. Why do I avoid conflict? It’s like a reflex. I just hate the thought of someone being upset with me—it’s paralyzing, really. And I can’t seem to say no. Been meaning to exercise that no muscle but when someone asks, “Hey can you help me with this?” Even if I have a billion things on my plate, I’m like, “Sure, no problem!” And then I start to feel resentful once I’m knee-deep in it, which is so backward. Low conscientiousness—ugh, my bedroom is a disaster zone. Clothes everywhere, half-finished assignments just hanging around, it’s a miracle I can find anything. I tell myself I’ll get organized, maybe tomorrow. But the idea of putting my stuff in order feels like climbing a mountain. Open-mindedness is my saving grace, I guess. I love exploring new ideas, different perspectives, and just getting lost in thought. People find me odd sometimes because I can jump from talking about existentialism to the merits of pizza toppings in under five minutes. I have no problem with that. It’s kind of liberating, really. Kind of makes me forget about the mundane worries if I’m diving into a philosophical rabbit hole or obsessing over the newest Netflix documentary. I could binge-watch documentaries for days. It’s like exploring the world without leaving my couch. But then again, I tend to get overwhelmed by all this information. I’m always thinking about how everything connects. Is that why I struggle with focus? My mind feels like a room full of TVs all playing different shows at once. Then there’s that looming fear of missing out, seeing my friends hanging out without me. It’s like a punch to the gut every time, but somehow I convince myself I’d rather stay in with my thoughts and snacks. There’s comfort in solitude, I guess. But is it loneliness? I ponder that a lot. I scroll through social media, watching people live their lives in high definition, and I feel like a ghost sometimes. Just hovering around, wanting to join but never quite feeling brave enough. There’s always that voice in my head saying, “What if they don’t want you there?” So, I end up being the friend who’s always understanding, always supportive but never the one who initiates plans. It’s a trade-off I suppose. People like my calm nature, I’m like the emotional support animal in human form. But I can’t help but feel like I’m missing out on something. There are days I think about just tearing out the fear and throwing caution to the wind, just saying, “Hey, let’s all go to the beach!” Like it’s that easy, right? But then the reality hits; what if nobody wants to go? Or worse, what if they do and I’m the one dragging the mood down with my worries? My mind feels trapped, but the thoughts keep flowing. Yesterday, I saw a post about the beauty of vulnerability. It spoke to me. There’s a unique strength in being open, but the risk of being judged keeps me modest. How do people do it? Be so carefree and loud and unapologetically themselves? I wish I could tap into that. Sometimes I think I’m an enigma, even to myself, like a puzzle that never quite fits together. I crave connection yet shy away from it. It’s exhausting, trying to reconcile these two sides. I want to explore everything—arts, music, philosophy—but at the same time, I struggle to even start. I see so many paths but get stuck paralyzed by the choice. If I could just make a leap, maybe dive into something new without overthinking, I could be anyone, right? But here I am, clinging to my safe space, hoping for change while fearing it all at once. And so it loops on, this constant dance of desire and dread, as if life itself is one giant awkward social gathering, and I’m stuck in the corner, watching and wondering when I’ll finally join in.

okay so it’s funny how my brain is like a torn up road map that nobody wants to follow but somehow I still drive along these routes filled with potholes and dead ends. like I was thinking about that new cafe downtown and how everyone keeps posting about it on social media and it’s supposed to have the best espresso I guess but I can’t even think of going there alone cause what if I sit there and just sip my coffee while scrolling mindlessly through my phone and everyone around me is laughing and having fun it feels like being an outsider watching a movie from the backseat of a car. sometimes I think I’d rather just stay at home with my cat Luna who’s probably plotting to kill something right now or at least keep me company while I spiral into my thoughts about how then I have to figure out what to eat later and what if I run out of food and my fridge looks like a desolate wasteland yet I have no motivation to go grocery shopping ugh. I mean when I do go out it’s like a massive effort just to put on pants unless I’m heading to a class where people are going to judge me based on my crumpled notes or the fact that I didn’t even bother to highlight anything I’m so disorganized it’s almost impressive how I can lose my train of thought while writing a paper or reading an article half the time I’m just like where was I going with this and it’s only three paragraphs in. the other day I flipped through my notes just to find out I wrote a bunch of random thoughts instead of anything useful like "what is the meaning of life?" and "why do I keep eating cereal for dinner?" which is weird but it’s comforting that crunch of the cereal or maybe it’s just the milk. I should cook more. I think about making those fancy dishes I see on Instagram but then I see the mess involved and I just ditch the idea because cooking requires this level of execution that I can never seem to muster and then I’m left looking at a microwave dinner instead which feels like a betrayal to my culinary aspirations. but hey, sometimes the microwave is just too easy so I guess there’s that. and then there’s the matter of friendships. I have a few close ones and I find them exhausting sometimes but also I know they’re important right? but when they invite me to stuff I’m like what if I say the wrong thing or make it awkward? I can’t keep up with small talk. did I even send that text from last week? oh my god did I ghost them? I’m always second-guessing my social skills like this weird little bug in my brain that won’t shut up and I wish I could just let it go but the what ifs are so loud. it’s frustrating because I want to be more laid back but then there’s always this tension like someone might call me out for being too weird or not caring enough which leads me to overthink my responses and maybe I just shouldn’t say anything at all. every time I walk through campus, I feel like a ghost passing through a crowd of real people who are enjoying their lives like all these bright colors and I’m in black and white like I’m part of the background noise the hum of activity mixes with my own anxiety and I can’t even enjoy the campus art installations because my mind is racing with thoughts like do people like what they’re doing? do they see me? some days I just want to be invisible like a shadow slipping through the cracks of it all. but then again, I have this wild imaginative side that wants to explore every single idea possible and I get fixated on things like the thought of traveling to those random countries nobody talks about or spending weeks backpacking with just a notebook and a pen and just writing down every crazy thought that crosses my mind. that part of me wants to break free from this mundane cycle but then the other part of me is like "okay but what if you get lost?" so there’s this constant tug-of-war between my desire for adventure and the fear of stepping out of my comfort zone. I mean what even is a comfort zone, right? sometimes I think it’s like a cage made of soft pillows that I can’t seem to escape. I could sit in my room writing poetry or sketches of ideas for stories but probably not put any of it out because the fear of rejection looms like a dark cloud overhead. so I just let them stack up in my mind and maybe one day I’ll burst and just throw them out there but until that day comes, I’ll just be here worrying about whether I locked the door before I left and if I should really watch that horror movie I heard about. too many options and too many thoughts and not enough space in my head for all these tangents to actually form something coherent. sometimes I want to just hit reset on my brain or maybe just get a good night’s sleep but then I’m also afraid of what I might dream about because then that just opens up another can of worms.

Okay so I just remembered that time my roommate and I tried to cook together and it was a total disaster. I mean, we had this whole plan mapped out, like we were going to make tacos from scratch, and I was so excited because I love cooking with friends, it’s all about the teamwork, right? But then, halfway through, she realized we didn’t have enough tortillas, which is kind of a big deal when you’re making tacos. Like, what are we supposed to do without tortillas? Just eat some ground beef and cheese? Gross. I felt this wave of panic wash over me because I hate disappointing people, especially when they get excited about something. So I suggested we just go to the store, but it was like 10 pm, and I could feel my heart racing thinking of how it was getting late and I had this weird anxiety about running into someone we knew. Social anxiety is such a funny beast, right? I thrive on being around people but sometimes the thought of just bumping into someone from high school sends me into a spiral. What would I say? How would I look? I mean, I just wanted to make tacos and hang out. So anyway, we decided to take the chance and go to the store. All the while, I could tell my roommate was getting stressed too because she kept checking her phone like every five seconds. I could feel myself getting a little twitchy because I just wanted everything to go smoothly. We got to the store, and it was practically deserted. I felt the relief of not running into anyone I knew, but then immediately felt bad for the staff who were clearly trying to close up. I wanted to say something encouraging to them, you know like, “Hey, you’re doing great!” But it felt awkward and out of place. I’m always on the lookout for making sure other people feel good, so when I don’t say anything I feel this heaviness. We grabbed the tortillas and then we saw this really amazing salsa that looked homemade and I got super excited and was like, “We NEED this!” and my roommate looked at me like I was crazy, but I just can't help it when things spark joy. So we ended up getting the salsa and also some chips for good measure. Which is kind of funny because now I’m thinking how I just couldn’t resist that impulse. Control issues or something? I have to keep everything in line, but when I see something cool, I just go for it. Later we got back to the apartment, and we made the tacos, and oh my goodness, they turned out so good. Like, I was feeling so proud of us and somehow it felt like we had conquered the world. After we ate, we ended up talking about stuff, and I remember sharing some personal stuff I hadn’t even thought about. I think with her, it just felt easy to open up. That’s really important to me—having those conversations. I need connection on a deep level, but sometimes it’s like my emotions kind of get the better of me, and I get anxious about what I say. Like, was it too much? Do I sound needy? I can never tell and that makes me feel awkward, but she seemed to appreciate it and that made me feel relieved. But then she mentioned something about how she felt overwhelmed with classwork and I got anxious again because I'm always worried about being too needy or too much for people, if I’m going to pile on my stuff on top of theirs, and then all of a sudden it’s just a mess and nobody has fun. It’s exhausting trying to keep this balance, you know? So I just started asking her questions about school, how she was handling it, but I think I get overly concerned about making sure she's okay, maybe to a fault. I guess I want to be a good friend and I sometimes go overboard. So we were sitting there, and I was getting anxious about how late it was getting, like I wanted to keep talking, but I also was thinking about all the stuff I had to do tomorrow. It’s like I can’t turn off my brain, it just goes and goes and all the while I’m trying to focus on the moment. I think that’s why I tend to overplan everything—I want to make sure I’m getting stuff done, and I just feel a little bit fidgety if I don’t. But that night was really nice, even with all that swirling around in my head. I just wish sometimes I could find that perfect balance where I feel good being myself without all that worry. So now it’s late, and there’s this mix of satisfaction about the tacos and anxiety about tomorrow, as I sit here thinking I should probably get to bed but I kind of just want to keep chatting with her. Life is weird that way. It’s all these little moments strung together and I just hope I make the most of them.

I still can’t believe I forgot to send that email. I guess I just didn’t care enough, but then again, what’s the point? Emails pile up anyway. Stressing over them feels pointless. People ask for responses like I owe them something. I don’t owe anyone anything. Just because they need my input doesn’t mean I have to give it, right? But then I worry they’ll think I’m rude or that I’m not doing my part, whatever that even means. This weird pressure from society to be responsible, to contribute, to be lovable is exhausting. Maybe I’m a bad person for being this way. What is “bad” anyway? It’s like I’m in a maze of expectations I can’t escape. I wonder if everyone else feels like this or if they’re just acting. Sometimes I feel like a spectator watching everyone be social and happy, like it’s a show I don’t understand. I think about my last group project. I didn’t contribute much, but honestly, the whole setup felt chaotic. They kept trying to force ideas on me, and I was like, no, let’s talk about something we really care about instead of these boring templates. My ideas felt too big, too weird for them. I have this wild imagination, but it doesn’t always translate well into small talk or team dynamics. I don’t know. Meeting people doesn’t feel natural. I can’t stand the awkward pauses. It’s like a silent countdown to uncomfortable small talk. Why do people feel the need to fill every second with noise? Silence is okay! I think silence has its own rhythm, but most people act like it’s a bad thing. I can appreciate it, but others just fidget or look away like they’re waiting for a lifeboat or something. And then there’s the whole idea of “fitting in.” I don’t want to fit in; I want to be unapologetically weird, but then there’s this constant nagging worry that comes creeping in, asking if I’m even being accepted anywhere. It’s like my mind is a pendulum swinging between being super open to new ideas and experiences, which is cool, but I can’t help but feel anxious about how I’ll be perceived while doing it. I want to explore everything and push boundaries, but the anxiety ties me down. I’m often lost in thoughts about how others see me, toxic comparisons that spiral into endless loops of doubt and self-criticism. I think about friendships and how hard they are, like what does it mean to be a friend anyway? My idea of friendship seems to clash with the reality of what’s out there. They want brunch dates and movie nights. I would rather discuss the meaning of life over a chaotic storm of thoughts. I want to dive deep into the bizarre and unexplored realms of conversation. But then, I’m met with blank stares or polite nods. It’s like I’m throwing pebbles into a deep well, waiting for a splash, but all I get is silence echoing back. I guess that’s probably too much for most people. I think maybe I bring out some uncomfortable vibes, and that makes things harder. Why does everyone else seem to be doing fine? How can they just go out and socialize without succumbing to this gnawing anxiety? It’s baffling. It’s like I’m on a different wavelength. The pressure to act normal, to smile and nod even when I can feel those anxious thoughts bubbling under the surface—ugh, it’s exhausting. The world sometimes feels so disconnected from how I experience it. I want to see everything in vivid colors, and yet, I feel trapped in black-and-white. I’m drawn to exploration but terrified of judgment. I remember that time I tried to join a club. I thought it would be a fresh start, but as soon as I walked in, I froze. Everyone knew each other and was locked in their own world of inside jokes. I felt awkward just standing there. I should’ve left, but that felt worse. Why couldn’t I find common ground? There’s this heavy weight of social anxiety that clings to me. The conversations feel like a tidal wave, and I’m just bobbing on the surface, trying not to drown. And then on the flip side, I crave experiences that push boundaries—not the mundane kind, things that make me question everything, ideas that challenge all I thought I knew, but how do you get there when you can’t even connect with the people around you? How do I share my world with anyone when I can barely share a casual joke? It’s like I’m on this rollercoaster of emotions, all highs and lows, never a flat ride. I want to explore, yet I feel too fragile to dive into those unknowns. It’s complicated. So many thoughts swirling, but they don’t seem to connect. God, sometimes I just wish it all felt a little easier.

It's that weird feeling when your phone buzzes and you think it's a text from someone but then it's just a notification. Like, why do I even keep my phone on? I mean, I don’t really want to talk to anyone but I also want to feel connected, I guess? Social media is exhausting. I scroll through and see everyone's lives looking so bright and perfect while mine is just, well, whatever it is. Sometimes I think about posting something but then I chicken out because what if it seems boring? I don't want to bother anyone with my mundane thoughts. I feel like everyone's always got something interesting to say and I'm just like, "Hey, check out this sandwich I made." Who cares, right? It’s not even that great, just two pieces of bread and, like, some cheese. I keep thinking how I should go to the grocery store because I’m almost out of bread, but that means interacting with people and ugh, the small talk. "How's your day?" "Good, how's yours?" Just feels so fake sometimes. And what if someone wants to have a longer conversation? What if they ask about my classes? I don’t want to talk about that, I don’t even know how I’m doing most of the time. Grades are probably fine, but it’s the feeling of waiting for an email that might ruin everything. I might just wear sunglasses and avoid eye contact. Then again, what if I run into someone I know? So awkward. I guess I could just order groceries online but that means I have to think about what I even want to eat. Ugh. The decision fatigue gets real. I remember last week when I tried to cook something new. A disaster, honestly. I totally messed up the recipe, not that I really followed it anyway. Shoulda measured things but I eyeball everything and it usually ends up as a mess. Kitchen smells weird and I can hear the neighbors laughing. Probably at something funny on TV or something. I just sat there, stirring this strange concoction, hoping it would taste better than it looked. It didn’t. I ended up making eggs instead because at least I can't mess that up too badly. I miss my mom’s cooking. She always knew how to make everything right. Whenever I try, I just get a bit sad thinking about how she would do it so much better. Sometimes I wonder if I should reach out to her. It's been a while. But then I feel annoying for bothering her. She has her own life, right? I just start thinking about how I might say something and then I think “What if she doesn’t respond” and then “What if I ruin her day?” She probably doesn’t need my anxieties, who does? It's easier to keep everything bottled up because sometimes it feels like just existing is such a chore. I tried to tell a friend about how I feel sometimes but then it turned into her sharing her problems and I felt bad for making it all about me. So I just nodded and smiled, offered some generic advice like “Maybe you should talk to someone?” like I'm the expert when I can barely talk myself. I don’t want to sound whiny or needy. It’s just that feeling like I’m too much is always there, creeping in. I guess that’s why I prefer being alone; it’s much less pressure. I keep thinking about how I have a paper due soon but the idea of starting it is overwhelming. It’s just a paper on a book I read months ago, but the thought of piecing my thoughts together like I’m some master writer gives me anxiety. What if my ideas don’t make sense? What if I don’t have enough references? The clock is ticking and I'm sitting here thinking about how I should be writing instead of rambling about food and feelings. A part of me feels like I can’t keep up with the world. Everyone seems so put together and I’m just here, wondering when I’ll get it all together, if I'll ever get it together. I think about my future a lot—what if I end up in a job I hate? Can I even handle office politics? I’d probably just sit in the corner, drowning in my thoughts, avoiding group lunches. I’d miss out on the bonding and feel left out. Should I be more adventurous? But what if I don’t like adventure? Anxiety prompts weigh heavy, and it’s cyclical. It’s hard to break free. How do people just take chances? Who do I even want to be?

Okay so I just remembered that time I told my roommate I’d wake her up and I totally forgot. It was like 7 a.m. and I had this wild dream about a talking cat, and when I woke up, I was like, “Wait, did I really promise to wake her up for that 8 a.m. class?” It’s weird how my brain just forgets things when it’s overloaded with all this stuff, but I can remember random details like the cat’s name was Sir Fluffington. It’s always the little things, right? Anyway, she was so mad at me. I hate disappointing people but sometimes I just get so wrapped up in my head and there’s so much noise, and I guess I can’t always be the best roommate. Ugh, I feel that familiar pit in my stomach. Maybe I should’ve left a sticky note. Why don’t I ever think of simple solutions? Speaking of solutions, I need to get my laundry done. My closet looks like a war zone, and I can’t even find anything. I swear I’m drowning in clothes but can’t let go. Why is it so hard to throw away that old band tee? I mean, yeah, I haven’t worn it in five years but it reminds me of that concert, all that energy and adrenaline. People say to let go of things but how do you let go of memories? I could just toss it, but that would feel like losing a piece of my past, and it’s all jumbled up in my head. Sometimes I think about how much emotional baggage we carry without even realizing it. Like, there’s no weight limit on this metaphorical baggage. But then again, there are days where I just want to throw caution to the wind and go on an adventure, like jumping into a road trip with friends at 2 a.m. Just spontaneous stuff, you know? I love when my friends are down for anything. It makes me feel alive. The thrill of the unknown and just not knowing where we’ll end up. It’s like a burst of excitement, but then there’s always this underlying anxiety. The “what ifs” start creeping in. What if the car breaks down? What if we run out of gas in the middle of nowhere? It’s maddening, but that’s me, I guess. Tension and excitement all mixed up. I feel like I’m on this rollercoaster all the time. And man, my brain really likes to run. I can’t just sit still. Even when I’m hanging out with friends, I feel the need to keep the conversation going. Otherwise, it gets awkward, and I can’t stand that. I’ll talk about the most random things, like how many jellybeans are in a jar or the latest Netflix show, just to fill the silence. It’s exhausting sometimes. Not everyone can keep up with that energy. Maybe it makes me hard to deal with. My friends tell me it’s endearing, but deep down, I worry they’re just saying that to be nice. I have to keep the vibe positive, but then I feel this pressure to make them happy and I don’t want to push them away. It’s a delicate balance. Okay, but like, what’s with all these new diet fads? I’m not even interested, but then suddenly everyone’s pushing keto or no-carb or whatever. I just want to eat what makes me feel good, honestly. I love food. The experiences around food are everything. I hate that stress I sometimes feel about my own meal choices. I’ll scroll through TikTok and feel so overwhelmed by trends. And then there are people who look flawless and they make it seem so easy, like they don’t have existential crises after eating a slice of pizza. Why can’t I just chill? And then there’s all this pressure to perform academically. I hate that it sometimes makes me feel like I can’t be myself. I should be studying but instead I’m pacing, talking to myself about what I need to do but also thinking about how everything is going to fall apart if I don’t do things perfectly, which I know is irrational but it still gets to me. I keep getting reminded I have to get a good job after graduation, and it’s like I’m just a cog in the machine. I mean, I want to enjoy my life, not just check off boxes. It’s all so conflicting. I should probably take a deep breath or something. I wonder if anyone else feels this way. Or maybe I’m just spiraling. I need to reach out to someone. But who? I don’t want to bother them or sound like a drag. It’s hard to find that balance. It’s a lot, really. Sometimes I just want to shout, “Can we all just be real!” but then I worry people will think I’m too much. Why is life so full of contradictions? I think I’ll text my best friend and see if she’s free to grab coffee later. Maybe just talking it out will help. It usually does.

I just realized I forgot to ask my roommate to pick up the laundry again. This is getting ridiculous. I mean, how many times can I forget? It's like I have this mental block against it. Why am I even in this shared living situation? I hate dealing with the noise, the clutter, just everything. I get so overwhelmed with the constant presence of others. Sometimes I wish I could just disappear into my room and not have to deal with anyone. People are exhausting. They want to talk about their day, their dreams, their plans. I can’t even keep up with small talk. It feels like a chore. Why can’t people just read the room? You know? I just want to sit in silence. Silence is golden, but no one appreciates it. They think I’m weird for not wanting to engage. I just can’t muster the energy. I’d rather scroll through my phone than listen to boring stories about other people's lives. Maybe I should get a sign that says “please don’t talk to me.” I don’t even know how I ended up in this study. They say that understanding yourself is key, but honestly, who even cares? Self-discovery sounds so cliché. Just another buzzword on a wall poster in some self-help seminar I’d never attend. I remember back in high school when everyone was so excited about prom or whatever. I just wanted to stay home and play video games. It was like I was missing something fundamental that everyone else had. I didn’t get the appeal of dressing up and dancing with people I barely knew. My friends would pressure me to go, and I always backed out at the last minute. I never understood why they all wanted to go through that stress. I mean, the anxiety of even thinking about it made my stomach twist. I’d rather binge-watch a show, preferably something dark and twisted. People love their feel-good movies, and I’m just sitting here thinking, “yeah, no thanks.” I find comfort in solitude. It’s where I can think, where I can breathe. I feel like in a crowd, all eyes are on me, judging me. They don't know what it's like to feel that pressure. I am acutely aware of every little thing. I remember that one time I accidentally spilled my drink at a gathering, everyone laughed, and I turned crimson. It’s like a spotlight shining right on my face highlighting my flaws. Who wants to be the center of attention? Not me. I don’t do well in those situations. I feel out of place. Like a misplaced puzzle piece, just awkwardly jamming myself in with a bunch of other pieces that fit perfectly together, while I just don’t. Can we talk about anxiety for a second? It’s insidious and debilitating. Sometimes I wake up in the middle of the night just thinking about random things I didn’t do right. Like, did I even finish that assignment? Did I forget something important again? It’s exhausting. I feel like I’m chasing my tail. I should be doing laundry but instead I’m just lying in bed, staring at the ceiling like it holds answers to my endless questions. I can almost hear my brain ticking like a clock, counting down to the next thing I didn’t do. Yet here I am, wasting time doing nothing. Procrastination is my middle name, but it’s not just that. It's like the weight of the world sits on my shoulders, and I can’t get anything done. But how can anyone focus in this environment? There’s always something happening around me, random noises, conversations, laughter. It makes my skin crawl. I want to slam my door and shut it all out. But then the guilt sets in. I feel bad for wanting to isolate. What if they think I hate them? But I don’t hate them. I just need space. I need time to recharge. I can’t pull myself out of my head. It’s a never-ending cycle of self-doubt. Oh, and how about group projects? Ugh, don’t even get me started on that. Honestly, they should be illegal. I can’t stand working with people who don’t care or don’t pull their weight. It just gives me even more anxiety. And what if we have different ideas? I can’t even voice my thoughts without feeling like they’ll be shot down. Why can’t everybody just work alone? There’s a certain peace in solitude that I crave. Okay, I’m rambling at this point. Why does it feel so cathartic to vent? I don’t even know if this study will help me get anywhere. But here I am, spilling it all out. Maybe writing it down makes it less real. Maybe I’ll delete it later. Or maybe it’ll just linger in my mind and continue to eat at me. Whatever. At least it’s out — for now.

okay so I’m just sitting here thinking about how chaotic my room is again I mean I just can’t deal with it at all like why do I have so many clothes on the floor and dishes piled up in the corner it’s not like I meant for it to get this bad but every time I think about cleaning I just get overwhelmed and it feels pointless like what’s the point of even trying when I know I’m just going to mess it up again and then there’s that one shirt that I really like but I can’t find it because it’s buried under everything and I really don’t want to dig through that mess to find it makes me anxious just thinking about it I wish I could just like snap my fingers and it would be clean but that’s not how it works and I keep avoiding it which makes me feel worse and now I’m in this cycle of avoidance and anxiety and ugh just thinking about it makes my heart race. Then there's that group project I have and honestly the thought of it is just making me more stressed because I don’t want to rely on anyone else and I don’t think they’ll pull their weight anyway like I know that sounds bad but it’s just how I feel and I don’t trust people like that I’d rather do it all myself and just get it over with but then that brings up the time management issue because I can’t even manage my time right now and I have all these deadlines creeping up on me and it’s suffocating like why is being a student so hard and especially when it feels like everyone else has it together and I’m just here struggling to keep my head above water. It’s like they don’t even see what I’m dealing with or maybe they do but they just don’t care and it makes me feel so isolated and I hate that feeling because then I start doubting myself and my abilities like am I even cut out for this program or should I just give up and do something else but if I do that then what? Maybe I should just lock myself in a room and study all day but then I’d probably just spiral further into this hole of anxiety and now I’m thinking of worst-case scenarios which doesn’t help at all and I know I should probably be more positive but I just can’t seem to find that part of me that wants to look on the bright side like what is there even to be bright about right now. And relationships oh god don’t even get me started on that I have like zero social life right now and part of me is okay with that because I just can’t handle the stress of trying to maintain friendships when I can barely keep up with my own life and yet there’s this nagging feeling that I should be out there meeting people but going out makes me feel so anxious like what if I say something stupid or what if they don’t like me and I can’t stand the thought of being rejected or judged and it send me spiraling again like maybe I’m just better off alone but then I think about how lonely that is and I don’t want to be lonely but I also don’t want to deal with the complications of social interactions. And then there’s that weird existential dread creeping in again like what am I even doing with my life am I on the right path or am I just wasting time and I don’t even know what I want to do after college like everyone else has their lives figured out but I’m just floating around with no direction and it’s frustrating and I just want to scream because there’s so much pressure to have everything sorted out by a certain age but who even decides that anyway and why do I have to conform to this timeline that doesn’t even make sense for me it’s all just so overwhelming. Sometimes I think I should just take a break but taking a break makes me feel guilty because if I’m not working on something then what’s the point and I don’t give myself permission to rest which isn’t healthy but I can’t make myself stop because that little voice in my head is always nagging me like you’re lazy you should be doing something productive and it just doesn’t shut up and I hate it. I can’t even focus on the things I enjoy because I’m too busy stressing out about everything else and it feels like it’s a cycle that I can’t get out of and I think maybe I should talk to someone about it but that thought freaks me out even more like what would I even say and would they even understand and it’s just easier to avoid it and shove it down but then it builds up and I feel even worse and I don’t know why I can’t just let myself feel things without getting overwhelmed. Going in circles it’s like I’m stuck in this hamster wheel of anxiety and frustration and I just want to break free and figure things out but everything feels hard and messy and I don’t know how to make it okay and it’s exhausting just thinking about it all I wish I could just pause for a moment and breathe but even that feels like a challenge lately.

okay so I can’t believe how fast this semester is flying by like where did all the time go it feels like just yesterday I was moving into the dorms meeting everyone and now we’re halfway through the term already I really love my roommate she’s super chill and we always end up talking late into the night about everything and nothing I wonder if that’s normal like do other people bond that way over silly stuff like sharing snack hacks or swapping playlists I should invite her to join me for a study group next week it’s always better with friends I’ve been thinking about that a lot like how studying alone can feel productive but there’s something so energizing about collaborating with others everyone brings something different to the table plus I love hearing people’s takes on the same material we can laugh it off when one of us gets confused or stumbles on a concept it makes the whole thing feel less daunting and I really just hate the idea of being stressed out too much it’s so draining like I can already feel myself getting a little anxious about finals creeping up even though I know I’m totally prepared I just want to keep the vibe positive and help others stay focused too when I notice someone struggling in class I always want to offer a hand or a listen it’s just how I am sometimes I think it’s almost like I can sense it in the air when someone’s off it’s wild how connected we all are in that way like last week in econ I noticed Jenna zoning out and I nudged her and asked if she was okay she smiled and said she was just really overwhelmed but we ended up chatting after class about study tips and it helped her so much I felt good about it too like when you can help a friend or even just anyone it brings this rush of happiness like the tiny victories matter so much okay but what’s the deal with group projects though they can be a real nightmare I mean why do they always end up with one person doing all the work and the others just riding along I’ve been in both situations and it never feels good either way like being the one carrying the team but also being the one who feels guilty when you don’t pull your weight like I get it sometimes life gets busy but c’mon communicate people I personally prefer to keep things organized I think it’s the conscientiousness in me coming through I make lists and schedules for everything I love having a plan it’s comforting and helps me keep track of deadlines and all those assignments that just seem to pile up out of nowhere I mean sometimes I wonder how professors expect us to manage like five papers, two presentations, and a midterm all in the same week who has that kind of time no one I know I need to start working on my time management skills though I feel like I’m pretty good at it but there’s always room for improvement right maybe I could create a calendar that visually blocks out time for study sessions and breaks I heard that’s super effective I want to keep my grades up and really show my professors I can handle this but also I want to enjoy college like I don’t want to be the person who’s always stressed out and studying in the library when everyone else is out having fun so I try to balance it like catch up with friends, go to parties, but still hit the books and I feel like it’s worth it I thrive in social settings like they fill me with energy I swear I could spend hours talking with people and I usually end up meeting someone new every time I go out even if it’s just to grab a bite or hang out between classes one time at the dining hall I struck up a convo with this girl from my chemistry class and we ended up becoming study partners that was super random but also really cool like I love how easy it is to make connections with others but sometimes I wonder if I give off too much of a “hey I’m your new best friend” vibe it’s all genuine though I just enjoy being around people and sharing experiences but then there are moments when I feel like I should tone it down like when I’m in a more serious class and everyone seems focused and I’m just over here smiling and trying to make jokes and I think it’s because I’m so agreeable too like I want people to feel comfortable and happy but maybe I need to learn when to dial it back a bit so there’s that balance I guess it’s like I have this desire to lift others up but I also need to read the room more effectively it’s definitely something I’ve been reflecting on it’s important to recognize when a situation calls for seriousness instead of just trying to keep it light and fun but in my heart I want everyone to feel good and if I can help even just a little I’ll always jump in to do that oh and this weekend I’m going to that concert with my friends I’m super excited about it it’s going to be packed and the energy in the crowd is going to be insane can’t wait!

I can’t find my keys again. Why is it that I always lose things? I swear they just vanish into thin air. There’s probably a black hole in my room sucking in all my important stuff. Sometimes I think about how messy my space is, and then I don’t do anything about it. I should really try to clean it up because it clutters my mind but cleaning sounds so boring. I’m good at not caring, but then it builds up and I feel this pressure like I’m drowning in my own stuff. Do other people feel this too or is it just me? The chaos of my mind blends with the disorder of my surroundings. Ugh, I was supposed to read that article for class but I got sidetracked on the internet. I waste so much time. Maybe I should put my phone in a different room when I’m trying to focus. But then what if I miss something important? I think I get more anxious about missing out than about actually doing the work. Is that a normal thing? I mean, I know I don't really care about small talk, just the big ideas. I mean, I love ideas, they’re my thing, but follow-through? Can't do it. I’m not even sure what I’m saying right now. I remember this time last year I was so stressed about exams and I kept checking my grades. It was this awful cycle—stress, anxiety, checking, realizing I hadn’t studied enough, panic, then scrabbling to catch up. I should have taken a break but breaks make me feel worse because I know I should be working. It’s a lose-lose situation, really. Maybe I should just accept that I’m not going to be that perfect student who has a neat planner and checks everything off. I mean, planners are for people who can stick to a plan. Thinking about the future makes my heart race. I have no idea what I want. Everyone else seems to know. I just float along, trying to catch things that sound interesting. Like, I took that random philosophy class because I thought it would be fun, but then I got swamped with essays and I’m like why did I do this to myself? I should be more responsible. And yet, I’m drawn to this class on existentialism. I love the question of life’s meaning or the lack thereof. Do we even have a purpose? I could talk about that forever but I don’t want to talk to people. Talking to people is draining. Sometimes I feel like I need to pull away from the world and just breathe, but then I get anxious because I feel like I’m not making connections. Ah, I hate how interpersonal relationships feel like a chore. I mean, I like deep conversations but small talk? Not interested. If I have to hear another “What do you think about the weather?” I might scream. Just get to the good stuff, please! I want to dive in, but people seem to prefer the shallow end. It's like swimming with weights on. I wish I could be more like my friends who can just chat about nothing and feel fine about it. I just want to rant about everything that’s wrong with the world at least once a week, but can I get that? No. People want to talk about weekend plans, which is boring. I’d rather be discussing the implications of social behavior in response to trauma. That’s more interesting, right? Speaking of trauma, my mind just spirals into all the things I worry about. What if I can’t find a job? What if I disappoint everyone? What if I’m forever stuck in this cycle of procrastination and doom? I hate feeling inadequate, but every time I read something brilliant, I feel like I’m not even in the same league. It’s infuriating because I know I’ve got potential. Why can’t I just use it? Maybe I should take a break from social media. It’s this constant barrage of everyone else’s successes, and it makes me feel worse about my own. Can’t they tone it down? Success shouldn’t feel like a competition, but sometimes it does. And then I just end up comparing myself even when I know it’s not fair. It’s like standing outside a lavish party, knowing you weren’t invited but still feeling the need to peek in. There’s always this nagging thought in the back of my head; am I missing out or am I avoiding something that’s not meant for me? Because honestly, the more I think about social situations, the more I’m convinced they’re incredibly overrated. Anyway, I’ll find those keys eventually. They’re probably under a pile of laundry or something. And I’ll get through this month, and the next, and the one after that. That’s all that matters. I think.

Okay so I just got out of class and I’m thinking about how many people I talked to today. Like, every time I walk in, there’s someone waving. I love that feeling. It’s like, I get a little boost, you know? I can’t help but smile and wave back. Oh, and there’s that thing with the group project. Everyone seems to be pulling their weight, which I totally appreciate. I’m really good at keeping things organized, sending out reminders, making sure everyone knows what they need to do. That feels satisfying. But sometimes, it’s just a little too chaotic. Like, I thrive on that structure. I think a lot about deadlines, and how if we just keep our communication open, we can avoid all the “I thought you were doing this” drama. I hate that. Let’s just stick to the plan and keep it moving, right? I don’t mind being the one who coordinates everything. I actually prefer it. And then there’s the social aspect of it. I wonder if I talk too much. People seem to enjoy my stories but sometimes I think, am I monopolizing the conversation? In the dining hall yesterday, I was recounting that trip to the beach last summer, how we found that hidden cove. Everyone was laughing and leaning in, asking questions, that energy is just electric. I love it! But I always have to check myself. I think it’s cool how I can turn any mundane day into a great anecdote. Last week, I went bowling with some friends, and I was so competitive. I mean, I get excited about stuff like that. Not that I take it seriously, but it’s fun to cheer for each other. That’s the best part about being around people. Encouraging them, seeing them light up. It’s interesting because even though I can be a bit controlling when it comes to plans or projects, I never want to hurt anyone’s feelings. I would rather change my own plans than make someone uncomfortable. Oh, and there’s that guy in my philosophy class. We argue a lot because he’s so contradictive about everything. And he’s fun to debate with, I think that’s what keeps the class alive, but he approaches it like it’s a competition while I’m more about learning and understanding. Sometimes I wish he’d chill, but it’s okay. Everyone’s got their own style. And I respect that. Wait, did I eat lunch? I can’t remember yesterday's meal either. Was it leftovers? I should definitely text Andrea about that new taco truck. We went last month and it was amazing! I love trying new places with friends. It’s like an adventure every time. I always look forward to those spontaneous dinners out. Some people don’t like trying new foods, but I can’t imagine being that way. I mean, what if I miss out on the best dish ever? And speaking of food, I need to plan a little gathering at my apartment. Nothing crazy, just a few friends over for game night. I can’t wait to bust out the board games and make popcorn and just chill. That’ll be great. I have to make sure to keep it simple though. I can already picture everyone arriving, laughing, eating snacks. The laughter is my favorite part. What’s the deal with late-night study sessions? I don’t mind them at all but some people get stressed, and I’m like “calm down!” We’ve got this. It’s not the end of the world if we don’t ace this exam. Just try your best and we can have fun after, that’s what I say. Life is too short to stress over grades, right? Just do your part and enjoy. I should probably reach out to some people who haven’t shown up lately. One girl in my literature class hasn’t been around much and I hope she’s okay. I’ll send her a message just to check in. I think that’s important, keeping that connection. It feels good to be there for others. I enjoy being the person who can lift someone’s mood with just a text or a funny meme. Like, “Hey! Just wanted to say you’re awesome!” A little positivity goes a long way. Okay, so I need to wrap this up, but I’m excited about the weekend. I want to hit the farmers' market, grab some fresh veggies, maybe run into friends there. And then a brunch plans? I can’t wait. Socializing makes everything better, doesn’t it? I love the buzz of people, the chatter, the laughter, and the good vibes. It fuels me. Alright, let’s go get this day moving!

It’s funny how I keep thinking about that conversation I had with Sarah last week. I kind of wish I hadn’t said that thing about her shoes. Totally uncalled for, right? I mean, she probably thought I was trying to put her down or something when I was just making a joke. I hope she didn’t take it wrong. But who am I to assume? People can be sensitive. I know I am. I can’t stand it when someone says something that gets under my skin, and then I spiral into thinking about it for days. Like, can someone really just say something totally innocent and it ruins my whole week? I can’t handle that pressure. What if I end up offending someone else next time? I overthink everything, it’s exhausting. Then there’s that group project that everyone is working on. Ugh, how am I going to get through it? Do I even want to? No, I’m sure they’re going to do it without me. I mean, I don’t really want to do the work anyway, but at the same time, I don’t want to look like I’m slacking. I guess I just end up hoping they won’t need too much from me because I really struggle with deadlines and I never know where to start. Every time I think about it I feel this tightness in my chest, like there’s this weight pressing down and I can’t think straight. Just want to curl up on my bed and watch some bad reality show and let the world pass me by. Who even cares about grades, right? But then I care. Always falling into that trap of caring too much when it comes to what others think. Maybe I should just ask for help, but then I’d have to talk to someone. My heart races just thinking about it, like I’m drowning in social anxiety. What’s wrong with me? I mean, I’m usually nice and polite, aren’t I? At least I try to be. But people can be so unpredictable. It’s like, sure, let’s all just smile and pretend everything is perfectly fine, but deep down I’m just waiting for the other shoe to drop. I can’t start a conversation with new people — it feels like trying to climb Everest. That party last month? Sucked. I kept thinking everyone was looking at me. They probably weren’t. They probably didn’t even notice I was there. But in my head, it was like I was standing in bright lights while they all laughed and shared inside jokes. I ended up in the corner with a drink, just nodding and pretending to enjoy the music but really wishing I could go home. And classes, oh boy. I walked in today and immediately felt overwhelmed by all the faces. What if I accidentally make eye contact with someone and then we feel this awkward connection? Like, do I wave? Do I smile? Is it a missed opportunity if I don’t? I just want to blend in, but blending in seems impossible when my mind is racing. I should take notes, but then I’m worried about how my handwriting looks, and if I’m missing something important while I’m being all neurotic over penmanship. Sometimes I wish I could just yell, “Hey, can we all just chill for a second?” but I know that’ll never happen. Then I think about family. I love them, I really do. But the constant questioning about my life choices? It’s a lot. “Are you dating anyone? Where do you see yourself in five years?” I’d rather not think about it because it makes my stomach turn. I feel like they’re judging me. I mean, I’m not the most ambitious person and they don’t seem to understand why I’m okay with that. But I can’t just say, “Hey, I’m fine being in the background.” That sounds weak, right? Okay, so random thought: I wonder how my favorite show is going to end. Probably not well. They always do that. Just when you think things are getting better, bam, a plot twist that makes you want to throw something at the screen. It’s like life is a series of plot twists built on bad decisions and missed signals. Makes me feel so alone, like I’m in this bubble where everyone is out there living their best lives while I’m struggling with the simplest tasks. I hope it gets better. I keep saying that, but maybe it just won’t. And that’s okay, right? I’ve been telling myself that a lot. Maybe I can accept that I’m not the life of the party, but who says I have to be? Just need to keep pretending, or maybe one day I’ll find my comfort zone, and it won’t feel so scary. What if everything is fine? What if I could just let go? But then I realize that letting go means putting myself out there, and that thought makes me want to hide again.

Okay so I was just thinking about how I haven’t really talked to anyone today. It's only noon and I’m already feeling that familiar dread of having to be social later. Group projects are the worst. I mean, what if they notice I’m not contributing much? It’s not that I don’t want to, but the thought of being awkward makes me want to curl up and hide somewhere. I feel like I’m always just on the edge of panic about being judged. I keep replaying that time in high school when I froze during a presentation. Everyone's eyes just... boring into me. I can feel it even now. Why can’t I just be chill like other people? They all seem to talk so easily, like they’re not afraid of what others think. I can’t even decide what to wear to class without worrying. Do they think I look weird? I should probably change my shirt again or put on something more fitting. But what does fitting even mean? It’s such a rabbit hole to go down. Speaking of clothes, I was supposed to do laundry last week. It’s piled up in my room, and I can’t even see the floor anymore. It’s such a chore, and honestly, I just don’t care enough to deal with it. Sometimes I think about just buying new clothes instead of washing the dirty ones. But that’s dumb, right? I mean, it’s a waste of money, and my roommate would definitely judge me for that. She's always so put-together and I can’t help but feel bad about myself when I see her. How do people manage to keep everything organized? They must have some secret I don’t know about. I wonder if it has to do with how they were raised. My parents were always busy, and I never learned how to keep my life in order. It feels overwhelming to even try. I wouldn't even know where to start. And then I think about how I probably shouldn’t dwell on it, but I do anyway. It’s like my brain is stuck in this loop of anxiety. Lunch today, I guess, will just be leftovers. I have some pasta from three nights ago, maybe I should throw in some frozen vegetables to make it look healthy? I’ll probably burn it anyway like I always do. Cooking stresses me out, and I can never tell if it’s even worth the effort. Why does everyone insist on cooking for fun? It sounds exhausting. Oh, I should probably text Sarah later to see if she wants to grab coffee or something. Ugh but what if she says no? I always worry that people don’t actually like me. Like, what if I’m just the last resort? It’s so cringy to think about going out and then just sitting in silence because I can’t think of anything to say. What if I accidentally say something dumb? I feel like I’m rambling. Social anxiety is such a drag. And deadlines are creeping up too. I need to finish that paper by Friday. Well, I should probably start it by tomorrow, but I can’t even open the document without feeling that weight in my chest. I wish I could just avoid school responsibilities altogether. Just drop everything and go somewhere peaceful. But that’s not possible. I have bills to pay. I have to do my reading tonight as well. I bought those books, and they just sit up on my shelf staring at me. Sometimes I feel like I should be reading them because that would somehow make me more interesting. I hate being boring. But then I remember how much I dislike deep conversations. They make me feel so exposed like I’m laying my soul bare. Is that why I don't open up to people? I get so anxious thinking about how they might react. Overthinking every little detail. It’s exhausting. Maybe I’ll just scroll through Instagram for a little while. Everyone seems to have exciting lives. Travel, achievements, cute pets. I can’t even get out of my room half the time. I wonder how they do it. Do they struggle like I do, or are they just better at hiding it? It’s not that I want to be them, but something in me craves that connection, the excitement, the laughter. But then the moment I think about reaching out, that tight knot forms in my stomach again. I just don’t want to feel judged or like a burden. I don’t want to be a bother to anyone. Okay, so maybe I'll just text Sarah and see what happens. If she says yes, awesome. If not? Well, that’s just how it is, right? Not everyone is going to like me or find me interesting. I just need to breathe and take it one step at a time, and keep my expectations low. I’ll manage. I probably will. I just need to remember that.

Socks on the floor, they’re kind of a problem. I mean, it’s not like I’m messy, right? Just organized chaos. I thrive in spaces that feel lived in. I like warm, inviting clutter – books stacked up, a half-empty mug, my phone charging in the corner. The thought of cleaning feels exhausting and boring but then again, maybe I just like the idea of it more than the actual doing of it. I wonder how many people feel like I do about that? Like changing the world would be easier than cleaning my room. But everything else feels more interesting than a tidy room. My mind wanders to all those articles I read about “minimalism” and how freeing it is. But it’s just not me. I want colors and textures and things that tell stories. That painting on the wall? I don’t even know what it is, but it moves me somehow. Okay so, what’s next? I think about how much I enjoy quiet time, well not quiet in the sense of silence but more calm. I could sit in a park or a café, anywhere really, and just people-watch. Is that weird? I like to imagine who they are, what they do, where they’re going. It’s like a game, right? Their clothes tell stories, their conversations are like snippets of a life I’m not part of. Extraverted people probably think I’m strange for finding joy in that but honestly, I could sit for hours soaking it all in. And it’s not lonely. It’s vibrant. I feel connected to the world in those moments, even if I’m not speaking to anyone. Why do I always end up thinking about connection? It’s fascinating. I feel deeply for people. I want to help them. Agreeableness, I guess. It’s like a superpower, but I don’t feel super. I just want to listen, to support. I wish people could see that more because sometimes I feel like they think I'm just avoiding confrontation. But really, I just don’t want to hurt anyone's feelings, and I believe in understanding.
