There’s this old couple I see at the café every Saturday. They have this warm vibe, always holding hands over their coffee. I wonder if they have any idea how much joy they spread just by existing together. Makes me think about relationships, love, or whatever that means in the grand scheme of things. Speaking of love, I can’t believe I forgot to text Alex back. He sent me a meme ages ago and I really appreciated it. It’s embarrassing how often my brain just goes “next!” instead of answering. I hope he isn’t mad. But then again, maybe it’s just how friendships work—people get caught up in their lives. I mean, I’m not mad at him when he does it. Everything just feels busy sometimes but not in a frantic way. More like a layering of moments. My brain is always full and jumping from thought to thought. It’s like trying to capture butterflies in a field, beautiful but scatterbrained. I’ve been thinking about traveling lately. I want to go to new places where I can feel the air and taste the food and listen to languages I don’t understand but find interesting. It’s a comforting thought, just imagining it. My mind starts racing with possibilities. Japan? Yes! The cherry blossoms! The food? I could eat sushi every day. Or maybe Italy for the pasta and the old architecture. I get so curious about how people live in different parts of the world, what their day-to-day looks like. Maybe it’s because I crave experiences that stretch my thinking. I don’t need to stay in one place forever. Actually, it’s funny because sometimes I feel more at home in other cultures than my own, not that I dislike my culture. It’s just that there’s a whole world out there filled with colors I’ve never seen. Oh! Just thought of that time I tried cooking Indian food for the first time. It was a disaster. Spices everywhere and I burnt the rice but it felt so good to do something outside my usual range of meals. I want to keep trying things like that. What else is on my mind? Future plans? I don’t know if I need all that pressure. Maybe just keep moving forward. I don’t want to be constrained. Just dreaming feels good. I wish I could bottle that feeling, you know? It’s like the world is full of surprises, I just have to be open to them. I wonder how many people close themselves off because they’re afraid. I’ve never really been afraid of new things. I dive in. Like going to that improv class last month. It was hilarious and awkward, but also liberating. No expectations. Just me and all that vulnerability. It’s in those moments I feel the most alive, the least like I’m trying to fit into a box.

The clock says 2:38 PM. I should be studying but I can't focus. I start thinking about the last party I went to. It was loud. Everyone was laughing and shouting, and I was just standing in the corner nursing a drink. Why do people have to be so loud? I like quiet spaces. I wish I could have just brought a book and sat down somewhere. But that would be weird, right? What would they think of me? A lone wolf in a sea of social butterflies. I mean, I don’t get how they do it. Just bouncing around talking to everyone as if they care, and maybe they do. I don't know. But I can't help but think I’m being judged, it's like I have this spotlight on me and everyone's eyes are boring into my skin, like I could just evaporate if I don’t keep moving. And I think, why put myself through that? Is it normal to feel this way? Sometimes I think maybe I’m the only one. I scroll through social media and everyone's smiling, having fun, and I’m like, yeah right. Filters and angles can make anyone look happy. I scroll past endless photos of friends together, and I can't help but wonder if they ever look at me and feel sorry for me in some way, like poor lonely me. I get these spikes of anxiety thinking about group chats; there's pressure to always respond. I generally don’t want to. Half the time I just want to throw my phone out the window and be left alone. Not that I'd actually do it; it’s my lifeline in many ways. It connects me to the world. Kind of ironic, huh? I crave connection but also dread it. Food. I can think about food and how I can never seem to decide what to eat. Maybe it’s a metaphor for my life—always indecisive. I hate grocery shopping, like all the choices are taunting me. Do I want chocolate or chips? Ice cream or cookies? And it’s always something unhealthy and then I feel guilty about it. It’s like I’m always on this tightrope of wanting what I want and knowing I should eat healthily. But sometimes that rebel in me is too loud. Can you be both enthused about something and feel guilty at the same time? I think yes, of course! I just remembered how I stumbled on that documentary about minimalism. Such an interesting concept. Living with less and freeing yourself from stuff—what a liberating thought. But then I think, do I really want to give everything away? All those books, the clothes I never wear, the random trinkets I have—there's something comforting in having them, even if I don’t need them. I mean, I know I am supposed to feel free when I declutter, but letting go can be hard. What if I need that shirt someday? Or that book I swore I would read and haven’t? Then I start to feel overwhelmed again, my thoughts spiraling into a puddle of worries. What if I fail my next exam? What if I don’t get a job after graduation? What if I’m stuck in this low-paying gig forever? All my peers seem to have their lives together. They network, they talk about job offers and internships. Meanwhile, I’m here, wondering if I’ll ever be good enough for anything. Anxiety feels almost like a friend sometimes, always right there with me, comforting and suffocating at the same time. And speaking of friends, what does it mean to be a good friend? Expectations feel heavy. I want to be supportive, but it’s exhausting. My mind feels like a rollercoaster that never stops. I mean, should I always reply immediately? Do I have to come up with good advice? Can’t I just listen? But if I don't respond quickly, will they think I don’t care? Ugh. Overthinking at its best. Then there are those moments when I feel high on creativity. Ideas flow, and I can’t get them out fast enough. Writing, drawing, thinking about building something out of nothing. I love that. It makes the chaos worthwhile. I want to explore every topic under the sun, dive into new cultures, learn languages, but then life pulls me back down into reality and I have to deal with the mundane crap like bills and responsibilities. Time is confusing too, where does it even go? I look at the clock and hours have passed without my noticing. This is my brain—it’s a whirlwind. Do all my thoughts make sense, or am I just rambling? Wait, did I even answer that question I started with? I'm not sure if there are answers. Maybe all these feelings just exist together, like an uncomfortable house guest that I can't quite shake off.

Okay so I just got back from that study group, it was pretty fun actually. Everyone was kind of all over the place, but in a good way like brainstorming ideas and bouncing things off each other, I love that energy. I mean, is there anything better than collaborating with friends? It’s like our ideas merge into this awesome, chaotic creativity. We definitely went off on tangents, like when Jenna started talking about her trip to Europe and how she tried to speak French but ended up ordering a croissant in Spanish. Classic Jenna. My friends are hilarious, they just get me, like we can talk about anything and it feels so natural. Speaking of Europe, I want to travel more. There’s so much to see! I remember reading about that castle in Germany with the stunning views. I can totally see myself there, exploring the winding streets and trying out all the local foods. I want to try everything, even the weird stuff. Food is such a big part of culture, and it’s like, how can you really experience a place without tasting it? I wonder if I’d get lost in the streets or if I’d just dive into a café and start chatting with the locals. That sounds fun. And talking about food, I’ve been trying to eat healthier lately. I made this huge salad with everything I could find in the fridge and it turned out amazing. Spinach, chickpeas, avocado, and I made my own dressing out of olive oil and lemon juice. Who knew I could whip something like that up? Cooking can be such a stress relief, tossing things together, feeling creative. I should make more time for it. My roommate always looks at me funny when I experiment, but she always ends up liking it so whatever. I’ve been thinking about my classes too. I really enjoy my psychology class; the way we talk about human behavior is so fascinating. I love discussing theories and learning how our minds work. It’s like peeling back the layers of an onion, finding all these different aspects of ourselves. I totally relate to the ideas of different personality types, I guess that’s why I’m so into the study I’m part of for our research project. I want to figure out how people tick, how they interact, and what makes them different. It’s like a giant puzzle, and I want to see how I fit into it. And my friends! We’ve been planning a camping trip for next month. I love camping; it’s the best way to just unplug and be in nature. I can’t wait to sit by the campfire, roast marshmallows, and just talk about everything. There’s something magical about nighttime in the woods, especially with friends. I get so excited picturing the stars. I bet I’ll have a million stories to tell afterward, especially the ridiculous ones where something goes wrong, like a raccoon stealing our food. I’m totally that person who will remember the funny moments over anything else. Thinking about laughter makes me smile. I love being around people who can make me laugh and also who appreciate my sense of humor too. I think it’s such an important part of friendships. The more I laugh with my friends, the deeper those connections feel. We can be ridiculous together and just embrace that awkward silliness. It’s the best. I just started a new book too, it’s about mindfulness and being present. It’s interesting how they talk about appreciating the little things; I think I’m good at that. I notice the small details all the time, whether it's a cool tree in the park or a random cat on the street. I feel like if you’re open to it, there’s beauty everywhere. And I love how it encourages self-reflection. I find myself jotting down thoughts in my journal—nothing super serious, just what I’m feeling in the moment. It helps me process everything. And then there’s social media, I mean, wow, that’s a double-edged sword, right? I get that it connects us, but sometimes it feels overwhelming with all the noise. I use it to keep in touch with people, to share fun moments, but I’ve been trying to be more mindful of my usage. I want to make sure I’m present in my life and not swiping away those precious moments. I miss my family too, haven’t seen them in a while. I should probably plan a visit soon. Family gatherings are always a blast, filled with laughter and stories from our childhood. Nothing beats that feeling of belonging, like no matter what, they’re always in my corner. I feel lucky for that. So many thoughts, it's like my brain is a whirlwind of ideas. I’d love to hear what everyone else is thinking too. That exchange of thoughts, energy, and perspectives—it’s what keeps life interesting. Gosh, I can’t wait to see what happens next!

Okay so, where do I start? I just finished watching this documentary on deep-sea creatures. They’re so bizarre, like why do they glow? Is it for communication or to attract prey? Just the thought of it makes my mind race. I love learning about weird stuff—that’s what keeps me going. Sometimes I feel like I have too much curiosity though, like I can’t settle on one thing. Like right now, I should be focusing on the essay that’s due next week but my brain is bouncing around like a pinball machine. What is it with brains? Why can’t they just focus on one thing at a time? I’m doing this study—what was it? Oh right, it’s about people’s behaviors, which is fascinating but also exhausting. Speaking of exhausting, social events drain me sometimes which sounds ridiculous because I’m so outgoing. I can talk to a wall, but then afterward it’s like I’ve run a marathon. I love meeting new people but then when I’m in a room full of strangers I feel this chaotic energy building up inside me. I have to keep the conversation going, and then I leave feeling both high from the interaction and emotionally spent. It’s this weird duality, like I’m addicted to the thrill of socializing but also terrified I’ll say something stupid. Oh man, that reminds me of last week at that party. I thought I was being charming, but somehow I ended up discussing the nuances of existential philosophy with this guy I barely knew. Like, who does that? And then I caught the look on other people’s faces, half-interested but mostly just confused. I probably looked like I was trying too hard, which I really hate. Why is it so hard to just relax? My mind keeps churning through possible topics—what if I had talked about movies instead? Everyone loves movies. Then again, with my neurotic streak, what if they didn’t like the film I mentioned? Or what if they thought I was pretentious for suggesting some indie flick? I second guess myself so much it’s exhausting. I probably overthink it all, like way too much. Sometimes I wonder if I’d be better off just being more agreeable and going along with the crowd but I can’t help it. I want to express my opinions, even if they rub people the wrong way. I mean, I just can’t pretend to like something I don’t. That would be so disingenuous. And then the whole conscientiousness thing kicks in—like when I start planning something, I go all out. My planner is color-coded and bursting with sticky notes and reminders. I love it but then sometimes it becomes a burden. Like, what if I forget something important? I have this constant pressure to stay organized and on top of everything. It just feels like juggling a million balls in the air. I’m not sure how I keep it all straight sometimes. Traveling is something I’m always thinking about. I can’t help it; it feels like this endless adventure waiting to happen. The thought of discovering a new city, trying new foods, learning different cultures—it’s thrilling. I mean, I wouldn’t want to go somewhere too touristy. I’d rather dive right into the local scene. I thrive on that. But then what if something goes wrong? What if I get lost or miss a flight? My brain works overtime on that stuff, it’s like I’ve already planned out all the worst-case scenarios. But the weird thing is, I still crave the spontaneity. I want to embrace the unknown but then there’s that voice in my head going—what if you end up in a terrible situation? Even when I’m trying to be open-minded and experience everything, my anxiety can really get in the way. It’s a constant push and pull. Okay back to doing that study—what’s it called again? Right, it’s about people and their behaviors in social contexts. I wonder how much my extraversion and neuroticism play into my daily interactions. Do others feel this way? Are they just better at hiding it? It can be disheartening to think everyone else has it figured out while I’m stumbling through conversations. And I think about how I’m always chasing the next social event, but maybe I need to take a step back and just enjoy the quiet sometimes. There’s beauty in those moments too, isn’t there? I just have to remind myself that it’s okay to take a break. But wow, that’s easier said than done! All these thoughts swirl around, and sometimes I wish I could just turn it off, like a light switch. But then again, that quirkiness is what makes me, me. It’s chaotic and messy but also vibrant. Maybe I wouldn’t trade it for anything—except maybe a little bit of peace and quiet.

okay so my phone just buzzed and it's probably a group chat message but I’m not in the mood to check it I mean sometimes I feel like they talk way too much about nothing like someone’s new hair color or the latest episode of a show I don’t watch I just wish they’d plan a movie night or something instead but they never do and that's annoying it's like why even have a group chat if we're not gonna do anything productive or fun together I kind of prefer one-on-one conversations anyway they feel deeper more real I like just sitting with someone and talking about random stuff like what they ate for dinner or how their week is going I don’t care about the small talk but I like hearing about people’s lives. I think about my roommate sometimes she’s really nice and we get along so well but there’s something about her that makes me feel like I have to be on my best behavior I don’t know why it’s just a vibe and I can sense it even when she’s not trying to be judgmental but she’s always really peppy and outgoing and I wonder if I should be more like her but then I think no that would be exhausting like why do people always have to be so loud and social? but she makes friends easily and I admire that I just don’t want to have to try that hard then again there’s something comforting about being alone in my room just watching my shows or reading the same book for the fifth time I don’t mind the repetition I guess it’s like a comfort blanket I never want to be challenged or exposed to new ideas I like the routine it’s simple it feels safe. And I find myself getting anxious if I have to go somewhere unfamiliar like a party or a gathering I can feel my heart race if I think about it too long so I just avoid it and stick to the things I know which is the same few restaurants and the same coffee shop and that one friend who gets me and we can just sit in silence and be okay with that sometimes I bring snacks I like food so much I could eat the same thing every day and be content with it I don’t know how people crave variety I started playing video games again it’s nice to just dive into a world where I don’t have to think about reality but then I always wonder if I’m wasting time and I should be doing something more productive like homework or projects I keep telling myself I’ll do it later but later keeps getting pushed back and it’s not that I don’t care I just get lost in my head about what I should be doing instead of what I am actually doing I think that’s part of the low conscientiousness thing I read about it once or twice and it kind of made sense but I still don’t want to change it guess I’m just comfortable being me and that’s fine even if it means I might miss deadlines which feels bad but then I think well maybe it just isn’t that important in the grand scheme of things there’s also this stress from family expectations like they want me to excel and be successful in the way they see it but success is just so subjective right? I don’t want to be a doctor or a lawyer or anything that’s gonna take years of painstaking work and stress. I want a simple job that pays the bills and gives me enough time to unwind I don’t see why that’s not enough I just want to find fulfillment in the little things not in climbing a corporate ladder or some obscure definition of success that doesn’t resonate with me at all and sometimes I think about traveling but then I remember how awful airports are and how everything is so crowded and then I think no thanks I’m fine with my hometown and the places I know and the people I love even if they’re just a few I like our small gatherings and just talking about life or watching movies together it makes me happy I guess you could say I thrive in those small moments where it’s just easy and no pressure to perform or impress anybody I wonder if I should get a hobby I mean a real one that’s more than just scrolling through Instagram and watching YouTube but what would that even be? drawing seems like it would be too much effort and I’d probably hate what I made anyway and I’ve tried writing but I just don’t know if I have anything worthwhile to say I mean who really cares? so I’m kind of stuck in this loop of wanting to do something but not wanting to put in the effort to find out what it is and that’s okay right I guess I’ll stick with my comfort food and the same old routine it’s just where I feel at home and there’s really no need to force change when I’m content with the life I have even if it’s not what everyone else thinks I should be doing.

Okay so I can’t stop thinking about how I have to finish that group project but like nobody has told me anything and I want to make sure it’s perfect because if it’s not then what if the professor thinks I didn’t do my part and I care so much about what people think of me especially in group settings and it’s just so stressful sometimes because everyone is counting on me and I want to be that reliable person like I really do. I mean I like being around people, I love being part of a team but it gets overwhelming, especially when they don’t respond or they’re late with their parts, or they don’t even care and I’m left picking up the pieces like I have to stay on top of everyone, sending reminders like "hey did you finish that?" and it’s exhausting. I worry about annoying them though, like am I too pushy? I just want everyone to be on board because the last group project was such a disaster and I had to redo my whole section by myself last minute and I really don’t want that to happen again. Oh, and I need to call my mom later. She’s always asking about my grades and how I’m doing, which is sweet but sometimes I wish she’d just chill. Like, I get it, she cares, but the pressure is intense and I know she’s expecting me to do great things, and I want to but what if I don’t? It’s like this constant nagging voice in my head that tells me I need to do better, and then I start overthinking everything like should I take that extra class next semester? I feel like I should because everyone else is doing it but then again, I’m already so busy and I don’t want to burn out either. I’ve been trying to keep a schedule but my planner is getting messy - doodles in the margins, color-coded highlights, sticky notes everywhere and sometimes I don’t even want to look at it because it just reminds me of how much I have to do. But of course, I can’t ignore it because if I do then I’ll forget something critical and then it’ll haunt me forever, like that time I forgot about an exam and I swear I can’t get that kind of embarrassment out of my head. But maybe I should just try to relax more? I love hanging out with friends, I really do, I just need to coordinate times that work for everyone and sometimes it’s like herding cats trying to get everyone to agree on something. I crave those moments though when we finally all make it and we can laugh and joke about life and I forget about the anxiety bubbling underneath. It’s like two sides of my life – I need to be responsible and study and make sure everyone is on task, but then I want to be this fun-loving person who can go out and dance and enjoy life but it feels like a constant push and pull. Like, I can’t even enjoy a party without thinking about what I should be working on when I get home. The noise, the people, the loud music and flashing lights, it’s all so enticing but then I start to think about my responsibilities and how I should really be studying for that test coming up. And then there’s social media. I love scrolling through my feed, seeing everyone’s happy faces, and it’s so easy to get lost in it but at the same time I can’t help but compare myself to everyone else and feel like I’m not doing enough. Everyone seems so put together and I’m stuck here trying to juggle everything, feeling like I’m barely keeping my head above water. Like, why is everyone else going on trips and living their best lives while I’m just trying to figure out how to balance school and work? Maybe I just need to be more disciplined or figure out how to say no more but then again, I don’t want to miss out on anything because what if this is the best time of my life and one day I look back and regret not going out with friends? Also, the weather. It’s getting colder and I hate it. I don’t want to deal with layers and layers of clothing because that just adds to my anxiety of needing to look perfect. Like, what if I wear something and it doesn’t work out and I feel uncomfortable? And I really don’t like the cold, it makes it tough to enjoy being outside. I don't know, maybe I should just accept it and try to make it work, like find fun activities that can get me moving instead of hiding in my dorm. So, back to that group project. Ugh, I should really just call everyone tonight and see where we’re at. Just do it, right? I mean I can’t just sit here overthinking it, I need to take control. Right? I’ll feel so much better once I do.

Okay so I just noticed a crack in the wall and I’m wondering if I should get that fixed but is it really worth the trouble I mean it’s just a crack right but what if it gets bigger and then mold starts to grow and then it spreads and then I have a whole mess on my hands why do I always look at the negative side of things when I try to be optimistic but then again it’s good to be cautious if I don’t think about these things who will? Should I call maintenance now or wait until something else happens? I hate being that person who complains about every little thing but who else will call it out if I don’t. Speaking of calling things out, I was reading an article about climate change and honestly it makes me really sad sometimes. The way they talk about it like it’s already too late and how polar bears are losing their homes and I feel for them on a deep level. I mean it’s not just bears, right? It’s everything. The coral reefs, the trees, the insects, the whole ecosystem and all I can do is recycle and reduce waste but what more can I do? It feels like a drop in the ocean. Maybe I should volunteer somewhere but then would I be making a real difference or is it just another box to tick off? I’ve always thought experience is what matters most but I often second guess myself like am I really doing it for the right reasons or am I just trying to impress someone? Why do I care so much about what others think anyway? It’s kind of funny how I get stuck in this loop of overthinking but I don’t feel anxious about it, more like curious. Like I want to understand what’s happening in my mind. I read somewhere that it’s good to journal but I don’t really know if I have the time or if I would do it right. I mean my thoughts are kind of all over the place, and do I need structure? I don’t know, but the idea of structuring my thoughts is interesting. What if I made lists instead? Like lists of things I want to do this semester or things I want to learn. A part of me wants to dive deep into philosophy like existentialism because sometimes I feel like I’m just drifting through life. And I wonder if other people feel like that. They always seem so busy and engaged in their projects. I mean what do people even talk about? Small talk has never been my strong suit. I always end up talking about things I care about and then I see their eyes glaze over. It’s like, how do we connect on a different level? Oh, I just remembered that time I tried to connect with that group during orientation. Everyone was so loud and boisterous, and I was just trying to slip in a comment about the reading we were discussing but it was like I was invisible and then I thought, do I want to be like them? Do I need that energy? Sometimes I think I’d rather be cozy with a book or maybe a puzzle but that sounds so boring to other people. Why does everyone think they need to be out and about all the time? I love deep conversations but they’re rare as gold. I had one the other day about the nature of happiness, and I felt so alive. It was like my brain was firing on all cylinders, but then the conversation ended and I was back to the silence, which is comforting in a way, but lonely too. I should probably look into joining a club or something that aligns with my interests. Something that can offer that blend of community but also individual exploration. I wonder if there’s a book club that reads classics or non-fiction or something more niche like philosophy related to science. I get excited thinking about meeting people who also love to dig deep into those topics. It feels good to think that there are like-minded souls out there. But then if I start talking about these deep philosophical ideas do I come off pretentious? Are they gonna roll their eyes and think I’m just trying to show off? Ugh, I overthink that all the time. What’s the line between being passionate and coming off as a know-it-all? I don’t want to be that person either. But then again, does it even matter? It should matter to me that I’m being true to myself but sometimes that feels like a tightrope walk. Still I think about starting something like a podcast or a blog where I could just explore these ideas. It could be freeing to just let it all flow without judgment. What if I just made it for myself, who cares if it never gets published? There’s something so appealing about creating just for the sake of creating. I should really go grab my laptop and jot down those ideas.

It's weird how people can just talk and talk and it feels like they don’t even notice you’re there, like, I don’t get it. I mean, you can’t always be in the spotlight, right? Sometimes it’s better just to observe. People go on about their lives, their thoughts spilling out without a care, and I’m just here, minding my own business. The same old routine. Classes, assignments—this study I’m supposed to be part of but it feels pointless. I don’t really care about connecting with people or what they think. I like it that way, but then I wonder if it makes me a loner or something. Whatever that means. I see people getting anxious over grades, social events, friendships. Not me. I get a C, it is what it is. I don’t need to stress over a stupid paper. What’s the point? I’ll finish it, it’ll be fine, but the stress? Nah. It's tiring just thinking about it. And those group projects. Why is it so hard to get everyone on the same page? I just want them to do their part, I do mine, we turn it in, and we move on. I can’t stand the small talk. People act like they need to fill the silence. It’s such a waste of time. I just want to get to the meat of the matter. Just say what needs to be said and get it done. All that chit-chat about weekend plans, movies, whatever, feels forced. Why can’t they just be okay with not saying anything? I hate how they look at me when I don’t jump in. Like, what? I’m just listening. Maybe I’m more of an observer by nature, and right now I’m supposed to be participating in some sort of conversation but what even is there to say? I could shout my opinion, but why? I don’t have a strong opinion on anything. Does it make me dull? Or more realistic? Is realistic even a thing? I remember I went to this party once. Just to see what the fuss was about. Everyone was all over the place. I don’t know why they like it. Overpriced drinks, loud music, not even good music, just a bunch of noises. I stood in the corner, drink in hand, people dancing, shouting, I wanted to duck out early, but you’ve got to at least make an appearance, right? So I stood there. Watching. Some guy spilled beer all over my shoes. Who even spills beer anymore? Is it that hard to hold a cup? I didn’t say anything. He probably wouldn’t even care and I didn’t want to draw attention. Eventually, I left. I made an excuse, said I had an early class even though it was Saturday. I kind of like it this way though. Being alone. There’s no pressure, no fake smiles. I can just be. No one asking what I’m thinking, no need to pretend. I don’t need to share my feelings or opinions. It’s almost a relief. I think about how people post everything on social media. Their food, their experiences, their lives—it’s overwhelming. Do they really care if I see their breakfast? No, please, just eat it. I don’t have to see that. People are so transparent, it’s almost funny. What’s wrong with having a private life? I like the quiet. I find comfort in the absence of noise. Sometimes I think about moving. Somewhere isolated. Just a small cabin in the woods or something. No neighbors. No one to bother me. Just trees and maybe a few animals. Sounds peaceful. All that nature around. I’d probably just read, maybe write—I wouldn’t show anyone anyway. I can’t imagine sharing my thoughts like that. Who cares what I think? But that idea of being away, no phones, just me, it’s tempting. I’d be okay if I never had to deal with people again. But then what? What if I need something? Or what if I end up bored? I don’t know what I’d do. I spend enough time in my own head anyway. Maybe that’s why I don’t connect with others. They just ramble on about nothing while I’m thinking about something deeper, something that takes time to unravel. It’s not that I’m cold or unfeeling. I just don’t want to waste my time on activities that lack meaning. I can’t sit and listen to gossip or small talk about the latest trends. What’s the point? Give me something real, something I can chew on. Life is too short to fill it with meaningless noise. And yet here I am, typing away, sharing these random thoughts. I guess it’s a way of sorting out my mind. I just wish it was easier to navigate this whole college experience without all the drama. It's like a soap opera. Enough already.

It's 1:47 PM and I just had the weirdest conversation with Mark in the hallway. He thinks he can convince me to join the chess club. I don’t even know how to play chess. Why would I want to spend my afternoons in an echoey room with a bunch of people who think three hours of staring at a board is a good time? I like people, but chess sounds so boring. I’d rather chat about the latest Netflix series or pop culture gossip. Speaking of which, did you see the latest on that influencer drama? It’s wild. How do they even get into these situations? The vending machine is out of my favorite chips again. Classic. I could really go for something salty right now. Maybe I’ll just grab a soda instead. Too many calories, though. Ugh, decisions. I hate making decisions on snacks. If only life were simpler, you know? Just grab what you want and not think about it. Okay, back to classes. I’m supposed to be studying for that history exam but honestly, what's even the point? I mean, who really cares what happened in 1776, or whenever? I’d much rather hear about what’s happening now. Current events, TikTok trends, all that jazz. I just don’t care about dry facts and dates. That class is dragging me down. Can we just talk more about people, what they said, what they felt? Oh, and Jenny said she’s hosting another party this weekend. I’m definitely going because it's always a blast. Last time was epic; we played beer pong and I actually won a couple of rounds. I don’t even usually play sports but that game was soooo much fun. I should probably text her to see what I can bring. Maybe some chips? Or should I bring something else? It's kinda weird being the one who always brings snacks but somebody's gotta do it, right? I really need to clean my room. It’s such a mess. Clothes everywhere, half-empty energy drink cans on the desk and that weird pizza box that I swear has been there since last month. Yeah, I should probably do that, but only maybe after the party. I can’t think about cleaning all the time. What’s the fun in that? I can’t even remember the last time I had a clean living space for more than a day. Maybe that's why I’m always so distracted. I remember that time we had a spontaneous road trip to the beach. Decided on a Friday and left Saturday morning. We didn't even pack properly. Just threw a bunch of stuff in the car and hoped for the best. My phone was dead half the time, but who cares when you have the ocean in front of you? When we got there, we just jumped right in the water. No sunscreen, no worries. Just living in the moment, you know? That’s my kind of life. It’s weird how people can take things so seriously. Like, why waste time worrying? I get that some people need structure or a plan but that’s just not for me. I thrive on the chaos, the unpredictability. Who cares if I haven’t really studied for that exam? I’ll wing it. It’ll be fine. Plus, I’m pretty good at talking my way through things. I can charm my way out of a corner if I need to. I don’t get why people stay in their comfort zones all the time. Yeah, it’s comfortable but boring. Live a little! Explore! I remember when I just started college; I took random classes just for the heck of it. I think I dropped a few because they were too structured or didn’t have enough interaction. Classes with too much reading just put me to sleep. I want people around me, talking, laughing, challenging each other. The campus is buzzing with people, and I love it. You see the same faces, but everyone seems to be in their own world, plugged into their phones or rushing off to the next thing. Sometimes I think about just yelling out, “Hey! Let’s hang out!” but that’d be too awkward, right? Or maybe it wouldn't be? Maybe I should try that one day. Just see what happens. Time flies, and I've got to wrap this up. I need to check my messages, see if anyone's down to grab a bite. I can't be alone too long. It’s just more fun with company. I just don’t get how some people can be introverted. Life is so much more exciting when you’re around others. I feel like every day should be filled with laughter and noise. So much more to do, so much more to experience. Let’s go live a little.

7:45 am and I’m already thinking about the day ahead. I love mornings, they're so full of potential. What’s on the agenda? Classes, meeting friends for lunch, maybe some time in the library. I thrive in vibrant environments. People buzzing all around me, that's my jam. I can’t imagine being alone all day, that would just drain me. I mean, how do people do that? Don’t they miss the energy? I practically vibrate when I’m with others. Just the thought of group projects makes me smile, especially when everyone is bouncing ideas off each other. I get a kick out of brainstorming, it’s like a little mini-team sport. I remember last week, we had this group assignment for psych class. We ended up at the campus cafe, it was crazy! The coffee was flowing, laughter everywhere, and the ideas just kept building. Someone suggested a theme based on humor in psychology, and suddenly, we were sharing our favorite memes and YouTube clips. This one guy, Matt, found a video of cats reacting to psychology experiments and we couldn't breathe from laughing. That’s the kind of collaboration I live for. What about the time I accidentally walked into the wrong lecture hall? I was on my way to an economics class but got distracted by a hundred voices in a room down the hall. It turned out to be a seminar for philosophy. I didn’t know anyone in there but just sat down because I was curious. I’m always curious! New ideas, new experiences, it’s like a buffet of life I can’t resist. I ended up chatting with this amazing girl, Sara. She had the coolest theories about existentialism. We exchanged numbers, and I totally ended up going to dinner with her and some of her friends later that week. I love that about being extroverted—it’s like an ever-expanding network of interesting people. I can’t help it, I just want to get to know everyone. Then there’s my volunteering at the local shelter. Everyone thinks I’m crazy for waking up so early on weekends but honestly, it’s the highlight of my week! The dogs are so excited to see us, and I can’t even. Their energy just lifts me. I keep thinking about adopting one, but I travel so much. Maybe later. The volunteers are such a great mix of personalities, and I feel like we click instantly. You make a connection over shared goals, and that’s a feeling you just can’t beat. Speaking of connections, I've been playing around with the idea of starting a podcast. I can already picture the guests! I want to interview people about their journeys, their quirks, their dreams. I think it would be so rewarding to create a platform where everyone can share their stories. People have such incredible experiences, and everyone can learn something new from each other. Why is that not more common? Just talking and connecting, that’s where the magic happens. I wonder if there’s a psychology behind why I love this so much. Just thinking about how people perceive each other gets me fired up. Like, why do we click with some and not others? It’s fascinating to dissect those social dynamics. I’d love to write a thesis on that. Imagine combining psychology with sociology and cultural studies. My head spins just thinking about all the avenues of research to explore. Mid-September is coming, which means it’s apple picking season! I can’t wait to organize a trip with some friends. Road trips are like the ultimate bonding experience. Loud music, windows down, snacks everywhere. I’ve already got a playlist started. We’ll go to that orchard upstate, the one with the cider donuts. I can already taste them. Sweet, soft, and slightly warm. Pure bliss. I need to remember to bring my camera. Those photos will be perfect for my Instagram, highlighting all those smiles and sunshine. Then there’s fall break right after that. This will be my first real trip with friends, just the five of us hitting the coast. I’m so excited! The ocean, the beach, bonfires at night. I’m already plotting what games to bring. I need to keep everyone entertained, you know? Charades, board games, truth or dare; the laughter just never stops! And then there are classes. So much to learn, I feel like I’m drinking from a fire hose sometimes, but I love it! It’s hard to imagine getting bored. I want to dive into everything. New ideas, new perspectives—give me all of it! I can’t shake this feeling that if I’m not learning something new every day, I’m somehow wasting my time. My friends tease me about it, but I can’t help it. The world is so rich with experiences; I just want to soak it all in. Okay, I could keep going forever, but I feel a little emptiness in my stomach. Maybe I’ll grab a snack before heading to class. I just have this overwhelming excitement for today and everything that this semester brings.

Okay so I just saw this old book on my shelf that I forgot I had. It’s one of those classic novels that everyone says you should read but I just never got around to it. It’s sitting there, collecting dust. It’s weird how I used to love reading for hours but now I can’t seem to find the time or energy. Maybe it’s because I’m so busy with school stuff and work and trying to keep everything in order. I like lists, I have lists for everything. Grocery lists, to-do lists, plans for the week, it helps me feel more secure. I think about how I can be so structured in some parts of my life but then I see my notes and they’re a mess. Handwriting all over the place. I should really work on that, maybe make them neater. There’s something comforting about neat writing. I like to make everything tidy. I guess that’s part of being conscientious. Then there are the people in my life. I don’t mind being around them but sometimes it feels draining. I prefer small groups or one-on-one conversations. It’s not that I don’t like people. I just don’t feel the need to have a big social circle. Quality over quantity, right? I always try to be nice to everyone, help out where I can. It’s easier to get along when you keep things simple and friendly. Even when people are rude or difficult, I just try to stay calm and let it slide. I remember this one time in class—this guy wouldn’t stop interrupting the professor. It was so frustrating but I just looked at him and kept my mouth shut. Not worth getting into an argument. Just focus on what I can learn and not let that guy get to me. Classes this semester are okay but sometimes I feel like I’m just going through the motions. I know it’s important to perform well, but I don’t get that excitement like some other students do. Maybe it’s because I’m not really into the subject matter. Like, I chose my major for practical reasons but sometimes I wonder if I should’ve picked something more adventurous. But then again, adventurous sounds risky. I like routines, I like knowing what I’m getting into, I like laying out a path and following it. There’s a comfort in predictability. On weekends, I tend to keep things low-key. I was invited to this party last week and I didn’t go. Instead, I stayed home and organized my notes from last semester. It was nice. I listened to music, had some snacks, and felt productive. I don’t get how some people can stay out late and then still function the next day. I need my sleep. I like going for walks to clear my mind. Just a quiet stroll, maybe through the park. I see families, people walking their dogs, kids playing. It’s nice to be around all that but I don’t want to get too involved. I guess that goes back to being agreeable. I want to support but don’t need to dive fully into social chaos. Sometimes I think about my future. It’s overwhelming. Finding a job that fits, being responsible. I keep thinking about what I can do after graduation. I have this detailed plan laid out, step by step. It feels good having a roadmap. Others talk about traveling or trying out new experiences right after college and I think, “How can you do that without a stable job?” It’s just not practical, and I don’t like being unprepared. Budgeting, saving, it all makes sense to me. I don’t want to take risks that would leave me in a tight spot. People seem surprised when I say that I don’t want to engage in a lot of risky or spontaneous things. I guess they think it’s boring or something. I don’t know, maybe they're projecting their own needs. I feel content. There’s enough excitement in my life with school and work. I find joy in the little things, like helping a classmate understand a difficult concept or finishing an assignment ahead of schedule. These small victories mean a lot to me. Yesterday, I made dinner for myself and it turned out surprisingly well. Just a simple pasta dish but the satisfaction was nice. Cooking is another little hobby I enjoy, even if it’s not adventurous like trying out new recipes every week. There’s something about chopping vegetables and watching water boil. Everything has its place. Everything in order. I think that’s what keeps me grounded.

Okay so I was thinking about how I should probably be studying right now but my mind is just all over the place like I can’t focus on anything for more than a few seconds, every thought leading to another thought and then I’m trying to catch up with the rabbit holes it goes down, like where did I put my keys last night and why do I always forget them in the fridge or on the kitchen counter or under a pile of clothes that somehow keeps multiplying on my floor. The laundry is a disaster. Should I really care if my roommate gets annoyed? She’s always making those little comments about how I need to clean but I mean who really even cares when you’re swamped with projects and assignments that seem to multiply like rabbits. Speaking of projects, did I remember to send that email to that professor about my presentation? I think I did but what if I didn’t and what if I made a total fool of myself in class yesterday rambling about some random article that had barely anything to do with the assignment but I found it interesting and I couldn’t help myself? It’s like sometimes the words just spill out of me like that’s who I am, just a big pile of words waiting to come out, so why hold back? But then there’s the awkward silence when nobody else chimes in and I’m just standing there with all these thoughts, and everyone else is looking at their phones like whatever I just said didn’t matter at all. Do I even matter? Maybe I should start talking less? Nah, I love talking and being out there but then again I also get so anxious when I’m by myself, like every thought spirals into this big cloud of worry and negativity. My friends are all great, they always say they need someone like me in their lives to balance things out, but sometimes I feel like I’m just this pressure cooker waiting to explode because there’s so much going on in my head. I have to keep saying to myself, “Breathe, just breathe” when I’m in public situations, you know when everyone’s watching and waiting and you can feel the weight of the world on your shoulders? It’s just a classroom but it feels like a stage and guess what? I’m NOT an actor, just a human trying to figure out how to survive all this chaos. The party last weekend was wild though, right? It was a complete mess but I loved every minute. I met all these new people, faces blending together like a weird abstract painting. At one point, I found myself talking to a philosophy major who was raving about existentialism, and I had no clue what he was talking about but I just nodded along because he was so passionate, and it’s contagious when someone talks with that kind of energy. And then there was that moment when the music changed and everyone just started dancing like no one was watching, and I jumped right in without thinking about it. I mean who cares what people think, life’s too short to be boring and I just want to soak it all up, while the clock is ticking and ticking and time keeps slipping through my fingers. But then there are days when I can’t seem to get out of bed and I’m just staring at the ceiling wondering if it’s worth it. I mean why do I have to do all this? All the studying and the projects and the endless cycle of exams? It feels exhausting and I don’t want to be that person who is always stressed out. I want to be able to blow everything off and just live in the moment but that nagging voice is always there like “You need to be responsible” or “Get your act together.” My head is an absolute whirlwind sometimes. I really wish I could be more organized but it just doesn’t happen; lists confuse me. Lists help some people but for me, they just get lost in the chaos of everything else. Why do I even care about grades anyway? We’re all just trying to figure it out at the end of the day. But maybe I do care, just a little. I want to prove something to myself and the world, but what is it? I’m not sure. The pressure to succeed can be overwhelming. Then I remember that feeling of freedom, being out there and not worrying about anything. That feeling is intoxicating and I need more of it. I’ll figure it out. Or I won’t, but it’s a rollercoaster and I’m strapping in for the ride. And what’s with my obsession with new experiences? Never-ending curiosity, always wanting to explore, taste, try. I should be more focused on the here and now, but I want to visit that new restaurant downtown, even if I can’t spare the cash, because I’m just drawn to adventure like a moth to a flame. I think I’ll go do that. Forget studying for now. Life’s too short.

Pizza rolls in the microwave smell really good right now. I remember the first time I tried them. A friend brought over a bag and I was like, “What are these?” They looked like little pillows of joy, and now they’re one of my go-tos when I’m home alone. It’s fascinating how food can hold memories, like the way a specific snack can remind you of a moment in time. I wonder if my friend remembers those pizza rolls too. I don't actually keep in touch with them much anymore. It's funny how people drift apart. I wonder if they think about me, or if they’re busy living their lives like I am, swirling in this chaotic mix of being a student, trying to figure things out but really just playing it by ear most of the time. I think I prefer things that way, honestly. Rigid schedules can be suffocating. Who has time to worry about deadlines all the time? I mean, I’ll get things done, but I’m more about the flow. Like, I can sit in a class and just absorb everything, and then it’s like a light bulb moment where everything clicks together, but if I try too hard to cram it all into my head, it won’t make sense. Synthesizing ideas is so much better than memorizing. Why is that? It feels good to understand concepts rather than just pushing facts into my brain, which is why I love the classes that let me express myself creatively. Speaking of expression, I’ve been thinking about joining that art club on campus. I’ve always loved doodling and sketching, just letting my pen dance over the page, and somehow there’s something deeply satisfying about watching a blank canvas transform into something meaningful, or just weird. It’s like traveling without moving, you know? You can explore different sides of yourself, different ideas, all while just chilling at a table with some friends or even by yourself, with the faint hum of conversation around. I’ve always been more comfortable in smaller groups though—too many people and I start feeling overwhelmed, but in a good way, like when you eat too much dessert and it’s too sweet but you don’t care because you’re happy. I feel good about helping people too. It’s such a nice feeling when someone asks for assistance, and I can be there for them. I remember when a classmate needed help with studying for finals last semester. I just sat with them in the library, and it felt like we were just two explorers on a mission, diving into the books together, sharing thoughts and laughs. That day, the stress of finals melted away and we became allies of sorts. It was simple, yet powerful. Helping others is a form of connection that I really value. As much as I prefer my solitude, moments like those remind me of the beauty of companionship, in the quiet, unsaid understanding. Oh, and traveling! I’ve been thinking so much about where I want to go next. I really want to see Japan. The blend of tradition and modernity there is like nothing else. I could wander through shrines and then suddenly be blasted with neon lights and bustling streets. So many layers to uncover, and I can already imagine the food—the sushi, the ramen, the street food! I want to dive headfirst into new experiences, learning about cultures different from mine. It excites me to think about meeting new people, hearing their stories. That thrill, the unknown, is what drives the curiosity inside of me. But then there’s the practical side of me, the one that knows I’m supposed to be focused on classes and internships and all that. But who really follows that rigid plan? I feel like I’m all over the place but in a good way. I get random ideas and I just go for it. Last week, I took an impromptu bus ride to a nearby city just to explore. I didn’t even have a map. I just walked around, discovering quirky shops, street performers, and grabbing snacks from food trucks. It was exhilarating, like a mini-adventure just waiting to happen. I think I thrive off that spontaneity. Do I need to have my life figured out? Nah. I can keep things loose and airy. I’m okay with the messiness, even if it’s not perfectly aligned with what people expect. I just want to explore, learn, feel, create, and sometimes eat pizza rolls, and that’s a decent way to live, right? Just riding the waves of life, and enjoying the moments as they come.

Five minutes ago I was looking at my phone, scrolling through news articles that mostly annoy me. Why do people care what this celebrity wore to an event? It’s just fabric and designer brands screaming for attention. I mean, it's interesting in a way, but the obsession is bizarre. I guess it’s just how society works. People need their distractions, their idols. I don’t get it, though. I’d much rather read about advances in technology or a new discovery in neuroscience. It fascinates me how the brain works, it’s like a complex puzzle with so many pieces—sometimes I wish I could dive into that world deeper than I already do. Maybe I should look into cognitive neuroscience more seriously, but it feels like it’s always changing, always new, and sometimes I wonder if I’d be overwhelmed by it all. I have a stack of books on my desk, some half-read, some waiting to be cracked open. Glad I got that bookshelf, but it’s running out of room. I should probably donate some of the ones I know I won’t touch again but part of me feels like I can’t let go. What if I need one for a random reference someday? That’s the part of me trained to think ahead, plan everything out, be meticulous about my choices. It’s exhausting. Sometimes it feels like I’m putting together a complex algorithm of my own life. Everything needs to fit perfectly, or else what’s the point? I wonder how others manage to be so carefree. Or maybe they’re not. Maybe it’s all a facade. It’s hard to tell. Why is it that my brain jumps from one thought to another so rapidly? I can feel the racing thoughts composing an orchestra of randomness, and yet I’m so focused on this one task. I can hear someone outside laughing loudly. Ugh, people can be kind of annoying, like why would you be so loud when some of us are trying to concentrate? I mean, I appreciate laughter, I really do, but sometimes it feels disruptive. I think about how I never really liked large gatherings. They drain me. I wonder if that’s strange. People talk about how fun it is, the energy in the room. But I feel like I’m in a swirl of chaos, trying to predict every interaction, every conversation, while they seem to just… float. I envy that carefreeness sometimes, but mostly it just makes me withdraw more. Maybe it’s just not in me. I like my quiet evenings alone with my thoughts and my books. I’d rather contemplate the mysteries of existence than engage in small talk about the weather or what happened last weekend. Yet, it feels like I should be doing more—connecting more. There’s this underlying guilt, like I’m missing a crucial part of life, but then I come back to my solitude and I feel relieved. I wonder if those connections are worth the energy they require. It’s a cycle, really. Okay, so earlier I had to write an essay for class. I got so into researching, I honestly lost track of time. I wish I had more opportunities to delve into subjects that pique my interest like that. There’s so much out there to learn. I was reading about the philosophy of language, how words shape our reality. It’s fascinating! But there’s always a point when I hit a wall, where I can’t take in any more information. At that point, it feels indulgent. Am I just hoarding knowledge like some kind of squirrel? At least I can find comfort in knowing that I’m constantly striving for more—more understanding, more clarity. I often think about how little I care for social approval. It’s liberating in a way, but then there are moments where I question what I’m doing. Am I missing out? I guess I just value integrity over fitting in. I’d prefer to be true to myself than conform. That’s probably why group projects can feel like torture. Everyone has different motivations, and I can’t help but just want to get things done right without all the extra fluff. But then I remind myself that it’s about finding a balance, learning to navigate through all these styles of working together. I wonder how others view me—am I seen as rigid? Probably. But I don’t mind being the reliable one. I like structure, just makes sense. Speaking of structure, I really should start organizing my time better. I have so many deadlines coming up and I can’t afford to fall behind. Time management is key in this chaotic world of academia. I get restless when I’m not productive. I can’t stand wasting potential. I need to remember to breathe, though. Sometimes my own expectations are suffocating. What am I even doing? I need to just focus, breathe, and keep moving forward. The exploration never really stops, does it?

I was thinking about how colors can change your mood like blue is calming and yellow is happy and sometimes I wonder why I struggle with picking colors for my room maybe it reflects something deeper like what if I need to pick a color that matches my personality but I’m not really sure what that is all the time. It’s funny, I mean, I like so many things but I can never settle down on one. I was looking at art online and there’s this one abstract piece with so many splashes of color, it’s like the artist was just throwing paint everywhere but it somehow makes sense and I think that’s like life sometimes, right? Just splashes of this and that and you can’t control it so you just flow with it but can you even really flow with it if you’re always overthinking everything. Well, maybe not overthinking but just thinking too much—I guess it’s like being friendly and open to experiences while also kind of floating, hovering in a dreamlike state where I want to explore but I don’t know how or when. Okay, so remember that time I tried to talk to a stranger at that coffee shop? I mean I didn’t really try hard but I made eye contact and smiled they smiled back and then I felt this rush of warmth like a little victory but then I felt stupid because I didn’t have anything to say. It’s like I want connection without the small talk but small talk is so inevitable. It’s like why is it so hard to jump into something deeper, something real when everyone just wants to talk about the weather or the latest TV show? But then again maybe people talk about those things because they’re scared of something deeper too. Like maybe everyone just wants to float on the surface and never dive down. I can dive down. I can dive down to those deeper places but it takes a lot of energy and maybe some people don’t have that or maybe they don’t want to. That’s fine too, I guess. I just wish people knew how much they matter, like the little things like the way someone laughs or their choice of shoes can tell you about their whole world if you just pay attention. Attention, that’s a tricky word because I want to absorb everything but sometimes it all feels overwhelming, like looking at a buffet of experiences and I can't decide on just one dish. I remember my friend mentioned they can’t understand how I can get lost in thought for so long, like it’s an escape but it’s also a prison of my own making, if that makes sense. Maybe it's just my brain—like a kaleidoscope spinning constantly, images shifting and changing. You can see patterns but you can’t really grasp them because they’re always transforming into something new. And speaking of transformations, I remember that concert I went to last month. The band was so good. I didn’t even know all their songs but it didn’t matter because everyone around me was just feeling it, you know? There’s something electric about a collective experience although when I’m in a crowd, sometimes I feel small like I’m just part of the background. But then, there’s this moment when the music swells and I forget everything else, even the fact I’m kind of an introvert. It’s liberating and then overwhelming when I realize how much energy is around me. How do people survive in a world that moves so fast? It’s hard. Sometimes I think I need to slow things down. I think about living in nature maybe just sitting by a river or under a tree with a book or a sketchpad—that sounds perfect I can just listen to the sounds of life, the rustle of leaves and water flowing and maybe birds chirping. I could draw or write poems. I wonder if I'll ever actually do that though. I think of all the things I want to do and how my brain is this giant library packed with ideas but the shelves are messy. I’ve got more ideas than I have time to execute and it makes me a bit restless. I’m too agreeable sometimes, I think, because I’ll go along with whatever someone else wants just to keep the peace. Is that a good thing or a bad thing? It’s both, I guess. It feels good to help others but then I wonder if I lose myself in the process. I mean, is there a balance? I’d like to think there’s a balance somewhere between helping others and being true to myself. Ah, balance. Maybe I should just focus on enjoying life moment by moment. Yes, that sounds nice. I should practice that more, live in the now and embrace all the colors and sounds and ideas without worrying about the mess or the noise or the deeper meanings that seem to elude me sometimes. Just be.

Okay so I just got out of class and everyone was really talkative today which was nice but also kind of overwhelming. I mean I love being around people, I really do, but sometimes it’s like too much energy? Like everyone’s trying to outdo each other with stories and jokes and I’m just sitting there nodding and hoping to join in but I don’t wanna interrupt anyone. I had this idea for a joke but it didn’t seem like the right moment to drop it in you know? I hate missing my chance to be funny because then I feel like I’m just a background character in my own life. This girl in the front was so charming though, so bubbly, and it makes me want to be like her but I also think I’ll never be that effortlessly cool. It’s kind of exhausting just keeping up sometimes but I can’t imagine being quiet or reserved. That’s a whole different kind of uncomfortable. Oh, and then someone mentioned a party on Friday night and I can’t decide if I want to go. On one hand, I live for that kind of thing. I mean, I’m the person who’s usually in the middle of the dance floor making a fool of myself and loving it. But then again, what if it’s not fun? What if it’s too loud or everyone’s in their own little groups? I hate that feeling of being out of place. And the thought of being at the party and not having anyone to talk to just makes me anxious already. But if I don’t go, then I’ll feel like I’m missing out on something big. FOMO is basically my middle name. Ugh but what if it’s a flop? That’s the worst case scenario. And I don’t know. I really hate flops. What’s even worse though is wasting an outfit and time getting ready for nothing. My roommate will probably give me a hard time if I don’t go. She’s so social and it makes me feel like I should be too. Like she just naturally connects with people and me? I try but it’s like I can feel the nerves bubbling under my skin. And that reminds me, I have these exams coming up and I’m starting to feel the pressure. It’s like every time I start studying, my brain just goes blank. I know I have to stay organized and on top of it but there’s so much pressure and I feel like I can’t breathe. I made color-coded notes which helped a bit but then I couldn’t find the green highlighter and that sent me into a spiral. Why do I get so worked up over such small things? I wish I could be more chill about that. It’s like everything has to be perfect or I freak out. I can feel that tightness in my chest. People say you just have to breathe and take things one step at a time but all I want is for all this stress to just go away. What if I mess up the exams? What if I don’t do well enough? I can’t even think about that right now because then I just start going down this rabbit hole of anxiety. I need to just stick to my study schedule, stay disciplined, but no too disciplined, right? Because I also need to have fun. Balance. That’s the word. I keep hearing people say that, but it’s so hard to find it. Balance. I feel like I am my own worst enemy. Sometimes I just want to be that person who doesn’t give a damn about grades or what anyone thinks. Just carefree and living their best life. Why can’t I just do that? Then again, I really can’t imagine being so open about everything. I feel like I need to protect myself, like if I let people see all the chaos in my head, they wouldn’t want to be around me. That’s silly right? But it’s how I feel. I keep things in, tucked away, and when I do let people in, it’s like this big rush of noise and life. Am I too much? I feel like I’m too much sometimes. Anyway, there’s this coffee shop nearby that I’ve been dying to check out, I heard they have amazing pastries. And maybe if I go there, I can just sit and write down my thoughts, sort through everything a little. I love coffee, that smell, and just the vibe of a busy coffee shop — it feels like a second home, even if I don’t know anyone there. I should invite my roommate to go with me. She's great company, very supportive. We get along so well. Maybe it would help me feel more balanced? But then again, what if I just distract myself and end up not studying? It’s such a push and pull in my head. Crazy how much I care about what everyone thinks but then how I really want to have fun. Need to figure that out. I guess I just keep going back and forth.

I can’t help but dwell on the number of unread emails piling up in my inbox. It's overwhelming, really. What if I miss something important? A reminder? A deadline? Ugh, deadlines. Why do they have to exist? I really should get better at managing my time but, honestly, what’s the point? I put things off until the last minute not because I’m a procrastinator, but because I’m just not interested enough to do them ahead of time. Everyone else seems so organized, so put together. Sometimes I wonder if they’re faking it. Are they like me? Maybe they’re just more adept at hiding their chaos. It’s exhausting to be around people who seem to have their lives in order. I can’t even keep my room clean. I’ve got clothes everywhere. Laundry? What’s laundry? It’s a mountain of fabric I keep ignoring. I can smell the dirty socks wafting through the door. My mom would be horrified. She’s always on my case about cleanliness. But seriously, life is too short to worry about socks. Then there’s the whole socializing thing. Group projects in class? Kill me. I’d rather crawl into a hole. The thought of trying to communicate my ideas and having to rely on others just makes my skin crawl. They look at you, anticipating your input, and I just freeze. Like, what do they want from me? I can’t just throw out ideas like it’s nothing. Then there's the anxiety. What if my idea is stupid? What if they laugh? I can’t take it. So instead, I sit there quietly, nodding and pretending to be engaged while my mind is racing about what I should have said. The silence is deafening. But some people don’t get that. They just keep talking, and I can feel the pressure building in my chest. It’s suffocating. I want to scream, but all I can do is smile and nod along. That one time in high school when I was forced to give a presentation? Never again. My hands were shaking, my throat was dry, and I could feel the heat rising in my face. Everyone’s staring, judging. What if I forget everything? I probably blacked out a bit. I just don’t want to be in front of people. I’d take a thousand math problems over speaking in public. Those math problems don’t judge. They’re just numbers. Just patterns. But people? They’re unpredictable. I keep picturing judgmental faces in my mind that aren’t even there. It’s like a curse. I want to be invisible sometimes. Just fade away into the background. Yet, I find myself in these group chats. I refuse to engage, but my phone buzzes non-stop. I see the messages piling up, and I think about what they’re all talking about. I should probably join in. What if they’re planning something fun? What if they invite me? But then I remember how it goes. I’ll show up, and then what? I’ll stand awkwardly in the corner while they all laugh. I’m not laughing. I never laugh. It’s like my humor is broken. I see memes that everyone else is sharing but they never resonate. I think, “What’s so funny about that?” I can’t find the joy in their inside jokes, and they notice. I can see them glance at me, wondering why I’m not chuckling along, and I can feel their pity. Or worse, their annoyance. Ugh. There’s also the constant worry about my future. What am I doing with my life? Everyone’s got plans. They’re so driven. I’m just coasting along, surviving somehow. I think about jobs and internships and it absolutely terrifies me. The thought of being stuck in an office – or worse, a cubicle – is enough to make my skin crawl. I want to be free but at the same time I’m terrified of the unpredictability ahead. What if I’m just floating through life without purpose? I try to convince myself it’s okay to not know. Everyone’s figuring it out. But deep down, I know I’m not like everyone else. I can’t shake that feeling. It never leaves me. Then there are days when I think I should just give in, stop resisting the advice of everyone around me. Maybe I should try to be more outgoing? But it’s exhausting. I don’t want to put on a mask and pretend to be someone else. Maybe I’ll just stay quiet. Better to observe than to risk being part of the chaos. I feel like I’m just stuck in this loop. It never ends. I should really just do laundry, close those emails, and stop thinking about how alone I feel at parties. But it’s all just too much. I’m tired. So tired.

So, I was just thinking about how I really don’t mind being alone. I mean, there’s comfort in solitude, isn’t there? Like, people go out to socialize and party, and I’m just here, scrolling through my notes, making lists of things to do, feeling perfectly content with my own company. I don’t need the chaos of loud parties or crowded spaces. In fact, the whole small talk thing is exhausting. I always wonder why people feel the need to chat about the weather when we could get right to the point, but I guess that’s just how social interactions work. I don’t want to offend anyone by skipping those casual niceties. It’s always a balancing act, social norms are tricky. Then there’s this assignment looming over me. I should just get it done, but procrastination is sneaky. I tell myself, “Oh, I’ll start it in just a few minutes,” but those minutes turn into hours easily. I like sticking to a plan, but I find myself sidetracked by the most ridiculous things, like organizing my books by color instead of author. Why does that even matter? It’s not like I’m going to memorize the psychology principles I need for that exam just because my books look pretty. I guess it's the satisfaction of crossing something off my to-do list that appeals to me. I feel accomplished. Feels good to tick the boxes, even if it’s just organizing my bookshelf or cleaning my desk. Sometimes I wonder if I’m too agreeable. Like, I say yes too often when I probably should say no. I want to help, but I can end up stretching myself too thin. Group projects are tough. I end up doing most of the work to keep the peace. It’s not that I mind helping others, but there’s this underlying frustration when people don’t pull their weight. I don’t want to be the bad guy and confront them about it. I keep hoping they’ll surprise me and step up, but they never really do. This semester, I need to establish boundaries. Just say it: “No, I can't take on that task.” Easy in theory, but actually doing it? A whole different story. I wonder how people make small talk at parties. I mean, what do you say? “How’s the weather?” “What’s your major?” It always feels forced. I’d rather get to know someone through deeper conversations, but that’s not how it usually works, is it? Most conversations float around the surface. I’d love to skip to the part where we talk about beliefs, passions, and dreams. But that’s risky, isn’t it? Who wants to open up too much when it’s so easy to just nod and smile? I prefer those rare moments when I find someone who feels the same way. It’s like finding a rare gem in a sea of small talk. Then there are times I wonder if I should be more open to new experiences. Everyone's off traveling, trying new foods, doing spontaneous things, and I'm here, craving my routine, sticking to what I know. It feels safe. There’s comfort in knowing the outcome. I don't do so well with uncertainty. I prefer to study something thoroughly before diving into it. Sometimes that means missing out, but my mind tells me it’s okay. “You don’t need to be different,” it whispers. “There’s nothing wrong with being your own person.” But I can't help but feel a twinge of curiosity whenever I see friends post about their wild weekends. But then again, the thought of being in a crowded place just for the sake of being spontaneous sends me into a bit of a spiral. And about that one friend who changes plans last minute. Ugh. It drives me crazy. I respect spontaneity, but some consistency would be nice. Like, if we planned to study together Tuesday at five, let’s just stick with that, right? Why is it so hard for people to keep their word? I know life happens, but come on. I appreciate reliability. Makes life easier when you know what to expect. And I mean, I get it. I really do. People are balancing classes, jobs, friendships, everything. But I put effort into planning things. I wish others would too. Anyway, I need to get back to that assignment. It’s just a paper, but it seems harder than it is. I’ll be fine once I sit down and focus. The fear of not doing well hangs over me, but I’ll push through. I always do. That’s what being conscientious is about, right? And honestly, it’s a relief to know I can rely on myself for that. I mean, even if it takes a bit longer to get things done sometimes, I always make sure the work is solid. Just keep going, one step at a time, and everything will fall into place.

I keep glancing at my phone even though it's just a notification for a group chat I’ve been half a part of for weeks. It’s mostly memes and inside jokes that I don’t always get, but I can’t help but feel this pull to be included, to jump in, contribute, and get some likes you know? That satisfaction of a little thumbs up or a laughing emoji is like instant validation, it’s the best feeling. I often find myself thinking about social stuff, like who’s hanging out with who, and I hope I’m not missing out. Did I get invited to that party? Maybe I should text Jamie? I wish I was better at remembering things. I mean, what do I even bring to the table? I like people. I really do! The more, the merrier. But sometimes I get anxious like what if they don’t like my jokes, or what if they think I’m too much? Ugh, there’s always that nagging voice in my head it's exhausting. It’s louder when I’m about to join a group or even when I meet up with friends. I need to focus on the fun, the laughter, the good vibes. But recently it’s been hard to shake that feeling that I can't measure up somehow. I know I shouldn’t think like that, but here we are. I can’t stand conflicts either, speaking of anxiety. I mean, who has time for that? If someone’s upset, I just want to fix it. Like, can we hug it out? I just want everyone to be happy and have a good time, but some people don’t realize that not everyone wants to be babied. But how can you not want to be friends with everyone? I can’t understand it. I don’t get the satisfaction of being mean or keeping grudges. I’m all about that positivity, which is probably why I end up feeling battered by my own feelings. Oh! Yesterday at lunch, I spilled sauce all over my shirt. I was trying to impress Claire, of course, showing off how I can handle the spicy stuff, but it backfired—literally. I was laughing, but inside I was dying a little. Like, ‘Oh great, now she thinks I can’t manage basic eating’. Honestly, I gotta work on my coordination. I told myself I’d wear the old shirt again today because it loves chaos like the rest of my life. I can't keep things neat—my notes are all over the place from class, too.
