I should've organized them, but then I'd miss out on the next flash of inspiration or a conversation just bubbling up next to me. And then there are the days I just want to stay in and chill. The social pressure can be overwhelming, though; everyone seems so excited to go out and do stuff, and I just sit there thinking about how comfy it is to not put on jeans. Why does it feel so hard to say ‘no’? I end up going anyway, but then I’m the one who spills the soda or trips over a chair. I mean, really? Maybe I’m just not good at managing all these social situations because I don’t plan ahead. But I want to enjoy everything! It’s the thrill of being around friends, sharing stories, and cracking jokes. What if I just started texting everyone to see if they want to hang out with me? But then what if they all say no? That’s just one more scenario for my anxious mind to munch on. I like to think I’m friendly enough, but I don’t always know how to keep those friendships alive. Sometimes, I just assume if I haven’t heard from someone, they’re avoiding me, but maybe they’re busy? It’s such a circus in my head. I crave connection but fear rejection at the same time. Why can’t I just chill? It's like there’s a giant tug-of-war going on and I’m the rope. I remember being a kid, making friends easily. Like, what happened? Was it school, or did I just overthink everything as I grew up? I see my cousins and siblings, and they’re all doing those fun activities, bonding over everything, and here I am worrying if I should’ve texted them back sooner. I sometimes think that if I could just calm down and stop getting lost in my head, life would be easier. I guess that's why I need to keep moving, keep talking. The moment I stop is the moment my thoughts turn ugly, spiraling down into doubt. I’ll just keep juggling my social life with these unpredictable feelings until I figure it out. Life, huh? Why does it have to be complicated all the time?

Okay so I was just thinking about that party last weekend. Totally wild, like everyone was there, and I danced like an idiot but it was fun. Who cares if I’m terrible at it? The music was bumping, and the energy in the room was just insane. I love when people are all hyped up, you know? But then again, I don't really get why some people can't just let loose. Life is too short for awkwardness. If you mess up, just laugh about it and keep going. I don’t always get the whole “being nice” thing. Why should I always care about what others think? Just be real. The less I have to filter, the better. Then there’s the whole situation with my roommate. She keeps saying I need to clean up more. Ugh, like I don’t mind a bit of clutter. It’s just stuff. It’s not like I have rats or anything. I mean, I can’t be the only one with a few empty snack bags lying around, right? Her need for order is exhausting. Maybe I should just tell her to get over it. But then she gets moody, and it’s not like I want to deal with that. I don’t like moody people. I want things to stay upbeat. It’s like she doesn’t get that not everyone is gonna conform to her neat little world. I don’t have time to worry about that, I have more important things going on. Like making plans for the next hangout, which I’m definitely going to be the one organizing because if I leave it to them, it’s gonna be a total snooze fest. Speaking of planning, what’s with everyone posting their travel plans on social media? Like, we get it, you're in Bali sipping lattes and looking happy. It’s cool and all, but can we just talk about real-life stuff? I don’t care about following someone’s perfect little trip. I want to hear about the mess-ups and the spontaneous adventures. I want to hear about the weird food they tried or the time they got lost in a sketchy neighborhood. Also, how can people afford to travel all the time? I barely have enough for takeout. I thought college was supposed to be where we all live lavishly on ramen and dreams. Instead, everyone’s flexing on Instagram and I'm just here debating if I should even get lunch today. Okay, but honestly, who thought it was a good idea to have group projects? They should ban that nonsense. Nobody ever wants to take charge. First, you get assigned to a group, and then it becomes this weird dance of figuring out who’s actually going to do the work. I want to just do my part, get it done, and move on. But nooo, I end up chasing everyone down like some kind of academic babysitter. I swear, I’d just rather take a solo grade and call it a day. But then I think, is that too harsh? I sometimes wonder if I should be a little nicer, but it’s tiring to play nice all the time. I guess it’s about boundaries or something—they keep telling us about that in my psychology class. Oh, I remembered I should probably grab a snack. I’ll just check what I have in the fridge—probably some leftover pizza. Pizza is the best. You can eat it for breakfast, lunch, or dinner, and it’s always good no matter what time you eat it. And the toppings—don’t even get me started on toppings! Pineapple on pizza? Absolutely, I’m all for it. People can debate over that for ages, but I reckon if you like it, eat it. Don’t care if it’s controversial. Food is food. And what’s up with people who don’t like spicy food? I mean, come on. It’s not that hard to handle a little heat! I can’t even imagine a life without jalapeños. And those hot sauces? Amazing! I keep stocking up on them, but then I feel like I have too many and they start to collect dust. But then again, can you ever really have too many hot sauces? I don’t think so. They just add so much flavor to everything. Okay, I should get back to that paper due on Friday. Not that I’m worried about it, I’ve done this last-minute thing too many times to stress. But let’s be honest, I probably won’t even get to it until the night before. It’ll be fine. I can crank it out. Time management is overrated anyway, right? I mean, who actually enjoys following rules? I’d rather just go with the flow, see where the night takes me. This life is all about spontaneity, not plotting everything out like some kind of robot. Who wants that? I’m just here to have fun and enjoy the ride.

okay so I was just thinking about how much I love meeting new people it's like every time I walk into a room I feel this buzz like hello here I am everyone let’s connect it's exhilarating like an instant energy boost I remember that one time at the party last month I just walked up to this group and started talking about the most random stuff and they were all laughing and it felt great as if we had been friends forever it just flows naturally I mean why not right life is short and there's so many interesting stories to hear from others like that time I met the guy who had backpacked through seven countries in one summer and he had these insane tales of crazy adventures and near-misses like that time he almost got lost in the Andean mountains or met a llama who stole his sandwich it's wild how just one conversation can lead to so many ideas and inspiration but then again sometimes I wonder how people can be so closed off like there’s this wall that goes up as soon as they hear a new voice or a joke or an opinion that’s different from theirs it literally baffles me not everyone sees the world through the same lens and that’s totally okay like embrace it! what’s the point of living in a bubble just to keep your ideas safe don’t you want to mix things up? I think that’s why I love going to different events or workshops because it pushes my boundaries and exposes me to so many ways of thinking and being the last art workshop I went to was mind-blowing I mean I can't draw to save my life but just being around all those creative people focusing on their own interpretations and ideas was electrifying and so freeing I was like yes I love this feeling and then my mind drifts back to social media, like it's this double-edged sword you know? on one hand, you can connect with people all over the globe and share your thoughts and experiences like nothing I’ve ever seen before but then again there’s all that negativity too it’s like a breeding ground for arguments and misunderstandings and I just can't with the trolling I honestly don’t get how some people thrive on conflict I mean what’s the return on investment for that? I’d rather post a silly meme or promote something cool going on rather than get dragged into some ridiculous debate about pineapple on pizza or whatever it is they fight about these days oh and speaking of food I really want to try that new Thai place that opened up down the street I love how food can bring people together like the last time I went out with a big group we couldn't decide where to eat so we ended up at this fusion taco joint that had everything from kimchi to jerk chicken and the flavors were insane not even kidding I think we spent that whole night eating, laughing, sharing stories and just enjoying each other’s company there’s truly something magical about sharing a meal like it breaks down those initial barriers and allows you to connect on a different level also, I need to get back to my studies but ugh who cares about all the textbooks right now? I mean sure, deadlines are looming, but there’s just something about the thrill of spontaneity that is so much more appealing like the other night I randomly decided to go on a midnight drive with some friends just blasting tunes and singing off-key and it was liberating, just throwing caution to the wind for a while it's the kind of stuff that makes you feel alive I remember last week I went to this lecture on sustainable living and wow the ideas presented were just so radical and forward-thinking I was practically on the edge of my seat the whole time I can’t fathom how someone can just live their life without thinking about these things like sustainability is the future and honestly why wouldn't you want to be part of that conversation? I started creating this vision board at home with all these ideas and initiatives I want to implement in my life it’s like planning a roadmap to become the best version of myself I guess I mean, who doesn't want that? It's all about pushing the envelope trying new things and not being afraid to stand out or take a different path I love how diverse the world is and how there’s always something new to learn and explore and just when you think you got everything figured out life throws you a curveball like that time I learned how to rollerblade at twenty-one, and it was like learning to walk all over again but in the best possible way just a reminder that you're never too old to try something new so yeah just let it all happen don’t stress the small stuff because honestly, it’s all about the connections and the experiences right? we make our own adventures and live for the moments that take our breath away or make us laugh until we cry and if I can fill my life with that then I will be one happy person!

Cereal boxes are such weird shapes. Like, how do they even decide the dimensions? Anyway, I was thinking about breakfast and how it can really set the mood for the day, but honestly, I've been skipping it sometimes. Just go straight for lunch. I love lunch. Sandwiches, wraps, anything you can throw together and eat on the go. I usually add way too much stuff, like did I really need three kinds of cheese? But it all feels so good when you’re eating it. Food is like a party in your mouth, right? Speaking of parties, I really want to throw a spontaneous gathering. Like, text everyone and just say, “Come over for snacks and games.” I thrive off that energy, the excitement that comes with those last-minute plans that make you feel alive! It's funny how my friends are a mix of personalities. Some are super chill, and I’m the one jumping in with ideas. "Let’s go to this new art exhibit!" or “Have you tried that taco truck?” I get so pumped thinking about it, and then everyone else is like, “Yeah, okay, that sounds cool,” but I can see the flicker of hesitation in their eyes. Sometimes I wish they’d ride the wave with me more. That’s the thing about me—I’m always ready for the next adventure, but I want them to jump with me. Everyone’s so busy, I get it, but sometimes I think people won’t remember the textbook stuff years down the line; they’ll remember the experiences we shared, the laughter, the craziness. What if we just said yes more often? It wouldn’t hurt. Oh, that reminds me of last weekend! We went to that music festival, and it was a blast. The energy was insane. So many different people, all vibing together, dancing like nobody was watching. I love that feeling—the crowd swaying in sync, laughter ringing out, the smell of popcorn and sweat mingling in the air. I remember grabbing a giant pretzel and sharing it with my friend, who said it was the only way to eat a pretzel, and I absolutely agreed. What’s better than sharing food with friends? Sometimes we get sidetracked, and I end up talking about random things like alien theories or how I think we should have holographic concerts. Who wouldn’t want to see their favorite band in 3D? It’s like being in a video game! But sometimes, I get these stray thoughts about future plans or what I’m going to do once college is over. I know I should probably be thinking of internships or job hunting, but honestly, the idea of sitting in an office for hours makes me feel boxed in. I can be social and engage, but give me a desk job and my spirit sits like a deflated balloon. What if I just took off traveling instead? The world is huge, and there’s so much to explore. I should just grab a backpack, buy a plane ticket, and go. I wonder how long it would take before I started missing my friends. Probably not long at all! I’d just make new ones wherever I go. I mean, that’s the beauty of it, right? Meeting people from all over, learning about their lives, their cultures. My phone is always buzzing. I should probably turn it off sometimes, but I get so FOMO. What if there’s a party happening somewhere and I’m not invited? It’s that nagging voice that tells me to keep engaging, keep chatting, keep planning. I love planning! Even if half the time it falls through, it’s like I’m gathering all this potential energy, ready to explode into a good time. And when things do work out spontaneously? It's worth it. Just last week, we ended up at that hidden speakeasy downtown because we weren't set on any plans—they had live jazz, and we just danced and laughed until our sides hurt. I try to be understanding, especially when friends are going through tough times. Sometimes they need space, and I get it. I just wish they’d see that I’m always here if they need distractions or a shoulder to lean on. I can’t help but feel fiercely protective, wanting to gather everyone and cheer them up. But everyone deals with stuff differently, and I respect that. It’s about finding balance. I’m the loud and silly one, the sunshine in the group, but I can also be the listener. That’s what matters, right? Building those connections, having those deep chats, and making memories that last a lifetime. It’s all part of the ride, isn’t it? I just want all of us to enjoy the party while we can!

It's so exciting when I think about how many different things I can explore in a day, like I just stumbled on this new coffee shop and the vibe was amazing, so many people talking and laughing, and I felt this rush because everyone was engaging so much, I wanted to jump in and ask all about their lives, what their plans are, or just share the joy of being there, like a little community forming in one tiny space. And then there's anxiety creeping in like “oh but what if they don’t want to talk to me?” but I just tell myself that people love to share stories if you give them the chance. Then I think about how I always want to connect, like I crave it so much, it’s like oxygen sometimes. It takes me to such high places, like when I went to that album release party last week, the energy was through the roof, people were dancing, I joined in, even though I can’t dance to save my life, but no one cared, we were all just having fun, living in that moment. Then the thought hits me about how I need to finish that research paper for psychology class. It’s due next week and I haven't even started, yet part of me believes that if I just dive in with a bunch of ideas, it'll all magically come together, and with my notebook open, filled with little sketches and thoughts, I can't help but feel inspiration bubbling, but also nervous energy—what if it's not good enough? But it’s not the end of the world. I’ll get it done. I always do… right? I’ve got to stay organized though. I have these color-coded notes that make me feel like I have my life together. I love making lists! Lists for groceries, to-do lists for the day, even a fun list for my next adventure. The more colorful the paper, the better. Oh, and the other day, I was thinking about how many things I love—art, music, people—and how much I just need to absorb everything around me like a sponge. It’s overwhelming and exhilarating at the same time. I want to try that pottery class but then, what if I'm terrible at it and everyone makes masterpieces while I'm sitting there struggling to shape clay into a lump? But then again, I could meet new people! That's always worth the risk. I’ve got that fear of failure, mixed with this excitement about the unknown. It's like a rollercoaster ride in my head. Sometimes I wish I could just turn off that voice that says “don’t mess up” but then I think, who would I be without it? Maybe a little less motivated, or a little too carefree? I keep coming back to the idea of travel. It's such a big part of who I am. I want to see the world, all these cultures just waiting to be explored, the food, the music, the people, my mind races with all the places I want to go, and this urgency builds up. But, of course, there’s that crippling thought about budgets and time constraints, like how am I going to fit in a trip to Europe before graduation? I could just be spontaneous, right? Last minute tickets and hostels, just pack a bag and go. But what about responsibilities? What about work-study? And then I think about how nice it would be to just let loose, to be carefree for a moment. Sometimes I overthink everything so much that I feel paralyzed. I want to be the girl who travels to Japan for the cherry blossoms but also say yes to every coffee invite because connections matter so much to me. And friendships, they are everything. I want to be everyone’s friend, I want them to feel supported and loved. But will they think I’m too much? Too friendly? That’s the thing about being high in agreeableness; it can feel like I’m walking a delicate line between being open and being overwhelming. What if they don’t want to share their secrets with me or I accidentally push too hard? I care so deeply about what others think of me and it keeps spinning like a wheel in my head, and while I love discussions about feelings and relationships, sometimes it feels like a juggling act. Oh, and here’s a funny thought—I get so excited about the tiniest things. Like a new book release, or a friend's engagement. I think about how my heart just swells with joy when I hear good news, like celebrating every little achievement as if it’s some monumental event, although I sometimes feel like I’m a little over the top for it. But life is short, right? I want to celebrate everything. It’s about the moments we create. I think I can finally do that pottery class. I’ll bring cookies for everyone. That will break the ice. And then we’ll laugh and create something beautiful, like the memories we’re all making together.

okay so I was just thinking about how wild it is that there are so many genres of music out there like sometimes I’m in the mood for jazz and other times I need something upbeat like pop and then there’s always that moment when you discover an artist that just hits you perfectly and you’re like why did I not know about this person before it’s like a little secret you get to share with everyone and when you do it feels so good to be the one introducing someone to something they’ll love it’s like giving a gift but without the wrapping or the cost haha but then there are those moments when you talk about a band and everyone looks at you like you have three heads it’s weird how tastes can be so different like someone is obsessed with heavy metal and then I’m just over here vibing to acoustic covers in my room which by the way has way too many plants I think they might be taking over at this point I mean just last week I realized I couldn’t see my desk anymore because the pothos has grown so wild but then again plants are kind of like people you know they need attention and care otherwise they just wither away and then I wonder if I’m giving enough attention to my friends or if I’m that person who’s always talking and never listening and oh man I really hope I’m not that friend because I love hearing what everyone else has to say everyone has such interesting stories especially when they’re animated about them I mean, who doesn’t light up when they talk about something they’re passionate about? also, sometimes I feel like I have a million ideas in my head like I want to travel to Japan and eat sushi while taking pictures of cherry blossoms then I turn around and think about going to a music festival and just dancing like a fool it’s hard to pin down one thing sometimes I wish I could just hit pause on life and experience everything the world has to offer and not worry about the consequences like growing old or losing touch with people I care about but that’s just it isn’t it? we think so much about what could happen that we miss what’s right in front of us like me sitting here thinking about how lucky I am to have such great friends and family I mean last weekend we had this amazing barbecue at my friend’s house and everyone just brought something different and it turned into this huge potluck of deliciousness and laughter I swear we laughed so hard my sides hurt and isn’t that what life’s about anyway? Just sharing moments and creating memories that you can look back on while sitting in bed at 2 AM like I am now and thinking what if I had gotten my life together sooner or spent more time studying instead of procrastinating by searching random facts about space and learning that octopuses have three hearts like who even came up with that but it’s cool because it just shows how expansive knowledge can be and how fascinating the world works but I often get lost in the rabbit holes of the internet sometimes I’m just scrolling and then suddenly realize I’ve been on the same page for an hour but I think it’s kind of fun just diving into the unknown even if it’s a little chaotic it keeps things interesting which is like how I think life should be just a little chaotic but also grounded in the people you love like my cousin who is living in Europe right now and keeps sending these amazing pictures of her adventures and she’s always like you have to visit it’s so beautiful and I keep saying I will but then life gets busy, right? there’s always something that gets in the way like assignments and work deadlines and don’t even get me started on the stress of exams I mean like can’t they just make them open book so we can actually enjoy learning instead of cramming everything the night before and filling our brains with unnecessary facts that we forget the moment we walk out the exam hall? but I guess that’s part of the journey learning to balance work and play and figuring out what matters like my friends they ground me in a way that makes everything feel lighter like when we sit in a circle just chatting for hours about everything from our dreams to our silly little fears like what if I never find my passion or what if I end up alone someday and they always remind me that it’s okay to feel lost sometimes because that’s when the best adventures happen and I totally believe that because every time I thought I had it all figured out something completely unexpected came up and changed my path which is kinda scary but also thrilling and I think it’s important to embrace that uncertainty because life is all about the twists and turns and you never know what’s waiting around the corner waiting to surprise you but then again sometimes having those little moments of quiet helps too like when I just lay on the grass and watch the clouds go by and make shapes out of them or the feeling of letting go of everything for a second to just breathe because it reminds me that life is so much more than grades or jobs or anything it’s literally about the journey and the connections we make and still looking forward to whatever’s next because I want to keep discovering and learning and growing and what even is this life if we’re not constantly evolving right?

Okay so the clock says 3:47 PM and I just got back from that group project meeting and everyone was so fired up, which is great. I love when the energy is high like that, and we brainstormed so many ideas it was hard to keep track but in a good way. I noticed Jamie was really into it, like super passionate, talking about how we can incorporate social media strategies into our project. I mean, it makes sense, right? Everyone's on their phones all the time, might as well use that to our advantage. I ended up suggesting we create some funny TikToks to grab attention and everyone laughed and seemed excited. I wonder if I should have pushed for that more. Maybe I should've taken the reins instead of just throwing it out there casually. But it looks like we’re going to really crush this project. I can feel it. Then I walked past the library and saw that huge poster about a poetry slam coming up next week and thought, "Wow, I should really join." But then I reminded myself that I don’t really write poetry, weird right? I love words and language but poetry? I get nervous with that. I can articulate thoughts and ideas in conversations but writing them down in that artistic way feels different. But it could be fun to try! It’s all about stretching my comfort zone, right? I can totally see myself on stage with a mic, or maybe not, just standing there, freezing, while everyone stares at me. I’d need to practice. I should go to the event, definitely. Support my friends or maybe even meet new people who are also nervous. I love people, they bring so much to the table when you really get to know them. I started doodling in my notebook during the meeting, just random shapes and lines that don’t really mean anything, but I can’t help it. My hands feel the need to move while my mind races through ideas. Doodling is like a brain dump. Speaking of brain dumps, I need to organize my notes for the history class tomorrow. It’s all about the French Revolution, which is pretty interesting when you think about how it changed everything—the way we think about rights, freedom, governance. I mean, that’s still relevant today! And I love that! I enjoy discussions that dive deep into those connections. Sometimes I catch myself wondering how the ideas from those historical events seep into our daily lives. I wish the class would let us debate more, that would be so cool. Getting to throw around ideas with classmates and seeing where we land. A little chaos in a controlled environment. And I’d totally enjoy it. Speaking of controlled chaos, I can’t believe how busy next week is! Three exams! I’m a little anxious but not too much—just the right amount of adrenaline to keep me on my toes. I find that I actually thrive in those situations. Like, my brain clicks and things come together. Study groups help me a lot because I get to bounce ideas off others, which makes remembering things easier. Plus, I can’t keep my mouth shut, you know? I love explaining concepts. It makes the material stick. I remember last semester, I led a study group and everyone was vibing. We had snacks, and I brought some weird flavored chips as a joke, and they ended up being a hit. Like why do they taste so good? I should’ve gotten the brand, they deserve a shout-out. I think about how those little moments build relationships. It’s the shared laughter, and the late-night cramming, the “oh my gosh, did we really just talk for three hours?” conversations that forge connections. I actually adore how spontaneous everything feels. I thrive on the unexpected and the new. I mean, one night we decided on a whim to hit up that karaoke bar, and I ended up belting out an 80s ballad, which I totally butchered but everyone was singing along. It felt amazing to just let loose! What else? Oh, right! I promised to go volunteer at that animal shelter this weekend. Dogs are my ultimate weakness. I can’t help it, they’re just so pure and loving. I love the way they wag their tails and bounce around when you walk in. I could literally spend hours there. If only I could take one home… but my apartment is tiny and there’s no way my roommate would agree to it—that’s a definite no. I might need to find a way to convince her. Then there's that upcoming concert. I think about how it’s going to be such a blast. I love live music. The energy in the crowd, everyone singing together, it’s unbeatable. I think I’ll wear that bright yellow shirt to match the vibe—there’s something about colors that amps up the whole experience! Time's ticking, and I can see little bits of paper scattered around my desk. I really need to clean up. But then again, isn’t a little mess part of the artistic chaos of life? Okay, focus. There's so much to do, but I’m just so excited to see where all these experiences take me.

Okay so I’m sitting here thinking about my to-do list again I always think about my to-do list it's like my brain is a giant sticky note cluttered and full at the same time. I have that paper due next week and I’m honestly pretty excited about it because I chose a cool topic but the pressure is already creeping in. Will I do it right? What if I forget something important? I hate that feeling of forgetting, like when you’re in class and the professor asks a question and you just blank out. It’s so embarrassing. I can’t believe I even raised my hand that one time only to freeze on the answer... and the whole class just stares. Ugh, it was the worst. And then there’s my roommate, he always wants to hang out and I feel bad for saying no but sometimes I just need that quiet time you know? He means well, he really does, but I can get overwhelmed really easily. I wonder if he thinks I’m standoffish? I just prefer solitude sometimes, and it’s not personal, it’s just me. I’d rather curl up with a book than go out to a party where there are too many people and too much noise. Plus, social anxiety is real and it just hovers around like a shadow. I guess it’s part of being high on neuroticism. What if I say something stupid? What if I trip over my own feet trying to leave the room? It’s like a never-ending cycle of “what ifs” and I’m just not sure how to break it. But then again I like being open to new experiences at least that part’s good. Like I want to try new things, see new places even if I agonize over every little detail before hand. I don’t want to be stuck in my own bubble. I wonder if it’s possible to find a balance between wanting to explore and wanting to hide away in a cozy corner. I think about traveling a lot, but I always stop myself because... money. Everything costs so much and saving is like pulling teeth. Not to mention the whole process of planning. Do I really want to endure airport security? It’s so stressful and I get so anxious about making sure I have everything packed. And what if my flight gets delayed? Or heaven forbid, canceled? Speaking of canceled, I heard about that new cafe opening up nearby and it sounds cool like a little community hub but also just thinking about going there gives me a headache. I wonder what the vibe will be like. Will there be too many people? What if I can’t find a seat? And I keep imagining myself just sitting there sipping a coffee, pretending to work but really just people watching. Is that weird? Probably. But it feels safer than actually getting involved in conversations. Plus people are fascinating. Every person has a story, a background, and I want to know them but at the same time I want to stay at arm’s length. I should probably make an appointment too. Therapy could be helpful. I’ve thought about it a lot especially since I just need to talk to someone who gets it. It’s hard to articulate everything swirling in my head and I want to learn how to manage my anxiety better. I mean it’s exhausting just to go through the daily routine and it feels like I’m juggling emotions 24/7. I need that space to breathe, to express everything pent up. I just want a release. Sometimes I think about how people seem so carefree and I’m over here overthinking every single move. I guess being high in agreeableness means I care a lot about how people perceive me but then it gets tangled up with my own insecurities and it all blends into this heavy fog I can’t seem to shake off. And okay, food. Food is always there but I don’t like cooking too much it stresses me out and the dishes pile up and it just becomes this whole chaotic thing. Eating out can be overwhelming too like deciding where to go and what to order and if it’s worth it and what if I don’t like it and then I feel bad wasting money. God, it’s all just a lot. I keep saying I’ll just stick with cereal or something simple but even that feels like a struggle sometimes like I better not get tired of it or it’ll just be another thing I’m anxious about. Why is everything so complicated? But then I remember moments that make me smile. Like the time I went to a concert alone and I was nervous at first but then once the music started it felt like I was part of something bigger. The energy, the crowd, I felt like I could just lose myself in the moment. Or that time I helped a stranger find their way and they smiled at me like I made their day just a little better. I guess those are the moments that keep me going, the tiny connections that remind me that even amidst the chaos, there’s a sliver of joy to be found somewhere.
